faq.question: How many days are ideal for a Kashmir trip?
faq.answer: An ideal Kashmir trip should be around 7-10 days, if you're looking forward to a comprehensive experience. However, for a shorter trip of 4 to 5 days, you can experience only a few tourist places and interests. Depending on your Kashmir trip plan, you'll need to consider the experiences and locations you want to enjoy, as well as the pace of travel.

faq.question: Is Kashmir safe for tourists?
faq.answer: Yes, Kashmir is relatively safe for tourists. The Kashmir tourist packages planned are relatively safe, especially for covering destinations such as Pahalgam, Gulmarg and Srinagar. In the past few months, there have been significant improvements across infrastructure and security, especially for the near-zero crime rate for tourists. Kashmir has a very welcoming environment for tourists as well.

faq.question: What is the best time to visit Kashmir for snow?
faq.answer: Winter is the best time to visit Kashmir if you wish to experience snow. Therefore, you can book winter Kashmir holiday packages, between December and February, especially to experience snow. During winter, Kashmir receives maximum rainfall, and the valley will turn into a winter wonderland. January brings heavy snowfall in Kashmir, which is the coldest month.

faq.question: What kind of clothes should I carry?
faq.answer: You must carry warm clothes to visit Kashmir. Woollen, fleece, and thermals should be good. It is advisable to wear your clothes in layers. You will need thermal trousers, a windproof jacket, and comfortable shoes. If you're planning to visit any cultural place, make sure to carry a headscarf to respect local customs. Other essential accessories include sunglasses, gloves and woollen socks.

faq.question: Can I use my mobile phone in Kashmir?
faq.answer: Yes, you can use your mobile phone in Kashmir. However, if you have a prepaid SIM card from any other state in India, you must buy a local SIM card. This is done in Jammu and Kashmir for security reasons. Postpaid SIM cards from other states will work. It is advisable to purchase a local tourist SIM card. These are available at the railway stations, airport and other shops. The different mobile network providers in Jammu and Kashmir include Jio, Airtel and BSNL.

faq.question: What are the must-try foods in Kashmir?
faq.answer: Some of the must-try foods in Kashmir include slow-cooked lamb curry, Rogan Josh, Tabakh Maaz, Goshtaba and a yoghurt-based mutton or chicken curry, Yakhni. Apart from its delicious range of non-vegetarian dishes, Kashmir also has some of the best vegetarian dishes for everyone to try, such as Dum Aalo and Lyader Tschaman (cheese cooked in turmeric gravy). During cold winters, you may just try the traditional Kahwa.

Kashmir.Introduction: The Paradise on Earth, Kashmir, is one of the top destinations to visit for several travellers. Whether you want to experience the thrill of adventure, the natural beauty of snow-capped mountains and green meadows, or the beauty of rich cultural heritage, Kashmir is where you need to be. The Kashmir tour packages help you witness adventurous treks, serene landscapes, and romantic getaways - something suitable for everyone. The best Kashmir tour packages offer unforgettable experiences, including witnessing the snowy slopes of Gulmarg, houseboat stays on Dal Lake, and more. You can choose from a variety of Kashmir holiday packages, from those for families to adventure seekers and honeymoon-goers. You can customise your preferred Jammu Kashmir package deals to fit your needs.

For faster travel and convenience, you can opt for the Kashmir tour package with flight. Although it will cost slightly more, you will get a comprehensive experience around the place. The best Kashmir trip package is designed to fit your budget and travel needs, offering a transparent price for a Kashmir tour package. Whether you're travelling solo, with friends or with family, you can choose from the wide range of Kashmir travel packages that offer a memorable experience around this magical valley. This is a once-in-a-lifetime experience, so don't miss out on all the thrill and fun.
Kashmir.Why Kashmir is the Jewel in India's Crow: When booking your Kashmir tour packages, be sure to list all the top places you'd like to see. Being the Crown Jewel of India, Kashmir is home to some of the most beautiful places on Earth. For this reason, it is often referred to as Heaven on Earth. In fact, there's a famous couplet, "If there is paradise on the face of the earth, it is here, it is here, it is here," truly describing the beauty of Kashmir. Upon visiting Kashmir with your Kashmir travel packages, you would get to see the snow-capped mountains, vibrant culture of Kashmir, beautiful flowing streams, relaxed pristine lakes and lush green meadows. It is truly the destination for nature lovers. But even adventure seekers can visit this destination and enjoy a variety of activities. Some of the top scenic places that make Kashmir the Crown Jewel of India include Pahalgam, Sonmarg, Gulmarg and the Dal Lake of Srinagar. Apart from being such a beauty, Kashmir is often referred to as the Crown Jewel of India because of its rich history and deep cultural roots. The land of Kashmiri Pandits and Kashmir Hindus, the age-old traditions, customs and heritage are preserved to date here. Kashmir is also popular for its handicrafts such as Pashmina shawls, papier-mache art and designed carpets. If you plan to get some souvenirs from your Kashmir trip, you should definitely consider buying these. Kashmir is also a popular destination among pilgrims and spiritual people because it is home to some of the most essential temples, Sufi shrines and mosques. The Amarnath Cave is one of the top pilgrimages for Hindus. This temple is dedicated to Lord Shiva and is open only for a specific period (due to weather and safety concerns) for the pilgrims to set out on the Amarnath Yatra. Other places of religious importance in Kashmir include the Shankaracharya Temple and Hazratbal Shrine.
Kashmir.Planning Your Kashmir Trip: Essential In: Before booking your Jammu Kashmir tour package, it is extremely crucial to plan your trip. For this reason, you should check all important information, such as the Kashmir tour package price, the best time to visit, and more. Below is all the information that you'll need to have a fun and exciting trip to Kashmir.
Kashmir.Best Time to Visit Kashmir: Summer is the best season to book your Kashmir tour package. This Heaven on Earth has the best to offer during Spring as well. However, winter and autumn may not be the most favourable times to visit Kashmir due to the extremely cold weather. Booking your Kashmir trip plan during the summer ensures that you can visit the tourist places and enjoy the fun activities. Therefore, you book the best Kashmir tour packages between March and late August. The temperature ranges between 15°C and 30°C during summer, offering a comfortable experience. Moreover, it's during this time that you can truly soak in the natural beauty of Kashmir, with its vast blue skies and lush green meadows, as part of your Kashmir travel packages. If you want to experience snow, you may plan your visit between October and March. During winters, Kashmir experiences heavy snowfall with the temperature ranging between 2°C and 11°C. The Kashmir trip cost during this time is slightly lower because of off-season. If you're lucky enough, you may get to see snow and witness the winter wonderland of Kashmir in Gulmarg, where you can enjoy different winter sports.
Kashmir.How to Reach Kashmir?: Booked your Kashmir trip package? Here's how you can reach Kashmir via different modes: By Air: For this, you'll have to choose the Kashmir tour package with flight. Srinagar Airport (SXR) is the primary airport of Kashmir, India, which is well-connected to major cities of India such as Delhi, Kolkata, Bangalore and Mumbai. All the major airlines operate to Srinagar. The main airport is located around 12 km from the city centre. By Train: If you want an affordable Kashmir trip cost, travelling via train may be a convenient option. The closest railway station to Kashmir is Jammu Tawi, situated 290 km from Srinagar. Trains operate to Jammu Tawi from major destinations of India such as Chennai, Kolkata, Mumbai and Delhi. After reaching Jammu Tawi by train, you can either take a bus or hire a taxi to reach other parts of Kashmir. By Road: The Kashmir tour packages usually don't include road trips to Kashmir from other parts of India. However, if you're planning one, it surely can be an unforgettable experience. Taking your own car or hiring one should help you enjoy a road trip to Kashmir. There are three popular routes that you can take to reach Kashmir by road, via Delhi, Chandigarh and Amritsar. You can plan your Kashmir travel packages and choose the best road option that fits your needs.
Kashmir.Safety & Travel Tips: Before booking Kashmir holiday packages, it is important to check the safety and travel tips. While Kashmir is relatively safe for tourists, it is important to monitor the security situation. Here are some of the top safety and travel tips to follow on your Kashmir trip: You should book your Kashmir travel packages with only approved operators. Avoid visiting restricted or remote areas as part of your trip. Considering the high security of the region, always carry digital copies of your essential documents. The network in Kashmir may be slow, which means UPI payments won't work. Therefore, it is advisable to carry cash. If you don't have a postpaid mobile connection, you can purchase a local prepaid SIM card in Kashmir. Stick to the popular tourist spots that are declared safe and avoid travelling at night. You should carry essential items, especially medicines, if you're planning to visit higher altitudes on your Kashmir package. Pack your clothes according to the weather, and wear clothes in layers as the temperature can significantly change in the mountains. Follow local news before finalising the Kashmir tour packages. After visiting, stay connected to your tour operator. Wear modest clothes and respect the local culture. If you want a budget-friendly Kashmir tour package price, book your trip during discounts or around winter.
Kashmir.Permits and Regulations: You don't need any general Inner Line Permit (ILP) with your Kashmir tour packages for visiting major destinations such as Gulmarg, Sonmarg or Pahalgam. However, if you're visiting any border areas near the Line of Control (LoC), you may need special permits. Foreigners will also need special permits to visit some regions in Kashmir. As a tourist, you must carry your valid photo ID. International tourists must have a visa for India and a valid passport. Prepare a proper Kashmir trip plan, and depending on the regions you'll be travelling to, apply for Border Areas Permits or Foreign Tourist Permits.
Kashmir.Curated Kashmir Tour Packages by Thomas: Ready to explore the heavenly beauty of Kashmir? Let's discuss some of the best Jammu and Kashmir tour packages to choose from by Thomas Cook. Introduction to Packages Thomas Cook brings a wide range of Kashmir tour packages for everyone. You can book any of the Kashmir travel packages wherein you get to enjoy, relax, or set out on an adventure. Some of the top Kashmir tour packages to choose from by Thomas Cook are as follows: Diverse Categories of Kashmir Packages Kashmir has something to offer everyone. Therefore, the Kashmir tour packages are designed accordingly. Below are some of the popular Kashmir trip package categories to choose from are as follows: Honeymoon: The honeymoon Kashmir tour packages are designed for newlywed couples. It is ideal for couples who wish to witness the beauty of snow-clad mountains and cosy stays in shikara rides. Family packages: The family Kashmir holiday packages usually cover the major destinations. You can select the duration according to your preference, allowing you to discover hidden gems. Adventure packages: The adventure Kashmir travel packages are designed for thrill seekers. From trekking to rafting, you can explore the adventurous side of Kashmir. Whether you are a solo traveller or travelling with your friends, you can book these activities. Spiritual packages: The spiritual packages for Kashmir are designed for pilgrims who want to visit the mosques or temples. Kashmir has a rich cultural past and religious history. Therefore, you can book these Kashmir tour packages and explore these spiritual places.
Kashmir.Top Attractions & Experiences in Kashmir: The Kashmir holiday packages take you across a wide range of attractions and experiences that you won't ever forget. Some of the top attractions that you can explore on your Kashmir tour packages include the following: Gulmarg Gulmarg is one of the top destinations that you must visit during your Kashmir tour packages. Gulmarg's gondola is the second-longest and second-highest cable car globally. A visit to the Apharwat Mountain on the gondola ride handles around 600 people per hour. Most of the winter sports activities are around this place. Srinagar Srinagar is the capital of Kashmir, which is a crucial part of Kashmir travel packages. Upon your visit to Kashmir, you'll explore the historic gardens, vibrant culture and Dal Lake. Do not visit the Mughal Gardens. The backdrop of the Zabarwan Mountains offers the perfect blend of nature and cultural visuals. You must also visit Shankaracharya Temple and Pari Mahal. Sonmarg Often referred to as the Meadow of Golden Light, Sonmarg is a popular destination that you must not miss out on. Most Kashmir tour packages consider Sonmarg as a major hill station, taking you across beautiful landscapes. The scenic beauty, green meadows, lovely streams and majestic mountains of Sonmarg are perfect for trekking. Adventure lovers can also try various activities such as river rafting, hiking, skiing and more. Betaab Valley Betaab Valley offers a perfect view of the free-flowing Lidder River. This place received its name from the Bollywood movie Betaab. This valley is located around 15km away from Pahalgam, in the Anantnag district of Jammu and Kashmir. Dachigam National Park Dachigam National Park is a protected area spread across an area of 141 square kilometres. This national park houses the endangered Hangul deer or Kashmir stag. Apart from this, you can also witness the local wildlife of the region, such as Himalayan black bears, leopards and other bird species. Kashmir Valley Kashmir Valley is one of the largest Himalayan Valleys in the shape of an elliptical saucer. It is surrounded by the Pir Panjal and Karakoram ranges. The Kashmir Valley offers a mesmerising view of the surrounding region. The valley stretches across 140kms and is one of the top places to visit for adventure seekers and mountaineers. You can book adventure Kashmir tour packages to experience this beauty. Gurez Valley Situated at an altitude of 2,400m above sea level, Gurez Valley is surrounded by snow-capped mountains. This valley offers an impeccable view of the natural beauty of Kashmir. It is known for offering a serene and peaceful environment. Upon your visit to the Gurez Valley, you can explore the lakes and dense forests. Yusmarg If you wish to explore lush green meadows as part of your Kashmir tour packages, you must explore Yusmarg. This beautiful valley is located in the Budgam district and often referred to as the Meadow of Jesus. The grassy pastures here have sheep, offering a stunning view of the landscape. You can also enjoy a pony ride. You will see nomads grazing their cattle at Tosa Maidan. Doodhpatri This underrated gem is something you must miss out on. Yet another beautiful destination in the Budgam district, Doodhpatri is located 43km from the main city of Srinagar. Apart from green meadows, the area is dotted with creeks and waterfalls. It is a true natural retreat for all the tourists.
Kashmir.Things to Do in Kashmir Beyond Sightseei: From sightseeing to adventure activities, Kashmir tour packages offer you a wide range of experiences. Here are some of the top adventure activities you may indulge in upon your visit to Kashmir: Skiing in Gulmarg If you're a lover of winter sports, you must definitely book your Kashmir travel packages during winter. Visit Gulmarg to witness the beauty of a winter wonderland and enjoy the thrill of skiing. The snow-capped mountains are your grand stage where you make your descent along the snowy slopes. Shikara Ride in Dal Lake A shikara ride in Dal Lake is one of the must-try activities on your Kashmir tour packages. It feels very serene, peaceful and calm. Setting out on a shikara ride during the evening will offer a quiet atmosphere. The lavishly decorated shikaras float all around the Dal Lake offering a beautiful view of the snowy mountains. Enjoy a stay in the boathouse Do not miss out on the experience of staying in a boathouse. This is one of the best activities you can do in Kashmir. The houseboat stay offers a very dreamy experience, especially during the evening when the sun sets. Enjoy a kahwa on the boathouse and soak in the rich flavours of delicious Kashmiri cuisine. Take a Gondola Ride in Gulmarg Apart from skiing, you should also enjoy a gondola ride in Gulmarg. Book one of the Jammu and Kashmir tour packages that includes this activity as well. Step into the winter wonderland on your aerial chariot. As the gondola takes you to a height, you watch the true beauty of Kashmir unfold. The higher you go, the better the view of Gulmarg you get. Go for Boating at Mansar Lake On your Jammu Kashmir tour package, you must try boating on Mansar Lake. This lake offers an emerald view of Kashmir. As you said, across this aquatic haven, you'll get to learn about the ancient myths and legends of the place. Birds flying across the blue skies create a divine and peaceful view. Go for a Pony Ride in Pahalgam Although a very simple activity, a pony ride in Pahalgam is something you must not miss out on. As a part of your Jammu tour packages, this iconic experience is one of the best ones to indulge in. Along the way, make sure to enjoy the scenic charm of the surrounding region and witness the simple, yet innocent joy. The tour guide may take you across the Lidder River on your ride, explaining to you about Himalayan folklore. River Rafting One of the most thrilling activities to enjoy in Kashmir as a part of one of your Kashmir tour packages is river rafting. The activity is usually conducted around the Zanskar and Lidder rivers. You can raft and flow along the raging rhythm, enjoying the surrounding beauty. Trekking There are numerous trekking spots in Kashmir that you must visit. The Tarsar Marsar trek is one of the most popular ones that you can indulge in. Along the way, you'll witness the beauty of mighty shadows and quaint hamlets, especially the raw beauty of Kashmir. For trying these thrilling activities, you'll have to book adventurous Kashmir travel packages.
Kashmir.Essential Tips for a Smooth Kashmir Trip: Once you've booked the Kashmir trip package, you will have to start planning early. This is to ensure that you don't miss out on anything during your trip. Here are some of the key tips to follow: Packing checklist: Irrespective of the season you've booked the Kashmir tour packages for, it is crucial to carry warm clothes. You should carry comfortable shoes, thermal warmers (because nights can be cold during summers too), sunglasses and caps. Light woollen clothes and jackets are a must. Connectivity: Prepaid SIM cards from other Indian states are not valid in Kashmir. Therefore, you may carry a postpaid SIM card. If not, you may purchase a local prepaid SIM card upon reaching Kashmir to stay connected. Mobile network coverage may not be strong or non-existent in high-altitude areas. Health and Safety: Always carry your personal medicines while on your Jammu and Kashmir tour packages. You should check the weather conditions in Kashmir and pack your clothes accordingly. After all, you wouldn't want to fall sick on your trip. Respect Local Culture: Kashmir is a primarily conservative region. Therefore, it is important to dress modestly. You should also respect the local customs to prevent any unwanted attention. Before entering any mosque, temple or other religious places, open your shoes outside the premises. Ask for permission before taking photographs. Responsible Tourism: Kashmir is one of the most beautiful destinations in India. Therefore, you wouldn't want to spoil the beauty by littering around. It is advisable to use eco-friendly materials and avoid plastic water bottles. If you're looking for a more affordable Kashmir tour package, consider staying in local homestays. As a part of boosting responsible tourism, support the local economy. Save all Emergency Contacts: You must stay informed about the security situation in Kashmir. For this reason, you'll need to save all emergency contacts, in case you need them. For that purpose, you'll have to save contacts for ambulances, tourist helplines, police and fire. If your Kashmir tour packages coincide with any political events, it is advisable to consult the experts. It is extremely important to book a Kashmir trip package after considering all the options. You must stay updated about all the security advisories and plan your trip responsibly. Following these tips mentioned above should help you enjoy your Kashmir package with complete peace of mind.

faq.question: What is the best time to visit Punjab other than winter?
faq.answer: Besides winter, the best time to visit Punjab is during monsoon (July-September). You can find ideal Punjab family tour packages for this period on our page.

faq.question: What is the best place for birdwatching in Punjab?
faq.answer: For birdwatching in Punjab, you can check out Harike Wetland and Bird Sanctuary. so, make sure to add a quick visit to this site on your Punjab tour itinerary.

faq.question: Is Punjab a good starting point for a northern India hill station vacation?
faq.answer: Yes, Punjab is considered a gateway to northern India hill station holidays. You can even find Punjab packages that come with nearby hill stations included.

faq.question: Are there any beaches in Punjab?
faq.answer: No, there are no beaches in Punjab. It is a landlocked state in India.

faq.question: Is Chandigarh a part of Punjab?
faq.answer: Chandigarh is a union territory however, it is the joint capital of both Punjab and Haryana. You may find Punjab travel packages that include stays in Chandigarh.

faq.question: What are the best ways to reach Punjab?
faq.answer: Both travelling by flight and train are the best ways to reach Punjab. If you are starting your journey from a neighbouring state, you can also opt for travel by bus or hired car. Most Punjab family tour packages have the option of flights as travel inclusions.

faq.question: What are some must-visit places in Punjab?
faq.answer: Some prominent Punjab places to visit are Amritsar, Bathinda, Ludhiana, Pathankot, etc.

faq.question: Is Punjab a good destination for a short family trip?
faq.answer: Yes, Punjab is an excellent location for a short trip. You can get good deals on Punjab tour packages for families at Thomas Cook India.

faq.question: What is the distance between Amritsar from Delhi by road?
faq.answer: The road distance between Amritsar and Delhi is around 450 km. Some popular Punjab travel packages offer the option of selecting Delhi as the departure city.

faq.question: What are some must-try desserts in Punjab?
faq.answer: Some must-try desserts in Punjab are Jalebi, Gulab Jamun, Lassi, Kulfa and Phirni.

Punjab.Introduction: The beautiful state of Punjab is located in the northern region of India. It shares its border with the Indian states of Rajasthan, Haryana, and Himachal Pradesh and the union territory of Jammu and Kashmir. The union territory of Chandigarh serves as the capital of both Punjab and its neighbouring state of Haryana. Apart from being a gateway to northern India tourism, Punjab is a major vacation and tourist hotspot on its own. Its historical roots and landmarks, beautiful natural landforms, unique regional culture, religious sites, and famous cuisine are just some examples of what this state has to offer in terms of tourism. The authenticity of the region and its welcoming nature make a trip to Punjab even more delightful. To maximise your vacation fun and ensure that everything remains smooth sailing, you can go with the Punjab tour packages offered by Thomas Cook India. These Punjab tour packages are professionally created so you can relax and enjoy your trip without spending hours on or worrying about travel planning. In addition, there are over 20+ Punjab tour packages with different itineraries and price ranges, so you can have a wider palette of options. While these travel packages take care of accommodations, meals, sightseeing and other tour essentials, you can learn more about the destination from our Punjab tour guide. For more details on popular Punjab holiday packages, regional COVID-19 tourist guidelines, transportation options, etc., go through the following sections of this page.

Punjab.Punjab COVID-19 Travel Guidelines: After the lockdown situation, Punjab tourism resumed in full force. Now is a perfect time for a tour of Punjab, as most hotels, markets, transport, and popular sightseeing destinations are operational. However, it is important that you learn and abide by the regional COVID-19 travel rules and regulations during your trip to Punjab. While the majority of the travel restrictions have been lifted, there still remain a few crucial ones that you have to follow. You can learn more about it through our extensive COVID-19 Punjab Travel Guidelines page. Also, you may have to go through mandatory thermal screenings at airports, railway stations, hotel entrances, etc.
Punjab.Best time to visit Punjab: Look for Punjab holiday packages in accordance with the best time to visit the destination. Generally, the winter season is considered the best time to visit Punjab. It usually stretches from October or November till March and offers a delightful climate overall. The average daytime temperature usually remains around the mid to low 20s, while the nighttime temperature can drop below the low 10s. The late nights and early mornings are especially chilly, while the daytime is best suitable for exploration and sightseeing. In addition, the major events and festivals during winter in Punjab attract a massive crowd of visitors. Some examples include the Lohri festival, Basant Panchami, Hola Mohalla, etc. As the temperature runs chilly with cold breezes, make sure to pack some warm clothes. Also, book your flight tickets and hotels for the trip in advance due to the peak season traffic. You can get more details on the best Punjab tour packages with price and duration for winter on Thomas Cook India. The next major season for Punjab tourism is during the monsoon. It stretches from July to September and helps cool down the region with rain showers following the summer heat. Even though the average temperature range is 25 to 30°C, daytime temperature can run a bit high. The weather remains humid and wet for the most part. Many consider the monsoon the best time to visit Punjab to witness the regional natural glory enhanced by the rain. In addition, shoulder season discounts on Punjab holiday packages, hotels and flights are also quite favoured by travellers during this period. Another significant season for Punjab tourism is summer. It stretches from April to June and is an off-season for a tour to Punjab. This is because summer in Punjab is very hot, and the average temperature can range from 29 to 45°C across the region. Alongside the hotness, the high humidity makes exploration and sightseeing difficult for tourists. However, some consider travelling to Punjab during this period for things like the Baisakhi festival, summer outdoor activities and farm stay experiences. In addition, the tourism traffic remains thin during summer, and you may use Punjab as a gateway to your northern India hill station trip. Take a look at our Punjab tour guide on the best time to visit for more details. If you are interested in a farm-stay experience in Punjab during summer, you can find some great deals on Punjab travel packages. You can get more details on summer special Punjab tour packages with price and duration, like this one, on Thomas Cook India.
Punjab.How to reach Punjab: As Punjab offers both domestic and international travel connectivity, tourists can easily reach the state. Depending on your trip’s start location in Punjab, travel budget and preferred time, you can choose between air, train or road travel options. Here are the best ways to reach Punjab: Reach Punjab by air Reach Punjab by rail Reach Punjab by road Travelling within Punjab Reach Punjab by air If your tour budget allows it, travelling to Punjab by air is the best option you can go for. It is the fastest and most comfortable way of reaching Punjab for both domestic and international visitors. The major international airport serving Punjab is located in Amritsar, while the airports in Chandigarh and Ludhiana can be a great choice for domestic travellers. Travellers can easily find daily flights to Punjab from the major cities in India, including Mumbai, Kolkata, Delhi, etc. For instance, a direct flight to Punjab from Kolkata can take about 3 hours, making it the best transport option. In addition, international flights from and to Qatar, Singapore, the UK, UAE, etc., are also operated on this route. Reach Punjab by rail The whole region of Punjab is well-connected by an extensive railway network. It helps create a link between major cities in Punjab and other parts of India. The main railway stations of the state are in Amritsar, Ludhiana, Pathankot, Jalandhar, Chandigarh and Ferozepur, which are also some of the major starting points of a Punjab trip. Most trains that connect the state with other parts of India pass through Delhi. Some of the main express trains that run from and to Punjab are Amritsar Shatabdi, Golden Temple Mail, Swarna Shatabdi, Shan-e-Punjab, Moga Shatabdi, etc. For instance, individuals travelling from Delhi, Uttar Pradesh, Maharashtra, Haryana, and Madhya Pradesh can opt for the Punjab Mail train. Travelling to Punjab by train is one of the best economical transport options. Reach Punjab by road Individuals, who want a good road trip for their Punjab vacation, can choose to travel by car or bus. Multiple well-managed roadways connect the state with major cities in India, especially those in the northern and eastern regions. For instance, individuals travelling from Hyderabad to Punjab can easily get on the NH 44 in their car and drive to their trip’s starting point. In addition, scheduled interstate buses run to various cities in Punjab from neighbouring states, including Haryana, Rajasthan, Delhi, etc. You can get more details on the best transportation options from our handy Punjab tour guide. Travelling within Punjab In terms of getting around in Punjab, visitors can opt for buses, intercity trains, taxis, rental cars, auto rickshaws and so on.
Punjab.Best honeymoon destinations in Punjab: Punjab is a good solo or group holiday location and a fantastic honeymoon destination within India. Its varied culture, authentic regional cuisine, bewitching greenery, unique blend of simple and modern lifestyles and historical landmarks are just some of its key attractions for newlyweds. The main honeymoon destinations within Punjab are Amritsar, Ludhiana, Jalandhar and Chandigarh. They are easily accessible within the state and offer a lot of romantic things that couples can do, including spending time by the gardens, doing fun activities at amusement and water parks, going on a street food hunt, and so on. Besides these popular spots, here are some other prominent honeymoon destinations within Punjab that you can consider: Pathankot- It is one of Punjab's most famous tourist places. It lies amidst the illustrious foothills of Dalhousie and Kangra, close to the Chakki river bank. Pathankot also works as a major gateway to Mata Vaishno Devi and Amarnath trips. The vibrant scenic views of nature, waterfalls and serene atmosphere are some of its main couple’s attractions. Some must-visit sites in Pathankot are Mukteshaw temple, Shahpurkandi Fort, Ranjit Sagar Dam and Nurpur Fort. Mohali- The planned city of Mohali is another crucial honeymoon destination in Punjab. It is also famous for its rich natural beauty, along with its cricket stadium, hockey stadium and shopping markets. Mohali is a fantastic off-beat spot for newlyweds. Some popular tourist attractions in and near Mohali are Rose Garden, Mataur Lake, Mansa Devi Temple, Sukhna Wildlife Sanctuary, etc. It is also one of the most famous places in Punjab for a short trip. Kapurthala- Kapurthala is a great honeymoon spot for new couples and a beautiful tourist destination in Punjab. It is a major religious centre for Sikhs and a prominent historical site within Punjab. Its extensive architecture and famous landmarks reek of Indo-Saracen and French styles. Kapurthala is also popularly referred to as the “Paris of Punjab''. Some key couples' attractions in and near this city are Shalimar Gardens, Panch Mandir, Kanjili Wetland, Jagatjit Palace and Jagatjit Club. Kapurthala is also one of Punjab's most popular tourist spots for a week-long vacation. You can find more about honeymoon destinations and packages within India here.
Punjab.Best places to visit in Punjab: There are lots of popular tourist attractions around the state that you shouldn’t miss during your trip to Punjab. Here are some of the best places to visit in Punjab: Amritsar Ludhiana Chandigarh Jalandhar Patiala Bathinda Amritsar Amritsar is Punjab’s second-largest city and a massive cultural and religious hub. It is home to Sri Harmandir Sahib, the Golden Temple, which is a very important site of pilgrimage for Sikhs and one of the most important places in Punjab. The rich local culture and atmosphere of the city, paired with beautiful shops and street food, make Amritsar one of the best places to visit in Punjab. Apart from the Golden Temple, some other Amritsar tourist places are the Wagah Border and Jallianwala Bagh. Ludhiana Ludhiana is the largest city and one of the most famous historical places in Punjab. During your visit to this city, you will be able to witness the agricultural side of the state, along with its massively widespread natural beauty. In addition, Ludhiana is also a major tourist location within Punjab for its cultural and historical side, depicted by tons of famous landmarks. One can also find an array of shopping areas, restaurants, sports stadiums and much more within the bounds of this city. The Phillaur Fort in Ludhiana is one of the most important historical monuments of Punjab. Some other must-visit places in the city are Maharaja Ranjit Singh War Museum, Museum of Rural Life, etc. Chandigarh Chandigarh is the joint capital of Haryana and Punjab. It is a major destination that you shouldn’t miss out on during your tour of Punjab. It is one of the most beautiful planned cities in India, known for its brilliant architecture, natural beauty and well-connected roads. Chandigarh is also one of the cleanest cities in India, popularly called by its nickname “The City Beautiful”. It is home to famous sites like the Rock Garden, Zakir Hussain Rose Garden, Pinjore Garden, Skuhna Lake and International Dolls Museum, which are popular tourist attractions in Punjab. Jalandhar Next on the list of the most beautiful places to go to in Punjab is the city of Jalandhar. It is one of the oldest cities in the northern part of the state and a major hub for culture and history. It is home to many important religious sites, tourist landmarks and famous monuments of Punjab, making it ideal for a short trip. Some of the best places to see in Jalandhar are Niku Park, Wonderland, Pushpa Gujral Science City, Shaheed-e-Azam Museum, and Devi Talab Mandir. Patiala Apart from being one of the largest cities, Patiala is one of the best tourist places in Punjab. The city is surrounded by vibrant natural beauty and blessed with remarkable architectural work of different styles from various periods of history. The Bahadurgarh Fort in Patiala is one of the most crucial historical monuments of Punjab. Some other prominent sites within Patiala for tourists are Kali Mata Mandir, Sheesh Mahal, Moti Bagh Palace, etc. It is also a great place to buy gifts, souvenirs, and regional craftworks. Bathinda The city of Bathinda is very amusing and considered one of the best places in Punjab for tourists. It is one of the oldest cities in the state, with a rich history and tons of famous tourist landmarks. With its regional and cultural beauty, sightseeing opportunities and great availability of accommodations, Bathinda is a must-visit off-beat site in Punjab. Some of the crucial places in and near Bathinda are Rose Garden, Qila Mubarak, Lakhi Jungle, Bahia Fort, etc.
Punjab.Best hill stations in Punjab: The incredible holiday destination of Punjab works as a fabulous gateway to many popular hill stations in India due to its geographical location. Tourists commonly include Punjab as the starting point of their hill station trip to northern India. Here are the best hill stations near Punjab that you can visit: Morni Hills- It is a very famous tourist site in the Panchkula district of Haryana. It is about 50 kilometres by road from Mohali and makes for a great hillside holiday escape near Punjab. It is blessed with vibrant greenery, scenic landscapes, and a soothing atmosphere. Its key tourist attractions include Morni Fort, Thakurdwara Temple, Tikkar Taal, outdoor adventure activities, and so on. It is one of the best places to visit near Punjab. Parwanoo hill- This is another prominent hill station located near Punjab, just 91 kilometres from Patiala. Tourists can find a perfect mixture of hillside ambience and the sparkles of busy city life. It is a popular spot for hiking, trekking, and cable car rides, while its apple gardens draw tourists from everywhere. Some popular places to visit in Parwanoo include Timber Trail, Gorkha Fort, Kali Mata Temple, etc. You can add Parwanoo hill to your Punjab tour itinerary. Shimla- It is one of the most popular tourist places near Punjab, just 120 kilometres from Mohali. Apart from its divine natural beauty, Shimla is home to many popular tourist sites of interest, including The Ridge, Mall Road, Green Valley, Kufri, Jakhu Temple, etc. Its availability of skiing, ice skating, ropeway rides, and toy train rides also make it a popular hill station in the northern region of India. You can easily get Punjab trip packages that include Shimla and other popular nearby hill stations. Others- Some other popular hill stations near Punjab are Barog, Nalagarh, Dharamshala, etc. If you are going on a family vacation, you can find Punjab family tour packages that include nearby hill stations like Dharamshala and Shimla.
Punjab.What to eat in Punjab?: The local cuisine and street food shops are some of the major attractions in Punjab for foodies. So, during your vacation, make sure to try these food items in Punjab: Butter chicken- It is a renowned dish in Punjabi cuisine that you must try during your visit. Butter chicken is a gravy-rich delicious dish made with chicken, yoghurt, Kashmiri Lal mirch and a variety of other spices and ingredients. This delicacy is best served alongside Rumali Roti or Naan bread. Lassi- This delicious drink is the pride of the region, beloved by every Indian. Lassi is a flavourful, yoghurt-based, creamy sugary drink, often accompanied by fruits and other toppings. It is best served chilly and helps soothe your body with its refreshing taste during the summer heat. Dal Makhani- You may have tried various types of dal, but nothing beats Punjab’s Dal Makhani in taste. It is made with a blend of kidney beans and black lentils and has a thick creamy texture. It is best served alongside Naan bread or Tandoori roti. Paratha- is considered one of Punjab’s staple food items. It is a flatbread made with a mixture of atta (whole wheat flour), ghee (or oil), water, and salt. It has many popular variants, including “aloo paratha”, which has a stuffing made of potato mix. It is best served with pickles, ghee, or curd on the side. Paneer tikka- This vegetarian dish is popular among all food lovers around the nation. It is made with soft Indian cottage cheese, marinated in various spices, and then chargrilled slowly. Its mouth-watering taste makes it one of the must-try foods in Punjab.
Punjab.Shopping in Punjab: While browsing through Punjab packages for holidays, make sure to learn about the must-buy gifts and souvenirs. Here are some examples: Punjabi Jutti- Punjabi Jutti is a very popular traditional footwear in Punjab. They are beautifully designed and available under a wide colour palette. Punjabi Jutti is available for both men and women and is sold all over Punjab. They are one of the most famous things in Punjab that you can buy for yourself or as gifts for friends and family. Find the best Punjab tour packages for families at Thomas Cook India. Bracelet/Kada- Punjabi Kada is very important to the Sikh religion and regional culture. These Kadas are said to be a symbol of courtesy and concealment and represent a link to the almighty. It is a fantastic spiritual gift for your loved ones, and you can find them at shops near Gurudwaras across Punjab. Jaggery and dry fruits- Punjab is well-known for its fruits and sweet dishes. For a perfect gift to friends and family members, you can shop for jaggery and dry fruits in Punjab. They are not only delicious but also quite nutritious. Traditional women's wear- Punjab is a hub for traditional women’s wear shopping. Some popular items include the Phulkari, Patiala Salwar, Punjabi Ghagra, and Parandi. So ladies, make sure to shop your heart out and add these traditional women’s wear to your wardrobe. Some ideal shopping areas in Punjab are Rainak Bazar in Jalandhar, Akalgarh Market in Ludhiana, Phase 5 market in Mohali, Lahori Gate Market in Amritsar, etc.
Punjab.Things to do in Punjab: If you are wondering what to do during your tour to Punjab, here are some great options you can consider: Witness Punjab’s Rural Olympics at Kila Raipur- Located near Ludhiana, Kila Raipur hosts a unique rural sports festival that is famous across India. It is popularly referred to as the “Rural Olympics of Punjab” and features exciting events like bull-cart race, tug of war, kabaddi and so on. It usually takes place during February at a 4-year interval and is a must-watch thing for your Punjab tour. Make sure to add a stay at Kila Raipur to your Punjab tour itinerary. Take a holy dip in the Golden Temple- While in Amritsar, make sure to visit and spend some time amidst the divine atmosphere within the Golden Temple. You can also take a holy dip in the Amrit Sarovar, which is believed to cleanse your soul of bad karma. Visit the Attari-Wagah Border- Make sure to witness the famous Attari-Wagah border ceremony at the border, just 30 kilometres from Amritsar. It is a lowering of flags ceremony practiced by the security forces of India and Pakistan since 1959. It takes place every day, just before sunset, and attracts a huge crowd. Explore the major cities on foot- The major cities of Punjab have a lot to offer in terms of unique architectural styles, local shops, delicacies, traditions, culture, and so on. During your vacation, make sure to explore the cities on foot to get the best travel experience.
Punjab.Adventurous Things to do in Punjab: There are many adventure places in Punjab that you can check out. Here are some adventurous things to do in Punjab: Wildlife Safari- You can go on a thrilling safari ride to witness wildlife and nature running on its beautiful course. A major example includes going on a Ludhiana Zoo Tiger Safari ride. Bird watching- Another popular adventure activity that you can do in Punjab is go bird watching. One prominent location is the Harike Wetland and Bird Sanctuary, which spreads over three districts, Kapurthala, Ferozepur, and Amritsar. Water and other outdoor activities- If you are visiting Punjab for a vacation, especially during the summer, make sure to avail yourself of the various water and outdoor activities. For this, you can visit the amusement and adventure parks across the major cities of Punjab. Some examples include slide pool, ziplining, rappelling, boating, bamboo bridge crossing, etc.
Punjab.Interesting facts about Punjab: While you are looking at Punjab trip packages and choosing the best time for your visit, make sure to learn more about the destination also. Here are some interesting facts about Punjab that you should know about: The name “Punjab” is a combination of two Persian words, “Panj” and “aab”, relating to “the land of five rivers.” Sutlej, Chenab, Beas, Jhelum and Ravi are the five main rivers flowing through the region. Punjab is the birthplace of the religion of Sikhism, where Guru Nanak laid its foundation back in around 1500 CE. Punjab is the 19th-largest state in terms of total area and the 16th-largest state in terms of the total population in India. The Golden Temple in Punjab’s Amritsar is considered the most important and holiest site of pilgrimage for the Sikhs. Ranjit Singh donated the gold above the Gurudwara. Punjab is the birthplace of several famous folk dances in India, like Bhangra, Dhamaal, Gatka, Jhumar, and Jugni. Punjab’s capital city, Chandigarh, is also the joint capital of Haryana. Punjab and Haryana share a common High Court called the “High Court of Punjab and Haryana.”

Yamunotri.Introduction: Yamunotri is the beginning of the river Yamuna located in the Garhwal division of Uttrakhand. It is a foremost important pilgrimage in Hinduism. Yamunotri Tour Packages design the tour to provide full comfort & guidance to the tourist. Yamunotri is the seat of Goddess Yamuna crafted beautifully with black marble and wears silver ornaments. Yamunotri offers a picturesque view to the pilgrims. Every year millions of Hindu pilgrims visit Yamunotri.
Yamunotri.Most Popular Yamunotri Tour Packages: Tour Packages NameDurationPrice Char Dham Yatra By Helicopter5 Nights 6 DaysRs. ₹232 500Char Dham Yatra By Helicopter with Kedarnath Darshan and Maha Abhishek Puja 5 Nights 6 DaysRs. ₹238 500Char Dham Yatra By Helicopter With Kedarnath Stay And Rudrabhishek Puja5 Nights 6 DaysRs. ₹242 500 Yamunotri Glacier is the start of the Yamuna river. The foothills of the Kalind Mountain are the place of the worship where the temple dedicated to Goddess Yamuna has been constructed. Yamuna river is considered to holy like the River Ganga and is considered as the second sacred river of Hindus. Yamuna river flows through many states taking care of the civilization and finally meets the Ganga in Allahabad, U.P. The Chota Chardham Yatra in Uttrakhand is to cover four different ranges: Kedarnath, Badrinath, Gangotri & Yamunotri. Yamunotri Dham can be reached by covering a trek of 13kms. Hanuman Chatti & Janki Chatti are the places to begin the trek to Yamunotri. Both of them are an extremely beautiful place offering the views of the Great Himalayas, the Yamuna river and The Ganga river. One can spot the river Ganges also that flows beside the Yamuna river. There are hot water springs nearby the temple, where the devotees prepare potatoes & rice tied in a muslin cloth. The potatoes and rice are offered to the Goddess in the form of Prasad. Suryakund is the foremost Kund of Yamunotri. Gauri Kund is also very famous in Yamunotri, the water of this kund is not very hot, and many pilgrims bathe in this warm water spring. It is believed that Gauri Kund has the healing properties for different types of skin problems including acne and body pains. As Yamunotri is located at an altitude of 3293 metres, it remains closed from November to April due to chilly winters. The entire Yamunotri is made inaccessible to the mankind as it receives abundant snowfall and faces minus degree temperature. The entire river is frozen and stands still. Once the temperature reaches to normal, it flows in full flow. Yamunotri opens on Akshyatithi and closes on Diwali. Both the opening and closing ceremony of Yamunotri is carried out by rituals and chants. Yamunotri is a beautiful place where the temple stands straight surrounded by the Himalayan range and Yamuna River flowing just beside the temple. The Goddess Yamuna stands in the form of a black marble idol inside the temple. faq.question: How many days are ideal for a Ladakh trip?
faq.answer: You should allow at least 8 to 10 days to really explore Ladakh without hurrying. This allows you to acclimate for one to two days in Leh before touring the neighbouring monasteries, Nubra Valley, and Pangong Lake. A 12- to 14-day Leh Ladakh tour is ideal if you're eager to travel by road from Manali or Srinagar because it's more adventurous but takes longer. There are shorter 5- to 6-day Ladakh Tour Packages available, but they only cover the highlights, and the tempo could feel hurried.

faq.question: Is Ladakh safe for solo female travellers?
faq.answer: Indeed, Ladakh is regarded as one of India's safer areas. There is relatively little crime, and the people are friendly and courteous. However, you should take conventional measures, such as booking accommodations from reputable companies, telling someone your daily itinerary, and staying away from remote areas after dark. While some choose more exclusive Leh Ladakh car tour Packages, many ladies even like Leh Ladakh group vacation packages for business.

faq.question: What is the best way to travel within Ladakh?
faq.answer: Hiring local taxis is the most popular method, particularly if you have Leh Ladakh vehicle tour packages included in your Leh Ladakh Tour Packages. Although they are less frequent, public transportation options, including buses and shared taxis, are also accessible. In order to experience the exhilaration of riding through Khardung La, adventurers frequently rent motorcycles.

faq.question: Do I need special permits for certain areas?
faq.answer: Yes, in places like Hanle, Pangong Tso, Tso Moriri, and Nubra Valley, Indians need Inner Line Permits, and foreigners need Protected Area Permits. These can be scheduled online, in person at the DC office in Leh, or frequently as part of specially designed Ladakh Tour Packages.

faq.question: What kind of clothes should I carry?
faq.answer: Layering is essential in Ladakh because the weather can change suddenly. Bring strong trekking boots, a windproof parka, fleece jackets, and thermals. Put on gloves, UV sunglasses, and woollen caps. Light cottons or T-shirts are fine for Leh's midday temperatures, but warm layers are required at higher elevations.

faq.question: Is there an age limit for visiting Ladakh?
faq.answer: Ladakh is challenging due to its altitude, yet there is no recognised limit. Children under three and those with heart or respiratory disorders should stay away from them or consult a doctor. Seniors who acclimate gradually can also enjoy the trip.

faq.question: How far is Ladakh from Delhi by road?
faq.answer: Delhi is nearest place to Ladakh to start your bike/road trip. The distance of Delhi to Leh Ladakh by road via Leh Manali Highway is around 1011 km and the total travel time is approximately 24 hours.

faq.question: Do I need a medical certificate to take the Chadar trek?
faq.answer: Yes. You will need to undergo a medical checkup at SNM Hospital Leh before Chadar trek. Even if you already have a certificate or medical report from your doctor, you need to take this test. Without this you will not receive ALOTA clearance and permission from the Wildlife Department to undertake the Chadar trek. Also, you will need 3 days to acclimate to the reduced oxygen level before taking the trek.

faq.question: What altitude is Ladakh situated at?
faq.answer: Ladakh is about 3000 meters or 9800 feet above mean sea level. It drops from 5,753 metres (18,875 feet) above sea level at its source near the China border to 3,620 metres (11,880 feet) at its snout.

faq.question: Is Ladakh safe to visit?
faq.answer: Yes, Ladakh is absolutely safe to visit as there is no active conflict in the area. Also, the tourist destinations are far from the political instability.

faq.question: How many days are required for a Ladakh tour?
faq.answer: There are various tourist places and also hidden places that must visit when travelling to Ladakh. A week or 8 days is sufficient time for a Ladakh tour.

faq.question: Is it safe to travel to Leh in August?
faq.answer: In August, both the Srinagar-Leh and Manali-Leh highways remain open. Remember that Ladakh is a rain shadow region, which means that the Himalayan highlands' cold desert blocks rain clouds, resulting in only drizzles and light rain. Planning a Ladakh trip in August won’t be a problem.

faq.question: Does the internet work in Ladakh?
faq.answer: Yes, towns and villages in Ladakh have internet connectivity. In Leh Area, you will have access to a full network and 4G internet.

faq.question: Which is the best time to visit Leh Ladakh?
faq.answer: The Leh Manali Highway opens up via Rohtang Pass from mid-May to September, making it the best time to visit Ladakh by road. The months of mid-September to mid-October are ideal for visiting Leh and Ladakh, as the region is bathed in flamboyance along the Srinagar-Leh and Manali-Leh roads.

faq.question: Is there snow in Leh in May?
faq.answer: No, you won’t find snow in Ladakh in the month of May. You will only find hard ice and not fresh snowfall.

faq.question: What is the coldest month in Ladakh?
faq.answer: With temperatures ranging from -2°C to -14°C, January is the coldest month in Ladakh.
