New Zealand.Best time to visit New Zealand: New Zealand’s climates ranges from tropical with sun kissed beaches and spiffing trudging weather to cold with traversing around the region on magical skis and snowboards. The best time to visit New Zealand is between the months of December to February, when you can let the adventure seeker in you come out and play. Summer in New Zealand calls for extreme exploration. Go tramping amidst deep cut valleys, silent meadows and majestic forests at the Tongariro National Park. Or spend the last day of the year under the stars in east-coast Gisborne. The entire country is on vacation as summertime approaches. With people gearing up for Christmas and shops fully packed, this island country is getting ready for a grand party. If you wish to take the road less travelled, go for the low season discounted New Zealand packages between June to August. This time of the year calls for half-asleep warm beach towns and gleeful ski and snowboarding rides. December to February (Summer): This is hands down the most popular time of year for a New Zealand tour. You'll have long, sun-drenched days ideal for hiking, swimming, and outdoor activities. Beaches are bustling, national parks are filled with trekkers, and you can enjoy fun summer festivals. Just keep in mind that, because this is the high season, flights, accommodations, and New Zealand packages will be more expensive, so early booking is a good idea. June to August (Winter): Love snowboarding or skiing? Go for a winter trip to New Zealand, particularly to Queenstown and Wanaka. You can experience winter festivals, warm cafes, and stunning snow-capped mountains. Added benefit, you'll find lower rates for most New Zealand packages beyond ski resorts. March to May (Autumn): If you prefer fewer people but still want to indulge in a mild climate, this is your sweet spot. The sceneries have lovely autumn colours, and hence it's ideal for vineyard excursions and country drives. You will also get more competitive rates on New Zealand tour packages during this off-season shoulder period. This is a nice time to relax and appreciate nature as well as culture. September to November (Spring): This is when New Zealand is alive once more. You'll witness blooming flowers, lambs in the fields at birth, and warmer days gradually making their return. It's less crowded, cheaper, and wonderful if you prefer scenic drives and nature walks. Whatever your passion may be, whether it’s adventure, culture, or relaxation, New Zealand tour packages have something for everyone. All you have to do is match your travel style to the season, and you're ready for an unforgettable New Zealand vacation. Visit the Thomas Cook website to check out discounted New Zealand tour packages during this period.
New Zealand.How to reach New Zealand: By Air Given the distance from New Zealand to India, the only practical way to reach New Zealand would be by booking a flight. Regular flights are available from all the major cities in India; Delhi, Mumbai, Chennai, Kolkata, Hyderabad and Bengaluru to Auckland. Almost all international flights land in Auckland which is identified as New Zealand’s largest city. From Auckland, domestic flights connect you to several airports across the country. If you are travelling from India, chances are that you will have common stop-over locations in Singapore, Kuala Lumpur, Hongkong and Bangkok. This depends upon the airline of selection. However, if you are an international traveler, direct flights are available from Australia, USA, Canada, Singapore, Korea, Malaysia and Japan. Make sure you book your tickets well in advance, especially if you are travelling in the peak season. New Zealand is bustling with activities all year long, resulting in busy airports. Flight time from the capital takes approximately 16 hours and 55 minutes. However, quite a few airlines take as long as 38 hours depending on the transit destination and waiting period. One can choose between any of the following airlines to travel to New Zealand - Jet Airways, Emirates, Cathay Pacific, Air India, Air Asia, Thai Airways, Singapore Airlines and Malaysia Airlines. Auckland International Airport is the busiest airport in New Zealand, situated at a distance of 32 km from the city centre. With over 21 million passengers, this is known to be the largest airport in New Zealand. From duty-free stores, restaurants, bars, cafes, ATM’s and currency exchange services, one can find just about anything under the sun. Upon arrival, individuals can avail of the bus, taxi or hotel shuttle or train services to reach their required destination. New Zealand is also home to other efficient airports such as Christchurch Airport, Dunedin Airport, Queenstown Airport and Wellington Airport. By Sea As much as you would want it, India does not offer direct cruises to New Zealand. However, you will have to travel to Australia if you are keen enough to travel to New Zealand via sea. Other ports through which you can go to New Zealand via sea are Brisbane, Sydney, Melbourne and Adelaide. With Thomas Cook’s Explorer Dream Cruise (Auckland - Sydney ), all the seekers of the sea are in for a treat. The cruise starts from Auckland, taking you to Sydney within a span of 7 days. Travelling within New Zealand By Rail - You can travel by train between this island country’s three main cities – Auckland, Wellington and Christchurch. You can easily navigate your way across Auckland that covers the city centre till Waitakere in the east and Pukekohe in the south. Take the Northern Explorer if you wish to travel from Auckland to Wellington and enjoy picturesque locations of the Northern island whiz past you. Hop on to the Coastal Pacific if you are looking to have an immersive travel experience as you witness the Canterbury Plains on one side and the Pacific Ocean coastline on the other. By Road - You can enjoy the wonders that the wide roads of New Zealand have to offer; dotted with majestic mountains and vast crystalline lakes by driving through it. If you are in Auckland, you could take a road trip to Rotorua through Coromandel Peninsula which is home to breath-taking beaches and woodlands.
New Zealand.Places to visit in New Zealand: The powdery white hills of New Zealand will take you back in time, reminding you of a certain poem. The glorious mountains and pristine lakes whisking past you while the wind plays with your hair will bring back the charm of long drives. Alluring landscapes at every location will leave you wondering if you could be standing right in front of a painting. This is just a preface of New Zealand for you. If you wish to further turn the pages of this striking masterpiece right till the end, here are a few places to visit as a part of your New Zealand tourism package that spell enchantment. Auckland Wellington Christchurch Bay of Islands Milford Sound Queenstown Auckland Blissful thermal springs and heavy sweeps of rainforests meet breezy west-coast beaches and idyllic islets. If you are a water baby, hop on to one of the many yachts at Waitemata Harbour. Embark on an unforgettable sailing adventure with your family. Delve into darkness on a boat as you swing off onto the Waitomo River. Watch creatures of the night glow in the dark. You are surrounded by ivory white little lights that twinkle. These are not stars, but glowworms. Wellington Wellington’s starry streets are dotted with theatres, inviting museums and crafty art galleries. Head to this city’s quaint cafés at Mount Cook to enjoy a strong cup of coffee with a proper Kiwi style breakfast comprising beans, toast and eggs. Walk up to the Mount Victoria viewpoint and be greeted with far-reaching views of steep fiords lying between New Zealand and Antarctica. Christchurch In the process of a creative transition after the Canterbury earthquake, Christchurch has bounced back brighter and bolder than ever. A walk to remember as you meander along 30 tranquil riverside hectares of floral heaven at the Botanical Gardens. Or shopping for vintage clothing, surfboards or delicate hardware from beautifully refurbished Victorian buildings. The choice is yours. Bay of Islands The delightful company of playful dolphins and wondrous whales await. Indulge in world class diving, kayaking, big-game fishing or yachting when in the Bay. Head to Urupukapuka Island to listen to a melodious bird song. Hike extensive trails or laze around on its bed of numerous beaches. Milford Sound Experience the world-famous fiord in ways more than one. Be it the Milford Sound Cruise that covers Stirling Falls and a seal colony. Or a challenging hike to the Key Summit. Criss-cross your way up to the Summit and you will be rewarded with breathtaking views of the mighty mountains. Queenstown The flurrying adventure capital of New Zealand’s South Island is ornamented with crumbling mining towns, picturesque vineyards and bungee jumping points. If you are visiting during the winter season, make your way to the slopes of The Remarkables and Coronet Peak to engage in some adrenalin pumping skiing. Motueka Saltwater Baths, Nelson Tasman If you crave a place which seems like it's your private infinity pool, you have to visit the Motueka Saltwater Baths located in the Nelson Tasman area. It was constructed during the year 1930, and it could well be considered as one of the world's earliest infinity pools. If you can plan a visit at peak tide during early morning or afternoon, you may even have this entire place to yourself. You will be floating at the point where the sea and sky converge, creating pure magic. It's one of those places where a well-thought-out New Zealand tour package can provide you with a people-free experience. Mount Stokes, Marlborough Sounds If you enjoy a good vista after a bit of a hike, climb Mount Stokes. It's Marlborough Sounds' highest point, and the vistas of the inlets, islands, and sea are breathtaking. On your New Zealand tour packages, this is where you stop and take a breath. And, you may even catch a glimpse of Powelliphanta, a giant carnivorous snail, which can only be seen here. Ōpārara Arches, West Coast Your New Zealand adventure will not be complete without walking into what appears to be a fairy-tale forest. Deep in Kahurangi National Park, the Ōpārara Arches are enormous limestone arches surrounded by dense, ancient rainforest. A peaceful 2 km walk takes you there. If you love photography or are simply quite in awe, you'll love it. Stewart Island, Southland Want to spot a wild Kiwi? You need to visit Stewart Island. It's isolated, serene, and the night skies are astronomical. It's a designated International Dark Sky Sanctuary, so if you're in luck, you may even spot the Southern Lights. Most New Zealand tour packages leave it out, so make sure that you don't omit it. Castlepoint, Wairarapa If you like both the beach and history, Castlepoint is ideal. The lighthouse here is something out of a postcard. The easy strolls, the peaceful lagoon, and the opportunity to see fur seals make it a destination that you should not miss on your New Zealand tour packages. Blue Lake (Rotomairewhenua), Nelson Lakes National Park If you're captivated by crystal-clear water, you need to include Blue Lake in your New Zealand itinerary. Nested within Nelson Lakes National Park, Blue Lake is the world's clearest natural freshwater, its visibility can reach up to 70–80 metres. The hues change between bright blues and greens, depending on the light. Although you can't swim here (it's sacred to Māori), standing on its shore is meditative. You can easily incorporate this into your New Zealand packages for touring the South Island's off-the-beaten-path places. Wharariki Beach, Golden Bay Wharariki Beach is like stepping into another world. When you're organising your New Zealand trip, particularly in the South Island, make time for this isolated beach close to Farewell Spit. The enormous rock arches, dunes whipped by the wind, and tidal pools brimming with frolicking seals make it a photographer's paradise. If you're interested in offbeat adventures, most New Zealand tour packages now offer guided walks here to assist you in capturing sunset at its most dramatic.
New Zealand.Foods to Eat in New Zealand: New Zealand’s culinary classics are dominated by scrumptious seafood and sugary items of confectionery. Be it fried bread, a wealth of shellfish or some heartwarming kawakawa tea, New Zealand’s food scene is brimming with flavor. Do try these delicious dishes that will certainly make you wish for more than one cheat day! Afghans - If you have a sweet tooth, you are in for a treat. These sweet bite sized morsels are made of cornflakes, flour, butter and sugar mixed with cocoa and glazed with chocolate icing. These are then topped off with crunchy walnuts as a finishing touch. Enjoy this sweet snack at one of New Zealand’s charming bakeries with a steaming cup of coffee or hot chocolate. Crayfish - Hark at all the seafood lovers out there! Eat like the locals do as you relish this delicacy loved by the New Zealanders. Small lobsters are cooked in a subtle sauce giving you a slightly sweet, palatable flavor and a meaty, tender texture. This dish will make your taste buds tingle. One bite and you will know why the locals are cray-cray for Crayfish! Fried bread - For all the health-conscious people out there, fried bread is a healthy alternative to doughnuts. These crispy crumbs can be picked up from night markets or local bakeries. Find some comfort on a cold day by pairing these crunchy crumbs with a hot soup or some stew. Desserts in Auckland - If you are a chocolate connoisseur, head to Aotea Squares’s OKO. Dig into delicious caramel mousse and dark chocolate crumble topped off with hot chocolate sauce. Make your way to Giapo, and bite into frozen sweet treats that resemble works of art.
New Zealand.Things to Do in New Zealand: New Zealand feels like a fairytale with its old-world charm intact. Our New Zealand packages make sure they capture each and every experience. Here are some things to do while you are in New Zealand. Make sure you add them to your shortlisted New Zealand tour packages. Embark on sunset gondola rides in Queenstown Say hello to playful penguins in Auckland Wander along the Wellington Waterfront Embark on sunset gondola rides in Queenstown Explore the adventure capital of the world. Sunset gondola rides and a scrumptious buffet meal are some of the experiences waiting to be embraced. Marvelous views of Queenstown amaze you as you whisk 450 metres above the ground. Head to the Moke Lake and pick your favourite picnic spot. Or spend a night by the lake as you fall asleep to the nocturnal sounds of nature. Say hello to playful penguins in Auckland Watch these adorable creatures waddle about on the land with their heads held high. While they gracefully dive into the water with sharp precision and perfection. Watch out for the world’s smallest penguin- the korora species that come ashore at night in the Tiri Matangi island. You can also spot the yellow-eyed penguin around the Catlins region. Wander along the Wellington Waterfront If you are looking for a leisurely wander with no particular agenda, head to the Wellington Waterfront. Stop by old-word cafes, bakeries and eateries that dot the Waterfront. Explore the city’s oldest market and take back home some souvenirs and mementos to remember this trip forever. It is now no secret that there are a range of activities and things to do in New Zealand. This fusion of dense rainforests, classic culinary experiences, cheery beaches and ancient buildings is what intrigues and hence tempts you to opt for our New Zealand tour packages. What are you waiting for? Head to our Thomas Cook website and book your New Zealand family tour package or New Zealand honeymoon package right away!

Coorg.Introduction: Coorg is a hill station located in the south of India, in Karnataka. It is famously known for its coffee plantations. Coorg tourism is only getting better especially after the pandemic people realised the importance of nature more than ever. Coorg is the largest producer of coffee in India. You will end up smelling the rich aroma of coffee every day of your tour to Coorg. It sounds fascinating and yummy, doesn’t it? Time to book one of our Coorg trip packages! Though it is a hill station, it doesn’t get too chilly. The climate is a harmonious balance of sunny days with cool breezes blowing and some humid days during the monsoon, but overall, Coorg has an ideal climate throughout the year. We offer Coorg travel packages for all seasons. This climate and the lush greenery attract people from all over the country for a Coorg tour. Many people living in Bangalore love driving to Coorg for the weekend which boosts Coorg tourism, so they go for one of the one-day Coorg packages. Officially known as Kodagu, Coorg is popularly called ‘The Scotland of India’ for its spectacular landscapes and natural beauty. There are several Coorg trip packages waiting to be booked by you! There are several viewpoints from the hills to see the town of Coorg which we include in our Coorg packages. As a part of all our Coorg travel packages, you can also visit the elephant camp, and wildlife sanctuary and marvel at the beauty of many waterfalls. Coorg is also home to a Shiva temple that is a must-visit. Built in 1820, this temple is unique because it is a Hindu temple with shades of Islam in its architecture. It has a dome-like structure as a part of the temple which you can see on your Coorg tour. It is believed that the shivling in this temple was brought by King Lingarajendra all the way from Kashi. One of the loveliest places to see in Coorg. Most people don’t believe it, you can see it for yourself if you choose any of our Coorg travel packages but there is also a large Tibetan population living in Coorg. The Golden Temple monastery, opened in 1963 is home to around 600 monks and 16,000 refugees. Our Coorg trip package has let you witness this. It is said to be the second-largest Tibetan settlement outside of Tibet. If you are someone who is spiritual, this will be one of the best places to visit in Coorg. Make sure to include the temple and the monastery in your Coorg itinerary.

Coorg.Best Time to Visit Coorg: Coorg experiences a tropical monsoon climate, but because it is at 3000 feet above sea level, it experiences a pleasant climate throughout the year, so select any of our Coorg travel packages, today. The peak time to take a Coorg tour is the winter season because it is the best time to visit Coorg. Summer In the summer, temperatures can reach up to 35 degrees Celsius in the afternoons. The temperature ranges from 26 to 35 degrees Celsius; however, the nights are very pleasant—temperatures during the night range from 15 to 20 degrees Celsius. We have many Coorg tour packages available in the summer too so take a Coorg tour! Monsoon Coorg experiences heavy rainfall in June, July and August. Rain gradually decreases in September. During rain, it is wet, humid and cool. Temperatures range from 21 degrees Celsius to 24 degrees Celsius during the day, and at night, temperatures experience a dip and fall between 12 degrees Celsius and 7 degrees Celsius. Don’t forget to pack an umbrella on your Coorg trip. We can help you pick the best among all Coorg tour packages so you can enjoy the misty mountain air to the fullest. This is your sign to take a Coorg tour. Winter Winters in Coorg are deeply loved by people because it does not get too cold, just perfect for a good cuddle and snuggle or to curl up in your blanket with a steaming cup of coffee and a good book! We have Coorg tour packages that are less hectic and have more time for relaxation. Temperatures fall between 11 degrees Celsius and 5.5 degrees Celsius. So, make sure to pack some warm and cosy clothes on your Coorg trip. All in all, there are many places to visit in Coorg and you can choose the weather best suited to you for a Coorg tour with one of our Coorg travel packages.
Coorg.How to reach Coorg?: Coorg, a popular destination, is accessible by various modes of transport. If you're wondering how to reach Coorg, here are your options: By Rail Coorg does not have a railway station; the closest railway station is Mysore. From Mysore, it is 107 kilometres away, which is a good 2-hour drive. Mysore is very well connected to all parts of India, so a train is an excellent and affordable option for your Coorg tour. So, your Coorg tour plan should work out cheaper for you! We can add rail tickets on all our Coorg travel packages. By Road A lot of people drive down from Bangalore and Mangalore as a weekend getaway. While Bangalore is almost 6 hours away, Mangalore is just over 3 hours away. Many people love road trips and drive down from Maharashtra and Kerala. From Kochi, you would take about 9 hours to drive, from Mumbai, you would take about 19 hours to drive. So, if you are in for an adventure and love to drive the scenic route, this would be a great option for you! We can then plan your Coorg holiday package starting from the day you arrive and not include the travel cost at all! By Air Your Coorg tour plan might be far more expensive if you choose to fly. Flying into Mangalore Airport or Bangalore Airport are the two options available. From Mangalore airport, it will take you about 3 and a half hours, as it is 143 kilometres away. From Bangalore airport, it is a longer drive, about 5 and a half hours away since it is almost 300 kilometres away. When you book one of our Coorg trip packages, you get a cab service too. Coorg tour packages can be with or without air tickets. Coorg can be reached by rail, road, or air, but it requires some road travel regardless of your choice. We have cabs available in all our Coorg travel packages. Our Coorg tour packages can be tailor-made according to your travel options.
Coorg.Things to Do in Coorg: If you are making a plan for a Coorg trip, you have a lot in store. Here are some of the best things you should do when you take one of the Coorg tour packages. Deep dive into spirituality A traveller gets to experience two very different religions up close in Coorg when you select one of our Coorg tour packages. You can see Buddhism at its finest in the Namdroling monastery, also known as Golden Temple. 600 monks and around 16,000 refugees live here. You can visit this place and watch them pray and meditate in the prayer hall. It is so serene and peaceful that you come back feeling a lot more composed and calm. This place has the second-largest Tibetan population outside of Tibet. Entry is free, and you can enjoy some yummy Tibetan food like momos and thukpa. Another religion to experience in Coorg is Hinduism. Coorg is the birthplace of one of the sacred rivers of India, Kaveri. Talakaveri is a temple where people can see the starting point of the river. Another great temple to visit, in all our Coorg travel packages, is the Omkareshwar temple, built by a king centuries ago because of a sin he committed. He was advised to build this temple as a way to deal with his guilt. The shivling in this temple was brought all the way from Kaashi. It's totally worth adding to your Coorg itinerary, isn’t it? So, select what you love best among all our Coorg tour packages. Discover Wildlife One can witness flora and fauna at its peak in Coorg when you select one of our Coorg tour packages. Being a hill station that’s been largely untouched by the evils of modern-day society, it is home to many endangered species of birds and animals. Just at the border of Coorg is the very famous Nagarhole National Park, which is also a tiger reserve. Book one of our Coorg trip packages to see a tiger! Take a one-day safari and make a memory to last a lifetime! If you are a nature enthusiast, make sure to visit on your next Coorg trip. We have many Coorg tour packages with this option. A health hike Coorg is like a small town shown in the movies; travel back in time by booking one of our Coorg trip packages, when there are no Ubers and Olas available at the drop of a hat. Walk around; trek the hills all around you. The climate is such that you will burn calories without breaking a sweat. Sounds like it is worth booking one of our Coorg trip packages. Take a moment to soak in the beauty of the hills, lush green nature and fresh air that surrounds you. In today’s day and age, a walk in nature is truly a privilege, gift and blessing all wrapped up in one! You must make some time for this in your Coorg sightseeing for the day! This won’t be included in any Coorg tour packages but must be taken seriously at any cost. Feel the coolness of water Coorg has so many waterfalls to visit, each standing out for different reasons. When you select one of our Coorg tour packages, you will be taken to the best waterfall spots. While one has a small hike involved, the other has some rocks you can sit on while you enjoy the sound of water and feel the coolness of water on your skin. Some waterfalls are not too deep, and you can even swim in them with your loved ones next weekend or month if you book one of our Coorg trip packages right away! It sounds so much fun, right? The best part is that the government charges a very small price for the beauty you get to experience in return! Most Coorg travel packages include one or two waterfalls. A walk to remember in the coffee plantations Coorg’s speciality, the thing people travel for is Coorg’s coffee. One of the best things to do in Coorg. The awesome thing when you book one of our Coorg trip packages is not the fact that you can smell coffee in the air all day long, it is the fact that you can actually watch your coffee being made from scratch, from the farm to your cup! A lot of owners open up their estates for tours, and it is the cutest thing you could do in Coorg. We include only the best coffee estate on our Coorg packages. At the end of one of our Coorg trip packages that you booked, you get to have coffee that is freshly made from the beans ground right in front of you. You suddenly develop a sense of understanding and gratitude for what goes behind the joy you experience on a daily basis when you have your cup of coffee for the day. Make sure that your Coorg travel package includes this because it is considered one of the top Coorg tourist attractions. It is a must even if you are visiting for a day, so include it in your Coorg one day tour. Shop till you drop Coorg has so many things to buy, it is a shopper’s paradise. Our Coorg packages will ensure you take home many gifts. A cook's paradise, mainly because you get organic spices here, brought to you straight from the farm, may it be pepper, cardamom, nutmeg or even dry fruits, all locally grown and sold to tourists. There is hardly any scope for manipulation. When you book one of our Coorg trip packages, you can also take home fresh coffee powder. The stock runs out on a daily basis, so rest assured that your coffee powder was probably a bean a week or two ago. You also get homemade chocolates and homemade wine, both of which are local specialities that should not be missed at any cost. Ensure that your Coorg sightseeing package includes an ample amount of time for shopping. Go back in time Coorg has a fascinating history. The Kodava clan began in this very region, and you can discover more about them at the fort and in Raja’s tomb. For history buffs, this is one of the most memorable places to see in Coorg. It also has the ancestry home of General Thimayya, which has been converted into a war memorial that you can see if you book one of our Coorg trip packages. We love adding these places to all our Coorg packages. Discover his contribution and sacrifices to our nation, and be amazed by what you end up learning on a holiday! All these places are Coorg tourist places enjoyed by all age groups and easily accessible, and the government has been kind enough to charge a small amount. You get to be a part of history, you get to experience what it was like to live and die in the olden times, with honour. If you are a history buff, there are many Coorg tour packages meant for this purpose. Go off-grid for a hot minute Coorg is a hill station, a small town, cut off from the everyday fancies of the big city, which is a blessing in today’s times. Switch off your phones, go for a long walk around the town, sign up for a group hike, or even consider going to a nearby garden and reading a book while lying in the grass or even looking at the clouds moving in the sky and the trees gently swaying in the breeze. This might not be included in our Coorg packages but we want you to make time for it! Make time for yourself on this trip, some time for the heart, mind and soul. In your Coorg travel make time to breathe. Play with elephants The Dubare Elephant Camp is one of the top places to visit in Coorg. You get to pet elephants, feed elephants and even ride on them for forty-five minutes. That’s not just the best part, to get to the camp, you have to take a small boat ride, which is also so refreshing. You also have the option of living in the cottages in the elephant camp if you are a big elephant enthusiast and you think being close to them heals something in you. It is definitely one of the best places in Coorg. You have to include this in your Coorg itinerary. Relish the local food Coorg’s local food is a gift passed down from generations of Kodavas, the native people of Coorg. The two famous curries eaten in Coorg are Pandy Curry and Baimbale Curry. Pandy curry is the pork curry which has been prepared for time immemorial since the time wild boars visited the farms of Kodavas. Kodavas used to hunt them and eat their meat. However, now hunting is banned, so pig meat is used instead. This curry is spicy and quite aromatic. The next curry that is deeply loved is the bamboo shoot curry, also known as Baimbale curry. Bamboo shoots are soaked in water for 2-3 days before the curry is prepared. The chilli and coriander will be a treat for your taste buds. Both of these curries are enjoyed with Akki Roti, Kaddambuttu and Nuuputtu. Akki roti is rotis made out of rice, Kaddambuttu are rice dumplings and Nuuputtu are thin noodles made of rice. So, you must try and eat these on your Coorg trip and that will happen only if you include it in your Coorg tour itinerary.
Coorg.Places to visit in Coorg: Golden Temple The Golden Temple should not be mistaken with the one in Amritsar. This one’s equally special. The Golden Temple in Coorg is a Buddhist monastery also known as the Namdroling Monastery. You can see monks walking around, praying and meditating. It is a peaceful place with a meditative atmosphere. You can also enjoy some Tibetan delicacies at the cafe within the monastery’s complex. It is a three-story building with 600 monks and 16,000 refugees residing, making it the second-largest Tibetan settlement outside of Tibet. This is one of the best places to visit in Coorg. Omkareshwar Temple Omkareshwar temple was built in 1820 by King Lingrajendra II because he had sinned. He had killed an innocent Brahmin man due to political aspirations. This death deeply affected him, he started having many nightmares, and under the instructions of some astrologers, he built this Shiva temple. The shivling in this temple was brought from Kashi back then. The architecture of the temple has elements of both Hinduism and Islam. Visiting this temple is a must because it leaves you with a sense of calm and divinity. This temple is located in the heart of Coorg, i.e. in the main area of Madikeri. Don’t forget to add this to your Coorg sightseeing package if you are spiritual! It is one of the best places in Coorg! Coffee Estates The highlight of Coorg, its main attraction is the numerous coffee estates found in every nook and cranny of Coorg. A lot of these estates are open to tourists for natural plantation walks. It is an absolute delight to walk with your friends and family and learn how coffee beans come into existence. Many of these walks end with a steaming cup of coffee with beans from the estate itself. The information and insights you gather about coffee will be a memory of a lifetime. There’s something so beautiful in watching the process of something being made from scratch, like coffee, that most people love. Well, that surely sounds like a fun experience. So, go offline and out of range for a few hours and walk around these lovely, lush green coffee estates in Coorg on your next Coorg trip because this will always be one of the best tourist places in Coorg! Dubare Elephant Camp This is one of the top places to visit in Coorg. People from all age groups will enjoy this particular activity. Tourists can go to this camp by taking a short boat ride. During the summer, during summers the river dries up so they can walk to the camp. In the camp, you get to feed the elephants and also take an elephant ride which is 45 minutes long. If you are someone who loved the documentary Elephant Whisperer, then this place is just for you! Here, elephants are not treated like circus animals, in fact, they are in their natural habitat and are trained by naturalists. If elephants fascinate you a little too much, you can consider spending a night at the cottage in the camp. You can then spend an entire day in the company of some mighty and adorable elephants. Plan your Coorg itinerary while keeping these adorable and mighty elephants in mind. Nagarhole National Park At the border of Coorg is the Nagarhole National Park. It is a tiger reserve with the biggest tiger population after Corbett and Kaziranga. Nagarhole has many pretty streams, rich in wildlife and abundant vegetation. Along with tigers, you can find many birds and animals like leopards, elephants, sloth bears, gaurs, sambhars and antelopes. Nagarhole means serpent's hole in Kannada. So, this has to be one of the top Coorg tourist attractions, and it is unmissable! Waterfalls Coorg has some majestic waterfalls like the Abbey Falls, Iruppu Falls, Burude Falls, Mallalli Falls, and Devarakolli Falls. Each of these falls will be a sight for your sour eyes. Entrance to all these falls costs less than 100 rupees. Some might require you to trek a little, making the whole experience adventurous and worth the wait. These falls thunderously roar in the monsoon, and it is advisable to check with the locals before you pay a visit. Most waterfalls will leave you with a feeling of peace, and the chatter of your mind will simply disappear. Kaveri’s clean water will leave you impressed, for sure! This addition to your Coorg trip plan is mandatory! Talakaveri temple Talakaveri temple is in the Brahmagiri hill range. It is 50 kilometres away from Madikeri. It is considered to be the birthplace of the river Kaveri, making it an auspicious temple. People are often found throwing coins and making wishes in the natural spring inside the temple. Talakaveri will help deepen your meditative state of mind and is the perfect addition to your soulful trip in Coorg. Add this to your Coorg tour itinerary because it has been one of the most famous tourist places in Coorg for ages! Raja’s seat Raja’s seat’s literal translation is the ‘King’s seat. In olden times, kings used to sit here and enjoy the scenic view of the mountains. It is mesmerising even today. Now, the Raja’s seat has a park around it where families can stroll and kids can play. The entry fee is a nominal amount of 5 rupees and you can spend a few hours here playing with your kids, looking at some pretty flowers or watching the musical fountain that happens in the evening. It is also a sunrise and sunset point, making it one of the most famous tourist places in Coorg. Do consider adding this to your Coorg trip plan. Consider adding this to your Coorg city tour plan for the day! General Thimayya’s Memorial General K Thimayya served in the Indian Army as Chief of Army Staff from 1957 to 1961. His contributions can often be overlooked by the younger generations, and that’s why it was deemed fit to convert his ancestral home in Coorg into a war memorial. If you are a history geek, this memorial can be a great afternoon excursion for you. You get to see the entire story displayed in the form of pictures in his house, you can also see his attire, furniture, and weaponry, all brilliantly displayed at different points of the story. There is so much to read and learn at this place, you will walk out feeling proud and inspired. You must add this to your Coorg itinerary. You wouldn’t need a Coorg travel guide for this as it is all self-explanatory! Madikeri Fort Madikeri Fort was built in the 17th century. It was rebuilt and renovated by Tipu Sultan and the Britishers. The Britishers added a lovely church inside the fort. There is also a palace inside the fort. You must visit this place to witness history come alive in front of you! Getting a Coorg travel guide could be extremely helpful here, they will ensure you don’t feel lost and will enlighten you with the smallest of details. You could add this to your Coorg city tour plan for the day. Raja’s tomb The tomb has been built on the hills and it is the only tomb in India for Hindu rulers since Hinduism doesn’t have the concept of burial. The tomb has been dedicated to the mortal remains of the Kodavu kings of Coorg. This place is also known as Gaddige. It has beautiful gardens all around, which can be enjoyed by visitors for a nominal fee of 2 rupees. Built-in 1809, this place has a touch of Muslim architecture. The idea of building the tomb came from the inspiration of Shah Jahan’s tomb for his wife, the incredible Taj Mahal. Do visit this place on your next Coorg trip.
Coorg.What to shop in Coorg?: Coorg is a shopper’s paradise and all for the right reasons. Most things you buy here are for your tummy, so buying them means new flavours getting added to your life left, right and centre. Things can be a little pricey considering it is a tourist destination, but you can surely give bargaining a try! It is important to keep in mind to always carry cash around since you might lose internet connectivity being on the hills, so don’t always rely on UPI transactions, especially in the monsoon season. Always carry cloth shopping with you, so you can do your bit of not using plastic bags. You must wear comfortable walking shoes and spare a couple of hours on your tour to Coorg for shopping. We make Coorg vacation packages keeping this specifically in mind. Some things to buy from Coorg on your next tour to Coorg are as follows- Coffee You just can't come back from a Coorg trip without sacks of coffee. The plantations here give you some of the cleanest, chemical-free beans you'll ever taste. You can try out various flavours before you purchase. Be it South Indian filter coffee powder or the legendary steel filter sets, you're covered. Most Coorg packages offer visits to estates, where you can even witness beans becoming your morning coffee. Wine Local fruit wines (non-alcoholic and free of preservatives) in Coorg are a delight. These have been hand-made by generations of women, and it is a must-have experience on your Coorg tour. It's worth including in your Coorg packages itinerary. Honey Large-scale honey production has come to a slowdown, but whatever is left is natural, raw, and yum. Do take home a bottle or two. Well-planned Coorg tour packages will take you to genuine local vendors. Organic Spices Along with coffee, for generations, people have also grown spices on their estates. Spices like cardamom, nutmeg, and pepper are commonly grown here, along with different types of dry fruits. You find organic, chemical-free spices at local spice markets — and yes, bargaining is encouraged. Most Coorg packages include spice markets where they go. Handmade chocolates Locally produced chocolates, with different flavours of fruits and nuts, are a true delight. Always superior to the factory-produced ones. Your trip to Coorg will be sweeter for it. Saris Look into purchasing a Coorgi sari, which is pure silk with exquisite work. It's stylish and differently cut. Many of the Coorg packages can arrange visits to sari weavers if you request.
Coorg.Interesting Facts About Coorg: Some awesome facts will convince you to book your Coorg tour today! Coorg is the largest producer of coffee in India. It is serious business. The Ministry of Commerce has set up a Coffee Board in Coorg to ensure quality coffee production all year. The Coffee Board looks into introducing advanced methods of coffee cultivation and farming, exporting licensing and providing subsidies. Our Coorg packages will take you to the best coffee plantations. So, when will you go for your tour to Coorg? Coorg was an independent state until 1956. In 1956, it was merged with Mysore, now known as Karnataka. Find out cooler facts about India related to Coorg on your tour to Coorg. Winemaking is a popular passion that women in Coorg practice at home. These wines are made of different fruits and are free of all kinds of chemicals and preservatives. Our Coorg tour packages always take you to the finest wine making businesses. Your tour to Coorg will end with sweet memories People native to Coorg are known as Kodavas. Kodavas were warriors and agriculturists from ancient times, and to this day, they guard their farmlands. If you find this fascinating, well you have to get on that flight to see them in person on your Coorg travel. Women in Coorg wear their saris in a way that looks heavily inspired by Greek culture. There has been a crazy theory going around for decades now that suggests that people in Coorg are descendants of Greeks. Apparently, a part of Alexander’s army stayed back and married local women. Witness this on your tour to Coorg! So, are you on board to go on a tour to Coorg?

faq.question: How many days are ideal for an Andaman trip?
faq.answer: You'll find that most itineraries recommend 5 to 7 days if you're looking at Andaman packages. This allows you ample time to visit Havelock, Neil Island, and Port Blair leisurely. For a more comprehensive experience, it is advised to spend nine to ten days in unusual locations like Baratang or Diglipur. How much you want to immerse yourself in island life will determine which Andaman tour package is best for you.

faq.question: Is Andaman safe for solo travellers/families?
faq.answer: One of the best features of an Andaman Nicobar package is safety. The inhabitants of the islands are quite kind, and there is little crime on them. You'll feel comfortable travelling alone, even if you're a woman. However, you should take some basic safety precautions, such as avoiding remote regions at night. With family-friendly resorts, exciting water sports, and safe beaches, Andaman family tour packages are made with kids in mind.

faq.question: What are the must-do activities in Andaman?
faq.answer: Adventure and leisure should coexist in your Andaman Nicobar tour package. To explore colourful coral reefs, try snorkelling or scuba diving at Havelock and Neil. Radhanagar Beach, frequently regarded as one of Asia's greatest, is a great place to spend a leisurely afternoon. Don't miss the Light and Sound Show at Port Blair's famous Cellular Jail, which transports you back to India's liberation struggle. The mud volcanoes and limestone caverns on Baratang Island make for an exciting day trip as well. Additionally, you'll have more time to enjoy these events without worrying about connections if you purchase Andaman tour packages with flight.

faq.question: Do I need to carry cash, or are cards accepted everywhere?
faq.answer: In the Andamans, cash is king, even though some eateries and resorts take cards. Although there are ATMs in Havelock and Port Blair, they frequently run out of money at the busiest travel times. For your Andaman family tour or if you're on a longer Andaman Nicobar tour package, it's best to have enough cash on hand. Though smaller businesses and boat operators will nearly always ask for cash, UPI payments are starting to catch up in larger communities.

faq.question: What kind of clothes should I carry?
faq.answer: Light cotton and linen clothing are ideal because of the tropical temperature. For the beaches, bring t-shirts, shorts, and swimwear. Remember to include a high-SPF sunscreen, sunglasses, and a wide-brimmed hat. A lightweight jacket or shawl is appropriate for evenings, particularly when travelling by ferry. It is polite and advised to dress modestly when visiting temples or tribal reserves.

faq.question: Can I visit all the islands?
faq.answer: It is impossible to visit every one of the Andaman and Nicobar group's 572 islands. Port Blair, Havelock (Swaraj Dweep), and Neil (Shaheed Dweep) are typically included in your Andaman holiday packages for families or couples, with optional excursions to Baratang, Diglipur, or Jolly Buoy. Off-limits are restricted places, particularly those inhabited by indigenous people. The good news is that you already have a lot of diversity thanks to the accessible islands.

Andaman.Introduction: Imagine entering a realm where white sand beaches and turquoise waters meet, where time seems to stand still, and each sunset is a private performance for you alone. A trip to the Andaman Islands promises just that. The Andaman Islands offer a total escape into adventure, history, and nature; they are not just another beach vacation spot. You'll sense the magic all around you, whether you're lounging by the shore, snorkelling with colourful marine life, or exploring lush tropical forests. You'll discover that every Andaman package, from romantic retreats to adventurous escapes, caters to a distinct type of traveller. An Andaman family tour package can offer you the ideal mix of comfort and enjoyment if you're travelling with loved ones. Andaman travel packages include everything from island hopping to water sports, catering to those who want all-inclusive experiences. A comprehensive Andaman Nicobar tour package that allows you to experience the unspoiled splendour of both areas may even tempt you. Selecting the ideal Andaman trip package is like creating your own perfect vacation, as there are numerous options available. The best part is that you just have to take the first step to discover the new story that awaits you in every Andaman tour package.

Andaman.Why the Andaman Nicobar Islands Should B: The Andaman and Nicobar Islands package is the ideal tropical retreat if you've been dreaming of it. Here is why you shouldn't miss this place. Every Corner Offers Adventure The islands offer unparalleled opportunities to experience life beneath the surface, including scuba diving, snorkelling, and seawalking. You don't have to worry about arranging because the majority of Andaman trip packages include water activities. Beaches That Look Like Postcards Imagine seeing the turquoise seas change colour as the sun sets while strolling barefoot on snow-white sands. Every Andaman excursion feels like heaven because of beaches like Elephant Beach, Vijaynagar, and Radhanagar. You may decide how much adventure or relaxation you desire with Andaman packages. Ideal for Families An Andaman family tour package guarantees a seamless fusion of sightseeing, relaxation, and kid-friendly activities if you're travelling with loved ones. The Andaman holiday package is stress-free because resorts frequently tailor stays for families. Simple and Adaptable Travel These days, getting to these islands is easy with Andaman travel packages and Andaman tour packages with flights. You can choose a package that suits your time and budget, whether you choose a short or long trip to the Andaman and Nicobar Islands. In addition, compared to other tropical locations, the Andaman tour package cost is quite affordable. A Combination of Heritage and Nature Beyond beaches, your Andaman travel packages will take you to places like the historic Cellular Jail in Port Blair, the limestone caverns of Baratang, and the mud volcanoes of Diglipur. You will feel a part of the story that each location has to tell.
Andaman.Planning Your Andaman trip: Essential In: The Andaman Islands are most likely the first location that springs to mind when you envision an island vacation full of beaches, woods, and adventure. But it's helpful to know a few basics before you leave. Let's go over the key points that will ensure a stress-free, enjoyable, and unforgettable journey to the Andaman Islands.
Andaman.Best Time to Visit Andaman Nicobar: Your Andaman trip's scheduling can have a significant impact. The islands have a tropical environment, and October through May are the ideal times to go because of the nice weather and quiet seas. Peak Season (October to March): This is the busiest period, particularly for those who enjoy beaches and water sports. Activities covered in the majority of Andaman holiday packages are ideal because of the consistently pleasant weather. Moderate Season (April to June): April through June is the moderate season; it's a little warmer but still ideal for snorkelling, island hopping, and relaxing on the beach. Quieter beaches are available during this season if you're thinking about Andaman tour packages for couple's vacation. Monsoon (July to September): Although there will be fewer tourists and cheaper Andaman packages during this time, heavy rain and choppy seas may cause problems for inter-island transport.
Andaman.How to Reach the Andaman Islands?: There are two primary routes to get to the Andaman islands. By Sea: Government-run ships depart from Chennai, Kolkata, and Visakhapatnam for those who are more daring. The trip, which lasts 60 to 70 hours, offers a distinctive maritime experience. For a more immersive experience, sea travel may be included in your Andaman Nicobar package. By Air: You may fly directly to mainland destinations like Delhi, Mumbai, Chennai, Kolkata, and Bengaluru from Port Blair's Veer Savarkar International Airport. Flights last between two and five hours. To save you the trouble of making separate reservations, a lot of operators now provide Andaman tour packages with flight.
Andaman.Travel Documents & Permits: Don’t forget to gather the following documents: Indian nationals: For Indian nationals, neither a passport nor a visa is required. It suffices to have a photo ID issued by the government, such as a voter ID or Aadhaar. However, access to some restricted tribal regions requires specific permits. Foreign nationals: Foreign nationals must have a current passport and an Indian visa. Additionally, you will want a Restricted Area Permit (RAP), which is readily obtained at the seaport or airport in Port Blair. The RAP can be renewed for an additional 15 days after its initial 30-day validity. Although some islands are excluded, your operator will typically walk you through the process if you're ordering an Andaman and Nicobar Islands package.
Andaman.Inter-Island Travel: There are various ways to hop between islands when exploring the Andamans. Road and Ferry Combo: Ferries and road trips are frequently combined when visiting North Andaman, such as Rangat, Baratang, or Diglipur. These transfers are already included in several Andaman holiday packages. Ferries: The most popular and practical way. While government ships are less expensive but slower, private companies like Makruzz offer quick, air-conditioned transportation. Helicopters: Usually used by locals and government representatives, although occasionally offered to tourists for a fee.
Andaman.Curated Andaman Nicobar tour packages by: A variety of Andaman travel packages are available from Thomas Cook to suit different tastes and price ranges. You can select an itinerary that works for you, whether you're searching for luxury getaways, affordable options, or a combination of the two. Flights, lodging, guided tours, and transfers are all included in most Andaman tour package cost, saving you the trouble of organising each component independently. These Andaman holiday packages, which range from quick three-day getaways to in-depth weeklong excursions, guarantee that you get the most out of your stay on the islands. Andaman.Top Attractions & Experiences in Andaman: Planning an Andaman trip is about more than simply the beaches; it's about history, adventure, peace, and life-changing events. These attractions are essential to include in any Andaman trip package, whether you're planning a short vacation or a longer trip. Port Blair's Cellular Jail National Memorial This famous location transports you to the era of India's independence movement. You can practically hear the echoes of the past as you move through the Cellular Jail's hallways. The evening's poignant Light and Sound Show enhances your stay; don't miss it. A must-see for anyone interested in culture and tradition on any Andaman Nicobar travel itinerary. Havenlock Island's Elephant Beach If you want excitement and enjoyment, this is the place to be. It's a centre for adventure, offering everything from jet skiing and sea walking to scuba diving and snorkelling. Water sports at Elephant Beach are likely included in your Andaman tour package cost. Ideal for people who enjoy adventure and the ocean. Radhanagar Beach, Havelock Island The sea gleams against white sand at Radhanagar, which is frequently regarded as one of Asia's best beaches. It's the ideal location for a peaceful stroll, a swim, or simply relaxing and taking in the sunset. Due to its beautiful environment, this beach is a highlight of many Andaman tour packages for couple. The Island of Baratang Go to Baratang for an unusual adventure. It's a memorable trip because of the mangrove boat cruises, mud volcanoes, and limestone caverns. For those who like discovering the unique wonders of nature, this jewel is typically included in high-end Andaman holiday packages. Neil Island (Dweep Shaheed) Neil Island offers a calmer pace for those who prefer it. It's perfect for snorkelling and viewing coral reefs, with beaches like Bharatpur and Laxmanpur. Another highlight is the bridge made of natural rock. For a tranquil getaway, couples frequently select this stop on their flight-inclusive Andaman trip packages. Snorkelling and Scuba Diving One of the best places to dive in India is the Andamans. At Havelock, Neil, and North Bay, you may find crystal-clear waters, coral reefs, and vibrant fish. Scuba diving is an adventure you shouldn't pass up, so be sure it's included in the Andaman tour package cost you're evaluating. Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose Island) Ross Island Ross Island, the former British capital, is now home to haunting ruins that have been overtaken by banyan trees. You can stroll past barracks, bakeries, and historic cathedrals as peacocks and deer roam freely. The combination of nature and history enhances your Andaman packages. Rajiv Gandhi Water Sports Complex, Port Blair This facility provides glass-bottom boat rides, parasailing, and jet skiing for individuals who want everything in one location. It's a popular inclusion in Andaman family tour packages because families really like it.
Andaman.Things to Do in Andaman Beyond Sightseei: It's simple to imagine beaches, sunsets, and historical sites when you think about an Andaman trip. But if you go away from the typical sightseeing, there's a lot more to uncover. You can engage in water activities, discover wildlife, sample local culture, and create lifelong experiences with well-planned Andaman packages or a customised Andaman and Nicobar Islands package. Snorkelling and Scuba Diving Some of India's most abundant coral reefs may be found in the Andamans. You can view reefs, turtles, and colourful fish at places like Jolly Buoy Island and Elephant Beach. Check to see whether diving sessions are included in your Andaman honeymoon packages; they are well worth the money. Sea Walk Underwater At Havelock or North Bay, put on a special helmet and take a walk around the sea floor. It feels like you've entered a completely different universe, surrounded by colourful fish and corals. The highlight of many Andaman tour packages for couple is this bizarre experience. Fishing for game Deep-sea fishing excursions are well-liked by people who love the excitement of the pursuit. You may even catch barracuda or marlin. This activity is frequently included in luxury Andaman holiday packages targeted at thrill-seekers. Karting at sea Sea karting at Corbyn's Cove is a must-do if you enjoy speed. This is an exciting addition to your Andaman family tour with the opportunity to operate your own powerful boat. Kayaking at Night Imagine canoeing through serene mangroves while the sky is starry, and then all of a sudden, the water begins to shine bioluminescently. You may try it close to Havelock or Port Blair, and it's magical. These are the experiences that make Andaman holiday packages truly unforgettable. See the Volcano of Barren Island How frequently can you witness an active volcano? Barren Island boat trips are uncommon but unforgettable. This is frequently included in upscale Andaman Nicobar packages. Chidiya Tapu birdwatching Known as "Bird Island," this is a bird lover's heaven. As the sun sets, take in the sight of soaring eagles and vibrant parakeets. It is frequently on the itinerary of Andaman family tours. Observing Turtles in Kalipur You can see endangered turtles coming ashore during breeding season. It's humble and ideal for an Andaman packages with family, providing kids with treasured memories. Savour regional food and culture Without sampling the flavours of the islands, a trip isn't complete. Must-try dishes include coconut-based treats, barbecued fish, and fresh seafood curries. Shopping for native items at markets like Aberdeen Bazaar is a great way to get a deeper cultural connection than most Andaman tour packages with flight allow. Hiking in National Parks Trekking here offers amazing vistas, from the flowery paths of Mount Harriet to the strenuous peaks of Saddle Peak. These walks transform a basic Andaman packages into an adventure if you're a nature lover.
Andaman.Essential Tips for a Smooth Andaman trip: Although it's wonderful to plan your trip to the Andaman Islands, it's crucial to be well-prepared due to their isolated location. A little planning guarantees a stress-free vacation, regardless of whether you've reserved premium Andaman tour packages with flights, affordable Andaman packages, or a custom Andaman Nicobar package. Here are some crucial pointers to make the most of your island getaway. The packing checklist: When planning your Andaman holiday packages, you should travel light but wisely. Footwear: For walking or trekking, pack durable sneakers and sandals or flip-flops for the beach. If you intend to wade across rocky coasts, water shoes are a great addition. Clothes: Wear flowy dresses, t-shirts, and shorts made of cotton or linen. Bring modest clothing, like shirts or long pants, for evening events or temple visits. Remember to include a beach cover-up, several swimsuits, and a towel that dries quickly. Health & Personal Care: In the tropical sun, a wide-brimmed hat, sunglasses, and a high SPF sunscreen are essential. Include any necessary medications, insect repellent, and a basic first aid kit. Devices & Documents: Keep IDs, ferry tickets, and permits in a waterproof pouch. To get the ideal beach photos, a waterproof camera and a power bank will be useful. A well-packed backpack adds even more convenience to an Andaman family tour package if you're travelling with children. Banking & ATMs: In most locations, cash is king. There are numerous ATMs in Port Blair, although during busy times, the ones on Havelock and Neil may run out of money. Since cards are rarely accepted in smaller eateries, stores, and cabs, carry extra cash. Although larger resorts that are part of a high-end Andaman Nicobar tour package may typically take cards, it's wise not to depend only on them. Connectivity Across the islands, connectivity can be inconsistent. In Port Blair and Havelock, Airtel is reasonably dependable, whereas BSNL provides the most extensive coverage. However, don't count on fast internet, particularly on tiny islands or during ferry voyages. Get offline maps before you leave and give your loved ones access to your itinerary. Although most resorts have Wi-Fi, it's frequently sluggish. Bring a local SIM card in case of emergencies for a smooth Andaman packages. Responsible Tourism There are fragile ecosystems on the islands. You can increase the significance of your Andaman tour package by taking these thoughtful steps: Do not collect seashells or touch corals. Avoid restricted tribal areas and show respect for native customs. Bring reusable bottles rather than single-use ones. Always heed lifeguard advice, particularly when participating in water activities that are part of Andaman tour packages for couple's vacation. Emergency Contacts Save the following numbers: National Emergency Number (112), Police (100), Ambulance (102), Fire (101), and Tourist Helpline (1363). Having things close at hand brings comfort, particularly while travelling with family.

Mysore.About Mysore Holiday: Mysore is one of the most beautiful tourist destinations in India. From beautiful palaces to unspoilt natural attractions, you will have plenty to do and see when you get a good Mysore sightseeing package. Mysore is a very accessible place as well, with its own airport and railway station. The city is beautiful and organised and offers some great attractions, making it an ideal holiday spot. Many people get the Mysore packages and visit this city as Mysore is historically very rich and has a strong cultural heritage. It is home to the world-famous Mysore Palace, which apart from being a global attraction, is also a majestic structure that would leave you awestruck when you visit it as a part of your tour to Mysore. Mysore tourism allows you to explore the city, find some real gems, and come back fascinated and delighted from your Mysore travel.
Mysore.Most Popular Mysore Tour Packages: Tour Packages NameDurationPriceKarnataka - Coorg Mysore And Ooty6 Nights 7 DaysRs. ₹33 500Southern Serenity Escape7 Nights 8 DaysRs. ₹44 100Best of Tamil Nadu and Karnataka Tour9 Nights 10 DaysRs. ₹50 700Tamil Nadu - Ooty Mysore Bangalore5 Nights 6 DaysRs. ₹27 100Karnataka - Coorg Mysore Bangalore5 Nights 6 DaysRs. ₹31 600
Mysore.Best Time to Visit in Mysore: As mentioned above, there are different seasons and times when Mysore tourism picks up. Here’s a detailed look at the best time to visit Mysore: Season Best Time to Visit Mysore Weather & Temperature Why Visit? Winter October to March Mild weather with temperatures ranging from 15°C to 27°C. Bright sunshine during the day, cool breeze in the evenings. Ideal for exploring Mysore's top tourist attractions with pleasant weather. Peak tourist season with vibrant city life. Summer April to June Hot with temperatures reaching up to 39°C. Cooler evenings with a pleasant breeze. Fewer crowds at Mysore's famous tourist spots. Ideal for a peaceful and less crowded sightseeing experience. Monsoon July to September Cooler temperatures with frequent rainfall. The city and its attractions look more vibrant and lively. Less crowded, offering a serene experience of the city's beauty. Mysore Palace looks especially majestic during this time.
Mysore.How to reach Mysore?: There are multiple travel modes and options when you wish to reach Mysore. Here are some of the best ways to travel to the Mysore: Travel Mode Details Advantages By Air Airport: Mandakalli Airport (12 km from Mysore town) Flight Routes: Connects to Indian cities like Hyderabad and Chennai. Fast and time-saving, ideal for a hassle-free start to your Mysore tour. By Rail Connectivity: Mysore is well-connected by train to major Indian cities. Ease of Access: Direct trains available, making it a popular travel option. Cost-effective, Convenient and well-connected. No need for individual bookings; trains take you directly to the city. By Road Routes: Drive from Bangalore (approx. 3 hours), or take buses from cities like Bangalore and Hyderabad. Scenic Drive: Enjoy beautiful landscapes and village views en route. Flexibility: Ideal for self-driving or hiring a cab; easy exploration of Mysore upon arrival.
Mysore.Places to Visit in Mysore: Mysore is known as the city of palaces. There are many beautiful palaces and other historical monuments that serve as the most fascinating places to visit in Mysore. When you book one of the Mysore travel packages, you get to see many of these attractions which include: Mysore Palace When you get the Mysore trip packages and prepare to go to the city, you will hear a lot about its palace. The city of Mysore is synonymous with its grand Palace, which has been the official royal residence for over a century. The current version of Mysore Palace was built in 1912, but the original palace, which stood in the same place, was built in the 14th century. It was burnt and destroyed in a blaze, after which the current one was built. Include it in your Mysore sightseeing and have a rewarding experience. Brindavan Gardens Another one of the places to visit in Mysore, Brindavan Gardens is a stunningly beautiful attraction that you cannot miss when on a Mysore city tour. It is a sprawling garden with a beautiful landscape. It has a lake inside where you can try boating. There is also a beautiful fountain that has a ‘dance’ show. A trip to Brindavan Gardens is included in most Mysore tour packages, so you will be able to visit it. Jagmohan Palace Reach the city with one of the Mysore trip packages and explore attractions like this gorgeous palace, located in the heart of the city. Jagmohan Palace used to serve as an alternative residence for the Royal Family members. It was built in 1861 and now mainly functions as an arts and culture centre. It has an art gallery and an auditorium, among other attractions. The architecture is beautiful and you must see this gorgeous palace when you go on your Mysore city tour. Mysore Sand Sculpture Museum The Mysore Sand Sculpture Museum is another one of the fascinating Mysore places to visit. You can see a huge display of attractive structures and sculptures made out of sand. It is unique and should feature in your Mysore itinerary. Sri Chamundeshwari Temple On your tour to Mysore, you can visit one of the most revered temples not just of Mysore, but also of the entire southern Indian region, the Chamundeshwari Temple. It is a beautiful structure situated on Chamundi Hill. It is a very beautiful temple dedicated to Goddess Chamundeshwari. Since it is a well-known temple, it is included in most of the Mysore travel packages. Karanji Lake Karanji Lake is another one of the best places in Mysore. It is a huge lake with a butterfly park next to it and a huge aviary. It offers some stunning views of the landscape around. It is very serene and peaceful and ideal for a trip with your family. Include it in your Mysore tour plan and have a great time. Ensure you choose one of the better Mysore travel packages that allow you to see all the beautiful Mysore tourist attractions listed above.
Mysore.Best Honeymoon Destinations: A tour of Mysore and Ooty can be a great way to spend your honeymoon. It is a wholesome tourist destination with many attractions and activities to pursue. However, if you really want to make your honeymoon extra special, consider adding a few other destinations to your Mysore tour and have the most memorable vacation with your life partner. Here are some great options for you: Wayanad Located at a manageable distance of 117 km from Mysore, Wayanad is a beautiful hill station and one of the best honeymoon destinations in South India. You can visit all the famous tourist places in Mysore and then head over to Wayanad by cab or take a bus. If you book one of the best Mysore travel packages, you can also get the conveyance included. BR Hills Another jewel among the hills, BR Hills is one of the closest hill stations to Mysore, located just 87 km away. It is not a very crowded tourist destination, so you get to spend some precious, quiet moments with your beloved in this pretty place. Get one of the best Mysore travel packages, including a trip to BR Hills and experience true serenity. Mangalore Karnataka is one of the most developed states in India and has some of the best cities, including Mangalore. If you have some time on your hands, extend your Mysore honeymoon and head over to Mangalore to experience a combination of natural beauty and man-made marvels. From beaches to small hills, from stunning museums to swanky restaurants, you will find it all here. Make a proper Mysore travel plan and include a short visit to Mangalore as well. Bangalore Bangalore is one of the biggest and most famous cities in India. When you make a Mysore tour plan, keep some time in it for a quick Bangalore visit too. Bangalore would be a great addition to your honeymoon plan as it is the ideal place for a young couple. From adventure activities to clubbing, you will never have a dull moment in Bangalore with your sweetheart next to you! As a young couple, you should look to get the best of the Mysore travel packages and include some other destinations after you finish exploring the best places to visit in Mysore. Consider going to some of the places mentioned above and have an unforgettable honeymoon.
Mysore.Interesting Things to do in Mysore: There are some very interesting things to do in Mysore. Some of them are: Go on a heritage walk As mentioned above, Mysore has a strong heritage and culture. You must explore every bit of this heritage when you plan your Mysore tour. There are some interesting heritage walks organised by the locals. You need to pay a small fee for it, but you get to walk with like-minded people and a very well-informed Mysore tour guide who tells you all about the places you see. Go on a temple walk Similarly, you can also go on a temple walk. Mysore is known for its many temples. While some are very famous, some are hidden from the public eye, but stunning nonetheless. Go on a temple walk with an experienced Mysore tour guide and discover not just the temples, but also learn about their glorious past. Include this very fulfilling activity in your Mysore itinerary. Visit the GRS Fantasy Park If you are going on a Mysore tour with your family, include a trip to the GRS Fantasy Park in the Mysore itinerary. The kids love being here as there are water slides, thrilling rides and a lot of fun activities to try out. The park is situated within the city, so you can easily pay a visit and enjoy it with your loved ones. Take a cycling tour And finally, consider taking a cycling tour when you explore the best tourist places in Mysore. It is an amazing experience and you get to see the attraction at your own pace. Bicycles are available for rent. You can go on your own, or join a group of other cyclists and have a gala time. This may not be included in the Mysore trip packages, so look to get this included separately in your Mysore itinerary. Skydiving Many Mysore tour packages include adventure activities. One of the most thrilling adventurous things to do in Mysore is Skydiving. You get to jump out of a plane and descend with a parachute while experiencing the thrill of a lifetime! It is not common to find skydiving places easily, so do give this a try when you go on your Mysore tour. As you can see, there are some extremely enjoyable and interesting things to do in Mysore. If you are travelling with your family, friends or your partner, find the most suitable activities and try them out. Include them in your Mysore itinerary and live it up!
Mysore.What to Eat in Mysore?: When you book Mysore tour packages, indulging in the city's rich culinary offerings is a must. Here's a list of must-try local delicacies that will make your Mysore trip packages even more memorable: Mysore Masala Dosa: A crispy, tangy flatbread made from rice flour, enjoyed across India. Whether for breakfast, lunch, or dinner, this iconic dish is a must-have when exploring Mysore. Bisibelebath: This aromatic rice and pulses dish originated in the kitchens of Mysore Palace. Savor its rich history and flavor during your Mysore trip. Mutton Pulao: For non-vegetarians, Mysore's mutton pulao is a treat. With tender meat and aromatic spices, this dish stands out from the traditional North Indian Biryani. Filter Coffee: Karnataka’s coffee plantations make Mysore’s filter coffee exceptionally fresh and flavorful. Don't miss it on your tour to Mysore. Mysore Pak: This world-renowned sweet, made with ground pulses, sugar, and ghee, is a delightful end to any meal. Make sure to try it during your Mysore city tour. There is a huge variety of foods to try when you go for a Mysore city tour. Many of the Mysore trip packages include meals, so you may find the hotel offering you breakfast and dinner. Even then, try to get some local meals in the Mysore tour itinerary and savour some wonderful flavours.
Mysore.Interesting Facts about Mysore: Exploring Mysore is not just about its attractions; it's also about its rich history and culture. Here are some intriguing facts that will enhance your experience when you book Mysore trip packages: Oldest Seat of Education: Mysore is home to one of India’s oldest centers of learning, with Maharaja's College dating back to 1864. First Private Radio Station: India’s first private radio station was established in Mysore in 1935 by Mr. M.V. Gopalaswamy, showcasing the city's early technological advancements. 500-Year-Old Kingdom: The Wadiyar dynasty has ruled Mysore since 1399, making it the longest-running kingdom in Indian history. Discover more about this royal lineage with a Mysore travel guide. Oldest Zoo in India: The Mysore Zoo, founded in 1892, is the oldest zoo in India and houses rare species of animals and birds. Biggest Dasara Celebration: Mysore’s Dasara Festival is the grandest celebration in the city, lasting 10 days and featuring vibrant parades, fireworks, and traditional dances. These fascinating facts about Mysore make it an alluring tourist destination. Explore the Mysore packages and choose the one that suits you the best to easily visit this majestic city with your loved ones. When you reach the place, hire a good Mysore travel guide and learn about the city in depth.

Chandigarh.Introduction: If you have never been to Chandigarh, then you are in for a treat because this city is unlike any other metropolitan city in India. Make sure that your Chandigarh tour package includes most if not all the places we have recommended below, because trust us this spectacular city will leave you spellbound and mesmerised.Chandigarh Trip Package Himachal Holiday Packages No.Of Days Price* Heavenly Himachal - Buy 1 Get 1 Free 6 Nights/7 Days Rs. 18,495.00 Heavenly Himachal - Ex Chandigarh - Land Only (Summer Special) 6 Nights/7 Days Rs. 21,995.00 Himachal - Manali - Chandigarh - Ex Chandigarh 5 Nights/6 Days Rs. 20,900.00 Heavenly Himachal - Land Only 6 Nights/7 Days Rs. 24,490.00 Manmohak Himachal - Veg Spacial 6 Nights/7 Days Rs. 20,900.00 As most of you know, Chandigarh is a planned city, and everything from roads, museums, universities, suburbs, drainage systems, artificial lakes, rock gardens are planned to the T. But what most people don't know is that the brainchild behind all this planning was a brilliant Swiss architect known as Le Corbusier. He is the brainchild behind famous tourist attractions such as The Open Hand Museum, Sukhna Lake, The Government Music and Art Gallery etc. These are just a few of the many wonderful attractions that add to the character of this humble city. In addition to all this is another striking attraction known as the Nek Chand Rock Garden. Nek Chand was a government employee who designed this magnificent garden that is home to numerous stone sculptures that are a work of true genius. Chandigarh holds within itself many surprises and hidden secrets. And a visit here is must and the best part is that is Chandigarh is conveniently located on route to many popular destinations in Himachal. So, you can always plan a few days before and after to explore this immaculately designed city. West Bengal.Introduction: The state of West Bengal lies amidst the eastern region of India. It has a lot to offer to both domestic and international visitors in the form of its diverse culture and tradition, rich history, hill stations, forests, deltas, wildlife, beaches, cuisines and much more. All these varieties of experiences make West Bengal a marvellous holiday location within India. To enjoy your vacations to the fullest, you can go with the West Bengal tour packages provided by Thomas Cook India. These West Bengal tour packages with well-researched travel itineraries are offered at great prices. So, instead of handling the complicated core planning process, you can just relax and enjoy your ultimate holidays in West Bengal. You can also get the option of “customised holidays” with certain West Bengal tour packages for a more personalised travel experience. Take a look at our handy West Bengal tourist guide for a more prompt overview.
