West Bengal.Most Popular West Bengal Tour Packages: Thomas Cook India offers over 20 West Bengal holiday packages that you can pick from. Among them, here are the most popular West Bengal travel packages:Tour Packages NameDurationPriceNorth East Delight With Pelling6 Nights 7 DaysRs. ₹39 000Fascinating Sikkim and Darjeeling6 Nights 7 DaysRs. ₹45 100Darjeeling and Gangtok with Pelling7 Nights 8 DaysRs. ₹50 400Gangasagar Yatra Ex Kolkata - Dakshineswar, Belur Math, Kalighat Darshan3 Nights 4 DaysRs. ₹23 700West Bengal - Queen Of Hills3 Nights 4 DaysRs. ₹24 300
West Bengal.Best time to visit West Bengal: The three main West Bengal tourism seasons are winter, summer and monsoon. Winter, usually ranging from late October or November to March, is often considered the best time to visit West Bengal. The temperature varies between northern and southern West Bengal but usually remains within the low 20s overall. Foggy mornings and chilly weather are its main features. Days remain clear, making it ideal for sightseeing and exploration. The key attractions of this period include the snow-covered hill station experiences and tons of delightful festivals and events, including Durga Puja, Christmas, New Year, etc. Monsoon in West Bengal usually stretches from July to September. The temperature is hot but not unbearable in the southern part of the state, while the northern region experiences a cooler range of 13 to 19°C. Rain showers are typical throughout, adding to the region’s natural beauty. Many tourists consider monsoon the best time to visit West Bengal for its exuberant greenery, mesmerising village-side sightseeing, outdoor activities, shoulder season crowd and much more. Summer in West Bengal, usually from April to June, offers a different perspective on tourism. The average temperature runs very hot and can reach around 45°C during the daytime. Summer heat and high humidity make sightseeing and exploration quite difficult for tourists. Its main attractions are hill stations, beaches and wildlife watching. During this time, you may find some good discounts on West Bengal tour plans and hotels. Also, check out our detailed West Bengal tourist guide to find more details on the best time to visit the destination.
West Bengal.How to reach West Bengal?: Here are the best ways to reach West Bengal for your vacation: Reaching West Bengal by air Reaching West Bengal by rail Reaching West Bengal by road Reaching West Bengal by road Travelling within West Bengal Reaching West Bengal by air: Airports in West Bengal offer both domestic and international air connectivity. Some of its main airports include Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose International Airport in Kolkata, Bagdogra Airport in Siliguri and Kazi Nazrul Islam Airport in Asansol-Durgapur. For instance, if you are travelling from major Indian cities like Mumbai, Delhi, Bangalore, etc., you can easily get a direct flight to Kolkata, which can take anywhere between 2-3 hours. Don’t forget to book your flight tickets for the trip in advance. Reaching West Bengal by rail: There are various major railway stations operating throughout the state, including Howrah Railway Station, Sealdah Railway Station, New Jalpaiguri Railway Station, Asansol Junction, etc. All these stations connect West Bengal with almost every major city in India, including Bangalore, Mumbai, Delhi, etc., with daily and weekly trains. Reaching West Bengal by road: Individuals travelling to West Bengal by road from neighbouring states, like Bihar, Orissa or Jharkhand, can easily opt for interstate buses or hired cabs to reach the state by road. In addition, West Bengal also has great road connectivity with other major Indian cities. For example, if you travel from Chennai to Kolkata, you can easily take the NH 16 route along the east coast. While visitors from New Delhi can opt for NH 2 to reach West Bengal, and so on. Reaching West Bengal by water: If you want to travel to West Bengal by water, you can do so to some extent. For example, if you are travelling from Andaman and Nicobar islands, you can opt for a passenger ship service to Kolkata. Travelling within West Bengal For the aspect of travel in West Bengal, you can opt for domestic flights, rental cars, taxis, intercity trains, rickshaws, and so on. Tram and underground metro rail services are also available in major cities, including Kolkata.
West Bengal.Best honeymoon destinations in West Beng: The soaring high hill stations, lush green forests biome and serene beaches make West Bengal a fantastic location for a dream honeymoon. The most popular destination of which is Darjeeling, covered with beautiful snow-capped mountains, gorgeous views, rich local culture, delicious cuisine and so on. If you are interested in spending a lovely time with your partner in this hill station, consider this special honeymoon West Bengal tour package that includes Darjeeling. Some other prominent honeymoon destinations in West Bengal are Lava and Lolegaon- Located within Kalimpong district, Lava and Lolegaon make up for a fantastic getaway for newlyweds. Some of their key attractions include scenic views, beautiful lakes, mesmerising forests, etc. Bakkhali- It is a small beach town along the coastline and a perfect place for a slow-paced honeymoon experience. Some of its main attractions are bike rides along the beach, dense mangrove forest, delectable local dishes, etc. Purulia- It is one of West Bengal’s 23 districts and a famous honeymoon destination. The jaw-dropping beauty of lakes, forests, hills, historic landmarks, etc., are some of its main attractions.
West Bengal.Best places to visit in West Bengal: Here are some of the best places to visit in West Bengal for an ideal vacation: Kolkata Sundarbans Shantiniketan Jaldapara National Park Murshidabad Chandannagar Kolkata Besides being a great starting point for the trip, Kolkata is one of the best places to visit in West Bengal. The hundreds of years old city history and rapid modern growth create a magnificent blend that attracts tourists from all over the globe. Some of its major attractions include Victoria Memorial, Fort William, Indian Museum, ECO Park, Jorasanko Thakur Bari, Alipore Zoo, street food and so on. Sundarbans A prime West Bengal best tourist spot for nature and wildlife enthusiasts is the Sundarbans National Park. It is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and is popularly known for being the world’s largest contiguous mangrove forest. It is home to a variety of regional wildlife, including Royal Bengal tigers, leopards, saltwater crocodiles, olive ridley turtles, varieties of snakes, etc. It is also famous for its boat rides, trekking, and jungle exploration activities. You can look into West Bengal tourism packages that specifically include Sundarbans. Shantiniketan Shantiniketan is a small town, amidst West Bengal’s Birbhum District, has a crucial link to Nobel Laureate Rabindranath Tagore. This is where he resided and established the world-renowned Visva Bharati University. It is now a massive tourist attraction in West Bengal, drawing the attention of both domestic and international visitors. Some key attractions of this place are Tagore’s Ashram, Rabindra Bhavan Museum, Poush Mela, Amar Kutir, Tagore’s artwork, etc. It is an ideal location for a small tour in West Bengal. Jaldapara National Park Located on the Eastern Himalayas’ foothills within Alipurduar District, the Jaldapara National Park is a must-visit tourist place in West Bengal. Besides incredible views of nature, it is a key location for thrill-seekers to enjoy car safaris, elephant rides, wildlife watching, and so on. The rich biodiversity of Jaldapara National Park is home to various animals, including Asian elephants, swamp deers, bison, one-horned rhinoceros, tigers, and so on. You can add this key location to your West Bengal itinerary. Murshidabad West Bengal’s Murshidabad is a sensational place for tourists who are interested in learning and witnessing the region’s history. From the ruling of Nawabs to life under British colonial rule, this historical city has gone through key phases of Indian history. You can find traces of bygone era reeking throughout the local architecture, culture and tradition. Some key tourist attractions in Murshidabad include Hazarduari Palace, Nizamat Imambara, Footi Mosque, Moti Jheel, etc. It is a popular destination for a weekend tour in West Bengal. Chandannagar Chandannagar, in West Bengal’s Hooghly district, was a major French Colony. Located on the bank of the glorious Hooghly river, this small city is a great off-beat location you can visit during your trip to West Bengal. You can find the French influence throughout the city’s architecture and key landmarks. Some major attractions in Chandannagar include the famous Strand Ghat, Chandannagar Museum, Sacred Heart Church, Patal Bari, etc. Make sure to check out our handy West Bengal travel guide on the best places to visit in the state.
West Bengal.Best hill stations/ best beaches in West: The best part about a tour to West Bengal is that you can enjoy both hill station and beachside experiences. Here are the best hill stations and beaches in West Bengal: Best hill stations in West Bengal Darjeeling- Darjeeling is the most popular hill station in West Bengal. Its authentic charm, local culture, bewitching mountain views, waterfalls, monasteries, etc., make it an astonishing holiday getaway. Some of its main attractions are tea gardens, Tiger Hill, Batasia Loop, toy train rides, and so on. Darjeeling is also a great location for a weekend trip in West Bengal. Mirik- It is a popular hill town close to Darjeeling. Mirik is a perfect offbeat location for a short relaxing break, surrounded by magnificent lakes and mountains. Its main attractions include Orange Orchards, Mirik Lake, Pashupati Nagar, etc. Other hill stations- Some other popular hillside destinations in West Bengal are Kalimpong, Kurseong, Siliguri, Dooars, Rimbik, etc. Best beaches in West Bengal Digha- It is one of the most popular beaches and weekend destinations in West Bengal. The calming waves, beautiful sceneries, delectable seafood and other fantastic beachside experiences make it a famous vacation spot for couples and short family trips. Mandarmani- It is another prominent beach destination you can visit during your trip to West Bengal. The serene waters, soothing atmosphere and brilliant accommodations attract visitors from all around. Other beaches- Some other popular beaches in West Bengal are Shankarpur beach, Bakkhali beach, Tajpur beach, etc.
West Bengal.What to eat in West Bengal?: Here are some examples of food in West Bengal that you should try during your trip: Mochar ghonto- This scrumptious dish creates a delicate blend of spice and sweetness. Its main ingredients include banana flower, potato, minutely crushed coconut and a certain mix of spices. Mangsho kosha- It is a unique type of mutton curry made with various spices. It offers a massive burst of flavour and leaves a delightful taste in your mouth. It is an iconic local food item, popularly served alongside luchi, steamed rice and parathas. Roshogolla- The world-famous Bengali sweet dish, Roshogolla, is a must-try food during your trip. This chenna-made ball-shaped spongy dessert remains soaked in a sugary syrup, creating the ultimate food for people with a sweet tooth. Chingri macher malaikari- For your tour to West Bengal, make sure to add this rich curry dish as a must-try item. It is prepared with Golda Chingri (large prawns), onion, ginger, red chilli, turmeric, coconut milk and a variety of other spices. Misti doi- Among the famous Bengali desserts, Misti Doi is a common household favourite. It is a type of sweetened fermented yoghurt, often sprinkled with roasted almonds, cashew nuts, raisins, etc., on top. Also, check out our simple West Bengal tourist guide on restaurants and foods.
West Bengal.Shopping in West Bengal: Here are some iconic things you can go for shopping in West Bengal as gifts or souvenirs: Sarees- West Bengal is a hub for saree shopping with its variety of regional designs, distinct materials and intricate detailing. The three main traditional saree types you can buy in West Bengal are Tant, Kantha and Balachuri. They are available across various stores in Kolkata. Darjeeling tea- The iconic Darjeeling tea is a must-buy item during your trip to West Bengal. Its signature aroma and taste have made it widely popular among tea lovers around the globe. It can be a bit costly but makes up for a great gift for yourself or family and friends. The best place to buy it is from shops in Darjeeling. Terracotta craftworks- Terracotta art pieces are cheap but beautiful craftworks you can buy as gifts or souvenirs in West Bengal. They are intricately designed pieces, often available in brown or black colour, capturing the regional art and history. Some popular terracotta items are horses, religious figurines, jewellery, etc., and can be found across Kolkata and Bankura. Kalighat paintings- The Kalighat paintings are made with one of the most iconic art styles of the region. These paintings mostly incorporate a religious theme and help hold the essence of Bengal’s iconic culture and artistic heritage. They are widely available near the Kalighat temple. There are tons of famous markets and shopping areas across West Bengal. Among them, the most prominent location for shopping in Kolkata, where you can find nearly anything. Some of the main markets in Kolkata are New Market, Gariahat Market, Hatibagan Market, etc.
West Bengal.Things to do in West Bengal: Here are some fun activities to do in West Bengal: Sunrise and sunset watching by the mountains- The majestic hill stations of West Bengal offer some breathtaking sunrise and sunset views. You can enjoy these views with your loved one and get some brilliant pictures to add to your West Bengal holiday collection. Experience the nightlife- The nightlife across major towns and cities in West Bengal is truly iconic. You can enjoy your evenings in the company of brilliant foods, drinks and live entertainment at popular restaurants, dance clubs and pubs. A key place with exuberant nightlife in West Bengal is Kolkata. Explore the vintage side of the region on foot- A huge part of India’s history is captured within the admirable architecture of various cities and towns in West Bengal, which you can explore on foot. Some iconic locations include North Kolkata, Cooch Behar, Malda, Bankura, etc. Catch a sports game- One of the best things to do in West Bengal is to watch a thrilling game of football or cricket in a massive stadium-filled atmosphere. You can find games across major stadiums in West Bengal, like Eden Gardens, Salt Lake Stadium, etc.
West Bengal.Adventurous things to do in West Bengal: Here are some adventurous things you can do in West Bengal: Snorkelling- If you are into the idea of snorkelling, you can participate in this fun activity at West Bengal’s Tajpur beach. It is a comparatively lower crowd area where you can enjoy this thrilling underwater activity in the serene waters. Paragliding- The adrenaline-pumping paragliding adventure activity is available at various places in West Bengal. Darjeeling and Kalimpong are the most popular choices due to their renowned beauty. Rafting- You can go river-rafting in the Teesta river for an exhilarating rafting experience in West Bengal. This is also a perfect group adventure activity you can do during your holiday trip. Trekking- It is one of the most popular adventure activities you can do in West Bengal. There are several mesmerising hiking trails and trekking destinations in the region, with the popular ones being the Sandakphu trek, Buxa-Jayanti trek, Darjeeling trek, and so on.
West Bengal.Interesting facts about West Bengal: Here are some interesting facts about West Bengal that you should know: The iconic Howrah Bridge doesn’t have a single bolt or nut and was built entirely by riveting. West Bengal’s capital Kolkata (Calcutta), was also the capital of British India before it was shifted to New Delhi. Kolkata is said to be the birthplace of Indian-Chinese cuisine in India. The oldest operating port in India is the Kolkata Port, founded in 1870 by the British East India Company. UNESCO declared Kolkata’s famous Durga Puja an “Intangible Cultural Heritage” by UNESCO. Darjeeling’s Ghum Railway Station is the highest railway station in India. The Indian Museum in Kolkata is the largest and oldest museum in the country.

Ottawa.Introduction: Ottawa holds significant importance in the history of Canada. It rose from a construction base where the Rideau Canal diverged from the Ottawa River. The history of Ottawa dates back to 1820 when this project was started. In the past, Ottawa was known as ‘Bytown’ and the new name only came into use in 1854. Ottawa Tour Packages No. of Days Price* East Coast Of Canada 6 Days / 5 Nights Rs. 174500 Ontario & French Canda- By Cosmos 8 Days / 7 Nights Rs. 92325 Best Of Eastern Canada- Trafalgar 9 Days / 8 Nights Rs. 157125 Canadian Delights 11 Days / 10Nights Rs. 314500 Scenic America 15 Days / 14 Nights Rs. 400500 Best of USA and Canada 21 Days / 20 Nights Rs. 487000 Ottawa was designed keeping holiday seekers in mind. It developed with time as the parliament buildings were constructed in 1865. Its rich cultural life bestows this city with many universities and several research institutes. Being Canada’s capital, the joy of travelling here is very unique and one of its kind. With so much to see and explore, Ottawa makes sure that your time will be consumed in the most joyful way. Ottawa Holiday packages take you to Parliament Hill. The splendour of the gorgeous Parliament buildings constructed with Victorian gothic sandstone will leave you spellbound. Rideau Canal is another historic attraction which was originally constructed between 1826 and 1832 to create a secure supply and communications route between Montreal, Québec, and Kingston, Ontario. It's also a UNESCO World Heritage site and one of the city's most beloved features. You can also explore ByWard Market, Canadian War museum and National Gallery of Canada among the prime highlights of Ottawa Tourism. Top places to visit in Ottawa Parliament Hill is the most popular attraction of Ottawa. Its historic significance and architectural beauty makes a favourite part of Ottawa packages. The National Gallery of Canada is an architectural masterpiece constructed with prism like glass towers. This beautiful building has several galleries which display aboriginal art, Canadian art ranging from religious works to the Group of Seven, European Impressionism, and temporary exhibitions. Canadian museum of nature is another favourite destination included in all the Ottawa Packages. Those who love to know more about Dinosaurs must visit this beautiful place. Notre Dame Basilica is a beautifully constructed catholic place of worship. Some of the prime highlights of this building are interior mahogany carvings by Philippe Parizeau and figures of the four evangelists, prophets, and apostles by Louis-Philippe Hébert. Experience the best of Ottawa Packages with Thomas Cook Thomas Cook makes sure that your Ottawa holiday packages show you the best of this city in the most relaxing way possible. Not only Ottawa, but Thomas Cook provides a plethora of tour packages for international holidays for major destinations around the world. faq.question: How many days are ideal for an Ayodhya trip?
faq.answer: The type of experience you're seeking will determine how many days you need. A one to two-day tour of Ayodhya is sufficient for a brief spiritual visit, covering the Ram Janmabhoomi Mandir, Hanuman Garhi, and a serene evening by the Saryu River. However, allow three to four days if you want to take your time, see more temples, and enjoy the aarti. A 5- to 6-day Ayodhya tour package is ideal for individuals who wish to get further into the local culture and sacred towns, particularly when paired with Prayagraj or Varanasi.

Ayodhya.Introduction: Ayodhya immediately springs to mind when you consider a journey that combines spirituality, history, and cultural diversity. Often referred to as the centre of the Ramayana, this holy city is situated along the placid banks of the Sarayu River and is regarded as the birthplace of Lord Rama. Ayodhya tour packages make it simple for you to experience the city's soul most seamlessly if you've been thinking about taking a spiritual vacation. The magnificent Shree Ram Janmabhoomi Mandir is undoubtedly the highlight of every trip. By choosing an Ayodhya Ram Mandir tour package, you can see this architectural wonder in all its splendour and have time to see other historic temples and ghats. Many pilgrims like to combine their pilgrimage with a trip to Varanasi, and if you wish to experience the mystical atmosphere of both towns simultaneously, a Kashi Ayodhya tour package is ideal.

An Ayodhya vacation package gives you the ideal balance of comfort, spirituality, and local flavour, whether you are travelling alone, with family, or in a group. With improved connections, modern infrastructure, and expanding pilgrim amenities, an Ayodhya journey is now more comfortable than ever. Immersion in centuries of faith, culture, and devotion is more important than merely going to a sacred location. Now is the ideal time to go on this incredible journey if you've been waiting for the appropriate opportunity.
Ayodhya.Why Choose Ayodhya for Your Next Holiday: Ayodhya is a place that will genuinely stick with you if you've been considering a vacation that involves more than just sightseeing. Allow me to explain why you should plan your next vacation to this historic city. An Unmatched Spiritual Retreat Ayodhya is more than just a city; it is the hallowed birthplace of Lord Rama. You can sense the merging of faith and history as you stroll through its alleyways. Discover ancient temples, tranquil ghats on the Sarayu River, and Ramayana tales that come to life with each step on an Ayodhya trip. Selecting from a variety of Ayodhya tour packages ensures that you travel comfortably and don't miss any of the important religious sites. A Trip Outside of Ayodhya Many tourists enjoy visiting Varanasi as part of their trip. If you wish to experience the energy of both cities, Varnasi with its captivating Ganga aarti and Ayodhya with its ageless devotion, a Kashi Ayodhya tour package is ideal. When combined, they produce a whole and profound experience. The Magnificent Experience at Ram Mandir The city's focal point today is the newly opened Shree Ram Janmabhoomi Mandir. Choosing an Ayodhya Ram Mandir tour package guarantees that you will have ample time to appreciate the temple's magnificence and heavenly ambience. The temple has gained international recognition, making Ayodhya one of India's most popular religious destinations. Cultural Events and Festivals That Illuminate the City You will witness the city at its most enchanting if you schedule your trip to Ayodhya during holidays like Ram Navami or Diwali's Deepotsav. The mood is spectacular, cultural acts fill the air, and millions of diyas light up the night sky. Combining Urban Comforts with Tradition The way the city is changing is what really makes an Ayodhya package alluring. Your Ayodhya vacation package is as pleasant as it is spiritual thanks to the new international airport, the renovated railway station, and the contemporary hotels. The convenience of contemporary amenities combined with the allure of age-old customs gives you the best of both worlds.
Ayodhya.Planning Your Ayodhya Trip: Essential In: One of the most rewarding trips you will ever do is organising your trip to Ayodhya. Here is all the information you need to know before purchasing your Ayodhya tour packages in order to make it genuinely unforgettable.
Ayodhya.Best Time to Visit Ayodhya: The scheduling of your Ayodhya trip is crucial if you want it to be both pleasant and meaningful. Winter, which runs from October to March, is the ideal season because of the cold, pleasant weather, which is ideal for walks along the Sarayu ghats and temple visits. Your Ayodhya tour packages will be even more memorable during festivals like Ram Navami and Diwali's Deepotsav, which fill the city with lights, chants, and lavish celebrations. The monsoon season brings rain and humidity, while the summer months of April through June can be quite hot. Therefore, winter is definitely the best time to visit in Ayodhya if you want the best experience with your Kashi Ayodhya tour package.
Ayodhya.How to Reach Ayodhya?: It's now easier than ever to get to Ayodhya, which makes your vacation package there hassle-free and seamless. The Maharishi Valmiki International Airport has direct flights to Delhi, Mumbai, and Kolkata if you'd rather fly. As an alternative, you can land at Chaudhary Charan Singh Airport in Lucknow and take a picturesque three-hour journey to Ayodhya. Local rickshaws are constantly accessible outside the station, and Ayodhya Dham Junction offers great train connectivity to major Indian towns. With highways connecting Ayodhya to Varanasi, Prayagraj, and Lucknow, road travel is similarly convenient. Road and train alternatives make it simple to see both locations in a one journey if you're choosing a Kashi Ayodhya travel package. Your Ayodhya tour packages provide a stress-free, connected start whichever of the options you select.
Ayodhya.Accommodation & Food Options: There are many different lodging alternatives to fit your budget when you reserve an Ayodhya Ram Mandir tour package or any other Ayodhya package. The city is prepared to welcome visitors from all over the world with everything from cosy guesthouses and mid-range hotels to brand-new luxury establishments. Darshan and visiting are made easy by the abundance of hotels close to the Ram Mandir and other temples. Ayodhya's cuisine is incredibly diverse, flavourful, and entirely vegetarian. Your travel package to Ayodhya gives you a genuine flavour of the local way of life, whether it's through spicy poori-sabzi, crunchy kachori, or zesty street chaat. Remember to sample the desserts. Ayodhya is well-known for its peda and laddoos, which are ideal for taking home.
Ayodhya.Travel Permits & Local Regulations: Since Indian visitors do not require specific licenses, travelling with an Ayodhya vacation package is typically straightforward. However, stringent security measures are in place when visiting the Ram Mandir and other significant sites. Although free locker facilities are available nearby, cell phones, backpacks, and electrical devices are not permitted on temple property. Ayodhya is a very religious city; thus, always wear modest clothing. Your operator will help you navigate local laws if you have reserved a tour package to Ayodhya Ram Mandir or Kashi Ayodhya, guaranteeing a seamless and traditional Ayodhya experience.
Ayodhya.Curated Ayodhya tour packages by Thomas: Thomas Cook has created some amazing Ayodhya tour packages specifically for you if you've been dreaming of exploring the holy streets of Ayodhya, seeing the recently constructed Ram Mandir, and taking in the centuries-old devotion. These packages combine the best aspects of comfort, culture, and spirituality, saving you the trouble of organising every little thing yourself. What's very unique is that a lot of itineraries combine Ayodhya with other sacred locations, including Varanasi and Prayagraj, to provide a holistic spiritual experience. You can find something that fits your time and goals, whether you're looking for a quick Ayodhya trip package or a longer Kashi Ayodhya tour package. Thomas Cook guarantees a seamless, well-guided, and unforgettable trip, whether you're searching for a family-friendly or a highly spiritual Ayodhya Ram Mandir tour package, or even a solo getaway.
Ayodhya.Diverse Package Categories: Selecting a package that fits your travel preferences is made simple with Thomas Cook. A deeper look at the various Ayodhya tour package options is provided below: Spirituality Packages: The Anant Kashi Ayodhya Yatra, which combines Varanasi and Ayodhya, or the Ramayan Yatra, which leads you to significant locations connected to Lord Rama's life, are good choices if devotion and darshan are your main objectives. If you want an immersive trip package to Ayodhya Ram Mandir, these are ideal. Individual & Tailored Packages: Thomas Cook provides personalised choices if you're travelling alone or want a more flexible itinerary. These allow you to pick guided excursions, go at your own speed, or even prolong your stay with an additional Ayodhya package. Family packages: Are you and your loved ones planning a trip? Important sites, including Hanuman Garhi, Kanak Bhawan, and the nightly aarti at Saryu Ghat, are included in family-friendly Ayodhya vacation packages. These itineraries are made to strike a mix between comfort and worthwhile activities so that parents, grandparents, and kids can all have fun together. Honeymoon and Couples packages: Some couples find satisfaction in starting their married life with blessings at the Ram Mandir, despite the fact that Ayodhya is recognised as a spiritual city. This is made possible by special packages designed for couples, which also include peaceful activities like a boat ride along the Sarayu in the evening. Combined Pilgrimage Packages: One of the most popular options is the Kashi Ayodhya travel package. It enables you to experience both the holiness of Ayodhya and the energy of Varanasi's ghats in a single journey. To give your journey even more depth, some also include Prayagraj, where you can visit the Triveni Sangam.
Ayodhya.Top Attractions & Experiences in Ayodhya: You are entering a city where mythology, devotion, and culture permeate every nook and cranny as you arrange your tour to Ayodhya. Here are the must-see sights and activities that will make your trip unforgettable, regardless of whether you select a Kashi Ayodhya tour package or a comprehensive Ayodhya Ram Mandir tour package. Ram Mandir and Ram Janmabhoomi This is the centrepiece of every travel package to Ayodhya. The holy birthplace of Lord Rama is the site of the recently opened Shree Ram Mandir. It is the first stop on every tour of Ayodhya, and the building is stunning, the atmosphere profoundly spiritual. Kanak Bhawan Golden idols of Rama and Sita adorn Kanak Bhawan, which is said to have been given to Goddess Sita by Queen Kaikeyi upon her marriage. This temple offers you an insight into the city's regal heritage if you're travelling to Ayodhya on a package. Hanuman Garhi Pilgrims typically visit Hanuman Garhi before visiting the Ram Mandir. Dedicated to Lord Hanuman, this temple dates back to the tenth century and is perched on a hill overlooking the city. It is a location where folklore and faith coexist harmoniously. Ram Ki Paidi and Saryu Ghats The Sarayu River's ghats are ideal for taking a holy dip or seeing the evening aarti. Millions of diyas illuminate the banks during Deepotsav, making it a mystical highlight of any itinerary for an Ayodhya travel package. Temple of Nageshwarnath Kush, the son of Rama, is credited with constructing this ancient temple, which is dedicated to Lord Shiva. It is a fantastic addition to any tour package of Ayodhya Ram Mandir because of its architecture and mythology. Guptar Ghat Guptar Ghat is serene, spiritual, and a must-visit for anyone seeking a peaceful place to think during their Ayodhya vacation package. It is thought to be the location where Lord Rama made his last voyage to Vaikuntha. Deepotsav and Ramlila's Cultural Experiences Experience Ayodhya's customs beyond its temples. Shop for religious artefacts, take in the Ramlila performances, or, if you're lucky, attend the Deepotsav celebration. These experiences greatly enhance the memory of your Kashi Ayodhya trip package. Lata Mangeshkar Chowk This area is a contemporary monument with a large veena installation honouring the renowned singer. It combines devotion with artistic expression, giving your Ayodhya trip a more cultural feel.
Ayodhya.Things to Do in Ayodhya Beyond Sightseei: Temple visits and darshan are typically the first things that spring to mind when you think of an Ayodhya tour. However, there is a lot more in Ayodhya for those who like to venture a bit off the beaten path. You can find experiences that go beyond temple hopping and allow you to interact with the city in distinctive ways with carefully thought-out Ayodhya tour packages, including combined alternatives like the Kashi Ayodhya trip package. These are eight things you must not overlook: Watch the Evening Aarti: Instead of joining the enormous crowd at Ram Ki Paidi, observe the aarti from the water. You can see thousands of diyas lighting up the river in a panoramic perspective from a boat. This type of recollection is what really sets your Ayodhya package apart. Experience the Saryu by Boat: Taking a boat ride on the revered Saryu River is among the most tranquil activities you can engage in. Try visiting during twilight; the water's reflection of the lamps' golden hue creates a unique experience. Ask if this can be included in the Ayodhya vacation package you have reserved. Take Part in Heritage Walking Tour: You can explore lanes where legends of poets, kings, and saints still abound on guided heritage walks. They frequently highlight lesser-known temples and shrines, adding richness to your Ayodhya visit. Cycle or Take a Walk Along the Ghats: Explore Ayodhya's ghats on foot or by renting a bicycle if you enjoy leisurely touring. You will encounter peaceful nooks where residents sit in contemplation, wash their clothes, or pray. It's a lovely chance to see everyday living outside of the popular tourist destinations. Participate in Parikrama: Participate in one of Ayodhya's parikramas with the devotees if you're up for a spiritual adventure. Although the Panchkoshi route is well-travelled, the Chaturdashkoshi is an experience of a lifetime for those who are truly daring. This ends up being the high point of many people's Ayodhya Ram Mandir tour packages. Discover the Heritage of Faizabad: Gems like Gulab Bari and the Tomb of Bahu Begum can be found in Faizabad, which is only 8 miles distant. Even if your Ayodhya tour package doesn't include a brief stop here, it's still worth making the detour. See a Ram Lila Performance: Do not miss a live Ram Lila if you are there during the Sharad Navratras. The performances here are steeped in history and give your Ayodhya package a touch of culture. Shop and Enjoy Local Culture: Spend the last part of the day among the busy bazaars. Get spiritual literature and brass diyas, and sample the rich, sweet, and mouthwatering local peda. Long after your vacation is over, it serves as a comforting reminder of Ayodhya's allure.
Ayodhya.Ayodhya Trip Cost & Budget Planning: Organising a tour to Ayodhya involves more than simply seeing temples; it also entails making the trip comfortable without going over budget. Thomas Cook offers Ayodhya packages that start at about ₹29,500 per person, especially when you include neighbouring holy places like Varanasi and Prayagraj. For instance, Thomas Cook's 6-day Kashi Ayodhya travel package starts at ₹38,900, while their more comprehensive Ayodhya–Prayagraj itinerary starts at ₹29,500. Your selection of lodging, transportation, inclusions, and, of course, the length of your stay all affect the total cost. Everything from lodging to guided sightseeing is covered if you choose a pre-made Ayodhya Ram Mandir tour package, which makes it simpler but a little more expensive. However, when you travel alone, you have more freedom to adjust your spending plan. If you make careful plans, you can afford a basic two- to three-day tour package to Ayodhya. Here are some budgeting tips to keep in mind: Options for Accommodations Three-star hotels in the middle range: ₹1,500 to ₹3,000 per night Dorms and guesthouses on a budget: ₹350 to ₹500 per night Luxurious hotels and resorts: more than ₹4,000 per night Transport Spending limit: ₹300–₹600 (bus/train) Moderate: ₹1,000 to ₹5,000 (low-cost flights to Lucknow or AC trains) Luxury: over ₹10,000 (private shuttles and first-class flights) Costs of Food Restaurants in the middle range: ₹300 to ₹500 daily Local restaurants and street food: ₹100 to ₹150 daily Hotel meals or fine dining: ₹1,000 to ₹2,000 daily Costs of Sightseeing Spend between ₹50 and ₹100 on a boat ride on the Saryu River. The majority of temples offer free admission. Cultural performances or local guides: ₹500–₹1,000 Money-Saving tips Due to the increasing demand for Ayodhya, book your hotels and flights in advance. To avoid crowds and expensive prices, go during the week or during the off-season. Use cars and bicycle rickshaws for affordable local transportation. For a more economical route, fly to Lucknow and take a taxi to Ayodhya. Hotel rates might be reduced by staying outside of the city centre.
Ayodhya.Essential Tips for a Smooth Ayodhya Trip: Your trip will be much more comfortable and stress-free if you keep a few things in mind, whether you are travelling yourself or with a carefully planned Ayodhya tour package. First and foremost, make sure to organise your itinerary well in advance. You can save time and effort by knowing what you want to see at Ayodhya's many major monuments, which include the famous Ram Janmabhoomi Mandir, Hanuman Garhi, and Kanak Bhawan. To avoid missing out on important activities, make sure you balance the time spent in both locations while selecting a Kashi Ayodhya tour package. Here are some additional tips: Pack and dress well: The best options are light luggage, modest temple wear, and comfortable cotton clothing. Only bring necessities like ID, water, and simple medications. Since many things are prohibited inside religious complexes, stay away from valuables. Make reservations in advance: Particularly once the Ram Mandir opens, hotels and guesthouses fill up fast. Whether you're travelling alone or with a party, make sure you reserve your lodging well in advance. Time is of the essence: To avoid large crowds, go to Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Mandir early in the morning (about 7:00 AM). Verify whether "Sugam Darshan" is included in your Ayodhya Ram Mandir trip package for expedited entry. Staying hydrated is essential: Keep a water bottle with you at all times because summers can get very hot. Although digital payments are generally accepted: keep a small amount of cash on hand for local transactions and vehicles. Keep a proper ID with you at all times, as checkpoints may need it. Don't limit yourself to sightseeing: If your vacation package to Ayodhya falls during the holiday season, partake in a Ram Lila performance, visit local bazaars, and sample peda and other sweets. You should wear comfortable shoes because you will be walking a lot. Lightweight slip-ons are useful because most temples require you to leave your shoes outside.

To make your visit go more smoothly, many itineraries even include VIP darshan at temples with an Ayodhya Ram Mandir tour package. Making a reservation with Thomas Cook guarantees that you concentrate on devotion and exploration rather than practicalities, regardless of the length of your pilgrimage or your Ayodhya trip package.

Badrinath.Introduction: Think of spiritual places and, Badrinath would top the list. Enjoy relaxed trips to Badrinath with Badrinath Yatra. Situated in Chamoli district of Uttarakhand, Badrinath is one of the four Chardhams. This holy pilgrimage is one of the most sought after by most Hindus.
Badrinath.Most Popular Badrinath Trip Packages: Tour Packages NameDurationPriceDo Dham Yatra By Helicopter 3N3 Nights 4 DaysRs. ₹148 500 Char Dham Yatra By Helicopter5 Nights 6 DaysRs. ₹232 500Char Dham Yatra By Helicopter with Kedarnath Darshan and Maha Abhishek Puja 5 Nights 6 DaysRs. ₹238 500Char Dham Yatra By Helicopter With Kedarnath Stay And Rudrabhishek Puja5 Nights 6 DaysRs. ₹242 500 The best time to visit Badrinath is from May to November. May and June are generally extremely crowded as this is the peak pilgrimage season. Badrinath is well connected by road and railways. The nearest airport is the Dehradun airport, and the nearest railway station is Rishikesh railway station. Helicopter services are available from Dehradun and a few other places. While in Badrinath the one place everyone will visit is the Badrinath temple dedicated to Lord Vishnu and situated on the banks of the Alaknanda River. Take some time out to see the majestic snow- covered peak, Neelkanth which stands behind Badrinath. The local markets with handicrafts and local food are a colourful treat to the eyes and the soul. Places to Visit in Badrinath Significance More Details Badrinath Temple One of the prominent dhams among the Chardham, this temple is dedicated to Lord Vishnu and is an important place of pilgrimage. More Details About Badrinath Temple Charanpaduka Located 3kms above the Badrinath Temple, this place is known to have a footprint of Lord Vishnu More Details About Charanpaduka Bheem pul The Bheem Pul is a beautiful water body. Legend has it that Bheem threw a big rock to make way for Draupadi. It is also the place where the Saraswati river merges with the Alaknanda river. More Details About Bheem pul Panch Dharas and Pancha Shilas This place is known for it’s five beautifully flowing streams and five rocks. The Indira dhara is the most beautiful among them all. The five rocks are of mythological importance and are named Narad, Narsimh, Barah, Garur and Markandeya Shilas. These are found around the Tapt Kund. Panch Dharas and Pancha Shilas Ganesha Cave The Ganesha Cave is known to be the cave where Ved Vyasa dictated the Mahabharata. More Details About Ganesha Cave Sheshnetra This is another holy site and it is believed that Lord Vishnu took retreat under the snake Ananta shesha. The rocks there have imprints. More Details About Sheshnetra Badrinath is a destination that most people would like to visit at least once in their lifetime. Thomas Cook Badrinath Yatra allow travellers to have a relaxed travel without any worries. Badrinath package tours take care of every aspect of travel and allow travellers to experience the trip like never before. Badrinath is strategically located amidst the mountains and therefore the snow-capped mountains and the lush forests make the trip even more enjoyable.

Antarctica’s isolated terrain boasts rich species of wildlife, enticing travelers from across the globe.

Gangotri.Introduction: Are you ready to set out on a spiritual journey that will cleanse your soul and connect you to the divine? The Gangotri Yatra is one of the most revered pilgrimages in India, offering a peaceful escape to the majestic Himalayan peaks and the source of the holy River Ganges. With Thomas Cook, you can make this sacred journey a reality through our exclusive Gangotri Yatra packages that ensure a seamless and enriching experience.
Gangotri.Most Popular Gangotri Yatra Packages: Tour Packages NameDurationPrice Char Dham Yatra By Helicopter5 Nights 6 DaysRs. ₹232 500Char Dham Yatra By Helicopter with Kedarnath Darshan and Maha Abhishek Puja 5 Nights 6 DaysRs. ₹238 500Char Dham Yatra By Helicopter With Kedarnath Stay And Rudrabhishek Puja5 Nights 6 DaysRs. ₹242 500
Gangotri.Why Choose Gangotri Yatra Packages?: The Gangotri Yatra is not just a trip; it’s an experience that will stay with you for a lifetime. It takes you to the ancient Gangotri temple, dedicated to Goddess Ganga, located at an altitude of 3,042 meters in the Uttarkashi district of Uttarakhand. This sacred spot is where the Ganges, in its purest form, is believed to have descended to Earth. Whether you’re seeking spiritual fulfillment, a break from the everyday routine, or a journey that connects you to the rich heritage of India, Gangotri tours offer it all. Gangotri trip packages are designed to cater to every need of a traveler. From comfortable accommodation to experienced guides and easy transportation, you can rest assured that your spiritual tour will be hassle-free. Thomas Cook ensures that each trip is meticulously planned, allowing you to focus on what truly matters—your connection to the divine.
Gangotri.What’s Included in Our Gangotri Yatra Pa: When you book a Gangotri yatra package with Thomas Cook, you get more than just transportation and accommodation. Our Gangotri packages include: Comfortable Accommodation: Stay in well-equipped hotels and dharamshalas near the temple for a relaxed and peaceful experience. Experienced Guides: Knowledgeable and friendly guides who provide historical and spiritual context to enhance your journey. Smooth Travel Arrangements: We handle all aspects of your travel, including transportation from Delhi, Haridwar, or Dehradun, to Gangotri, ensuring a smooth and stress-free experience. Local Exploration: Discover nearby attractions like the Surya Kund and the Bhagirathi Shila, which are integral to the history and culture of the region.
Gangotri.How to Reach Gangotri?: One of the most common questions travelers have is, how to reach Gangotri? Thankfully, Thomas Cook makes the process simple. You can reach Gangotri by road, with buses and private vehicles available from major cities like Dehradun, Rishikesh, and Haridwar. For those coming from outside Uttarakhand, the nearest major airports are in Dehradun (Jolly Grant Airport), and the nearest railway station is in Rishikesh. Our Gangotri trip packages also offer comfortable transportation to and from the temple, ensuring that your journey is easy and stress-free.
Gangotri.Best Time to Visit Gangotri: The best time to visit Gangotri is between May and October when the weather is most favorable for a pilgrimage. The winter months (November to March) are harsh, with heavy snowfall, making the roads impassable. During the summer months, the weather is cool and pleasant, perfect for both sightseeing and spiritual activities. If you want to experience the Gangotri temple at its most vibrant, you can plan your visit during the Gangotri Ganga Dusshera Festival, celebrated every year in May-June. This is a wonderful time for those looking to experience the cultural vibrancy of the region, alongside their spiritual journey.
Gangotri.Things to Keep in Mind: Before you embark on your Gangotri Yatra, there are a few things to keep in mind: Weather Conditions: As the region is located in the mountains, weather conditions can change rapidly. Always carry appropriate clothing and essentials like sunscreen, hats, and comfortable shoes. Health Considerations: The high altitude of Gangotri can cause altitude sickness. Make sure you are in good health and consult your doctor before the trip. Local Culture and Customs: Respect the local customs, especially when visiting religious sites. Always follow the guidelines provided by your guide to ensure you have a smooth spiritual experience. Travel Documentation: Ensure that you carry valid identity documents like your ID card or passport, especially if you're traveling from outside India.

Our team of travel experts is dedicated to providing you with a hassle-free experience, allowing you to focus entirely on your spiritual exploration. From well-organized travel itineraries to tailored Gangotri trip packages, we ensure that your pilgrimage is enriching, safe, and spiritually fulfilling.

Serbia.Introduction: Europe is a continent that has some marvellous countries. They are an epic mix of culture, traditions, history and great geography. Serbia is one such country that might be small in size but everything else about them is larger than life. To experience the best of Serbia take a Serbia Tour Package. Serbia Tour Packages Serbia Tour Packages No. of Days Price* Balkan Special – Serbia (Flights not included) 4Nights/5 Days Rs.24,900 Serbia is a country that has some extreme weather and the best time to visit Serbia would be from mid -April to the end of June. The capital city of Belgrade is well connected to all the major cities in the world. Serbia, as a country is friendly, welcoming and lots of fun. Belgrade is a well- known party capital in Europe and rivals Berlin. Serbia is a cultural pot of wonders. This is also where ancient traditions and modern ideas co-exist. So when in Serbia don’t shy away from saying zdravo (hello) or živeli (cheers), which is even better. When in Belgrade, don’t miss the River Cruise. The River cruise will show you a side of the city away from the bustling crowds. Belgrade stands on the confluence of the Danube and Sava and is the only European city to do so. Soak into the history as you visit the prominent landmark of Beogradska Tvrdava. See the old city, Big War Island and many such places of interest that will transport you to a different era. Places to visit in Serbia Places to Visit Significance Beogradska Tvrdava This prominent fortress stands in the heart of Belgrade at the confluence of the Danube and Sava rivers. The fort is a witness to Roman, Byzantine, Ottoman, Serbian and Austrian empires. This place has history written all over it. River Cruise The River Cruise is an interesting way of seeing the city with a different perspective away from the crowds. Fruska Gora Home to the 17 functioning monasteries of the Serbian Orthodox Church. This place is an important part of the Serbian history. Sremski Karlovci This museum town has some interesting things to see. The Cathedral of Saint Nicholas. This is also where ‘Wineball’ is celebrated every year. The place is well known for its wineries which make wine from a variety of grapes. Petrovaradin This is the fortress built by the Austrians. The fort hosts the famous EXIT festival. Princess Ljubica's Residence Have a glimpse into the 19th century life in Serbia here. BeerGrade Pub Crawl Tour If you love beer, take this tour. If you don’t still take this tour and drop by at the taverns and listen to the good old Serbian tales. Dorcol District Let your hair down and party like there is no tomorrow at the clubs and pubs here. Zemun Take the Zemun Tour. The tour begins from Avijaticarski Trg and shows you around the place. There is a monument built for the World War II heroes. The oldest elementary school in Serbia is also here. Buvljak at New Belgrade The biggest flea market you will see. Pamper yourself to some retail therapy. There is a lot to see in Serbia and Belgrade alone. Experience the best of Belgrade with Serbia Tour Packages from Thomas Cook. Book a package and be rest assured of a great trip with memories to last a lifetime.

Madhya Pradesh.Introduction: Madhya Pradesh is the reason you love to travel. That faraway-from-home feeling, with the convenience of actually being close enough. For all you have to do is a journey to the heart of India, to discover a land so beautiful, diverse, majestic and blessed with nature, that it will leave your wanderlust truly satiated. Little wonder that in recent years, Madhya Pradesh tour packages have become hugely popular, for families, friends, couples and soloists alike. Madhya Pradesh trips are ready-made for you to plan an impromptu escapade, or they can be tailor-made for an advanced departure. Whichever path you select, they both lead you to this spectacular destination that’s got everything you want and then some. Discover Madhya Pradesh tourism packages that are imagined, drafted and curated by Thomas Cook’s experienced world wanderers and travel experts, in collaboration with the most reliable and prominent logistics, lodging, food and activity partners. Ensuring all your needs and wants are well taken care of so that you can fully immerse yourself in the marvels of MP tourism, and go home feeling refreshed and recharged for life ahead. For an in-depth view of what to expect and what’s on offer, check out our packages. Where you can get an in-depth view on the things to do in Madhya Pradesh, best time to visit Madhya Pradesh and what Places to visit in Madhya Pradesh, amongst other valuable information to book the most suitable Madhya Pradesh tour package for you.
Madhya Pradesh.Most Popular Madhya Pradesh Tour Package: Tour Packages NameDurationPriceMalwa Jyotirling Darshan Yatra4 Nights 5 DaysRs. ₹37 900 Gujarat And Madhya Pradesh - Char Jyotirlinga Tour7 Nights 8 DaysRs. ₹46 600Jyotirlingas Darshan - Mahakaleshwar Omkareshwar Darshan2 Nights 3 DaysRs. ₹23 700Natures Basket - Bhopal Pachmarhi4 Nights 5 DaysRs. ₹31 600Narmada Jyotirlinga Darshan Yatra 4 Nights 5 DaysRs. ₹32 400
Madhya Pradesh.Highlights of MP Tour Packages: Madhya Pradesh is where much of India’s diversity collides. The state sits at the heart of the country, sharing its landlocked borders with Uttar Pradesh, Chhattisgarh, Maharashtra, Gujarat and Rajasthan. Modern-day Madhya Pradesh is a place with a fascinating history that can be traced back to genuinely archaic times. Indeed, many relics have been discovered here dating 1500 BC! Fast-forwarding through the centuries, Madhya Pradesh witnessed many an empire rise and fall. Each building their palaces, monuments, towns and sacred sites along the way. See them all or just a select few, with Madhya Pradesh tourism packages, from the Bhimbetka rock shelters to the temples of Khajuraho and Gwalior Fort. But a glimpse of this rich history and culture is not all that Madhya Pradesh tourism is famous for. The state is also home to abundant and flourishing natural heritage. The kind found only in a handful of places around the world. This includes six major wildlife reserves, including twenty-five sanctuaries and the magnificent Vindhya and Satpura mountain ranges, that serve as home to lush floral and countless endemic as well as migratory species. And yes, this includes the most sought after animal of all — the enigmatic tiger. Wildlife or safari Madhya Pradesh tours are your best bet to witness tigers in the wild. The fun doesn’t stop there. Madhya Pradesh has vibrant festivities and celebrations, as well as a tribal culture that defies the modern age. Sample them on your MP tour packages to partake in their colourful customs and traditions. Madhya Pradesh tourism packages are available from all major cities and towns across India comprising of Mumbai, Hyderabad, Kolkata and Cochin. This has been curated for your ease and convenience, by our travel experts. Make your next holiday, your best holiday, only with Thomas Cook.
Madhya Pradesh.How to reach Madhya Pradesh: Madhya Pradesh’s central location and the fact that it is a vibrant tourism hub, means there is great connectivity to and from its cities. Reaching MP is more convenient that you think. You can expect to travel for your Madhya Pradesh holiday via your preferred mode of transport — air, road or rail. Reaching MP by Air If you choose to fly into Madhya Pradesh, you can land at any one of five airports — Indore, Bhop-al, Gwalior, Khajuraho and Jabalpur. Find flights easily from all major Indian cities like Mumbai, Delhi, Kolkata, Kochi, Bangalore amongst others, run by all the popular national airlines as well as some international carriers. The airports in Madhya Pradesh also welcome international flights from hubs like Dubai, Singapore and Bangkok. Reaching MP by Rail With its strategic location, Madhya Pradesh is rightly a major railway junction, with trains coming in from all parts of the country. Of the main stations across the state, Bhopal Railway Station of-fers the most connectivity, serving as a key stop for popular trains like Rajdhani Express and Sha-tabdi Express. Some of the other stations include Gwalior, Indore, Jabalpur, Ratlam, Ujjain, Bilas-pur and Bina. Railway journeys to and from your Madhya Pradesh holiday are effortless and com-fortable, especially with the right travel partners making all your arrangements. Reaching MP by Road Many national highways converge in Madhya Pradesh, so if you are looking for an epic road trip, you’ve found one! You can drive down from any of the neighboring states, or even from beyond. If you don’t wish to drive yourself, private cars and taxis for available for long-distance hire from all major Indian cities. Meanwhile, many public and private bus services ply to and from Madhya Pradesh cities and towns, ranging from deluxe to ultra-luxurious. Travelling within Madhya Pradesh Once you have arrived into Madhya Pradesh, moving around is relatively straightforward. For really long journeys, domestic flights are most convenient and time-saving. For shorter distances, road journeys are most common. That way, you get to see the places in between the destinations, the real Madhya Pradesh tourism. Indore to Mandu, Bhopal to Pachmarhi and Orchha to Khajuraho are top rated road trips within the state. While buses and taxis are available too, it is best to book them in advance as part of your Madhya Pradesh tour package.
Madhya Pradesh.Best time to visit Madhya Pradesh: For those wondering what’s the best time to visit Madhya Pradesh, the answer depends on the kind of MP tour packages you are looking for, as well as the annual seasons. The state boasts a mixed topography, meaning different temperatures can be seen at varying altitudes. However, the general seasons are summer, monsoon and winter. Generally, the rainy and cold months are considered the best time for Madhya Pradesh holidays. This is because summer months from March to May, can see temperatures soar over a blistering 40°C. So unless you are planning a Madhya Pradesh tour to places like Pachmarhi Hill Station or Kanha Wildlife Sanctuary, that have their own cool micro-climate, it is best to avoid a summer Madhya Pradesh holiday. Further along the year, the rain gods appear. Making June to September a lovely time to visit Madhya Pradesh and enjoy the lush scenery, safari parks, natural wonders and a bit of sightseeing too. From October to March, temperatures in Madhya Pradesh can drop as low as 2°C. Still, it is the ideal time to book a MP tour packages for a complete experience of all that the state has to offer. Wear your favourite woollens and venture out on heritage sightseeing tours, sacred visits, wildlife safaris, adventure activities, shopping and more.
Madhya Pradesh.Places to visit in Madhya Pradesh: Kanha Tiger Reserve – Must visit on your Madhya Pradesh Tour Only on a MP tour package can you visit the very jungles that inspired The Jungle Book. Find your inner Mowgli and experience the thrill of being surrounded by wild animals, in their glorious natural habitat. In the case of Kanha National Park, that means hectares of forest in the Maikal Range that are home to creatures big and small, including the Bengal tiger, panther, jackal and native Barasingha deer, and hundreds of flora species too. Jungle lodge stays and open-top safari rides are the best experiences when visiting the national parks of Kanha Tiger Reserve, with all bookings taken care of in advance, when you choose to visit with a Thomas Cook Madhya Pradesh tour package. Gwalior Fort – A place to watch out for on your trip to Madhya Pradesh A significant architectural and cultural highlight of Madhya Pradesh holidays, is the magnificent Gwalior Fort. Built by Raja Man Singh Tomar of the Tomar Dynasty in the medieval era, the fort is a unique spectacle, in a city that boasts a tremendous royal lineage. A large, imposing structure, adorning exotic carvings, the Gwalior Fort sees a blend of Indian and Chinese architectural styles and motifs. As you follow the meandering pathway to the top of the hill where it is firmly perched, you can see the one of a kind rock carvings of the Jain Theerthankaras. Once up close, feast your eyes on the breathtaking dragon-carved pillars, among other native regional motifs. Dhuandhar Waterfalls – A major attraction on your MP trip Make your way to Jabalpur for one of Madhya Pradesh tourism’s breathtaking natural wonders, the Dhuandhar Waterfalls. The name describes these magnificent falls perfectly, as they plunge 30 metres below, creating a powerful misty, smoke-like spray that fills the air. Even kilometres away, its roar can be heard. What makes this incredible sight even more mesmerising, is the fact that it is located on marble rocks of the Narmada River, a captivating natural spectacle in itself. Of course it’s one of the most visited spots of Madhya Pradesh tourism! Where you can, not only witness the falls from special viewing stations, but also go on a relaxing sunset boat ride, an exciting cable car ride or peaceful picnic by the riverside. Rajwada Palace – A mist visit historic palace in Madhya Pradesh In a long line of historic royal landmarks dotting Madhya Pradesh, Indore’s Rajwada Palace is one you shouldn’t miss. Built by the Holkar Dynasty over 200 years ago, is this marvellous wood and stone structure that sits right in the centre of the city, as an eternal symbol of its grand heritage. Influences of Maratha, Mughal and European architectural styles visible across its now-restored facade. On your visit, walk through the grand entrance archway before making your way to the courtyard, gardens and accessible halls. On certain days, there is an evening light and sound show performed at the palace, that is an enjoyable cultural experience for all ages. Bhimbetka Rock Shelters – Do include on your Madhya Pradesh tour While Madhya Pradesh tourism packages promise plenty of time-travelling, did you know that at one of its must-visit spots, you can go as far back as 30,000 years ago! At the Bhimbetka Rock Shelters, an archaeological site that dates back to prehistoric times — to the stone age. Discover over 500 rock shelters, many of which have cave paintings too. A truly fascinating glimpse of the story of humankind, located right here in the centre of India. From there, you can also visit the noteworthy historic towns of Bhojpur and Hoshangabad. All Madhya Pradesh tours that stop at Bhopal give you the opportunity to make a day trip to Bhimbetka.
Madhya Pradesh.Things to do in Madhya Pradesh: Madhya Pradesh is a hub of adventure and spirituality. So, on your Madhya Pradesh trip, there's plenty of things to do and explore. Here are some exciting activities and sights that you won't want to miss. Go on an adventurous safari on your Madhya Pradesh trip Wildlife is an inherent part of Madhya Pradesh tourism. So you would be remiss not to go on a safari at one of its many exotic sanctuaries, national parks and reserves. An experience that brings you closer to one another, while giving you a close-up view of beautiful wild animals, including tigers, deer and panthers amongst countless others. Madhya Pradesh’s many jungles are also a top destination for avid birders. Some of the famous national parks to visit on your MP tour packages include Kanha National Park, Panna National Park, Pench National Park, Bandhavgarh Reserve and Satpura Wildlife Park. On your next Madhya Pradesh tour, Trek to Doopgarh A magical trek up the highest peak of the Satpura Mountains is an experience to remember for a lifetime. Doopgarh is found in the pristine Pachmarhi Biosphere Reserve. Make it in time for sunrise or sunset (for the extra adventurous), to catch stunning vistas of the colourful sky above and the lush valleys below. There is a fairly established route, so even amateurs are welcome. During the Madhya Pradesh tour, you can catch breathtaking views of the sunrise or sunset, and be surrounded by gushing waterfalls and natural beauty. Along the way, you’ll be treated to gushing waterfalls and natural bliss. For Madhya Pradesh tour packages that are booked between October to February, this is an experience not to be missed. Sail through scenic marble rocks in MP The Narmada River flows through Madhya Pradesh, holding many exquisite sights along its waters. The most picturesque of all, are the marble rocks. These prehistoric, towering white marble rocks have been carved into by the flowing river, creating an enchanting scenery akin to a beautiful painting. Get on one of the boat rides on site, to witness the natural phenomenon with stunning 360 degree views. An experience that is romantic for two, and thrilling for groups. Take a spiritual tour of the temples across Madhya Pradesh Madhya Pradesh is a temple-hopping paradise, with ancient, medieval and modern sacred places of worship found across the land. Many of them double as architectural and heritage sites too, such as the famed Khajuraho Temples. Madhya Pradesh has entire temple towns, like Ujjain, Orc-ha, Maheshwar and Omkareshwar, where many believers and spiritual seekers make the pilgrimage for divine blessings. Popular temples to visit include the Bhojeshwar Temple, Teli Ka Mandir at the Gwalior Fort, and the Sun Temple. Experience a thrilling cable car ride The Dhuandhar Ropeway in Madhya Pradesh is the absolute adrenaline-pumping way to see the Dhuandhar Waterfalls powerfully cascading and the shimmering Bhedaghat marble rocks, with a sensational bird’s eye view. While it doesn’t function in the monsoons, if your Madhya Pradesh holiday is at any other time of the year, visit the ropeway on the Narmada for a heart-racing, un-forgettable time.

From historical landmarks to natural wonders, there are countless things to do in Egypt that cater to all interests. Whether you're marvelling at the Sphinx, sailing down the Nile, or exploring the vibrant souks, Egypt promises a truly unique and unforgettable experience. Egypt travel is a journey through time, offering glimpses of a civilisation that has fascinated the world for centuries. An Egypt trip is not complete without visiting the famous tourist places in Egypt, such as the Sphinx, the temples of Luxor and Karnak, and the Valley of the Kings. These Egypt tourist attractions are sure to leave you in awe of their grandeur and historical significance. Egypt.Best Time to Visit Egypt: The best time to visit Egypt is during the cooler months, from October to April. This period is ideal for Egypt tourism as the temperatures are pleasant, ranging from 15°C to 25°C. It's perfect for exploring the best tourist places in Egypt, such as the pyramids of Giza, the temples of Luxor, and the bustling streets of Cairo. Many Egypt tour packages are designed to take advantage of the mild weather, offering extensive itineraries that cover the top places to visit in Egypt. Season Months Climate Description Winter December - February Cool and comfortable (15°C - 25°C) Peak tourist season; perfect for sightseeing without extreme heat. Egypt travel packages include visits to iconic landmarks, desert safaris, and Nile cruises. Spring March - April Warm and pleasant Great for outdoor activities as the weather is warm but still comfortable. Suitable for a mix of cultural experiences and beach relaxation. Summer May - September Hot (especially in the south) Off-peak season due to intense heat; best time to visit coastal areas where temperatures are more moderate. Lower tourist numbers for a more intimate experience. Autumn October - November Mild temperatures Beginning of the cooler season; thinner crowds compared to winter. Ideal for exploring cities and their rich culture and history.
Egypt.How to Reach Egypt: Planning a tour to Egypt from India is an exciting endeavour, and understanding the various modes of transportation available will ensure a smooth journey. Here’s a detailed guide on how to reach Egypt ,covering different options to help you choose the best method suited to your Egypt travel plans. Mode of Transportation Description Pros Cons By Air The most convenient and popular way to reach Egypt from India. Direct and connecting flights available from major Indian cities (Delhi, Mumbai, Bangalore, Chennai) to Cairo. Fast (6-7 hours for direct flights), frequent flights, good connectivity with major airlines. Can be expensive if not booked in advance. By Road and Rail Not recommended due to the geographical distance and the need to cross multiple international borders. None Highly challenging, time-consuming, complex visa and border crossing requirements. By Sea Less common but offers a unique experience; cruise liners operate from Indian ports like Mumbai to Egyptian ports such as Alexandria or Port Said. Leisurely travel, luxury amenities on board, scenic views. Long journey duration (several days), more expensive than flying, limited schedules.
Egypt.Best Places to Visit in Egypt: Egypt, a land of timeless allure, offers an abundance of destinations that captivate the imagination and evoke a sense of wonder. Here are the most popular and must-visit destinations that should be included in any Egypt tour packages, ensuring an unforgettable journey through history and culture. Cairo: The bustling capital city, Cairo, is often the starting point for most Egypt travel packages. Home to the iconic pyramids of Giza and the enigmatic Sphinx, it’s one of the top places to visit in Egypt. Don’t miss the Egyptian Museum, housing an extensive display of ancient artefacts, including the treasures of Tutankhamun. Khan El Khalili Bazaar, with its vibrant markets, offers a taste of local life and culture. Aswan: Aswan , with its tranquil Nile views and rich history, is a key destination in many Egypt holiday packages. The Philae Temple, dedicated to the goddess Isis, and the Unfinished Obelisk are among the best places to visit in Egypt. A visit to the Nubian villages offers a glimpse into the unique culture and traditions of the Nubian people. Luxor: Luxor, often referred to as the world’s greatest open-air museum, is a must-visit on any Egypt itinerary. The city is home to some of the best places in Egypt, including the Karnak and Luxor Temples. The Valley of the Kings, where pharaohs were laid to rest, is another highlight. Egypt trip packages often include a Nile cruise from Luxor to Aswan, offering breathtaking views of ancient temples along the way. Sharm El Sheikh: For those seeking sun and sea, Sharm El Sheikh is a top choice. Renowned for its stunning coral reefs and crystal-clear waters, it’s one of the most famous tourist places in Egypt for diving and snorkelling. Ras Mohammed National Park is a must-visit for nature enthusiasts. The vibrant nightlife and luxurious resorts make it a perfect spot for relaxation. Abu Simbel: Abu Simbel , with its colossal statues and impressive temples, is a highlight of any tour to Egypt. The temples of Ramses II are among the top places to visit in Egypt, showcasing incredible ancient engineering and artistry. Siwa Oasis: For a unique and off-the-beaten-path experience, Siwa Oasis offers serene beauty and rich cultural heritage. Explore the ancient ruins of the Temple of the Oracle and bathe in the natural springs. It’s a hidden gem that many Egypt travel guides recommend for those seeking tranquillity. Alexandria: Founded by Alexander the Great, Alexandria is a coastal city that combines ancient history with a Mediterranean charm. Places to see in Egypt's second-largest city include the Catacombs of Kom El Shoqafa, the Roman Theatre, and the modern Bibliotheca Alexandrina. The city’s blend of history and modernity makes it a favourite in many Egypt travel packages. Egypt trip packages offer a diverse range of experiences, from the ancient wonders of Cairo and Luxor to the natural beauty of Sharm El Sheikh and Siwa Oasis. With a well-planned Egypt itinerary, you can explore the best places in Egypt and create memories that last a lifetime. Whether you're captivated by history, culture, or natural beauty, the top places to visit in Egypt promise an adventure like no other.
Egypt.Best Honeymoon Destinations in Egypt: Egypt offers a romantic and exotic setting for honeymooners, with its blend of ancient wonders, stunning landscapes, and luxurious resorts. Here are some of the best unique honeymoon destinations in Egypt that will create unforgettable memories for newlyweds, along with recommendations for Egypt tour packages and travel options from India. Luxor and Aswan Siwa Oasis Sharm El Sheikh Alexandria Cairo and Giza
Egypt.Interesting Things to do Egypt: Egypt land steeped in history and culture, offers a wealth of activities for travellers seeking unique and memorable experiences. Whether you're exploring ancient ruins, diving into the Red Sea, or wandering through bustling markets, there are countless things to do in Egypt that cater to all interests. Here are some intriguing activities to include in your Egypt packages. Explore the Pyramids of Giza: No tour to Egypt is complete without visiting the Pyramids of Giza, one of the most famous tourist places in Egypt. These ancient wonders, including the Great Pyramid and the Sphinx, offer a glimpse into the country’s rich history. Many Egypt tour packages include guided tours of these iconic landmarks, allowing you to delve deep into their fascinating stories. Dive and Snorkel in the Red Sea: The Red Sea is renowned for its crystal-clear waters and vibrant coral reefs, making it a paradise for diving and snorkelling enthusiasts. Destinations like Sharm El Sheikh and Hurghada are top spots for underwater adventures. Many Egypt holiday packages include diving excursions, providing equipment and guidance for both beginners and experienced divers. Cruise the Nile River: A Nile River cruise is one of the best ways to experience Egypt’s scenic beauty and historical sites. Sailing from Luxor to Aswan or vice versa, these cruises offer a luxurious and relaxing way to see some of the best places in Egypt. Along the way, you’ll visit magnificent temples, such as Karnak and Philae, making it a highlight of any Egypt travel package. Stroll Through Cairo’s Khan El Khalili Bazaar: For a taste of local culture, visit Cairo’s Khan El Khalili Bazaar. This bustling market is a vibrant maze of shops selling everything from spices and jewellery to traditional crafts. It’s an excellent place to experience the lively atmosphere of Egypt’s capital and pick up unique souvenirs. Many Egypt travel guide recommend this market as a must-see during your Egypt trip. Visit the Temples of Abu Simbel: The temples of Abu Simbel carved out of a mountainside, are among the most impressive monuments in Egypt. A trip to these temples, often included in Egypt trip packages, allows you to marvel at the colossal statues of Ramses II and his queen, Nefertari. The temples’ relocation to avoid flooding by the Aswan High Dam is a testament to modern engineering and preservation efforts. Hot Air Balloon Ride Over Luxor: A hot air balloon ride over Luxor provides a breathtaking perspective of the ancient temples and the Nile River. This early morning adventure offers stunning views of the Valley of the Kings and the surrounding landscape. It’s a unique activity that adds a touch of magic to your Egypt travel experience. Relax in the Siwa Oasis: For a more tranquil experience, head to the Siwa Oasis. Surrounded by the vast desert, this oasis offers natural beauty and a peaceful retreat. Explore the ancient ruins, bathe in Cleopatra’s Bath, and watch the sunset over the Great Sand Sea. Siwa is a hidden gem that many Egypt tour packages highlight for those seeking relaxation and nature. With its rich history, diverse landscapes, and vibrant culture, Egypt offers a myriad of interesting activities for travellers. From exploring ancient pyramids to diving in the Red Sea, each experience adds to the allure of this fascinating country. Incorporating these activities into your Egypt tour packages ensures a comprehensive and unforgettable journey through one of the world’s most intriguing destinations.
Egypt.Famous Foods in Egypt: Dish Description Key Ingredients Common Serving Style Koshari A popular Egyptian street food combining lentils, rice, pasta, topped with spicy tomato sauce, and crispy fried onions. Lentils, rice, pasta, tomato sauce, fried onions Served in a bowl or plate, often as a main meal. Mahshi A variety of stuffed dishes with vine leaves, zucchini, or eggplant filled with a mixture of rice, herbs, and sometimes minced meat, simmered in tomato sauce. Vine leaves, zucchini, eggplant, rice, herbs, tomato sauce, minced meat (optional) Served hot, often as a side or main dish. Ful Medames A traditional breakfast dish made of slow-cooked fava beans seasoned with garlic, olive oil, and lemon juice, typically served with bread and vegetables. Fava beans, garlic, olive oil, lemon juice, Egyptian bread (baladi bread), hard-boiled eggs Served as a hearty breakfast or snack. Ta'ameya (Egyptian Falafel) Egyptian version of falafel made from ground fava beans mixed with herbs and spices, deep-fried until crispy. Fava beans, herbs, spices, pita bread, salad, tahini sauce Served in pita bread with salad and tahini sauce. Molokhia A nutritious soup made from finely chopped leaves of the molokhia plant, cooked with garlic, coriander, and sometimes meat. Known for its slimy texture. Molokhia leaves, garlic, coriander, chicken or rabbit (optional), rice, Egyptian bread Served over rice or with Egyptian bread. Baklava A sweet pastry made of layers of thin pastry filled with chopped nuts and sweetened with syrup or honey, enjoyed during festive occasions and celebrations. Thin pastry layers, chopped nuts (almonds, pistachios), syrup or honey Served as a dessert, often cut into small pieces.

Guwahati.Introduction: Get ready to escape to wonders of Guwahati with our Guwahati packages. The scenic and spell-bound beauty of Guwahati will keep you in awe all through your trip.
