faq.question: How can I go to Masai Mara from India?
faq.answer: There are direct flights available from Mumbai international airport to Nairobi regularly based on the part of India from where you travel, you will need to take if flight, train, or cab ride to Mumbai and fly to Nairobi. From there you can either take another cab ride to Masai Mara or book a short flight to the airstrip at your destination.

faq.question: How many days do you need in Masai Mara?
faq.answer: People book Masai Mara holiday packages in order to fully immerse themselves in the wildlife experience in the wilderness. Spotting these indigenous animals and birds may take some persistence. Therefore, it is best to book Masai Mara packages for three to seven days based on the types of experiences in which you want to indulge.

faq.question: Can we stay inside Masai Mara?
faq.answer: Yes, there are dedicated areas in Masai Mara where you can set up accommodation at hotels or base camps. When you book your Masai Mara packages with Thomas Cook, you have the option to choose the kind of accommodation you prefer. You can even speak to the travel advisors for guidance on accommodation in the different regions of Masai Mara.

faq.question: How should I dress for the Masai Mara?
faq.answer: There are no strict dress codes that you need to follow for your Masai Mara trip. However, when on a Masai Mara jungle safari, you may want to opt for comfortable and loose clothing in order to partake in all the activities. During Masai Mara tours on hot air balloons and at nighttime, you may want to carry your jacket or sweater as the temperature can drop considerably.

faq.question: Is yellow fever vaccination required for Kenya from India?
faq.answer: Yes, if you are booking a Masai Mara tour package from India, you are required to provide a certificate that is internationally valid and proves that you are inoculated against yellow fever.

faq.question: Is there a risk of malaria on safari?
faq.answer: Most safaris in Africa are safe from malaria, especially during the months of summer. Still, it is always ideal to carry malaria pills or consult with a physician at your base camp if you are bitten by a mosquito, when on your Masai Mara tour packages from India to be on the safer side.

faq.question: What month is best for hot air balloon rides on Masai Mara tours?
faq.answer: The beauty of the Masai Mara wildlife safari is exquisite when the weather is warm, the trees are in full bloom, and the indigenous animals and birds are grazing along the river beds. This is why taking a hot air balloon Masai Mara trip is ideal from the month of April to October.

faq.question: Who should not ride in a hot air balloon?
faq.answer: The cost of hot air balloon Masai Mara tours may seem a bit steep but the incredible experience is totally worth the price. however, for certain people, the hot air balloon ride can be risky such as: Those who have recently undergone a major surgery Those who suffer from persistent joint pain Those who have difficulty standing for long periods of time without any assistance Those who have recently indulged in deep scuba diving Women who are pregnant.

Masai Mara.Introduction: Vacations are meant to enrich the soul and enlighten our minds to discover a world beyond our comfort zone. Masai mara trip packages from Thomas cook serve that purpose to the fullest. This natural reserve in Kenya is named after the local masai population who unexpectedly came across a land that they did not quite expect. Soon the Masai Mara jungle safari became a coveted holiday for tourists from around the world who wished to witness wildlife such as lions, African bush elephants, cheetahs, leopards, and others in their natural habitat Regarded as one of the 10 beautiful wonders of the world and ranked as one of the prestigious 7 natural wonders of Africa, this national reserve sharing its borders with the Serengeti national park offers a unique experience with scenery to die for. Masai mara holiday packages are among the top-rated international travel packages from India.
Masai Mara.Table of Content: Most Popular Masai Mara Tour Packages How to reach Masai Mara Travelling within Masai Mara Best time to visit Masai Mara Masai Mara COVID-19 tourist guidelines Best Places to Visit in Masai Mara What to eat in Masai Mara Things to do in Masai Mara Forex/Currency exchanges in Masai Mara Visa for Masai Mara People also ask about Masai Mara Tour Packages
Masai Mara.Most popular Masai Mara tour packages: Among the various international travel deals that Thomas Cook India offers, here are the most popular Masai Mara holiday packages: Tour Packages NameDurationPriceSouth African Trio - Winter11 Nights 12 DaysRs. ₹459 300Kenyan Pride6 Nights 7 DaysRs. ₹202 000Kenyan Trails8 Nights 9 DaysRs. ₹231 200Kenyan Dhamaka - Winter4 Nights 5 DaysRs. ₹237 400Kenyan Extravaganza - Winter6 Nights 7 DaysRs. ₹270 200
Masai Mara.How to reach Masai Mara: When you book your masai mara tour packages with thomas cook, reaching your destination comes with adventurous options. you can reach masai mara by flight or by road., but either way you have to take a detour. One of the most popular options is to take a flight to Nairobi and a car to reach masai mara. You can also plan your trip to masai mara from India by taking a flight to your destination with a layover in Nairobi. This saves time if your masai mara packages are designed for a shorter duration. Another popular option is to travel to Mombasa by flight on a subsequent flight to the masai mara wildlife safari or opt for a cab service from Mombasa airport to your destination. If you are not pressed for time, the latter is a better option as it offers an opportunity to explore the surrounding of masai mara on the way.
Masai Mara.Travelling within Masai Mara: The black cotton soil that covers the entire national reserve of masai mara can be a bit difficult to trudge through. Thus, a 4x4 vehicle is what you need to get through the difficult terrain. These cars are well equipped to provide power to all four wheels at the same time which makes it easier to traverse through the black cotton soil unless it has turned into bog due to rainfall. Fear not though, because, with appropriate tools at your disposal, you can get yourself out of this situation quite easily, that is if you choose to drive yourself of course. The other option is to have a professional drive you on your masai mara tours as they are well equipped to navigate the soil in its various conditions and provide necessary assistance to your survival during your masai mara jungle safari.
Masai Mara.Best time to Visit Masai Mara: Ideally, when booking your masai mara package from India, you must consider the seasonal changes that affect the terrain of the natural reserve. getting stuck in the bog or not getting to spot the animals and birds which you seek for your enriching experience is not the best option. The best time to visit masai mara is during the dry seasons, especially during the months of July to October. This is the peak time when the animals migrate towards water bodies thus making your masai mara trip fulfilling when you spot them. If you wish to book a masai mara package from India on a budget, you can opt for the off-season time when the establishments around the masai mara reserve offer special prices at discounted rates. The months of November to March are when the brief rainfall ends and the birds migrate to the masai mara jungle. The atmosphere is quite appealing and makes for a great time to book your masai mara honeymoon package where you can relax with your spouse.
Masai Mara.Covid-19 tourist guidelines for Masai Ma: Africa is one of the few continents that was affected during the covid-19 outbreak. In order to maintain the level of safety for the inhabitants, there are certain covid-19 guidelines laid out for tourists to follow when booking the masai mara package from India.
Masai Mara.Best Places to Visit in Masai Mara: In the middle of the wilderness lies a wide array of beautiful places to visit in Masai Mara. The inhabitants of the wilderness, the masai locals, have rich history and information to share that may astound you. Make some time for your itinerary for your masai mara trip package to experience the fine world that exists to make this national reserve so unique. The masai village - the masai mara national reserve is named after the local Maasai population spotted the beautiful land of wilderness around them. Take some time to venture into the Maasai village during your masai mara tours to learn more about the area as well as the rich history that dictates the livelihood around this hidden gem in Kenya. The ol kinyei conservancy - the area belongs to the inhabitants of the local Maasai village who offer rest camps and various forms of accommodation for those participating in the game drive. The masai river - most people book their masai mara package from India to catch a glimpse of the wildebeests that traverse from the Serengeti to the mara river for hydration. It is one of the primary sources of water for animals as well as the inhabitants around the region. Lake Victoria - is one of the popular attractions in the masai mara packages. Check out the serenity of nature surrounded by dense forest lands. You can also take a boat ride across the lake for a tropical experience. Mfangano island - located in the midst of lake victoria, mfangano island offers a camp for an immersive experience when enjoying your masai mara tour packages. Narok museum – it is an institution dedicated to exhibiting various remains from the ancient masai inhabitants and their cultural heritage. It provides a window into the past of masai mara and the cultural phenomena that influence the present. The mara triangle - is the entryway through which the wildebeests from the Serengeti enter the masai mara national reserve to enjoy the abundance of the mara river for hydration during the dry seasons. The masai mara national reserve – is the epitome of a natural ecosystem that invites indigenous species of birds and animals to live peacefully and safely in the bosom of nature. If you book your masai mara packages during the peak season, you can catch a glimpse of the infamous wildebeests, cheetahs, zebras, impalas, leopards, elephants, giraffes, and other rarely cited species of wildlife. During the off seasons, the masai mara national reserve is populated by hibernating birds that make the experience of your masai mara jungle safari all the more interesting. The musiara swamp - is the perfect location to catch a glimpse of the rare wildlife that inhabit or migrate to masai mara. You can take game drives, which are essentially the masai mara tours in 4x4 vehicles around the jungle to spot the animals in their natural form.
Masai Mara.What to Eat in Masai Mara: Food in masai mara is just as unique as the inhabitants of the region and their ability to adapt to living harmoniously with nature. While one of the traditional dishes of masai mara involves mixing blood with milk for healing purposes and to nourish the body, most of the cuisine is extremely friendly to the dietary specifications of tourists. As a part of your masai mara packages, you can choose to indulge in the local practices of ritualistic cooking and healing or you can opt for a meal plan that is best suited to your sentiment. Most of the national reserve rest camps and hotel accommodations provide traditional Indian food included in your masai mara package from India. The chefs are incredibly talented in recreating popular recipes such as chapatis, dal, curries made of kidney beans or chickpeas, vegetarian delicacies made of fresh green leaves and vegetables, and even the popular Indian snack called samosa. Combining influences from Indian cuisine as well as the African cultural norms of using fresh vegetables and fruits in their menu, the chefs preparing the breakfasts, lunches, and dinners for your masai mara trip package are also well-versed with delicious soup recipes. Freshly made soup out of roasted tomatoes, grilled vegetables, butternut squash, green leaves, avocado, etc. up with vinaigrette made out of passionfruit is a popular choice among vegetarians on their masai mara tours. Sometimes, the menu may also involve Italian delicacies such as pizza with thin crusts and meat and vegetable toppings or arrabiata penne pasta during buffets and banquets. You can also indulge in freshly plucked black grapes, tamarind, amla, sapota, etc. For an authentic Kenyan experience during your masai mara trip from India.
Masai Mara.Things to do in Masai Mara: When you opt for a trip to masai mara from India, you are inclined to expand your horizons and witness the stunning beauty of this naturally blessed region. Therefore, you may want to include in your itinerary for masai mara holiday packages the following activities to further enhance the experience of visiting this hidden gem in Kenya: The game drive The most popular activity among those who book masai mara packages is the game drive. Basically, it is an opportunity to take a drive in a 4x4 vehicle foreign masai mara wildlife safari through the forest lands and catch a glimpse of the top five indigenous species off the land. King of the jungle, i.e., the African lion traverse with their pride to protect one another while defending their land. you are bound to come across a pride of lions on your masai mara tours. Contrary to the king of the jungle, leopards are a bit more challenging on the spot during your masai mara jungle safari. Be patient and keep a lookout on treetops to catch a glimpse of these exquisite animals. African bush elephants are known to be the largest mammals on land throughout the globe. These calm but easily excitable giants are easy to spot as they move together while seeking resources for food and water. The black rhinos of Africa have been poached nearly to extinction for their horns. This makes it especially difficult to spot one of these divine creatures in the wilderness. However, if you drive to the sand river sanctuary, a black rhino population is closely monitored and guarded for preservation. Lastly, the most dangerous animals that you can spot on the game drive are the African buffaloes which are popular for their unpredictable behaviour. He perceived distance if you catch a glimpse of one as the only predators who can take them down are the mighty kings of the jungle. Taking a trip to the mara crossing Mara crossing is the gateway that allows the infamous wildebeests to make their way from the Serengeti to the wilderness of masai mara. Along with them antelopes, zebras, and gazelles also migrate into the jungle which is part of the masai mara wildlife safari experience. The symbiosis between the animals is a teachable moment for humankind to learn how to live with each other harmoniously. The zebras contribute their excellent vision to locate and avoid predators who may harm them during their stay in the savannah while the wildebeests contribute their highly tuned olfactory sensors to track water and food for all the different herds. As these herds attempt to cross the mara river which is infested by Nile crocodiles, they must work with each other to make it across the waterbed. Many lives are lost at this time but witnessing this natural phenomenon is an experience to enrich the human soul. Indulging in the traditional cultures of masai When you plan a masai mara trip from India, you must certainly broaden your horizons by taking a walk through the Maasai village and indulging in the local traditions and culture. You can take part in the festivities although drinking the milk and blood combination does not appeal to all, even though the tribes strongly believe in its healing capability. If you are invited into the home of a local masai, make sure to remove your shoes before entering the household. If they offer food, wash your hands before and after the meal and be polite in your interactions while you learn about the eras gone by. Partaking in a hot air balloon ride While a masai mara jungle safari is one of the top attractions and must make its way to your to-do list during your masai mara travel packages, for a better view of the wilderness, you could take a hot air balloon ride. Enjoying the panoramic views of the forest lands and villages underneath as you soar through the skies makes for memorable masai tours. Not only is it an exquisite experience for a family but if you have chosen a masai mara honeymoon package, then the bush breakfast is a treat for you and your spouse that you will never forget. The hot air balloon is large enough to accommodate 15 to 20 people at $400 to $500 per head. A professional pilot is in charge of gliding the balloon across the sky for your scenic masai mara tours while a crew of ground control officers ensures the direction and safety of all the passengers. Whatever you do, try not to miss this exclusive experience while on your masai mara trip.
Masai Mara.Forex currency exchange and Masai Mara: When travelling for your masai mara travel packages, you need to have Kenyan shilling available for purchases around the masai mara jungle safari. The exchange rate of the Kenyan shilling against Indian rupees is 1.52, and fortunately, Thomas cook offers forex currency exchange information and assistance to make this process easier. You can find all the forex currency exchange before your masai mara trip here
Masai Mara.Visa applications for travelling from In: Booking your masai mara trip from India comes with an obligation to have a valid visa to travel to the east African country safely abiding by the laws set by the Kenyan government. Fortunately, the application process for a Kenyan tourist visa it's completely online and you can submit the required documents to receive your visa within three to five working days. Here is a list of the documents that you need to submit when applying for a Kenyan visa for your masai mara packages: A filled-out online application A passport that was issued by the government of India within the last 10 years and is valid for the next six months. A scanned copy of your details as per the passport The entire travel itinerary is as per your masai mara holiday packages. Your flight information to and from your masai mara trip. Information about your accommodation and any applicable coupons and vouchers that you may have used. You can pay for your Kenyan e-visa using a MasterCard, visa card, discovery card, or American Express card in order to enjoy your masai mara tours from India without any hassle. The charges for a Kenyan e-visa are approximately Rs. 4500 including the handling charge of the card that is used.

table_of_content: Best Time to Visit the Netherlands
Things to Do in the Netherlands
Netherlands.Introduction: The Netherlands, a country rich in culture, history, heritage, and art, is in western Europe with overseas territories in the Caribbean Sea. A true jewel of Europe’s crown, this country is one of the most fascinating travel destinations.
Netherlands.About Netherlands:: The Netherlands is a beautiful country filled with canals, national parks, lakes, dunes, and other habitats. In the Netherlands, summers are moderately warm, while winters are cold and freezing. The changing weather creates beautiful dunes and landscapes, which attract tourists from all over the world. It is a magical place covered in ancient forests, hills, moorlands, and extensive pasture lands. The Netherlands has a strong connection with water. Approximately, 26% of the Netherlands is below sea level. The country's three major rivers are The Rhine, The Meuse, and The Scheldt.
Netherlands.Most Popular Netherlands Tour Packages:: Tour Packages NameDurationPriceSensational Europe - Summer 202614 Nights 15 DaysRs. ₹412 000Europe for All - Summer 20267 Nights 8 DaysRs. ₹186 900Grand Bargain Tour Of Europe - Summer 202614 Nights 15 DaysRs. ₹416 700European Extravaganza - Summer 202616 Nights 17 DaysRs. ₹498 800European Extravaganza With 3 Nights Mediterranean Cruise - Summer 202619 Nights 20 DaysRs. ₹535 300 We can also curate a Netherlands tour package according to your preferences and include cities of your choice. It will be inclusive of expenses on flights, meals, and stays. If you wish to go for a Europe trip, covering different cities in different countries, check our Europe tour packages.
Netherlands.Quick Guide for Netherlands: Temperature 41℉ to 57.9 ℉ or 5℃ to 14.39℃ Currency Euro Language Dutch, English Capital Amsterdam Population 1,76,96,000 Popular Tourist Destinations Amsterdam, Delft, Rotterdam, Eindhoven, Maastricht, Hague Time Zone UTC + 01:00 Area 41865 km2 Country Code +31
Netherlands.Best Time to Visit the Netherlands: The Netherlands is a year-round celebrated tourist destination as it has mild temperatures. But there are some months which are preferred more by travelers. The best month to visit the Netherlands is June. Many people also visit between September and November as it is the time when beer and food fests begin. During this time, various activities take place all over the country. The balance of peacefulness, fun, and pleasant weather makes the experience memorable. The temperature at this time ranges between 8℃ and 19℃. The Netherlands is a famous destination for tulip festivals and tulip gardens. April is the best month to visit the Tulip Gardens. During this time, it is slightly costly and crowded as well but that is because this is one of the liveliest times in the country that every nature-lover enjoys. July and August are also peak months. Many tourists visit from North America and Europe since it’s their summer break. Tranquil weather and low-lying coastal land are safe from extreme temperatures. December to March is the calm time to visit the Netherlands. In December, the footfall is low due to Christmas and New Year's Eve. During this time, trips are less costly. Visitors can enjoy freezing temperatures and various festive carnivals. The temperature at this time is around 5–9℃. Some Special Events: You can schedule your visit according to these special events: Scheveningen New Year’s Dive on 1st January Carnival in Holland on 26th Februry Easter in Holland on 17th April King’s Day on 27th April Liberation Day on 5th May Sinterklaas on 5th December New Year's Eve
Netherlands.How to Reach the Netherlands: The Netherlands is a fairly accessible place as it is well-connected by air, water and land travel mediums. The following are the various modes of transportation you can choose from to reach the Netherlands: By Air: The Netherlands has five international airports: Schiphol Airport, Hague Airport, Eindhoven Airport, Groningen Eelde Airport, and Maastricht Airport. Schiphol Airport is near Amsterdam. Being the most prominent and the biggest airports in the Netherlands, it is the landing ground of flights from airline services all over the world, like Qatar Airlines, Air India, KLM, British Airlines, and Lufthansa. Schiphol Airport is in reach of a well-interlinked railway network, that can ferry you to any major city in the country. You can catch a train that drops you right below the main airport hall, making travel easy with your luggage and time-saving. All Indians will need a Schengen Visa and a valid passport to enter the Netherlands by air. By Road: There are also tourists who prefer reaching the Netherlands by road. India and the Netherlands are 7,685 kilometers away. If someone wants to give it a try, it takes 4 days and 10 hours of nonstop driving to reach the Netherlands, and the journey in between is wonderful and unique in its own way. If anyone has time, they should invest it in a road trip. By Water: Only one Silver Whisper Cruise sails from Mumbai to Amsterdam. It reaches in 140 days, and takes you through 62 countries on the way! From the UK, three ferries can take you to the outskirts of Rotterdam and Amsterdam. When you are in the Netherlands, you can choose the water mode for inter-country travel as well. Water is one of the most famous modes of travel among tourists; you shouldn’t miss the chance you are here. A few modes of water travel are listed below: Ferries: Ferry rides are easy to fall in love with. In Amsterdam, you can travel by ferry free of cost! You just need to find out the schedule of ferries to pick the one you need. The famous ferry rides ones are from Central Station to Buiksloterweg, reaching the EUE Film Museum, and from Amsterdam Central Station to Noord District of Amsterdam. Water Bus: Water bus is a new experience for Indians but you will have to pay for this ride. Usually, a water bus runs from Rotterdam to Dordrecht. If you are cycling and want to take the water bus, you can carry it along on the bus. Water Taxi: Many tourists love to travel in water taxis. The fare is dependent on the number of people and the distance. It's a unique experience and must be there on your list as well. You can get a water taxi to take you from Amsterdam to Rotterdam. Keep the following things in mind for traveling within the country: Cycling: The Netherlands is one of my favorite spots for cycling. If you love this activity and are a sustainability enthusiast, you would love to cycle here. The flat Dutch terrain is perfect for cyclists, who can reach almost everywhere on their bikes. Needless to say, you will find a high cycling traffic, so be careful while riding it. Train: Railway network of the Netherlands is very strong and is the primary mode of transport, being one of the fastest routes to travel within the country. The Netherlands has three types of central railways: Intercity, Sneltrein, and Sprinter and all cost around $25. The fast Intercity direct trains are more convenient when traveling to another city, but they could be slightly more expensive. In the western region, the frequency of each train is every 5 minutes, but when you go towards the countryside, you might have to wait for an hour to catch a train. The local lines are run by Arriva, Connexxion, Syntus, and Veolia, whereas Nederlandse Spoorwegen runs the main one. You will never be disappointed by their train services, which take you through different scenic locations of the Netherlands. Bus: Buses are one of the standard modes of transportation and are one of the convenient ones for shorter travels but they are not so fast. If you plan for a more extended trip, you might have to change 2-3 buses to reach their destination but could be considered an economical option. Arriva, Connexxion, EBS, Qbuzz are the leading bus companies. For a better experience, it's always advisable to ask around once you reach the Netherlands as many cities have their own bus companies. There are night bus services from Amsterdam, Rotterdam, and Hague. Metro and Trams: The main cities of the Netherlands like Amsterdam and Rotterdam have large metro and trams. You will also find these in Hague and Utrecht. Car: You can travel by car in two ways, either hire a taxi or rent a car. However, hiring a taxi could be slightly expensive and is usually not preferred. It is better if you drive a car than a taxi.
Netherlands.Famous Places in the Netherlands: City Name Places to Visit Things to Do: Amsterdam: Bloemenmarkt, Vondelpark, Rijksmuseum, The Anne Frank House, and other beautiful places. Go cycling, enjoy the shopping streets, take the canal cruise Rotterdam: Rotterdam Zoo, Climbing Fun Park, The Euromast Tower, De Markthal. Rotterdam Boat Tour Delft: The Old Canal, Delft City Hall, The Vermeer Delft Museum, The Princess Court, and many more. Learn the insights of Delft Pottery, visit museums, and climb the Bell Tower Maastricht: Natural History Museum, the Old Town Walls & Hell Gate, St. Pietersberg Caves, Bonnefanten museum Visit the red church, walk around this beautiful city Edam: Edam Cheese Market, Volendam Museum, Paard Van Marken Explore windmills and historic town, Cheese Tasting Eindhoven: Van Abbe Museum, Philips Museum, DAF Museum Visit Speelpark De Splinter, Go cycling around the city Giethoorn: Museum De Oude Aarde, Museum Gloria Maris Spend some quality time and admire the charming canals Haarlem: Grote Kerk, Ten Boom Museum, Frans Hals Museum Visit the quirky cafes, climb a landmark windmill, go shopping, cycle on the beach, go for beer tasting. Volendam: Volendam Museum, Paard Van Marken, and many more Go fishing and try the different restaurants Best Honeymoon Destinations The Netherlands has various landscapes, rich food, and is the epitome of history. It can be one of the best destinations to plan a honeymoon where the couple can experience a blend of adventure, calm, and tranquility. Below are the best places to visit for a honeymoon couple: Amsterdam: Scenic bike rides, beautiful canals, and some amazing cafes will help you spend some quality time with your partner. You can also get lost in the history of this beautiful place. Kinder Dijk: If you have seen the beautiful pictures of windmills all over social media and you want to experience it in real life, Kinder Dijk will never disappoint you. You will be enticed by the 19 monumental mills of this small village. People from all over the world come here, and it is included in the UNESCO world heritage site. Texel Island: Head to Texel Island for a cozy honeymoon during the summers. You will be mesmerized by the incredible sand dunes and salt matches covered with lavender. There are plenty of beachside resorts and honeymoon-friendly bungalows. This could be your best honeymoon destination if you love beaches and nature at its best blend. Hague: As beautiful as Amsterdam, this robust scenic city is a hub for all museum lovers and history buffs. So just spend some time here with museums, scenic beauty, and some good food.
Netherlands.Places to Visit in Netherlands: De Efteling: It is the oldest, and by far, the most famous tourist attraction in the Netherlands. It is one of the oldest theme parks situated in the town of Kaatsheuvel. Themed around culture, nostalgia, and romance, this park will take you on a fairytale-like trip! Rijksmuseum Amsterdam: Rijksmuseum is a Dutch national art and history museum with around 8,000 exhibitions of works of art and historical artifacts. It showcases the art of famous artists like Frans Halls and Rembrandt. Burger Zoo: It is one of the most prominent zoos in the Netherlands. It has a wide variety of unique flora and fauna. You can enjoy safaris and visit rainforests, mangrove swamps, indoor deserts, and aquariums. Burger Zoo houses exotic animals like Dusky leaf monkeys, hog deer, golden jackals, and many more. A visit to this zoo will make you appreciate the diversity in nature. Diergaarde Blijdorp: It is one of the oldest zoos in the Netherlands. Here, you can voyage under the sea in an oceanium. This zoo also has African Gorilla Island, Taman Indah, Crocodile River, and Ice Cave for polar bears. This place is a must-see for nature lovers and animal enthusiasts. Attractiepark Slagharen: It is an adventurous theme park located in Slagharen. It is sectioned into six themed sites—Indian, Yellowstone, New Orleans, Wild West, Jules Adventureland, and Mexican. It has around 40 different types of attractions. Cauberg: It is one of the prominent hills of the Netherlands and a major tourist attraction. It is famous for hosting cycling tournaments. Eyserbosweg Hills: These hills are also famous for their cycling-friendly terrain. If you love to cycle in picturesque locations, you should visit Eyserbosweg Hills. Scheveningen Beach: Being one of the major tourist attractions, this beach is famous for its beach bars, small shops, and pier. It is a palace that offers a beautiful blend of good food, drinks and scenery. The food and drink setup covers a vast area on the beach, so it never appears overcrowded. Zandvoort Beach: This beach is only 30 kilometers away from Amsterdam and is a favourite of young people. Here, you can explore sand dunes, food shacks and go surfing on ocean waves. Famous Dutch Delicacies Food is a thread that connects everyone. When you are in the Netherlands, you must try some authentic delicacies to satisfy your taste buds: Haring or Hollandse Nieuwe: It is one of the most famous Dutch dishes, and if you are a fish lover, it is something you wouldn’t want to miss. The raw herring is served with chopped raw onions and gherkins. There is a Dutch way to have it as well. You need to hold the tail and have it upwards and feel that you are in the Netherlands. Kroket: One of the famous Dutch snacks is the Kroket. They are originally made of beef or veal, but now they have different flavors like chicken satay, shrimps, and even vegetarian. It could be one of the options for vegetarians in the Netherlands. A Kroket is a deep-fried roll covered in breadcrumbs. The stuffing is lip-smacking and is usually accompanied by bread and mustard sauce. Poffertjes: They look like small pancakes but are much fluffier. They are made with buckwheat flour and yeast and served with butter and powdered sugar dollop. During winter, having fresh Poffertjes is quite common in the Netherlands. Stroopwafel: Stroopwafel is one of the most popular pastries from the Netherlands, and you cannot miss it once you are in the Netherlands. It is a unique cookie baked in the waffle batter and sliced horizontally. The two thin layers of Stroopwafel are filled with sweet and sticky syrup called Stroop. It is the specialty of Amsterdam. However, you can find it almost everywhere in the Netherlands. Patat: The Dutch version of French fries is Patat. It is thicker and has various ingredients like mayonnaise, ketchup, curry, or peanut sauce. This is comfort food for all travelers who want easy options. Kaas: Dutch and Cheese have a deeper connection. If you love cheese, then you must try it the Dutch way. They are the largest Cheese Exporters, and their cheese is the perfect match between hard and soft cheese. “Edam” and “Gouda” are popular cheese destinations. Dutch people can have cheese with their breakfast, lunch, and dinner. Stamppot: Stamppot is a traditional Dutch dish made with mashed potatoes, carrots, kale, endive, and many more vegetables. It is something people like to have during cold winters. Hagelslag: They are chocolate sprinkles over the sandwich. The Dutch love their sandwich, and the combination of chocolate and sandwich is something which everyone with a sweet tooth would like to indulge in. Indonesian Food: The Netherlands is a hub of Indonesians, you may find various Indonesian restaurants in the Netherlands. Kebbling: It’s dutch comfort food, and you can find it almost on every street in the Netherlands. It’s a deep-fried fish served with some mayo and tartar sauce. It is one of the most common snacks which fish lovers must try.
Netherlands.Things to Do in the Netherlands: You can do some exciting activities in the Netherlands, such as: Cycling on the flat Dutch terrain: It is a popular mode of transport across the country, about 36% Dutch people bicycle daily. Shopping: The Netherlands is popular for shoppping custom clogs, Dutch licorice, chocolate bonbons, handmade wallets, and much more. Trying Dutch Delicacies: Kroket, poffertjes, kaas and patat, among others, are to die for! Adventurous Activities: Kitesurfing Dutch Coast, canoeing, splash touring, bungee jumping from Scheveningen Pier are just a few of the things you can do. Shopping in the Netherlands The Netherlands is one of the most popular destinations for shopping. People from all over the world come to travel and shop. Below are the few recommended places you can think of while you go to the Netherlands to shop and enjoy the shopping spree! De Negen Straatjes: The nine shopping streets in Amsterdam give you a distinctive shopping experience with a view. These shopping streets straddle the Central Canal ring of the city. These streets are filled with vintage shops, boutiques, specialty shops, and cafes. When you are in Amsterdam, you must shop with a view! Van Oldenbarneveltstraat: This place in Rotterdam has a feel of Paris. With a wide variety of luxurious boutiques, fancy shoe shops, and delightful cafes. The finest leather products are available here. It has a variety of Dutch, Swedish, and French brands. Witte De Withstraat: This is one of Rotterdam's most popular shopping sites where you can spend all your day! They have a variety of High-end shopping products, décor, and a lot more. They also have art institutions and galleries to look up after your shopping. Passage: This is a perfect rainy day shopping place in Hague. The architecture of this shopping place will take you back to the 19th century. It is a paradise for retail shopping. Kalverstraat: This is the only pedestrian street in Amsterdam with a mix of clothing stores and several shoe stores. It has local as well as international brands for you to shop for. If you happen to shop here, many of your shopping requirements will be ticked off. Taste of India in the Netherlands: Few of us miss the Indian palate abroad. But when in the Netherlands, you don’t have to worry as there are good Indian places which will please your Indian tongue. A few of them are mentioned below: Shah Jahan: Shah Jahan is an authentic Indian restaurant that provides you with an amazing blend of Indian spices, fresh ingredients, and perfect portions. The dishes portray our India most humbly, and they look and taste delicious! It is in Amsterdam. 91 Spices: This place in Rotterdam is one of the best places that serves perfect paneer and chicken dishes. It is a perfect place to take your family when in Rotterdam. The staff is warm, and the ambiance is young and fancy. The spices used in the food will give you a feel of India. What’s Up India: Serving the perfect combo of kullhad chai and classic Indian snacks, this place in Rotterdam is a must-try. What’s Up India will give you an Indian feel, right from waiting impatiently for your food filled with ghee and lots of spices, soft and supple rotis, their food will also fill your heart and your stomach. They also have Indo-Chinese options that will make you greedy and numb your taste buds. Koh-I-Noor: Named after the great Indian possession Kohinoor, this place is just like one in Amsterdam. It’s a family-friendly restaurant with an extensive menu, and their chicken tikka is something you will fall in love with. Bay’s Dream: Located in Hague, this restaurant is perfect for some hot food. It is a small, comfortable place for all your food dreams to come true. This place is a “Yes, yes!” when in Hague. Some Thrilling Adventurous Activities Any vacation we take, we plan for at least one or two action-packed activities, which would give us an adrenaline rush. The Netherlands offers various activities that you can think of doing. Some of them are as follows: ZipLine in Rotterdam: Rotterdam’s Euro Mast reaches 100 meters at its peak and provides a panoramic view. The Zipline takes approximately 15 seconds and is managed by professionals for safer execution. Bungee Jump from Scheveningen Pier: This seaside suburb or Hague gives you an opportunity to bungee jump in the Netherlands with a sea level of 60 meters. It is the only bungee jump in the Netherlands. Ice Skating on the Canals of Amsterdam: When the temperature drops below one, the canals of Amsterdam freezes, making it perfect for ice skating. Parachute Jumping: The Island of Texel in the north of the Netherlands is famous for parachute jumping. The organization offers several jumps after you are trained by professionals. All safety measures are taken care of by the authority. Go Mountain Biking: Keeping aside all the gentle cycle paths, the Netherlands is home to thrilling tracks for mountain biking. The Hoge Veluwe National Park is well known for mountain biking and challenging terrains. This Park is in the center of an ancient glacier ridge formed during the ice age and has distinctive landscapes. Biking here is a combination of nature and thrill. Some Interesting Facts about the Netherlands The Dutch are the tallest in the world. The Netherlands is the most densely populated country in Europe. Lowest-lying country in Europe, i.e., about 26% of its people live below sea level. 6th happiest country in the world. Dutch people are the healthiest globally and are the most physically active in Europe. The Dutch invented the first stock market in the world. There are more bicycles than people in the Netherlands. There are around 1000 windmills in the Netherlands. Tulips aren’t native to the Netherlands. The Dutch turned red carrots to orange. The Dutch eat more Licorice than any other nation in the world. King’s Day used to be called Queen’s Day. Dutch Culture, Traditions, and Habits The Netherlands culture is diverse in different regions of the country. The Dutch take pride in conserving their cultural heritage, and the government also supports it. The popular symbols of Dutch culture are wooden shoes, lace caps, tulips, and windmills. Painting and sculptures are also major parts of their culture. The Netherlands has a diversified religious population, including Chirstians, Muslims, and Hindus. Christianity is the most practiced religion in the region. The famous Dutch festivals are the Spring Opening of Keukenhof Gardens, Flower Parade, King’s Day, Holland Festival, and many more. Dutch people are highly disciplined and hardworking. They consider their family as their foundation. They are tolerant and raise their children without gender bias. They are highly private and love their food. Visa Process for IndiansIn order to travel to the Netherlands, all Indian citizens must have a Schengen Visa. Additionally, note the following for your Visa Application: You need to submit your passport or other travel documents issued within the last ten years. You need to fill out an application form. Each person traveling will have a different form. A consent form is required for a minor from their parents. In the country where your visa processes happen, the Visa Application Center takes your biometrics and your digital photograph. Usually, people apply six months in advance, and the center declares within 15 working days whether your application is accepted or rejected. Enter traveler details and delivery options. Make an online payment. Save order confirmation receipt. Places You Can Think of Visiting from The Netherlands 1. France 2. Switzerland 3. Belgium 4. Germany 5. Denmark 6. Austria 7. The United Kingdom 8. Luxembourg 9. Ireland 10. Sweden The Netherlands is just not a country; it's a feeling. With such a variety of landscapes, this country has a lot to offer. We at Thomas Cook constantly work to make your travel an unforgettable experience. faq.question: Q. Is Andhra Pradesh an ideal location for a solo budget trip?
faq.answer: A. Yes, Andhra Pradesh is a great solo holiday destination on a budget. You can browse through our professionally picked and affordable Andhra tour packages for a great deal.

faq.question: Q. What is the off-season for tourism in Andhra Pradesh?
faq.answer: A. The monsoon, which ranges from July to September, typically remains the off-season for tourism in Andhra Pradesh.

faq.question: Q. Should I pack warm clothes for the Andhra Pradesh trip in winter
faq.answer: A. Winter in Andhra Pradesh usually gives out pleasant weather throughout its course. However, it is recommended that you pack a few light jackets or woollen clothing to keep yourself protected against the early morning and late night cold breezes.

faq.question: Q. When is the best time to visit Lambasingi in Andhra Pradesh?
faq.answer: A. The period of November to February is considered to be the best time to visit Lambasingi. You can find suitable Andhra Pradesh tour packages on our website.

faq.question: Q. Is there a camping activity offered in Araku Valley?
faq.answer: A. Yes, camping is a key activity you can avail of during your Araku Valley visit.

faq.question: Q. What are some famous places for local handmade saree shopping in Andhra Pradesh?
faq.answer: A. Mangalagiri town, Pochampalli, Anantapur district, etc., are just a few examples of local handwoven saree shopping in Andhra Pradesh. These locations are also great examples of AP best tourist places.

faq.question: Q. How to reach Andhra Pradesh by road from Tamil Nadu?
faq.answer: A. You can take NH 40, NH 205, NH 234, NH 219 or NH 716 by road to reach Andhra Pradesh from Tamil Nadu.

faq.question: Q. What are some adventure activities available in Andhra Pradesh?
faq.answer: A. Adventure activities in Andhra Pradesh include paragliding, parasailing, camping, trekking, scuba diving, surfing, and so on.

faq.question: Q. What are some must-see waterfalls in Andhra Pradesh?
faq.answer: A. The Rampa falls, Ubbalamadugu falls, Talakona falls, and Katiki falls are popular waterfalls in Andhra Pradesh.

Andhra Pradesh.Introduction: The state of Andhra Pradesh (AP) is located in India’s southeastern coastal region alongside the Bay of Bengal. Amaravati is the de facto capital of Andhra Pradesh, while Hyderabad acts as its de jure capital. It is one of the most famous tourist sites in all of India, brimming with wonderful local traditions and culture. There are many places to visit in AP, which is why it is often regarded as a “complete” travel destination. This title really does justice in defining the brilliant tourism aspect of the state, with its numerous heritage sites, citadels, naturally-formed grand landscapes, a number of beaches, diverse wildlife, caves, divine temples, ancient ruins, delectable local cuisines, and much more. All of these combined make Andhra Pradesh a fantastic holiday getaway perfect for short and long breaks alike. On top of that, a trip to Andhra Pradesh is quite favourable for those who are looking for a versatile travel experience within a budget. Hence, if you are planning a trip solo or with friends and have selected Andhra Pradesh as your next holiday point, make sure to check out some of the best Andhra Pradesh tour packages by Thomas Cook for a glorious journey. Besides a trip to make memories with friends and family, Andhra Pradesh is also a perfect honeymoon location with its massive range of different experiences. This marvellous holiday destination is easily accessible from all the major cities in India, and you can check out our handy guide on Andhra Pradesh tourism for a quick overview.

Andhra Pradesh.Best time to visit Andhra Pradesh:: Even though you can pack your bags and take a trip to this majestic Indian state at any time, winter is considered the best time to visit Andhra Pradesh . To start off, winter in Andhra Pradesh is the shoulder season in terms of overall tourism traffic. It offers a great window for individuals who are looking for a comparatively lower crowd but don’t want to go during the off-season. This period ranges from mid-late October to February and features overall pleasant weather. Temperature ranges from 16 to 25°C during winter in Andhra Pradesh, with an average of 20°C. You will not feel overly cold or hot during this season, and the outdoor weather tends to remain sharp and clear for the most part. In addition, major festivals like Makar Sankranti, Pongal, Ugadi, etc., are also celebrated during this time. Hence, many prefer winter as the ideal time to visit Andhra Pradesh. Even though the temperature doesn’t drop too low during this season, you should pack light jackets and warm clothes to protect yourself against the early morning and late night breezes. If you are travelling to Tirupati from Hyderabad, you may be interested in our AP tourism Hyderabad to Tirupati package . Besides this, tourism in Andhra Pradesh is also quite popular during the summer and monsoon. Summer in Andhra Pradesh is the peak season for tourism, mostly due to school summer vacations. This season expands from March to June, with hot weather reigning throughout. The temperature usually ranges between 25 to 45°C with an average of 40°C. This season's key highlights for tourists are the hill stations, waterparks and beaches. Pack sunscreens and light clothes to protect yourselves against the hot summertime in Andhra Pradesh. Also, book your travel, transportation and accommodation in advance to avoid being unavailable during the peak season. Monsoon in Andhra Pradesh is considered the low (off-peak) season for tourism. Even though it sees comparatively lower tourist traffic, some people prefer planning their trips during this period, i.e., between July and September. Monsoon temperature in Andhra Pradesh ranges between 25 to 35°C, with an average of 28°C. After a scorching summer heat, it gives the region a sigh of relief with cooler nights and rainfall, all while the natural beauty of this place becomes more vibrant. If you are looking for a low rush season to visit Andhra Pradesh, monsoon can be a great choice but be aware that rainfall may hinder your travel plans. You can find more details about the Andhra Pradesh tour across different seasons on our page.
Andhra Pradesh.How to reach Andhra Pradesh?: If you have chosen a trip to Andhra Pradesh for your next holiday, you can rest assured about easy transport availability. The state and international connectivity are well-linked to different parts of the country. Here are the available ways to reach Andhra Pradesh: Reaching Andhra Pradesh by air: The Rajiv Gandhi International Airport in Hyderabad is considered to be one of the best ways to reach the Indian state of Andhra Pradesh by air. Hyderabad will be the AP’s joint capital until 2024 and works as a well-connected transportation hub for tourism. You can take a look at our AP tourism packages from Hyderabad for exciting details. Other than that, there are 6 major airports in Andhra Pradesh, three of which are international, while the rest are domestic. The three international airports in AP include Vishakthapatanam, Tirupati and Vijayawada, while the three domestic airports in AP are Rajahmundry, Uyyalawada Narasimha Reddy airport (Kurnool) and Kadapa. This makes reaching Andhra Pradesh by air very accessible from major cities in India, with daily domestic flights, alongside direct international flights. Reaching Andhra Pradesh by train: Besides air, rail is the second best travel option that you can opt for to reach Andhra Pradesh. Both Vijayawada junction and Visakhapatnam junction offer great railway connectivity with the major cities in India, including direct trains from and to Mumbai, Delhi, Kolkata, Chennai and Bengaluru. Some people also prefer travelling to Hyderabad’s Nampally station and then taking a connecting train or bus to reach the desired destination in Andhra Pradesh. Reaching Andhra Pradesh by road : If you wish to reach Andhra Pradesh by road, you will be glad to hear that it also features great road connectivity. You may choose to drive in your private car or take an inter-state bus scheduled for any of the major stops in Andhra Pradesh. The roads connecting Andhra Pradesh to the rest of the country are quite well-maintained, making up for a comfortable road trip. If you are travelling from Odisha, you may take NH 16, NH 326 or NH 326A routes. Similarly, if you are starting your road journey from Chhattisgarh, you may take NH 26 or NH 30. Browse through our carefully sorted AP tourism packages for exciting and affordable deals. Travelling within Andhra Pradesh: In terms of transport within the state, Andhra Pradesh offers tons of options that you can choose from. This includes driving in your private or rental car, taking intra city radio taxis or cabs, regular or luxury air-conditioned buses, etc. You can go with the old and reliable auto rickshaws for local or nearby travel within Andhra Pradesh. Best honeymoon destinations in Andhra Pradesh If you are planning an ideal location for a honeymoon within the country, look no further than Andhra Pradesh. Due to its wide variety of experiences, fun activities, local delights and more, couples across the nation plan their honeymoon amidst the gorgeous natural beauty of this place. Apart from the well-known Vizag and Vijayawada, here are some examples of AP tourism places that can also serve as romantic honeymoon destinations: Nellore : Besides being one of the best places to visit in Andhra Pradesh, Nellore makes up for a fantastic honeymoon destination. It features an array of different experiences all in one location, including many romantic things to do. For example, you can visit the beautiful and well-maintained gardens, explore the natural beauty of the Penchalakona waterfalls, local lakes and forests, or take a romantic stroll through the wet sand of the Mypadu beach along with your loved one. Nellore also has great availability of accommodations and transportation within budget. Chittoor : Chittoor is one of the most popular honeymoon destinations and sightseeing places in Andhra Pradesh. It is famous for its vivid flora and fauna, grand temples with beautiful architecture, sites of historical significance and lots more. In terms of romantic things to do in Chittoor, you can explore and soak in the magnificent natural beauty of the Horsley Hills with your better half. For a more adventurous experience, you can visit the Kaundinya Wildlife Sanctuary. Rajahmundry : Rajahmundry offers many romantic things and spots to visit. This includes spending quality time chatting with your loved one while taking a boat ride in the Godavari river. There are many other famous tourist points of interest around Rajahmundry that couples can check out, including Pushkar ghat and the natural beauty of Maredumilli. In terms of accommodations, you can find great rental properties and five-star hotel rooms in this location. Check out our Rajahmundry Andhra tour packages for a detailed idea. Manginapudi beach : Andhra Pradesh houses numerous beaches you can check out for your honeymoon. The Manginapudi beach is a famous place of Andhra Pradesh, just 11 km from Machilipatanam. It offers an astonishing beach experience and great visuals, making it a popular point of interest for couples. If you are in search of some great honeymoon tour packages in India or any part of the world, make sure to check out our professionally picked honeymoon packages. Best places to visit in Andhra Pradesh: Each location in Andhra Pradesh can offer you a unique perspective on the whole tourism aspect of the region. Here is a list of the most famous tourist places in Andhra Pradesh that you should add to your list: Tirupati : Tirupati city is one of the most popular sites of tourist attractions in Andhra Pradesh. It is widely regarded as the state’s spiritual capital and is home to various historic temples of the Hindu religion, including the Tirumala Venkateswara temple. Some other popular attractions in and near Tirupati are Kapila Theertham, Talakona Waterfall, ISKCON Tirupati, Chandragiri Palace and Fort, etc. Take a look at our Tirupati Andhra tour packages for a great deal. Amaravati : The de facto capital, Amaravati, is one of the best places to visit in Andhra Pradesh. It is an iconic place for individuals who are interested in Buddhism and ancient archaeological wonders. Some of the key attractions in and near Andhra Pradesh’s Amaravati include the Amaravati Stupa, Amaravati Museum, Amareswara Temple, Dhyana Buddha Statue, etc. Vijayawada : Vijayawada is a major hub for heritage sites, hills, ancient caves, local sightseeing locations, historic architecture and much more. Some of the most popular sites to check in and near Vijayawada includes the Undavalli caves, Bhavani island, Kondapalli fort, Besant road, Bapu Museum, Riverside Rajiv Gandhi Park, Kanaka Durga Temple, Mogalarajapuram Caves, etc. It ranks highly on the list of AP's best tourist places. Kurnool : The city of Kurnool is one of the best tourist places in Andhra Pradesh. It is a landmark for individuals interested in history and the state’s local culture. The best way to reach Kurnool is by road and train. Some iconic tourist attractions in and near Kurnool include Yaganti, Oravakallu Rock Garden, Mahanandi Temple, Belum Caves, Kurnool Fort, Ahobilam Temple, Mantralayam, and Buddha Statue. Visakhapatnam : Visakhapatnam is one of the most popular tourist places in Andhra Pradesh and is widely known as Vizag. It is a significant state destination that features many things to do and places to check out, making it a great spot for solo and group visits. Some of the most famous places in and near Andhra Pradesh’s Vizag are Rama Krishna Beach, Katiki Waterfalls, Borra Caves, Indira Gandhi Zoological Park, Rushikonda Beach, Kailasagiri, Yarada Beach, Submarine Museum, Dolphin’s Nose Lighthouse, VUDA Park, Visakha Museum, Ross Hill Church, Thotlakonda, etc. Guntur : Guntur ranks among the top famous places in Andhra Pradesh. It has a lot to show in terms of tourist attractions, and some examples in and near Guntur include Ethipothala Falls, Kondaveedu, Uppalapadu Nature Conservation, Kotappakonda Trikuta Parvat Gam, Mangalagiri, Downtown, Krishna Barrage and Suryalanka Beach. Best hill stations and beaches in Andhra Pradesh One of the best parts about taking a trip to Andhra Pradesh is that you will be able to see both mesmerising hill stations and spellbinding beaches. You can find more details about the best beaches and hill stations in Andhra Pradesh in the section below: Best beaches in Andhra Pradesh : Andhra Pradesh’s massive coastline is home to several popular beaches and attracts both domestic and international tourists. Many of the charming beaches in the state lie amidst the Visakhapatnam district. Here are some examples of the best beaches in Andhra Pradesh: Visakhapatnam beaches : The list of famous beaches in and near Vizag includes Rishikonda beach, Yarada beach, Bheemili beach, Gangavaram beach, Lawson’s Bay beach, Ramakrishna beach, etc. RK beach is one of the most popular tourist spots with a cool atmosphere and peaceful waters and features the INS Kurusura Submarine Museum. Check our Majestic Vizag Andhra Pradesh tour packages for a great deal. Other popular beaches : Some must-visit beaches and AP best tourist places include Manginapudi beach in Machilipatnam, Mypadu beach in Nellore, Uppada beach near Kakinada, etc. Best hill stations in Andhra Pradesh Besides beaches, Andhra Pradesh also covers some of the most famous hill stations in India. Here are some examples of the best hill stations in Andhra Pradesh: Araku valley- Andhra Pradesh’s Araku Valley is one of India's most mesmerising hill stations. It is located about 120 km from Vizag and is home to lush green forests, grand landscapes, and beautiful waterfalls. Some important must-see things in Araku valley include Katiki Falls, Tribal Museum, Borra caves, etc. Horsley hills- Chittoor district’s Horsley Hills ranks on the list of major tourist places in Andhra Pradesh. With an elevation of 1290 metres, this hill station features fascinating visuals and exuberant flora and fauna. Other popular hill stations- Some must-visit hill stations in Andhra Pradesh include Nagari hills, Maredumilli, Tirumala, Lambasingi, Nallamala hills, etc.

Some of the most famous places in and near Andhra Pradesh’s Vizag are Rama Krishna Beach, Katiki Waterfalls, Borra Caves, Indira Gandhi Zoological Park, Rushikonda Beach, Kailasagiri, Yarada Beach, Submarine Museum, Dolphin’s Nose Lighthouse, VUDA Park, Visakha Museum, Ross Hill Church, Thotlakonda, etc. Guntur : Guntur ranks among the top famous places in Andhra Pradesh. It has a lot to show in terms of tourist attractions, and some examples in and near Guntur include Ethipothala Falls, Kondaveedu, Uppalapadu Nature Conservation, Kotappakonda Trikuta Parvat Gam, Mangalagiri, Downtown, Krishna Barrage and Suryalanka Beach.
Andhra Pradesh.Best hill stations and beaches in Andhra: One of the best parts about taking a trip to Andhra Pradesh is that you will be able to see both mesmerising hill stations and spellbinding beaches. You can find more details about the best beaches and hill stations in Andhra Pradesh in the section below: Best beaches in Andhra Pradesh : Andhra Pradesh’s massive coastline is home to several popular beaches and attracts both domestic and international tourists. Many of the charming beaches in the state lie amidst the Visakhapatnam district. Here are some examples of the best beaches in Andhra Pradesh: Visakhapatnam beaches : The list of famous beaches in and near Vizag includes Rishikonda beach, Yarada beach, Bheemili beach, Gangavaram beach, Lawson’s Bay beach, Ramakrishna beach, etc. RK beach is one of the most popular tourist spots with a cool atmosphere and peaceful waters and features the INS Kurusura Submarine Museum. Check our Majestic Vizag Andhra Pradesh tour packages for a great deal. Other popular beaches : Some must-visit beaches and AP best tourist places include Manginapudi beach in Machilipatnam, Mypadu beach in Nellore, Uppada beach near Kakinada, etc. Best hill stations in Andhra Pradesh Besides beaches, Andhra Pradesh also covers some of the most famous hill stations in India. Here are some examples of the best hill stations in Andhra Pradesh: Araku valley- Andhra Pradesh’s Araku Valley is one of India's most mesmerising hill stations. It is located about 120 km from Vizag and is home to lush green forests, grand landscapes, and beautiful waterfalls. Some important must-see things in Araku valley include Katiki Falls, Tribal Museum, Borra caves, etc. Horsley hills- Chittoor district’s Horsley Hills ranks on the list of major tourist places in Andhra Pradesh. With an elevation of 1290 metres, this hill station features fascinating visuals and exuberant flora and fauna. Other popular hill stations- Some must-visit hill stations in Andhra Pradesh include Nagari hills, Maredumilli, Tirumala, Lambasingi, Nallamala hills, etc.
Andhra Pradesh.What to eat in Andhra Pradesh: Other than visiting the popular tourist sites in Andhra Pradesh, make sure to check out the wide variety of local cuisines the region has to offer. Here are some examples of must-try Andhra Pradesh delicacies: Pulihora : This can be described as a more spicy variant of tamarind rice and is a quite popular dish during festive seasons and family gatherings. Curry leaves, mustard seeds, tomatoes, and tamarind are the major ingredients of this Andhra delicacy, making up for a brilliant flavourful blend. Andhra Chicken Biryani : Biryani is a pretty self-explanatory dish. However, Andhra Pradesh’s chicken biryani brings forth a new meaning to this widely popular Indian cuisine. This delicacy is made using a well-forged mix of spices, aromatic herbs, an accent of vegetables, and juicy chicken. Gongura pickle Ambadi : The Gongura pickle Ambadi is an excellent must-try local Andhra Pradesh delicacy. It is made with Ambadi leaves and gives a unique spicy taste, which goes along with chapatis and rice. Pesarattu : This dish is one of the most famous breakfast items in Andhra Pradesh. It is a spicy version of dosa that tastes amazing, along with some coconut chutney. Chepa Pulusu : If you love fish, you will crave Andhra’s cuisine called Chepa Pulusu. It is a regional dish prepared with fish, tamarind, spicy herbs, curry leaves, and other ingredients. It has an extraordinary taste and is widely served across various restaurants in the state. What to shop in Andhra Pradesh? Local arts and handicrafts in Andhra Pradesh feature beautiful articles you can buy as gifts or souvenirs. Here are some examples: Budithi art : Andhra Pradesh’s Budithi artwork is famous around the globe. Local artisans use alloys to create both modern and traditional art pieces. Some examples include flower pots, cooking utensils, planters, etc. You can purchase Budithi artworks at Budithi village within the district of Srikakulam. Durgi stones : Durgi stones are exceptional works of art found in Guntur’s Durgi village. This style of carving is believed to have started around the 15th century AD, and artisans used soft limestone and granite to create these pieces. Some examples include carved idols, paperweights, lampshades, etc. Sarees : Andhra Pradesh’s local handworks include a variety of beautifully made sarees. Some examples are the cotton-made sarees of Mangalagiri town, silk-made Dharmavaram sarees of Anantapur district, and silk and cotton-made sarees of Pochampalli. Wooden crafts : Besides stone and metal carved art pieces, Andhra Pradesh also features exquisite wood-carved artworks. You can shop for it in Etikoppaka in Vizag district and Kondapalli. If you are in search of popular markets for shopping in Andhra Pradesh, you can check out Kurupam market, TSR complex, and other local shops and malls in Visakhapatnam. You can also check out the malls and markets on Eluru Road, MG Road, and Besant Road if you are looking to shop around in Vijayawada. You can refer to our page for more details on shopping in Andhra Pradesh. Things to do in Andhra Pradesh: Individuals looking for exciting and fun things to do in Andhra Pradesh will find it a perfect place. Here are some interesting things you can do in Andhra Pradesh: Soak in the beauty of the beaches : As you can see, Andhra Pradesh is filled with numerous serene beaches that are generally massive tourist attractions. If you are looking for a calming experience with a backdrop of the soothing sound of the waves, consider spending some time on the beaches of Andhra Pradesh. You can also take a romantic walk over the wet beach sand with a loved one as the sun sets for the day. Go on a city tour : The state is home to several popular and iconic cities in India, all of which can offer a completely different perspective on your journey. You can explore the cities and everything they have to offer at a pace you like. Some examples include going on a city tour of Majestic Vizag, Kurnool, Vijayawada, Guntur, etc. Visit religious places : There are hundreds of religious temples all across the state of Andhra Pradesh. Spending time visiting these temples can help widen your spiritual outlook and give some insights into the beautiful architectural works of ancient times. Some examples include the Mahanandi temple, Tirupati temple, Mallikarjuna Swamy temple, etc. Explore the historical sites : If you are a history buff, you can go on a quest to explore the various important historical places of Andhra Pradesh. Some examples include Amaravati, Lepakshi, Kurnool, Konda Reddy Buruju, Gooty Fort, Penukonda Fort, etc. Adventurous things to do in Andhra Pradesh: Apart from being one of the best tourist locations in India, Andhra Pradesh is also a major location for adventure tourism. Here are some examples: Camping : Andhra Pradesh also features many options in terms of camping. Some of the key camping locations include Gandikota, Araku Valley, Jadderu, Paderu, etc. Trekking : Many tourists throughout the year visit Andhra Pradesh for its unique trekking experience. From famous hill stations to natural scenic routes amidst forests, there are many locations in Andhra Pradesh where you can trek solo or with a group. Some key trekking locations include Ahobilam, Talakona Waterfalls, Kadalivanam Caves, Nagalapuram, etc. Water sports : Boat tours and adrenaline-pumping water sports are the major adventure activities in Andhra Pradesh. This includes jet skiing, waterskiing, parasailing, canyoning, rappelling, surfing, and much more. Gandikota and Suryalanka beach are the major sites for water adventures. Cave exploration : Cave exploration is yet another massively popular adventure activity in Andhra Pradesh. During these explorations, you can enjoy their natural beauty and satisfy your adventurer’s curiosity. Some examples of caverns and caves include Borra caves, Belum caves, Undavalli caves, Akka Mahadevi caves, and Mogalarajapuram caves. Wildlife adventure safari and tour : If you want to witness regional wildlife, which includes tigers, hyenas, black bucks, leopards, elephants, and so on, make sure to check out AP’s exciting adventure safari and tour. Some popular sites include Coringa wildlife sanctuary, Rollapandu wildlife sanctuary, Koundinya wildlife sanctuary, and Rayala Elephant Reserve. Interesting facts about Andhra Pradesh If you are interested in making Andhra Pradesh your next trip location, do extensive research before your visit to ensure that you don’t miss out on anything. Here are some interesting and lesser-known facts about Andhra Pradesh: Andhra Pradesh is the home to the world’s only pillar that remains suspended in the air. It is popularly referred to as “the hanging pillar” and is an integral part of the Lepakshi temple in a small village within the district of Anantapur. It is surrounded by mysteries and theories and is a popular tourist attraction. The de facto capital of Andhra Pradesh, Amaravati, is a small town situated within the district of Guntur. It is believed to have a heritage that is over 2000 years old, making it one of India’s oldest places. Lambasingi, a small village amidst the Eastern Ghats of Chintapalli Mandal, is the only place in all of south India that experiences snowfall. It is a famous hill station in the state and is often called the “Kashmir of Andhra Pradesh”. Some famous nearby Lambasingi tourist places include Kothapally Waterfalls, Susan Garden, and Thajangi Reservoir. The banyan tree called Thimmamma Marrimanu in Andhra Pradesh’s Anantapur district holds the Guinness world record for being the largest banyan tree, which was set back in 1989. Its canopy covers an area of around 19,107 square metres.

You can refer to our page for more details on shopping in Andhra Pradesh.

faq.question: Can I visit Mumbai with my family?
faq.answer: Yes. Mumbai is one of the most sought-after vacation spots in India, especially for family trips. You can also include it as a part of a bigger Maharashtra trip.

faq.question: Is Mumbai a suitable spot for honeymoons?
faq.answer: Yes. Mumbai boasts several tourist attractions, luxury resorts and romantic destinations for married couples, making it a great place for honeymoons. For the best tourism experience, opt for Mumbai trip packages by Thomas Cook designed for couples.

faq.question: What is the ideal duration for the Mumbai trip?
faq.answer: The ideal duration of a Mumbai tour should be between 4 to 5 days. You can find Mumbai trip packages on our page starting with just 2 Nights/ 3 Days, for short vacations. There are also Mumbai packages for longer holidays available on our website.

faq.question: How far is Mumbai from Lonavala?
faq.answer: The distance from Mumbai to Lonavala by road is around 82 kilometres. Find budget-friendly Mumbai packages featuring Lonavala on Thomas Cook.

faq.question: What are some of the best beaches in Mumbai?
faq.answer: Some of the top beaches in Mumbai that tourists can visit include Juhu Beach, Girgaum Chowpatty Beach, Versova Beach and Madh Island Beach.

faq.question: What are the top tourist attractions in Mumbai for history buffs?
faq.answer: Some of the top tourist attractions in Mumbai for history buffs are the Gateway of India, Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj Terminus, Elephanta Caves, Haji Ali Dargah, Kanheri Caves, etc.

faq.question: How much does a Mumbai trip cost?
faq.answer: The Mumbai trip cost can vary depending on the tour duration, total members, choice of accommodation and transfers, etc. Consider checking out Thomas Cook for affordable Mumbai packages. For reference, the Mumbai tour package price for a short 2-night/3-day trip is INR 17,599.

faq.question: What are some nearby hill stations from Mumbai?
faq.answer: Some of the best hill stations near Mumbai City are Lonavala, Mahabaleshwar, Matheran, Igatpuri, Panchgani, etc.

faq.question: How far is Mumbai from Pune?
faq.answer: Pune City is around 150 kilometres away by road from Mumbai City.

Provide the necessary details and complete online payment to confirm booking. This is how you can easily book Mumbai tour packages on Thomas Cook.

Mumbai.Introduction: Mumbai, previously called Bombay, is one of the most important cities in the country. This megacity is the capital of the Indian state of Maharashtra and is the de facto “financial capital of India”. Home to an estimated population of over 12.5 million, Mumbai is a massive centre of commerce, fashion, education and entertainment. It is also a renowned vacation spot, known for being a melting pot of diverse cultures and traditions, technological and urban developments, numerous natural and man-made tourist attractions, etc. It is popularly referred to by its several nicknames, like “the city of dreams” or “the city that never sleeps”, which gives a subtle hint of what tourists can expect during their visit. While on your Mumbai trip, you can find the essence of beauty and serenity hidden amidst the daily chaotic lifestyle of its residents. You will be able to enjoy its dynamic tourism experience with every street you take and every corner you pass.
Mumbai.Table of Contents: Top Trending Mumbai Tour Packages Best time to visit Mumbai How to reach Mumbai Best Places to Visit in Mumbai Best hill stations/Beaches in Mumbai Best honeymoon destinations in Mumbai Things to do in Mumbai Adventurous things to do in Mumbai What to Eat in Mumbai What to shop in Mumbai? Interesting facts about Mumbai People also ask about Mumbai Tour Packages
Mumbai.Most Popular Mumbai Tour Packages 2025: Tour Packages NameDurationPriceMumbai A Dream City2 Nights 3 DaysRs. ₹17 800 Maharashtra - Mumbai with Shirdi Darshan3 Nights 4 DaysRs. ₹23 100Maharashtra - City of Dream Mumbai with Nashik3 Nights 4 DaysRs. ₹23 400Maharashtra - Delight of Maharashtra4 Nights 5 DaysRs. ₹28 600Maharashtra - Scenic Lonavala with Mumbai4 Nights 5 DaysRs. ₹31 200 Whether you are looking for a fun time with your loved ones by the beach or want to learn about the country’s past, Mumbai travel opens up a gate of diverse experiences. The city boasts an array of remarkable tourist attractions ranging from sites of historical and religious importance to sites of modern-day entertainment and beaches. Alongside Mumbai sightseeing, tourists can indulge in various other things during their trip, such as trying out delicious and affordable street food, engaging in fun and adventurous activities, getting into the authentic shopping scene, etc. If you find this interesting, consider planning your trip with the Mumbai tour packages offered by Thomas Cook. These Mumbai packages are professionally designed with the aim of bringing the ultimate tourism experience to individuals without any compromises. From short holidays to long vacations, you can find all kinds of Mumbai tour packages that match your tourism needs on our website.
