As for further information regarding popular Mumbai packages, the ideal time to visit, the popular local dishes, the best Mumbai places to visit, etc., go through this page.
Mumbai.Best time to visit Mumbai: One of the most essential aspects of a Mumbai tour is knowing when to schedule it. While the city remains an ideal holiday destination for the good part of the year, there are some specific times when tourists can make the most out of their visit. So, before browsing the available Mumbai tour packages list and taking your pick, consider choosing the right time for your visit. Go through the following pointers to find a suitable time for Mumbai travel; Mumbai in summer The summer season in Mumbai starts around March and stretches till May. The days remain hot and humid, with an average temperature range of 28° to 35°C. The high humidity can cause some discomfort in terms of outdoor tourist activities, such as sightseeing and adventure activities, which can be a major dealbreaker. So, it is better to avoid scheduling your Mumbai tour during these months. On the other hand, summer is considered the best time to go to Mumbai for budget trips. Late May is considered the best month to visit Mumbai to engage in water adventure activities and try street foods like “golas” to beat the heat. Find Mumbai tour packages for all seasons on our website. Mumbai in monsoon Monsoon begins around June and lasts till September, bringing heavy downpours across the city. While the weather remains cool, the frequent rain showers can be really unpredictable. You can expect an average temperature range of 20°C to 30°C. Monsoon is considered a great time for Mumbai travel if you want to witness its remarkable natural beauty and elegance. Exploring the rain-soaked city streets, enjoying warm tea and snacks by the Marine Drive, spending some romantic time by the Worli Sea Face, etc., are some of the best things to do in Mumbai during these months. Many also consider monsoon the best season to visit Mumbai due to its comparatively lower tourist traffic. If you have scheduled your Mumbai tour during monsoon, make sure to pack your rain gear. Find Mumbai tour packages for the monsoon season on Thomas Cook. Mumbai in winter Winter, from October to February, is popularly regarded as the best season for Mumbai tours due to the favourable conditions. The clear sky and pleasant weather are ideal for visitors to engage in all kinds of outdoor tourist activities, such as Mumbai sightseeing, general exploration, adventure activities, etc. The average temperature range during these months stays around 17°C to 34°C. Tourists also get to participate in a variety of local festivals and events during this time, which solidifies winter as the best time to visit Mumbai for most kinds of tourism needs. If you are visiting around this period, consider packing warm clothes for the trip. Find great deals on Mumbai tour packages for winter months on our website.
Mumbai.How to reach Mumbai?: A major decision you have to make before looking into Mumbai tour packages is selecting the mode of transport for the trip. Most of the Mumbai package holidays by Thomas Cook are equipped with the choice of adding “optional flights” to make your trip go smoothly. Here are some of the best ways to reach Mumbai for a trip; By air The fastest way to reach Mumbai is by air. The city is home to the Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj International Airport, which is a major international airport serving the city and the MMR (Mumbai Metropolitan Region). It is located around 20 kilometres from Mumbai CST. The airport has two terminals, and major airlines, such as IndiGo, Spice Jet, Go Air, Jet Airways, etc., operate from here. The airport helps link Mumbai with other major cities in the country. For travel reference, a direct flight from Kolkata to Mumbai takes around 2 hours and 45 minutes, and from Bangalore, it only takes an hour and 40 minutes. If you have scheduled your Mumbai tour during the peak season, make sure to book flights and hotels in advance. By rail Train is another popular transport mode that you can opt for during your Mumbai travel. It has a great rail network, and travelling to the city by train is both economical and fast, making Mumbai tourism accessible for many. The two major rail terminuses in the city are the Chhatrapati Shivaji Terminus (CST) and the Mumbai Central Railway Station. You can easily find regular and weekly long-distance trains operating on these routes, connecting Mumbai to major Indian cities like Chennai, Hyderabad, Delhi, Kolkata, etc. By road Tourists planning a Mumbai trip from within Maharashtra or a neighbouring state can consider road travel to reach this famous city. It features a great road network, which links to major cities in India. These roads are well-maintained, creating a great setting for a memorable trip. The major highways passing through the state connect Mumbai to states like Delhi, Gujarat, Andhra Pradesh, etc. You can also travel to Mumbai by bus if you wish to.
Mumbai.Best places to visit in Mumbai: When planning a Mumbai tour, consider making a list of all the places you want to cover during your visit. It will help you choose the right planned trip from the wide list of offered Mumbai package holidays. Here are some of the best places to visit in Mumbai Gateway of India The Gateway of India is one of the top places to visit in Mumbai, around 23 kilometres from the airport. This famous arch-gateway, inaugurated in 1924, is a remarkable monument that showcases the Indo-Islamic architecture of that era. It used to serve as a symbolic ceremonial entrance to India for important British officials before independence. Hire a Mumbai tour guide if you want to know more about this tourist attraction. Explore the Gateway of India and other well-known tourist attractions with the Mumbai package holidays by Thomas Cook. Sanjay Gandhi National Park Covering an area of 87 square kilometres, the Sanjay Gandhi National Park is among the best places to see in Mumbai. Found as a standard inclusion in several Mumbai package holidays, the park is a great site for wildlife enthusiasts and picnickers. Major animals found within the park include tigers, leopards, crocodiles, etc. So, consider opting for Mumbai package holidays that feature a visit to this popular park. Consider hiring a Mumbai tour guide if you want to enhance your experience visiting SGNP. Marine Drive The iconic Marine Drive is one of the best tourist places in Mumbai. It is a 3.6-kilometre-long, arc-shaped promenade offering some of the most alluring panoramic views in the city. Tourists love to spend some peaceful time at this renowned hangout spot, in the company of friends and hot tea. Make sure to look for Mumbai package holidays featuring a visit to the Marine Drive. Powai Lake Powai Lake is an incredibly beautiful man-made lake, just 10 km from Mumbai Airport. Included in the itinerary of many Mumbai package holidays, the lake is among the best places to see in Mumbai. Enjoying the beauty and serenity by the lakeside with your loved ones is one of the best things to do during a Mumbai trip. Enjoy the well-known charms and wonders of the city with the Mumbai package holidays presented by Thomas Cook. Elephanta Caves History buffs can look into Mumbai package holidays featuring a trip to the renowned Elephanta Caves. It is a well-known tourist attraction on Gharapuri Island, approximately 10 kilometres by water from Mumbai City. It is a UNESCO World Heritage Site featuring a collection of cave temples dedicated to Hindu deities, mainly Lord Shiva, and a few Buddhist caves. You can find various elaborate artworks of gods and goddesses at this site, such as Trimurti, Ardhnareshwar, Gangadhar, etc. Learn more about the place when visiting by hiring a Mumbai tour guide. Most popular Mumbai package holidays feature a sightseeing trip to the Elephanta Caves.
Mumbai.Best hill stations/ best beaches in Mumb: One of the best parts of Mumbai tourism is the diverse range of attractions it has to offer. So, consider picking from the best Mumbai holiday packages that let you enjoy your dream vacation. Here are some of the best hill stations and beaches in and near Mumbai; Beaches Juhu Beach Besides being the longest beach in the city, Juhu Beach is also one of the top Mumbai tourist attractions. It is a great place to hang out and watch the sunset with your friends and loved ones. Tons of celebrities live in the nearby locality, and the street food in the area deserves separate praise. If interested, look for Mumbai holiday packages featuring a Juhu Beach visit. Versova Beach Versova Beach is one of the top places to visit in Mumbai. Though small, the tranquil ambience and soothing panoramic views create a delightful vibe. People visiting the nearby Versova Fort like to drop by this beach to spend quality time with their families. Explore Versova Beach and other renowned tourist places in the city with the Mumbai holiday packages by Thomas Cook. Girgaum Chowpatty Girgaum Chowpatty is one of the most famous tourist places in Mumbai. It is a public beach along the Queen’s Necklace and a usual hangout spot for locals and tourists. Those visiting Marine Drive can’t simply miss checking out Girgaum Chowpatty. The street food here is amazing, and you can also opt for a horse or camel ride along the sandy beach. You can easily find this beach featured in many popular Mumbai holiday packages. Hill stations Lonavala The twin hill station, Lonavala-Khandala, is one of the most famous and easily accessible weekend getaways near Mumbai. Located at an elevation of 624 m, it is just a 2-hour drive away from the city. The dense greenery, peaceful atmosphere, serene lakes, towering hills, historic forts and caves, etc., are some of the main attractions of Lonavala-Khandala. You can easily find Mumbai holiday packages that also feature Lonavala. Matheran The automobile-free, quaint hill station, Matheran, is just 83 kilometres from Downtown Mumbai. Nestled at an elevation of 800 m in the Sahyadri range of Western Ghats, the place is known for its unblemished natural beauty and jaw-dropping scenic views. The main attractions of Matheran are Louisa Point, Charlotte Lake, One Tree Hill Point, Panorama Point, etc. Explore the remarkable nearby hill stations during your visit with the Mumbai holiday packages by Thomas Cook.
Mumbai.Best honeymoon destinations in Mumbai: Mumbai is one of the most sought-after honeymoon destinations in Maharashtra. There are tons of romantic places in and near the city, perfect for newly married couples to visit. Here are some of the best spots that you can visit while on a honeymoon in Mumbai; Hanging Gardens The Hanging Gardens is one of Mumbai's best places for couples. Built in 1880 by Mr Ulhas Ghapokar, it is a beautiful terrace garden on the top of Malabar Hill, overlooking the Arabian Sea. It is also known as Pherozeshah Mehta Gardens and is just opposite the famous Kamala Nehru Park. Honeymooners love to spend some time amidst the peace and beauty of this place. Enjoy a memorable vacation with your significant other with the honeymoon special Mumbai holiday packages by Thomas Cook. Chota Kashmir Honeymooners on their Mumbai trip can consider visiting the renowned Chota Kashmir. It is one of the most beautiful places in Mumbai, located at the centre of the Aarey Colony. It is a stunning garden spread over a wide area, featuring lush greenery and a tranquil atmosphere. It is also a highly sought-after picnic spot in the city. Besides spending quality time with your loved ones, consider opting for a romantic boat ride in the massive “Kashmir Lake”, which spreads over 4 acres of land. Make your honeymoon special with the couple’s Mumbai holiday packages on our website. Madh Island In recent times, Madh Island has become one of the top Mumbai best places for couples. The place features a cluster of quaint villages, beautiful vistas and vast green farmlands. Honeymooners love to spend a few days at one of the several available nearby hotels and luxury resorts. Some of the major attractions near Madh Island that couples can check out include the Madh Fort, the Madh Church, Killeshwar Mahadev Temple, etc. Browse through our list of top Mumbai holiday packages to find a plan that suits your vacation needs.
Mumbai.Things to do in Mumbai: Alongside looking for suitable Mumbai holiday packages, consider adding fun activities to your trip itinerary to maximise your tour experience. Here are some fun things to do in Mumbai; Visit Mumbai Film City One of the most popular things to do in Mumbai is to go on a Film City tour. The Film City in Mumbai is located in the Goregaon (East) neighbourhood, close to the famous Aarey Colony. Covering a massive area of 520 acres, it is among the top Mumbai places to visit, especially if you want to learn more about Indian cinema and the process of filmmaking. Make sure to dedicate a day or a half to this tour while on your Mumbai travel. Many popular Mumbai trip packages feature a Film City tour as a standard or additional inclusion. Watch the sunset at Worli Sea Face Individuals looking for slow-paced fun activities while on their Mumbai tour can consider going sunset-watching. It is one of the most romantic things to do in Mumbai, and there are several ideal locations for this activity. However, witnessing the breathtaking views of the sunset at Worli Sea Face as the sea waves lash at the shoreline is a mesmerising experience of its own. It is also among the top cool places to visit in Mumbai for nightlife. Enjoy your vacation to the fullest with the Mumbai trip packages by Thomas Cook. Go on a ferry ride at Gateway of India Taking a ferry ride at the famous landmark “the Gateway of India” is among the best things to do in Mumbai. While on this ride, you will also be able to witness several other major iconic landmarks in the vicinity, such as the Taj Mahal Hotel Palace. The ferry rides are often equipped with a knowledgeable Mumbai tour guide who will state additional facts about these landmarks and other things, making the ride interesting. The Mumbai trip packages available on our website are designed to make your vacation a memorable one.
Mumbai.Adventurous things to do in Mumbai: Besides casual fun and slow-paced activities, tourists on their Mumbai tour can take their trip to the next level by engaging in various thrilling adventures. Some Mumbai trip packages feature adventure activities as a standard inclusion. Here are some of the most noteworthy adventure things to do in Mumbai; Enjoy thrilling rides at an amusement park The city of Mumbai is home to several prominent amusement parks, designed for people of all age groups. Enjoying thrilling rides at one of these parks is among the top adventurous things to do while on your Mumbai trip. One such place is the Essel World, which is among the most well-known amusement parks in the city. Go-karting If you are looking for an adventure activity where you can challenge your friends in a head-to-head competition, consider Go-karting. It is one of the most thrilling activities you can do while on your Mumbai Cruise travel, popular among both locals and tourists. Zooming through the race track while taking sharp corners in a go-kart makes for an amazing time. The best Mumbai tourist places to visit for this activity are Powai, Wadala, Chembur and Andheri West. Watersports Mumbai offers great opportunities in terms of watersports and adventures. One of the best places in Mumbai where you can engage in such activities is the famous Juhu Beach. A few examples of watersports available at this beach include jet skiing, banana boat riding, speed boat riding, sofa boat riding, Ringo boat riding, parasailing, etc. Enjoy your time in the city with fun adventures and other activities by going with the Mumbai trip packages by Thomas Cook.
Mumbai.What to eat in Mumbai?: While looking for suitable Mumbai trip packages, consider making a list of must-try local dishes for your visit. A Mumbai trip is never complete without indulging in the wide range of delicious dishes. Here are some of the best foods to try on a Mumbai tour; Batata Vada A very popular local food item that tourists can try on their Mumbai trip is Batata Vada. Whether it is for breakfast or a quick snack, this delicious food item is suitable for all occasions. It is a type of dumpling-shaped fast food, made using mashed boiled potatoes and a mixture of lime juice, coriander leaves, green chillies, etc. It is then dipped in gram flour batter and fried to give it a crunchy texture. Ragda Pattice Another mouth-watering local dish to try on a Mumbai trip is Ragda Pattice. It is a dish prepared by combining fried “pattice”, made of mashed potato, and Ragda, spicy rugged-flavoured chickpeas. It is best consumed along with sweet and spicy chutney and chopped onions. Vada Pav Vada Pav is one of the most iconic dishes in Mumbai that you can try. It is a go-to street food for all Mumbaikers, satisfying hunger on a budget. This dish mainly contains a Vada, which is made using mashed boiled potatoes and a few other ingredients, stuffed inside soft buns. The recipe changes from shop to shop, so consider trying a few. Ask your Mumbai tour guide for the best places to visit for delicious Vada Pavs. The Bombay Sandwich As the name suggests, the Bombay Sandwich is an authentic dish in the city that tourists can try during their Mumbai tour. It is a type of sandwich made using buttered white bread, which contains slices of cucumbers, boiled potatoes, tomatoes, onion rings and a few other items. It is then cut into small triangles and grilled, to add crunchiness to its already mouthwatering taste. Puran Poli Another local delicacy to try on your Mumbai trip is Puran Poli. It is a simple yet widely popular dish, usually prepared on festive occasions. Puran Poli is a type of flatbread, made by mixing sugar or jaggery (gur) and yellow gram (chana dal). A few spices are added to enhance its taste, and it is then mixed with wheat flour dough and rolled out to be roasted on a flat pan.
Mumbai.What to shop in Mumbai?: Make sure to go for Mumbai trip packages that give you opportunities to take part in the Mumbai shopping experience. Here are some of the best things to buy and markets to visit in Mumbai; Clothing and accessories If you are into fashion, the street shops and major markets in Mumbai will be the right place for you to visit. You can find nearly anything in the city, from branded clothes to traditional, block-printed sarees, available in all kinds of colours, quality and budget. You can also shop for other fashion accessories, such as handbags, sunglasses, hand-woven shawls, etc., at these markets. Where to buy: Colaba Causeway Market is one of the best places to visit in Mumbai for this. Art Mumbai is a city of creative minds, and people from all over the globe visit this place to purchase top-quality art pieces from local artists. Whether it is paintings, sculptures, or artefacts, you can find some of the finest works in the city that you can purchase as gifts or souvenirs. Where to buy: One of the best places to visit in Mumbai for art shopping is Kala Ghoda. Jewellery If you are looking for traditional and impressive jewellery works, Mumbai is the right place for you. From major outlets to small family-owned businesses, find the latest fashion statement in terms of jewellery in the city. You can also find inexpensive streetwear jewellery if you are shopping on a budget. Where to buy: Zaveri Bazaar is one of the top Mumbai places to visit for jewellery shopping.
Mumbai.Interesting facts about Mumbai: Before you look into the offered Mumbai trip packages, consider checking these lesser-known facts about Mumbai to get a better picture of the destination; Today’s Mumbai is an amalgamation of 7 islands. Originally, Worli, Mahim, Parel, Little Colaba, Mazagaon, Colaba and the Isle of Bombay comprised Bombay. The first city in the country to have an active bus service is Mumbai in Maharashtra. The Sanjay Gandhi National Park is home to the famous Kanheri Caves, which is believed to be over 2400 years old. The city of Mumbai is home to several UNESCO World Heritage Sites, such as the Victorian and Art Deco Ensemble of Mumbai, the Elephanta Caves and the Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj Terminus. Founded in 1928, the Juhu Aerodrome in Mumbai was the first airport in India. It is also the oldest airport in India. The first-ever passenger train in India ran from Mumbai. It was on April 16, 1853, covering a 34-km route between Bori Bunder (Bombay) and Thane. The second-most expensive house in the world is in Mumbai. It is called “Antilia”, which is the residence of the famous Indian businessman Mukesh Ambani. The largest slum on the continent is located in Mumbai. It is called “Dharavi”, which gives a contrasting but unique perspective to tourists on the lifestyle in the city. The well-known author of “The Jungle Book”, Rudyard Kipling, was born in Mumbai City. The Film City in Mumbai covers a massive area of 520 acres and features 20 indoor studios. It is so vast that around 1000 film sets can be built there at once. Roughly 900 films and several TV shows have been filmed at this location since its dawn.

faq.question: In South Africa, which month is the hottest?
faq.answer: February is the warmest month of the year, with an average maximum temperature of 26.5°C (79.7°F).

faq.question: What is the best time to visit South Africa?
faq.answer: The best time for a South Africa trip is from May to September, when the northern regions' cooler winter months provide ideal viewing conditions for a South Africa tour. From November to February, it can be rainy, but it's the best time to go birding, and temperatures stay warm at 22°C to 33°C.

faq.question: Is South Africa safe to visit?
faq.answer: There is a lot of crime in South Africa, including rape and murder. The risk of violent crime for visitors traveling to popular tourist destinations is usually low. The South African government places a high priority on protecting tourists, and tourism police are stationed in a number of cities.

faq.question: Which is the best month to visit Cape Town?
faq.answer: The months of March to May, as well as September to November, are ideal for a visit to Cape Town. These shoulder seasons are known for their beautiful weather, fewer crowds, and lower prices on South Africa packages.

faq.question: How many days are enough for a South Africa trip?
faq.answer: A South Africa trip lasting 8-10 days is ideal.

faq.question: Which are the must-visit places in South Africa?
faq.answer: Table Mountain, Bloukrans Bridge, Cape Peninsula Tour, Cango Wildlife Ranch, and other attractions are must-visit places in South Africa. Of course, you should not overlook Game Drives in a Safari Park.

faq.question: Is a visa required for Indian citizens to visit South Africa?
faq.answer: Yes, Indian citizens require a visa to visit South Africa. So, if you want to visit India on a tourist visa, you'll need to apply for one.

faq.question: Is South Africa a good place to go on a honeymoon?
faq.answer: South Africa is well-known throughout the world as one of the most romantic honeymoon destinations. Both Cape Town and the Cape Peninsula are worthwhile destinations for a romantic getaway. The city will never cease to amaze you with its best nightlife, thrilling adventure activities, and endless scenic beauty, and the food and wine scene is perfect.

faq.question: Do I need a visa for a South Africa tour?
faq.answer: If you want to visit South Africa, you must have a valid visa in order to enter the country. A visa will be issued for a visit for tourism or business, and it will be valid for 90 days. You can apply for a Visitor's Visa or a Transit Visa depending on your intended visit.

faq.question: What are the languages spoken in South Africa?
faq.answer: There are eleven major languages spoken in South Africa: Afrikaans, English, Swazi, Sotho, Swazi, Ndebele, Venda, Zulu, Northern Sotho, Tsonga, and Xhosa.

Spring (September to November): One of the best time to visit South Africa is around this time. Particularly in the Western Cape, wildflowers are in bloom, and the temperature is lovely. With fewer tourists, it's also a fantastic time to see animals. During this period, a lot of South Africa tour packages provide itineraries that emphasise the natural world. Winter (June to August): The best time to visit South Africa for a wildlife safari is during this dry season. Because animals congregate near waterholes, it is simpler to identify the Big Five. South Africa.How To Reach South Africa?: It's thrilling to plan your trip to South Africa, and it's made easier if you know how to reach South Africa. Here's how to get to this beautiful country, whether your goals are a cultural exploration, a coastal getaway, or an exhilarating safari with the best things to do in South Africa. 1. By train Unfortunately, India and South Africa do not have direct train connections. International train travel from India is not feasible due to the great distance and topography, even though South Africa itself has a good rail network between major cities. Therefore, if you're thinking about South Africa tour packages, train travel is only a possibility after you're in the country. It's excellent for short-distance travel between popular South Africa tourist spots and for picturesque roads. 2. By Road Although it is technically possible, travelling by car across the Middle East and Africa to get to South Africa is quite impractical. Thousands of kilometres, difficult terrain, and several foreign borders, including the Sahara Desert, would all be part of the adventure. Once you're in South Africa, road travel is best used to explore the sights. Therefore, you shouldn't rely on this route for your South Africa package tour. 3. By Air Flying is the most practical method to begin your visit to South Africa. From places like Delhi, Mumbai, and Chennai, major airlines like Emirates, Qatar Airways, and British Airways have connecting flights to Johannesburg and Cape Town. With layovers, the average flight lasts between 13 and 16 hours. To get a complete perspective of the Cape and the surrounding waters, you can trek or ride a cable car. Kruger National Park: Kruger is typically at the top of the list of South Africa tour packages. It's among the greatest locations for Big Five sightings and exhilarating wildlife safaris across expansive terrain. Blyde River Canyon: This verdant canyon, which is one of the biggest in the world and is a part of the picturesque Panorama Route, is a true gem when it comes to South Africa guided tours and sights to visit. Robben Island: One of the most famous places in South Africa is this historic island. You may experience Nelson Mandela's legacy and the nation's struggle for independence up close on a ferry voyage. Valley of Desolation: This is one of the most unusual places to see in South Africa if you're looking for striking scenery. The ethereal atmosphere created by towering rock formations and deep valleys is ideal for adventurers. Beaches/ Hill Stations to Visit Here are some of the South Africa tourist places with beaches and hill stations: Boulders Beach: Do you want to go swimming with penguins? South Africa.South African Currency: Understanding the local currency is crucial when organising your trip to South Africa so that you may make sensible financial decisions and have a hassle-free trip. The South African Rand, represented by the letter R and denoted by the international currency symbol ZAR, is the currency used in South Africa. Coins with denominations of 10c, 20c, 50c, R1, R2, and R5 are available, as are banknotes with denominations of R10, R20, R50, R100, and R200. To add an element of exploration to your stay, a Port Blair package frequently includes ferry journeys to other islands. Marine Life and Adventure A tour package to Port Blair will not let you down, whether you enjoy snorkelling, scuba diving, or simply looking at coral reefs. Numerous Port Blair holiday packages include guided water activities, and the undersea environment is very amazing. Ideal for Everybody There are things to keep everyone busy, whether you're travelling with children or planning a family vacation to Port Blair. Port Blair packages for couples include beautiful sunsets, candlelit meals by the sea, and seclusion. The best part? The Port Blair trip cost is very adjustable; depending on your budget, you can arrange a luxurious retreat or a more straightforward holiday. To put it briefly, Port Blair allows you to customise your vacation to your liking.
Port Blair.Planning Your Port Blair Trip: Essential: Having a good understanding of the necessities is helpful when organising your Port Blair vacation package. Knowing the ideal time to travel, how to get there, where to stay, and any permits you might need will make your trip go much more smoothly. Let's see all the information you need to know before reserving your Port Blair vacation packages. Best Time to Visit Port Blair The tropical environment of Port Blair, which can be both hospitable and surprising, is what makes it so charming. Because of the bright skies and good weather, October through May is the most popular time of year. The best time to visit Port Blair for outdoor sightseeing, water activities, and visiting neighbouring islands is during this peak season, which runs particularly from October to February. Finding a decent Port Blair package at a fair price is made easier from March to May, when the temperature rises but the crowds disperse. The monsoon season, which runs from June to September, is best avoided unless you don't mind slower travel because of the heavy rains and choppy seas that frequently result in ferry cancellations. How to Reach Port Blair? Flying into Veer Savarkar International Airport is the fastest and most practical option; direct flights are offered from Chennai, Kolkata, and Bengaluru, and there are also linked flights from Delhi and Mumbai. Although the trip takes three to four days, passenger ships depart from Chennai, Kolkata, and Visakhapatnam for those who would rather take their time. When included in a Port Blair tour package, Port Blair can be an adventure in and of itself. Some luxury cruises also include Port Blair in their itineraries, giving visitors multiple options for how to reach Port Blair comfortably and conveniently. Accommodation & Food Options There are many options available, regardless of your budget. Budget-friendly guesthouses and cottages are available for those looking for simplicity, while luxury resorts and boutique hotels are available for those booking premium Port Blair vacation packages. While honeymooners seek sea-view accommodations, which are sometimes included in Port Blair packages for family frequently choose budget motels near jetties. The food is a feature here, with easy access to international cuisines, Indian specialities, and fresh seafood. Port Blair tour packages are stress-free when it comes to meals because a lot of eateries provide vegetarian, Jain and pure-vegetarian cuisines. Travel Permits & Local Regulations While a valid government ID is required for check-in, Indian citizens do not need to have permission in order to enter Port Blair. It's crucial to abide by the stringent prohibition against entering tribal lands. A Restricted Area Permit (RAP), which is often granted at the airport or seaport upon arrival and is valid for 30 days with potential extensions, is required for foreign visitors. Both a current passport and an Indian visa are necessary. Before deciding on your Port Blair vacation packages, be aware that internet access can be inconsistent and that boat timings can be impacted by unforeseen weather conditions. Always spend a little extra and include some buffer days in case last-minute adjustments drive up the expense of your Port Blair trip. Your Port Blair family vacation or romantic Port Blair couple's vacation will become a journey full of treasured memories if you arrange it properly.
Port Blair.Curated Port Blair tour packages by Thom: Introduction to Packages Port Blair is frequently the first place that springs to mind when you think of the Andaman, and Thomas Cook ensures that it becomes more than simply a starting point. Without worrying about the specifics, you can enjoy the ideal fusion of history, beaches, and island charm with well-crafted Port Blair trip packages. There is a Port Blair package that is ideal for you, whether you are travelling alone, with a significant other, or with family, because each one is designed with a variety of travel preferences and price ranges in mind. Package Categories Port Blair packages for couple Couples and honeymooners can anticipate romantic cruises, candlelight meals by the beach, and accommodations at resorts with a view of the ocean. These carefully planned Port Blair vacation packages provide seclusion, comfort, and treasured memories. Port Blair packages for family Itineraries that strike a balance between leisure and sightseeing are ideal if you're organising a family vacation. These Port Blair travel packages include cosy accommodations, age-appropriate food choices, and excursions to sites including Cellular Jail, Ross Island, and Corbyn's Cove. Theme-based Holidays From luxurious getaways to affordable vacations, every Port Blair vacation package is designed to fit your mood. Thomas Cook offers choices to suit your preferences, whether you're looking for adventure, total leisure, or a cultural immersion. Individual or Group Getaways There are Port Blair tour packages that emphasise adventure and local culture for lone travellers or groups of friends. Consider island hopping, scuba diving, or exploring secret shops and cafes at your own speed.
Port Blair.Top Attractions & Experiences in Port Bl: Port Blair is the first place that springs to mind when you think of the Andamans; it is a place where culture, history, and scenic beauty all coexist. There is a lot to choose from here, whether you are arranging your trip using one of the many Port Blair tour packages or planning your own itinerary, knowing the top places to visit in Port Blair can help you make the most of your stay. From historic landmarks to natural wonders, there is a lot to explore here. Let’s see the eight must-see sights that add value to any Port Blair vacation package. Ross Island Ross Island transports you back in time and is only a short boat trip away. Giant trees now entwine the remains of colonial bakeries, churches, and government buildings. It's a weird experience, with friendly deer and peacocks roaming freely. This is a day trip that is included in many Port Blair trip package, and it is well worth the time. Cellular Jail A trip to the famous Cellular Jail is a must for any Port Blair itinerary. It used to house freedom fighters during British control and is also known as Kala Pani. Walking through its hallways and taking in the Sound and Light Show, which is an emotional recounting of India's fight for freedom, will give you chills. Corbyn's Cove beach This crescent-shaped beach is nearest to Port Blair town and is surrounded by swaying coconut palms. It's ideal for leisurely swimming, jet skiing, parasailing, and more. The Japanese bunkers along the route give your beach trip a touch of the past. Due to its convenient location and vibrant environment, this beach is frequently included in family vacation packages to Port Blair. North Bay Island Known as "Coral Island," this location is a water lover's paradise. Scuba diving, snorkelling, and even the uncommon sea walk experience are available. The lighthouse, which you will also recognise from the Indian 20 rupee note, is not to be missed. North Bay gives the perfect amount of adventure to any Port Blair vacation package. Naval Marine Museum of Samudrika This museum, which is kept up by the Navy, allows you to see the marine biodiversity of the Andaman Islands up close. The five galleries here offer a more thorough understanding of the ecosystem of the islands, including everything from corals and shells to vibrant fish species. The Marine National Park of Mahatma Gandhi This park, close to Wandoor, is a treasure for anyone who loves the outdoors and wildlife. It is made up of 15 islands and allows you to jump to Red Skin Island and Jolly Buoy, which are both known for their immaculate reefs. Children love the glass-bottom boat excursions here, which makes it a great option for Port Blair packages for families and couples. Chidiya Tapu Bird Island is the place to go if you want to see beautiful sunsets. Birdwatchers will be able to see a variety of species here, and the hike to Munda Pahad rewards you with expansive views of the coast. This is frequently the focal point of couples' Port Blair packages. Anthropological Museum This museum is a must-see if you're interested in culture. It showcases the crafts, artwork, and customs of indigenous groups, such as the Onges and Jarawas. Short documentaries add depth to your Port Blair vacation package, making the trip even more enlightening.
Port Blair.Things to Do in Port Blair Beyond Sights: Beyond history and beaches, Port Blair offers adventure, one-of-a-kind experiences, and the chance to fully enjoy island life. Selecting from the several Port Blair tour packages will reveal that the true allure of this location is found in its activities rather than its sights. Let's look at several fascinating pursuits that go much beyond standard sightseeing. Barren Island Volcano Imagine travelling 135 kilometres across the Andaman Sea to see the only active volcano in India. Even though you are unable to enter the island, it is a bizarre sight to see smoke billowing from its rocky slopes. Dolphin swimming is a fun addition to any Port Blair package because dolphins frequently swim near your boat. Baratang Island There are many surprises to be found in Baratang. It's exciting for both children and explorers, with attractions like limestone caverns, bubbling mud volcanoes, and even a ferry journey through mangrove creeks. It's like getting three experiences in one when you include Baratang in your Port Blair vacation package. Adventures in Trekking The 16-kilometre hike from Mount Harriet to Madhuban is ideal if you enjoy hiking on natural pathways. Your route is lined with rare birds, dense woodlands, and vibrant butterflies. Chidiya Tapu offers beautiful pathways for a shorter climb that culminate in one of the most breathtaking sunsets you will ever witness. This walk is a hidden gem that is highlighted in many Port Blair holiday packages. Kayaking in Bioluminescence at Night Here, every paddle stroke illuminates the sea. The phytoplankton illuminates the seas on a new moon night, giving the area below you a mystical, starry appearance. Long after your Port Blair vacation package is over, it will remain in your memory. Water Adventures One of the best things to do at Corbyn's Cove is scuba diving. Try a semi-submarine trip, which is a highlight of most family vacation packages to Port Blair, if diving seems too risky. This journey allows you to sit comfortably while viewing coral reefs via large glass windows. The self-drive Sea-Kart is an exhilarating activity that adds a particular touch to any Port Blair packages for couples. Savour regional markets and flavours Food and shopping are essential components of any trip. Port Blair has something for everyone, from vegetarian favourites at Annapurna to seafood delights at New Lighthouse Restaurant. Remember the Aberdeen Bazaar, which is a great place to buy handicrafts made from bamboo and shells. Take a Shot at Game Fishing Anglers will find heaven in the waters surrounding Port Blair. The excitement of sport fishing here, which includes Giant Trevally, tuna, and marlin, gives your vacation a special flavour.
Port Blair.Essential Tips for a Smooth Port Blair T: It's thrilling to plan your trip to Port Blair, but a little planning will help your trip run smoothly. These pointers can help you steer clear of typical travel pitfalls, whether you're creating a custom plan or booking Port Blair tour packages for the first time. Get Ferry Tickets Securely Early Ferries are Havelock and Neil's lifeline when it comes to island hopping. Tickets sell out fast during the busiest times of the year. If ferries are included in your Port Blair vacation package, confirm reservations in advance to prevent last-minute anxiety. Select the Proper Season The best weather occurs throughout the dry months of October through May, when the skies are clear and the waves are calm. When deciding on your Port Blair package, time is crucial because monsoons can cause delays in ferry schedules and water sports. Make a Well-Balanced Travel Plan You have ample time to see the main attractions without hurrying if you stay for six or seven days. Always allow for weather-related delays by including a buffer day, as most carefully planned Port Blair trip package do. Keep the Correct Documents on You Indian tourists only require a legitimate photo ID, not a PAN card. Foreign nationals need to maintain their passports and visas and stay up to date on the most recent RAP regulations. A lot of travel packages to Port Blair walk you through this procedure. Be Ready for Restricted Internet Access Mobile and internet signals are inconsistent outside of Port Blair town. Take advantage of this opportunity to decompress from technology and concentrate on experiences rather than screens. Preserve Cash Not all places will accept Handy Cards, particularly smaller islands. Because ATMs can run out of money, be sure you have enough before you leave. You can better control the cost of your trip to Port Blair by accounting for minor charges. Pack Wisely Essentials include sunscreen, insect repellent, beachwear, sturdy sandals, and light cotton clothing. When you're on a boat, a waterproof bag is invaluable because it protects your electronics and your memories. Make Sensible Transport Choices While taxis are more effective for full-day tours, autorickshaws are useful for quick trips. Scooter rentals on Havelock and Neil are affordable and enjoyable, and this is frequently mentioned in Port Blair travel packages. Honour the Islands Steer clear of plastic bags, keep your hands off corals, and practice eco-friendly behaviour. Never try to penetrate tribal territories and show consideration for cultural sensitivities.
Port Blair.Why Book Your Port Blair Tour with Thoma: Making a reservation with Thomas Cook makes your trip to the ideal island retreat simple. From flights and transportation to accommodations and attractions, their Port Blair tour packages are designed to handle every aspect so you can concentrate on taking in the golden beaches and blue oceans. Intimate Port Blair packages for couples are available to add that additional spark to your vacation, while families can choose carefully created Port Blair packages for the whole family. The best aspect? Expert support is provided for every Port Blair travel package, saving you time, frustration, and needless logistical concerns. Thomas Cook's Port Blair holiday packages are an intelligent and dependable option because of their clear pricing, which lets you know how much your trip will cost up front.

Shillong.Best Time to Visit in Shillong: Deciding the best time to visit Shillong is crucial to fully enjoy its numerous attractions. Here’s a season-wise guide to help you plan your trip and make the most of the Shillong tourist places. Season Months Temperature (°C) Highlights Best for Spring March to April 15°C to 22°C Blooming flowers, clear skies, vibrant landscapes, Ward’s Lake, Lady Hydari Park, Shillong Peak Exploring top attractions like Ward’s Lake, Lady Hydari Park, and Shillong Peak; outdoor activities like boating and hiking. Summer May to June 18°C to 25°C Cool and comfortable climate, lush greenery, Elephant Falls, Umiam Lake, local markets Sightseeing at Elephant Falls, Umiam Lake, and local markets; enjoying extensive tours without the discomfort of heat. Shillong.How to reach Shillong?: Reaching Shillong, the charming capital of Meghalaya, is an essential part of planning your visit. Whether you're opting for Shillong tour packages, a personalised Shillong trip plan, or just a spontaneous getaway, knowing the best modes of transportation is key to a smooth journey. Here's a comprehensive guide on how to reach Shillong through various means. Mode of Transport Details By Air Nearest Airport: Lokpriya Gopinath Bordoloi International Airport, Guwahati (120 km from Shillong) Alternative Airport: Umroi Airport (30 km from Shillong, limited connectivity) Travel Time: 3-4 hours by taxi or bus. Shillong tour packages often include airport transfers. By Road Highway: National Highway 40 connects Shillong with Guwahati and nearby cities Travel Time: 3-4 hours from Guwahati by private taxi, shared cab, or state-run buses. Shillong packages often include road transportation. By Rail Nearest Station: Guwahati Railway Station (Well-connected with major Indian cities) Travel Time: 3-4 hours by taxi or bus from Guwahati. Many Shillong holiday packages include rail and road transfers for convenience. By Water Unique Experience: Combine a boat trip on the Brahmaputra River with other transport modes for an adventurous Shillong trip plan. Ideal for exploring different travel routes to Shillong. By Bus Services: Regular bus services by Assam State Transport Corporation (ASTC) and Meghalaya Transport Corporation (MTC) Budget-Friendly: Comfortable and cost-effective option for travellers on a budget. Shillong packages often include bus transfers.
Shillong.Places to Visit in Shillong: Shillong, the capital of Meghalaya, is a picturesque hill station renowned for its stunning landscapes, vibrant culture, and pleasant climate. A popular destination for tourists, Shillong offers a plethora of attractions that cater to all kinds of travellers. Here are eight must-visit places in Shillong that you should include in your itinerary. Umiam Lake: One of the top Shillong places to visit, Umiam Lake, also known as Barapani, is a vast and serene reservoir surrounded by lush hills. It is a perfect spot for picnics, boating, and water sports, making it a favourite among tourists. The tranquil beauty of Umiam Lake makes it one of the best tourist places in Shillong. Shillong Peak: For panoramic views of the city and its surrounding hills, Shillong Peak is the place to be. Standing at an altitude of 1,965 metres, it is the highest point in Shillong. It is one of the best tourist places in Shillong for those who love photography and nature. The peak is a popular inclusion in Shillong tour packages. Elephant Falls: Elephant Falls is one of the most famous tourist places in Shillong. This three-tiered waterfall is easily accessible and offers breathtaking views. The falls are named after an elephant-shaped rock that was once present at the site. A visit to Elephant Falls is a must on any Shillong one day tour. Ward’s Lake: Situated in the heart of the city, Ward’s Lake is a beautiful man-made lake surrounded by lush gardens. It is one of the most charming places to visit in Shillong. Visitors can enjoy boating or take a leisurely stroll around the lake. This lake is a highlight of the tour to Shillong. Lady Hydari Park: Named after the first lady of the state, Lady Hydari Park is a well-maintained park with a mini zoo and a beautiful rose garden. It is one of the most peaceful places to see in Shillong, ideal for families and nature lovers. The park’s serene environment makes it a popular spot in Shillong travel packages. Don Bosco Museum: A visit to the Don Bosco Museum is essential for understanding the rich cultural heritage of Northeast India. This seven-storey museum showcases a vast collection of artefacts, photographs, and traditional costumes. It is one of the significant Shillong tourist attractions for history and culture enthusiasts. Police Bazaar: No tour of Shillong is complete without visiting Police Bazaar, the city’s main commercial hub. It is one of the best places to visit in Shillong for shopping, dining, and experiencing the local lifestyle. From traditional handicrafts to trendy apparel, this market has something for everyone. It’s an essential stop in Shillong holiday packages. Laitlum Canyons: For those seeking adventure and stunning vistas, Laitlum Canyons is one of the top places to visit in Shillong. Known as the ‘end of the hills,’ this spot offers breathtaking views of the surrounding valleys and is perfect for trekking and photography. It is a must-visit in any Shillong trip plan. Shillong’s diverse attractions make it a captivating destination for all kinds of travellers. Whether you’re booking Shillong travel packages or planning an independent tour to Shillong, these eight destinations should be on your list. From natural beauty to cultural riches, the Shillong tourist places offer a memorable and enriching experience. Ensure these spots are included in your Shillong trip packages to fully enjoy what this charming hill station has to offer.
Shillong.Best Hill Stations/ Best Beaches in Shil: Nestled amidst the undulating hills of Meghalaya, Shillong beckons travellers with its tranquil hill stations and serene scenic spots. Explore these picturesque destinations during your Shillong tour: Best Hill Stations in Shillong Cherrapunji (Sohra): Just a scenic 54 km drive from Shillong lies Cherrapunji, famed for its verdant landscapes and abundant rainfall. As you wind through the mist-covered hills, you'll encounter Nohkalikai Falls, India's tallest plunge waterfall, plunging dramatically into a turquoise pool below.Monsoon enthusiasts will revel in the lush greenery and the romantic allure of rain-kissed valleys, making Cherrapunji a must-visit during the rainy season. Dawki: Approximately 80 km from Shillong, Dawki enchants visitors with its crystal-clear Umngot River. Renowned as one of the cleanest rivers in India, Umngot's transparent waters allow visibility up to several feet deep, creating a surreal experience for boat riders gliding over its surface. The town sits on the India-Bangladesh border, adding a cross-border charm to its serene ambience. Enjoy leisurely boat rides or kayak excursions, soaking in the breathtaking beauty of the surrounding hills and the lush greenery along the riverbanks. Mawlynnong: Venture 90 km from Shillong to discover Mawlynnong, often hailed as Asia's Cleanest Village. Here, meticulously maintained gardens and pathways lined with flowering plants greet visitors. The highlight is the bamboo skywalk, offering panoramic views of the surrounding hills and Bangladesh plains in the distance. Immerse yourself in the Khasi culture as you interact with locals known for their hospitality and sustainable living practices. Shillong Peak: Within the city limits, Shillong Peak offers panoramic views of the cityscape, the Khasi Hills, and the plains of Bangladesh on clear days. Rising 1,965 meters above sea level, it's the highest point in Shillong and a popular spot for tourists and locals alike. Capture stunning vistas from the observation platform, where cool mountain breezes invigorate the senses. It's an ideal spot for early morning sunrise views or evening sunset panoramas, offering a serene retreat from the bustling city below. Best Beaches While Shillong itself doesn't have beaches, nearby scenic spots offer serene landscapes akin to beachfront: Umiam Lake (Barapani): Just 15 km from Shillong, Umiam Lake is a man-made reservoir surrounded by emerald hills. Originally built as a hydroelectric project, it has become a popular destination for water sports enthusiasts and nature lovers alike. Boating, kayaking, and fishing are popular activities in its serene waters. Picnic spots dot the lake's shores, providing perfect spots to unwind and enjoy the scenic views. Sunset over Umiam Lake is particularly magical, casting hues of orange and pink over the tranquil waters and reflecting the surrounding hills. Laitlum Canyons: Located approximately 25 km from Shillong, Laitlum Canyons offer panoramic views of deep gorges and mist-covered hills. Known as the "End of the World," this secluded spot provides a bird's eye view of the sprawling valley below, dotted with quaint villages and terraced slopes. The trek to Laitlum Canyons rewards adventurers with breathtaking vistas and an opportunity to immerse in the tranquillity of Meghalaya's rural landscapes. It's an ideal spot for nature walks, photography sessions, or simply unwinding amidst the serene ambience. Krang Suri Waterfalls: Embark on a 90 km journey to Krang Suri Waterfalls, nestled in the Jaintia Hills. The cascading turquoise waters against a backdrop of lush green forests create a mesmerising spectacle. The natural pools formed at the base of the falls invite visitors for a refreshing dip or swim, offering a cool respite amidst the tropical surroundings. Surrounded by pristine wilderness, Krang Suri is a haven for nature enthusiasts and photographers seeking to capture the raw beauty of Meghalaya's landscapes. Plan your exploration with Shillong tour packages to uncover these hidden gems and create lasting memories amidst the serene landscapes of Meghalaya.
Shillong.Adventurous things to do in Shillong: Shillong, with its picturesque landscapes and rugged terrain, offers ample opportunities for adventure enthusiasts. Whether you're seeking adrenaline-pumping activities or thrilling experiences in nature, here are four adventurous things to do in Shillong that you shouldn't miss. River Rafting on Umngot River: Experience the thrill of river rafting on the crystal-clear waters of Umngot River in Dawki, near Shillong. Known for its transparent waters and breathtaking scenery, Umngot River offers a thrilling adventure amidst rocky cliffs and lush greenery. The rapids here provide an exciting challenge for both beginners and experienced rafters alike. Include river rafting on Umngot River in your Shillong holiday packages for an exhilarating day trip. Caving at Mawsmai Caves: Explore the mysterious underground world by caving at Mawsmai Caves, located near Cherrapunji, about 55 kilometres from Shillong. These limestone caves are known for their natural rock formations, stalactites, and stalagmites, creating a surreal environment for adventurers. Shillong tour guide tours are available to navigate through narrow passages and chambers, providing a thrilling experience of spelunking. Include a visit to Mawsmai Caves in your Shillong sightseeing tours for an adventurous twist. Trekking in David Scott Trail: Embark on a trekking expedition along the historic David Scott Trail, named after a British officer who established this route in the early 19th century. The trail stretches from Mawphlang to Lad Mawphlang, passing through dense forests, cascading waterfalls, and quaint villages. It’s a perfect blend of adventure and natural beauty, offering stunning views of the Khasi Hills. Trekking along the David Scott Trail is a must-do for adventure enthusiasts visiting Shillong. Zip Lining at Mawphlang: For a dose of high-flying adventure, try zip lining amidst the scenic landscapes of Mawphlang, a village known for its sacred forest and cultural heritage. Zip lining allows you to soar across valleys and forests, enjoying panoramic views of the surrounding hills. It’s a perfect activity for adrenaline junkies seeking an adventurous escapade in Shillong. Add zip lining at Mawphlang to your Shillong trip packages for an unforgettable adventure. Shillong, with its rugged terrain and natural beauty, offers an array of adventurous activities that cater to thrill-seekers and nature enthusiasts alike. Whether you’re rafting on the Umngot River, trekking along the David Scott Trail, exploring Mawsmai Caves, or zip lining at Mawphlang, each experience promises excitement and unforgettable memories. Plan your adventure-filled trip with comprehensive Shillong travel packages that include these activities, ensuring a thrilling and rewarding journey through this enchanting hill station. Whether guided by a Shillong tour guide or exploring independently, these adventurous things to do in Shillong will add excitement and joy to your visit.
Shillong.What to Eat in Shillong?: When exploring Shillong, a vibrant culinary scene awaits, offering a blend of local Khasi delicacies and influences from neighbouring states. Here are six must-try delicacies that promise to tantalise your taste buds during your visit: Jadoh: Savor the essence of Khasi cuisine with this aromatic rice dish cooked with pork and local spices. Often enjoyed during festive occasions, it's best paired with dain thlen, a spicy local chutney. Bamboo Shoots: Discover the versatility of bamboo shoots in Shillong's cuisine. Try Tungrymbai, a fermented bamboo shoot paste with pork and spices, delivering a unique blend of sour and savory flavors. Dohneiiong: Experience the rich, earthy flavors of this pork curry made with black sesame paste. Paired with steamed rice, it's a comforting dish that showcases local ingredients. Momos: These Tibetan-style dumplings, filled with minced meat or vegetables, are a popular snack in Shillong. Enjoy them steamed or fried, with a spicy tomato-based dipping sauce. Pumaloi: Indulge your sweet tooth with this traditional rice cake steamed in turmeric leaves, mildly sweetened with jaggery and coconut milk. It's a festive treat that's hard to resist. Ki Kpu: End your meal with this mildly alcoholic local beverage made from fermented rice. Brewed with indigenous herbs, it offers a slightly tangy taste and a glimpse into Khasi traditions. Include these must-try delicacies in your Shillong itinerary to immerse yourself fully in the local culture and culinary delights of this enchanting hill station.
Shillong.Interesting Facts about the Shillong pla: Here are some interesting facts about Shillong that every traveller should know before embarking on their journey to this picturesque hill station: Scotland of the East: Shillong is often called the "Scotland of the East" due to its stunning landscapes and pleasant climate, reminiscent of the Scottish Highlands. Cleanest Village in Asia: Mawlynnong Village, near Shillong, is celebrated as the "Cleanest Village in Asia," showcasing eco-friendly living in pristine surroundings. Music Capital of India: Known for its vibrant music scene, Shillong hosts numerous festivals and concerts, earning it the title of "Music Capital of India." Living Root Bridges: The living root bridges of Meghalaya, including those near Shillong, are natural wonders created by guiding the roots of rubber trees over centuries. Cherrapunji's Record Rainfall: Cherrapunji, close to Shillong, holds records for the highest rainfall, contributing to the lush greenery and biodiversity of the region. Shillong Peak: The highest point in Shillong, offering panoramic views of the city and surrounding hills, is a must-visit spot for breathtaking photography. Sacred Groves: Meghalaya's sacred groves, revered for their spiritual significance, are biodiversity hotspots protected by local communities. Khasi Matrilineal Society: The Khasi people follow a matrilineal system, tracing lineage and inheritance through the mother's side, offering unique cultural insights. Meghalaya's Caves: The state is home to some of the longest and deepest caves in India, attracting adventure seekers for thrilling caving expeditions. Polo Grounds: Shillong boasts one of the oldest polo grounds in the world, where matches are still held, reflecting the city's rich sporting heritage. Whether you're exploring the "Scotland of the East," discovering music festivals, marvelling at living root bridges, or immersing yourself in the matrilineal culture of the Khasi people, each aspect adds a layer of fascination to your Shillong itinerary. Plan your tour to Shillong with comprehensive Shillong packages to ensure you don't miss out on these intriguing facts and experiences that define this enchanting hill reach

faq.question: 1. How much does a 7-day Australia trip from India cost?
faq.answer: A 7-day Australia trip from India costs around ₹80,000–₹1.2 lakh per person, including flights, hotels, meals, and activities.

faq.question: 2. Is Australia a visa-on-arrival country for Indian citizens?
faq.answer: No, Indian travellers need to apply online for an Australian visitor visa before arrival.

faq.question: 3. Can I use my Indian driver’s license in Australia?
faq.answer: Yes, you can use an Indian driver’s license temporarily, but carrying an International Driving Permit is recommended.

faq.question: 4. When is the best time to visit Australia?
faq.answer: The best time to visit Australia is spring (Sep–Nov) or autumn (Mar–May) for mild weather and fewer crowds.

faq.question: 5. How much does food cost in Australia?
faq.answer: Meals in Australia cost about 15–20 AUD for budget options and 30–45 AUD at mid-range restaurants.

faq.question: 6. What’s the smoothest way to get around Australia?
faq.answer: Fly between cities for long distances and use trains, buses, or rental cars locally.

faq.question: 7. Is language a barrier for Indians visiting Australia?
faq.answer: No, English is the main language in Australia, making communication easy for Indian travellers.

faq.question: 8. Which Australian cities should I definitely visit?
faq.answer: Must-visit cities in Australia include Sydney, Melbourne, Brisbane, and Perth for iconic landmarks, vibrant culture, stunning beaches, and shopping experiences.

faq.question: 9.Is there a visa on arrival in Australia?
faq.answer: No, visa on arrival is not available in Australia.

faq.question: 10. How many days are enough for an Australia tour?
faq.answer: We recommend planning an Australia trip for at least 10 days, but the longer the better. You could travel to the outback or visit national parks in ten days or a fortnight. Overlanding through the heart of the country, exploring the Northern Territory, or touring Tasmania could all be done in three weeks or more.

Australia.About Australia: With endless things to discover, Australia is an enchanting continent situated between the great Indian Ocean and the South Pacific Ocean. Ravishing cityscapes, beautiful country sides, fancy suburbs and quaint square contribute to a multicultural landscape. From crystal blue waters to red and golden terrains, the wilderness here is simply amazing. Its scenic mountains, iconic beaches, plunging reefs, enormous deserts and lush green forests will leave you spellbound. Australia is home to some exotic animals like Kangaroos and Koalas. Oh, and how can we forget about the largest coral reef in a color palette you cannot imagine! If you are an adrenaline junkie, Australia has many amusement and theme parks that will feed away all your wishes. A trip to Australia gives you the opportunity to swim with exotic marine creatures and spot some magnificent whales and sharks. Try taking a surfing class or engage in watersports like snorkeling, kayaking, diving and much more during your Australian trip! The Aussie way of life today falls back to its history of its tribal roots and its mid-European influences. Even today, that history is seen in Australia’s monuments, museums and people. Australia is also a hub for arts, commerce, sports and recreation. Taking a trip to Australia, you will have the luxury of exploring the gorgeous landscapes of Sydney, Melbourne, Perth, Brisbane, Gold Coast and much more! Oh, and an amazing culinary experience will also leave you speechless. Eating their staple cookies and fast foods is something you must do on your Australian tour. From beaches to parks, museums to clubs, stadiums to reefs, the possibilities are infinite. From the food to the flora and fauna, there are many surprises to unravel. Australia is also a place of renowned educational institutions and diverse ethnicities.Australia tour packages offer an easy and convenient way to explore the country. With these Australia packages, you can get the best of everything that Australia has to offer, including its amazing landscapes, rich history and culture, and exciting activities. So why not go for an Australia tour package today and enjoy the ultimate Australian experience!In addition, if you're looking for a more customizable and budget-friendly option, you can opt for Australia packages. These packages allow you to tailor your itinerary to suit your interests and budget, giving you the flexibility to choose where you want to go and what you want to do. Whether it's a relaxing beach vacation, a thrilling adventure, or a cultural exploration, there's an Australia package for everyone. Book your Australia package today and embark on a journey of a lifetime!
Australia.Best time to visit Australia: The best time to take a trip to Australia is during the spring and autumn seasons. The spring season emerges in September and lasts up to November while the autumn season starts from March to the month of May. Spring is the best time to visit the national parks and to go on a hiking trip. During the fall, you can explore the beauty of wetlands, rainforests and the wildlife on your Australia trip. Being a country with a wide range of outdoor activities to offer, every season comes in with different experiences in Australia. The summer season brings in warm sunny days that provide some great opportunities like exploring the beaches, diving, snorkeling, surfing, taking a road trip and much more. The summers in Australia begin from the month of December and last up to February. During the winters (June to August), you can indulge yourself in great outdoor activities in Australia like skiing and mountaineering.
Australia.How to reach Australia: Travelling to Australia with Thomas Cook India’s Australian tours is an easy affair as you can board a flight from all the major cities in India like Delhi, Mumbai, Bangalore, Cochin, Kolkata, Ahmedabad, Trivandrum, Hyderabad and Chennai. These major cities are well connected with other domestic airports in India. Hence, it provides a smooth journey for travelers planning to fly to Australia. There are ten airstrips connecting the Island to India, Melbourne and Sydney being the busiest ones. There is a wide range of carriers to choose from that cover the air route between India and Australia. You have the liberty to select from Cathay Pacific, Thai Airways, Qantas Airways, Cathay Dragon and much more depending on your preferences and comfort. However, there is only one direct flight to reach Australia from India which boards from New Delhi and lands in Sydney, operated by Air India, which takes about 12 hours 25 minutes to get there.
Australia.Best Places to Visit in Australia: Sydney is a marvelous harbor location blessed with world class restaurants, fancy cafes and pubs, multicultural neighborhood, white sand beaches, world heritage sites and much more! Being in Sydney, you can spend a day marveling at the beautiful Sydney Opera house or unwinding at the iconic Bondi beach sipping on some fresh juice and soaking up the sun. With its beautiful gardens, thrilling amusement parks, street shops and the amazing zoo, Sydney will feed all the expectations you have from a vacation in Australia. Here are some of the best places to visit in Australia. Melbourne Uluru Gold Coast, Queensland Brisbane Great Barrier Reef, Queensland Tasmania Perth Melbourne Melbourne is a cultural hub and is the most vibrant city in Australia. Uncover the history within Melbourne’s beautiful alleys or spend a day attending a sporting event, sipping on your drink. Right from treating your taste buds to some fancy, artisanal coffee to giving yourself retail therapy at the famous Queen Victoria market, Melbourne Tourism will make all your wishes come true. This place offers an array of lavish clubs, alluring museums, wildlife parks and an Aquarium that will make you go gaga! Uluru Witness million stars shining bright in the night sky in this spiritual heart of Australia. Ancient red sandstone landscapes, spellbinding culture and the beautiful sunrise that brightens the whole land are just a few reasons why you must visit Uluru. A panoramic view of the red Australian desert is something you should experience and make memories of during your trip to Australia. Gold Coast, Queensland If you are a water person and hitting the beaches and taking a dip is what your perfect holiday sounds like, the Gold Coast is the place of interest. Blessed with serene beaches surrounded by crystal clear blue waters and a wide range of water sports to indulge in, this city is a haven for beach lovers! But not just that, Gold Coast provides thrilling amusement and theme parks to feed the adventure junkie in you! With a variety to choose from, be it a humongous aquarium or forests to venture, the list never ends! Brisbane When in Brisbane, you will be surprised by the list of things and attractions it has to offer. From adventure to art, this place will fill you up with experiences you will cherish for lifetime. This river town has vibrant nightlife, a serene riverfront, amazing restaurants, pretty coffee shops and an alluring art scene. During your trip to Brisbane, you can visit the biggest koala sanctuary in the world and sip on some amazing wine at the Granite Belt. Great Barrier Reef, Queensland Paradise on Earth is here! Rainbow coloured corals and abundant marine life is what you can experience at this astonishing tropical destination. The Great Barrier Reef Tourism is home to 6 types of Turtles and kite shaped manta rays. It is blessed with several white sand beaches to spend your holiday in. And how can you forget whale watching while in the Great Barrier Reef? You can spot some alluring Dwarf Minke Whales, Bottlenose Dolphins and Humpback Whales at this place. Tasmania Escape the hustle bustle of city life and soak up the serene views of this elegant Island. Home to some astonishing museums and pretty art galleries, this place will never fail to please you. Oh, and how can you forget being in Tasmania and not spotting a Tasmanian devil? Scrumptious seafood to eat and locally brewed beers are just a few things one should try in Tasmania. Pretty shops, fancy cafes and welcoming people fill up the streets of this beautiful Isle. Perth Best of both worlds, rightly named, Perth is a fusion of natural and man-made wonders. Astonishing street art, gourmet restaurants, fancy marketplaces, beautiful gardens, alluring skyscrapers and crystal-clear blue waters - these are just a few among the long list of things to do in Perth has to offer. And how can you forget to visit the Rottnest Island when in Perth? Home to 63 beaches, Rottnest Island will feed all your beach cravings. From relaxing at the beach to indulging in thrilling water sports and spotting some giant whales to soaking up in music and art, Rottnest Island offers all of it!
Australia.Foods to Eat in Australia: When in Australia, how can you miss on the lip-smacking Aussie delicacies? With its unique and quintessential dishes, Australia will surprise your taste buds and leave you amazed. From delicious biscuits to meats you may never find in any other continent, here’s some food you must try during your Australian tour. Meat pies - A delicacy that one must try during their trip in Australia is meat pies. Yes, you heard it right! This traditional food consists of minced meat and gravy wrapped in a pie. You can grab this at a food truck or bakeries in Australia. Kangaroo meat - You don’t find this anywhere else but in Australia. It's red meat which can be grilled, used in burgers or even make sausages. To ease into the experience of eating, you can try eating it in a stew or a pie. Lamington - Lamington is a lip smacking square shaped sponge cake which is coated with chocolate icing and shaved coconut. This yummy dessert has its own day too! 21 June is celebrated as National Lamington Day. Enjoy an afternoon sipping on your favourite hot beverage and munching on a piece of Lamington with cream or jam. The Lot Burger - If a normal burger is what you are expecting this to be, you are wrong. The traditional loaded burger comes with pickled beetroot and a fried egg. You might also find a big chunk of pineapple in your burger, but it only adds to the amazing taste of this huge burger! Vegemite - Sounds weird right? This Nutella like spread tastes nothing like it. This paste is made with extracted yeast and tastes a bit salty and bitter. But it is the most popular spread which can be found in every household in Australia. Try it with your toast and savour it in its taste. Tim tams - This, without doubt is Australia’s favourite and most delicious cookie. Happiness is in taking a bite of soft cream between two biscuits covered entirely in chocolate. And to add on to the excitement, it is available in 12 different flavours! Australian holidays without eating Tim tam are incomplete.

Take a stroll through the Botanic Gardens and explore its flora and fauna. Indulge in a gourmet Greek dinner at Greek Precinct, which has the largest expat Greek population in the world. Have a chat with the locals and get to know a bit more about their lifestyle and cultures. Indulge yourself in a world class dining experience and take part in workshops with the locals for a unique and authentic experience. Your Australian trip is incomplete without witnessing millions of stars shining bright over your head at this alluring setting. Catch an amazing show performed by dolphins at the Gold Coast Get an opportunity to watch the dolphins perform at the Sea World. While you are there, you can try their thrilling roller coasters and monorails. Catch some other popular shows like fish detectives, SpongeBob SquarePants, Dora’s best friend’s adventure and much more! To get your adrenaline pumping, visit amusement and theme parks like Dream world, Whitewater World, Warner Bros. Movie World and Wet ‘n’ Wild. For an out-of-the-world experience, visit Sky point Observation Deck for a 360 degrees panoramic view. And how can you forget the beautiful sandy beaches? Relax or take a dip in the blue clear waters at the beaches during your Australian tour. Hug a koala in Brisbane What could be better than playing with cute Koalas at the biggest Koala Sanctuary in Brisbane? Take a bike ride and explore the beautiful Botanic gardens in Brisbane. Go on a picnic at the Brisbane River and click some amazing snaps to add to your Australian holiday memories. For a thrilling experience, take a ride in the gigantic Wheel of Brisbane and experience a witness the beauty of this city from the top. Visit the Australian zoo and see some exotic beautiful animals. Spend a day at Noosa and enjoy the river playground and alluring National Park on your Australian trip. Swim along exotic marine life and explore the underwater coral life at the Great Barrier Reef Your Great Barrier Reef venture is incomplete if you don’t indulge in scuba diving and snorkeling. Explore the vast aquatic life by taking a dive in these blue waters and swim along with some exotic underwater creatures. If you are lucky, you will find Nemo! Spot four immersion exhibits like crocodiles, koalas, kangaroos and pythons at the Wildlife Habitat Port Douglas. Enjoy a close encounter with some of the unique reptiles and animals in Australia at the Hartley’s Crocodile Adventures. Spot some large crocodiles, cassowaries, quolls and various species of snakes at this zoo. Sail over the alluring ocean waters and spots some whales and dolphins swimming around. Sip on a chilled beverage and unwind at the Whitehaven beach. Get a thrilling river sledding experience in Tasmania Are you up for experiencing a thrilling adventure? Try the adrenaline pumping sport of river sledding and feel the rush. Indulge in retail therapy at the local market which consists of an array of shops you can choose from. Stroll through the lanes of Tasmania and feast on some mouthwatering bagels and doughnuts. Sip on some delicious wine from local wineries. Take a bite of a juicy apple at Willie Smith’s apple shed and try on some handmade Belgian-style chocolates. Set up a camp beside the beautiful Franklin River in Tasmania during your Australian trip. Spot some cute kangaroos at Perth Head to the Heirisson Island and pet some cute, adorable kangaroos! Take an amazing selfie with them and add it to your Australian trip memories. Spend a night treating your taste buds, especially with scrumptious seafood at one of the classic restaurants in Perth. Go on a shopping spree at a local market or a stylish mall to pamper yourself and buy some amazing souvenirs. Take a peaceful walk across the Claisebrook Cove Bridge and witness the beautiful lights on the bridge at night. Explore the Kings Park or ride a bicycle by the Swan River. Experience a thriving cafe and nightlight experience at Leederville. Swim, dive, surf and snorkel and soak up in the sun at the pristine beaches in Perth.
