Switzerland.Best time to visit Switzerland: Selecting the best time to go Switzerland varies based on what experience you prefer. Here's all you need to know to arrange your ideal Switzerland tour packages: Summer (June to August) – Peak Season: If you are a sunshine lover, this is the best time to go Switzerland. The climate remains between 20°C to 25°C. You can trek in the Alps, take a boat ride on Lake Geneva, and take a cable car ride in Lucerne. You will also be able to witness festivals such as the Swiss National Day on 1st August. But as it is tourist season, Switzerland package tours and accommodations might be slightly on the expensive side. This is also the best month to visit Switzerland if you want to attempt local Swiss cuisine, such as cheese fondue in outdoor cafés. Spring & Autumn (April-May & September-November) – Off Season: These months provide you with a serene Switzerland holiday with fewer crowds. The temperature is between 10°C and 15°C. You can visit vineyards, experience autumn colours, and explore Rhine Falls. And, you'll have more affordable Switzerland packages and hotel prices. It's a laid-back time to indulge in Switzerland cuisine and culture. Winter (December through February) – Snow Wonderland: If snow and skiing are at the back of your mind, this is the best season to visit Switzerland. The temperature falls below 0°C, and the Alps become a winter sports haven. Zermatt and St. Moritz resorts are must-visit places in Switzerland for skiing. The Zurich and Geneva Christmas markets provide a festive atmosphere for your Switzerland tour. Book your Switzerland tour packages well in advance, as they're full here. So, based on what you prefer — adventure, festivals or serene beauty — you can choose the best time to go Switzerland for you. Wherever you go, there are always great Switzerland places to visit in store for you.
Switzerland.How to reach Switzerland?: Let us guide you through how to reach Switzerland with ease so that you can organise your Switzerland tour without any hassle and not miss the stunning Switzerland tourist places. Whether you are looking for adventure, scenery or cuisine, here's how you access it. By train If you're already in Europe and considering how to get to Switzerland from nearby countries, the train is a great choice. You can take direct trains from Paris, Milan, or Munich to Zurich, Geneva, or Basel. High-speed trains such as TGV and EuroCity ensure you arrive fast and in comfort. That way, you'll already be enjoying the scenery even before you get to Switzerland tourist destinations. If you’re keen on a lot of travel, a Eurail Pass can make exploring different Switzerland packages easier and cheaper. By Road You can also drive from Germany, France or Italy. If you are an avid road trip fan, you will love travelling over scenic landscapes to Switzerland. There are also regular buses connecting these countries. This is ideal if you are looking for a multi-country tour as part of your package trip to Switzerland. By Air In all honesty, the most convenient and fastest way for you to begin your Switzerland trip packages is by flight. There are direct and connecting flights from Indian cities such as Mumbai, Delhi, Bengaluru and Chennai. Geneva, Zurich, and Basel are Switzerland's key airports. Zurich Airport, in particular, is extremely well connected to other Switzerland tourist sites by trains and buses. Carriers such as Swiss Air, Emirates, Qatar Airways, and Lufthansa operate very frequently on this route. Duration of the flights is approximately 8 to 14 hours with layovers depending. When you arrive, it is very easy to get to your preferred locations using public transport. Virtually all Switzerland holiday tours originate from these large cities, so you're good to go immediately.
Switzerland.Best Places to visit Switzerland: If you're booking a Switzerland package trip, the following are the places to see in Switzerland, where you have to go first: Jungfraujoch (Top of Europe): Your Switzerland trip is not complete without a stop here. Board the picturesque train and arrive at Europe's highest railway station. You'll walk through ice passages in the Ice Palace, have meals at restaurants that are as high as mountains, and enjoy breathtaking glacier vistas. This is one of the best places to visit in Switzerland for beginners. Interlaken: If you're an adventure-seeker, you'll adore Interlaken. Located between Lakes Thun and Brienz, it's heaven for skiing, hiking, and skydiving. Your tour operators in Switzerland will typically include it in each package tour of Switzerland. Lucerne: Lucerne is a dream for your Switzerland tour. You'll get to walk along Chapel Bridge, look at the Lion Monument, and walk cobbled streets lined by medieval structures. A day excursion to nearby Mount Pilatus provides panoramic Alpine vistas you wouldn't want to miss on your Switzerland vacation. The Matterhorn (Zermatt): A visit to this famous mountain is a necessity. You will get to hike or cable car around it, and springtime, specifically, is utterly beautiful. Down below lies the quaint village of Zermatt, providing the quintessential Swiss experience, perfect for a honeymoon in Switzerland as well. Lauterbrunnen: It's like entering a fairy tale in Lauterbrunnen. The valley has 72 waterfalls, green pastures, and stunning cliffs. Staubbach and Trümmelbach Falls are the standout points. Be it paragliding or trekking, Lauterbrunnen is a featured destination in most Switzerland tour packages. Your Switzerland tour guide will assure you that these are the best Switzerland tourist destinations to visit.
Switzerland.Beaches/ Hill Stations to Visit: Even though it is landlocked, your Switzerland tour packages deliver lakeside beaches and dreamy hill stations. Check out these stops: Lido di Lugano: When you book your Switzerland tour, request Lugano. Get an Italian vibe in Switzerland at this resort, where they have sandy beaches, pools, and good vibes. You can enjoy the stunning beauty of Switzerland while enjoying the warm and relaxing atmosphere you would find at a beach resort in Italy. Presumably, most tour packages for couples visiting Switzerland include this amazing destination. Aare Beach (Thun to Aarau): This is one of the most crowded "beaches" in Switzerland. Ideal for sunbathing and swimming in summer—do not forget your swimsuit if your Switzerland trip is in June to August (optimal time to visit Switzerland for lake enjoyment). Gornergrat (Zermatt): If you're looking for serious Alpine drama during your Switzerland tour, this is the one. You get to see 29 mountains above 4000 metres, including the Matterhorn. It's a staple in nearly every Switzerland tour package. Mount Pilatus (Lucerne): For those who love hill stations, Pilatus is exciting. Legends say Pontius Pilate is buried there!! You have the chance to ride the world's steepest cogwheel railway, with magnificent views along the way. If your Switzerland tour is during May to September, it is the best time to go to Switzerland for those who love clear vistas. Stanserhorn: Think of a cable car with an open-air deck and a revolving restaurant at the top. That is Stanserhorn, gliding over meadows and chalets, and perfect for those looking for a romantic twist to their Switzerland honeymoon package. These Switzerland tourist spots combine relaxation and thrill, perfect for all Switzerland tourism enthusiasts.
Switzerland.Temples to Visit in Switzerland: Switzerland is not all Alps and chocolates—there are tranquil temples you can visit as well. They bring spiritual serenity to any Switzerland tour: Sri Sivasubramaniar Temple (Zurich): In case your Switzerland trip packages include Zurich, make it a point to visit this stunning South Indian temple. For Lord Murugan, this temple is vibrant yet serene. You can even catch cultural festivities here—it's an interesting addition to Switzerland tourist attractions. Jain Temple (Geneva): You'll adore this if peace is what you're looking for. The delicate marble craftsmanship and serene ambience are soothing. Several packages feature it if you request, especially—perfect if you desire a varied Switzerland tour package price that offers value. ISKCON Zurich Temple: Your Switzerland vacation package can be home here. This ISKCON temple, dedicated to Lord Krishna, has kirtans, bhajans, and sattvic food. Ideal if you're on an extended Switzerland tour and need a spiritual respite. Radha Krishna Temple (Bern): If your Switzerland visit has Been as part of it, do not miss this little temple. Saturday satsangs and bhajans keep it happening. A pleasant spiritual side trip during your trip to Switzerland package, particularly if visiting other historical sites in Bern to explore in Switzerland. Hindu Temple Basel: This temple is for the Hindu community in northern Switzerland. If the tour packages you've selected for Switzerland will have you arriving in or going through Basel, the temple will be worth it for the experience. You'll engage with local people and experience Switzerland tourism from another perspective. These temples add something extra to your experience, making the packages you explore in Switzerland richer with experience.
Switzerland.Food to Eat in Switzerland: When you organise your Switzerland tour, it's not only the snow-covered Alps and shining lakes that will leave you spellbound — it's the hearty, comforting Switzerland food that envelops you like a cosy blanket. If you're booking Switzerland tour packages, be sure to schedule some time for these wonderful dishes. Rösti: Golden, crispy grated potatoes, often with bacon, eggs and cheese on top. There is a top-notch Valaisanne-style Rösti to be had at Walliser Kanne in Zermatt. After all, indulging in rich food after discovering the places to visit in Switzerland is among the best things to do in Switzerland. Raclette: Imagine this — scraped, melted cheese over boiled potatoes with onions and pickles. Sounds divine, doesn't it? Go to Raclette Stube in Zurich. It is ideal for a warm evening after checking off some destinations to explore in Switzerland, so plan your trip around the best time to go Switzerland. Basler Mehlsuppe: This flour soup, which has been roasted, is a fixture at the Basel Carnival. Although you won't be here during February, it's something that you can have a taste of at Restaurant Schlüsselzunft in Basel. Basel is itself one of those not-well-enough-advertised destinations in Switzerland, so do not omit it. Fondue: You just can't go without dipping crusty bread into hot, melted cheese seething with white wine and garlic. The place? Café du Soleil in Geneva — it has been offering this old favourite for over 400 years. An absolute must while on any Switzerland trip packages, particularly in winter months. Zürcher Geschnetzeltes: Veal diced in a sauce of creamy white wine and mushrooms — Zurich's pride. Drop by Kronenhalle in Zurich; its old-world aura serves the dish well. Add it to your Switzerland packages that include Zurich. Papet Vaudois: Mashed potato and leek with juicy sausage - the heart of the Vaud canton. Try it at the Brasserie de Montbenon in Lausanne when you're on the French side of your Switzerland trip packages. Vacherin Mont d'Or: A wine and garlic-baked soft cheese by season. Enjoy the best of it between September and April at Jura village cheese shops. It is rare, so be sure to inquire from your guide if your Switzerland tour packages take place within the period. Polenta & Braised Beef: This is the rustic dish from Ticino, the Italian-speaking part. Don’t forget to try this at Grotto della Salute in Lugano, especially on a cool evening. Each trip to Switzerland ought to be seasoned with those flavours. Among the mountain tops and lake shores, find the time to sit down, take one's time, and get absorbed in tradition. This is one of the best things to do in Switzerland, as important as any view or climb.
Switzerland.Switzerland Currency: When organising a Switzerland trip, you're going to want to be well-prepared to deal with money. The Swiss Franc (CHF) is the official currency of Switzerland. Although you may find prices advertised in Euros for some businesses, particularly tourist centres, Swiss Francs are where the majority of the action lies. So, when organising your Switzerland trip finances, it is useful to have some CHF to hand. Here’s a quick and easy breakdown for you: Banknotes: Banknotes come in denominations of 10, 20, 50, 100, 200, and 1000 francs. The notes are colourful and specially designed, which is symbolic of Switzerland's nature. They launched their Ninth series of banknotes in 2016. Just to inform you, the 50-franc note (green) was launched first, then the 20 (red), 10 (yellow), 200 (brown), 1000 (purple), and lastly the 100-franc (blue) note. Coins: You get coins of 5, 10, 20, and 50 centimes, as well as 1, 2, and 5 francs. These come in useful while making minor payments when visiting the scenic places to see in Switzerland. Exchange Rate: Right now, 1 Swiss Franc is approximately ₹90-95. But do keep watch before you settle down on Switzerland holiday packages because prices do change slightly. Some tips for your trip: Preparing for some Switzerland shopping? Don't forget about the tax-free shopping benefit! If you spend more than CHF 300, you can get a VAT refund – very handy when calculating your Switzerland holiday budget. You can pay with a card nearly everywhere, but it always makes sense to bring some cash with you, particularly if you visit small towns or bazaars on your Switzerland travel tour. If you're choosing between various Switzerland tour packages for couples or families, always ask if the price of the Switzerland tour package includes currency exchange tips or assistance. Occasionally, few package trip to Switzerland organise simple currency exchange at airports or hotels. When you book your package vacation to Switzerland, you'll enjoy discovering the fantasy-like places to visit in Switzerland – from snow-covered Alps to peaceful lakeshores. Sorting your money correctly is one little step that will make your entire experience of Switzerland holiday packages and Switzerland trip packages easy and hassle-free.

faq.question: How much does a Dubai tour package cost?
faq.answer: Dubai ranks among the top-visited destinations in the world. There are more than 35 Dubai tour packages available at Thomas Cook India. There are packages that can be customized as per the user’s requirements. Also Thomascook have offers on selected dubai tour packages.

faq.question: What is the best time to plan a Dubai tour?
faq.answer: People love traveling to Dubai all around the year. If you are still looking for the best time for a Dubai tour to enjoy the artificial beaches, outdoor activities, exciting events, and live entertainment. If you like summers, June to August is the best time. Whereas if you want a pleasant climate November to March is recommended for a Dubai trip as The winter season starts to fly.

faq.question: Which are some of the most visited places on a Dubai trip?
faq.answer: There is an endless list of famous places in Dubai and a plethora of things to do on a Dubai trip. Dubai really has it all, with everything from larger-than-life theme parks to impressive shopping malls. In recent years, Dubai has emerged as one of the most urbane and trendy destinations in the world. In fact, you will either need a month-long vacation or several shorter trips to explore the entire Dubai. Few famous attractions include Burj Khalifa at the top, Legoland, Dubai Aquarium, IMG world of adventure, etc.

faq.question: What is the most convenient mode of transportation in Dubai?
faq.answer: A very simple answer to that is ‘Dubai metro’. The Dubai metro is one of the world's most advanced metro systems and is the most convenient mode of transportation in Dubai. There are also many other modes of transportation which one can choose once in Dubai are taxis, buses, the monorail, and water taxis. To book a cab in Dubai, call a taxi or use Uber or Careem, which are both app-based cab services. If you want to go exploring, you could also rent a taxi.

faq.question: Will I get a visa on arrival in Dubai?
faq.answer: As an Indian passport holder, you can get a visa on arrival in Dubai for a maximum of 14 days provided you meet the requirements. Simply disembark from your flight at Dubai International Airport and proceed to immigration, where a free 30-day visit visa will be stamped on your passport, and enjoy your Dubai tour.

faq.question: How long is the flight from Dubai to Mumbai?
faq.answer: The flying time for your Dubai trip from Mumbai is 3hrs 10mins to 3hrs 35mins. The distance from Mumbai to Dubai is approx. 1920 kilometers.

faq.question: Do female travelers need a hijab in Dubai?
faq.answer: No, ladies traveling to Dubai need not wear a hijab while sightseeing or moving around the city. There no such dress code. However, ladies should cover their heads and shoulders while visiting mosques and certain public places on their Dubai trip.

faq.question: Can I use my Indian driver's license in Dubai?
faq.answer: No! with the regular Indian driver's license you cannot DRIVE in Dubai, or anywhere else in UAE.

faq.question: When is the Dubai shopping festival held?
faq.answer: The Dubai festival is now the world's longest-running shopping festival. It begins at the end of December every year and ends by early February. There is a number of activities such as live shows and events, raffles, performing arts, concerts, etc.

faq.question: Is Dubai open on Fridays?
faq.answer: Fridays are known for Jummah Prayers in Dubai and hence banks, government, and private offices are shut as it is a weekly off. However, all the tourist places, malls, restaurants, shopping centers are open except few shops which shut down for an hour or so. So, if a Friday comes in your Dubai trip, it wouldn’t be a problem.

faq.question: Is Dubai an expensive city?
faq.answer: Yes, Dubai is an expensive city, but not exorbitantly so for travelers on a holiday. Dubai ranked 58th most expensive city in the world. Yet, the city offers relatively good value for money for all their travelers.

faq.question: How far is Dubai from Abu Dhabi?
faq.answer: The distance is approximately 170 Kilometres. You can choose the mode of transport accordingly. Buses in Dubai are very comfortable, one can also take a taxi. However, traveling by road or metro is convenient and a better option on a Dubai tour.

faq.question: What are some of the activities to do in during your Dubai tour
faq.answer: Visiting the BurjKhalifa, go skydiving, have a lake ride at the Dubai Fountain, go on a desert safari with a BBQ meal, ride the rides at the Wild Wadi waterpark, go hot air ballooning, and more are some of the things you can do on your Dubai tour.

faq.question: What currency is used in Dubai?
faq.answer: The currency in Dubai is the dirham.

faq.question: How many days is enough to explore when on Dubai tour?
faq.answer: There are no fixed number days to see everything Dubai has to offer. A minimum of Three days, on the other hand, puts you in a comfortable position from which to see some of the city's top attractions.

faq.question: Is it necessary to exchange money before visiting Dubai?
faq.answer: Yes, having cash before you arrive allows you to obtain transportation and other necessities such as food and drink more quickly and a hassle-free Dubai trip.

faq.question: Is Dubai a safe place to visit for tourists?
faq.answer: Yes, Dubai is generally safe to visit for tourists since Dubai has a very low crime rate.

So, pack your bags and get ready for the ultimate desert vacation. Read on to find out more about your dream Dubai vacation.
Dubai.The Best Time to Visit Dubai: Winter, from November to April, is the best time to book your Dubai package with Thomas Cook India. Dubai has two main seasons, summer and winter. Between May and October, the summer season takes over Dubai and the temperatures average between 24°C and 43°C. On the other hand, the winter temperatures are relatively pleasant and range between 19°C to 32°C, making it the ideal season for a Dubai holiday. Winter is also the season when Dubai receives most of its rainfall. These showers will make your Dubai tour a pleasant experience without hampering your Dubai sightseeing. The temperate winter allows you to explore Dubai’s beaches and indulge in outdoor activities such as picnics, desert safaris and dune bashing during your Dubai vacation. The days see warm sunshine and bright blue skies while the evenings are fanned by the gentle desert winds. If you’re a shopaholic looking for amazing deals during your Dubai holiday, January and February are your months to visit Dubai. The Dubai Shopping Festival starts in January and continues into February and offers incredible deals, as much as a 75% discount, on electronics, jewellery, fashion and more.
Dubai.Honeymoon in Dubai: Dubai is the perfect honeymoon destination for couples looking to honeymoon in fast-paced cosmopolitan cities with tons of excitement around every corner. When you book a Dubai tour package don't forget to check out our offers for your dubai honeymoon, you book a trip to an exotic paradise that fuses its native Arabian traditions with all of the luxuries of the West. Dubai is a cultural melting pot where you can rub shoulders with people from all over the globe, not to mention enjoy a diverse medley of cuisines. Then, of course, there is the rugged beauty of the desert and the adventures you can experience during your Dubai tour, whether you plan on enjoying a day at the races or taking a desert safari to watch the exotic shimmy of a belly dancer. Thomas Cook India gives offer on Dubai trip packages to guarantee your honeymoon lasts longer than the time you actually spend in Dubai thanks to all the memories you will create together. During your romantic Dubai holiday, there are plenty of fun-filled activities to squeeze into your Dubai tour package ranging from shopping at the massive Dubai Mall to sailing along the Dubai Creek in a dhow whilst enjoying a romantic dinner and soaring up to the observation deck at the Burj Khalifa to add a spark of adventure to your marriage.
Dubai.How to Reach Dubai: The Dubai International Airport is a world-class terminal and the busiest one in all of UAE. It is well connected to almost all the major Indian cities such as Mumbai, Delhi, Chennai, Bengaluru, Kolkata and Hyderabad making it an easy task to reach Dubai. You can easily get regular direct or connecting flights into Dubai from any of these cities. Some of the well-known airlines that operate flights to Dubai include Emirates, Etihad, FlyDubai, Air India, SpiceJet and IndiGo. Dubai’s airport sits 5 kms outside the city but is well connected with public transport. If you cannot find a flight to Dubai, the next best option is to fly into Sharjah, which is Dubai’s closest neighbour. You will need a visa before you land in Dubai. Getting a UAE visa is easy, and you can get yours in 4-5 working days. However, if you hold a valid US, UK or Schengen visa, you do not need a separate visa for Dubai. All you must do is fill up a form after landing. Local Transport Within Dubai The public transportation system in Dubai - metro, buses, trams, abras, ferry, and water taxi - are managed by the RTA. The best part is that all these services use the same ticket type - the NOL card. You don't need to worry about purchasing a different ticket for each service. You could alternatively rent a car to drive yourself around provided you possess the prerequisite driver’s license.
Dubai.Places to Visit in Dubai: Starting out as a tiny fishing village, the emirate of Dubai has become the pearl of the Middle East. Your Dubai holiday is set against the backdrop of one of the most sought-after holiday destinations in the world. Therefore, your list of places to visit in Dubai must be suitably updated to reflect the best of its larger-than-life, world-class entertainment, its parks and resorts, its grand malls. Here are the best places to visit in Dubai: The Burj Khalifa - Tallest Building in Dubai Dubai Mall - Biggest Mall in Dubai Madinat Jumeirah The Dubai Fountain - Magical Medley of Lights, Water & Sound Mall of Emirates - Luxury Shopping Destination in Dubai Dubai Aquarium – Unique Underwater Zoo Beaches in Dubai JBR Open Beach Umm Suqeim Beach Kite Beach Marina Beach Jebel Ali Beach The Burj Khalifa - Tallest Building in Dubai Piercing the sky, the Burj Khalifa dominates Dubai’s landscape and is visible from miles around. The observation deck on the 124th floor opens to some of the most stunning vistas of Dubai and the desert around. It is said that you can watch the sunset twice in the same day at the Burj Khalifa! Take in the views as you enjoy a sundowner at the highest lounge on the planet located on the 154th floor. This is one experience in Dubai you should not miss. Dubai Mall - Biggest Mall in Dubai To call Dubai a shopper’s paradise would be quite an understatement. The retail experience in Dubai goes beyond just shopping. Not only do you enjoy amazing discounts, but you’re also a part of an extravaganza that knows how to pamper shoppers. The Dubai Mall, right next to the Burj Khalifa, is the best example of shopping indulgence. You will be spoilt for choice with more than 1,200 shops and 150 restaurants. It also houses an indoor theme park, the Dubai Aquarium and Underwater Zoo, an ice-skating rink and a majestic indoor waterfall. With so much to explore, you can easily spend one whole day at the Dubai Mall. Madinat Jumeirah If any place in Dubai is the seamless blend of the past and the present, it must be the Madinat Jumeirah. Its name translates as ‘City of Jumeirah’. This beautiful resort complex by the sea takes its design inspirations from the traditional strongholds of Dubai. It encloses a multitude of private enclaves and luxury hotels built in the traditional style but equipped with all the modern amenities. The best way to explore this luxurious complex is on a traditional wooden arba that takes you around at a leisurely pace. The Dubai Fountain - Magical Medley of Lights, Water & Sound A captivating water, music and light spectacle in Downtown Dubai, The Dubai Fountain is the world’s tallest performing fountain. At over 900 ft in length – equivalent to over two football pitches – The Dubai Fountain is situated on the 30-acre Burj Lake and performs to a selection of different melodies. The fountain has a unique design comprising five circles of varying sizes and two arcs and features powerful water nozzles that shoot water up to impressive heights. When operational, the fountain has over 22,000 gallons of water in the air at any given moment. Mall of Emirates - Luxury Shopping Destination in Dubai The mall of the Emirates in Dubai is a must visit place on any Dubai tour . It offers multiple entertainment activities to tourists under one roof and is one of the largest malls in the world and has over 630 brands under one roof covering an area of 233,467 sqm. With many fascinating activities in one place such as Magic Planet, VOX Cinemas, Theatre of Rhodes, and the jaw-dropping Ski Dubai, visiting this mall during your Dubai holiday will never disappoint you. Dubai Aquarium – Unique Underwater Zoo Dubai Aquarium & Underwater Zoo is home to thousands of aquatic animals, comprising over 140 species. Over 400 Sharks and Rays live in our 10 million litre tank, including Sand Tiger Sharks, Giant Groupers, and a host of other marine species. A series of animal encounters and diving experiences allows visitors to come face-to-face with the aquatic residents. From Small-clawed Asian Otters to Sharks and Rays, there are plenty of memorable moments to be had with your friends and family at Dubai Aquarium & Underwater Zoo during your Dubai holiday. Beaches in Dubai Dubai is well known as a luxury travel destination, but it is also home to miles and miles of pristine beaches with soft white sands and clear water. These beaches are located on the temperate, aquamarine-blue Persian Gulf that is known for its calm seas and beautiful weather. Make it a point to visit these 5 beaches in Dubai during your Dubai trip. JBR Open Beach The biggest beach in Dubai, the massive JBR Open Beach has fluffy white sand and warm, beautiful turquoise waters. Here you can rent jet skis to zip across the waves and scooters to zip across the promenade that lines this beach. Make sure to check it our on your nest Dubai package. Umm Suqeim Beach For Insta-worthy pics of the sea with the Burj al Arab in the background, head to Umm Suqeim Beach. Also known as Sunset Beach this wide, flat beach is where the Burj is located. This beach is also one of the few surfing spots in Dubai. A must visit beach on your trip to Dubai. Kite Beach Kite Beach is very popular with Dubai's foodies as many food trucks assemble here during the day and evening. Also, many little restaurants and cafés line Jumeirah Beach Road, which runs along this beach. Marina Beach For a luxurious beach experience, head to Marina beach. The Dubai Marina is a luxury residential area and Marina Beach is a section of sandy beach that runs parallel to it. It is a wide, beautiful beach with soft white sand. At Marina beach you're also close to Restaurants, shops, little cafés, and ice-cream shops that line the promenade. Your Dubai holiday is incompletes without a trip to this luxury beach. Jebel Ali Beach Jebel Ali is about 30 minutes south of downtown Dubai and the emirate's newest recreational zone. Home to the world's largest man-made harbour, the area is home to many long, sandy beaches where you can find yourself virtually alone. Besides typical beach activities and water sports, Jebel Ali is a popular dive area.
Dubai.Foods to Eat in Dubai: From hearty and flavourful street food to decadent Michelin-starred creations, you can get it all in Dubai. The flavours of the city will infuse your heart with their sheer variety. Here are our picks of food that you should taste in Dubai. Al Harees Mehalabiya Ghuzi Stuffed Camel Al Harees This dish is a culinary delight that takes hours to make, with ingredients that are remarkably simple. This dish is a must try on your Dubai package. Nothing but salt is added to a pot of wheat and lamb or camel meat, and these are cooked until the texture is smooth and uniform. The resulting mixture slowly baked overnight. The result is nothing short of alchemy. In a land famed for the spice trade, Al Harees is a remarkably simple dish that will Bring delight to your palate during your Dubai trip. Mehalabiya This refreshing pudding is a hit with the kids. Be sure to try some during Your Trip to Dubai with your family. Mehalabiya’s is decadently flavoured with rosewater and pistachios and tastes like a cool, calm desert breeze. It is perfect for those who seek an after-dinner treat that isn’t too sweet. You cannot miss out on this luscious dessert on your Dubai holiday package. Ghuzi Also called khuzi, this dish consists of a whole-roasted lamb or goat served on kebab skewers with vegetables and hazelnuts over a bed of pilaf or fragrant rice. It is easily one of the most popular dishes in Dubai because it is considered a whole meal in itself. It’s also happens to be the national dish of the United Arab Emirates, which means any Dubai trip without ghuzi is an incomplete one. Stuffed Camel Named by the Guinness World Book of Records as the largest foods you can eat, a stuffed camel is one of the most opulent meals you can sit down to on your Dubai holiday. Cooked on a spit and stuffed with chicken, eggs, fish and even whole sheep stuffed camel is considered a traditional food in Dubai, served at weddings and Bedouin feasts. This meal could very well be the highlight of your Dubai trip. You know, you need to try this on your Dubai holidays!
Dubai.Best Restaurants in Dubai: The best restaurants in Dubai you can eat at during your Dubai holiday are: La Petit Maison, Dubai - French/Italian Zeng He, Dubai - Modern Chinese Zuma Dubai - Japanese For Indian cuisine on your next trip to Dubai you could visit: India by Vineet Carnival by Tresind Basanti & Co.
Dubai.Things to Do in Dubai: Dubai is one of those holiday destinations that does not let you sit still. Between its malls, theme parks and beaches, there are a range of activities that will keep you busy on your Dubai holiday. While most of the activities can be done without venturing outside, there are those that cannot be contained indoors. Here are our recommendations of things to do in Dubai. Visit Ferrari World Make a splash at Wild Wadi Ride a Hot Air Balloon Realize Your Bollywood Dreams in Dubai Witness A Miracle in the Desert Frame Your Dubai Holiday Soar Over Dubai with a Zip line Visit Ferrari World Get fast and furious at this amusement park in Abu Dhabi. The first Ferrari branded theme park ever built it houses the Ferrari Karting Academy, The Ferrari Store, The Junior Grand Prix, The Mamma Rossella, the turbo Track, The Formula Rossa, The Flying Aces, and much more, Ferrari World has its own adventure to offer. The excitement and thrill you will experience on your Dubai packages with offers. From the highest loop on the planet to the fastest rollercoaster in the world, twist and turn yourself upside down on these fantastic rides designed to let you have a thrilling experience. If you are thrilled by speed, this is going to be the highlight of your Dubai holiday. Make a splash at Wild Wadi Wild Wadi Water Park in Dubai is world famous and features amongst the best outdoor water parks in West Asia. It also holds the distinction of having housed the largest water slide outside of North America. Taking a day trip to experience the rides, slides and many swimming pools is one of the best things to do in Dubai and guarantees to make your Dubai tour a whole lot cooler. Ride a Hot Air Balloon Soar over the mesmerising Dubai desert away and take a peaceful and relaxing ride in a hot air balloon while you can marvel at the stark landscape. Take in the hypnotic desert vistas as you soar over the dunes and the occasional oasis on your Dubai package and make it a complete trips. Each basket holds a maximum of 24 people and there’s plenty of room for you and your family. Don't miss this wish to take a break from the fast paced city during your Dubai tour. Realize Your Bollywood Dreams in Dubai Bollywood Parks, Dubai is a larger than life interactive Bollywood theme park that brings alive the magic of Indian cinema and its popular characters while you hunt down Gabbar Singh with a 3D laser. Attend grand musical performances at the magnificent Rajmahal, and soar above the sky in the 4D theatre with ‘Krrish’ as all your Bollywood dreams become a reality. Don’t miss the Bollywood Boulevard for some chaat and kulfis if you get homesick during your Dubai holiday. A true Bollywood fanatic would not miss this on his Dubai tour. Witness A Miracle in the Desert Dubai always finds ways to enchant you with its stunning architecture and splendid attractions. However, this whopping 72,000-sqm garden blooms with over 150 million flowers based installations takes the cake. The Miracle Garden hosts an award-winning life-size Airbus 380 made up of 500,000 flowers. It also has floral heart passages you can walk through and an 18-meter high Mickey Mouse for a grand photo op. This is one Dubai attraction that will turn your Dubai holiday into a journey for your soul. Frame Your Dubai Holiday Known amongst one of the tallest buildings in the world, Dubai Frame is quite literally a picture frame that has been visited by millions. Situated at a height of 150 metres, it offers beautiful views of the magnificent old and new Dubai, the glimmering water of the Gulf, and shimmer of the sands. A view that must be included on your Dubai tour. Walking on the spectacular glass bridge, that connects the towers of the frame, gives the feeling of walking on air and is quite a thrilling experience and is one of the most unique things you can do during your Dubai holiday. Soar Over Dubai with a Zip line Get the best view in all of Dubai with the XLine at Dubai Marina, provided you stop screaming and keep your eyes open. This zipline is the world’s longest urban zipline that lets you see the Dubai Marina beachfront, snazzy high rises, and the vast expanses of the city cruising at a speed of 80 kmph. You can experience this rush during your solo Dubai tour or in tandem i.e. along with a partner strapped to you.
Dubai.Dubai Shopping Festival: The 29th edition of the Dubai Shopping Festival is all set to host visitors from across the globe for a shopping extravaganza! Dubai exudes a different aura and energy during the shopping festival. It is more than just a shopping festival, it is an experience that stays with you for many years. The unique combination of cosmopolitan culture, magnificent skyscrapers, beautiful beaches, extravagant malls, and people from around the world, make Dubai during the shopping festival one of the best destinations in the world.
Dubai.Shopping in Dubai: When it comes to shopping in Dubai, you will be spoilt for options. No surprise then that it is known as a shopper's paradise. Home to the world's largest shopping mall, The Dubai Mall, Dubai is not only about glamour and grandeur. Beneath the modern facade of the city lies an old-world charm of traditional souks where you can find everything from personalized perfumes to gold. We have made a list of the must-buy things in Dubai so that you can shop without worry! Gold Jewellery - The list of shopping in Dubai won't be complete without it, as Dubai is known as the 'City of Gold'. You can pick pretty gold pieces at a lower price compared to other parts of the globe. Arabic Attars and Oudh - Both Attars and Oudhs are renowned for their aromatic fragrance and are used widely by Arab men and women. Traditional Oudh has a very distinct smell and is slightly expensive, whereas Attars can be customized by blending scents of your choice. Arabic Coffee and Kava Pots - Arabic coffee is pure coffee with a slightly bitter but refreshing taste. If you love coffee, this is one of the best things you can buy in Dubai. The pots are generally made of copper, and these can be used daily or as decorative pieces. Arabian Dates - You will find a wide assortment of dates in Dubai. Taste and pick up the ones you like the most. Majdool, Shokari and Ajwa are very popular and tasty. Pashmina Shawls - Made from real goats' hair and silk, the price tag is a little heavy on the pocket. You can always choose the viscose or silk versions for an alternative if you are on a budget. Persian Rugs - Persian rugs are known for their intricate designs and detailing. The pricing depends on the size, detailing and quality of the rugs. You can also find affordable options in the souk. Spices - These are extremely flavourful and are sold at affordable prices in local markets. This is the best way to bring home some authentic Middle Eastern flavours. Hookah Pipes - Even though you can find them everywhere, nothing beats the authentic Arab ones. There is a wide range of options to choose from, so be spoiled for choice till you find the perfect piece. Arabic Lanterns and Lamps - They are known for their beautiful filigree work and intricate patterns. These lamps are made of glass and often ornamented with beautiful stonework. Sweets - There are multiple options to choose from - Baklava, Maamoul and Knafeh, to name a few. These are a must-try when in Dubai. Camel Milk Chocolate - This is one of the most delicious things you can bring back from Dubai. Initially only found in Dubai, now it is exported to other countries.
Dubai.Adventurous Things to Do in Dubai: Did you know that Dubai offers its fair share of thrills as well? There are multiple adventurous things to do in Dubai that you must experience while you are there. We offers to Dubai trip to enjoy adventurous activities in Dubai.Here are few Adventurous things to do in Dubai. Dune Bashing - This is one of the most popular adventure activities in Dubai and also in demand with families as the minimum age limit is 3 years. You can get into an SUV or a Hummer and get driven around at high speeds while bashing the sand dunes. You can also combine this with a desert safari. Sand boarding - It is similar to skiing but in a desert. It is fun to slide down through the sandy dunes of Dubai. It holds one of the top spots in Dubai adventure places. Minimum age limit is 13 years. Skydiving - Your Dubai trip is incomplete without watching The Palm Islands. What better to watch it than from an altitude of 14,000 feet above sea level. You will have an instructor with you throughout, so feel the adrenaline rushing while you fall at speeds of 200 kmph. Minimum age limit is 18 years. Shark Diving - Fascinated by sharks? Dubai Mall allows you to dive in with the sharks. This mall has a huge aquarium where shark diving takes place, and it's an entirely safe experience to participate in. Minimum age limit is 10 years. Zip lining - With the world's longest zip-lining track, it is one of the extreme Dubai adventure places. Located at the height of 1800 feet, you get a panoramic view of the city while cruising at speeds of 60-70kmph. Minimum age limit is 10 years. Flyboarding - It is one of the unique and thrilling experiences in Dubai. Imagine flying over the water on a saddle attached to a water propulsion device. You can enjoy the landscape of Dubai while cruising at speeds of 40 kmph. Wakeboarding - This involves getting on a wakeboard and gliding through the water. The initial lift requires skills but is easily manageable once you are outside the water. Just maintain a straight posture and lean back while you are on it. There are many other activities that you can try in Dubai, such as deep-sea fishing, hot air balloon and Dubai helicopter tour, to name a few. There is something for everyone here!
Dubai.Best Places to Stay in Dubai: Dubai is a well-known luxury travel destination. With so many resorts in Dubai, there are countless options to choose from. There are multiple luxury hotel brands available, so choosing the best hotel in Dubai for your stay can be a task. We've made a list of the best hotels in Dubai to choose from, so you don't have to wonder about where to stay in Dubai. Our offers on Dubai packages offering the best hotels, sightseeing and other activities. Atlantis, The Palm - Nothing defines luxury the way Atlantis does, with signature underwater suites having glass walls. It is located on the top outer edge of the Palm Jumeirah, facing the Persian Gulf. Atlantis also has other attractions such as The Lost Chambers Aquarium, Dolphin Bay, and Aquaventure waterpark. Four Seasons Resort Dubai at Jumeirah Beach - Located on the shore of the Arabian Gulf, this resort provides the ultimate five-star experience. From beautiful sea-facing rooms and suites to a host of amenities, you can stay in luxury while indulging yourself in multiple activities. Burj Al Arab, Jumeirah - The only seven-star hotel in the world, this luxury resort has its peninsula near the Jumeirah beach. It has 201 duplex apartment suites that come with their private butlers. With multiple cuisines to choose from, this resort has nine restaurants run by the hotel's Michelin-starred culinary team. When you leave the hotel, you can either drive or be driven in a supercar! Bulgari Resort Dubai - This resort in Jumeirah Bay Island belongs to the famous Italian jeweller. This embodies luxury, with only 101 rooms and 20 suites. They have their yacht club and marina. DoubleTree by Hilton Hotels Dubai - Situated right at the end of JBR's The Walk shopping street, this high-rise resort has no regular rooms. They are all fully-furnished one, two or three-bedroom apartments with a kitchen and patio, making this one of the best hotels in Dubai for families. They have their own private beach, and the resort is very close to the metro station, which also makes it easy to explore the various tourist attractions. Sheraton Jumeirah Beach Resort - This mid-range beach resort is located very close to Hilton along JBR's The Walk. They have a large beachfront area and also a huge garden. There are five on-site restaurants to choose from which serve a variety of cuisines such as Chinese, Italian and Middle Eastern. You can also enjoy your meal at the poolside/beachside cafe.

Things to Know While Visiting Puri
Puri.Introduction: Puri is one of the holiest places for Hindus. It has the famous Jagannath Temple which is one of the most important places to visit in Puri. You can book any one of the good Puri tour packages and visit this beautiful city that houses the Jagannath Temple. The temple has a rich history and is also one of the oldest surviving temples in the country. It dates back to the 12th Century. It is believed to have been constructed by King Indradyumn, a Malava King. There are many legends surrounding the origin of the temple. However, the temple was rebuilt in 1112 by Anantavarman Chodaganga, a ruler of the Eastern Ganga Dynasty. The temple, which is the biggest highlight of any Puri trip, has a lot of history and mystery associated with it. The temple is dedicated to Lord Jagannath and his siblings. It is 215 ft high. When you book the Puri holiday packages and visit the town, you will see a hoisted flag at the top of the temple, which is always blowing in the opposite direction of the wind. This is fascinating and an occurrence that science has been unable to decode. Another significant inclusion of the Puri trip must be the Ratha Yatra. Rath Yatra is an annual festival where the idols of the Jagannath Temple are carried in a chariot pulled by thousands of devotees. It is a spectacle like none other and is one of the most important cultures of Puri. It is held in July and is one of the most amazing Puri tourist attractions. There are many such interesting happenings and cultures that you can discover when you go on a Puri tour. Thankfully, some excellent Puri holiday packages take you to this magical place and allow you to have the best trip. Take a look at this article to learn more about the place, its history, and all the reasons why you must visit Puri as soon as possible.
Puri.Most Popular Puri Tour Packages: Tour Packages NameDurationPricePuri Getway2 Nights 3 DaysRs. ₹14 000Bhubaneswar And Puri Darshan Yatra 3N3 Nights 4 DaysRs. ₹17 400Scenic Odisha5 Nights 6 DaysRs. ₹31 500Bhubaneshwar Bhitarkanika And Puri6 Nights 7 DaysRs. ₹33 200Puri Bhubaneswar Darshan Yatra 4 Nights 5 DaysRs. ₹36 000
Puri.Places to Visit in Puri: The Jagannath Temple in Puri is the most famous tourist attraction. However, there are several other places to visit in Puri that you must not skip. From the gorgeous beaches to the globally renowned Chilka Lake, there is indeed a lot to explore on a Puri trip. Here’s a list of the best places to visit in Puri: Temples Puri is predominantly a religious destination and this is why you need to include a lot of temple visits in your Puri itinerary. While a visit to the Jagannath temple is the most important part of the Puri holiday packages, you can also enhance your Puri trip by visiting the other temples in the town. These include the Vimala Temple, The Sakshigopal Temple and the Shree Gundicha Temple. Beaches Puri is a coastal town with some of the most beautiful beaches in India. Apart from the temples, the beaches are the highlights of Puri tourism. Hence, you must spend a lot of time at these gorgeous beaches, which surely are some of the most spectacular places to visit in Puri. You can enjoy water sports, swimming, or simply relaxing on a beach chair while sipping some cool coconut water. Chilka Lake A Puri travel will take you to some amazing places including lakes. Chilka Lake is one of the most beautiful attractions of Odisha. It is a natural water lake that offers picturesque views. You can enjoy the beauty of nature, go for a relaxing boat ride and have a wonderful time with your family. A trip to Chilka Lake may be included in the Puri holiday packages, so read the list properly when planning your Puri itinerary. Konark Yet another important place to include in the Puri holiday packages is Konark. Konark is one of the most beautiful and historically significant places in Odisha. It is just about a one-hour drive outside the town of Puri. It has the famous Sun Temple which is thronged by thousands of pilgrims who come on their tour to Puri. Bhubaneshwar Bhubaneshwar is the capital city of Orisha. It is one of the biggest and most modern cities in India. It also has the airport that is closest to Puri, so your tour to Puri may start from here. The best Puri travel packages from leading agencies like Thomas Cook often include air tickets, so you will get to visit this beautiful city on your Puri travel. Keep this list of places in mind when you plan your Puri travel. Get one of the best Puri travel packages and you will get to see most of the attractions.
Puri.Temples to Visit in Puri: As mentioned above, Puri is one of the holiest places in India. This is predominantly because of the Jagannath temple. Here is a list of all the temples in Puri you can explore on your Puri travel: Jagannath Temple The iconic Jagannath Temple is one of the best places to see in Puri. You can visit this temple and seek the blessings of lord Jagannath and his siblings, Subhadra and Balabhadra. It is an ancient temple that is very pious and attracts tourists from all over the world. You will see a visit to the temple included in most of the Puri holiday packages. Vimala Temple A beautiful temple located inside the premises of the Jagannath Temple, the Vimala Temple is an important highlight of Puri tourism. It is a Sakhi Temple and is visited by many pilgrims every day. Hire a good Puri travel guide to know the history of the temple in detail. Sakhigopal Temple The Sakhigopal Temple, dedicated to Lord Gopalnath, is an ancient temple situated on the outskirts of Puri. It forms an integral part of every Puri sightseeing trip. It has stunning architecture and is said to be a place where prayers are answered. Choose from among the best Puri trip packages and get to experience this beautiful place up close and personal. Shree Gundicha Temple Shree Gundicha Temple is the garden house of Jagannath and his siblings and is used during the Rath Yatra, thereby making it one of the most significant Puri places to visit. Get it included in the Puri trip packages and see the beautiful idols and architecture it has.
Puri.Timings of the temples in Puri: When you get any one of the best Puri travel packages and visit the city, keep an eye on the temple timings so that you can visit them properly and maximise your Puri sightseeing experience: Temple Opening Time Closing Time 1. Jagannath Temple 5.30 am 9 pm 2. Vimala Temple 5 am 10 pm 3. Sakhigopal Temple 6 am 9 pm 4. Shree Gundicha Temple 6 am 9 pm
Puri.Things to Know While Visiting Puri: Going on a Puri tour is exciting, but when you get the best Puri tour packages and visit the city, you have to follow a few rules, as customs and traditions are integral to the place. Here are some handy guidelines for you to remember: Attire for temple visits After you buy the Puri tour packages and start packing for the trip, you must remember that there are certain dressing guidelines for the Jagannath temple and other Puri tourist places. Denim pants are prohibited in the temple for men and women. Women are expected to cover up and wear loose, respectful clothes. Remember this when you go for your Puri tour. Photography permission There are many things to do in Puri, and you will come across many interesting places. You would surely wish to capture the moments, but it is vital to get permission before you do so. Many places in Puri have photography restrictions, so ask your Puri travel guide to inform you about the rules. Day trips You can take several day trips from Puri and visit some great places like Chilka, Konark and Bhubaneswar. Explore the best Puri trip packages and see which one allows you to take day trips. You can see the city and its surroundings and make the most of seeing the best places in Puri and its surroundings. Beach safety A day at the beach is an essential part of your Puri city tour, but you must understand that the waters in the Bay of Bengal are quite choppy. You have to follow the beach safety protocols at all times. Keep these handy tips in mind when you go on your Puri trip. Once that is done, you can maximise the Puri travel packages and have the best possible time with your loved ones.
Puri.Best Time to Visit Puri: Before you explore the Puri packages and locate the most suitable one, you must be aware of the best time to visit Puri. There are distinct seasons in the city, and you will experience different climates at different times of the year. Here is a detailed lowdown: Summer in Puri Summer in Puri sets in by March and lasts till June. The weather is very hot and humid at this time. There is bright sunshine and a warm breeze blouse throughout the day. Summer is not an ideal time to go on a Puri tour, as the climate is quite uncomfortable. However, you get some great deals on the best Puri packages and can have a nice holiday. Monsoon in Puri The monsoon in Puri is quite intense. It sets in by the end of June and lasts till the end of August. There is very heavy rainfall with storms. Since it is a coastal town, cyclonic weather is often seen. This is why a monsoon Puri trip is not the best idea. The beaches are also out of bounds, and the water sports are closed. Winter in Puri Winter is the best time to visit Puri. It starts to cool down from October and stays pleasant till February. There is nice sunshine at all times and the evenings are very nice and comfortable. Since it is a coastal place, it does not experience very cool weather making it the perfect time to plan a Puri travel. Now that you are aware of the best time to visit Puri, book one of the best Puri packages and have a wonderful Puri tour.
Puri.Quotes and Inspirations: Puri is a divine place. A tour to Puri can enrich your body and your soul. The temples are serene and the Natural views are placid. A trip to Puri gives you the power to connect with the universe and the divine powers and also go on a soul-searching journey. Hence, explore the Puri packages and spend your next holiday there.
Puri.What to Eat in Puri?: Puri is also a food lover’s haven besides being a pilgrimage spot. From the divine prasad at the temples to the fresh seafood by the beach, you will find a huge variety when you look to explore the famous food in Puri. Whether you prefer a vegetarian meal or you are a fish lover, the Puri famous food items will never disappoint you!
Puri.Shopping in Puri: A Puri trip plan can only be completed with some shopping. There are many interesting things to shop for in Puri, like: Artefacts from the beach A walk on any Puri beach will allow you to pick up interesting artefacts like jewellery, mirrors and home decor items, all made from items related to the ocean, such as sea shells. Buy them and carry them back as a memory of your wonderful Puri trip. Souvenirs You get very nice souvenirs related to the Jagannath Temple in Puri. From miniature temples to fridge magnets, make sure you shop for some cute souvenirs to take back with you. Hire a good Puri tour guide and ask him to take you to the local markets from where you can shop for the best souvenirs. Sweets Some of the most essential parts of Puri food are the sweet dishes. You get a variety of sweets that you must pack and take back. From the famous khaja to the delicious chena poda, you will get to buy the best sweets right here in Puri.

Argentina.Introduction: Argentina is a vibrant cultural and mystifying hub of South America. It is one of the most popular destinations on the continent, known for its sassy tango, delicious meat, and dairy products. Argentina is a go-to for skiing adventures, discovering indigenous and gaucho traditions, and exploring national parks bustling with biodiversity. The country’s unbelievable sights of glaciers, rock formations, steppes, deserts, and canyons are the most mesmerizing for anyone planning a trip to Argentina.Discover the beauty of vineyards in Mendoza, architectural marvels of Buenos Aires , history of the southernmost city of the world, Ushuaia, and the spectacular glaciers in Patagonia with Thomas Cook’s Argentina all-inclusive vacation packages. Discover the most exciting things to do, places to eat, things to shop, attractive places to visit during your laid-back sojourn in Argentina.
Argentina.Argentina Travel Guidelines Post COVID 1: Indian nationals planning travels to Argentina Argentina is open to all and is welcoming tourists. Check travel restrictions and other travel guidelines for Argentina.
Argentina.About Argentina Holidays: Experience the multicultural marvels and folklores of Argentina in the heart of its laid-back cities, beautiful countryside, and stunning national parks. Whether you’re planning a luxurious vacation in the country’s best resorts, backpacking with a tight budget, or coming for your honeymoon to an off-beat destination, a holiday in Argentina is no less than a dream for any traveler.The country will charm you with its thunderous waterfalls at the Devil’s Throat trail, the glacial hikes will leave you in sweet pain, and the agreeable climate of the country will make all your walks, strolls, and saunters around Buenos Aires lovely! The country will entice you with its delectable Empanadas, Asado, Alfajores paired with smooth bottles of Malbec galore wine and seduce you with its serenades and sassy tango, samba moves.And just as you begin to find yourself comforted in Argentina's natural and cultural marvels, locals surprise you with their enthusiasm for football! The stadiums are bustling with cheer and passion; you’ll find that Argentina lives for football.
