Greece.Places to Visit in Greece: Greece is a treasure trove of ancient history, stunning landscapes, and vibrant culture, offering a myriad of enchanting destinations for travellers to explore. Here are some of the most popular and must-visit places to see in Greece: Athens: The capital city of Greece, Athens, is a bustling metropolis renowned for its rich history and iconic landmarks. Visit the Acropolis, a UNESCO World Heritage site, to marvel at the Parthenon, Erechtheion, and other ancient ruins that offer a glimpse into Greece's glorious past. Don't miss exploring the historic neighbourhoods of Plaka and Monastiraki on your journey with Greece holiday packages from India, where you can immerse yourself in traditional Greek culture. Mykonos: Known for its vibrant nightlife and cosmopolitan atmosphere, Mykonos is a favourite destination for party-goers and sun-seekers alike. Explore the narrow streets of Mykonos Town, lined with boutique shops, chic cafes, and traditional tavernas. Relax on the beautiful beaches of Paradise and Super Paradise, or dance the night away at one of the island's famous beach clubs during your journey with Greece holiday packages from India. Santorini: Santorini is arguably one of the most picturesque destinations in Greece, with its whitewashed buildings, stunning sunsets, and crystal-clear waters. Explore the charming villages of Oia, Fira, and Imerovigli, perched on the cliffs overlooking the Aegean Sea. Be sure to visit the volcanic beaches of Perissa and Kamari for a relaxing day by the sea when travelling with Greece holiday packages from India. Delphi: Delphi, located in central Greece, was once considered the centre of the ancient world and is home to the famous Oracle of Apollo. Explore the archaeological site of Delphi, including the Temple of Apollo, the Theatre, and the Sanctuary of Athena Pronaia. Take in panoramic views of the surrounding mountains and olive groves from the slopes of Mount Parnassus. Crete: Crete is the largest of the Greek islands and offers a diverse range of attractions for visitors to enjoy. Explore the ancient Minoan ruins of Knossos and Phaistos, or hike through the breathtaking Samaria Gorge. Relax on the pristine beaches of Elafonissi and Balos, or wander through the charming villages of Chania and Rethymno when travelling with Greece holiday packages from India. Meteora: Meteora is a unique and awe-inspiring destination located in central Greece, famous for its surreal rock formations and monasteries perched atop towering cliffs. Marvel at the breathtaking scenery as you hike or drive through the rocky landscape, and visit the monasteries of Meteora, which date back to the 14th century. Explore the interior of these monastic retreats, adorned with stunning frescoes and religious artefacts, and soak in the spiritual atmosphere of this UNESCO World Heritage site. Meteora is truly one of the best tourist places in Greece, offering a glimpse into the country's rich cultural and natural heritage. Don't miss the chance to include it in your Greece travel packages for an unforgettable experience. Rhodes: Rhodes is a captivating island in the Aegean Sea, known for its medieval charm and beautiful beaches. Explore the historic Old Town of Rhodes, a UNESCO World Heritage site, where you can wander through cobblestone streets lined with ancient ruins and medieval architecture. Visit the imposing Palace of the Grand Master and the medieval Street of the Knights, and don't miss the opportunity to relax on the sandy shores of Lindos Beach or Anthony Quinn Bay. These are just a few of the top places to visit in Greece, each offering its own unique charm and attractions. Whether you're interested in history, culture, or simply soaking up the sun on a beautiful beach, Greece has something for everyone. Explore picturesque villages like Makrinitsa and Tsagarada, where traditional stone houses are nestled among chestnut and olive trees. Take scenic hikes through the Pelion forest, home to diverse flora and fauna, and enjoy panoramic views of the Aegean Sea from Mount Pelion's summit. Best Beaches in Greece: Elafonissi Beach, Crete: Located on the southwest coast of Crete, Elafonissi Beach is renowned for its pink sand, shallow turquoise waters, and unique natural beauty. Explore the shallow lagoon and sandbars that emerge during low tide, perfect for wading and snorkelling. Surrounded by protected nature reserves, Elafonissi Beach offers a serene and unspoiled environment for relaxation and recreation. Navagio Beach (Shipwreck Beach), Zakynthos: One of the most iconic beaches in Greece, Navagio Beach is famous for its crystal-clear turquoise waters and the wreck of a ship that ran aground on its shores. Accessible only by boat, this secluded beach is framed by towering limestone cliffs, creating a dramatic backdrop for sunbathing and swimming. Don't miss the opportunity to capture stunning photos of the shipwreck against the backdrop of the Ionian Sea. Greece.Adventurous things to do in Greece: Greece itinerary offers a plethora of adventurous activities for adrenaline junkies and outdoor enthusiasts. Whether you're a novice or an experienced kayaker, the Greek islands offer endless opportunities for adventure on the water. Rock Climbing in Meteora: Test your skills and conquer the towering rock formations of Meteora, a UNESCO World Heritage site located in central Greece. With its vertical cliffs and dramatic landscapes, Meteora is a rock climber's paradise, offering a variety of routes and challenges for climbers of all levels. Ascend to the summit of these ancient monoliths and be rewarded with panoramic views of the surrounding valleys and monasteries. Hiking the Samaria Gorge: Lace up your hiking boots and venture into an epic trek through the Samaria Gorge in Crete. This rugged canyon is one of the longest in Europe and offers breathtaking views of towering cliffs, lush vegetation, and crystal-clear streams. Follow the well-marked trail as it winds its way through the gorge, passing through narrow passages, crossing wooden bridges, and encountering native wildlife along the way. Scuba Diving in the Mediterranean Sea: Dive into the crystal-clear waters of the Mediterranean Sea and discover an underwater world teeming with marine life and colourful coral reefs. Whether you're a beginner or an experienced diver, Greece offers a variety of dive sites to suit every skill level. Explore underwater caves, shipwrecks, and submerged ruins, and encounter fascinating creatures such as sea turtles, dolphins, and octopuses. Skydiving over the Greek Islands: Experience the ultimate adrenaline rush with a tandem skydiving adventure over the Greek islands. Whitewater Rafting on the Voidomatis River: Experience the thrill of whitewater rafting on the Voidomatis River in northern Greece. Greece.Famous Foods in Greece: Greek cuisine is renowned for its delicious flavours, fresh ingredients, and Mediterranean influences. Here are five must-try delicacies that will tantalise your taste buds during your visit to Greece: Moussaka: Moussaka, a beloved Greek dish, features layers of minced meat (often lamb or beef), sliced potatoes, and eggplant, all topped with a rich béchamel sauce before being baked to golden perfection. The combination of rich flavours and hearty textures makes moussaka a favourite among locals and visitors alike. Be sure to savour this comforting dish during your Greece tour itinerary for a taste of authentic Greek cuisine. Greek Salad (Horiatiki): No trip to Greece is complete without indulging in a fresh and vibrant Greek salad. Made with ripe tomatoes, crisp cucumbers, tangy feta cheese, Kalamata olives, and fragrant oregano, Greek salad is a refreshing and healthy dish that perfectly captures the essence of Mediterranean flavours. Enjoy it as a starter or as a side dish with your main meal for a taste of Greece's famous tourist places. Spanakopita: Spanakopita is a delicious Greek pastry filled with spinach, feta cheese, onions, and herbs, encased in layers of flaky phyllo dough. Whether enjoyed as a snack, appetiser, or main course, spanakopita is a savoury treat that showcases the delicate balance of flavours and textures that Greek cuisine is known for. Don't miss the opportunity to try this classic dish during your tour to Greece. Souvlaki: Souvlaki is a popular street food in Greece, consisting of skewered and grilled meat (typically pork, chicken, or lamb) served with pita bread, tzatziki sauce, and a variety of accompaniments such as tomatoes, onions, and lettuce. Whether enjoyed as a quick snack or a satisfying meal, souvlaki is a must-try dish that epitomises the delicious simplicity of Greek cuisine. Baklava: Baklava is a sweet and indulgent dessert that has its roots in Greek and Turkish cuisine. Made with layers of crispy phyllo dough, chopped nuts (such as walnuts or pistachios), and sweetened with syrup or honey, baklava is a decadent treat that is sure to satisfy your sweet tooth. Enjoy it with a cup of Greek coffee or a glass of ouzo for a truly memorable culinary experience. Tzatziki: Tzatziki is a creamy and refreshing dip made with strained yoghurt, cucumber, garlic, and olive oil, seasoned with salt, pepper, and fresh herbs such as dill or mint. This versatile condiment is typically served as an accompaniment to grilled meats, and gyros, or as a dip for bread or vegetables. Its cool and tangy flavour makes it a perfect complement to the bold and savoury dishes of Greek cuisine. From savoury classics like moussaka and souvlaki to sweet treats like baklava, Greek cuisine offers a wide range of delicious dishes to excite your taste buds. Be sure to sample these must-try delicacies during your Greece trip plan for an authentic taste of Greece's culinary heritage. Don't forget to book your Greece packages to ensure a memorable and gastronomic tour to Greece through the best tourist places.

Greece.What to Shop in Greece: When it comes to shopping in Greece, there is no shortage of unique and beautiful items to take home as souvenirs. Whether you're looking for extra virgin olive oil for cooking or olive oil-based skincare products, you'll find a wide selection available for purchase. Visit local markets and olive oil shops in cities like Athens, Crete, and Kalamata to sample different varieties and purchase bottles to take home when travelling with Greece packages. Greek Honey: Another must-buy item in Greece is honey, which comes in a variety of flavours and types depending on the region and the flowers the bees feed on. Look for jars of thyme honey from Crete, pine honey from the Peloponnese, or wildflower honey from the mountains of northern Greece when travelling with Greece packages. You can find honey for sale at local markets, speciality food shops, and bee farms throughout the country. Handmade Ceramics: Greek ceramics are prized for their craftsmanship and beauty, with traditional designs dating back centuries. Greek Delicacies: Food lovers will delight in the variety of Greek delicacies available for purchase, including local cheeses, olives, herbs, and spices. Stock up on feta cheese, kalamata olives, oregano, and dried Greek herbs to recreate the flavours of Greece at home. Visit local markets, food shops, and delicatessens in cities like Athens, Thessaloniki, and Chania to sample and purchase these delicious treats. Traditional Textiles: Greek textiles, including rugs, carpets, and woven fabrics, are prized for their craftsmanship and intricate designs. Look for handwoven rugs made from wool or cotton, embroidered tablecloths and linens, and colourful scarves and shawls. Visit local artisans and textile shops in cities like Thessaloniki and Ioannina to browse a wide selection of traditional textiles. Greek Jewellery: Greek jewellery is known for its timeless beauty and intricate designs, with pieces ranging from traditional Byzantine-style crosses to modern, minimalist designs. Look for pieces made from gold, silver, and semi-precious stones, including rings, bracelets, necklaces, and earrings. Greece.Interesting Facts about the Greece place: Greece is a land steeped in history, culture, and natural beauty, with a rich heritage of fascinating facts and lesser-known tidbits waiting to be discovered. Here are 10 interesting facts about Greece that every traveller should know before starting their journey: Greece is often referred to as the birthplace of democracy, as it is the birthplace of Western philosophy, literature, and political thought. The ancient city-state of Athens is credited with laying the foundations of democracy, with citizens participating in decision-making processes through assemblies and councils. Greek mythology is an integral part of the country's cultural heritage, with a pantheon of gods and goddesses, heroes, and mythical creatures. From Zeus, the king of the gods, to Athena, the goddess of wisdom, Greek mythology is filled with captivating stories of love, betrayal, and adventure that continue to inspire artists, writers, and storytellers to this day. The Olympic Games originated in ancient Greece and were held in Olympia, in honour of the Greek god Zeus. The games were first held in 776 BC and continued for over a thousand years before being revived in their modern form in 1896. Today, Greece's Olympic heritage is celebrated with the lighting of the Olympic flame in Olympia before each Olympic Games. Mount Olympus, located in northern Greece, is the highest mountain in the country and is steeped in mythology as the home of the gods. According to Greek mythology, Mount Olympus was the residence of the 12 Olympian gods, including Zeus, Hera, and Poseidon. Today, the mountain is a popular destination for hikers and outdoor enthusiasts, offering stunning views and challenging trails. Greece is home to over 6,000 islands, each with its own unique charm and character. While only around 200 of these islands are inhabited, they offer a diverse range of landscapes, from rugged cliffs and sandy beaches to lush forests and picturesque villages. Some of the most famous tourist places in Greece are its islands, including Santorini, Mykonos, and Crete. Greece is home to 18 UNESCO World Heritage Sites, including iconic landmarks such as the Acropolis of Athens, the medieval city of Rhodes, and the archaeological site of Delphi. These sites are recognised for their cultural significance and contribute to Greece's reputation as a treasure trove of ancient history and archaeology. The ancient Greeks are credited with inventing theatre, with the first known theatre performances dating back to the 6th century BC. Theatrical productions were held as part of religious festivals, with performances featuring masked actors and choral singing. Today, Greece's theatrical heritage is celebrated with the annual Athens and Epidaurus Festival, which showcases both ancient and modern theatre productions. Greece is famous for its tradition of hospitality, known as "philoxenia." In Greek culture, guests are treated with warmth and generosity, and it is considered impolite to refuse hospitality. Visitors to Greece can expect to be greeted with open arms and offered food, drink, and friendship wherever they go. Greek cuisine is renowned for its fresh ingredients, Mediterranean flavours, and healthy cooking techniques. Staples of Greek cuisine include olive oil, feta cheese, olives, tomatoes, and fresh seafood. Must-try dishes include moussaka, souvlaki, Greek salad, and baklava, which can be enjoyed at local tavernas and restaurants throughout the country. The colours blue and white are synonymous with Greece, symbolising the country's stunning coastline and whitewashed buildings. The blue of the Aegean Sea contrasts beautifully with the white-washed walls and blue-domed churches that dot the landscape, creating an iconic and instantly recognisable image of Greece. From its rich history and mythology to its stunning landscapes and delicious cuisine, Greece offers a wealth of experiences waiting to be discovered. Whether you're exploring ancient ruins, lounging on sun-drenched beaches, or savouring traditional dishes, Greece is sure to captivate and enchant you. Be sure to book your Greece packages to make the most of your trip and discover the best tourist places in Greece.
Greece.Greece Culture: Greek culture is deeply rooted in traditions that have been passed down through generations, shaping the country's identity and way of life. Here are a few cultural habits and traditions that are followed in Greece: Family Values: Family plays a central role in Greek culture, with strong bonds between relatives and extended family members. Family gatherings are frequent and often revolve around food, with meals being a time for sharing stories, laughter, and love. Respect for elders is also important, with younger generations showing deference and seeking advice from their elders. Hospitality (Philoxenia): Hospitality is an integral part of Greek culture, with guests being treated with warmth and generosity. It is common for visitors to be offered food, drink, and even accommodation, as refusing hospitality is considered impolite. Whether you're invited into a Greek home or welcomed at a local taverna, you can expect to be treated like family during your Greece travel packages. Music and Dance: Music and dance are integral parts of Greek culture, with traditional folk music and dance being passed down through generations. From lively celebrations at weddings and festivals to impromptu gatherings in village squares, music and dance are a way for Greeks to express their joy, sorrow, and love for life. Coffee Culture: Coffee holds a special place in Greek culture, with coffeehouses (kafeneia) serving as important social hubs where friends gather to chat, play backgammon, and enjoy a cup of strong Greek coffee. Whether sipping a freddo espresso on a sunny terrace or savouring a traditional Greek coffee in a cosy café, coffee culture is an essential part of everyday life in Greece. Religious Festivals: Greece is a predominantly Christian country, and religious festivals play a significant role in Greek culture. Orthodox Christianity is the dominant religion, and religious holidays such as Easter and Christmas are celebrated with great fervour and devotion. From solemn church services to festive processions and feasts, religious festivals are a time for reflection, prayer, and community spirit. Respect for Traditions: Greeks have a deep respect for their cultural heritage and traditions, which are preserved and celebrated through festivals, rituals, and customs. From honouring ancient gods and goddesses to observing age-old superstitions and taboos, Greeks take pride in their cultural identity and strive to pass it on to future generations. From hospitality and family values to religious festivals and coffee culture, Greek culture is a vibrant heritage of traditions that reflects the country's rich history and heritage. Immerse yourself in Greek culture during your Greece travel packages to gain a deeper understanding and appreciation of this fascinating country.

By Road It is only feasible to tour to Baku by road if you are already in one of the neighbouring countries, like as Georgia, Russia, or Iran. Private cabs and long-distance buses are luxurious and often run. Routes from important border cities are handled by Avtovaghzal, the principal bus terminal. Many foreign tourists add a driving excursion over the Caucasus to their Baku holiday packages. It's a fantastic way to take in the scenery and visit undiscovered treasures. By Air The simplest and most practical method to start your Baku visit is still via air. Major Indian cities have direct and connecting flights to Heydar Aliyev International Airport in Baku. It has good connections to airlines, including Azerbaijan Airlines, Turkish Airlines, and Emirates, and is only 25 km from the city centre. To make your trip easier, the majority of Baku tour packages from India include airfare and airport transfers. The city greets you with contemporary charm as soon as you land, and there are many Baku attractions to discover. Flying is the quickest way to go to Baku, whether you're searching for a laid-back vacation package or an exciting Baku holiday packages.
Baku.Best Places To Visit Baku: This dynamic city has a lot to offer, whether your interests lie in spiritual heritage, beach relaxation, or ancient site exploration. With so many things to do in Baku, picking the correct spots can really make your trip more enjoyable. Together, let's explore best things to do in Baku. Famous Places to Visit Here are some of the places to go in Baku: Flame Towers: These eye-catching buildings represent contemporary Baku tourism and light up the night. There is no comparison to the view from nearby Highland Park. Icherisheher: The Old City (Icherisheher), a UNESCO World Heritage site, is a tangle of historic structures, cobblestone streets, and quaint courtyards. It's a must-see for history, gastronomy, and photography on any itinerary of best places to visit in Baku. Centre Heydar Aliyev: A favourite among art and architecture enthusiasts, this fluid-shaped skyscraper is an architectural wonder. An essential component of every Baku tour package. The Maiden Tower: This 12th-century tower, one of the most famous Baku tourist spots, provides breathtaking views of the city and the Caspian Sea. Most Baku trip packages include it as a crucial component. Palace of the Shirvanshahs: In the centre of the Old City, this exquisitely maintained 15th-century palace offers a glimpse into royal history. Beaches/ Hill Stations to Visit Here are some of the best places to see in Baku, when it comes to beaches and hill stations: Shikhov Beach: Families and water enthusiasts will adore this well-known beach, which is only a short drive from the city. An enjoyable choice for your Baku vacation package this summer. Crescent Beach: This beach has pools and cafes and is family-friendly, safe, and clean. One of the most tranquil Baku tourist places. Bilgah Beach: Bilgah Beach is a tranquil haven with golden sands and excellent resorts nearby. Ideal for a laid-back afternoon while on your tour of the best places to visit in Baku. Amburan Beach Club: This exclusive beach club on the Bilgah coast is the ideal getaway if you're seeking a little extravagance on your Baku trip packages. Dalga Beach Aquapark: A fantastic addition to any Baku tour packages from India, Dalga Beach Aquapark is a beach resort near Mardakan that is perfect for a day of sunbathing and water slides. Temples to Visit Ateshgah Fire Temple: Ateshgah Fire Temple is a historic and spiritual site that was used by Zoroastrians and Hindus. It is one of the most unusual sights, making it one of the best things to do in Baku. Juma Mosque: Juma Mosque is a serene monument with exquisite Islamic calligraphy and design that is located in the Old City. Bibi-Heybat Mosque: An architectural wonder and hallowed things to see in Baku, it offers a peaceful setting for introspection and cultural immersion. Taza Pir Mosque: Taza Pir Mosque is a calm, hospitable mosque that provides a glimpse of the city's spiritual life, despite not always being on the top tourist itinerary. Heydar Mosque: It is well-known for its imposing architecture and white marble façade, which captures the essence of Azerbaijan's contemporary Islamic identity.
Baku.Cities To Cover: You may experience the finest of both modern flair and old-world charm when you reserve one of the best Baku tour packages. The Old City, Icherisheher, a UNESCO World Heritage Site that adds a profoundly historical flavour to your Baku vacation, is undoubtedly where your tour of Baku things to see will start. Picture-perfect views from Highland Park, serene walks along Baku Boulevard, and architectural wonders like the Flame Towers are all included in the Baku tour package. Nizami Street is a great place for shoppers, and the Heydar Aliyev Centre is striking for its contemporary architecture. When choosing Baku packages from india, be sure to include the famous Maiden Tower, the ethereal Ateshgah Fire Temple, and the ancient petroglyphs of Gobustan on your itinerary of best things to do in Baku. These are Baku's top tourist destinations and are the best Baku places to visit. The best season to visit Baku with family are spring and fall, when the great weather allows you to take advantage of all that Baku has to offer.
Baku.How To Choose The Best Baku Tour Package: The quality of your Baku vacation can be greatly influenced by the tour package you choose. Here are some tips to help you pick the option that best suits your journey of Baku things to see: Choose your budget and travel style: Determine the number of days you wish to spend first. The majority of Baku packages last four to six nights, which is ideal for exploring the city and its neighbouring Baku attractions. There is a Baku packages for every traveller, regardless of whether they are planning an expensive vacation or a luxurious retreat. Recognise your point of interest: Do you have a passion for history? Seek a Baku vacation package that includes entry to Shirvanshah Palace, Maiden Tower, and the Old City. Into the outdoors? Ensure that Gobustan and mud volcanoes are included in the Baku tour. Prior to making a reservation, read reviews: Look through to find frank reviews. It assists you in selecting a well-reviewed Baku vacation package and avoiding Baku tour companies that don’t align with you. Check what's included?: Verify whether hotel accommodations, airport transportation, guided excursions, and meals are included in your Baku travel package. Group discounts are available on several Baku travel packages from India, but some also permit personalised arrangements. Consider the Season: The best seasons for your tour to Baku are spring and fall because they are neither too hot nor too chilly and are excellent for sightseeing. Make reservations in advance: During the busiest time of year, Baku trip packages sell out fast. You might also get better hotel selections and Baku trip cost from India if you book early.
Baku.Baku Currency: One important consideration while organising a trip to Baku is the Azerbaijani Manat, or AZN, which is the local currency. Before you travel to Baku, it's a good idea to learn a little bit about the official currency you'll be using, along with the best time to go Baku. One Manat is equal to 100 qəpik, which is comparable to paise, and the symbol for the Manat is ₼. The denominations of the coins (qəpik) are 1, 3, 5, 10, 20, and 50, while the banknotes are ₼1, ₼5, ₼10, ₼20, ₼50, ₼100, and ₼200. Knowing the current exchange rate is helpful when comparing the cost of a trip to Baku from India, as of late July 2025, one AZN is equivalent to roughly ₹51.53. However, as exchange rates change, make sure to check before travelling. Just so you know, it's difficult to find Azerbaijani Manats in India. Therefore, be sure to account for currency exchange when you reserve your Baku trip package. When they land, most travellers convert their US dollars or euros at banks, airports, or currency kiosks. The majority of stores, eateries, and lodging facilities in urban areas like Baku accept international credit and debit cards. However, it's a good idea to have some cash in Manat if you intend to visit the local bazaars or smaller places outside the city. Knowing how to handle your finances will help you plan your Baku vacation package effectively, whether you're travelling for a romantic weekend, a cultural retreat, or simply a new experience. The cost of a typical trip to Baku from India can vary depending on what is included, such as airfare, lodging, local transportation, and sightseeing. When looking at Baku travel packages, pay attention to what is provided because many of them include meals, transfers, and Baku tour guide. To put it briefly, arrange your money, review the specifics of your Baku vacation package, and you're ready for a fun and easy tour of Baku. Being financially prepared just enhances the city's exquisite fusion of architecture, culture, and Baku food!

table_of_content: Historical and Cultural Importance of Mathura
Traditions and Rituals Practised in Mathura
Things to Know While Visiting Mathura
Mathura.Introduction: Mathura is one of the most esteemed pilgrimages that one can go on in India. This holy city is closely tied to the pages of Hindu mythology and history and has always been a major destination for spiritual tourism. If you are interested in going on a holy journey to this city, you can consider opting for the Mathura tour packages by Thomas Cook. Find all you need to know about Mathura tourism on this page.
Mathura.Most Popular Mathura Tour Packages: Tour Packages NameDurationPriceUttar Pradesh - Varanasi Getaway2 Nights 3 DaysRs. ₹13 500Mathura Vrindavan Agra With Bateshwar Dham Tour5 Nights 6 DaysRs. ₹37 100
Mathura.Historical and Cultural Importance of Ma: The city of Mathura in Uttar Pradesh is a very important place in Hinduism. It is the birthplace of Lord Krishna, the eighth avatar of Lord Vishnu. Mathura is also a part of the Sapta Puri, meaning it is one of the seven cities considered sacred for Hindus. Just 15 km from Mathura is Vrindavan, Lord Krishna’s spiritual home and the twin city of Mathura. The mention of Mathura is even found in the Ramayana, the oldest Indian epic. This is why people of the Hindu faith, especially the devotees of Lord Krishna, are drawn towards this city for pilgrimage. However, Mathura’s immense cultural and historical heritage doesn’t just end there. It has been an active centre of Brahmanism, Jainism and Buddhism for decades in the state of Uttar Pradesh. The incredible Mathura culture comprises regional art, festivals and religion, making it an iconic site for not only spiritual but also cultural tourism. The rich history of Mathura can be dated back to 2500 years in the past. The land had been a part of many major empires, including the Kushan Empire under Kanishka. The mentions of this city can also be found in the accounts of the celebrated Chinese ambassador, Fa Hien, who came to India back in the 5th century CE. Explore the religious wonders and prominent temples at Mathura with the handpicked Mathura tour packages by Thomas Cook.
Mathura.Traditions and Rituals Practised in Math: The rich heritage, culture and tradition of Mathura have an immense impact on Indian culture. The regional art and festivals of Mathura are incredibly popular around the globe. The region celebrates major events and festivals like Janmashtami, Govardhan Puja, Guru Purnima, Rath Yatra, etc., which are marked by elaborate rituals, colourful dances and songs, and so on. There are also unique traditions followed in Mathura that are an important part of its culture, such as Raasleela, a dance-drama depicting Krishna’s dance with the gopis of Braj, Charkula, a dramatic dance with earthen lamps on the head of female dances during the time of Holi, Rasiya, traditional folk songs from Braj area, etc. If you want to experience these events, make sure to plan the Mathura itinerary accordingly.
Mathura.Places to Visit in Mathura: Here are some popular religious sites and unique attractions in the region that you can add to your Mathura itinerary; Other religious places to visit in Mathura Govardhan Hill Govardhan Hill is only 25 km from the city and is a major attraction that you can visit on your tour to Mathura. As per legend, Lord Krishna raised this hill with one finger to save his hometown from thunderstorms and heavy rain. He also stated that this hill is not distinct from him. This is why his followers worship and adore the hill same as him. Govardhan Hill is one of the most important religious places to visit in Mathura. Radha Kund It is one of the most prominent holy places to see in Mathura. On the behest of Goddess Radha, Lord Krishna created a lake, called Shyam Kund, so he could wash off his sin of killing a demon bull named Aristasura. Goddess Radha also created a lake with her friends, which came to be called Radha Kund. It is believed, whoever takes a dip in Radha Kund will be blessed with “Prem-Bhakti”. Vishram Ghat It is a sacred bathing ghat on the banks of the Yamuna River, only 3 km from Mathura city centre. One of the best things to do in Mathura for devotees is to visit this ghat to take a holy bath and perform traditional rituals. According to legends, Lord Krishna used this ghat as a resting place after defeating the wicked king, Kansa. It is one of the most iconic Mathura tourist places.
Mathura.Unique features of Mathura: Yamuna is one of the most sacred rivers according to Hinduism in India. The district of Mathura is situated on the beautiful banks of this river. People on their Mathura trip love to visit this river to embrace its natural beauty and take ritualistic baths to wash away their sins. The landscape of Mathura is also blessed by a majestic hill called Govardhana Hill, which is also an important sacred site. Nidhivan of Vrindavan, in Mathura district, is a sacred forest in the region, associated with the legend of Sri Krishna and many mysteries. You can hire a local Mathura travel guide if you want more information while visiting these sites.
Mathura.Temples to Visit in Mathura: Here are the most important and popular temples in Mathura, Uttar Pradesh; Shri Krishna Janmasthan Temple Shri Krishna Janmashthan Temple is one of the most sacred temples at Mathura. This shrine is constructed around the jail cell where Lord Krishna's parents were held captive by his cruel uncle, Kansa. Being the birthplace of Sri Krishna, the shrine holds a place of massive religious and cultural significance for Hindus. The complex also houses Keshavdev Temple, Garbha Griha Shrine, Bhagavata Bhavan, and Potra Kund. You can easily find Mathura tour packages featuring this temple on our website. Dwarkadhish Temple The Dwarkadhish Temple is one of Mathura’s most beautiful temples, known for its stunning architecture and intricate murals. The shrine is dedicated to Lord Dwarkadhish, a form of Lord Krishan called Dwarkanath. The black marble idol of the deity is located within the temple. Some of the finest works of craftsmanship are found within this temple in the form of ceiling paintings, wall murals, architectural patterns, etc. It is one of the top places to visit in Mathura and a common inclusion of popular Mathura tour packages. Radha Vallabh Mandir The Shri Radha Vallabh Mandir is one of the seven shrines of Thakur of Vrindavan. It is one of the purest symbols of the bond of love between Radha Rani and Lord Krishna, regarded as the rarest form of “Ras Bhakti”. It is the RadhaVallabh sect’s main shrine and one of the most important places to visit in Mathura for religious tours. The principal deity at this temple is Radha Rani, but there is no idol of the goddess in the shrine. The idol at the temple is considered the embodiment of both Radha Rani and Lord Krishna, highlighting their unified soul. Placed near the idol of Shri RadhaVallabh is a crown, which represents Goddess Radha. It is a standard inclusion in many Mathura tour packages. Banke Bihari Temple One of the most famous Mathura places to visit for spiritual tours in Vrindavan's Banke Bihari Temple. This holy shrine is dedicated to Lord Krishna, who stands in the famous “tribhanga” pose, which is bent at three angles. Here, “Banke” means “bent in three places” and “Bihari” means “supreme enjoyer”. The temple showcases the pure devotion and love of Swami Haridas Ji for Sri Radhe-Krishna. The darshan of the presiding deity at this temple is never continuous. It is done with intermittent pauses by the use of curtains being drawn on him. It is believed if a devotee looks into the eyes of the lord long enough, they will lose consciousness. It is one of the most visited temples in Mathura. Temple Name Opening Time & Closing Time Distance from Mathura city centre Shri Krishna Janmasthan Temple 5 AM- 12 PM & 4 PM- 9:30 PM 2 km Shri Dwarkadhish Temple 6:30 AM- 10:30 AM & 4 PM- 7 PM 3 km Radha Vallabh Mandir 5 AM- 12 PM & 6 PM- 9 PM 12 km Banke Bihari Temple 7:45 AM- 12 PM & 5:30 PM- 9:30 PM (Summer) and 8:45 AM- 1 PM & 4:30 PM- 8:30 PM (Winter) 11 km
Mathura.Things to Know While Visiting Mathura: Here are some things that you should know prior to the Mathura trip; If you are visiting Mathura during the Holi festival, consider wearing white clothing that you don’t mind throwing away as you are bound to get colours on you. When visiting the temples and any other sacred sites in Mathura, make sure to dress modestly and wear non-revealing clothes. Wear clothes that cover your shoulders and knees. Remove shoes outside the temple premises before entering for darshan. There are certain bans and restrictions on the sale of meat and liquor across various places in the district. Be sure to respect the local culture and traditions of Mathura. You can hire a Mathura tour guide if you want to learn about the local legends as you explore the sacred sites. Cameras and electronic equipment are not allowed inside many Mathura temples due to security concerns. Some even have restrictions on mobile photography.
Mathura.Best Time to Visit Mathura: Go through the following pointers to find out the best time for your Mathura trip; Summer in Mathura The summer season in this region covers the months of April to June. The weather is extremely hot during peak summer, while the humidity stays relatively low. Temperatures can rise up to 45°C during these months, which makes summer a bad time for Mathura sightseeing and spiritual tourism. Besides the low tourist crowd, you can get good discounts on hotels, transfers and Mathura tour packages during this season. Monsoon in Mathura Monsoon in the region starts in July and ends in September, bringing much-needed relief from the extreme summer heat. The average temperature range of 25-35°C and frequent rainfall don’t stop tourists and devotees from making a Mathura trip plan for this season. Many consider it the best time for Mathura travel as it coincides with Shri Krishna Janmashtami, a major festival in the region. Find Mathura tour packages for monsoon on Thomas Cook. Winter in Mathura Winter, which stretches from October to March, is the peak season for Mathura tourism. The soothing temperature and clear weather are ideal for temple visits and general Mathura sightseeing. However, do note that the mercury often drops to the low 10s during this season, so pack warm clothes for the Mathura trip. The popular festival of Braj ki Holi falls during this season, making it the best time to visit Mathura.
Mathura.Quotes and Inspirations: People often define the experience of their Mathura visit to be divine, which is something we felt during our visit. On our Mathura tour, as we were approaching the holy city, our hearts were overflowing with excitement. We were greeted with warm hospitality by the locals from the get-go, which induced a sense of belonging from deep within. As we were walking towards the revered Shri Krishna Janmasthan Temple, a quote from Krishna-Dwaipayana Vyasa was echoing in our minds. It read, “We behold what we are, and we are what we behold.” This statement is so true that we need a clear conscience to fully grasp its true meaning. After waiting in line for the Darshan for some time, we caught a glimpse of the idol in the temple and as we poured our hearts and souls through the praying hand gesture, we felt a moment of calm and happiness that resonated with everything we stood for. Our goal was to seek inner peace and divine guidance, which was fulfilled as we navigated through the holy sites of Mathura and made efforts towards learning more about its historical, cultural and spiritual heritage.
Mathura.What to Eat in Mathura?: Let the delicious local food fill your hungry stomach as you explore the famous Mathura tourist attractions. Here is a list of Mathura famous food items that you can consider trying on your visit; Aloo Kachori The love for Aloo Kachori is nationwide and if you want to try this deep-fried, crispy pastry stuffed with delicious potato filling with tangy and spicy seasoning, Mathura is a great choice. This famous food in Mathura is widely available as street food and is paired with yoghurt or hot or spicy chutney. Ghewar Ghewar is a famous food in Mathura, mainly prepared during the festive season. This dessert is made using gee, all-purpose flour, and milk, which is then fried into golden, honey-comb-shaped discs and soaked in syrup. Garnished with silver leaf, saffron and nuts, this Mathura food item has a sweet taste and soft texture that melts in the mouth. Rabri Rabri is a popular Mathura sweet dish, ideal for individuals with a sweet tooth. This Mathura food item is made from milk and sugar and has a creamy texture. It is an iconic traditional food item that you can try on your Mathura travel. Baingan Bharta Mathura cuisine mainly focuses on vegetarian recipes and Baingan Bharta is one of its most delicious dishes. It is made by roasting eggplants on slow flame, which gives it its iconic smoky flavour. It is then mashed with garlic, onions, tomatoes and a few basic spices. It is a must-try Mathura famous food item.
Mathura.Shopping in Mathura: Enjoy shopping for gifts and souvenirs at Mathura’s main markets, such as; Tilak Dwar Market This market, also known as the Holy Gate market, is one of the best places to visit in Mathura for shopping. It has plenty of shops selling all kinds of products, especially puja and religious items. It also has many sweet shops from where you can buy delicious sweets. Chatta Bazar On your Mathura tour, visit Chatta Bazaar. It is one of the region’s oldest bazaars, known for housing several shops selling beautiful clothes, religious souvenirs and silver and brass accessories. The popular Kanhaiya Cloth Market is also within its vicinity. Some signature products to buy at this market are silver anklets and Shringar clothes for idols. Krishna Nagar Market The Krishan Nagar Market is one of the best places in Mathura for shopping. It is a place where you can buy all your daily-use products, from vegetables to pet food, you name it, they have it. Some popular things to buy from this market are quality leather goods, souvenirs and dress materials. Lal Bazaar On your Mathura city tour, make sure to drop by Lal Bazaar. The variety of products available in this market is truly impressive. If you have the right bargaining skills, you can get great deals when shopping for gifts and souvenirs. Products available for purchase here include brass idols, religious items, household puja utensils, etc.

Moreover, apart from sunbathing, strolling on the warm sand and watching breathtaking sunrises and sunsets, Neil Island also offers its visitors the opportunity to engage in many thrilling activities such as glass-bottom boat rides, swimming and snorkelling.Overall, Neil Island is a veritably beautiful and superb travel destination that you must visit at least once in your life. Places to Visit Known For Bharatpur Beach Beautiful beach providing stunning views of ocean and sunsets, glass-bottomed boat rides,an ideal spot for swimming, basking and viewing huge varieties of brightly hued coral reefs found here Laxmanpur Beach Pristine white sands, clear blue water, unique rock formation or coral formation; shallow water allows snorkelling and swimming; an apt place to view lovey sunsets and bask in the tropical sun Howrah Bridge (Natural Bridge) Famous sightseeing spot, naturally formed bridge which is a continuation of striking rock formations from Laxmanpur Beach, formed over years of impact from seawater washing away parts of cliff ridges Sir Hugh Rose Island Also known as small Neil, nesting grounds for breeding turtles, wildlife sanctuary, requires a permit from Deputy Ranger Officer to pay a visit Neil Kendra Central location between Bharatpur and Laxmanpur Beaches, vantage point providing a brilliant look of the island seeming arc-shaped, small but attractive village market Sitapur Beach Remarkable open beach is widely known for limestone formations, gorgeous sunrises and enveloping blankets of thick green vegetation

Maison Waucquez, 20 Rue de Sables, Central Brussels Belgian Royal Museum of Fine Arts Divided into two parts namely Musee d’art ancient and the Musee d’art modern, this museum is a wide display of various fine arts that occupy an important place in the Belgian history. It is also referred to as the most mysterious places to visit in Brussels Place des Palais 7, Brussels Abbaye de la Cambre Housing an art college and the National Geographical Institute, this collection of beautiful French gardens is a treat to witness. Rue du Monastere, Saint Gilles Brussels Grand Place Last but not the least, located in the centre of the old town of Brussels; this palace is one among the famous monuments of the city. Melbourne is one such city where a person can experience four kinds of weathers in a single day. This is because Melbourne is on the boundaries of the hot inland areas & oceanic water. It can receive rainfall at any point of the day and witnesses dry summers and cool winters. The weather on average is pleasant. Fed Square is of artistic interest. It has got many marvellous pieces of Art that showcase the Australian history. Fed square has many art exhibitions, musicians, screening of films and the street artist will make you enjoy your time here. Southbank has got many tourist attractions like the tallest residential building names Eureka towers, Melbourne’s Art Centre, National Gallery of Museum. Casinos, bars and restaurants are also located in the Southbank. Melbourne Central is beautifully constructed with glass domes. Shopping, Cinemas, Food courts will definitely make you stay here and relax! Must Visit Places when in Melbourne: No. of Days Significance Royal Botanical Garden The lush green beautiful garden which is 20 minutes away from the city. Werribee Open Range Zoo Enjoy safari rides and over-night stay in this zoo. A very well-maintained zoo with rich native wildlife like Kangaroos, Koalas and other animals like zebra, monkeys, rhinos etc. Melbourne Central Beautifully constructed glass dome with 5 floors is one of its kind in the world. Shopping, Cafes, Cinemas are loved by all. Federation Square Famous for its Art museums, exhibitions, large screening of films. Southbank A must visits place of Melbourne. Casinos and cafes are quite popular here.

faq.question: What are Delhi holidays famous for?
faq.answer: Delhi holidays are considered to be a vibrant escapade, offering a history and heritage filled soul with a pulsating urban heart. They are most famous for the expansive list of famous monuments, from centuries-old to modern day marvels, for a richly amalgamated food culture, for its entertainment and nightlife experiences and as a base for excursions to

faq.question: What is the best season to visit Delhi?
faq.answer: The best season to visit Delhi is between November and February, which is the winter time. Temperatures can drop as low as 3 degrees, but with the right woollens you will be perfectly comfortable to enjoy your stay.

faq.question: What language is spoken in Delhi?
faq.answer: The main local language spoken in Delhi is Hindi, however, you can hear many other Indian languages being spoken across the city, owing to its multiculturalism. English is also widely spoken, especially around the tourism sector.

faq.question: What is the ideal duration for a Delhi tour?
faq.answer: To sample a bit of all that the city has to offer, it is advisable to book a 3-4 night Delhi tour package at the least, so that you have plenty of excursion and relaxation time. If you are looking for a Delhi holiday that takes you further into the golden triangle circuit (Delhi, Agra, Jaipur), then opt for a 6-7 night Delhi tour.

faq.question: Is Delhi safe for female or solo travellers?
faq.answer: Delhi’s reputation for safety has seen its ups and downs, but travellers needn’t worry. You can visit and explore the city safer as a tourist. It is a good idea to stick to your itinerary and to well-known places, especially when it comes to exploring the nightlife. Private taxis are available to ensure safe commute within Delhi.

faq.question: What should one pack for a Delhi tour?
faq.answer: What to pack for your Delhi tour purely depends on the season of your visit as well as your interests. If travelling in the summer, light cotton clothes is the way to go. For monsoon, be sure to carry some rain proof gear. While proper winter woollens are essential for a winter visit. Additionally, carry your own electronics and leisure travel items to stay entertained while on the go.

faq.question: What are some popular shopping areas in Delhi?
faq.answer: Some popular shopping areas in Delhi that you can explore during your Delhi tour are Connaught Place, Chandni Chowk, Sarojini Nagar, Dilli Haat, Janpath, and Khan Market.

faq.question: Which is the popular festival celebrated in Delhi?
faq.answer: One of the most popular festivals celebrated in Delhi is Diwali, the festival of lights.

faq.question: How can I get around in Delhi?
faq.answer: To get around in Delhi during your Delhi trip, you can use various modes of transportation such as the metro, buses, auto-rickshaws, taxis, and cycle-rickshaws.

faq.question: What are some traditional foods to try in Delhi?
faq.answer: The traditional food of Delhi that you must try during your Delhi tour includes dishes such as Chole Bhature or Kulche, Butter Chicken, Kebabs, Paranthas, Chaat, Biryani, and Nihari.

faq.question: What are the best souvenirs to buy from Delhi?
faq.answer: Some of the best souvenirs to buy from Delhi during your Delhi tour are handicrafts, silver jewelry, spices, tea, hand-woven carpets, leather goods, and traditional Indian clothes such as sarees and salwar kameez.

faq.question: What are some good areas to stay in Delhi for tourists?
faq.answer: Some good areas to stay in Delhi for tourists are Connaught Place, Karol Bagh, and Pahar Ganj, which are conveniently located and offer a range of accommodation options. Many Delhi tour packages include accommodations in these areas to provide easy access to the city's top attractions and amenities.

faq.question: Can I visit Delhi's monuments at night?
faq.answer: Some of Delhi's monuments can be visited at night, but the timings vary depending on the monument and the season. Some of the Delhi tour packages may include night tours of popular attractions, such as the Red Fort, Qutub Minar, and India Gate, which can be a unique and memorable experience.

Delhi.Best Time to Visit Delhi: Delhi, India's bustling capital, beckons travellers year-round with its rich history, vibrant culture, and diverse experiences. However, choosing the best time to visit Delhi can greatly enhance your travel experience and ensure you make the most of your trip with all the iconic places to see in Delhi. Each season in Delhi offers its own unique charm and allure, catering to different preferences and interests. Spring (March to April) Spring, from March to April, is another great time to visit Delhi, as the city bursts into bloom with vibrant flowers and lush greenery. The weather during this time is warm and pleasant, perfect for outdoor activities such as picnics in the city's beautiful parks and gardens. Explore the famous tourist places in Delhi, such as the Lotus Temple and Humayun's Tomb, or embark on a Delhi one day tour to discover the top attractions in the city. Winter (November to February) One of the most popular times to visit Delhi is during the winter months, from November to February. The weather during this time is mild and pleasant, with clear skies and cool temperatures, making it ideal for exploring the numerous Delhi tourist attractions. From iconic landmarks like the Red Fort and India Gate to the bustling markets of Chandni Chowk and Connaught Place, Delhi comes alive in the winter months, offering visitors a truly enchanting experience. Monsoon (July to September) Monsoon, from July to September, brings relief from the summer heat with refreshing showers and cooler temperatures. While the rains may disrupt outdoor activities at times, they also lend a magical charm to the city, washing away the dust and grime and rejuvenating its lush greenery. Explore the famous Delhi tourist places, such as the Qutub Minar and Akshardham Temple, or take a leisurely stroll along the banks of the Yamuna River, enjoying the cool breeze and scenic views. Summer (May to June) Summer, from May to June, can be quite hot and humid in Delhi, with temperatures often soaring above 40 degrees Celsius. While the scorching heat may deter some travellers, it's also the perfect time to explore the city's indoor attractions, such as museums, art galleries, and shopping malls. Plus, with fewer tourists around, you can enjoy a more relaxed and laid-back experience during the Delhi city tour. No matter what time of year you choose to visit Delhi, our Delhi tour packages are designed to provide you with the ultimate travel experience, ensuring that you make the most of your time in this vibrant and dynamic city. Explore the top places to visit in Delhi, immerse yourself in its rich culture and heritage, and create memories that will last a lifetime.
Delhi.How to reach Delhi?: Reaching Delhi, the vibrant capital city of India is easily accomplished through various modes of transportation, catering to travellers from across the country and around the globe. Whether you're arriving by air, rail, road, or water, Delhi offers convenient and accessible options to suit every traveller's needs for their journey with Delhi holiday packages. By Rail Delhi is well-connected to various parts of the country through an extensive rail network, with several major railway stations serving as important hubs. New Delhi Railway Station, Old Delhi Railway Station, and Hazrat Nizamuddin Railway Station are among the busiest terminals, offering a plethora of train services catering to both domestic and international travellers. From luxurious Rajdhani Express trains to budget-friendly local services, there are plenty of options to choose from when planning your tour to Delhi by rail. By Air Indira Gandhi International Airport serves as the primary gateway to Delhi, connecting the city to major domestic and international destinations. With numerous airlines operating flights for a tour to Delhi, travellers can easily find convenient options to reach the city. Upon arrival, taxis, buses, and metro services are readily available to transport visitors to their desired destinations within Delhi, making it a hassle-free option for those seeking to explore the city. By Water While Delhi is not directly accessible by water, nearby cities like Agra and Mathura offer boat services along the Yamuna River, providing a unique and scenic way to explore the region. Additionally, the National Capital Region (NCR) boasts several picturesque lakes and water bodies, where visitors can enjoy boating and water sports activities as part of their Delhi itinerary. By Road Tour to Delhi by road is a convenient option for those coming from nearby cities and states. National Highways such as NH1, NH2, and NH8 connect Delhi to major cities like Jaipur, Agra, and Chandigarh, offering smooth and well-maintained routes for road trips. Private buses, interstate buses, and taxis ply regularly between Delhi and neighbouring regions, providing travellers with flexible and affordable transportation options. No matter which mode of transportation you choose, a tour to Delhi is just the first step in your journey to discovering the rich culture, heritage, and vibrancy of this historic city. With our Delhi tour packages, travellers can seamlessly navigate the top attractions, embark on Delhi tour guide tours, and create lasting memories of their visit to the capital of India. Delhi.Places to Visit in Delhi: Delhi, the vibrant capital city of India, is a treasure trove of historical landmarks, cultural attractions, and culinary delights. From ancient monuments to bustling markets and serene gardens, Delhi offers a plethora of experiences for travellers to explore. Here are some places to visit in Delhi that should be on every visitor's Delhi itinerary: India Gate Standing tall as a memorial to the soldiers who sacrificed their lives during World War I, India Gate is one of Delhi's most iconic landmarks. Visitors can stroll along the lawns surrounding the monument, paying homage to the brave souls who fought for their country on their visit with Delhi holiday packages. Red Fort A UNESCO World Heritage site and an iconic symbol of India's rich history, the Red Fort is a must-visit destination when travelling with Delhi holiday packages. Built by Mughal Emperor Shah Jahan, this magnificent fortress showcases exquisite architecture and hosts the annual Independence Day celebrations. Qutub Minar Dating back to the 12th century, the Qutub Minar is the tallest brick minaret in the world and a UNESCO World Heritage site. Explore the intricately carved sandstone tower and its surrounding archaeological complex, which includes ancient ruins and tombs with Delhi holiday packages. Chandni Chowk Dive into the bustling heart of Old Delhi with a visit to Chandni Chowk with our Delhi holiday packages, one of the oldest and busiest markets in the city. From aromatic spice markets to narrow lanes brimming with street food stalls, Chandni Chowk offers a sensory overload of sights, sounds, and smells. Humayun's Tomb A masterpiece of Mughal architecture, Humayun's Tomb is another UNESCO World Heritage site that captivates visitors with its grandeur and beauty. Wander through the lush gardens and marvel at the symmetry and elegance of this historic mausoleum with our Delhi holiday packages. Akshardham Temple Embark on a spiritual journey at Akshardham Temple, a sprawling complex dedicated to Hindu culture and spirituality. Explore the intricately carved stone structures, lush gardens, and captivating exhibitions that celebrate India's rich heritage when travelling with Delhi holiday packages. Lotus Temple Renowned for its striking lotus-shaped design, the Lotus Temple is a Bahá'í House of Worship and a serene oasis amidst the urban chaos of places to visit in Delhi. Visitors can admire the temple's unique architecture and participate in silent meditation sessions. Gurudwara Bangla Sahib Experience the warmth and hospitality of the Sikh community at Gurudwara Bangla Sahib, one of the most prominent Sikh shrines in Delhi. Visitors can partake in the community kitchen (langar) and witness the serene beauty of the Sarovar (holy pond). With our Delhi tour packages, travellers can seamlessly navigate the city's top attractions, embark on Delhi tour guide tours, and create lasting memories of their visit to the capital of India. Let us be your trusted companion as you explore the best places to visit in Delhi and immerse yourself in its rich culture, heritage, and vibrancy.
Delhi.Best Hill Stations near Delhi: While Delhi is not blessed with hill stations or beaches within its borders, it serves as a gateway to some of the best hill stations and beaches in India. Travellers looking to escape the hustle and bustle of city life can explore these serene destinations, which are easily accessible from Delhi. There are no hill stations in Delhi, but some amazing places can be reached by car. Here are some of them: Shimla Nestled amidst the majestic Himalayas, Shimla is one of the most popular hill stations near Delhi. Visitors can explore the Mall Road, visit the historic Viceregal Lodge, and take a ride on the UNESCO World Heritage toy train to experience the breathtaking beauty of the surrounding hills with our Delhi travel packages. Nainital Surrounded by lush green hills and tranquil lakes, Nainital is a charming hill station known for its picturesque beauty and pleasant climate. Visitors can enjoy boating on Naini Lake, trek to Tiffin Top for panoramic views, and explore the vibrant markets of Mall Road with our Delhi travel packages. Mussoorie Fondly referred to as the "Queen of Hills," Mussoorie captivates visitors with its colonial charm and panoramic vistas. Highlights include a cable car ride to Gun Hill for sweeping views, a stroll along Camel's Back Road, and a visit to the serene Kempty Falls with our Delhi travel packages. Manali Renowned for its scenic beauty and adventurous activities, Manali is a paradise for nature lovers and adrenaline junkies alike. Must-visit attractions include Solang Valley for skiing and paragliding, Rohtang Pass for stunning views of the Himalayas, and Hadimba Devi Temple for its unique architecture.
Delhi.Delhi Nightlife: Hauz Khas Village Known for its unique blend of historic charm and modern nightlife, Hauz Khas Village offers trendy bars and cafes like Hauz Khas Social and Mia Bella. The village is a favourite spot in many Delhi travel packages, catering to both locals and tourists. Pubs and Bars in Connaught Place The heart of Delhi's nightlife, Connaught Place, boasts an array of lively pubs and bars. From the chic Social to the vibrant Lord of the Drinks, this area is perfect for those seeking a bustling atmosphere. Delhi travel packages often highlight Connaught Place as a must-visit for a taste of the city's nightlife. Rooftop Lounges For a more sophisticated evening, rooftop lounges such as Sky Lounge in Connaught Place and The Sky High in Ansal Plaza offer stunning city views. These lounges provide an elegant setting, making them popular inclusions in Delhi travel packages. Clubs in South Delhi South Delhi is home to some of the city's most famous nightclubs, including Kitty Su and Privee. These clubs offer energetic dance floors and top DJs, drawing in crowds looking for a high-energy night out. They are essential stops in any comprehensive Delhi travel package. Live Music Venues For music enthusiasts, Delhi's live music scene is vibrant and diverse. Venues like The Piano Man Jazz Club and Hard Rock Cafe host performances from both local and international artists. Delhi travel packages frequently recommend these spots for a night of live entertainment. Food and Night Markets For a more relaxed night, Delhi's food and night markets, like those in Chandni Chowk and INA Market, provide a rich culinary experience. Street food tours are often part of Delhi travel packages, offering a delicious exploration of the city's nightlife through its diverse cuisine.
Delhi.Best Honeymoon Destinations Near Delhi: Delhi, with its blend of rich history, vibrant culture, and modern amenities, offers a plethora of options for couples seeking a romantic getaway. Whether you're looking for a serene escape amidst nature or a luxurious retreat in the lap of luxury, Delhi trip packages have something to offer every honeymooner. Explore the enchanting landscapes of Rohtang Pass, stroll hand in hand through the apple orchards of Naggar, and indulge in adventurous activities like paragliding and river rafting. Delhi.Interesting Things to do in Delhi: Delhi, the vibrant capital city of India, offers a plethora of exciting things to do in Delhi and experiences for visitors to enjoy. From exploring historical landmarks to indulging in culinary delights, there's something for everyone to discover in this bustling metropolis. Visit the Museums and Galleries Dive into the rich cultural heritage of Delhi by visiting its numerous museums and art galleries among the Delhi places to visit. Explore the National Museum, which houses a vast collection of artefacts spanning thousands of years of Indian history and culture. Marvel at the exquisite artworks on display at the National Gallery of Modern Art, showcasing the talents of renowned Indian artists. For a glimpse into Delhi's colonial past, visit the Gandhi Smriti, where Mahatma Gandhi spent the last days of his life, or explore the heritage precinct of Mehrauli Archaeological Park, home to ancient ruins and monuments dating back to the Mughal era with our Delhi trip packages. Explore Old Delhi's Spice Markets Immerse yourself in the sensory delights of Old Delhi's spice markets, where vibrant colours and intoxicating aromas fill the air while touring the Delhi places to visit. Take a Delhi travel guide through narrow lanes lined with shops selling an array of spices, herbs, and dried fruits. Delight your taste buds with authentic street food delicacies like chaat, samosas, and jalebis as you navigate the bustling streets of Chandni Chowk. Don't forget to haggle with local vendors for the best deals and immerse yourself in the vibrant atmosphere of this historic neighbourhood when travelling with Delhi trip packages. Enjoy a Sunset Boat Ride Escape the hustle and bustle of the city and unwind with a peaceful boat ride on the serene waters of the Yamuna River. Watch the sun dip below the horizon as you glide past historic landmarks like the Red Fort and Jama Masjid, bathed in the golden hues of dusk. Delight in the tranquillity of the moment and capture breathtaking views of the city skyline as it comes alive with twinkling lights. Take a Food Tour Embark on a culinary adventure through Delhi's diverse food scene with a Delhi travel guide food tour. Sample a variety of local delicacies, from mouth-watering kebabs and biryanis to decadent sweets and desserts. Explore bustling food markets like Khari Baoli, Asia's largest spice market, and Sarojini Nagar, known for its street food stalls and vibrant atmosphere.
