Rishikesh.About Rishikesh Holiday: Nestled amidst the foothills of the Himalayas, Rishikesh is a quaint town renowned for its spiritual significance and breathtaking natural beauty. This is a comprehensive guide to the Rishikesh trip plan, offering insights into the most popular Rishikesh tourist attractions, Rishikesh sightseeing spots, and Rishikesh vacation packages available for travellers seeking an enriching experience in this divine destination. Rishikesh tour packages cater to the diverse interests of visitors, whether they seek spiritual enlightenment, adventure, or relaxation. From serene yoga retreats to thrilling white-water rafting adventures, there's something for everyone in Rishikesh. Travellers from various cities across India can embark on a tour to Rishikesh, with packages available from Delhi, Mumbai, Bangalore, Kolkata, and other major urban hubs. One of the highlights of a Rishikesh holiday package is exploring its myriad Rishikesh tourist places, including the iconic Lakshman Jhula and Ram Jhula bridges, which offer panoramic views of the Ganges River and surrounding landscapes. The town is also home to numerous ancient temples and ashrams, such as the Parmarth Niketan and the Beatles Ashram, where visitors can immerse themselves in spirituality and meditation. For those seeking adventure, Rishikesh holiday packages offer an array of thrilling activities, including trekking in the Himalayan foothills, bungee jumping, and camping under the starlit sky. Meanwhile, food enthusiasts can indulge in the diverse culinary offerings available in the town, ranging from traditional Indian cuisine to international fare. With its tranquil ambience and vibrant culture, Rishikesh is the perfect destination for rejuvenating Rishikesh holiday packages. Whether you're planning a solo Rishikesh tour plan, a romantic getaway, or a family vacation, Rishikesh holiday packages ensure a memorable and fulfilling experience. Let a knowledgeable Rishikesh tour guide lead you through the town's enchanting streets and hidden gems, as you embark on a journey of self-discovery and exploration in this mystical Himalayan paradise.
Rishikesh.Best Time to Visit Rishikesh: Determining the best time to visit Rishikesh largely depends on the preferences and interests of the traveller, as each season offers its own unique charm and experiences. Located in the foothills of the Himalayas, Rishikesh enjoys a moderate climate throughout the year, making it a desirable destination for tourists seeking spiritual solace, adventure, or simply a tranquil retreat amidst nature's bounty through Rishikesh holiday packages. Summer (May to June) Summer heralds warm temperatures in Rishikesh, with mercury levels soaring up to 40°C. While the scorching heat may deter some travellers, adventure enthusiasts flock to the town during this season for adrenaline-pumping activities like river rafting and cliff jumping with Rishikesh trip packages. Despite the heat, Rishikesh remains a popular destination due to its spiritual significance and serene ambience. Visitors can seek refuge in the shade of ancient temples and ashrams, or embark on a soul-searching journey along the tranquil banks of the Ganges on a Rishikesh one day tour. Spring (March to April) Spring is considered one of the best times to visit Rishikesh, as the weather remains pleasant with temperatures ranging from 15°C to 30°C. The blooming flowers and lush greenery add to the picturesque beauty of the town, making it ideal for outdoor activities such as trekking, camping, and Rishikesh sightseeing. Visitors can explore the best tourist places in Rishikesh, including the iconic Ram Jhula and Triveni Ghat, or indulge in adventurous pursuits like white-water rafting in the rejuvenating waters of the Ganges. Autumn (October to November) Autumn is widely regarded as the best time to visit Rishikesh, as the weather remains pleasant with clear skies and comfortable temperatures ranging from 10°C to 25°C. The town is enveloped in a festive atmosphere during this season, with vibrant celebrations like the Ganga Aarti attracting scores of devotees and tourists alike. Visitors can immerse themselves in the spiritual fervour of Rishikesh trip packages, explore its cultural heritage, and embark on memorable adventures amidst the breathtaking landscapes of the Himalayan foothills. Monsoon (July to September) The monsoon season brings relief from the summer heat, transforming Rishikesh into a verdant paradise adorned with lush greenery and gushing waterfalls. While rainfall may hinder outdoor activities, it adds to the town's natural beauty, creating a mystical ambience that is truly enchanting. Travellers can explore the best places to visit in Rishikesh, such as Neer Garh Waterfall and Neelkanth Mahadev Temple, or partake in meditation and yoga sessions offered by various ashrams and wellness centres. Ultimately, the best time to visit Rishikesh depends on one's preferences and interests, whether it be seeking spiritual enlightenment, embarking on thrilling adventures, or simply unwinding amidst nature's tranquillity. Regardless of the season, Rishikesh trip packages promise a memorable and fulfilling experience for travellers from all walks of life through the best places in Rishikesh.
Rishikesh.How to reach Rishikesh?: Reaching the picturesque town of Rishikesh is a seamless endeavour, thanks to its well-connected transportation network that caters to travellers from various parts of India and abroad. Whether by air, road, or rail, reaching Rishikesh is an easy feat, ensuring a hassle-free journey for those embarking on a Rishikesh city tour or a longer Rishikesh tour plan to explore the surrounding regions. By Road Rishikesh is well-connected to major cities across India via a network of well-maintained roads, making it an accessible destination for those travelling by road. State-run buses, private taxis, and self-driven cars offer flexible transportation options for travellers, allowing them to plan their Rishikesh trip according to their preferences and schedules. The town is easily accessible from cities like Delhi, Haridwar, and Dehradun, with scenic drives that offer breathtaking views of the surrounding Himalayan landscapes. By Air For travellers seeking a swift and comfortable journey, a tour to Rishikesh by air is the most convenient option. The nearest airport to Rishikesh is the Jolly Grant Airport in Dehradun, located approximately 35 kilometres away. From the airport, visitors can avail of taxi services or private cabs to reach Rishikesh within an hour. Several airlines operate daily flights to Dehradun from major cities such as Delhi, Mumbai, and Bangalore, making it easily accessible for both domestic and international travellers. By Rail Travelling to Rishikesh by rail is another popular option, especially for those seeking an affordable and comfortable journey. The nearest railway station to Rishikesh is the Haridwar Junction, located approximately 25 kilometres away. From Haridwar, visitors can avail of local buses, taxis, or auto-rickshaws to reach Rishikesh in less than an hour. Haridwar is well-connected to major cities across India, including Delhi, Mumbai, Kolkata, and Chennai, ensuring easy access for travellers from all corners of the country. Once in Rishikesh, travellers can explore the town's myriad attractions and embark on exciting adventures with the help of a knowledgeable Rishikesh tour guide. Whether it's a Rishikesh one day tour or a longer Rishikesh trip plan, Rishikesh offers something for everyone, from spiritual retreats to adrenaline-pumping activities amidst nature's splendour. With a plethora of Rishikesh tour packages and Rishikesh tour guide available, visitors can make the most of their time in this enchanting Himalayan destination.
Rishikesh.Places to Visit in Rishikesh: Rishikesh, nestled in the lap of the Himalayas and adorned with spiritual significance, is home to a plethora of captivating destinations that beckon travellers from far and wide. From ancient temples to breathtaking natural wonders, here are some of the must-visit places to visit in Rishikesh that promise an enriching and memorable experience: Triveni Ghat A sacred confluence of three rivers – the Ganges, Yamuna, and Saraswati – Triveni Ghat is a revered site where devotees gather to perform rituals and take holy dips. The evening Ganga Aarti ceremony at Triveni Ghat is a mesmerising spectacle that attracts visitors seeking spiritual solace and divine blessings among the Rishikesh places to visit. Laxman Jhula and Ram Jhula These bridges going over the Ganges River, are not only engineering marvels but also spiritual landmarks in Rishikesh. Laxman Jhula and Ram Jhula offer an ecstatic view of the landscapes, making them ideal spots for leisurely strolls and soulful contemplation among the Rishikesh places to visit. Parmarth Niketan A renowned ashram nestled on the banks of the Ganges, Parmarth Niketan is a sanctuary of peace and tranquillity. Visitors can partake in yoga and meditation sessions, attend spiritual discourses, and immerse themselves in the serene ambience of this spiritual haven among places to see in Rishikesh. Beatles Ashram (Maharishi Mahesh Yogi Ashram) The ashram gained popularity as the abode of the Beatles during their visit to Rishikesh in the 1960s. Today, it stands as a graffiti-covered relic, where visitors can explore the ruins, admire the vibrant artwork, and feel the lingering aura of creativity and spirituality. Neer Garh Waterfall Located amidst lush greenery and rocky terrain, Neer Garh Waterfall is a natural marvel that captivates visitors with its cascading beauty. Trekking through the picturesque landscape to reach the waterfall is an adventure in itself, offering stunning vistas and moments of serenity during your visit with Rishikesh trip packages. Rajaji National Park For nature enthusiasts and wildlife lovers, a visit to Rajaji National Park is a must-do with Rishikesh trip packages. A range of flora and fauna, including tigers, leopards, elephants and over 300 birds call it home, the park offers thrilling jeep safaris and nature trails amidst its pristine wilderness. These are just a few of the many enchanting places to visit in Rishikesh, each offering a unique blend of spirituality, natural beauty, and cultural heritage. Whether you're exploring on your own or with the guidance of a knowledgeable Rishikesh tour guide, these destinations promise to make your Rishikesh trip an unforgettable journey of discovery and enlightenment.
Rishikesh.Best Hill Stations/ Best Beaches in Rish: Rishikesh, though renowned for its spiritual significance and adventure activities, is not a hill station or a beach destination per se. However, it serves as a gateway to several captivating hill stations and serene beaches in the surrounding regions, offering visitors the opportunity to explore diverse landscapes and experiences during the tour of Rishikesh. Hill Stations near Rishikesh Mussoorie: Fondly known as the "Queen of Hill Stations," Mussoorie is a charming hill retreat located just a few hours' drive from places to visit in Rishikesh. Nestled amidst the Garhwal Himalayas, Mussoorie offers panoramic vistas of snow-capped peaks, lush forests, and cascading waterfalls. Visitors can explore attractions like Gun Hill, Kempty Falls, and Lal Tibba, with Rishikesh packages or indulge in leisurely walks along the Mall Road, lined with quaint shops and eateries. Dhanaulti Tucked away amidst the dense forests of Uttarakhand, Dhanaulti is a tranquil hill station located just a few hours' drive from Rishikesh. Offering panoramic views of the snow-capped Himalayas and lush green valleys, Dhanaulti is a perfect retreat for nature lovers and adventure enthusiasts alike. Visitors can explore attractions like Eco Park, Surkanda Devi Temple, and Potato Farm, or indulge in activities like trekking, camping, and nature walks amidst the serene surroundings with Rishikesh packages. Nainital Situated amidst the Kumaon hills, Nainital is a picturesque hill station renowned for its serene lakes and verdant landscapes. Boating in Naini Lake, exploring the Naina Devi Temple, and enjoying panoramic views from Snow View Point are some of the must-do activities in this charming town. Nainital also offers opportunities for trekking, birdwatching, and shopping for local handicrafts and souvenirs with Rishikesh packages. Beaches near Rishikesh Shivpuri Beach Located just 16 kilometres from Rishikesh, Shivpuri Beach is a serene riverside retreat nestled along the banks of the Ganges. Adventure seekers are drawn to this destination, enticed by exhilarating activities such as white-water rafting, kayaking, and camping amidst the picturesque Himalayan foothills. Visitors can also unwind on the sandy shores, partake in yoga sessions, or simply admire the tranquil ambience of the surroundings with Rishikesh packages. Byasi Beach Situated approximately 30 kilometres from Rishikesh, Byasi Beach is a secluded riverside paradise nestled along the banks of the Ganges. Known for its pristine sandy shores and crystal-clear waters, Byasi Beach offers a serene escape from the hustle and bustle of city life. Visitors can enjoy a range of adventure activities such as river rafting, rock climbing, and rappelling, or simply relax on the beach and soak in the breathtaking views of the surrounding Himalayan landscape with Rishikesh packages. Brahmpuri Beach Situated about 10 kilometres from Rishikesh, Brahmpuri Beach is another idyllic spot along the Ganges River, renowned for its pristine beauty and tranquil atmosphere. Visitors can enjoy a variety of water sports such as river rafting, body surfing, and cliff jumping under the guidance of experienced instructors. The beach also offers opportunities for picnicking, birdwatching, and nature walks along its scenic trails. While Rishikesh itself may not be a hill station or beach destination, it serves as an ideal base for exploring the natural wonders and scenic beauty of the surrounding regions. With Rishikesh tour packages, visitors can embark on memorable excursions to nearby hill stations and beaches, immersing themselves in the tranquillity and splendour of the Himalayas and the Ganges River.
Rishikesh.Best Honeymoon Destinations in Rishikesh: Rishikesh may not be the first destination that comes to mind for honeymooners, but its serene ambience, spiritual aura, and picturesque landscapes make it an enchanting choice for couples seeking a unique and memorable getaway. With its diverse range of experiences and enchanting landscapes, Rishikesh is the perfect destination for couples looking to embark on a romantic journey together. Whether you're seeking serenity, adventure, or pampering, Rishikesh tour packages offer something for every honeymooner, ensuring cherished memories that will last a lifetime.
Rishikesh.Interesting Things to do in Rishikesh: Rishikesh, nestled in the foothills of the Himalayas and blessed with spiritual significance, offers a plethora of interesting activities for visitors to indulge in. From spiritual pursuits to adrenaline-pumping adventures, here are three captivating things to do in Rishikesh that promise an enriching and memorable experience: White-Water Rafting Embark on an exhilarating white-water rafting expedition along the gushing rapids of the Ganges River, one of the most thrilling things to do in Rishikesh. Whether you're a seasoned rafter or a novice adventurer, there are rafting options available for all skill levels, from gentle stretches suitable for families to challenging rapids that test the mettle of even the most experienced rafters. Feel the rush of adrenaline as you navigate through the swirling waters, surrounded by the breathtaking scenery of the Himalayan foothills. Many Rishikesh tour packages include white-water rafting as a highlight activity, offering a thrilling and unforgettable experience for travellers. Trekking in the Himalayan Foothills Explore the breathtaking landscapes and scenic trails of the Himalayan foothills with trekking, one of the most rewarding things to do in Rishikesh. Lace up your hiking boots and set out on an adventure through verdant forests, cascading waterfalls, and picturesque villages that dot the countryside. Popular trekking routes near Rishikesh include the Neer Garh Waterfall Trek, the Kunjapuri Temple Trek, and the Chandrashila Summit Trek, each offering stunning vistas and encounters with the region's rich biodiversity. Whether you're a nature enthusiast, an adventure seeker, or simply looking to escape the hustle and bustle of city life, trekking in the Himalayan foothills of Rishikesh promises an unforgettable experience amidst the beauty of nature during the Rishikesh city tour. Yoga and Meditation Immerse yourself in the spiritual ambience of Rishikesh with yoga and meditation sessions, considered among the most serene things to do in the town. As the 'Yoga Capital of the World,' Rishikesh boasts numerous ashrams and yoga centres where visitors can participate in classes led by experienced instructors. Whether you're a beginner looking to learn the basics or an advanced practitioner seeking deeper insights, there's a yoga and meditation retreat in Rishikesh to suit every need. Spend your days practising asanas, learning breathing techniques, and meditating by the tranquil banks of the Ganges, experiencing a sense of inner peace and rejuvenation amidst the serene surroundings.
Rishikesh.Adventurous things to do in Rishikesh: Rishikesh, with its majestic Himalayan backdrop and the flowing waters of the Ganges, offers a plethora of adventurous activities for thrill-seekers. From water sports to aerial adventures, here are some exhilarating things to do in Rishikesh that will get your adrenaline pumping: Bungee Jumping Feel the rush of adrenaline as you take the plunge from a towering height with bungee jumping, another thrilling adventure activity on your tour to Rishikesh. Located near the town, the bungee jumping platform offers breathtaking views of the surrounding landscapes as you take the leap of faith and experience the exhilarating free fall before being gently lowered back to solid ground. It's an experience that will leave you with a sense of accomplishment and unforgettable memories. White-Water Rafting Embark on an adrenaline-fuelled white-water rafting expedition down the rapids of the Ganges River, one of the most popular adventure activities on your tour to Rishikesh. Experience the thrill of navigating through turbulent waters, dodging rocks and manoeuvring through challenging rapids under the guidance of experienced rafting instructors. Whether you're a beginner or an experienced rafter, there are rafting options available for all skill levels, making it an exciting adventure for everyone. Rock Climbing and Rappelling Challenge yourself both physically and mentally with rock climbing and rappelling, adventurous activities that allow you to conquer the rugged cliffs and rocky outcrops of the Himalayan foothills. With expert guidance and safety equipment provided, you can scale vertical rock faces and descend down sheer cliffs while enjoying panoramic views of the surrounding scenery. It's a thrilling experience that will test your strength, agility, and courage on your tour to Rishikesh while immersing you in the raw beauty of nature. Zip Lining Soar through the air like a bird with zip lining, an exciting adventure activity that allows you to traverse across scenic valleys and rivers at high speeds. Strap on your harness, hook onto the zip line, and get ready for an adrenaline-pumping ride as you zip past lush greenery and breathtaking landscapes on your tour to Rishikesh. With multiple zip lines of varying lengths and heights, zip lining in Rishikesh offers a thrilling experience for adventure enthusiasts of all ages. Whether you're seeking a heart-pounding adrenaline rush or a thrilling outdoor adventure, Rishikesh offers a wide range of exciting activities to satisfy your adventurous spirit. With Rishikesh tour packages tailored to include these exhilarating experiences, you can embark on an unforgettable adventure-filled journey in this Himalayan paradise.
Rishikesh.What to Eat in Rishikesh?: Rishikesh, known for its spiritual sanctuaries and breathtaking landscapes, also offers a delightful culinary experience with its array of delectable dishes. Here are five must-try delicacies that embody the essence of Rishikesh's vibrant food culture: Pahadi Cuisine Indulge in the traditional flavours of Pahadi cuisine, which is native to the Himalayan region and offers a unique blend of ingredients and spices. Some must-try dishes include Siddu, a steamed bread stuffed with spinach or lentils, and Kafuli, a thick gravy made with spinach, fenugreek, and local herbs. These dishes are often served with steamed rice or rotis and are known for their wholesome flavours and nourishing qualities. Aloo Puri Aloo Puri is a popular breakfast dish in Rishikesh that consists of fluffy puris (deep-fried bread) served with a spicy potato curry. The puris are crispy on the outside and soft on the inside, while the potato curry is seasoned with aromatic spices like cumin, coriander, and turmeric. This hearty and flavourful dish is often enjoyed with a side of tangy pickle and sweet chutney, making it a perfect way to start your day of exploration in Rishikesh. Masala Chai and Snacks Take a break from your sightseeing adventures and unwind with a steaming cup of masala chai (spiced tea) and some traditional snacks. Masala chai is a fragrant blend of black tea, milk, spices like cardamom, cinnamon, and ginger, and sweetened with sugar or jaggery. Pair it with some crispy Mathri (savoury crackers) or Samosas for a delightful snack that will warm your heart and soul. Street Food Delights Explore the bustling streets of Rishikesh and treat your taste buds to an array of tempting street food delicacies. From crispy pakoras (fritters) and spicy chaats (snack mixes) to piping hot samosas (deep-fried pastries) and sweet jalebis (syrup-soaked sweets), there's something to satisfy every craving. Don't miss trying the famous tourist places in Rishikesh and their speciality, Choley Bhature, which is a hearty combination of spicy chickpea curry served with fluffy deep-fried bread. Sweets and Desserts Round off your culinary journey in Haridwar and Rishikesh with some indulgent sweets and desserts that are sure to satisfy your sweet tooth. Treat yourself to some creamy Rabri (reduced milk dessert) or Gajar ka Halwa (carrot pudding) garnished with nuts and dried fruits. Alternatively, indulge in some refreshing Kulfi (Indian ice cream) or Malai Lassi (sweetened yoghurt drink) to cool off on a hot day. With its diverse culinary offerings, Rishikesh promises a gastronomic adventure that will tantalise your taste buds and leave you craving for more. Whether you're exploring the top places to visit in Rishikesh or enjoying a leisurely tour of the town, be sure to savour these delicious delicacies that are a testament to the rich food culture of this enchanting destination.

Rishikesh.Interesting Facts about the Rishikesh: Rishikesh, nestled in the foothills of the Himalayas and revered as the 'Yoga Capital of the World,' is a destination steeped in spirituality, natural beauty, and cultural heritage. Here are 10 interesting facts about Rishikesh that every traveller should know before embarking on their journey: Rishikesh is renowned as the "Yoga Capital of the World" and attracts thousands of yoga enthusiasts and spiritual seekers from around the globe each year. Rishikesh is home to two iconic suspension bridges, Lakshman Jhula and Ram Jhula, which offer breathtaking views of the Ganges River and the surrounding mountains. The Beatles visited Rishikesh in 1968 to study Transcendental Meditation with Maharishi Mahesh Yogi at the ashram now famously known as the Beatles Ashram. Apart from its spiritual significance, Rishikesh is also known as the "Adventure Capital of India," offering a plethora of adrenaline-pumping activities such as white-water rafting, bungee jumping, and trekking. Rishikesh serves as the gateway to the Char Dham pilgrimage sites of Yamunotri, Gangotri, Kedarnath, and Badrinath, attracting millions of pilgrims and tourists annually. Experience the mesmerising Ganga Aarti ceremony held every evening at the Triveni Ghat, where devotees gather to witness the ritualistic worship of the Ganges River. Indulge in the vibrant culinary scene of Rishikesh with its array of cafes and restaurants serving a variety of vegetarian and international cuisines, catering to all tastes and preferences. Rishikesh is dotted with numerous ashrams and yoga retreats where visitors can immerse themselves in spiritual practices, meditation, and holistic healing therapies. With its tranquil environment, serene surroundings, and abundance of yoga and wellness centres, Rishikesh offers the perfect setting for rejuvenation, relaxation, and self-discovery. Explore the hidden gem of Neer Garh Waterfall, a serene cascade nestled amidst lush greenery and offering a refreshing retreat from the hustle and bustle of the town. With its blend of spirituality, adventure, and natural beauty, Rishikesh offers a truly immersive experience for travellers seeking enlightenment, adventure, and rejuvenation. Explore the town's top places to visit, embark on thrilling adventures, and immerse yourself in its rich cultural heritage with Rishikesh tour packages tailored to your preferences.

Kolkata.Introduction: Embarking on a journey to Kolkata, often referred to as the City of Joy, promises an experience rich in culture, history, and vibrancy. Renowned for its colonial architecture, bustling markets, and mouth-watering cuisine, Kolkata offers a unique blend of the old and new, making it a must-visit destination for travellers. Whether planning a short Kolkata one day tour or an extended stay, Kolkata tour packages provide a comprehensive way to explore this enchanting city. Kolkata tour packages are designed to cater to a variety of interests and preferences. From historical tours that take you through colonial-era buildings and monuments to cultural tours that immerse you in the local traditions and festivals, there's something for everyone. A well-crafted Kolkata itinerary typically includes visits to iconic Kolkata tourist attractions such as the Victoria Memorial, Howrah Bridge, and Dakshineswar Kali Temple. These packages often come with the services of an experienced Kolkata tour guide, ensuring you don't miss out on any significant details. Kolkata travel packages also cater to specific interests such as culinary tours, which provide a taste of the city's famous street food and traditional Bengali cuisine. For those interested in art and culture, a tour to Kolkata can include visits to the Indian Museum, Tagore's House, and various art galleries. The flexibility of these Kolkata travel options means you can customise your Kolkata trip to suit your interests, whether you're an art enthusiast, a history buff, or a foodie. Kolkata packages offer an array of choices for exploring this fascinating city. Whether you're seeking a cultural immersion, a historical journey, or simply a relaxing holiday, Kolkata travel packages ensure a memorable and enriching experience. With so many Kolkata places to visit and Kolkata tourist attractions to explore, your tour to Kolkata will undoubtedly leave you with lasting memories.

Kolkata.How to reach Kolkata?: Kolkata, the cultural capital of India, is a city that attracts travellers with its rich history, vibrant culture, and delectable cuisine. Planning a tour to Kolkata involves not only selecting the best Kolkata tour packages but also deciding on the most convenient mode of transportation to reach this enchanting city. Here’s a comprehensive guide on how to reach Kolkata using various modes of transportation. Mode of Transportation Details Best For By Air Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose International Airport (CCU) is about 17 km from the city centre. Well-connected to major cities in India and abroad. Quick access from domestic and international locations. By Road Kolkata is connected by national highways and state roads. Esplanade Bus Terminus is the main hub for intercity buses. Scenic road trips and flexible travel itineraries. By Train Major stations include Howrah Junction and Sealdah Station, with extensive connectivity across India. Cost-effective and scenic travel with vast rail network. By Water Ferry services connect Kolkata via the Hooghly River, offering a unique and scenic approach to the city. Unique travel experiences and river cruises. Whether you prefer to fly, travel by train, take a road trip, or arrive by water, reaching Kolkata is convenient and accessible. With numerous Kolkata travel packages available, you can choose the mode of transportation that best suits your needs and preferences. Once you arrive, the best places to visit in Kolkata and the vibrant Kolkata tourism scene await, promising an unforgettable experience.
Kolkata.Places to Visit in Kolkata: Kolkata, the City of Joy, is a treasure trove of history, culture, and architectural wonders. A tour to Kolkata offers a glimpse into the rich tapestry of its past and present. Whether you're exploring the city for the first time or returning for another visit, there are several Kolkata tourist attractions that you simply cannot miss. Here are the best places to visit in Kolkata that should be on every traveller’s Kolkata itinerary. Victoria Memorial: One of the most iconic Kolkata tourist places, the Victoria Memorial is a grand marble building dedicated to Queen Victoria. Surrounded by lush gardens, this majestic structure houses a museum with an impressive collection of artefacts from the British colonial era. A Kolkata city tour is incomplete without visiting this landmark, which beautifully blends British and Mughal architectural styles. Kolkata tour guide often include this in their Kolkata packages, ensuring you don’t miss the detailed history it offers. Dakshineswar Kali Temple: For those interested in spiritual and religious sites, the Dakshineswar Kali Temple is a must-visit. Located on the eastern bank of the Hooghly River, this temple is dedicated to Goddess Kali and attracts devotees from all over the country. The temple complex, with its peaceful surroundings and stunning architecture, is one of the top places to visit in Kolkata. Howrah Bridge: A marvel of engineering, the Howrah Bridge is one of the most famous tourist places in Kolkata. This cantilever bridge spans the Hooghly River and connects Kolkata to Howrah. It is a bustling hub of activity, with thousands of pedestrians and vehicles crossing it daily. The bridge is especially captivating at night when it is illuminated, making it one of the best Kolkata places to visit for photography enthusiasts. Park Street: Park Street is the heart of Kolkata’s dining and nightlife scene. Lined with restaurants, cafes, and pubs, it is one of the best places in Kolkata to experience the city’s vibrant culture. Whether you’re looking for fine dining or a casual meal, Park Street offers a variety of options to suit every palate. Indian Museum: The Indian Museum in Kolkata is the oldest and largest museum in India. It boasts an extensive collection of artefacts, including ancient sculptures, paintings, and fossils. This museum is a highlight in many Kolkata travel packages, providing a deep dive into the region’s rich cultural heritage. Science City: For a fun and educational outing, Science City is an excellent choice. This sprawling complex features interactive exhibits, a space theatre, and a maritime centre, making it one of the best places to see in Kolkata with family. Marble Palace: A hidden gem among Kolkata tourist attractions, the Marble Palace is a stunning 19th-century mansion filled with antiques and artworks. The palace is a testament to Kolkata’s regal past and is one of the best tourist places in Kolkata for history buffs. Kolkata is a city that captivates with its blend of history, culture, and modernity. From architectural marvels like the Victoria Memorial and Howrah Bridge to spiritual havens like Dakshineswar Kali Temple, the best places to see in Kolkata offer something for every traveller. With numerous Kolkata holiday packages available, you can easily explore these famous tourist places in Kolkata and experience the charm of this vibrant city. Whether it’s your first Kolkata trip or a return visit, the city’s top attractions promise an unforgettable journey into the heart of Bengal.
Kolkata.Best Beaches/ Festivals/ Nightlife in Ko: Kolkata, a city celebrated for its cultural vibrancy and historical richness, offers delightful escapes to beaches and hosts vibrant festivals throughout the year. Whether you’re on a tour to Kolkata for leisure or exploring its cultural depths, here’s a detailed exploration of must-visit beaches, festivals, and nightlife hotspots that add charm to your Kolkata trip. Beaches in Kolkata Mandarmani Beach: Situated further south along the Bay of Bengal, Mandarmani Beach is renowned for its long coastline and adventurous water sports. Visitors can indulge in activities like jet skiing, banana boat rides, and ATV rides on the beach. It’s a perfect getaway for thrill-seekers and families alike, offering a refreshing break from Kolkata’s urban environment. Digha Beach: Located approximately 180 kilometres from Kolkata, Digha Beach is a favoured destination for both locals and tourists seeking a coastal retreat. Its serene sandy stretches and clear waters make it ideal for leisurely walks along the shore and enjoying breathtaking sunsets. Many Kolkata travel packages include day trips to Digha, providing convenient access for tourists looking to escape the hustle and bustle of the city. Festivals in Kolkata Durga Puja: Undoubtedly the highlight of Kolkata’s festive calendar, Durga Puja is celebrated with unparalleled grandeur and enthusiasm. This annual Hindu festival, dedicated to Goddess Durga, attracts millions of devotees and tourists alike. The city transforms into a spectacle of creativity with intricately designed pandals (temporary structures) showcasing elaborate themes and decorations. Kolkata holiday packages during Durga Puja often include guided tours to witness these artistic marvels and participate in cultural events that highlight the city’s spiritual fervour. Kolkata Book Fair: Held annually in January-February, the Kolkata Book Fair is Asia’s largest book fair and a significant cultural event in the city. It takes place at the historic Milan Mela grounds and attracts book lovers, publishers, and authors from around the world. The fair showcases a wide range of books across genres, along with literary seminars, cultural performances, and food stalls offering regional delicacies. It’s a must-visit for bibliophiles and offers a unique cultural experience during your Kolkata tourism. Nightlife in Kolkata Park Street: Known as Kolkata’s nightlife hub, Park Street exudes a vibrant energy with its eclectic mix of restaurants, pubs, and clubs. It’s a must-visit destination for those looking to experience Kolkata’s after-dark scene. Kolkata packages often include evenings on Park Street, allowing tourists to savour the city’s culinary delights and immerse themselves in its lively atmosphere. From fine dining to casual hangouts and live music venues, Park Street offers something for everyone seeking a memorable nightlife experience in Kolkata.
Kolkata.Best Honeymoon Destinations in Kolkata: Planning a honeymoon involves choosing the perfect destination that offers romance, relaxation, and memorable experiences. For couples opting for Kolkata packages, here are some enchanting honeymoon destinations near Kolkata that promise a delightful getaway: Digha: Digha, located just a few hours' drive from Kolkata, is a popular seaside destination offering serene beaches and breathtaking views. Couples can stroll along the sandy shores, indulge in water sports like jet skiing, or simply relax in beachside resorts. Digha's proximity makes it a convenient choice for a quick honeymoon retreat. Sunderbans: The Sunderbans, renowned for its lush mangrove forests and exotic wildlife, offer a unique and adventurous honeymoon experience. Couples can embark on boat cruises through narrow creeks, spot Royal Bengal tigers and enjoy serene sunsets. It's an ideal blend of nature and tranquillity, perfect for a romantic escape from Kolkata. Mandarmani: Mandarmani, known for its long stretch of beach and luxurious resorts, is another excellent choice for honeymooners. It offers opportunities for romantic walks along the beach, candlelit dinners by the sea, and adventurous activities like beach biking. Many Kolkata travel packages include Mandarmani as part of their honeymoon Kolkata tour itinerary, ensuring a memorable and relaxing experience. Tajpur: Tajpur, located along the Bay of Bengal coast, is a hidden gem known for its pristine beaches and tranquil ambience. Couples can enjoy secluded beach walks, indulge in water sports like snorkelling and kayaking, or simply relax in beachfront cottages. Tajpur’s serene surroundings make it a perfect escape for honeymooners seeking privacy and natural beauty. Shantiniketan: For couples interested in art, culture, and tranquillity, Shantiniketan provides a serene backdrop for a romantic honeymoon. This quaint town, founded by Rabindranath Tagore, offers picturesque surroundings, art galleries, and cultural performances. It’s an ideal destination for couples seeking a blend of heritage and peaceful retreat. Choosing the right honeymoon destination near Kolkata involves considering your preferences for adventure, relaxation, and cultural experiences. Whether you opt for the tranquil Sunderbans, the serene beaches of Digha and Mandarmani, or the cultural haven of Shantiniketan, each destination offers unique charms that create unforgettable memories of the Kolkata tour itinerary. With Kolkata packages designed for honeymooners, you can embark on a romantic journey that celebrates your love amidst scenic landscapes and cultural richness.
Kolkata.Interesting Things to do in Kolkata: Kolkata, with its vibrant culture and rich history, offers a plethora of experiences for travellers to indulge in. Whether you're exploring the city on a Kolkata tour or venturing out on your own, here are three exciting things to do in Kolkata that promise memorable moments: Explore the Historic Neighbourhood of Kumartuli: Kumartuli, nestled in the northern part of Kolkata, is a historic neighbourhood renowned for its traditional clay idol-making workshops. A visit to Kumartuli offers a fascinating glimpse into the artistry and craftsmanship behind the creation of intricate idols used in various festivals, especially during Durga Puja. Wander through the narrow lanes lined with workshops, observe skilled artisans at work, and witness the vibrant colours and creativity that bring these idols to life. It's a unique cultural experience that immerses you in Kolkata's artistic heritage and spiritual fervour. Indulge in a Culinary Adventure at New Market: New Market, also known as Hogg Market, is a bustling shopping destination in Kolkata that offers more than just merchandise. Dive into a culinary adventure as you explore the labyrinthine lanes of New Market, where you'll find a tantalising array of street food stalls, traditional eateries, and confectioneries. Sample local delicacies like kathi rolls, phuchkas, and mishti (Bengali sweets), and savour the flavours of Kolkata's diverse culinary heritage. From spicy snacks to sweet treats, New Market is a gastronomic delight that promises to tantalise your taste buds and satisfy your cravings. Cruise Along the Hooghly River at Sunset: Embark on a serene sunset cruise along the Hooghly River, which flows through the heart of Kolkata. Board a traditional wooden boat or a modern vessel and sail along the river as the sun dips below the horizon, casting a golden glow over the city skyline. Marvel at iconic landmarks such as the Howrah Bridge, Victoria Memorial, and Kolkata's historic ghats bathed in the warm hues of twilight. Some Kolkata travel packages include sunset cruises as part of their Kolkata itinerary, offering a romantic and picturesque way to explore the city from a different perspective. Experience the Cultural Extravaganza of College Street: College Street, often referred to as Boi Para (Book Town), is a vibrant hub of literary and intellectual activity in Kolkata. Take a leisurely stroll along this iconic street lined with bookstores, publishing houses, and academic institutions, and immerse yourself in the intellectual atmosphere that pervades the area. Browse through stacks of books on diverse subjects, engage in stimulating conversations with fellow book lovers, and soak in the ambience of this literary haven. Don't miss the opportunity to visit iconic landmarks like the College Street Coffee House, a legendary haunt frequented by renowned authors, poets, and intellectuals over the years. River Rafting on the Ganges: Experience the exhilaration of river rafting on the mighty Ganges River, just a few hours' drive from Kolkata. Navigate through the rapids and turbulent waters of the Ganges, surrounded by scenic landscapes and lush greenery. This thrilling adventure is perfect for adventure seekers looking to challenge themselves and enjoy the rush of adrenaline amidst nature's beauty. From exploring the artisan workshops of Kumartuli to cruising along the Hooghly River at sunset, Kolkata offers a myriad of exciting experiences for travellers to discover. Whether you're delving into the city's artistic heritage, savouring its culinary delights, or immersing yourself in its literary culture, Kolkata promises to captivate and enchant at every turn. With Kolkata tour packages designed to showcase the best of the city's attractions and experiences, you can embark on a memorable journey that celebrates the unique charm and vibrancy of Kolkata.
Kolkata.Interesting Facts about the Kolkata plac: Kolkata, fondly known as the City of Joy, is a vibrant metropolis steeped in culture, history, and diversity. Before embarking on your Kolkata tour, here are 10 fascinating facts that provide insight into this enchanting city: Cultural Diversity: Kolkata is a cultural melting pot with a diverse population, including Bengalis, Cultural Diversity: Kolkata is a cultural melting pot with a diverse population, including Bengalis, Marwaris, Gujaratis, Chinese, and Armenians, contributing to its rich cultural tapestry. Architectural Marvels: The city showcases a blend of colonial and modern architecture, with landmarks like Victoria Memorial, St. Paul's Cathedral, Howrah Bridge, and the Kolkata Metro. Literary Heritage: Kolkata is renowned for its literary legacy, associated with legends like Rabindranath Tagore and Satyajit Ray. The annual Kolkata Literary Festival is a global attraction for book lovers. Eden Gardens: More than just a cricket stadium, Eden Gardens is a historic venue that holds a special place in the hearts of cricket enthusiasts worldwide. Rosogolla's Birthplace: Kolkata is the birthplace of Rosogolla, a beloved Bengali sweet that has gained international acclaim. Durga Puja Festival: The city's largest festival, Durga Puja, transforms Kolkata into a vibrant carnival, drawing millions of devotees and tourists each year. Iconic Tram Network: Kolkata is one of the few cities in India with a functional tram network, offering a nostalgic journey through the bustling streets. Culinary Delights: Beyond Rosogolla, Kolkata offers a culinary adventure with dishes like Kolkata Biryani, Kathi Rolls, and Puchkas, making it a paradise for food enthusiasts. Hooghly River: The Hooghly River, a distributary of the Ganges, is integral to Kolkata's identity, offering scenic river cruises and ferry services. Intellectual Hub: Kolkata is home to prestigious educational institutions like Presidency University and Jadavpur University, producing numerous scholars, scientists, and Nobel laureates. Cultural vibrancy, and warm hospitality, offer a captivating experience for travellers. Whether you're exploring its architectural wonders, indulging in its culinary delights, or immersing yourself in its festive fervour during Durga Puja, Kolkata promises an unforgettable journey. With the Kolkata tour plan designed to showcase the city's best attractions and experiences, visitors can discover the charm and allure of the City of Joy at every turn.

faq.question: What can you not bring to Turkey?
faq.answer: As with many countries, there are certain limitations as to what you can carry in your luggage at your Holiday Inn Istanbul. carrying meat or products made of meat is banned and you may face serious consequences at the airport so avoid that. There are also limitations to the quantities of certain items but you can safely carry them into • Istanbul and they are as follows: • Chocolate – 1 kilogram • Honey – 2 kilograms • Fruits and fresh vegetables if any – 3 kilograms

faq.question: Is luxury shopping cheaper in Istanbul?
faq.answer: Istanbul in Turkey is a land of luxuries and the consumption of luxury products is quite high, which is why most suppliers purchase in bulk. This makes the cost of luxury products in Istanbul much cheaper than in other metropolis nations. You can definitely purchase a lot of luxury products at cheaper prices during your Istanbul vacation. You just need to ensure that the quantities do not exceed the limits set by the government as they have very strict rules against exporting luxury items without proper permits. When on your Istanbul tour packages take the time to enjoy your Istanbul vacation and shop for souvenirs for you and your loved ones.

faq.question: What clothes to wear in Istanbul?
faq.answer: As a metabolised city in the country of Turkey, Istanbul is pretty lenient about what you can wear. Normally, a lot of women in Istanbul dress in short-sleeved tops, shirts, dresses, and skirts, or loose trousers during the summer ward of the heat. men usually dress in long trousers and T-shirts or shirts. When planning to pack for your Istanbul holiday packages, you can carry shorts, skirts, dresses, T-shirts, shirts, or trousers. just make sure not to dress too provocatively and cover your body completely when visiting a mosque.

faq.question: Is WhatsApp allowed in Istanbul?
faq.answer: Using WhatsApp is technically banned in Middle Eastern countries including Istanbul in Turkey. However, if you use a virtual private network or VPN, you can bypass the lawful prohibition without actually breaking the law and safely use WhatsApp when on your Istanbul travel package. texting on WhatsApp does not have as many restrictions as it does on voice and video calls. so, if you stick to texting then you may not have to download a VPN at all. /p>

faq.question: How many days in Istanbul is enough?

Istanbul.Introduction: Istanbul is the largest city in Turkey and the hub of its capitalism. It houses the cultural heritage of this beautiful country and sports some of the most exquisite museums, architecture, marketplaces, and thriving statements of natural constructs. The Istanbul main attractions are stunning in their Beauty and if you book your Istanbul vacation during the best season to visit Istanbul, you can witness progress and divinity at its finest.
Istanbul.Most Popular Istanbul Tour Packages: Tour Packages NameDurationPriceGrand Bargain Turkey - Summer 20266 Nights 7 DaysRs. ₹213 400Turkish Extravaganza - Summer 202610 Nights 11 DaysRs. ₹232 400
Istanbul.How to reach Istanbul: When booking your Istanbul holiday packages, you need to take into consideration the mode of transportation which is only available by air travel. There are approximately 6 flights available from Mumbai, Delhi, and Ahmedabad to Take you to your holiday in Istanbul.
Istanbul.Travelling within Istanbul: Travelling within the city especially if you are in Istanbul, the best time to visit can be an adventure in itself. there are several options for public transportation as listed below: Bus Minibus Taxi Trams Metro Istanbulkart private cars private 2 Wheelers Ferries
Istanbul.The best time visit Istanbul: It can be difficult to determine the best time to visit Istanbul as the atmosphere of the city changes with the weather. The best time of year to visit Istanbul is during the shoulder seasons of summer and autumn. Ideally, the best month to visit Istanbul is during March or April when the sun is bright but the beauty of the city is astounding. However, if you are not a fan of summer then the best time to travel to Istanbul is during September and October when you can enjoy outdoor leisure during your Istanbul holidays.
Istanbul.Best places to visit in Istanbul: Istanbul is such a beautiful country that you must ensure that your Istanbul tour packages include the following locations in your itinerary or create your own when planning your Istanbul holidays: for a religious experience, visit the following places and marvel at the architecture as well as the serenity that you can find within: The Blue Mosque - It is located on the site of a Byzantine palace from earlier times, the Ottoman ruler Sultan Ahmed built the blue mosque in the 1600s after losing a battle against the Persians. Eyüp Sultan Mosque – a mausoleum dedicated to allegedly holding the burials of Abu Ayub Al-Ansari, a friend of the Prophet Mohammed. Ortakoy Mosque - you may have seen this religious institution featured on the postcards due to its architectural splendour making it one of the Istanbul main attractions. Chora church - one of the few well-preserved churches in Istanbul that was converted into a museum. Büyükada - an island in the sea of Marmara that is only 5 square kilometres in area, is one of the top places to visit in Istanbul for a soulful and spiritual experience. Yeraltii Camii - one of Istanbul's main attractions is the underground mosque that is open to everyone for a unique and spiritual experience. if you are travelling on a budget, the following are the must-visit places in Istanbul: The grand bazaar - it is one of the largest marketplaces that has been in existence since the 1400s and the ideal location to curate some memorabilia of your Istanbul vacation. Bebek - It is one of the off-beat but must-see places in Istanbul for an immersive experience as well as a palpably high vibe of positivity with scenic views all around. Camlica Hill - a quiet corner on the highest hilltop where you can witness the beauty of the city, especially if you take a tour during the best time of year to visit Istanbul. If you seek an immersive experience of Istanbul with historic significance, check out the following: The basilica Cistern - It is one of the best attractions in Istanbul that has contributed to providing water to the city Since the 6th century. The Hagia Sophia - one of the oldest structures, the Hagia Sophia is also one of Istanbul's main attractions. built in the 500s, it is a spectacle demonstrating the architectural genius of ancient times. Emirgan Park - it is a Heritage Park that was once passed down generations till it was declared to be public property, making it one of the best tourist places in Istanbul. Topkapi Palace - home to many sultans of the past, the Topkapi Palace is listed as one of UNESCO's heritage sites making it among the must-see places in Istanbul. Istanbul archaeological museums -The Istanbul archaeological museum is one of the best things to see in Istanbul to not only learn about the heritage of the city and the country but also about the Turkish influence around the world. Belgrad Forest - the dense forest land is home to several indigenous species of wildlife and flora and was once inhabited by older communities of Istanbul. For thrilling adventures during your trip to Istanbul from India, you can visit the following places: The Galata tower - one of the top things to see in Istanbul for couples where you can enjoy panoramic views of the city as well as a thriving nightclub. The Miniaturk - It is one of Istanbul famous places to visit for people of any age group. It demonstrates miniature structures of Turkey So you can explore the country without leaving the city of capitalism. Istanbul - It is an amalgamation of all the fun and best things to do in Istanbul Turkey with adventurous rides, shopping, and housing some of Istanbul top attractions. Taksim Square - One of the top places to visit in Istanbul if you want to have a great time while immersing yourself in the modern cultures of the city. The area is lined with exquisite restaurants and bars and a vibrant atmosphere. Istanbul Museum of Modern art – A testament to the monumental changes that have transpired in the city and the country of Turkey. The institution discovers both local and international artists and features their photographic artwork with valour.
Istanbul.What to Eat in Istanbul: Turkey is one of the few countries that have always indulged in healthy eating in the royal sense of the word. Every food item in Turkish cuisine is made with care and packed full of flavour that is unlike most cuisines from around the world. When enjoying your Istanbul vacation packages, make some time to check out the local eateries and taste the authentic flavours of Turkish food. Most of these dishes are already available in India and you may have tried them at some point or another but nothing comes close to having this experience in the place where these dishes originated. Here are the must-try items for your best things to do in Istanbul Turkey: Adana kebab - the Turkish variation of hot dogs made of ground meat of lamb or beef that is mixed with local spices and herbs and skewered before grilling. Ali Nazik kebab - a unique cold dish of grilled brinjals that are pureed and topped with yoghurt and lamb kebabs. Doner kebab – turkey's take on Tacos made with pita bread or focaccia bread filled with kebab meat of chicken, beef, or lamb on a vertical rotisserie that is thinly sliced and mixed with vegetables, hummus, and mayonnaise. Durum kebab - it is a meal in itself consisting of a tortilla filled with the traditional Turkish kebabs of marinated pieces of lamb, chicken, or beef mixed with onions, tomatoes, and Pickles served alongside French fries. Iskender Kebab - another meal-sized kebab combination of meat that is sliced thinly and served along with a topping of yoghurt and tomato sauce. It also comes with a side of pita bread and Baba Ganouj. Shish kebab - skewered chunks of meat from lamb, chicken, or beef are slowly grilled with a marinated house blend of spices and served with rice and vegetables. Testi Kebab - A clay-pot variation of kebabs that are slowly cooked alongside nuts and vegetables and served fresh when the clay pot is broken in front of you. Etli Ekmek - the Turkish variation of pizza that is made of thin flatbread crisped to the tee and topped with vegetables and ground meat. Kofte - a popular Curry food that has rapidly become a staple in India as well made of minced meat or vegetables that are bald and fried before dropping in a gravy of onions, tomatoes, and local herbs and spices. It is served alongside rice or you can consume it as a form of soup with meatballs. Lahmacun - it is the closest variation of pizza in Turkey served on crispy thin flatbreads topped with onions, ground meat, herbs, and a side of spices. Manti - we all love Italian ravioli but what if it is stuffed with a delicious combination of minced meat? That's what Manti is. These are bite-sized dumplings of sorts that are filled with your preferred choice of meat or vegetables and steamed before serving with tomato sauce and garlic-flavoured yoghurt. Pide - a combination of pizza pockets and pizza that is made of hollowed-out focaccia or baguette bread and filled with ground meat, vegetables, and a concoction of different cheeses. Kuzu Tandir - a smoky variation of kebabs that are skewered and roasted slowly before serving with a side of rice, vegetables, and yoghurt. Shawarma - A Turkish roll or burrito made of tortilla or flatbread made of wheat and stuffed with meat that is roasted on a vertical rotisserie and sliced thinly before mixing with onions, and mayonnaise. Sabbah - a variation of shawarma that consists of thinly sliced meat from a vertical rotisserie or chunks of falafel mixed with an egg salad and French fries wrapped in a tortilla or wheat flatbread. Meze - a form of appetiser that is usually served free of charge consisting of pickled or grilled vegetables presented alongside salads and a dip such as hummus or baba ganoush. Islak Burger - Turkey's very own take on burgers made with soft buns that are cut in half and smeared with garlic butter, tomato sauce, and a meat or vegetable Patty. Halka Tatsili - a Turkish variation of the Spanish churro which is essentially deep-fried pieces of dough dipped in syrup. It is one of the traditional foods of Turkey and is popularly served during holidays and festivals, so if your Istanbul travel package dates happen to be on a Turkish holiday you are in for a treat. Gozleme - a type of street food popular in Turkey is a flatbread that is stuffed with potatoes, spinach and cheese. Katmer - a breakfast food that is essentially a pastry layered with phyllo dough and sweet or savoury ingredients in between. Borek - a pastry that is layered with phyllo dough filled with cheese, spinach, or minced meat in between that is sweet and mostly served as a breakfast food. Turkish Coffee - popularly known in Istanbul as Kahve is made of arabica coffee beans that are ground to a thick paste and mixed with cold water, and sugar. It is famously enjoyed by the local Turkish population after meals since it helps with digestion. Baba Ganoush - grilled eggplants are mixed with onions, tomatoes, and local herbs and spices before blending into a smooth paste that is used as a dip for pita or focaccia bread. Hummus - a side dish or dip made of blended chickpeas mixed with tahini sauce and spices. Baklava - Finally, the infamous baklava cakes are small cubes of sweetened layers of phyllo dough cooked to perfection and topped with fine slices of nuts. It is very difficult to make but if done correctly, this sweet dish melts in your mouth.
Istanbul.Things to do in Istanbul: When planning your itinerary for your Istanbul tour packages, make sure Include the best things to do in Istanbul Turkey as follows: Try the local cuisine for some delectable Turkish food. Go shopping at the Grand Bazaar, spice bazaar, or explore the local markets. you will find some incredible souvenirs from your Istanbul vacation to take home. Get an authentic experience of Turkish relaxation at the popular bathhouses known as Hamam where you can opt for a body scrub followed by a massage before immersing yourself in a nourishing bath. Check out the local fishing grounds along Galata bridge with their short rail ride or a walk. Enjoy a cup of authentic Turkish coffee or Turkish tea after a beautiful meal. opt for a boat ride along the Bosphorus to catch a glimpse of the beautiful places to visit in Istanbul. Take an architectural tour of the city to check out the best attractions in Istanbul. Try a traditional hookah to make the most of your Istanbul holiday packages, that is of course if you are not averse to smoking. Enjoy the popular belly dancing arts of Turkey. Go on a museum expedition to explore the heritage, history, and traditions of Istanbul. Visit the smaller islands around the Mamar Sea for some historical indulgence in ancient cultures and their inhabited grounds

The Istanbul embassy located in Delhi allocates two types of visas for a trip to Istanbul from India in compliance with Turkish guidelines. You must submit the following documents along with your filled-in visa application in order to obtain your tourist visa as part of your Istanbul packages: A PPC or a police clearance certificate. A passport that is at least valid for the next six months. Bank statements proving that you have at least Rs. 3200 per day of travel. The details of your Istanbul tour packages including your flight tickets, accommodation, and all the Istanbul famous places that you will be visiting as part of your travel itinerary. based on these documents and visa fees, you will be granted either a sticker visa or an electronic visa to enjoy your Istanbul to our packages.

Sydney.Introduction: Ranked as the fifth ‘Most Liveable City’ by the Economist, the most popular megalopolis of Australia, Sydney is an effervescent city that is favoured by many for international tourism who throng the city to experience its multi cultural ambience, eclectic cuisine and the diverse and spectacular natural landscape comprising of enchanting coastlines, famous landmarks and multitude of walking and hiking trails.
Sydney.Most Popular Sydney Tour Packages: Tour Packages NameDurationPriceAmazing Australia - Winter9 Nights 10 DaysRs. ₹153 500Iconic New South Wales : Self-Drive Special6 Nights 7 DaysRs. ₹156 500Australia Adored11 Nights 12 DaysRs. ₹166 000Panoramic New South Wales : Self-Drive Special7 Nights 8 DaysRs. ₹186 800Best Of New South Wales - Self Drive Special7 Nights 8 DaysRs. ₹202 600 A buzzing city, there are several things to do in Sydney. Explore one of the most scintillating harbours in the world – Sydney Harbour which is further enhanced by the Sydney Harbour bridge. The glistening waterfront at Circular Quay is replete with outdoor restaurants and bars wherein diners can indulge in exotic cuisine and sip on cocktails whilst soaking in the magnificent views. Enjoy the harbour by cruising along in a ferry or walking along the promenade towards the bridge and across to the iconic Sydney Opera House. Take a guided tour of the Opera House to appreciate its splendid beauty. Other marvellous places include the St. Mary’s Cathedral, Sydney Tower, The Rock, Royal Botanical Garden, Torongo Zoo, Wharf of Manly that is home to cute mini penguins and Darling Harbour which is famous for its lively environment comprising of shops, restaurants and bars. Visit the Queen Victoria Building to splurge at the local brands, popular international retail stores and luxurious labels. Sun drenched and sparkling blue waters describe the famous Bondi beach that allures tourists for sunbathing, swimming and surfing. Embark on the fun 6km coastal walk from Bondi to Coogee that showcases spectacular views of sandstone clifftops, graffiti art adorning the walls and Waverly cemetery which is a spectacular sight. The beach is lined with chic cafes, restaurants and retail shops. A major tourist attraction and must visit during the Sydney tour is a day trip to the Blue Mountains, a World Heritage Site 60kms from Sydney. It involves a scenic drive passing through the glorious surroundings with stunning majestic mountains that are swathed in a beautiful blue hued haze derived from the vast eucalypt forests. Visit the breathtaking Jenolan Caves and engage in a walking trail or take the cable car to soak in the spectacular panaromic view of the beautiful rock formations known as the ‘Three Sisters’ from the Echo Point. Book the Sydney tour packages and international tour packages between September – May as the summer and spring season are perfect to explore the city on feet. Mahabaleshwar.Introduction: The quaint town of Mahabaleshwar in the Western Ghats is one of the most loved tourist destinations in the country. Located in Maharashtra’s Satara District at an elevation of 1,353 metres, roughly 120 kilometres from Pune, it has always been a renowned weekend getaway for locals seeking a quick and relaxing escape from the mundane daily lifestyle. The town is known for its remarkable natural beauty, illustrated via the mesmerising peaks, serene lakes, gushing rivers, cascading falls, dense greenery and so on. Besides being a site for nature retreats, Mahabaleshwar also holds a significant place as a renowned pilgrimage spot for Hindus in the state. It is because the holy Krishna River in India originates from the Western Ghats near Mahabaleshwar. Thousands of tourists, from the state as well as other parts of the country, like to visit this popular hill town for the purposes of tourism and pilgrimage. During your Mahabaleshwar tour, you will be able to witness some of the most majestic panoramic sceneries from all of the famous viewpoints in the area. In addition, Mahabaleshwar is also known for housing the sacred Mahabaleshwar Temple, the majestic Venna Lake, the jaw-dropping Chinaman’s Falls, etc. The local strawberry shopping, delicious street food, opportunities for fun and thrilling activities, etc., are some of the other major attractions of Mahabaleshwar Tourism. Whether it is a family holiday, a friend’s trip, a solo vacation or a honeymoon, Mahabaleshwar is one of the most ideal locations in Maharashtra for all kinds of tours and travels. If you are planning on visiting this hill station for your next vacation, consider taking your pick from the wide range of Mahabaleshwar tour packages by Thomas Cook. Make the days you spend at this hill station memorable with our Mahabaleshwar tour packages, which take care of several trip essentials, like accommodations, meals, flights, sightseeing, etc. You can find Mahabaleshwar tour packages for all kinds of budget ranges and trip durations on our website. If you want a more comprehensive trip experience, consider Mahabaleshwar tour packages that also include other famous vacation destinations in Maharashtra.
