Mahabaleshwar.Most Popular Mahabaleshwar Tour Packages: Tour Packages NameDurationPriceMaharashtra - Simply Mahabaleshwar with Mumbai 4 Nights 5 DaysRs. ₹32 400Hills of Maharashtra 4 Nights 5 DaysRs. ₹32 400Amazing Maharashtra6 Nights 7 DaysRs. ₹45 800
Mahabaleshwar.Best time to visit Mahabaleshwar: Mahabaleshwar is a great place for a vacation all around the year. It means you can easily find Mahabaleshwar tour packages for all seasons. However, the tourism experience can vary depending on the time of your trip. Check out the following pointers to get an idea of the season-wise Mahabaleshwar tour experience; Mahabaleshwar in Summer The summer season in Mahabaleshwar starts around March and extends till June. It is popularly considered the best time to go to Mahabaleshwar due to the favourable conditions. The weather remains clear throughout, and the temperature stays quite pleasant, favouring Mahabaleshwar sightseeing and other outdoor tourist activities. You can expect an average temperature range of 15°C to 35°C. It basically acts as a summer getaway in the region, and with the school holidays, the place remains packed with tourists. If you are looking for a family trip in the summer, March is the best month to visit Mahabaleshwar. Make sure to book your Mahabaleshwar tour packages, accommodations, transfers, etc., in advance to avoid issues with unavailability. Find suitable Mahabaleshwar tour packages for summer vacations on Thomas Cook. Mahabaleshwar in Monsoon Monsoon in Mahabaleshwar starts as soon as summer comes to an end. This season covers the months from July to October, bringing a slight fall in the temperature. Heavy rainfall in the area is pretty common, and the weather conditions remain misty and cold. The average temperature range during monsoon in Mahabaleshwar is 20°C to 25°C. The frequent rainfall often obstructs sightseeing plans and outdoor tourist activities, especially adventure activities. Also, the rain causes the roads to become slippery, increasing the risk of travelling by vehicles. However, some consider monsoon the best season to visit Mahabaleshwar as the rain highlights the ethereal natural beauty of the region. You can get some good discounts on accommodations and transfers due to the low tourist crowd this season, making it a great time to visit Mahabaleshwar on a budget. Find great deals on Mahabaleshwar tour packages for monsoon on Thomas Cook. Mahabaleshwar in Winter The winter season in Mahabaleshwar starts around November and ends in February. It is the best time to visit Mahabaleshwar if you are looking for a hill station vacation. The temperature during these months remains the lowest, with an average range of 10°C to 24°C. Tourists from neighbouring cities love spending time here amidst this cold temperature and regional natural beauty without needing to travel far off. Winter is also a great time for engaging in fun and thrilling activities in Mahabaleshwar, including trekking, rope climbing, cave exploration, etc. If you are visiting during these months, consider packing warm clothes for the trip. Additionally, winter is ideal for family trips and honeymoons. As it is also a peak time for tourism, consider booking Mahabaleshwar packages, hotels, transfers, etc., in advance. Find Mahabaleshwar tour packages for the winter season on our website.
Mahabaleshwar.How to reach Mahabaleshwar?: Alongside picking the ideal time for the trip and booking Mahabaleshwar tour packages, make sure to decide on the transport method. There are tons of ways to travel to Mahabaleshwar in Maharashtra, and you can pick the one that suits you the best. Here are the best ways to reach Mahabaleshwar for a vacation; By air: Travelling to Mahabaleshwar by flight is one of the fastest ways to reach the destination, especially for tourists from other Indian states. The hill town doesn’t have an airport of its own, and the nearest airport is the Pune International Airport, which serves the city of Pune and its nearby areas. It has great air connectivity, linking it to all major cities in India, including Mumbai, Bangalore, New Delhi, etc. For travel reference, a direct flight from New Delhi to Pune only takes 2 hours to complete. The airport is based in Lohegaon, a neighbourhood in Pune, roughly 120 kilometres from Mahabaleshwar. After landing there, you can simply book a rental car or take a bus or taxi to cover the remaining distance to Mahabaleshwar, which is around a 3-and-a-half-hour drive. If you are visiting during the peak season, complete the flight and hotel bookings in advance. Another popular alternative to Pune Airport is Mumbai International Airport, which is a bit farther but has great air connectivity. You can go with optional flights with the Mahabaleshwar packages offered on our website. By rail: Another great way to reach Mahabaleshwar is by train, which is a cheaper alternative to flights. As the hill town doesn’t have a train station of its own, individuals planning on travelling to Mahabaleshwar can catch a train to Wathar Railway Station, which is the closest railhead located at a distance of 60 kilometres by road. For the best connectivity, tourists can consider booking a train to Pune, which has great links with all major Indian cities, and from there, catch a train to Wathar. After that, tourists can simply cover the remaining distance to Mahabaleshwar by road in a rental car, taxi or bus. Alternatively, tourists can travel to Satara from Pune or Mumbai and hire a taxi to reach Mahabaleshwar, which takes around an hour. Find Mahabaleshwar travel package options for all kinds of tours on our website. By road: Tourists looking for road trips can consider travelling to Mahabaleshwar by car or bike. The hill stations feature great road links, especially for tourists visiting from Pune, Nashik, Alibagh, etc. With this method, you will be able to enjoy the scenic views along the way, enhancing your overall trip experience. Alternatively, state and private-run buses to Mahabaleshwar are also available, which you can take from any major city in Maharashtra. The popular Mahabaleshwar tour packages by Thomas Cook feature the choice of optional flights to make your trip smooth sailing.
Mahabaleshwar.Best places to visit in Mahabaleshwar: Having a list of the destinations you want to cover during your Mahabaleshwar trip makes it easier to choose from the available Mahabaleshwar tour packages list. So, make sure to look for Mahabaleshwar tour packages that include the tourist places you want to visit. Here are some of the best places to see in Mahabaleshwar; Chinaman’s Falls: The Chinaman’s Falls is one of the most beautiful Mahabaleshwar tourist attractions. Located a few kilometres from the bus stand, this waterfall attracts tons of tourists throughout the year, who visit to witness the remarkable sight of water falling from such a great height. The surrounding lush greenery and peaceful atmosphere make it one of the best places in Mahabaleshwar for picnics. You can easily find Mahabaleshwar tour packages featuring a visit to this tourist attraction. Mahabaleshwar Temple: The Mahabaleshwar temple is one of the most important Mahabaleshwar tourist attractions. Located close to the centre of Mahabaleshwar, this ancient 16th-century temple is one of the finest examples of the rich Maratha heritage. It is also a significant site for pilgrimage in the modern world. This temple, often included in many Mahabaleshwar holiday packages, is dedicated to the Hindu deity, Lord Shiva, and attracts devotees as well as tourists, who come to offer prayers and witness the beauty and serenity of nature. Visit the renowned Mahabaleshwar temple and other sites of spiritual significance in the region with the Mahabaleshwar holiday packages by Thomas Cook. Arthur’s Seat: Mahabaleshwar is home to several remarkable viewpoints and among them, Arthur’s Seat is quite exceptional. Often regarded as the “Queen of all viewpoints”, it is one of the best places to visit in Mahabaleshwar for jaw-dropping panoramic views. The view of densely covered valleys and low-floating clouds makes for an amazing experience. A trek to this viewpoint is one of the most fun things tourists can do in Mahabaleshwar. Find Mahabaleshwar holiday packages that feature this popular site. Wilson Point: Located at an altitude of 1439 metres, Wilson Point is among the most visited tourist places in Mahabaleshwar. People visiting the hill station love to come to this viewpoint early in the morning to witness the breathtaking sunrise. Sipping hot tea while glimpsing at the panoramic views from Wilson Point with your loved one makes for a memorable time. Most Mahabaleshwar holiday packages feature a visit to this site under sightseeing. Dhobi Waterfall: During your Mahabaleshwar trip, don’t forget to check out the spectacular Dhobi Waterfall. It is one of the most beautiful tourist places in Mahabaleshwar. The entire region surrounding the falls is covered by unblemished natural beauty. Tourists love to spend some time at this place in peace and quiet. Dhobi Waterfall is also a remarkable site for picnic and trekking activities. It is often seen as a standard inclusion in several well-known Mahabaleshwar holiday packages. Krishnabai Temple: The Krishnabai Temple is just a few metres from the renowned Mahabaleshwar Temple. It is among the top tourist attractions in Mahabaleshwar for spiritual tours. Built on top of a hill, this 19th-century temple has a statue of Goddess Krishnabai and Shiva Lingam. This temple, often included in many Mahabaleshwar packages, has a particularly interesting style of architecture. The view of the Krishna Valley and the smooth-flowing Krishna River from the temple is something you don’t want to miss out on. Cover all popular religious and natural landmarks in this hill station with the Mahabaleshwar holiday packages by Thomas Cook.
Mahabaleshwar.Best hill stations/best beaches in Mahab: Mahabaleshwar in itself is one of the most beautiful and highly sought-after hill stations in Maharashtra. You can easily look for Mahabaleshwar travel package options featuring well-known nearby hill stations or beaches. Here are some renowned hill stations and beaches near Mahabaleshwar; Hill stations in Mahabaleshwar: Panchgani: Panchgani is one of the closest hill stations to Mahabaleshwar, roughly 19 kilometres away. Nestled at an altitude of 1,293 metres, this quaint hill town gets its name from the five mesmerising hills surrounding it. Panchgani also features picturesque sceneries, lush greenery, romantic viewpoints, etc. Both Panchgani and Mahabaleshwar are sometimes regarded as twin hill stations. Explore the fascinating natural beauty with the Mahabaleshwar holiday packages by Thomas Cook. Lonavala: Lonavala is one of the most famous hill stations in Maharashtra, and thousands of tourists visit it every year for their hillside vacations. Often included alongside Mahabaleshwar holiday packages, Lonavala is only 170 kilometres from Mahabaleshwar. Located at an elevation of 624 metres, there are a lot of things to witness at this hill station, including lakes, waterfalls, dense greenery, towering hills, and so on. Find Mahabaleshwar holiday packages featuring Lonavala on Thomas Cook. Harihareshwar Beach: If you are looking for nearby beaches when choosing Mahabaleshwar holiday packages, consider visiting Harihareshwar Beach. Located in Harihareshwar Town in Raigad district, the place is known for its scenic beauty. The town is surrounded by four hills, giving it an enchanting look, and has two beaches on each side of the Harihareshwar Temple. One of these beaches is L-shaped and stretches 2 kilometres, while the other one is a straight stretch of roughly 2.4 kilometres. Go for Mahabaleshwar holiday packages for summer and enjoy a quick visit to the nearby beaches. Guhagar Beach: Located around 145 kilometres away, Guhagar Beach is a popular beach near Mahabaleshwar that tourists like to visit. It is one of the Konkan region’s longest beaches, known for its cleanliness, soft sandy stretch and surrounding beauty. On top of being easily accessible, the beach is considered safe for swimming and features tons of water sport activities for thrill seekers. Find the best deals on Mahabaleshwar holiday packages for all seasons on Thomas Cook.
Mahabaleshwar.Best honeymoon destinations in Mahabales: The hill town of Mahabaleshwar is a very popular destination for honeymoons in Maharashtra. If you too are interested, consider looking for honeymoon special Mahabaleshwar holiday packages on Thomas Cook. Here are some of the best romantic places you can check out with your loved one during your honeymoon in Mahabaleshwar Connaught Peak: Located just a few kilometres from the main market, Connaught Peak is one of the best tourist attractions in Mahabaleshwar for couples. It is the second-highest peak in the region, nestled at an elevation of 1400 metres, and is often included in many Mahabaleshwar travel package options. Formerly known as Mouth Olympia, Connaught Peak offers some of the most alluring visuals of the surrounding Krishna Valley, Pratapgarh Fort and the famous Venna Lake. Witnessing these beautiful, unspoilt sceneries from this peak with your significant other is one of the best things to do in Mahabaleshwar. Find Mahabaleshwar holiday packages featuring attractions like Connaught Peak on Thomas Cook. Venna Lake: Venna Lake is among the top places to visit in Mahabaleshwar for honeymooners. It is a remarkable man-made tourist attraction that catches the attention of tourists of all age groups and from different parts of the country. Often included in popular Mahabaleshwar holiday packages, the scenic charm and tranquil ambience of this place offer the perfect setting for honeymooners looking to spend some quality time in peace and quiet. Find Mahabaleshwar tours and packages featuring Venna Lake on Thomas Cook. Elephant’s Head Point: A Mahabaleshwar trip is incomplete without visiting the renowned Elephant’s Head Point. It is one of the most popular tourist destinations in Mahabaleshwar, especially for honeymooners. The Elephant’s Head Point, a common inclusion in several Mahabaleshwar travel package options, is considered the most picturesque viewpoint in the region, offering magnificent views of the Sahyadri Range. It also offers an amazing view of the Pratapgarh Fort, surrounded by Savitri Valley and Kova Valley. It is frequently seen as a standard inclusion in several Mahabaleshwar tours and packages.
Mahabaleshwar.Things to do in Mahabaleshwar: For all-inclusive Mahabaleshwar tours and packages, make sure to go through the options offered by Thomas Cook. During your Mahabaleshwar trip, here are some fun activities that you can consider trying; Boating at Venna Lake: Besides being one of the most iconic tourist attractions in Mahabaleshwar, Venna Lake is also a great spot for fun activities. Boating at Venna Lake is considered one of the most fun things to do in Mahabaleshwar. Some Mahabaleshwar travel package options may feature boating at the Venna Lake as an included activity. Go with the Mahabaleshwar tours and packages by Thomas Cook to enjoy your vacation amidst the hills without any compromises. Watch the sunrise from Wilson Point: While the region is home to several well-known viewpoints, Wilson Point is one of the best places in Mahabaleshwar for watching the sunrise. Starting the day of your trip with the breathtaking panoramic view of the surrounding landscape and the beautiful Sunrise from Wilson Point is one of the best things to do in Mahabaleshwar. It is a great activity to add to your choice of a Mahabaleshwar honeymoon package. Find budget-friendly Mahabaleshwar tours and packages on our page. Relax by Dhobi Waterfalls: For a more slow-paced experience, consider visiting the Dhobi Waterfalls in Mahabaleshwar. The sound of the cascading falls in sync with the tranquil nature, and bird chirping in the distance creates a truly out-of-the-world experience. Visiting the falls early in the morning, especially for picnics, is among the best things to do in Mahabaleshwar. Looking for suitable Mahabaleshwar tours and packages for short trips? Mahabaleshwar.Adventurous things to do in Mahabaleshwa: Mahabaleshwar tours and packages not only let you experience the regional beauty but also give you opportunities to engage in a wide variety of amusing activities. Thrillseekers looking for adventurous things to do in Mahabaleshwar can consider the following activities; Trek to Pratapgad Fort: Trekking is one of the most rewarding adventures to do in Mahabaleshwar. Among the various trails in the region, the trek to Pratapgad Fort is the most noteworthy one. Located on top of a hill, the journey to this fort lets you experience the surrounding beauty of the region under a new light. After reaching the fort site, you will be rewarded with incredible panoramic views of the entire region. Find ideal Mahabaleshwar tours and packages for your dream trip on our website. Go Karting: Those seeking new challenges while on their Mahabaleshwar tour can go for Go Karting adventures. Velocity Entertainmentz is one of the best tourist places in Mahabaleshwar where you can try this activity, alongside things like Air Hockey, Dashing Cars, Rodeo Bull, Bungee Ejection, etc. It makes for an amazing experience, especially if you are looking for fun activities to do with your friends. Make the most out of your hill station trip with the Mahabaleshwar tours and packages by Thomas Cook. Paragliding: Capture the magnificent beauty of Mahabaleshwar from a bird’s perspective by going for paragliding adventures. It is an adrenaline-rushing adventure that is not for the faint-hearted. You can try this activity at Panchgani, which is just half an hour’s drive from Mahabaleshwar Bus Depot. From adventure activities to scenic sightseeing, experience everything this hill station offers with the Mahabaleshwar tours and packages available on our website.
Mahabaleshwar.What to eat in Mahabaleshwar?: When it comes to a Mahabaleshwar tour, you just can’t miss out on trying the local delicacies. Here are some of the best local food items that you can try while on your Mahabaleshwar trip; Strawberry with cream: Mahabaleshwar is widely known for its delicious strawberries and lush berry farms. Hence, a Mahabaleshwar travel without tasting this famous fruit would be a missed opportunity. One of the most famous food items offered in the region is strawberry with cream. It is made by blending strawberries and creamy ice cream with a topping of delicious whipped cream. Mapro Garden is one of the best places in Mahabaleshwar to try this. Find the perfect Mahabaleshwar travel package for your vacation on our website. Chana: In terms of snacks, chana holds a special place in the heart of the locals of Mahabaleshwar. This is why, tourists from all across the state love to try the Channa Chaat or roasted chana during their Mahabaleshwar tour. It is a simple food item made with chana mixed with onions, chillies, etc. One of the most iconic places inMahabaleshwar where you can try this dish is Mama Chanawala. Chikki For those with a sweet tooth, make sure to try Chikki while on your Mahabaleshwar tour. It is a delicious snack made from groundnuts and jaggery. Depending on where you try it in the region, you can also get access to special chikki variants, containing strawberry, cashew and chocolate. You can find this sweet snack in the main market area. If you are looking for a Mahabaleshwar travel package for a short solo trip, check out this plan. Maharashtrian Thali: For a hearty meal to satisfy your hunger, try a traditional Maharashtrian Thali, ideal for both lunch and dinner. It is a thali-type dish containing a variety of things, such as roti, a variety of lentils, Koshimbir, vegetable curries, pickles, etc. Besides full veg, you can also opt for non-veg side dishes with this thali at some locations. It is delicious and nutritious, making it a must-try while on a Mahabaleshwar tour. Here’s a popular Mahabaleshwar travel package featuring Mumbai that you can consider. Masala corn and more: Corn is one of the prime food items sold across Mahabaleshwar. It is used to make a variety of dishes, loved by both tourists and locals. A few examples of corn dishes to try in Mahabaleshwar include oven-roasted corn on the cob, masala Corn, corn patties, etc. The Mahabaleshwar trip packages available on our website handle all the essential trip nitty-gritty, such as breakfasts.
Mahabaleshwar.What to shop in Mahabaleshwar?: Tourists on their Mahabaleshwar travel love to indulge in the local shopping experience. Here are some of the most popular things to buy and places to do shopping in Mahabaleshwar; Strawberries and fruit-based jams: Apart from trying strawberries with cream, tourists also like to shop for fresh strawberries and other fruit-based products, like jams, juice, syrups, etc., while on their Mahabaleshwar travel. These are of high quality, and besides personal use, they make for an excellent gift for close friends and family members. Here’s a Mahabaleshwar travel package with Lonavala that you can consider. Where to buy?: Some of the best places in Mahabaleshwar where you can shop for these items are Laxmi Strawberry Farm and Mapro Garden. Wooden artefacts and crafts: The traditional art and wooden crafts of the region are quite famous. Created by highly skilled local artisans, these items are made using materials like wood, bamboo, stone, fabric, etc. Wooden artefacts and crafts are great items for gift and souvenir shopping during a Mahabaleshwar tour. Where to buy?: The local shops in Mahabaleshwar market and the Devrai Art Village in Panchgani are some of the best places to buy these items in the region. Clothing and accessories: You can also shop for clothes, fashion accessories and other products during your Mahabaleshwar tour. The leather products that are sold in the area are quite good. In addition to clothes, you can also buy handmade slippers, handbags, fruits, etc. Where to buy?: The best place in Mahabaleshwar where you can go for this type of shopping is Town Bazaar.
Mahabaleshwar.Interesting facts about Mahabaleshwar: Look for great deals on Mahabaleshwar trip packages for all seasons on Thomas Cook. As for those seeking more details on the destination, consider checking out these interesting facts; During the reign of British rule in India, Mahabaleshwar was developed as a hill station to serve as the summer capital of the Bombay Province. Two of the most popular food products of Mahabaleshwar are strawberries and honey. So make sure to buy some during your visit. Mahabaleshwar’s Panchganga Temple is believed to be over 4,500 years old. Mahabaleshwar served as a jail for Chinese and Malay criminals in 1800 for three decades. Mahabaleshwar is one of the few places in India that features evergreen vegetation. It means no matter when you go for your Mahabaleshwar travel, you will always be able to witness lush greenery. Find great deals on Mahabaleshwar packages for all seasons on Thomas Cook. Malcolmpeth was the original name for Mahabaleshwar. It was named after a British official, Sir John Malcolm, during the days of the Bombay Presidency. The widely popular Pratapgad Fort, 20 kilometres by road from Mahabaleshwar, is privately owned by one of Chattrapati Shivaji Maharaj’s descendants, Udayan Raje Bhosale. In the pages of history, the first mention of Mahabaleshwar can be dated back to the year 1215.

Gir.Introduction: Gir National Park in Gujarat is the only natural habitat of Asiatic Lions. It is also a home for leopards and deers. The Sanctuary is accessible by the tourist only from Mid-October to Mid-June. Gir Tour packages take care of itinerary & comfortable stay. Gir also provides shelter to many species of Reptiles and Birds. Gujarat tour is incomplete without a visit to Gir National Park & Wildlife Sanctuary. Gir Tour Packages: Gir Tour Packages No. of Days Price* Gujarat Gir Calling 2 Nights/ 3 Days Rs.12,999 Colours of Gujarat 5 Nights/ 6 Days Rs. 17,999 Majestic Gujarat 6 Nights/ 7 Days Rs. 24,999 Fascinating Gujarat 7 Nights/ 8 Days Rs. 26,999 Gir Wildlife Sanctuary is a deciduous park that makes it an excellent habitat for the Asiatic Lions. Gir is worldwide famous for inhabiting Chowsingha- the only for horned antelope of the world. It is known that more than 200 species of exotic birds are present in Gir National Park. The total area of Gir National Park is 1,412 sq.kms. It also protects the endangered species of Long-billed and white-backed vulture. It has provided shelter to more than 40 species of reptiles including King Cobra, Russell’s viper, Saw-scaled viper and the Krait. One can find many Marsh Crocodile in one of the reservoirs of the Gir Sanctuary. Gir is managed by the Wildlife and Forest department of Gujarat along with some NGOs that want to protect the endangered species. Plenty of water reserve are available in Gir for the fauna to survive. Gir is the largest dry and deciduous forest of India. It is famous throughout the globe for its environment that protects and provides shelter to the endangered species of the world like Asiatic Lions, Chowsingha and some species of Vulture. A minibus takes the tourist around the Sanctuary for 30-45 minutes allowing you to spot many Lions, Python snakes near streams, monitor lizards, and marsh crocodiles in the Kamleshwar dam. Gir is not a safe place hence make sure you do not try jumping out of the jeeps/bus. Enjoy your ride at your risk. Many other carnivorous animals like Jungle Cat, Jackal, Hyena live here. Gir can be easily commuted through Rajkot or Jamnagar via train. It is 50kms away from Somnath. Make sure to visit World Famous Gir National Park & Wildlife Sanctuary whenever you visit Gujarat. Indonesia.Best time to visit Indonesia: When it comes to organising Indonesia trip packages, knowing the best time to visit Indonesia is crucial. With each season, Indonesia's tropical beauty varies exquisitely, and knowing the best time to go to Indonesia can help you get the most out of your trip. Peak and Dry Season: May to September There is no better month to visit Indonesia than now. Exploring beaches, temples, and volcanoes is made possible by the sunshine, clear skies, and placid waters. The temperature ranges from 25°C to 30°C, making it perfect for adventure and sightseeing. Early reservations are advised because July and August are the busiest months, particularly for Indian tourists booking Indonesia tour packages from India. Rainy Season: October through April There will be short, intense showers and verdant scenery throughout this time. You'll discover serenity, better prices, and more sedate activities even though it's the off-peak time of year for the majority of Indonesia packages. Since April is a transition month, it's a great time to begin your Indonesia packages and discover the best places to visit in Indonesia before the tourists return. Shoulder Season: May, June, & September This is the best time to visit Indonesia if you like fewer crowds. In addition to the same beautiful weather, Indonesia tour packages are less expensive. Beach lovers will appreciate May, and June will bring cultural delights like the Bali Arts Festival.
Indonesia.How to reach Indonesia?: It's time to make your dream of visiting Indonesia a reality if you've been seeing images of blue beaches, verdant rainforests, and historic temples. Here's how to reach Indonesia via plane, road, and even train (yes, really), whether you're going to book Indonesia holiday packages or are travelling independently. By train Flying is your best option if you want your Indonesia travel packages to start off successfully. From major Indian cities like Delhi, Mumbai, Chennai, Bengaluru, or Kochi, you can take a flight. The majority of flights go to Ngurah Rai International Airport (Bali) or Soekarno-Hatta International Airport (Jakarta). Depending on your itinerary, a typical flight lasts between five and seven hours. Air India, Singapore carriers, Emirates, and Garuda Indonesia are a few of the well-known carriers that offer flights. You may quickly connect to other things to see in Indonesia, like Yogyakarta, Lombok, or Bandung, when you land. Depending on the time of year and season, airfare costs for travellers comparing the Indonesia trip cost from India can range from ₹25,000 to ₹45,000. By Road This is for you if you enjoy road journeys, but it's not for the timid. Although it is technically possible to go from India to Indonesia by road, the trip is quite lengthy, more than 8,800 km. After passing through Malaysia, Thailand, and Myanmar, you would take a ferry to Indonesia. Although this journey seems exciting, it requires numerous visas and special international driving permissions. However, the Indonesia trip packages are a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity for adventurers who choose to make it a real expedition. By Air Although you cannot take a train all the way to Indonesia when thinking about how to reach Indonesia, you can begin your journey by travelling from India to Myanmar (via the Moreh-Tamu border). From there, you can travel to Malaysia and Thailand by road and train. After arriving in Singapore, you can go to many Indonesian islands via ferries and quick flights. If you enjoy leisurely travel and exploration, this route offers you a taste of Southeast Asia before arriving at your tropical destination.
Indonesia.Best Places to Visit in Indonesia: Although Bali can win your heart, Indonesia is more than simply Bali if you've been considering a trip there. With more than 17,000 islands, Indonesia is home to stunning vistas, historic temples, picture-perfect beaches, and cloud-touching mountains.
Indonesia.Famous Places to Visit: Yogyakarta: The cultural centre of Java is Yogyakarta. You will feel at home in this city if you enjoy art and history. Take in the captivating Wayang puppet performances, one of Indonesia attractions, as well as the Sultan's Palace and local marketplaces. Bali: The heart of Indonesian tourism is Bali, sometimes known as the "Island of the Gods." Bali offers it everything, from its lively nightlife in Seminyak to its verdant rice terraces in Ubud. It's among the top places to go in Indonesia to start your vacation, regardless of your interests in culture, beaches, or leisure. Komodo National Park: This UNESCO site is an adventurer's paradise and is home to the fabled Komodo dragons. It is a must-see on your Indonesia tour packages because it also has the well-known Pink Beach and breathtaking diving locations. Raja Ampat Islands: Imagine plunging into the crystal-clear waters of the Raja Ampat Islands, encircled by vibrant coral reefs. One of those Indonesia places to visit you won't soon forget is Raja Ampat in West Papua, which is a haven for both nature enthusiasts and scuba divers.
Indonesia.Beaches/ Hill Stations to Visit: Gili Islands: The Gili Islands are ideal for people seeking clear skies and tranquil waters. Just the sound of the waves and complete tranquillity—no traffic, no chaos. A must-have for any Indonesia trip. The Nusa Penida: This island, which is only a short ferry journey from Bali, has beaches like Kelingking and Diamond Beach as well as breathtaking cliffs. It's among the most enchanting Indonesian tourist places. Lake Toba, Sumatra: The largest volcanic lake in the world, Lake Toba in Sumatra, is encircled by tiny settlements and verdant hills. It's one of those Indonesian places to visit that offers you both tranquillity and natural beauty. Mount Batur, Bali: Bali's Mount Batur is a great place to trek if you enjoy sunrises. You will never forget seeing the sky above the volcano change colour.
Indonesia.Temples to Visit: Borobudur Temple: The enormous Buddhist temple known as Borobudur Temple is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and the pride of Central Java. It is very magnificent. It is one of the most well-known places to see in Indonesia. Temple of Uluwatu: This temple, which is perched high above the water, provides breathtaking vistas and nightly Kecak dance performances, making it the ideal fusion of culture and religion. Bali's Tanah Lot Temple: This temple, which is perched on a rock by the sea, is breathtaking at sunset. This must be seen if you're organising an Indonesia trip. Tirta Empul Temple: One of the most remarkable aspects of any journey to Indonesia is the opportunity to participate in a traditional purification rite at Tirta Empul Temple, which is well-known for its sacred spring water.
Indonesia.Cities to Cover: You will discover that when you plan your vacation to Indonesia that it is not a single location but rather a variety of amazing experiences dispersed over its numerous islands. Begin your Indonesia trip in Jakarta, the vibrant capital, where cutting-edge skyscrapers coexist with bustling marketplaces and mouthwatering Indonesian cuisine. After that, go to Yogyakarta, Java's cultural centre, which is well-known for the Prambanan and Borobudur temples. Discover Bali's rice terraces in Ubud, the beaches in Kuta, and the cafes in Seminyak, all of which are regarded as among the top places to see in Indonesia. In addition to a commemorative Rp 75,000 note, banknotes are available in denominations of Rp 1,000, Rp 2,000, Rp 5,000, Rp 10,000, Rp 20,000, Rp 50,000, and Rp 100,000. Although you'll use the higher-value notes more frequently, coins are also available. In the current exchange rate, 1 IDR is equivalent to around ₹0.0053, and 1 ₹ is equivalent to about Rp 188. faq.question: What is Phu Quoc’s weather like in peak season?
faq.answer: Between November and March, Phu Quoc enjoys clear skies, gentle sunshine, and very little rain — making it a prime time for swimming, snorkeling, and discovering the island’s highlights.

faq.question: Is Phu Quoc worth visiting for a holiday?
faq.answer: Yes! With its postcard-perfect beaches, rich cultural charm, and lush green surroundings, Phu Quoc is a fantastic choice for travelers from India looking for both relaxation and adventure.

faq.question: What can you do in Phu Quoc?
faq.answer: Popular activities include diving into coral-rich waters, lounging on the soft sands of Bai Sao Beach, sampling local delicacies at the night market, and visiting peaceful temples dotted around the island.

faq.question: When should you plan a trip to Phu Quoc?
faq.answer: October through March offers pleasant weather, ideal for exploring outdoor attractions, enjoying the coastline, and taking in the island’s scenic beauty.

faq.question: Is there nightlife in Phu Quoc?
faq.answer: Definitely! Whether it’s enjoying live music at a beach bar or browsing stalls at the vibrant night market, evenings in Phu Quoc have a relaxed yet lively atmosphere.

faq.question: How long should you stay in Phu Quoc?
faq.answer: Spending 2–5 days is usually enough to experience its beaches, markets, and cultural sites without rushing.

faq.question: How do you reach Phu Quoc from India?
faq.answer: The easiest route is to fly into Hanoi or Ho Chi Minh City, then take a short domestic flight to the island. Many tour packages from India include these connections.

faq.question: Phuket vs. Phu Quoc — which is better?
faq.answer: Phuket stands out for its nightlife and shopping, while Phu Quoc is better suited for travelers who prefer peaceful beaches, natural scenery, and a slower pace.

faq.question: Is Phu Quoc costly for tourists?
faq.answer: It can be as budget-friendly or luxurious as you want. Travelers can pick from affordable guesthouses to high-end resorts, enjoy inexpensive street food, and book tailored travel packages.

Phu Quoc.Best Selling Phu Quoc Tour Packages: There are various types of Phu Quoc travel packages available for you to explore. Phu Quoc.Best Time To Visit Phu Quoc: November to April is the best time to visit Phu Quoc. Winter is the dry season with less rain. You can go swimming because of the calm seas. The temperature during this time fluctuates between 25 and 28 degrees Celsius. December is usually the best month to visit Phu Quoc. This is the prime tourist season because of its lively ambience. You can try different water activities and sunbathing. March to May is the summer season when only a few people visit. With fewer crowds, you can enjoy the best things to do in Phu Quoc. The temperature ranges between 28 and 35 degrees Celsius, so make sure to carry enough sunscreen with you. This is the perfect time to enjoy a relaxed environment and go snorkelling or swimming. Avoid booking your Phu Quoc tour packages during the monsoon. Monsoon usually lasts in Phu Quoc from June to September. Although the temperature ranges between 25 and 35 degrees Celsius, the place experiences drizzles. As a result, you may not get to enjoy outdoor and water activities. However, accommodations are usually cheaper, and you get to witness the beauty of lush greenery around. Heavy rainfall during the monsoon also increases the risk of occasional storms, preventing you from beach visits.
Phu Quoc.How To Reach Phu Quoc?: Once you book your Phu Quoc tour packages, you can head out to explore How to Reach Phu Quoc the beautiful beaches, amusement parks, and so much more. Phu Quoc, despite being a part of Vietnam, is slightly different from other cities. The visa process for Phu Quoc tourism is also slightly different. If you book Phu Quoc tour packages from India and visit Phu Quoc directly, without stopping in other Vietnamese cities, you do not need a visa. You can be in Phu Quoc for a maximum of 30 days without a visa. However, if you travel to any other city in Vietnam during this period, you will need to have a visa. Here's how you can set out on your Phu Quoc tour By Train Visiting Phu Quoc Island directly by train is not possible. As it is an island, there is no direct train line in Phu Quoc. Therefore, you will have to take a ferry to explore Phu Quoc. The nearest train stations to Phu Quoc are Rach Gia and Ha Tien. Upon reaching these stations, you can take a ferry to Phu Quoc. By Road A road trip to Phu Quoc attractions usually takes long hours. Taxis are one of the easiest options. If you're a solo traveller, you can rent motorbikes to explore places to visit in Phu Quoc. However, for a road trip, you will have to first visit Rach Gia or Ha Tien from any of the other cities on Phu Quoc. After that, you can take a ferry ride to Phu Quoc, which would take around 6.5-8 hours. By Air Flight is usually the best option to reach Phu Quoc. If you are travelling from Hanoi or any other city in Vietnam to Phu Quoc, you must take a flight. An on-road journey would take around 15-30 hours or more, depending on the destination you're travelling from. On the contrary, the flight duration is around 1 hour and 30 minutes. You can also take direct flights from Delhi to Phu Quoc from India. The flight you take depends entirely on the type of Phu Quoc tour package you have booked.
Phu Quoc.Best Places To Visit Phu Quoc: Once you book Phu Quoc travel packages, you should explore some of the major attractions. Below are some of the top places to visit in Phu Quoc: Famous Places to Visit Vinpearl Safari Setting out on Vinpearl Safari is one of the best things to do in Phu Quoc. This semi-wild animal park offers a tour that lets you observe animals in their natural habitat. Grand World Phu Quoc If you wish to visit the city that never sleeps, Grand World Phu Quoc is one of the top attractions in your Phu Quoc travel packages. You can get a glimpse of different places around the world here. Bach Dang Night Market You must not miss out on exploring the Back Dang Night Market on your Phu Quoc trip. The night market is open from 3 PM to 11 PM. You can enjoy shopping, entertainment, and dining here. There are numerous pearls and shellfish products that you can buy as souvenirs. Beaches to Visit Sao Beach Sao Beach is one of the most popular Phu Quoc tourist places. This is one of the most popular beaches on the island because of its blue waters and clear sands. You can go here for snorkelling. This beach is the perfect place to soak in the natural beauty of Phu Quoc and relax. Long Beach The Long Beach stretches up to the West Coast. This is one of the best places to go in Phu Quoc. It is located very close to Duong Dong town. You can enjoy numerous dining and entertainment options. If you love beachside dining, you must check if this beach is in your Phu Quoc travel packages. Starfish Beach The Starfish Beach is located on the north side of the island. It is popular for its shallow and calm waters. You can witness the true natural beauty of Phu Quoc, where numerous red starfish are found. Khem Beach Khem Beach is a major part of Phu Quoc tourism for its crystal clear waters and white beach. Compared to other beaches, it has a very tranquil environment. You can dedicate around a day to just relaxing by the beach. Ong Lang Beach Ong Lang Beach is on the western coast of the island. It offers a more secluded environment on your trip to Phu Quoc. The tranquil environment here makes it an ideal place to relax. Temples To Visit Ho Quoc Pagoda Also referred to as Truc Lam Thein Vien, Ho Quoc Pagods overlook the entire sea around Phu Quoc Island. It is situated on the hillside. This is one of the most beautiful places to see in Phu Quoc. Not only does it offer a great view of the surrounding landscape, but it also has wonderful architecture. Cao Dai Temple This temple in Phu Quoc is located in Duong Dong. It is a strong reflection of the Cao Dai faith. You can witness the blend of different faiths here, like Christianity, Taoism, Buddhism, Islam, and Confucianism.
Phu Quoc.Cities To Cover: There are many Phu Quoc attractions contributing to the beauty of the place. One of the significant places to visit in Phu Quoc is Duong Dong. The main town of the island is popular for its night market. If you want one of the fun and best things to do in Phu Quoc, you must visit An Thoi. Here you can go exploring the majestic An Thoi Archipelago. Apart from that, you can find beautiful beaches at Ganh Dau, Ong Lang Beach, and Bai Sao. Phu Quoc tourism is also popular for a visit to the Ninh Fishing Village. The turquoise blue waters and white sand offer an unforgettable Phu Quoc tour experience.
Phu Quoc.How To Choose The Best Phu Quoc Tour Pac: Choosing the best Phu Quoc tour packages doesn't have to be too difficult. Phu Quoc.Phu Quoc Currency: Vietnamese Dong is the official currency in Phu Quoc. It is used for all transactions on the island. Phu Quoc is available in numerous denominations. The smaller bills facilitate regular payments, while the larger bills cover significant expenses. Frequently used denominations include 1,000₫, 2,000₫, 5,000₫, 10,000₫, 20,000₫, 50,000₫, 100,000₫, 200,000₫, 500,000₫. During your Phu Quoc trip, it's essential to be aware of the different types of currencies. If you are not familiar with the currency, you will face challenges with currency exchange. Numerous travellers overpay for certain services. Furthermore, while you explore places to visit in Phu Quoc, you may have difficulty using ATMs, too. When you visit Phu Quoc, you can visit numerous places to exchange your money. Banks are reliable places for exchanges. Not only do they offer great rates, but they also foster secure transactions in Vietnamese Dong (VND). Before starting your Phu Quoc trip, you can finally visit banks like Vietcombank and BIDV to exchange your money. There are exchange counters at Phu Quoc tourist attractions too. However, you need to be aware of the rates they offer and if they have any hidden fees. Before accepting the transaction, make sure to compare the rates. faq.question: What should I pack for a trip to the Maldives?
faq.answer: The Maldives is a tropical destination; this means you would be exposed to the sun rays for the time you are spending on the island. It’s surrounded by the most beautiful beaches - warm sunny weather and lots of watersports. It is a good idea to pack the following for your trip to the Maldives: light cotton garments - preferable light colors, evening wear, sandals or flip flops, swimwear, insect repellant, sunscreen, and sunglasses, carry a sun hat having good coverage to protect yourself from the harmful UV rays.

faq.question: Which is the cheapest month for a Maldives tour?
faq.answer: Months from August to October is the cheapest month for the Maldives tour where the cost of going to the Maldives is less than the normal cost to visit the Maldives. The peak season starts in December and lasts till February or March. If you are planning a budget vacation for the Maldives tour, August to October is the best month. The weather is not as wet as it is in the summer, and you will find a convenient hotel that is less expensive than luxury resorts.

faq.question: How far are the Maldives from Mumbai?
faq.answer: The approximate distance from Mumbai to the Maldives is around 1672 kilometers. The Maldives is a three-hour flight away from Mumbai. Airlines such as Indigo operate direct flights from metros like Mumbai, Delhi, Bangalore, and Cochin to the Maldives.

faq.question: Is the Maldives safe for female travelers?
faq.answer: Yes, the Maldives resort islands are very safe for solo female travelers. Many visitors to the Maldives travel solo including females, Maldives does not have any particularly bad reputation for being infamous for its crime scene. The Maldives recognizes Islam as its official religion, this means women should adhere to dress guidelines while visiting monuments such as mosques and can swim in swimwear and bikinis at specially designated bikini beaches. Apart from this, the Maldives are generally safe for all travelers, especially females.

faq.question: Can I consume alcohol in the Maldives?
faq.answer: As Islam is the officially recognized religion, sale, and consumption in public or private is banned among the local population. However, do not worry as none of the resorts are dry, you can buy and consume alcohol if you are an adult non-Muslim over 18. All resorts and cruises serve alcohol to tourists usually with a steep markup.

faq.question: What is the currency of the Maldives?
faq.answer: The official currency of Maldives is Maldivian Rufiyaa. You can purchase it from The Bank of Maldives Foreign Exchange counter, which is the only dedicated foreign exchange counter, is open at the airport for currency exchange.

faq.question: Are Indian rupees accepted in the Maldives?
faq.answer: The Maldivian The rufiyaa is the officially recognized currency in use however, US dollars are also accepted. Indian rupees are not. While USD remains the most recognized currency in the resort islands and is even accepted by restaurants and guesthouses on some of the Public Islands, most travel expenses are billed in major currencies such as USD, GBP, and EUR.

faq.question: How many days are required for a trip to the Maldives?
faq.answer: 5 days to a week is sufficient time to explore and go on a spiritusal retreat to the Maldives. There are a plethora of things that the Maldives has in store for you. In 5 days, you can comfortably visit all the major attractions.

faq.question: What is the temperature range in the Maldives and the water temperature?
faq.answer: The Maldives maintains a pleasant year-round temperature of 25-30°C, dropping just a few degrees at night. Every day, the sun shines for an average of 8 hours, and the water temperature seldom falls below 25°C. Also, the water temperature ranges from 28-30 degrees Celsius. This is an optimal temperature for going for a swim, and it is also ideal for diving or snorkeling on your Maldives tour.

faq.question: What is the best time to visit Maldives?
faq.answer: The best time to visit the Maldives is from December to April, as this is the peak tourist season. It's ideal for sight-seeing, cultural activities, water sports, and sampling local food. The weather is dry, low-humidity, and pleasant during these months, with a moderate temperature. April is the greatest month to go swimming in the water, with an average sea temperature of 30 degrees Celsius. During this season, one can see and experience the finest that the Maldives has to offer. All of the areas are densely filled, and the atmosphere is festive and bustling.

Maldives.Best Places to Visit in Maldives: Here are some of the most visited places in maldives. Male Feydhoo Hulhumale Island Utheemu Maafushi Male Despite its tiny size, Male is packed with experiences. This island city is dotted with beautiful buildings like the 17th century Old Friday Mosque, bustling markets like the Male Market and national monuments like the magnificent Mulee Aage Palace. With a population of 133,412 and a size of about 9.2 sq.km., Male is one of the most densely populated cities in the world. Here you can rub shoulders with Maldivians going about their everyday business with a smile on their face and the sun in their eyes. Explore the capital city's unique charm with our rainy season Maldives tour packages centred around Male. Some other notable places to see are the newer Grand Friday Mosque, the National Museum of Maldives, the Sultan Park, Rasfannu Beach, Sinamale Bridge, the Victory Monument, Jumbhoree Maidhan and the Tsunami Monument. Feydhoo The southernmost inhabited island of the Maldives, Feydhoo is the last speck of land in the vast expanse of the Indian Ocean, which means it is remote, quiet and serene as many tourists seldom venture this far. This makes it perfect if you're seeking a quiet, romantic Maldives holiday. For travelers exploring Maldives packages from India, Feydhoo offers a unique and tranquil experience. Places to visit include Gan Beach, Huludu Lagoon Beach and Meedhoo Jetty. Additionally, you'll find Hulhudhoo Harbor in the area. When planning your Maldives trip with Thomas Cook, consider including Feydhoo for an off-the-beaten-path adventure. Hulhumale Island Explore the vibrant marine life and stunning coral reefs with our Maldives tour packages, a diver's dream come true. Take a speedboat across the sparkling waters that surround Male and you’ll arrive at the Hulhumale Island, with its spectacular beaches, glass topped mosque, a beautiful promenade and serene, green neighborhoods. The most interesting fact about this island, however, is that it has been reclaimed from the sea, to meet the rising demand for land for development from space starved Male. It is connected to Male by the Sinamale Bridge, an architectural wonder. While on the island, not visiting the beautiful Hulhumale beach would be a crime. Have an afternoon picnic in Hulhumale Central Park, which is a stone’s throw away from the beach. Who are we kidding, in the Maldives, every place is a stone’s throw away from the beach! Thomas Cook India's curated Maldives tour packages will show you a side of Hulhumale Island few have seen before. Utheemu For a little history, head to the northernmost of the Maldivian island chains to the island of Utheemu – once the home of Sultan Mohammed Thakurufaanu, the leader who drove the Portuguese invaders from the islands in the 16th century. Utheemu Ganduvaru is the sultan's palace and is this tiny island’s claim to fame. Explore Utheemu's historic landmarks and museums, sheltered from the rain, in our informative Maldives tour packages. Maafushi If you’re going slightly off the beaten path, Maafushi is the place to be. Once ravaged by the tsunami, this island is now firmly back on its feet, with a variety of beautiful resorts and beaches that are best described as ethereal, Maafushi is the Maldives own little quiet corner. This is a beautiful pocket of the world to tuck yourself into. Stroll on the pristine beaches, laze at the Maafushi promenade and enjoy coffee at its small coffee houses. Vaadhoo Island Did you ever witness the sea glowing at night? Vaadhoo Island is renowned for its bioluminescent plankton, which lights up the sea like the stars in the sky. A trip there on your Maldives trip is enchanting, particularly for honeymoon couples. Visiting there during your trip is best from June to October. Thinadhoo Island For those couples looking for a peaceful but high-end experience, Thinadhoo Island is an unseen paradise. The beaches are peaceful, the water is surreal, and the coral reefs are among the finest in the Maldives. If your Maldives packages has to involve both luxury and seclusion, then this is where you should be. Dhigurah Island If adventure is your thing, Dhigurah is a must-see in your Maldives vacation. It's among the best spots to see whale sharks and manta rays. As opposed to the more touristy islands, Dhigurah provides a mix of high-end resorts and real Maldivian village life. A lot of Maldives packages nowadays include diving trips here. Hanifaru Bay Situated in Baa Atoll, Hanifaru Bay is a UNESCO-protected site where you'll get to swim with whale sharks and manta rays. It's one of the most incredible experiences you can have on your Maldives trip. But this location comes with seasonal restrictions, so look for availability in your Maldives packages.

Maldives.Maldives Visa: A Tourist Visa is granted to travellers of all nationalities when they visit the Maldives. A Tourist Visa allows you to stay in the Maldives for 30 days, but may be extended for another 60-day period. The full 30-day visa is not the right of the visitor to claim, but lies at the discretion of the Immigration to issue for a period of not more than thirty days and any period less than thirty days.
Maldives.How to reach Maldives: Flying is the quickest way to reach Maldives, with a flight from the Indian mainland to the Maldives lasting just about 3 hours. The country’s main airport is Male International Airport located on Hulhule Island in the North Male atoll, just off the coast of the island capital of Male. It is well connected internationally, especially with India, with direct flights from metros like Mumbai, Delhi, Bangalore and Cochin. The longest duration is about 6 hours from Delhi and the quickest is the flight from Cochin which gets you there in under 2 hours. Getting there, however, is the easy part; it is taking your return flight after your Maldives holiday has ended that is the tough part. From the airport, you can take a ferry, speedboat or a seaplane to the island of your choice within the Maldives. Relax and rejuvenate in overwater bungalows with our exclusive Maldives tour packages, the epitome of tropical luxury.
Maldives.Honeymoon in the Maldives: Sparkling crystal pools that are shrouded by graceful palms and lined with soft sand, where you can lay down on the beautiful beach at night and witness the unmatchable beauty of twinkling stars. Little spots of sand hidden amidst craggy rocks that disappear with the incoming tide that are perfect for a candle lit picnic. Tiny tidepools that are obscured by a ring of rocks forming a natural infinity pool, where you can take a dip with your partner in the turquoise waters. Such cosy spaces abound in the Maldives, allowing you a level of privacy that you always wanted but could never have at other beach destinations. On a honeymoon in the Maldives, you will feel the love blowing in the sea breeze that caresses your bodies. Maldives.Travelling within the Maldives: Since the islands are tiny with narrow roads, driving to get from one place to another is out of the question. You can walk or cycle across the tinier islands, whereas larger islands such as Malé have taxi services. The most excitement during Maldives holidays, however, is found during island hopping within the Maldives. Based on your budget you can choose different Maldives travel packages and take a leisurely cruise in a traditional Maldivian 'Dhoni', beat the waves in a speedboat, or a ferry or even take a yacht with a full bar and cabins, or you could fly between the islands in a seaplane.
Maldives.Best Time to Visit the Maldives: If you're going on a trip with Maldives packages, timing is everything in your journey. The Maldives experiences two seasons: dry and wet. Although there's no severe winter, the nation experiences peak, shoulder, and off-seasons, each with its advantages. Based on what you want—whether it's ideal weather, affordable offers, or a mix of both—here's when you should go for your Maldives tour. Peak season (November to April): The best time to visit the Maldives is from November to April. The island nation is warm and sunny year-round, but consists of a dry season and a wet, rainy season. There are pros and cons to visiting in each season. The best weather in the Maldives lasts from November and April, owing to minor to no precipitation and warm, tolerable temperatures. With warm weather, clear seas, and temperatures ranging from 25°C to 30°C, this is the peak time for snorkelling, scuba diving, and island-hopping. The water clarity is optimum, which is ideal for marine life enthusiasts. Nevertheless, being the peak season, advance booking of your Maldives tour packages is imperative in order to get the best resorts and experiences. However, this is also the busiest time of year and, as such, the room rates at resorts and hotels cost a little more than they normally would. However, since every resort sits on its own island, you won't have to fight the throngs of tourists like you might during the peak season at most other destinations. Book your Maldives tour packages today and embark on a journey to the Maldivian wonders that dreams are made of. Shoulder season (March to April & September to October): Wish to miss the peak tourist season and still experience good weather? The shoulder season of March to April and September to October is the best bet. You will still have good weather, although sudden showers might make an appearance. This is the ideal time to look out for Maldives packages at a reasonable price, as hotels and airlines give discounts. Surfing is great at this time due to excellent waves, which makes it a must if you are an adventure enthusiast. Experience the Maldives in a whole new light during the rainy season with our exclusive Maldives tour packages. Off season (May to August): Travelling on a budget? Off-season is when you can find the best deals on Maldives tour packages. This is the monsoon season, so you'll experience occasional rain, but not continuous. A few outdoor activities will be restricted, but resorts provide amazing discounts, and you can still indulge in spa therapy, underwater dining, and private beach experiences. If you do not mind rain, this is the perfect time for a budget-friendly yet not-to-be-forgotten Maldives vacation. Relax and rejuvenate in overwater bungalows with our exclusive Maldives tour packages, the epitome of tropical luxury.
Maldives.Beaches in the Maldives: The Maldives are home of the most ethereal beaches in the world. The Islands have fine, pearl white, soft sand that you can lie down and take a nap on, while the sea breeze fans you and the sound of the sea lulls you to sleep. Remember to watch out for the tide though. The beaches of the Maldives are nestled amidst beautiful coral reefs that you can explore via snorkelling and diving to give you vacation goals that can be ticked off your bucket list. If you're considering Maldives packages from India, here are some beaches you need to visit during your Maldives trip: Hulhumale Beach - The most popular beaches in the Maldives that you need to visit during your Maldives tours. Vabbinfaru Island Beach - Famous for jet skiing and water sports, Vabbinfaru adds an adrenaline rush to your Maldives tours. It promises excitement during your otherwise peaceful Maldives trip. Fulhadhoo Beach – For those seeking a romantic getaway, this secluded beach is perfect for couples looking at Maldives packages from India. Sun Island Beach1 72 026 – Located on the aptly named Sun Island, this beach is famous for its groves of tropical flowers. A must see during your Maldives trip. Dhigurah Beach – A beach where you can dive with whale sharks and explore the blue waters with these behemoths. A must on your Maldives package. Exploring these beaches as part of your Maldives packages ensures a diverse and fulfilling experience. Whether you choose a Maldives package for a serene escape or an adventurous Maldives trip, these beaches offer something for every traveler. Consider Maldives tours to make the most out of your visit, enjoying the best deals and curated experiences.
Maldives.Foods to Eat in Maldives: Light, fresh, tinged with every kind of spice – the cuisine in the Maldives is delicious, homely and comforting. Which means your Maldives tour package will be full of familiar flavours that somehow still feel exotic. With hits of chilli and the freshness of ginger, this food will make you sweat a little to cool you down a lot in the Maldivian sun. Here is a list to make you salivate: Maldivian Biryani Garudhiya Bis Keemiya Boshi Mashuni Mas Huni Mas Roshi Saagu Bondibai Maldivian Biryani This is a variant of biryani native to the Maldives that uses local spices such as pandan, lemon and lemongrass, which adds a new dimension of tropical flavour to the biryani you know and love. Garudhiya This fragrant fish soup is served with rice, chilli, lemon and onions and is perhaps the Maldives’ most famous dish. It is light, nourishing and most importantly, lip smackingly delicious if tuna soup is your thing. If not, there’re plenty of other options to choose from. Bis Keemiya Think of this as a samosa stuffed with hard boiled eggs, onions, shredded cabbage and spices, because that’s exactly what it is. One bite of this and you’ll either hate it completely or forget all about the plain old aloo-stuffed samosa back home Boshi Mashuni This is a mixed salad of shredded banana flowers and coconut all gently dressed with fresh, fragrant turmeric, cumin, lime and Maldivian chilli. This will keep you full, yet agile enough to explore the small islands on foot. Mas Huni To prepare this dish, smoked and shredded tuna is sprinkled with lemon, coconut and onions. Consider this a healthy tuna salad served with hot chapatis on the side. Roll it up for a quick snack on the go or pack some for a beach side picnic. Mas Roshi This is an evening snack made by steaming together flaked fish and a coconut dough. It is versatile as it is eaten for breakfast, between meals and even as an accompaniment to the main course of a meal. Saagu Bondibai The Maldivian version of sabudana kheer or payasam. This is a smooth, creamy pudding made with tapioca pearls and coconut cream, delicately flavoured with saffron and cardamom. Opting for one of the Maldives trip packages ensures you won’t miss out on indulging in this delightful dessert, often featured in traditional Maldivian meals.
Maldives.Things to Do in Maldives: Grab a bite underwater Witness the stars fall into the ocean Dive deeper into your Maldives holiday Spot some sea creatures Grab a bite underwater Many famous restaurants in the Maldives such as Ithaa at the Hilton Maldives and Subsix on Kudahuvadhoo offer diners the chance to eat a meal inside a glass walled dining room that is submerged underwater. Feel like a Bond villain as you sip on a cocktail surrounded by schools of fish and interesting conversations. This is one of the most unique experiences on any Maldives itinerary. Witness the stars fall into the ocean Add magic to your vacation to the Maldives with a trip to Vaadhoo Island in the Raa atoll. Here, you can witness what is best described as a sea full of stars when bioluminescent plankton cause the water to take on a surreal blue glow. Indulge in romance as you watch stars drop into the ocean, a mesmerising part of our honeymoon Maldives tour packages. Dive deeper into your Maldives holiday Maldives tourism has many tour operators who will equip you with the gear required for some adrenaline pumping, heart thumping water sports. The list is long and exciting but you can go fishing, surfing, parasailing, water skiing, wakeboarding, scuba diving, windsurfing, jet skiing, speed boat racing, banana boat riding and snorkelling. Thomas Cook India's Maldives tour packages will ensure you have a thrilling time on these islands. There’s something for everyone’s level of physical fitness and daring and if you wish to take up an activity, you can always sign up at one of the many training centers for beginners around the islands. Spot some sea creatures During your vacation to the Maldives, you can take a boat ride out to sea to spot some goofy dolphins playing around underwater. These friendly fish will swim up to your boat and if you’re lucky, you might even get to feed one or boop its snout. If you wish to go deeper, you can always get on a submarine to go whale watching. This is a breathtaking experience as you can observe these behemoths of the blue in their natural habitat. Our Maldives tour packages offer a taste of local culture, from traditional cuisine to cultural performances.

Some of the important highlights include the beautiful Toronto Islands. This collection of islets provides a great change from the usual skyscrapers of the city. Another prime destination of Toronto Tourism is St. Lawrence Market. It is located in city’s old town district. This pretty building is more than 100 year old and has seen a lot of ups and downs in the history of Toronto. Travelling in Toronto is also incomplete without paying a visit to the famous CN Tower. This 553 meter tall tower provides you an option to visit the top of the building and enjoying the views of the city. You can also visit one of the restaurants and enjoy some fabulous views of the city and Lake Ontario. Tickets to visit the tower are part of all the Toronto Packages but you can also visit the tower on your own for a dinner. Entertainment district is one of the favourite attractions here. Don’t forget to add this attraction in your Toronto Tour packages. Apart from this, you can also visit Harbourfront Centre, Kensington Market, Hockey Hall of Fame and Toronto Zoo. faq.question: What is the best time to visit Tamil Nadu?
faq.answer: In general, the winter months of November to February is the best time to visit Tamil Nadu. Compared to the hot and muggy summers, the temperature at this time is pleasant and noticeably cooler. It is comfortable to explore Tamil Nadu's attractions because the temperature is between 20 and 30 degrees Celsius (68- and 86-degrees Fahrenheit). Travellers should take advantage of the period to visit well-known locations like Chennai, Mahabalipuram, Pondicherry, Madurai, and Kanyakumari. It's crucial to keep in mind that Tamil Nadu's weather varies depending on the region, therefore it's wise to research the local weather before travelling to any particular locations.

faq.question: How to reach Tamil nadu?
faq.answer: Tamil Nadu, located in southern India, is well-connected by various modes of transportation. Here's how to reach Tamil Nadu:

faq.question: How many days are enough for a trip to Tamil Nadu?
faq.answer: A one week Tamil Nadu trip is enough for you to explore everything. The schedule completely depends on the visitors' points of interest, although tourists can visit places like Chennai, Madurai, Pondicherry, Kodaikanal, Rameshwaram, Ooty, Tiruchirappalli, Mahabalipuram, Kumbakonam, Thanjavur, and Hogekanal. Visitors who are interested in seeing hill towns can consider visiting Ooty and Kodaikanal. Travellers can go to Tiruchirappalli, Madurai, Mahabalipuram, Kancheepuram, and Thanjavur if they are interested in pilgrimage or historical sites.

faq.question: How much does a Tamil Nadu tour package cost?
faq.answer: The cost of Tamil Nadu package is determined by the places you want to go and how long you want to stay there. You can schedule guided tours of temples, seaside regions, or other attractions. There are many different Tamil Nadu tour packages starting from INR 7,000 per person on Thomas Cook.

Tamil Nadu.Introduction: Tamil Nadu has a unique place and charm in Indian tourism. From the flourishing cosmopolitan vibes of Chennai and the striking French-style buildings of Pondicherry to the quaintness of Kodaikanal and the emerald tea plantations of Ooty, Tamil Nadu ensures an unforgettable experience. Whether planning a Tamil Nadu trip for leisure, relaxation, or adventure, the state offers plenty of activities to indulge in. Tourists can choose to visit the famous religious citadels such as Madurai and Rameshwaram or travel to slightly offbeat destinations like Yercaud and Yelagiri to escape the humdrum of city life. Tamil Nadu also has a lot in common with several other Indian states. A few other destinations offer a similar and truly memorable vacation. Here are some options for those who have already previously traveled to Tamil Nadu and are looking for similar destinations.Browser other popular India tour packages - Kerala Tour Packages, Karnataka Tour Packages, Uttar Pradesh Tour Packages, Madhya Pradesh Tour Packages, Assam Tour Packages, Uttarakhand Tour Packages, Meghalaya Tour Packages, Himachal Pradesh Tour Packages. A melting pot of vibrant culture, exquisite destinations, and everlasting memories, Tamil Nadu is the heart of Incredible India. From several renowned UNESCO World Heritage sites to serene beaches and hill stations and fabulous wildlife, the beautiful destination has all segments of tourist attractions to ensure diverse and bountiful experiences. A trip to Tamil Nadu is sure to leave the visitor enthralled and captivated by its beauty for years to come.
