Official Language Tamil Other Languages Hindi, English, Kannada, Telugu, and Malayalam Best Time to Visit Tamil Nadu is typically very hot during the summer, but it is a good time to visit the hill stations in the state. The temperature is pleasant and cool during the winters, which is ideal for sightseeing and enjoying different adventure activities. Important cities in Tamil Nadu Chennai, Madurai, Coimbatore, Trichy, Vellore, Thanjavur, Kodaikanal, Rameshwaram, Kanyakumari, Thoothukudi, Chidambaram, Theni, Salem, and Mahabalipuram Famous Tourist Attractions Temples, Forts, Wildlife Sanctuaries, National Parks, Lakes, Waterfalls, and Museums Famous Food Idli, Sambar, Chicken Chettinad, Puliyodarai, Paruppu Payasam, Rasam, Mutton Kola Urundai, Pollachi Nandu Fry, Pongal, Murukku, and Filter Kaapi Things To Do Visit Madurai Meenakshi Amman Temple, Dhanushkodi Beach Point , Vivekananda Rock Memorial , Chidambaram Nataraja Temple, Mannavanur Lake, Nilgiri Mountain Railway , Adiyogi Shiva, VGP Snow Kingdom, and many more.
Tamil Nadu.Best Time to Visit Tamil Nadu: Tamil Nadu is a perennial destination. Tourists from across the world flock to the different cities of the state throughout the year. When planning a Tamil Nadu trip, one must have a clear understanding of the region’s tropical climate conditions. Packing can be done accordingly to ensure a great time. Geographically, Tamil Nadu experiences summers, winters, and the monsoon season. Though these seasonal changes are evident, the state remains humid and dry for the maximum time of the year. Here is a season-wise look at which is the best time to visit Tamil Nadu. Explore more about Best time to visit Tamil Nadu. Summer The summer season begins in March and ends in May. During this time, the heat is quite repressive, especially in cities like Chennai, Vellore, Madurai, and Thanjavur, where temperatures go up to 45 degrees Celsius. Summers, however, is a good time to visit the hill stations as the weather is breezy and pleasant. A trip can be planned to hill stations like Ooty, Yercaud, Nilgiris, and Kodaikanal or even the cities that lie towards the west of Tamil Nadu, such as Theni , Salem, and Coimbatore . Monsoon The monsoons in Tamil Nadu are from June till September. Although the temperature is slightly lower, i.e., between 25 and 30 degrees Celsius, it is still quite hot and humid. It is not advisable to travel to coastal destinations or even hill stations. Occasional cyclones along the Eastern seaboard pose a serious threat. Winter Winters in Tamil Nadu extend from October to February. The weather is not very cold as the temperature ranges between 22 and 30 degrees Celsius. However, the comfortable and pleasant breeze makes this weather ideal for sightseeing and exploring the cities. The beach destinations are lovely, and the water is not too cold. Besides, winter is a best time to visit Tamil Nadu as several events take place and festivals are celebrated throughout the state. From Pongal and to the world-famous Carnatic Music Festival, these events provide the ideal opportunity to experience the vibrant culture and traditions of Tamil Nadu.
Tamil Nadu.How to Reach Tamil Nadu: Tamil Nadu is very well-connected to the rest of the country via air and land. Here are the most popular ways to travel to this stunning destination. By Air There are 9 airports in Tamil Nadu, both international and domestic, that have frequent flights from the major cities of India and abroad. Chennai, the capital city, has the fourth busiest airport in India. Equipped with superior amenities, the airport operates out of 3 terminals and has daily flights from all Indian metropolitan cities such as Delhi , Mumbai , Bangalore , and Kolkata . Besides, it also handles direct flights from popular destinations like Singapore , Dubai , London , Frankfurt , and Mauritius . One can enter Tamil Nadu via Chennai, then easily travel to the rest of the state by road or rail. The airport is located about 21 km (13 miles) from the city center. By Train Traveling via train is the best option for a budgeted traveler. The entire state is very well connected internally by a network of Southern Railways. Various trains running through the major cities of the country connect to Tamil Nadu. The Chennai Railway Station, commonly known as Chennai Central, is the busiest railway station in Southern India. It receives trains from almost all towns and cities in India and also offers good connectivity to other regions in Tamil Nadu. Also, the Chennai Railway station is located quite close to many of the city’s famous tourist spots, such as Marina Beach, Elliot’s Beach , Egmore, and Kapalesara. By Road Tamil Nadu also has great road connectivity, thanks to its well-maintained network of National Highways. All bus transport in the state is handled by TSTC, i.e., Tamil Nadu State Transport Corporation and State Express Transport Corporation. The National Highway (NH) 67, 207, and 209 connect Tamil Nadu with Karnataka. NH 47, 49, 208, and 220 provide connectivity to Kerala, and NH 205, 219, and 234 connect the region with Andhra Pradesh . Several buses run between the many tourist attractions in Tamil Nadu. Once the Tamil Nadu tour is finalized, the most convenient bus option can be picked to ensure a smooth and hassle-free trip.
Tamil Nadu.Best Places to Visit in Tamil Nadu: Here are some of the best places to visit in Tamil Nadu : Chennai Pondicherry Coimbatore Madurai Mahabalipuram Kanyakumari Rameshwaram Mudumalai Nilgiris Auroville Hogenakkal Falls NawaghareN Tuticorin Kanchipuram Chennai Bustling streets, striking ancient temples, lovely beaches, and expansive shopping arcades, Chennai has all the elements to entice every kind of traveler. One of the biggest and the capital city of Tamil Nadu, Chennai is often regarded as the cultural hub of India. The beautiful city has preserved every ounce of its charisma through its journey of becoming Chennai from Madras. Apart from the many temples and beaches, Chennai also offers an array of fun and adventurous activities. Adventure junkies can indulge in cliff jumping, paragliding, river rafting, scuba diving, and camping for an amazing exploratory experience. Pondicherry A charming beach-side Union Territory in Tamil Nadu, Pondicherry is famously called the French capital of India. Previously a French settlement, the beautiful town is dotted with bougainvillea-draped French-constructed houses, vibrantly colored cafes, cobbled pathways, and countless churches. The place has an English vibe that ensures a one-of-a-kind experience. Tourist attractions such as Serenity Beach, Pondicherry Beach, Rock Beach, and the Botanical Gardens are a must-visit. Coimbatore Also known as Kovai, Coimbatore has a lot to offer to travelers. From its magnificent hills and the broad Western Ghats to enthralling waterfalls, the city truly sets itself apart in terms of its beauty and geography. Some must-visit attractions in Coimbatore are Adiyogi Shiva, Dhyanalinga Temple, Marudhamalai Hill Temple , Kovai Kutralam Waterfalls, and the Velliangiri Mountains. The city has breezy and pleasant weather throughout, thanks to the presence of Nilgiri Biosphere Reserve in the north. Madurai Madurai is the cultural capital of Tamil Nadu. Famously known as “The City of Temples” and “The Athens of East”, Madurai is steeped in rich and opulent history and deep-rooted ethos. As the legend goes, Madurai was built by cutting down a forest zone. During the naming of the city, the deity Shiva emerged at the site, and drops of nectar fell on the ground from his hair. Hence, the city got its name Madurai which translates to “sweetness” in Tamil. The world-renowned Meenakshi Amman temple , The Pudhu Mandapam, The Thirumalai Nayakar Palace, the Gandhi Museum , and the Vaigai Dam are some of the most exquisite tourist destinations here. Mahabalipuram Mahabalipuram is a small town located in the southeastern part of Tamil Nadu. The gorgeous place is a testimony to the magnificent cultural heritage of India. It is famous for its many ancient temples and monuments. The Shore temple, a group of three temples built in a single compound, is a popular tourist attraction. A few other must-visit places in mahabalipuram include the Pancha Rathas temple, the Crocodile Farm, the Indian Seashell Museum, Arjuna’s Penance, and the Cave temples. Kanyakumari Kanyakumari is a beautiful coastal town in Tamil Nadu, situated at the extreme southern end of the country. With spectacular environs, magnificent architectural marvels, and captivating seashores, Kanyakumari has emerged as a significant hub of art, culture, and religion in India. The mountainous terrains, hilly patches, primeval beach lines, and undulant valleys make the town a hot tourist spot. Some of the best places to visit in kanyakumari include Thiruvalluvar Statue, Vivekananda Rock memorial, Vattakottai Fort, and Padmanabhapuram Palace. Rameshwaram Rameshwaram is one of the Char Dhams of India and a famous Hindu pilgrimage town. Every year, the place witnesses a huge influx of devotees from across the world to pay homage to deities of Hindu mythology. Nestled at the tip of the Indian Peninsula, Rameshwaram offers unrestrained access to outstanding tropical beauty. Apart from its traditional legacy, the place is abundantly blessed with pristine beaches, underwater coral reefs, Water Bird Sanctuary, and spectacular landscapes. Mudumalai Mudumalai National Park and Wildlife Sanctuary is one of the top places to visit in Tamil Nadu . It is a haven for nature lovers, who can experience splendid wildlife and exotic flora and fauna in all its glory. With over 200 species of birds, 34 unique species of reptiles, and many animals, this national park is one of the best in the country. Spectacular scenic views can be witnessed on safari rides and treks. The Bandipur National Park and the Tiger Reserve are located in close proximity. Nilgiris Famously known as the Blue Mountains, Nilgiris have seldom failed to catch the attention of travelers who love the fresh and undulated beauty of nature. A part of the Western Ghats, Nilgiris is famous for its outlandish nature trails, mighty waterfalls, and exotic wildlife. One of the best attractions is Doddabetta Peak , the highest peak in the region that is surrounded by verdant stretches of Shola forests. The heritage train ride on the Nilgiris takes the tourist through the deep wooded, hilly slopes and over 250 bridges, promising a memorable experience. Auroville Auroville , or the City of Dawn, is a splendid township in the Villupuram district of Tamil Nadu. Apart from the tranquil atmosphere, the place offers tourists a unique opportunity to connect with themselves and nature. The Auroville Ashram was established by Mirra Alfassa, famously called as “the Mother”. The ashram is based on the guiding principles of Sri Aurobindo. Hogenakkal Falls Situated at the border of Tamil Nadu and Karnataka, Hogenakkal Falls is a delight for every tourist. The stunning cascade falls from an altitude of 700 meters, creating a spectacle reminiscent of Niagara Falls. A coracle ride or a trek to the nearest Melagiri Hills is sure to make the experience even more memorable. Dhanushkodi Dhanushkodi is often called India’s phantom town. Situated in the south of Rameshwaram, Dhanushkodi is a truly mesmerizing sight. Locating the place can be slightly tricky for tourists as it is an offbeat destination. But once there, it is a haven for all adventure and nature lovers. It is primarily surrounded by small fishing villages and is only situated 31 km away from Sri Lanka ’s coastline. Tuticorin Tuticorin is a popular city in Tamil Nadu best known for its beautiful seaports and beaches. Perched on the Coromandel Coast of the Bay of Bengal, the city is an excellent weekend getaway offering a plethora of tourist attractions. For those looking for a religious experience, the Lady of Snows Basilica, Shiva Temple, etc., are must-visit spots. Those who seek calm and solitude will appreciate beach parks such as Harbour Beach Park, Roche Park, Nehru Park, and Rajaji Park, which promise a tranquil experience. Kanchipuram Kanchipuram, famously known as the Golden City of Temples, is a significant Hindu pilgrimage site. It is the second holiest destination in India after Varanasi and is known across the world for its spiritual ambiance and beautiful temples showcasing architectural splendor. Every year, thousands of tourists flock to the destination to seek solace and divine bliss. The gorgeous city is also renowned for its Kanchipuram silk sarees woven out of pure mulberry silk.
Tamil Nadu.Best Hill Stations in Tamil Nadu: Ooty Kodaikanal Yelagiri Yercaud Ooty Known as “India’s Switzerland ”, Ooty is a spectacular hill station in Tamil Nadu that revitalizes the soul and offers divine sensory experiences. A part of the Nilgiri Biosphere Reserve, Ooty is well-known for its exquisite nature walks, well-cultivated tea plantations, and the wooded hills of Western Ghats. Kodaikanal Kodaikanal, a famous honeymoon destination, enthralls visitors with its enchanting scenic beauty. Situated in the hills of the Dindigul district in Tamil Nadu, a huge part of the hill station is covered with impenetrable forests, massive rocks, and mesmerizing waterfalls. Yelagiri Yelagiri is situated in the Vellore district of Tamil Nadu. The gorgeous destination is a cluster of fourteen hamlets, with Athanavur being the most important and capital town. Yelagiri is famous throughout the country for offering tourists the best trekking and climbing adventure activities. Even the Punganur Lake in the region is a well-known tourist spot. Yercaud Yercaud is a slightly offbeat and unexplored destination situated in the Salem district of Tamil Nadu. The rejuvenating hill station lies around Emerald Lake, which is also known as Yercaud Lake. The place is steeped in nature’s bountiful attractions and countryside aesthetic, making it an ideal place for nature enthusiasts, adventure seekers, and trekkers.
Tamil Nadu.Best Beaches in Tamil Nadu: Marina Beach Mahabalipuram Beach Covelong Beach Kanyakumari Beach Marina Beach Located in the heart of Chennai city, Marina Beach is one of the largest urban beaches in the world, stretching for a length of 13 km. The beautiful beach with rows of palm trees running parallel to the shoreline makes carefree strolls a revitalizing and exhilarating experience. The beach is always bustling with tourists, merry-go-rounds, and shops selling food and goods, making it a great place for a family holiday. Mahabalipuram Beach Located 58km south of Chennai, the Mahabalipuram beach is a magnificent place showcasing a perfect blend of nature resplendence and historical significance. Apart from several rock-cut sculptures and monolithic mountains, the beach includes the 8th century Shore Temple on its seashore. The charming beach is also well-known for offering swimming and windsurfing activities. Covelong Beach Pronounced Kovalam Beach , this is a beautiful beach that used to be a prominent port for the British and the French in the olden times. The sublime beauty of the white sands and the crystal clear waters ensure a tranquil vacation. The beach also has many historical monuments, mosques, forts, and churches close by, ensuring a great exploratory experience. Kanyakumari Beach Another pristine beach that should definitely be a part of the Tamil Nadu tour package is the Kanyakumari beach. Tucked on the southernmost coast of the Indian Peninsula, it is located at the confluence of the Indian Ocean, the Bay of Bengal, and the Arabian Sea. The beach is known for its most surreal sunrise and sunset views.
Tamil Nadu.Famous Food in Tamil Nadu: Tamil Nadu is a paradise for food connoisseurs, offering an eclectic range of culinary experiences to indulge in. The authentic vegetarian and non-vegetarian dishes exhibit a unique flavorsome palate to delight the taste buds. One of the best things about Tamilian cuisine is the varied range of spices, which are also exported to different nations. Tamarind, curry leaves, cardamom, pepper, chilies, mint, and cloves are widely produced in the state and added to almost all delicacies, imparting a scrumptious taste and incredible aroma. Let us now look at the most famous dishes to try on a trip to Tamil Nadu for an authentic culinary experience. Sambar Puliyodarai Rasam Chicken Chettinad Payasam Filter Kaapi Sambar Sambar, the mighty dish of Tamilian cuisine, is a lentil-based stew prepared from tomatoes and tamarind as its base. The delicious dish is a staple and a significant part of every meal in Tamil Nadu. Various vegetables such as carrots, yam, and okra are added to sambar along with a medley of spices, imparting a uniquely delightful and slightly sour taste. Puliyodarai The goodness of tamarind and rice come together in the form of this enchanting dish that is typically made during special occasions and festivals. It is offered to God as prasadam during festivals and then enjoyed by all. The dish has a tangy flavor that is accompanied by papadams or fryums to perfectly balance out the strong zest. Rasam Rasam is a tangy and piquant broth made with an array of spices, such as mustard seeds and curry leaves. One of the best main course dishes in Tamil Nadu, rasam, can be enjoyed with rice or simply on its own. Many places in the state offer different versions of the dish (with added meat or seafood), lending it a pleasant and agreeable taste. Chicken Chettinad This lip-smacking dish is a specialty of the Chettiyar community of Tamil Nadu. It is a true delight for all non-vegetarian food lovers as the dish is soaked in authentic flavors of roasted spices, bringing a unique taste to the palate. It is made from chicken marinated in yogurt, turmeric, and an assortment of spices. It can be devoured with steamed rice or flour paratha. Payasam A scrumptious dessert, Payasam is a famous second course in Tamil Nadu. The incredible dish is made by roasting the moong dal and then cooking it in a pressure cooker along with coconut milk, jaggery syrup, and a bunch of dry fruits. The dessert is not overly sweet and is made using jaggery. So, a slightly bigger helping of the traditional dessert can be relished without any guilt. Filter Kaapi No South Indian meal is complete without relishing a lovely cup of filter kaapi (filter coffee). It is customary for guests to be offered a kaapi before or after their meal. The kaapi is brewed in special metal equipment that resembles two cylindrical cups. The coffee has a rich and thick froth, making it even more delightful.
Tamil Nadu.Things to Do in Tamil Nadu: Visit Meenakshi Amman Temple Relish a hot cup of tea at the Doddabetta Tea Factory Rejuvenate senses at the Prana Spa, Kodaikanal Experience true bliss at the Brihadeeswarar Temple in Thanjavur Enjoy retail therapy in Chennai Visit Vivekananda Rock Memorial Hill-trekking in Kodaikanal Paragliding in Yelagiri Surfing at Kovalam and Mahabalipuram Jungle trekking at Mudumalai Mountaineering in the Western Ghats Visit Meenakshi Amman Temple Located in Madurai, Meenakshi Amman temple is a prominent Hindu temple dedicated to Goddess Parvati and her consort, Lord Shiva. The temple stands as a significant relic and holds immense importance in the hearts of all devotees. Almost all Tamil Nadu tour packages include a visit to this sacred site that encompasses 14 gopurams and over 33,000 sculptures. The Meenakshi Nayakkar Mandapam, i.e., the Hall of 1000 pillars carved with images of Yali, the famous mythical beast, is the star attraction of the temple. Relish a hot cup of tea at the Doddabetta Tea Factory A tea lover must not miss this activity. The Doddabetta tea factory is a great place to find out everything about the history of tea and the art of tea making. An executive guide takes the visitor through the factory, informing them about the entire tea-making process, which involves withering, fermenting, rolling, sorting, and much more. Finally, try out the different grades of tea for a blissful sensory experience. Rejuvenate senses at the Prana Spa, Kodaikanal The Prana Spa located in the verdant green stretches of Kodaikanal, away from the swarming city life, ensures a rejuvenating experience. The spa offers an array of body treatments to revitalize the mind, body, and soul. Experienced professionals at the spa can be consulted to understand precise body requirements, followed by Ayurvedic treatments, Chinese reflexologies, and Thai massages to help de-stress and detoxify. Experience true bliss at the Brihadeeswarar Temple in Thanjavur Situated in Thanjavur city, the Brihadeeswarar Temple is a sacred Hindu temple devoted to Lord Shiva. It is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and one of the biggest and oldest temples in India. In 2010, the temple turned 1000 years old. The main attraction of the monument is the Vimana or the temple tower that stands 66 meters tall. Also, the entrance features a huge statue of the Nandi bull carved out of a single rock. Enjoy retail therapy in Chennai From bustling streets to busy shopping arcades, Chennai is a wonderful shopping destination. The local markets in the city are well-known for offering authentic items such as handicrafts and textiles made by skilled artisans. Anna Salai is a famous local market that offers everything from jewelry and footwear to the most delectable food items. One can also find Tanjore paintings, stunning jewelry pieces from Kumbakonam , and luxurious hand-woven silk. A few other shopping areas include George Town, Egmore Street, Pondy Bazaar, and Nungabakkam. Visit Vivekananda Rock Memorial Located in Kanyakumari, the Vivekananda Rock Memorial was built in the year 1970. Set on a rock island, the remarkable monument houses a spectacular statue of the preacher Swami Vivekananda, who is believed to have attained enlightenment here. Many tourists come and meditate in tranquillity at the meditation hall. Visitors can comprehend the significance of the monument at the assembly hall and study hall. Hill-trekking in Kodaikanal Kodaikanal, the famous hill station, is home to three very popular trekking routes: Dolphin’s Nose trek, Mountain Beauty trek, and Echo Rock trek . But trekking in the hill station isn’t restricted to these routes. Plenty of hills and reserve forests, such as the Palani Hills Reserve Forest, offer challenging trekking routes. Paragliding in Yelagiri Yelagiri near Vellore offers the best paragliding experience, which attracts adventure lovers from all parts of the country. The activity typically takes place in the pre-monsoon season each year. The takeoff is from a maximum altitude of 2,000 feet. Many paragliding service providers operate in the area and offer week-long courses tailored to meet the needs of different age groups. Surfing at Kovalam and Mahabalipuram The beaches situated in the south of Tamil Nadu are great for indulging in water activities. Kovalam or Covelong Beach has secured an enviable position on the global map for surfing, thanks to its very popular yearly surf festival. Mahabalipuram is another destination that offers an incredible surfing experience because of its large swells. Places like Mumu Surf School in Mahabalipuram and Bay of Life Surf School in Kovalam provide surfing coaching as well. Jungle trekking at Mudumalai The Mudumalai Wildlife Sanctuary offers the best place for jungle trekking, drawing trekkers from all over the country. The dense forests with thick and unique vegetation offer the ultimate backdrop for adventure seekers. The sanctuary is home to leopards, tigers, elephants, and birds, which makes the destination more fascinating. Mountaineering in the Western Ghats The Western Ghats is among the oldest mountain ranges in India. Nowadays, they offer an excellent opportunity for rock climbing. Adventure enthusiasts who wish to partake in adrenaline-boosting activities can definitely try this out. But it is important to note that the activity is not regulated in the state, so it is crucial to go through only recognized service providers.
Tamil Nadu.Interesting Facts about Tamil Nadu: Formerly known as the Madras Presidency, Tamil Nadu is one of the 28 states of India. Before the British era, the state was ruled by different empires, including the Cholas, Pandyas, and Pallavas, that massively shaped each region’s culture, architecture, and cuisine. Here are some of the most interesting and lesser-known facts about Tamil Nadu. Geographically, Tamil Nadu lies in the southernmost region of India. On the eastern and southern sides, the state is surrounded by the Indian Ocean. On the western and northern sides, it is surrounded by Karnataka, Andhra Pradesh, and Kerala. The state has three mountain ranges, named Nilgiris, Anamalai hills, and the Western Ghats. The official language of the state is Tamil. Apart from Tamil Nadu, the language is also widely spoken in countries like Mauritius, Malaysia, and Sri Lanka. It is one of the oldest classical languages still in use today. Tamil Nadu has the biggest bus terminal in Asia as well as the most number of international airports in India. It is an industrial powerhouse with three major ports: Ennore, Tuticorin, and Chennai, which is more than any other Indian state. The traditional dance of Tamil Nadu is Bharatnatyam. Apart from this, many other folk dances are performed at festivals, such as Kuthu, Kolattam, Karagattam, and Villupattu. Tamilians celebrate many festivals; among them, Pongal is the most famous. Officially the Harvest Festival, it is a grand-scale festivity of abundance and bounty. Every year, the festival is held in January. One aspect of Pongal is Jallikattu, or bullfight, where courageous individuals grasp onto the bull’s hump and try to hold on while the bull attempts to escape. Tamil Nadu is blessed with abundant attractions and world-renowned sites. The Marina Beach in Chennai is the second longest beach in the world and witnesses an influx of over 50,000 tourists each day. The state is also home to over 30,000 temples. Kanchipuram in Tamil Nadu is world-famous for its silk sarees. According to mythology, the silk weavers in the town are the descendants of the legend, Markanda Sage, the master weaver of gods who is believed to have knitted tissue from the petals of the Lotus flower. Tamil Nadu is the largest producer of turmeric, bananas, tapioca, and flowers in India. It is also a significant producer of coconut, coffee, groundnut, and sugarcane. Lastly, large quantities of minerals such as mica, limestone, magnesite, quartz, bauxite, feldspar, and gypsum are found here.
Tamil Nadu.Tamil Nadu Culture: The southernmost Indian state is well-known for its enormously opulent heritage and culture. Everything in the state, from architecture to festivals to cuisines, radiates a powerful traditional feel, making it the real cultural hub of India. A majority of people in Tamil Nadu belong to the esteemed Dravidian Family and are a part of one of the most ancient civilizations in the world. Most Tamilians take the utmost pride in their prehistoric Tamil culture and make great efforts to preserve their 2,000 years old history. They comprehend the ethnicity of their culture and how it helps them to mark their identity in the world. Most Tamilians speak in their official language, i.e., Tamil, a derivative of Brahmi Script. They also place a great amount of significance on a person’s religion. Hinduism is the most dominant religion in the state, with over 88% of the population being Hindus. Muslims, Buddhists, and Christians together form the rest of the population’s proportion. Tamilians closely adhere to the many traditions and rituals, such as the caste system, religion, community traits, traditional attires, etc. Most men in the state can be seen wearing a shirt, lungi, and angavastra, i.e., a piece of cloth wrapped around the shoulders. They also wear Vibhuti on their foreheads. Women, on the other hand, wear cotton or silk sarees. During festivals, women adorn themselves in rich Kanchipuram silk sarees and jewels. The people of Tamil Nadu are also very interested in dance, music, and literature. For centuries, several traditional dance forms such as Bharatnatyam and many types of music, including Carnatic music, have flourished in the region. Tamilians have perfected the art of weaving, spinning, and chiseling. Their brilliance and fine craftsmanship are demonstrated in various art subjects like carvings, sculptor works, bronzes, etc. The many Hindu temples in Tamil Nadu depict the premium specimen of Tamil architecture with lofty gopurams and well-crafted deity statues all over the construction. World-renowned temples such as Meenakshi Sundereshwara Temple, Breehadeeshwara Temple, etc., are a testimony to the diversity of Hindu mythology and Tamil heritage.
Tamil Nadu.Tamil Nadu Festivals: Many different and colorful festivals are celebrated in different parts of Tamil Nadu throughout the year. These festivals are the very essence of the state’s vibrant culture and depict the year-old traditions that Tamilians proudly cherish. Planning Tamil Nadu tours around the time of these festivals is an excellent way to experience the magnificent celebrations and festive atmosphere of the state. Pongal Tamil New Year/ Puthandu Natyanjali Dance Festival Thaipusam Mahamaham Pongal Pongal , the Harvest Festival of Tamil Nadu, is the most enchanting celebration that lasts for 4 days. The festival celebrates the flourishing of harvests and offers tributes to the Sun God or Surya for bestowing the land with prosperity. The first day of the festival, known as Bhoghi, indicates a new beginning. Essentially, it marks the beginning of the new harvest season. The second day is the main festival of Pongal. The third day is Mattu Pongal, which involves worshipping the cattle. Finally, the festival culminates with Kanuma, invoking abundant blessings of prosperity for the families. Tamil New Year/ Puthandu Puthandu is celebrated in the mid of April, which marks the first month according to the Tamil calendar. On this day, women adorn their houses with beautiful Kolams, flamboyant chalk illustrations, at the entrance of their houses. It is considered a highly auspicious day for starting new things. Food also forms an integral part of this festival. People prepare the flavorful Maanga Pachadi, a sweet and sour dish made from raw mangoes, neem leaves, and the goodness of jaggery. Natyanjali Dance Festival Initially held at Chidambaram, Natyanjali Dance Festival is now presented in many cities of Tamil Nadu. It is a week-long festival where classical dancers from across the world come together to showcase their art of dance. These artists pay their tributes to Lord Nataraja (Lord Shiva) by performing various traditional dance forms like Kuchipudi, Kathak, Bharatnatyam, Pung Cholam, and Mohiniyattam. Most of these breathtaking performances take place in the outer area of Chidambaram Temple. Thaipusam Thaipusam is an incredible festival celebrated on a full moon in the Thai month of the Tamil calendar. The festival rejoices with the birthday of Lord Subramaniam, the son of Lord Shiva. On this day, people take vows and pledges and keep them for the rest of their lives. One can see the sight of Kavadi Bearers on this day, i.e., people carrying a Kavadi on their shoulders and dressed like a Pandaren. A Kavadi is a long stick with two pots hanging on each side containing offerings of milk and rice. Mahamaham Mahamaham is one of the most famous and vibrant festivals. Devotees can be seen dipping themselves in the spiritually blessed waters of the Mahamaham tank. It is believed that one dip in the water can help people cleanse their sins. Interestingly, the festival is only celebrated once every 12 years.

faq.question: 1. When Is the Best Time to Visit Uttarakhand?
faq.answer: The summer months of March and April is the best time to plan an Uttarakhand tour. Summer makes for a fantastic season to try out different adventure activities and to take part in the rigorous Char Dham Yatra. If you want to avoid the peak season, then the months of September and October are the ones to go for.

faq.question: 2. What Is Uttarakhand Famous For?
faq.answer: Uttarakhand is adored by travelers for a variety of things, but its scenic beauty, revered and architecturally beautiful temples, the picturesque Bhabar and Terrai regions etc. are what contribute most to its popularity and should not be missed out on a Uttarakhand tour.

faq.question: 3. How Far is Uttarakhand from Mumbai?
faq.answer: Uttarakhand is 1,692.6 kilometres from Mumbai which means it will take you 32 hours to cover the distance by car

faq.question: 4. What is the distance from Delhi to Uttarakhand?
faq.answer: Delhi is 349 kilometres (approx.) from Uttarakhand, essentially meaning 9hr 10min by car.

faq.question: 5. Is Uttarakhand safe for solo female travellers?
faq.answer: Solo female travellers have nothing to worry about in Uttarakhand. The State Government ensures that Uttrakhand is both safe and comfortable for solo tourists, especially women.

faq.question: 6. Does it snow in Uttarakhand?
faq.answer: Several travellers come to Uttarakhand to witness the snow during the winter season. From Mussoorie and Auli to Dhanaulti and Nainital many places in the state experienced snowfall.

faq.question: 7. What can I buy in Uttarakhand?
faq.answer: Local artisans in Uttarakhand create some striking handicrafts that you can buy and gift your loved ones. Apart from that, pashminas, home-made jams, pickles and even local wine is loved by visitors.

faq.question: 8. What language is spoken in Uttarakhand?
faq.answer: Primarily, Hindi is the language spoken most in Uttarakhand. The local languages of Uttarakhand include Garhwali, Kumaoni and Jaunsari.

faq.question: 9. What is Uttarakhand’s most famous food?
faq.answer: The simple, nutritious and delicious cuisine of Uttarakhand has number of lip-smacking items up its sleeves, the most famous being Kafuli, Bhang ki Chutney, Phaanu and Chainsoo, among others.

faq.question: 10. What are the best places to visit in Uttarakhand?
faq.answer: There are many interesting sites to visit in Uttarakhand. Nainital, Mukteshwar, Sattal, Bhimtal, Naukuchital, Corbett, Mussoorie, Rishikesh, Haridwar, Kedarnath, Badrinath, Ranikhet, Almora, and others are among the most popular you should not miss out on your Uttarakhand trip.

faq.question: 11.What is the best way to get to the Valley of Flowers?
faq.answer: GovindGhat, a mountain town in Garhwal, is the starting point for your trip to the Valley of Flowers. Take the 14-kilometer trail up to Gangharia, also known as GovindDham, which is at an elevation of 10,000 feet. A hike branches left from here, carrying you uphill for 5 kilometres into the Bhyundar Valley, also known as the Valley of Flowers.Frank Smythe, a mountaineer, discovered the Valley of Flowers in 1931 at an elevation of 10,997 feet, and named it after the region's diverse array of exotic vegetation. The valley stretches for ten kilometres and cannot be hiked in a single day.

faq.question: 13. How many days are sufficient for an Uttarakhand trip?
faq.answer: To at least see the most famous sights and experience some of the most sought after activities, a week-long trip to Uttarakhand is a good idea. Our Uttarakhand packages allow you to choose from 1 night and 2 days to 8 nights and 9 days.

Uttarakhand.Best Places to Visit Uttarakhand: If you're planning Uttarakhand trip packages, just know this state is brimming with natural beauty, adventure, and culture. Whether it’s the serene hill stations or the sacred rivers, Uttarakhand tourist places are perfect for anyone looking to soak in nature and history. Here’s a rundown of the ideal places to visit in Uttarakhand to make your Uttarakhand tour truly memorable: Nainital (The Lake District): Nainital is perhaps one of the most visited tourist destinations in Uttarakhand, primarily because of the eye-shaped Naini Lake. The town lies amidst hills and offers boat rides, scenic points like Snow View and Tiffin Top, and bustling markets. The best time for a trip to Nainital with Uttarakhand packages is between March and June, as the weather is perfect for a visit. Rishikesh and Haridwar: Among the attractions in Uttarakhand packages, the twin towns of Rishikesh and Haridwar have no competitors. Rishikesh is perfect for adrenaline junkies as well as those seeking peace. You can try river rafting on the Ganges or meditate by the riverbank. Meanwhile, Ganga Aarti at Har Ki Pauri in Haridwar is a divine experience that simply should not be missed. The best time to visit Uttarakhand for such towns is during the cooler months, from October to March. Mussoorie (The Queen of Hills): Mussoorie is one of the best places in Uttarakhand for a getaway. Its misty mornings and colonial charm are reasons why it tops the list in Uttarakhand tour packages. Some of the must-visit spots include Kempty Falls, Camel's Back Road, and Gun Hill. Strolls amidst nature are a treat in Mussoorie's lush trails. Auli: For those looking for unique places to see in Uttarakhand, Auli is a must. The main attraction here is the crystal-clear slopes and the excellent panoramic views of the Himalayas. It is also one of the prime centres for skiing. If you’re not a skiing lover, the cable car from Joshimath to Auli offers breathtaking views that you can’t find anywhere else. This might be one of the ideal places to visit in Uttarakhand during winter. Ranikhet and Almora (Offbeat): Ranikhet and Almora have serene retreats and also stand as one of the best places for nature lovers. These hill stations are full of pine forests and old temples. These places are meant for those who want some relief from the bustling and crowded tourist destinations in Uttarakhand packages. Jim Corbett National Park: If wildlife is on your mind, then Jim Corbett is one of the most captivating Uttarakhand places to visit. Renowned for its tiger population, the park also hosts safaris to spot other wildlife like elephants and deer. An overnight stay at a forest lodge adds a touch of adventure to your Uttarakhand trip packages. Beaches by the Riverbanks: While Uttarakhand packages are more famous for its hills, Rishikesh’s riverbanks, like the beaches of Ram Jhula and Shivpuri, are ideal for unwinding. You can relax on the sandy shores of the Ganges, which offers a beach-like experience in the mountains. Munsiyari: Nestled in the Pithoragarh district is Munsiyari, heaven for trekkers and nature enthusiasts. Besides the stunning Panchachuli peaks, Munsiyari is a picture-perfect town with eye-stopping scenery, unblemished beauty, and challenging trekking routes like Khalia Top and Milam Glacier. Plan a Uttarakhand trip without forgetting this pearl of a town. Peora: If you wish to cut yourself off from the world, Peora is where you should go. This off-the-beaten-path Kumaoni village is famous for its beauty, fruit orchards, and eco-tourism atmosphere. It's a perfect choice for a peaceful retreat and must feature in your Uttarakhand packages. Kausani: If you're looking to have a serene Uttarakhand experience, you need to be in Kausani. With panoramic Himalayan views, this little-known gem is all about nature. From having tea at Kausani Tea Estate, visiting Baijnath Temple, or having a serene retreat, this place has it all. Khirsu: Nested between oak and deodar woodlands, Khirsu is one of the quietest destinations for a Uttarakhand vacation. With breathtaking Himalayan scenery, trekking, and the picturesque Ghandiyal Devi Temple, the destination is a heaven for solitude lovers. Binsar: Binsar is a haven for wildlife enthusiasts and birdwatchers. Binsar Wildlife Sanctuary, Zero Point, and old temples are reasons enough to include them in your Uttarakhand tour packages. The tranquillity and splendour here are simply unbeatable! Chakrata: For an entirely offbeat experience, Chakrata is a total treat. It is situated at an altitude of 7,000 feet and provides adventure sports such as trekking, skiing, and caving. While booking Uttarakhand tour packages, include Chakrata for a wonderful experience. Cool Facts: The holy Ganga, India’s most revered river, originates from the Gangotri Glacier in Uttarakhand. It is a place of deep spiritual significance, attracting millions of pilgrims every year.
Uttarakhand.The best time to visit Uttarakhand: If you’re planning a Uttarakhand trip package, timing is everything to make the most of your visit. The best time to visit Uttarakhand depends on what you’re looking for in your Uttarakhand tour packages. Let’s break it down season by season. Summer season in Uttarakhand from March to June: For most travellers, summer remains the peak season and undoubtedly the ideal time to visit Uttarakhand. The weather is pleasant throughout the hill stations like Nainital, Mussoorie, and Auli. Daytime temperatures oscillate between 20 degrees and 35 degrees which is ideal for sight-seeing and exploration of so many places to visit in Uttarakhand. It is during this period when the demand for Uttarakhand tour packages is extremely high; hence it is advised to book in advance. Monsoon season in Uttarakhand from July to September: Monsoons are pretty tricky times to travel in as they can lead to heavy rain showers and cause landslides, thus disrupting the plans. It is an off-peak season, though, so if you enjoy the greenery and don't mind a bit of rain, it could be a great time for deals on Uttarakhand travel packages. But be cautious as roads can be slippery. Winter season in Uttarakhand from October to February: Winter is apt for those who love the snow and chilly weather. The ideal time to visit Uttarakhand for snowfall is between December and February, especially in Auli and Chopta. It is a less crowded time, perfect for someone looking for a serene experience with Uttarakhand trip packages. The temperature dips to 0°C or below; so you will need to carry proper warm clothing. Choose your Uttarakhand tour packages based on which season suits your plans, and get ready for an amazing experience in Uttarakhand.
Uttarakhand.How to reach Uttarakhand?: Planning your Uttarakhand trip packages starts with figuring out how to get there. Let me walk you through the best ways to reach this beautiful state and ensure your Uttarakhand itinerary goes smoothly. By Air: If you prefer flying, then Jolly Grant Airport is available in Dehradun city. It links up all the main Indian cities including Delhi and Mumbai. After you have touched down, you can easily find taxis or book transport as part of your Uttarakhand tour packages to reach various places to visit in Uttarakhand. By Train: Travelling by train is both convenient and budget-friendly. The three major railway stations connecting Uttarakhand to the other cities of the country are Haridwar, Dehradun, and Kathgodam. Based on your itinerary, you can travel by train to any of these stations and then reach your desired Uttarakhand tourist attraction by road. By Road: You can reach Uttarakhand by road with a flexible and scenic travel experience. The state is well connected through national highways and state roads. If you want to drive your vehicle or take the bus, it is the best mode for you when you want to travel at your own pace with Uttarakhand holiday packages. Whichever way you choose, your Uttarakhand travel is bound to be unforgettable with the right Uttarakhand tour packages!
Uttarakhand.What to eat?: When planning your Uttarakhand trip packages, make sure to explore the local flavours that make Uttarakhand food so unique. Here’s a list of must-try dishes and where to find them on your Uttarakhand travel packages. Aloo Ke Gutke: This spicy potato preparation is one of the must-try dishes in any Uttarakhand eatery, and its flavour is complete when enjoyed in any roadside dhabas of Almora. Perfect for a quick snack when you are on your trip with Uttarakhand travel packages. Bhatt Ki Churkani: This black soybean curry is high in protein content and can be relished in each corner of the state. You can enjoy it at local eating places in Dehradun to complement your Uttarakhand travel guide. Kafuli: For those looking for something healthy, Kafuli—a spinach-based curry—is a must-try. You’ll find it served at authentic restaurants in Nainital and Ranikhet. Adding this to your itinerary will be a treat for your taste buds. Bal Mithai: You haven't savoured the true essence of Uttarakhand cuisine, without indulging the fudge-like sweet in sugar balls, Bal Mithai. This has originated in Almora and should feature in the list of travel to Uttarakhand. Add these culinary gems to your Uttarakhand holiday packages for a complete experience!
Uttarakhand.Restaurants to Visit: When diving into Uttarakhand food during your Uttarakhand travel packages, make sure you visit these top restaurants for an authentic taste: Chotiwala Restaurant (Rishikesh):Chotiwala is another name that goes hand in hand with the tourism of Uttarakhand. The restaurant dishes out mouth-watering vegetarian dishes that truly and aptly represent the essence of Uttarakhand cuisine. Ideal for those visiting Uttarakhand to seek spiritual enlightenment yet a foodie at heart. Sakley's Restaurant & Pastry Shop (Nainital):For continental food and desserts, Sakley's is a great choice. It is an adorable shop located in the heart of Nainital. It’s perfect after a day spent at the top places to visit in Uttarakhand. Kalsang Friends Corner (Dehradun and Mussoorie):Known for its Tibetan and Chinese offerings, this spot is ideal for adding a bit of variety to your Uttarakhand trip plan. The momos and thukpa here are a must-try. The Birdcage (Mukteshwar): For an interesting view with a cup of coffee, The Birdcage offers you a fusion of local as well as global cuisine. This would be a wonderful addition to some lazy, laid-back Uttarakhand travel packages experience. Cool Facts: Behind the famous Badrinath temple, there is a hidden cave called the 'Vyas Gufa,' where it is believed that Ved Vyasa meditated and composed the Mahabharata. The cave is tucked away from the usual tourist routes, making it an intriguing spot for history buffs.
Uttarakhand.Honeymoon Places in Uttarakhand: If you’re planning a romantic Uttarakhand tour, this state is perfect for couples looking to create cherished memories. Let me guide you through the ideal places in Uttarakhand for your honeymoon. Lansdowne: This is a destination that is the best for couples in Uttarakhand for a quiet holiday. Pine forests and old colonial buildings surround the town of Lansdowne, where slow walks and views of scenic beauty are available. It is a place which often gets overlooked in the tourist map of Uttarakhand. Bhimtal: If you want a quieter version of Nainital, you will look no further than Bhimtal. With its picture-perfect lake and quaint surroundings, it happens to be one of the best Uttarakhand honeymoon destinations to visit for an intimate experience. Chopta: Chopta is known as the 'Mini Switzerland of India' and is a perfect destination for nature and adventure-loving couples. The trek to Tungnath and Chandrashila offers breathtaking panoramic views, making it one of the top Uttarakhand tourist attractions. Kausani: For those who love scenic views, Kausani is the destination that offers panoramic views of the Himalayas. It is a top destination to visit in Uttarakhand because of its sunsets and serene ambience. Providing tea gardens and the Rudradhari Falls, it is a perfect destination for quiet walks and cosy evenings. Add Kausani to your Uttarakhand trip packages to make your honeymoon truly special. Kanatal: Kanatal happens to be an ideal place for couples, who want to spend quiet and scenic time. Apple orchards and pine forests surround this beautiful village which is one of the best places to visit in Uttarakhand for romantic getaways. Camping and nature trails of Kanatal add a tinge of adventure to your Uttarakhand tour.
Uttarakhand.Shopping in Uttarakhand: When planning your Uttarakhand trip packages, don’t forget to include some time for shopping. Uttarakhand shopping is a delightful experience, and there are plenty of places where you can pick up unique items. Here are some top places to visit for shopping: Nainital's Mall Road: Perfect for candle shopping, woollen garments, and woodwork, no Uttarakhand tour itinerary can be considered complete without visiting Nainital's Mall Road. The beautiful candle shops selling candles in the shapes of animals and various fragrances can be great souvenirs. Bhotia Market: Bhotia Market has genuine Pashmina shawls, woollens, and handwoven carpets. It is a vibrant place where you can buy some of the best local artistry. Paltan Bazaar (Dehradun): For any Uttarakhand travel packages, Paltan Bazaar is the right place for affordable items, spices, handicrafts, and even Garhwali traditional jewellery. Lala Bazaar (Almora): Lala Bazaar in Almora is famous for its copperware and Angora wool products. Therefore, it is a must-visit for Uttarakhand tour packages. Did you know that the famous "Valley of Flowers" wasn’t always on the map?The Valley of Flowers, a UNESCO World Heritage site, remained hidden from the outside world until the early 1930s when it was discovered by British mountaineers. It’s only in recent decades that it has gained fame for its breathtaking meadows, making it an offbeat paradise for nature lovers.
Uttarakhand.Things to do in Uttarakhand: When planning your itinerary, make sure to pack it with experiences that showcase the region’s beauty and culture. Here are the top things to do in Uttarakhand to make your trip unforgettable. Trekking in the Himalayas:Hitting the trails is one of the adventure activities that Uttarakhand boasts of. Amongst the other popular trails among top Uttarakhand tourist spots, the Valley of Flowers and Roopkund Trek are touted for their breathtaking panoramic views. Jim Corbett National Park Wildlife Spotting:Spotting wildlife in Jim Corbett National Park should be on your list of places in Uttarakhand. It's one of the most exhilarating sites to see in nature. Whitewater Rafting in Rishikesh:If you are an adventure-seeker, then you are bound to visit the Ganges for river rafting in Rishikesh. It is also popular as the adventure capital of India. Rishikesh can be called the haven when it comes to offering such rapids, thus bestowing it as the major place in Uttarakhand travel packages for water sports. Visiting Temples and Ashrams:Exploring sacred sites like Kedarnath and Badrinath adds a spiritual touch to your journey and ranks among the ideal places to visit in Uttarakhand. Tasting Local food:Sampling foods in Uttarakhand is a must. From Aloo Ke Gutke to Bal Mithai, indulging in local delicacies is a wonderful way to experience Uttarakhand tourism at its finest.

Additionally, the easy-to-use platform of Thomas Cook and personalized customer support ensure that booking a tour is convenient and hassle-free. Their well-trained travel consultants are always there to help you choose the most suitable Uttarakhand tour package according to your interests and dates.
Uttarakhand.Interesting facts about Uttarakhand: Uttarakhand is a land full of natural wonders, a rich past and hundreds of years old legends. Here are some interesting facts about the place that tourists should know about: Uttarakhand is known by its nickname, Dev Bhoomi, which translates to “The Land of Gods”. The place is home to numerous religious shrines and landmarks and is connected to Hindu mythological texts. The two UNESCO World Heritage Sites in Uttarakhand are Nanda Davi National Park and the Valley of Flowers. The two of the most sacred rivers in India, Ganga and Yamuna, originate from the state of Uttarakhand. Uttarakhand is home to Nanda Devi Peak, the second-highest mountain peak in India. It has an elevation of 7,816 metres above sea level. The ancient practice of yoga originated in India thousands of years ago. Rishikesh in Uttarakhand is called the world’s Yoga capital due to the presence of numerous yoga institutes and meditation centres. Thousands of fitness enthusiasts and yoga practitioners visit Rishikesh from all corners of the world. Tehri Dam in Uttarakhand, located at an elevation of 260.5 metres above sea level, is the highest dam in India. The highest Shiva temple in the world, Tungnath, is located in Uttarakhand. It is believed to be thousands of years old and is a part of Panch Kedar temples. Jim Corbett National Park in Uttarakhand, established under British Raj back in 1936, is the first national park in India. The ancient language of Sanskrit is the additional or second official language of Uttarakhand following Hindi.

Mansarovar.What to Expect During Your Kailash Mansa: The Kailash Mansarovar Yatra is much more than just a physical journey; it’s a life-changing spiritual experience. Here’s a glimpse of what you can expect on this sacred trip: Visit the Holy Kailash Mountain: Revered by Hindus, Buddhists, and Jains alike, Mount Kailash is believed to be the abode of Lord Shiva. Its imposing beauty, shrouded in clouds and snow, exudes a sense of peace and power that cannot be described in words. Holy Mansarovar Lake: Known for its crystal-clear waters, Mansarovar Lake is considered to be one of the holiest lakes in the world. Sacred Temples and Sites: Along the way, you will visit several ancient temples and spiritual sites that are imbued with deep historical and religious significance. Mansarovar.Places to Visit in Kailash Mansarovar: Places to Visit Significance Lake Mansarovar Highest located freshwater lake, striking landscape with azure clear waters reflecting the stark white façade of Himalayas. Offers the first glimpse of Kailash Parbat. Chiu Gompa Monastery is adjacent, with Mt. GurulaMandhata, hot springs nearby, and unique wildlife. Lake Rashekshi Also known as Rakshas Tal or Ravana Lake. It holds Hindu mythological significance as the place where Ravana prayed to Lord Shiva. Known for glimmering transparent waters surrounded by velvety green meadows. Shaktipeeth Jaya Bhageshwori Temple Located beside the holy Lake Mansarovar, dedicated to Goddess Mansa—an avatar of Goddess Shakti. Dehra Phuk Pristine mountainous region known for trekking, waterfalls, and clear blue skies. It’s the starting point of Kailash Parikrama (Kora). Dolma La Spiritual journey route from Dehra Phuk to ZuthalPuk. Passes through Yamasthal and Shivasthal to reach Dolma La. En route, visit Gauri Kund and Parvati Sthal, considered holy sites. Gauri Kund Legendary lake mentioned in Hindu mythology. Known for its breath-taking beauty, lush vegetation, and crystal-clear water. Acts as a base camp for the journey to Kedarnath Temple. ZuthalPuk Also called the ‘Cave of Miracles’, it is the ancient holy abode of the great ascetic Milarepa.
Mansarovar.How to Reach Kailash Mansarovar: The journey to Kailash Mansarovar is as much about the journey itself as it is about the destination. By Road: You will travel by buses or jeeps from Tibet, Nepal, or China to reach the destination. The road journey is long, but the breathtaking views along the way will make it all worthwhile. Mansarovar.Things to Do in Kailash Mansarovar: Perform the Parikrama: One of the most significant rituals is the Kailash Parikrama, a walk around the base of the mountain. faq.question: When is the best time to visit North East India?
faq.answer: The ideal time of year depends on your preferences, but the area is stunning throughout the year. The months of October through February are ideal for animal safaris, sightseeing, and taking part in festivities like Hornbill. The hills experience nice weather during the summer (March to June), which is ideal for outdoor pursuits. Your journey to northeast India would be much more enjoyable if you chose the appropriate time of year.

North East.Introduction: North East India awaits you if you've ever wanted to travel to a place where rivers flow endlessly, mountains kiss the clouds, and every festival seems like a celebration of life. You can go on a tour through the eight dynamic states, each with its own unique character, culture, and stunning landscapes, with North East Tour Packages. Every destination feels like a separate universe, from the monasteries of Sikkim to the foggy hills of Meghalaya. Looking at the North East travel package is more than just reserving a vacation; it's a gateway to an area endowed with rare species, rainforests, and centuries-old customs. Every North East travel package offers life-changing experiences, whether it's relaxing in the peace of Tawang Monastery, strolling across Cherrapunji's live root bridges, or seeing one-horned rhinos in Kaziranga. A North East package combines adventure and tranquillity, as well as modernity and tradition, whether you're organising it for your family, friends, or even a single retreat. Every tourist who takes advantage of the North East India package finds a tale they will never forget.
North East.Why Explore North East India – Nature, C: You should definitely visit North East India at least once if you're searching for a vacation that feels unique. When you go with North East Tour Packages, you enter an area where nature, culture, and tradition are told around every corner. Allow me to explain why this land is so unique: Magnificent Landscapes: Every vista seems like a postcard, from the snow-capped mountains of Arunachal Pradesh to the undulating tea fields of Assam and the foggy lowlands of Meghalaya. Selecting a North East package will allow you to visit areas where the natural world is still unspoiled and unadulterated. Cultural tapestry: A region rich in customs, music, dance, and festivals, the area is home to more than 200 tribes. By choosing a North East package, you can take in the colourful Sangai Festival of Manipur, the Hornbill Festival of Nagaland, and the Bihu of Assam. Exotic Wildlife: Picture seeing the renowned one-horned rhinoceros in Kaziranga or getting a look at Sikkim's red pandas. Safaris and guided tours are frequently included in the North East package, providing you with the opportunity to see endangered species in their native environment. Distinctive Experiences: A package trip to North East India guarantees experiences that will last a lifetime, whether it's meditating in historic monasteries, walking on live root bridges, or taking a river cruise on the Brahmaputra. Calm Retreat: North East Tour & Travels offers you eco-friendly lodging, organic farms, and tranquil villages in contrast to popular tourist destinations. By scheduling a North East package, you can relax, take in the scenery, and rediscover yourself. A North East travel package is the solution if you're looking for a vacation that genuinely combines adventure, culture, and environment. This area is more than just a place to visit; it's an experience that will leave you with more memories and stories.
North East.North East India Tour Packages We Offer: Choosing the type of adventure you wish to take is more important than simply scheduling a vacation when considering North East Tour Packages. North East India tour packages are made to provide you with the greatest possible balance of culture, nature, and leisure, whether you're searching for a quick getaway, a family vacation, or a lengthy journey across several states. Here are a few well-liked choices for you to consider: Views of the Eastern Himalayas: This package trip to North East India, which concentrates on Sikkim and Darjeeling, is perfect for those who enjoy mountains. A genuine Himalayan experience awaits you as you see snow-capped peaks, Buddhist monasteries, and little toy trains. Assam & Meghalaya Delights: This package trip to North East India is ideal for those who enjoy lush, green hills, hazy waterfalls, and energetic cities. It typically includes Shillong, Kaziranga, and Guwahati, where you can take in tranquil lakes, tea gardens, and wildlife excursions. The Best of Assam's Rhinoland: This package trip to North East India brings you directly to Kaziranga National Park if seeing exotic wildlife is your idea of a vacation. It's short, inexpensive, and exciting, making it ideal for a short vacation. Four Sisters Expedition: This comprehensive package trip to North East India includes Assam, Meghalaya, Arunachal, and Nagaland for those who want it all. This type of journey to northeast India allows you to experience tradition, culture, and nature all at once. Customised and Family Tours: Because no two vacations are alike, North East India packages frequently include customizable itineraries. North East trip packages can be created specifically for you, whether you're looking for a customised experience or a relaxed family vacation.
North East.Sample Itinerary for a Package Trip to N: Booking a North East tour package is more than simply a vacation; it's an adventure, cultural, and nature-woven experience. Here is an example package trip to North East India that combines Assam and Meghalaya with a touch of Sikkim and Darjeeling to give you a sense of what your trip can entail. This is only one iteration; the package trip to North East India can always be tailored to your preferences and speed. 10-Day Itinerary for a Trip to North East India Day 1: Your journey to the northeast starts when you land in Guwahati. Enjoy a leisurely evening beside the Brahmaputra River and pay a visit to the well-known Kamakhya Temple. Day 2: Drive from Guwahati to Shillong, making a stop along the way at the stunning Umiam Lake. After checking into your accommodation, visit Police Bazaar to buy and eat locally. Day 3: You will visit the "abode of clouds" on this day of your North East India tour packages. Explore the enigmatic Mawsmai Caves, Nohkalikai Falls, and Seven Sisters Falls. Day 4: Discover the cleanest village in Asia, Mawlynnong, and take a stroll on a Living Root Bridge. Later, travel to Dawki to see the Umngot River's glistening waters. Day 5: Get to your lodge close to Kaziranga National Park after travelling to Assam. Visit neighbouring tea estates or take in cultural concerts in the evening. Day 6: You can witness wild elephants, one-horned rhinoceroses, and more on an early-morning elephant safari that is followed by a vehicle trip through the grasslands. Day 7: Drive or fly to Sikkim. Arrive at Gangtok around evening and stroll down M.G. Marg. Day 8: See Baba Mandir, Tsomgo Lake, and, if the weather permits, the high-altitude Nathu La Pass. These features are frequently the most treasured aspects of the North East India package. Day 9: Enjoy the tea gardens in Darjeeling by taking a drive there. Enjoy a steaming cup of pure Darjeeling tea in the evening as the sun sets over the hills. Day 10: Get up early to witness the dawn over Kanchenjunga from Tiger Hill, and then continue to Bagdogra. You can experience a variety of wildlife, adventure, and cultural events with North East Tour & Travels. The allure is that every day offers something fresh, from monasteries and rhinos to root bridges and Himalayan sunrises, whether you're booking short or extended North East trip packages. The North East trip price will vary depending on the length of time, the places you stay at, and the things you include, but the memories and stories you bring back are genuinely invaluable.
North East.Essential Information for Planning Your: It's exciting to plan a trip to North East India, but it does need some planning. Because of the region's diversity, which includes shifting weather patterns, landscapes, and cultural traditions, it's wise to be aware of a few key facts before booking your North East India trip package. Let's go over the key points you should remember to have a seamless and enjoyable journey. Best Time to Visit North East India Every season in the Northeast has something special to offer; the decision you choose will rely on your preferences. The most sought-after season for the North East India package is winter, which runs from October to February. It's the perfect time of year to visit Kaziranga National Park or take in Nagaland's Hornbill Festival since the skies are clear and the air is crisp. Sikkim and Arunachal Pradesh in the winter will provide you with plenty of snow if you're a snow lover. Summer, which runs from March to June, is also the best time to visit the North East, particularly if you like the cooler climate in the hills. Trekking and outdoor activities in Meghalaya and Sikkim are ideal during this time of year. Just bear in mind that because this is the busiest travel season, North East India package are typically a bit more costly. Most travellers steer clear of the monsoon season, which runs from July to September, due to the torrential rains that might result in landslides. However, Meghalaya in the rainy season is worth the trip if you enjoy breathtaking waterfalls and verdant surroundings. Please keep in mind that Kaziranga National Park is closed during this time. How to Reach the North East Region? With a number of practical options, travelling to the Northeast is easier than it appears on a map. Air travel is the simplest method. The primary entry point is the Lokpriya Gopinath Bordoloi International Airport in Guwahati, which has excellent connections to other major cities. Other airports such as Imphal, Agartala, Dimapur, Aizawl, or even Pakyong in Sikkim can be reached from there. West Bengal's Bagdogra serves as the typical entry point for visitors heading to Darjeeling or Gangtok. Guwahati, Agartala, and Dimapur are significant railheads if you're a train enthusiast. Remember that trains in this area are slower than those in other areas. Road trips are also typical, particularly for shorter distances between states. Booking a car with a local driver as part of your North East India travel packages is frequently the best option because roads in Assam and Meghalaya are generally acceptable, but in Arunachal Pradesh or Nagaland, the roads might be bumpy. Travel Guidelines and Safety Tips Although the Northeast is friendly and safe, following a few tips can help your North East India tour packages go more smoothly. While international visitors would require a Protected Area Permit (PAP), Indian nationals must have Inner Line Permits (ILP) in several states, such as Arunachal Pradesh, Nagaland, Mizoram, and Manipur. These should ideally be organised before you go. It's critical to show respect for regional cultures. When travelling to rural or tribal areas, always get permission before taking pictures of people and wear modest clothing. Since unexpected strikes (bandhs) can occasionally cause travel disruptions, keeping up with local news is also beneficial. If you're prone to motion sickness, stay away from bottled water, bring any necessary medications, and be ready for twisty mountain roads. When it comes to North East tour & travels, it's usually a good idea to book in advance, particularly during busy times when even low-cost accommodations fill up fast. Keep enough cash on hand, as ATMs can not always be functional, and a power bank can be extremely helpful in isolated locations with erratic electricity. Packing Tips for North East Travel Your destination and season will determine how you pack, but adaptability is essential. Bring layers of clothing for a North East holiday package. The best clothing for the day is cotton, and for the evening, sweaters or jackets are recommended. Remember to bring a bag cover and a waterproof jacket or poncho if you're travelling during the monsoon season. Here, shoes are quite important. While sandals are useful for informal outings, a decent pair of robust walking shoes will make treks and long walks more enjoyable. Carry necessities such as a tiny torch, sunscreen, bug repellent, and a reusable water bottle. A power bank and extra batteries are essential for anyone who enjoys photography. Finally, to save time at checkpoints, always have several copies of your ID proofs and permits on hand. When you have these fundamentals in place, your North East travel package becomes less about worrying and more about having fun.
North East.North East Trip Price and Budget Insight: The budget is the first consideration when making travel plans to North East India. In actuality, the North East trip price is negotiable and contingent upon your preferred mode of transportation. Before you book your North East Tour Packages, let's have a look at what to expect. Tour Packages NameDurationPriceAssam and Meghalaya6 Nights 7 DaysRs. ₹48 000Assam - Best Of Rhinoland2 Nights 3 DaysRs. ₹18 900Explore Shillong 3 Nights 4 DaysRs. ₹22 100Glimpses Of Eastern Himalayas4 Nights 5 DaysRs. ₹32 300Essential Assam and Meghalaya5 Nights 6 DaysRs. ₹41 400 Average Package Prices: Depending on comfort and inclusions, a 3-day trip package to Sikkim in northeast India can cost between ₹10,000 and ₹15,000 per person, while a week-long North East India trip package can cost anywhere from ₹25,000 to ₹40,000. With careful planning, a 10-day low-cost package trip to North East India that includes Assam and Meghalaya can be completed for ₹25,000. Food Costs: You can spend as little as ₹200 to ₹500 per day if you like eating at neighbourhood restaurants. You should budget between ₹1,200 and ₹1,500 per day for mid-range meals. One of the highlights of the majority of North East India packages is the opportunity to sample regional cuisine. Options for Staying: The cost of lodging varies depending on comfort. The nightly rates for hostels and guesthouses range from ₹500 to ₹1,500. Luxury stays under premium North East trip packages can easily surpass ₹6,000 to ₹12,000 per night, while mid-range hotels vary from ₹2,000 to ₹4,000. Sightseeing and Permits: The amount is increased by permits, such as Nathula Pass (₹400–₹500) and safari rides in Kaziranga. Activities under North East Tour & Travels, cable car rides, and local guides cost extra, but the experience is well worth it. Transport Fees: Depending on the season, flights to Guwahati typically cost between ₹3,000 and ₹10,000. Train travel is less expensive; sleeper class tickets typically cost less than ₹1,200. For group travellers seeking North East holiday packages, shared cabs are an affordable option because they typically cost between ₹300 and ₹1,000 each trip.
North East.Food and Accommodation in North East Ind: One of the finest ways to explore the culture of North East India is through its cuisine and distinctive accommodations. The best North East Tour Packages ensure that you experience the charms of each of these states. Local Cuisine to Try in the North East With rice, pork, and fermented foods serving as the foundation for many dishes, the cuisine is straightforward but flavorful. Try the traditional Khar or the zesty fish curry, Masor Tenga, if you're in Assam. While Nagaland is known for its smoked pork prepared with bamboo shoots, Meghalaya will pamper you with spicy Tungtap and smoky Jadoh. In Sikkim, you'll like the Tibetan-inspired Sha Phaley and steaming bowls of Gyathuk, while in Manipur, local favourites include Iromba and Nga Atoiba Thongba. These dishes are stories on a platter, not just food. To ensure you don't miss these genuine experiences, a well-designed North East India tour package always includes culinary trails. Stays Included in North East Tour Packages The variety of lodging options is equal to that of the food. Depending on your preferences, the majority of North East trip packages contain cosy guesthouses, boutique homestays, or cosy hotels. While mid-range hotels typically vary from ₹2,000 to ₹4,000 per night, budget-friendly options start at ₹500 to ₹1,500. Luxury North East holiday packages frequently include premium resorts and heritage properties, which can cost anything from ₹6,000 to ₹12,000 per night. Every encounter deepens your adventure, whether it's a homestay in Meghalaya with friendly local hosts, a resort with a view of the mountains in Gangtok, or a riverfront stay in Assam. You can choose a North East India trip package with North East Tour & Travels that strikes a mix between comfort and genuineness. Naturally, the price will change based on what is included, so before making a reservation, always verify the North East trip price. A well-planned North East holiday packages offers you the best of both worlds: genuine regional flavours and cosy accommodations that allow you to unwind after a day of exploration.
North East.Unique Experiences in the North East Bey: It's simple to imagine tea gardens, hazy valleys, and undulating hills when you think of the Northeast. Its people, wildlife, and crafts, however, are where the true charm can be found if you go past the typical tourist. Selecting the best North East Tour Packages allows you to go deeper and provides experiences that you will remember long after your trip is over. Tribal and Cultural Interactions Staying with indigenous tribes is one of the most fulfilling aspects of a North East India package. Imagine staying with the Apatani people, who are renowned for their sustainable rice-fish farming method, in Ziro Valley for a night. You get to experience a modest yet profoundly linked way of life, dwell in bamboo houses, and hear folklore. Meeting the Konyak tribe's tattooed elders in Nagaland's Mon district is like entering a living history book. You can even view the chief's residence at Longwa village, which is situated on the border between India and Myanmar, and hear their fascinating historical tales. In Majuli, Assam, where riverine pottery workshops and mask-making at Samaguri Satra bring the art and spirituality of Neo-Vaishnavite traditions to life, a different kind of cultural immersion is in store. It all comes together at festivals like Hornbill, where you can meet people from different tribes, take in traditional dances, and purchase handcrafted goods straight from the makers. Wildlife & Nature Tours The North East travel package might take you much beyond a jeep safari if you're a nature lover. You can raft down the Jia Bhorali River or hike woodland paths in Nameri National Park, where you can see endangered species like the White-winged Wood Duck. With species that are unique to Arunachal Pradesh, Eaglenest is a birdwatcher's paradise. faq.question: How much does a France trip from India cost for a week?
faq.answer: A 7-day France trip from India usually costs ₹1.1 lakh–₹1.8 lakh per person, depending on flights, stay, food, and sightseeing.

faq.question: What is France famous for?
faq.answer: France is known for the Eiffel Tower, the Louvre, Notre-Dame de Paris, the Palace of Versailles, Moulin Rouge, Disneyland Paris, high fashion, and fine wines.

faq.question: Do Indians need a visa for France, and how long does it take?
faq.answer: Yes, Indians need a Schengen visa to visit France. It costs around ₹6,500–₹7,500 and normally takes 2–3 weeks to process.

faq.question: What is the best time to travel to France?
faq.answer: The best time to travel to France is in spring (April–June) and autumn (September–October). Pleasant weather, blooming gardens, and fewer crowds

faq.question: How many days are sufficient to explore France?
faq.answer: Around 8–10 days are ideal to cover highlights like Paris, the French Riviera, wine regions, and small countryside towns.

faq.question: Which are the must-visit places in France apart from Paris?
faq.answer: Nice, Lyon, Bordeaux, Marseille, Strasbourg, and the Loire Valley are top picks beyond Paris.

faq.question: Is France good for shopping?
faq.answer: Yes, France is a shopping paradise with Paris boutiques, luxury brands, flea markets, and regional specialties like perfumes and wines.

faq.question: Is France an expensive country to visit?
faq.answer: France is on the pricier side, especially Paris, but budget stays, train passes, and local cafés can make travel affordable.

faq.question: Will language be a problem in France?
faq.answer: In tourist cities, you can manage with English, but in smaller towns, basic French phrases will help a lot.

faq.question: Is France suitable for a family vacation?
faq.answer: Yes, France is family-friendly with Disneyland Paris, beaches, castles, museums, and outdoor activities for kids and adults alike.

faq.question: What is the best time to visit Malaysia?
faq.answer: The best time to visit Malaysia is between the months of December to February.

faq.question: What documents are required to visit Malaysia?
faq.answer: Your visa, passport, and tickets are required to visit Malaysia. Note that you must apply for your visa at least 6 months prior to your date of travel.

faq.question: Is visa on arrival available for Indians in Malaysia?
faq.answer: No, you cannot obtain a visa on arrival in Malaysia. However, you can apply online for a Tourist Visa to Malaysia.

faq.question: How long can I stay in Malaysia as a tourist?
faq.answer: On a visitor visa, you can holiday in Malaysia for up to 90 days.

faq.question: What is the time difference between India and Malaysia?
faq.answer: The time difference between India and Malaysia is 2 hours and 30 minutes.

faq.question: What is the best way to travel to Malaysia?
faq.answer: The quickest way to travel to Malaysia is by booking a flight from India. The journey takes about 3 hours and 50 minutes from Chennai to Kuala Lumpur. Flights to Malaysia are available from all major Indian cities such as Delhi, Mumbai, and Chennai.

faq.question: What is the currency of Malaysia?
faq.answer: The Malaysia Ringgit is the official currency of Malaysia. You can buy Malaysia Ringgits from Thomas Cook India.

faq.question: Can I travel to Malaysia by sea?
faq.answer: You can take a cruise to Malaysia only from Singapore, Thailand, or Phuket.

faq.question: What are some common greetings in Malaysia?
faq.answer: Hello in Malaysia is usually "Selamat pagi." Good Afternoon is "Selamat tengahhari," and Good Night is "Selamat malam."
