table_of_content: Other such places to visit in Cambodia
Cambodia.Introduction: Cambodia has been one of the most famous tourist destinations in South East Asia and globally. Thailand borders this beautiful country to the northwest, the Gulf of Thailand to the southwest, Vietnam to the east, and Laos to the north. Cambodia is the perfect place to start the travel journey in South East Asia. Cambodia has a population of 17 million, of which about 26% is involved in the tourism sector, indicating a huge volume of tourists Cambodia receives each year. The major reason behind that is the astounding beauty of the country.With plenty of beaches and finger licking food, Cambodia offers a blend of nature and a rich culture. As the home of the Khmer Empire, Cambodia allows tourists to experience different cultures and traditions. Moreover, we will also get to learn about the wide history of the region. Not only this, but there are plenty of beaches in the country.Tourists have the option to enjoy the peace of serene islands as well as enjoy the great nightlife and parties. For instance, in the South of Cambodia, Koh Rong island has incredible nightlife. On the other side of the country, Koh Rong Samloem is a great place for a peaceful stay and witness sustainable tourism.Thus, for those looking to experience authentic islands, Cambodia is the place to go. With the option of a community homestay, tourists can experience a closer look into the lives of locals. With a plethora of trekking options, we will definitely learn a lot about the uncommon plants and animals. The wildlife tours in the region are full of adventure. These include a wide variety of animals, including bears, elephants, and tigers.Cambodia is also a heaven for foodies as it offers numerous luscious dishes. Crab, beef (lok lak) and fish (amok) are some of the most common foods in the region. These are consumed daily, usually with rice and noodles. Moreover, there are a number of food tours and cooking classes across the country.Another major reason that makes Cambodia the ideal travel destination is that we would not need to burn a hole in our pocket to travel around the country. As dollars are widely used across the region, there is no need to worry about the forex exchange.Cambodians are famous for their hospitality and will ensure that we have a great time in the country.
Cambodia.Most Popular Cambodia Tour Packages 2024: Tour Packages NameDurationPriceClassic Cambodia3 Nights 4 DaysRs. ₹40 300Cambodia Coastal Escapade6 Nights 7 DaysRs. ₹76 200Magical Vietnam with Cambodia9 Nights 10 DaysRs. ₹181 700Magical Vietnam With Ho Chi Minh And Cambodia11 Nights 12 DaysRs. ₹211 300Responsible Siem Reap3 Nights 4 DaysRs. ₹41 600
Cambodia.Quick Facts About Cambodia: Place Cambodia Currency use Riel (៛) Country Cambodia Time Zone UTC +7 (Indochina Time - ICT) Weather Hot and Humid Best Time to Visit November-May and December-February Places to Visit Angkor Wat, Phnom Penh, Koh Rong Official Language Khmer Famous Food Amok, Samlor Korko Famous Places Bayon Temple, Tuol Sleng Genocide Museum Currency : Cambodia’s official currency is Riel, which is denoted with ‘៛’. Weather : Being slightly above the equator makes Cambodia a tropical nation with year-round temperatures between 21 and 35 °C (69.8 and 95 °F). The temperature here is hot and humid. There isn't a severe winter here. Places to Visit : We can always find the perfect place in this incredible country as it offers something for everyone. Be it peaceful beaches like Koh Rong Samloem or places with great nightlife for party animals like Koh Rong.Official Language : Khmer is the national language of Cambodia and is widely spoken around the region. Nevertheless, the majority of people speak English in the country as well.Covid Guidelines :Before entering a country, it is always advisable to check its Covid guidelines. Cambodia is open to travel for travellers. People from all nationalities can enter the country. Hence, there are no specific rules for any specific country. Fully vaccinated tourists should not face any difficulties. All vaccinations are approved, and there are no criteria for booster doses. Fully vaccinated tourists only need a certificate to enter the country. While unvaccinated travelers have to undergo a Covid rapid antigen test when arriving in Cambodia.
Cambodia.Time to Visit Cambodia: It is always a good idea to learn more about the climate and weather of a destination beforehand to make the most out of the trip. Cambodia has two major seasons across the year. These are the Monsoon season and the Dry season. It is in the dry season when most tourists come to visit this country, and there is a fairly reasonable reason behind the same. During the dry season, the country sees less amount of rainfall. It typically lasts from November to April. This allows the tourists to enjoy the clear sky on the southern coasts.From February to April, the temperatures in Cambodia continue to rise as much as 40 degrees Celsius. Even in May and June, the temperature continues to be high. The humidity is also extreme during these times. This eventually results in rainfall.As soon as the monsoon season begins, Cambodia receives high rainfall. This is a result of high humidity. While it is extremely helpful for local farmers in order to grow costs, the rain often causes problems in remote areas. Due to this, it can be tough to travel in such regions. However, not all areas are impacted by it. Thus, we can also enjoy the greenery and scenic beauty. Flora and fauna during this season are at their peak. Hence, this can be a good time for those who cannot wait for long hours of wildlife exploration. Even though Cambodia has no official winter season, the months between December and February are quite cooler. This is why many tourists also prefer this time frame. Most of the time, January is the coldest month but even then, the temperatures are around 25 to 30 degrees Celsius. This is perhaps the best time to visit Cambodia and explore places like Phnom Penh, Temples of Angkor, Battambang, and Siem Reap. As there is comparatively less humidity, it becomes easier to visit a variety of temples and markets. However, this is why Cambodia witnesses more tourists during this time. Thus, we will have a better time if we are prepared for crowds. Many experts consider October a good time to visit Cambodia. This is because, at this particular time, the rainy season has come to an end. Moreover, the humidity is also quite low. Multiple temples across the country are also quiet in comparison to other months. Also, the tourists can find incredible discounts and offers to push down their expenses further. Lastly, this is a great time to visit the famous Tonle Sap Lake.We should keep all these points into consideration and choose to visit Cambodia during a time that we believe will be suitable for us.
Cambodia.How to reach Combodia: There are many ways to reach Cambodia from which we can choose to travel. Some of the most popular ways to reach Cambodia include:By Air : The most convenient way to travel from India to Cambodia is by air. Compared to any other form of transportation, air travel takes the least time. This is why the entire journey becomes extremely comfortable. We can take a flight from the majority of metropolitan areas in India like Mumbai, Delhi, Kolkata, and Chennai. While in Cambodia, the major airports include Phnom Penh International Airport and Siem Reap International Airport. Usually, there is a single stop on the journey from India to Cambodia. The typical flight duration is around 6 hours. We can choose from a variety of airlines including Singapore Airlines, Air Asia Philippines, Malaysian Airlines, and Thai Airways. It is always better to book the tickets in advance, especially in October and November, to save a considerable amount on flights.By Train : There are no direct trains from India to Cambodia. Thus, to travel by train, we will have to book a flight to Bangkok and then take a train and reach Cambodia. This is an ideal option for those looking to explore various regions in SouthEast Asia. Hence, taking a train can be time-saving when travelling between different Southeast Asian countries. This will also allow us to gain many new experiences and learn about the culture and traditions of different Southeast Asian nations. By Road : The journey from India to Cambodia by road can be quite adventurous. However, we must keep in mind that this will not be simple and easy. We will have to travel through numerous countries to reach the final destination, which can be quite expensive and more time-consuming. We will also need a number of documents when entering and leaving each country, such as passport, international driving permit, credit card and possible itinerary. Nevertheless, when it comes to travelling within Cambodia, it is best to rely on buses. This is because buses are very affordable and easily available in the country. When travelling in some of the remote areas, we can find plenty of tuk-tuks and many other modes of transportation.So, if we want to reach Cambodia in a short span with great ease, then the airlines are the best option. On the other hand, if we want to explore South East Asian regions as well, then we can choose to take an affordable flight to Bangkok and cover the rest of the journey by train. Throughout the pandemic, millions of people moved from one place to the other. Maybe, after more than two years of the pandemic, we are also wondering if it would be safe to travel abroad. YT Video Thomas Cook offers its customers a wide variety of benefits that allow us to stay free from all the travel-related worries. With Thomas Cook's Assured Safe Travel Program we get plenty of benefits, including free rescheduling and zero cancellation charges. Moreover, the covid insurance will cover the expenses in case we face any difficulties. So, focus on enjoying the holidays as Thomas Cook will take care of everything else.
Cambodia.Main Destinations of Cambodia: Cambodia's rich history and culture attracts millions of tourists from different parts of the world. Yet it is not the only thing that drives people to this Southeast Asian country. Its beautiful beaches and natural beauty are other major factors. Some of the most popular tourist destinations in Cambodia are: Angkor Wat (Angkor Archaeological Park) Koh Kong Conservation Corridor Phnom Penh Kampot Sambor Prei Kuk Ratanakiri Angkor Wat (Angkor Archaeological Park) Angkor Archaeological Park is undoubtedly one of the most popular tourist destinations in the country. The temples of the Angkorian era are incredible in size. Angkor Wat is among the world’s must-see ancient sites. Built between 802 and 1432, Angkor Archaeological Park was the largest city in the world during the mediaeval age. Today, it is the largest religious building in the world. Built by King Suryavarman II, this ancient temple can be visited between 5 am to 6 pm. Koh Kong Conservation Corridor Located in Cambodia’s Cardamom Mountains, the Koh Kong Conservation Corridor is the ideal place for boat tours and hiking. Encompassed with dense jungles, astounding waterfalls, and a beautiful river, Koh Kong Conservation Corridor has some eco-friendly places to stay. It also has options for homestay and guesthouse accommodation. Phnom Penh Phnom Penh is not only the capital of Cambodia but also its most populated city. Today, Phnom Penh has become one of the most dynamic regions of the world but it was deserted during the Khmer Rouge madness. The city has numerous historical sites that showcase the history of the country. Along with incredible architecture, it has many cafés and restaurants as well. Phnom Penh has numerous tourist attractions including the Royal Palace of Cambodia, National Museum of Cambodia, Tuol Sleng Genocide Museum, and Wat Phnom. The city also has a variety of transportation options including trains, buses, and flights. Kampot To spend some peaceful time in the country then Kampot is one of the regions that will provide us with much-needed relaxation time. The town is quite laid-back and has shop-house-designed infrastructure. Many tourists spend a long time in this region because of its beautiful surroundings and peaceful life. Nevertheless, Kampot also has many destinations that are worth visiting. These include Phnom Bokor National Park, Long Set Beach, and Phnom Chhnork Cave Temple. Sambor Prei Kuk Sambor Prei Kuk is a temple site that was established in the early seventh century. From an archaeological point of view, this pre-Angkorian temple is quite significant as it is Cambodia’s one of the oldest buildings. Some of the vital sites inside Sambor Prei Kuk include Prasat Yeay Pea, Prasat Tao, and Prasat Sambor. Ratanakiri Along with numerous temples, Cambodia offers its tourists a variety of adventure sports as well. Ratanakiri is one such natural region where tourists can try out a variety of sports. We can visit several villages by surpassing the long red-dirt roads that spread over Ratanakiri. This region is heaven for those who are interested in trekking. It has Veun Sai-Siem Pang Conservation Area as well as the Virachey National Park, which is home to many animals, including bears, tigers and elephants.
Cambodia.Best Hill Stations/ Best Beaches: Cambodia has numerous beaches and islands across the country. The scenic beauty of these beaches allows the tourists to have a peaceful and relaxing time. Some of the most popular beaches in Cambodia include- Ream Beach Saracen Bay, Koh Rong Samloem M'Pai Bai, Koh Rong Samloem Otres Beach Ream Beach Ream Beach is about half an hour away from Sihanoukville city. It is a part of the Ream National Park. This is another secluded beach where we can see a few fishermen and cafes that are run by families. There is also a mangrove wetland area near the beach. Ream Beach is the ideal place to escape the hustle and bustle of popular beaches and spend some days surrounded by nature. Saracen Bay, Koh Rong Samloem Koh Rong Samloem is one of the sister islands on Sihanoukville, along with Koh Rong. Saracen Bay once used to be a sleepy fish area but now it has become one of the best tourist islands. This region is very popular among couples and younger visitors. The island has a laid-back vibe that will allow us to forget all our worries and enjoy nature. M'Pai Bai, Koh Rong Samloem Located on the northern side of Koh Rong Samloem, M'Pai Bai is not as crowded as Saracen Bay. Thus, it is the perfect place for those who want to escape the crowds and spend quality time in a peaceful environment. M'Pai Bai is deserted for the most part and we can find some local fishermen nearby. This area has some hostels and guest houses. We can go on a hike to explore two nearby waterfalls as well. Otres Beach Even though Otres Beach is a part of the Sihanoukville area, it is not as crowded as other regions. This is because it is far from the major beach areas. Hence, we can experience the local vibe of the area. The water around Otres Beach is clearer than many other beaches. Moreover, it is surrounded by palm trees which give the place a spectacular look.Even though Cambodia is famous for its beaches, it also has a hill station. Bokor hill station is a bunch of French buildings built in the 1920s as a temperate mountain spot for colonial residents. Even though Bokor hill station was abandoned for a long time, it is now easily accessible because of recent developments. The region also has a waterfall that is usually dry in the high season but regains full flow during monsoon. Bokor Hill Station is highly popular among tourists because of its astounding views and great weather.
Cambodia.Places to Visit in Combodia: Cambodia has numerous temples as well as the largest religious building in the world. But there is a lot more to this country than the beaches and monuments. Choosing the right Cambodia Tour Packages will ensure that we get to explore all the must-visit places in the region and gain experiences that will be relished for a lifetime. Mondulkiri Siem Reap Prasat Preah Vihear Battambang Tonlé Sap Lake Kratie Mondulkiri The small town of Sen Monorom is the major tourist attraction in the Mondulkiri region. This place is ideal for those who are more inclined toward making their trip to Cambodia full of adventure. In the Mondulkiri province, there are many options for tourists to enjoy themselves, including trail-bike tours, exploring wildlife, and the popular Elephant Valley Project. Under the Elephant Valley Project, travellers can walk with the elephants of the sanctuary. It is important to keep in mind that riding the elephant is not allowed. Siem Reap Tourists usually consider Siem Reap a significant part of Cambodia’s must-visit places because Angkor Archaeological Park is situated in it. However, the town has a lot more to offer. This is one of the major centres of the region. Thus, we can find a variety of tours ranging from cycling tours to cookery tours as well. We can also find numerous traditional crafts around the area. In the evening, Phare the Cambodian Circus is also organised. Prasat Preah Vihear While Angkor Wat may be extremely popular among tourists, we cannot miss the Prasat Preah Vihear location. Prasat Preah Vihear is an incredible temple constructed to honour Lord Shiva. It is at the top of the Dangrek Mountains and offers a break-taking view of Cambodian floodplains. It is quite close to the border of Thailand. Both nations have claimed the temple as their own. This UNESCO World Heritage Sight is a must-visit for tourists in Cambodia. Battambang Battambang is a city in Cambodia that is perfect for those who want to spend some time away from the hustle-bustle of Phnom Penh. As compared to the capital, Battambang has a much more relaxed vibe. The northwest of Battambang is surrounded by small villages and rice fields. The city is also rich in its history as historical temples like Wat Ek Phnom, Phnom Sampeau, and Phnom Banan are quite nearby to the region. Due to all this, Battambang has become a significantly famous spot among travellers. Tonlé Sap Lake Tonlé Sap Lake is Southeast Asia's largest freshwater lake. It is considered the most significant waterway for Cambodia. This lake is also home to 170 floating villages whose livelihood depends on it. Tonlé Sap Lake is also a vital source of food for irrigation in Cambodia. Be it shops, schools, or houses; everything is made by sticking together bamboo and barrels. Kompong Luong and Chong Kneas are some of the popular villages around it. Kratie Kratie is the ideal place to experience the beauty of Mekong. Kratie has become popular over the years because of the dolphin-watching tours. Irrawaddy dolphins have been endangered in the Mekong. Many steps have been taken to ensure that they are protected. Tourism has played a major role as an alternative to fishing in the region.
Cambodia.Famous food in Combodia: Even though Cambodian cuisine has started to become popular in other parts of the world, the best way to experience the majority of Cambodian dishes is to visit the country. Thus, while planning the Cambodia trip, make sure to try out all the Cambodian cuisines. Some of the most popular dishes in the country include: Amok Samlor korkor Nom banh chok/Khmer noodles Kha trei svay kchai - Caramelised fish with green mango Amok Amok is undoubtedly one of the most popular dishes in Cambodia. Similar dishes to it can also be found in neighbouringf countries. The addition of Slok Ngor separates the taste of the Cambodian version from the rest of the world. The curry of the dish is made from kroeung and coconut milk. Initially, Amok was prepared only with snails or fish. However, over the years, chicken is also added to the dish. There are even some vegetarian versions of the dish as well. Samlor korkor Even though Amok is often considered the national dish of Cambodia, Samlor korkor is also extremely popular in the region as well. Samlor korkor has been a popular dish in Cambodia for hundreds of years. The great part is that it can be found even today across the country in different restaurants as well as local stalls. The soup is quite adaptable and can be varied as per the season. Even though any sort of meat can be added, pork belly and catfish are commonly used. Nom banh chok/Khmer noodles Nom banh chok is another Cambodian dish that is extremely popular in other parts of the world. At times, it is called Khmer Noodles in English. Nom banh chok is majorly consumed as breakfast. Thus, tourists usually find women who carry baskets that contain this dish. It is prepared with noodles and many other ingredients including fingerroot ginger, garlic, and turmeric. Quite often banana flowers, cucumbers, beans, edible leaves and flowers are out on top of the dish. Kha trei svay kchai - Caramelised fish with green mango Almost everyone has seen fishes that are quite active even when they are not underwater. These fishes are frequently seen jumping from baskets, trying to escape. Well, it is none other than the striped snakehead fish, also known as Trei roh. It can walk on land. That’s why it can move even when put out of water. It is the key ingredient of this dish. Initially, the steaks are kept in a pot and then caramelised with black pepper, fish sauce, garlic, tomatoes, and palm sugar. The dish is served with Asian basil and green mango on top.
Cambodia.Things to do in Cambodia: From visiting numerous temples to chilling on beaches, Cambodia offers its tourists plenty of fun activities. Here is a list of things to do on a Cambodia trip : Watching Apsara Dance Show Explore different Cambodian cuisines Witness the Phare Cambodian Circus Visit the Tuol Sleng Genocide Museum Visit Phnom Penh Palace Watching Apsara Dance Show One of the best ways to explore Cambodian culture is to watch the dance show of Apsaras in the Siem Reap region. Apsaras have always been a huge part of Hindu mythology. They used to dance in Lord Indra’s kingdom in heaven. This depicts that the Hindu culture is still deeply rooted in the Cambodian culture. Thus, watching this show will bring us even closer to the culture of Cambodia. The dance show of Apsaras is so deep-rooted in Cambodian culture that it is even drawn on the walls of Angkor Wat. Explore different Cambodian cuisines No trip is ever complete without exploring the different delicacies that the country has to offer. Thus, on our Cambodia trip, try a variety of dishes. Since the majority of these dishes are not easily available outside Cambodia, it makes much more sense to enjoy them as long as we are there. Most of the dishes in the region are non-vegetarian. Moreover, rice and noodles are commonly used in most cuisines. Witness the Phare Cambodian Circus Art is a major part of Cambodian culture. Hence, to truly understand the essence of Cambodian culture, we must watch the Phare Cambodian Circus. The circus showcases the rich history of the country. Moreover, the circus has earned a name for itself globally. Throughout the circus, we will witness performances including theatre, dancing, and singing. These acts showcase the numerous stories of the country. Phare Cambodian Circus is so famous that it has become a national act. Furthermore, numerous training centres have also opened in various parts of the country to train those interested in performing in the circus. The time of Phare Cambodian Circus is from 8 am to 8 pm. Visit the Tuol Sleng Genocide Museum When people think that Cambodia has a rich history, their mind will likely go in the direction that everything about its past has been glorious and peaceful. However, it is not the case. Moreover, it is equally important to showcase as well as analyse the other side as well. This is where the Tuol Sleng Genocide Museum plays a significant role. It is a remnant of the atrocities conducted in the Khmer regime. The Tuol Sleng Genocide Museum has been made in the memory of genocide initiated by Khmer rulers. It showcases the terrifying interiors and utilises the painful past to lead to a peaceful tomorrow. A trip to this museum is something that each tourist must do whenever they visit Cambodia. The museum is located in Phnom Penh and the timings are between 8 am and 5 pm. Visit Phnom Penh Palace The Royal Palace of Cambodia is something that tourists should aim to visit when choosing the Cambodia tour package. It is home to the royal family. The gigantic palace has many paintings that showcase the history of the country. Moreover, the architecture is also astounding. The timings are from 8 am to 5 pm.
Cambodia.Adventurous things to do: Adventurous activities are an assured way to make the Cambodia trip a memorable experience. Cambodia offers a variety of adventure activities, including mountaineering, rock climbing, sailing, fishing, hiking, and much more. Explore different parts of the country on a moped Before everything else, it is vital to ensure that the vehicle given to us fulfils all the safety standards. While riding around different parts of the country can be very exciting, we must follow the safety guidelines, including wearing a helmet and not driving above the given limit. Now, it is time to explore unknown territories and feel freedom. Tourists can easily rent a moto and visit different places in the country. Take care of a bear Sun bears and moon bears are some of the most loved animals in Cambodia. Tourists can opt for being a bear keeper for a day. We can work with the volunteers at Phnom Tamao Wildlife Rescue Centre. Wildlife tours can be incredible, but it will never be as exciting as taking care of a bear. Check out Kirirom’s Pine Forests Cambodia has numerous hiking trails, but nothing beats the experience of travelling in pine forests. Kirirom’s Pine Forests will leave us with a memorable experience as they are surrounded by breathtaking waterfalls and foot trails. Thus, it is the perfect place for those who are looking to explore wildlife and do trekking. Go around Siem Reap on a bicycle Exploring different areas around Siem Reap on a bicycle can be quite exciting. There is no need to carry our own bicycle as plenty of businesses offer numerous biking tours. Going from one part of the city to the other by cycling will allow the tourists to witness the true beauty of Siem Reap. Witnessing the marvellous sunrise is something that we will remember for a lifetime. Hike in Kep National Park Kep National Park has been a major attraction for hikers as well as trekkers for a long time. This park is the perfect place irrespective of whether we are well-versed in hiking or not. As the trails are marked with arrows, even beginners can go a long way without facing any difficulties. So, visit the Kep National Park and explore the new and exciting territory in a guided manner. As we continue our journey, we will get to see great wildlife.
Cambodia.Interesting Facts about Cambodia: There is much more to Cambodia than amazing beaches and historical monuments. Here are some unpopular facts about Cambodia- Cambodia is the only country with a building on its flagCambodia is unique from other parts of the world in a variety of ways. The majority of flags around the globe usually either have similar colours & different designs or the same designs & different colours. None of more than 200 countries have a monument on their flag. Yet, Cambodia added Angkor Wat to its flag, making it the only flag with a building. Angkor Wat is the largest religious building in the world. This makes the Cambodian flag quite distinctive. Funerals are a costly affair in the country It can be surprising to find out that funerals in Cambodia have an average cost of USD 9000. Moreover, they go on for 49 days. Many people usually put together the money they have accumulated over time to ensure the funeral cost is covered. Some even have to sell their possessions to do so. All this is done because funerals hold immense importance in Cambodian culture. The average age in Cambodia is less than 15 Today, many countries have a high median age. However, Cambodia is running in the opposite direction. We might be astounded to know that approximately 50% of the population of Cambodia is less than 15 years old. Furthermore, 60% of the population of this Southeast Asian nation is less than 30 years old. This is one of the reasons why Cambodia is full of young faces with a positive outlook on life. However, the reason behind such a young average age is not positive whatsoever. It is the result of genocide conducted during the rule of the Khmer Rouge. In a span of just 4 years, the population of Cambodia was reduced by 2-3 million. Birthdays are not a big deal in Cambodia Birthday is one of the most important days of people's life. Hence, they not only eagerly wait for it for weeks but also emphasise celebrating it in the best way possible. However, this is not the case with the majority of the population in Cambodia. Here, birthdays are not considered a major event. Some individuals do not even remember their birthdays; they are only aware of the season of their birthday.
Cambodia.Cambodian Culture: Cambodia has an extremely rich culture. It has been preserved by the people who have been following a variety of traditions for a long time. Gaining a deeper understanding of these cultures and traditions can make the trip to Cambodia even more worthwhile. Apart from choosing the right Cambodia tour packages, it is always better to learn more about the country so that we can enjoy our stay to the fullest. Here are some things we should know about the various culture and traditions in Cambodia: The majority of the rural Cambodian diet consists of rice and fish In the past, Cambodians had to deal with a shortage of food. However, the situation has improved to a great extent in the current times due to international help and political stability. Nevertheless, even today, the rural diet in Cambodia majorly includes fish and rice. Many other ingredients like mint and ginger can also be included. People put great emphasis on family life Even though people are attached to their families across all parts of the globe, Cambodians emphasise their families greatly. The families are generally bigger as extended family members are also closely associated. Distant relatives like uncles, aunts, and cousins usually celebrate most of the festivals together. Most Cambodians practice their religion Numerous temples are a clear indication that a majority of Cambodians are highly religious. Buddhism is the most followed religion in the country. Approximately 97% of people follow Theravada Buddhism in Cambodia. Even the younger individuals are highly engaged in religious activities. Most people visit temples during a variety of festivals. People also hang photos of Buddha in their homes.Superstitions are quite popular in Cambodia Superstitions are quite common in many Asian countries, and Cambodia is no exception. Many Cambodians believe in spirits and superpowers. Superstitions have become a regular part of life for most people. For instance, whenever a new enterprise is opened, it must be blessed. Cambodian festivalsFestivals are a major part of Cambodia’s rich history and culture. Plenty of festivals are celebrated in the country throughout the year. During festivals, people come together and make the most out of it. Tourists from different parts of the world also relish these festivities. Learning more about these festivals and traditions will allow us to become a greater part of them and rejoice in them to the fullest. Moreover, we will also be able to plan the Cambodia tour packages effectively to attend the festival we want. Here are some of the most popular festivals that take place in Cambodia: Khmer New Year Khmer New Year is one of the most prominent festivals in Cambodia. During this festival, people with Khmer roots return to their places in order to celebrate the end of the harvest season for three days. Farmers who have worked hard all year long also take a break. Throughout the festivities, people spend time with their families and go to different temples. King Norodom Sihamoni’s Birthday The birth and coronation of the current monarch of Cambodia, King Norodom Sihamoni is celebrated from 13th May to 15th May each year. This day remains of great significance for Cambodians as in the memory of Pol Pot and Khmer Rouge. Hence, the people express their gratitude toward the progressive government. Pchum Ben/Ancestor’s Day Pchum Ben translates to “to gather together a ball of food.” It is also popular as the Festival of the Dead and Hungry Ghosts Festival. People pay respect to the seven generations of ancestors on this day. Food is also distributed as part of the festival.Cambodian Independence Day Cambodian Independence Day is widely celebrated across the country to commemorate the end of French rule that lasted for over 90 years. This significant day is celebrated on 9th November in the Independence Monument. People from all walks of time come together to rejoice in this historical day. Cambodian Visa for Indians Gone are the days when getting a visa used to be stressful. Today, Indians can get an eVisa for Cambodia easily, even without reaching out to the Cambodian Embassy. eVisa is ideal for people who are interested in visiting the country as a tourist. It is vital to understand that Indian citizens require a visa to go to Cambodia, even for a relatively short span of time. Before departure, Indians can apply for the Cambodia eVisa. With the eVisa, we can spend an entire month In Cambodia for the purpose of tourism. In case, we need to spend more than a month or are going there for any non-tourism related purpose, it is ideal to contact the Cambodian Embassy in India for more information regarding the visa.Follow these steps to get a Cambodian visa: Filling the electronic visa form (for Indians) Use a debit card or credit card to pay for the visa Forward the visa application to be reviewed. It is worth noting that the Cambodian visa processing is quite fast. Moreover, many Indian citizens receive the approved visa within only 3 to 4 business days via email. There are majorly two types of visas Indians can choose from to visit Cambodia - Cambodia Tourist Visa for Indians and Cambodia Business Visas for IndiansIn order to receive a tourist visa, we would need a few documents including a digital photo, passport, email id, and a debit or credit card.Thus, before finalising the Cambodia package, make sure we have all the necessary documents.
Cambodia.Foreign Currency: Cambodia's official currency is the Cambodian Riel. However, the United States Dollar has been acting as the unofficial second currency of the country for a long time. The majority of local businesses in Cambodia would accept the USD without any issues. Moreover, if we receive a visa on arrival, we would also be required to pay the amount in USD. This clearly shows that US dollars should be the ideal choice for international travellers. While it is quite clear that USD will go a long way in Cambodia, many tourists still face many issues regarding a forex exchange. From getting the best rate for the bucks to choosing a reliable source, exchanging domestic currency for dollars can be complicated.Thomas Cook can help us in solving this issue. A rate card is available on the official website wherein we can buy and sell the currency of our choice. When it comes to buying, tourists can also get a travel card which is much more convenient than carrying cash all the time. We can also choose to sell INR to receive the currency of our choice. Moreover, there is an incredible option of Locked-in exchange rates. This will save us the worry of constant fluctuations in the market. The forex travel card can be loaded at the locked-in exchange rate and will ensure that the value of the amount remains the same irrespective of the fluctuations. Thomas Cooks understands the customer's pain points and aims to come up with the best possible travel-related solutions. Thus, investing in a travel card can improve the Cambodia tour package.
Cambodia.Other such places to visit in Cambodia: Cambodia has been a spectacular tourist destination over the years. Its temples, beaches, rich history and culture have won the hearts of millions of tourists. Today, there are incredible Cambodia tour packages available. Nevertheless, after visiting this beautiful country, one would love to explore other Southeast Asian countries as well. So, here’s a list of countries we can try if we loved Cambodia:Laos : Laos is a nation that could be ideal for those who love nature. Laos has also become a very popular tourist destination over the years. Its adventure sports can excite all the tourists. Similar to Cambodia, Laos also has a UNESCO heritage site: Luang Prabang. While it is not as famous as Angkor Wat, a good number of travellers visit Laos each year in search of new experiences. Indonesia : Many would argue that hardly any countries have more scenic beauty than Indonesia . Its divine beaches, incredible forests, and astounding hikes are a call for tourists from countries across the globe.Vietnam : Vietnam is one of the safest and most affordable tourist destinations not only in Asia but across the globe. Even wars could not vanish the marvellous beauty of this Southeast Asian country. With a rich history, beautiful infrastructure, and finger-licking food, Vietnam has become one of the favourite tourist destinations. The trip to Asia will be incomplete without visiting Vietnam once.

faq.question: What are the best places to visit in Spain?
faq.answer: Barcelona, Seville, Madrid, Granada, Mallorca, Bilbao, Ibiza, Canary Islands are some of the best places that you should not miss out on your Spain Trip.

faq.question: Is a Spain tour expensive?
faq.answer: When compared to many other Western European countries, Spain is known for being an affordable vacation destination. While visiting popular destinations such as Barcelona or Madrid may appear to be more expensive, it is still possible to visit on a budget, with an average Spain trip costing around €55-65 per day.

faq.question: Is it safe to visit Spain?
faq.answer: Spain is one of Europe's safest countries. Spain is generally thought to be a safe place to visit. In fact, Spain is among the world's safest countries, ranking in the top 10. According to locals, you should proceed with caution as you would anywhere else.

faq.question: What is the official currency of Spain?
faq.answer: The official currency of Spain is Euro.

faq.question: What is the cheapest time to plan a Spain tour?
faq.answer: The cheapest months to plan a trip to Spain are typically December to March, though early spring and late autumn can also provide affordable flights and hotels. Many vibrant markets and activities are dedicated to annual celebrations such as Christmas, Carnival, and New Year's Eve during the winter months.

faq.question: How much does a one-week Spain trip from India cost?
faq.answer: A one-week trip to Spain from India usually costs ₹95,000–₹1.6 lakh per person, depending on flights, hotels, food, and sightseeing choices.

faq.question: Why do tourists love visiting Spain?
faq.answer: Spain is popular for its sunny beaches, vibrant festivals, tapas culture, football fever, and architectural wonders like Sagrada Família in Barcelona.

faq.question: Do Indians need a visa to travel to Spain, and how much does it cost?
faq.answer: Yes, Indian citizens need a Schengen visa to visit Spain. The fee is about ₹6,500–₹7,500, and the process may take a few weeks.

faq.question: When is the best time to plan a Spain holiday?
faq.answer: The best time to visit Spain is in spring (Apr–Jun) and autumn (Sep–Nov) when the weather is pleasant and less crowded.

faq.question: How many days are enough for Spain?
faq.answer: To explore Spain comfortably, plan for at least 8–10 days, covering cities like Madrid, Barcelona, and Seville.

faq.question: Which cities in Spain should I not miss?
faq.answer: Top cities to visit in Spain include Barcelona (art & nightlife), Madrid (museums & shopping), Seville (flamenco & history), and Valencia (modern architecture & beaches).

faq.question: Is Spain costly for Indian travellers?
faq.answer: Spain is relatively cheaper than other Western European countries, with plenty of options for budget food, stays, and transport. Luxury travellers, however, can spend much more.

faq.question: What currency is used in Spain?
faq.answer: Spain uses the Euro (€). Cards are widely accepted, but having some cash helps in local cafés, small towns, or street markets.

faq.question: Will language be a barrier on a Spain trip?
faq.answer: In major tourist cities, you’ll manage easily with English. But in rural villages or smaller towns, knowing a few Spanish phrases can be helpful.

faq.question: Is Spain a good place to have a family vacation?
faq.answer: Yes, Spain is family-friendly, offering theme parks, beaches, cultural attractions, and safe cities suitable for kids and adults alike.

Spain.Introduction: Spain is one of the most popular places to visit in the world. It is a place where desire and history meet. From the lively cities of Madrid and Barcelona to the peaceful beaches of Costa del Sol and the fantastic architecture of Andalusia, Spain tourism will surely leave you with lasting memories. Spain has a great mix of history and modernity, making it a great place to visit, whether you are interested in art, architecture, music, or nature. Our all-inclusive Spain tour packages at Thomas Cook start at just ₹89,100 and include flights, lodging, meals, and guided tours of the sights. These Spain holiday packages are made to give Indian tourists a hassle-free, culturally rich Spain trip without having to worry about every little thing. Your trip will be jam-packed with exciting experiences, such as flamenco shows, Gaudí's strange buildings, and delicious Spanish food. Our most popular Spain tour packages from India make seeing secret gems and famous sites easy and comfortable. We have the best Spain tour packages available for those looking to experience the best of Spain tourism in one go. If you're planning a Spain trip from India, Thomas Cook can make it easier for you by offering custom Spain packages to create experiences that will last a lifetime.
Spain.Most Popular Tour Packages: Thomas Cook has a range of Spain tour packages that are perfect for people who are going alone, with friends or family, or with a partner. Take a look at these most common types: Tour Packages NameDurationPriceHighlights of Spain and Portugal - Summer 202611 Nights 12 DaysRs. ₹304 100Highlights of Spain Portugal And Morocco - Summer 202615 Nights 16 DaysRs. ₹335 100Highlights of Spain and Portugal With 3N Mediterranean Cruise - Summer 202614 Nights 15 DaysRs. ₹340 700Spanish Delights4 Nights 5 DaysRs. ₹89 600Highlights Of Spain6 Nights 7 DaysRs. ₹124 100 Family Tour Packages from India: These Spain packages, which are appropriate for all ages, include large accommodations, transportation that is safe for kids, and guided tours of some of the most popular Spain tourist places. These family tour packages from India include things to do in Spain, like going to the zoo, seeing flamenco shows, and going to theme parks. The trip lasts between 8 and 9 days and costs at least ₹1,56,400. With these Spain tour packages from India, the whole family will be comfortable and have a great time. Honeymoon Tour Packages: These Spain trip packages include romantic candlelight dinners, sunset cruises, and cosy stays near the Mediterranean. They are perfect for couples looking for a romantic getaway. It includes places like Granada, Seville, and Ibiza. Prices start at ₹1,23,300 and last for 6 to 7 days on average. To have experiences that will last a lifetime, check out these carefully selected Spain travel packages. Adventure Tour Packages: For people who would like to be thrilled, these Spain trip packages from India include paragliding, scuba diving, and hiking. Places like Costa Brava and Sierra Nevada are significant. The trips last between 11 and 16 days and cost from ₹3,09,700. These Spain tour itineraries combine history, nature, and adventure, all in one. Budget Tour Packages: Cheap Spain trip packages for students and backpackers include shared lodging, public transportation, and the most important places to see in Spain. From ₹89,100, you can go on trips that last 4 to 5 days. These Spain packages let you learn a lot about the country's culture without spending much money. The Spain trip cost ranges from ₹89,100 to ₹4,07,700 based on the length and features of each package. Some of our Spain tour packages from India also have early bird savings and holiday deals.
Spain.Best Time to Visit Spain: When is the best time to visit Spain? Knowing this can make your trip more enjoyable. Depending on what you like - festivals, weather, or crowds - here are some seasonal tips for booking Spain tour packages: Spring (March to May): Spring is generally considered the best season to visit Spain because of the warm weather (15°C to 25°C), the flowers in bloom, and the reduced tourist traffic. That makes it great for city trips and sightseeing, which are part of most Spain tour packages. Summer (June to August): Great for beach lovers, but towns can get hotter than 35°C. The Spain trip cost may increase during this time because it's the busy tourist season. Get the best prices on Spain tour packages from India by making your reservation early. Autumn (September to November): Due to the mild weather, grape harvest events, and cheaper flights, many people think this is the best time to go to Spain. It's a great time to go on Spain food trips and cultural Spain trip packages. Winter (December to February): It's cold in northern Spain, and it snows in the mountains. A good choice for cheap Spain packages and less busy touring during the off-season. Choosing the best month to visit Spain will help you get the most out of your Spain trip, whether you're interested in seeing Spain attractions or indulging in Spain cuisine.
Spain.How to Reach Spain?: Knowing the best travel choices is the first step in planning a Spain trip from India. Here's how to reach Spain, whether you want to take a straight trip or a route through Europe that takes in the sights. By Train Even though there isn't a straight train for your trip to Spain from India, taking the train is a fun part of the trip once you get to Europe. From places like Paris, Milan, and Zurich, high-speed trains like AVE (Alta Velocidad Española) make it easy to get to Spain. If you're booking multi-country Spain packages, these trains are an excellent choice because they're quick, beautiful, and on time. Many Spain travel packages come with Eurail passes that allow you to see other European countries before you even get to Spain. By Road You can drive to Spain from countries in Europe that are close by, like France and Portugal. Renting a car or making reservations for a bus between cities is a common method of how to go Spain if you are already in Europe. Your Spain trip will be more beautiful if you drive through the Pyrenees or along the Mediterranean coast. But this isn't a good idea if you're coming from India; it's better to add on to a trip within Europe. By Air How to reach Spain from India most practically involves flying. Spain.Best Places to Visit in Spain: Spain has beautiful towns, peaceful beaches, and spiritual sites that will appeal to all kinds of travellers. The Spain tourist spots have something special to offer, whether you're there for history, rest, or faith. Here is a list of the best places to visit in Spain, broken down by type. Famous Places to Visit The cultural and building history of the country is based on these towns. These places will probably be part of any of our Spain tour packages you book. Barcelona: The top places to visit in Spain are in this city, where art and architecture meet. It is home to Gaudí's famous Sagrada Familia and Park Güell. Madrid: Each and every Spain tour includes a stop in Madrid, the country's capital city. It is a cultural hub with royal castles, world-class museums, and a lively nightlife. Seville: This is one of the most lively Spain tourist places. It is famous for flamenco dance and beautiful Moorish architecture. Granada: Granada combines its Islamic past with modern charm, making it among the top places to visit in Spain on any Spain trip. Visit the famous Alhambra and walk through the old town. Valencia: It is one of the top-rated Spain attractions because it offers a unique blend of modern architecture and traditional culture. Beaches/ Hill Stations to Visit These coastal and mountain areas are perfect for those looking for natural beauty and rest in their Spain trip packages. There are a lot of Spain tour packages from India that include them. Ibiza: One of the top places to go in Spain, it offers peaceful spots, lovely beaches, and nightlife. Costa del Sol: Long stretches of sand, fancy villas, and water that is as clear as glass make this place a beach lover's dream. Sierra Nevada: This is one of the best hill towns in Spain. You can ski, hike and enjoy the mountain views. Mallorca: It is famous for its beautiful hills, turquoise bays, and quaint towns, making it an excellent place for love breaks and high-end Spain trip packages.
Spain.Temples to Visit: Spain is home to some of Europe's most architecturally stunning religious sites. These are must-see Spain places to visit for those interested in spiritual heritage. Sagrada Familia (Barcelona): A globally renowned basilica and spiritual symbol of Spain, included in all Spain holiday packages. Mezquita of Córdoba: It is an essential stop on any Spain tour because it is a rare example of Islamic and Christian architecture. Cathedral of Santiago de Compostela: A historic pilgrimage site and one of the most visited Spain tourist attractions. Burgos Cathedral: An awe-inspiring Gothic cathedral, often overlooked but a true hidden gem among things to see in Spain. These Spain things to see make every Spain trip more fulfilling, whether you're a history buff, beach bum, or spiritual explorer.
Spain.Cities to Cover: Spain is a very unique country, and each city is different, with its own mix of history, architecture, and the best things to do in Spain. Cities with history, culture, and the coast should all be on a well-thought-out Spain tour so that you can get the whole experience. In most Spain tour packages, these are the must-see cities: Madrid: The busy capital city has lively plazas, the Royal Palace, and world-class museums like the Prado. It's a great place to start your Spain trip because it's full of things to do in Spain. Barcelona: The Sagrada Familia and Park Güell are two examples of Antoni Gaud's architecture. All Spain packages include this city as a top choice because of its lively beach life and tapas bars. Seville: A beautiful city with Moorish castles, Gothic churches, and flamenco dancers. It embodies the beauty of Andalusia and is frequently the best part of many Spain travel packages. Granada: The beautiful Alhambra and the ancient Albaicín area are here. In this city, the Islamic past, Spanish culture, and Spain food all come together. Valencia: Famous for its modern City of Arts and Sciences and tasty Spain cuisine like paella. On a classic Spain trip, it adds a modern twist. If you visit these places, your Spain tour will show you the best of the country's history, modernity, and natural beauty.

Best places to visit in Mykonos
Mykonos.Introduction: The Greeks gave us a lot from civilization to delectable cuisine, but the best part is probably the island of Mykonos. This mystical land that extends up to 88.3 square kilometres is a popular holiday destination. Tourists book mykonos vacation packages from all around the world to experience the island of the winds for its luxury and serenity. Mykonos is not only an island for relaxation but also a popular port that is part of several international cruises. People don't just book mykonos holiday packages for the Mykonos stop attractions but rather also to let loose at the party hub of Greece. The white sandy beaches beckon friends, couples, and families for tranquil daytime activities while the vibrant nightlife is the perfect escape for a vacation.
Mykonos.Table of Content: Most Popular Mykonos Tour Packages Mykonos COVID-19 tourist guide How to reach Mykonos Travelling within Mykonos Best time to visit in Mykonos Best Places to Visit in Mykonos What to eat in Mykonos Things to do in Mykonos Forex currency exchange for Mykonos Visa application for Mykonos tour People also ask about Mykonos Tour Packages
Mykonos.Most Popular Mykonos Tour Packages: Tour Packages NameDurationPriceHighlights of Greece - Summer 20266 Nights 7 DaysRs. ₹253 500Highlights of Greece With Crete - Summer 20268 Nights 9 DaysRs. ₹340 300Highlights of Greece with Rhodes - Summer 20269 Nights 10 DaysRs. ₹350 800
Mykonos.How to reach Mykonos: When booking your mykonos tour packages with Thomas cook, you can choose how to travel from India to this beautiful island in Greece. Mykonos is connected to the mainland via the airport as well as the harbour. You can choose to fly to Athens and then take a ferry or boat ride across the Aegean sea or book a shorter flight to Mykonos airport from Athens. Travelling within Mykonos Travelling to the best places to visit in Mykonos is a thrilling adventure in itself. The most popular way to get around the island is by opting for public transportation such as a bus on land and water taxis that ferry people to the best places to go in Mykonos. If you are not fond of public transportation you can always opt for a private cab or take a walk around the island as most of the locals prefer. You can also rent a car and drive yourself or have the complete European experience with the scooter. Some places along the beaches of Mykonos also offer atvs to travel within the island.
Mykonos.Best time to visit in Mykonos: If you want to witness the best things to see in mykonos, you need to plan your mykonos travel packages during the best time of year to visit mykonos. Not only is the atmosphere all around warm and welcoming, but the top attractions are also beautiful beyond words during this time. The best time to go to mykonos is either during the month of September to October when autumn hits the island. It is certainly the best time to visit as the leaves fall all around against the wistful scenery in the background creating quite a romantic backdrop. The nightclubs are thriving and the restaurants and beach clubs are open till late making it the best time to travel to mykonos, especially with friends or with your spouse. Although autumn is the best time to visit mykonos, you can also book your mykonos trip packages for the later part of the spring season just before summer. It is the best month to visit Mykonos and the air is still a little crisp while the sun shines brightly on the Greek island. If you wish to go sightseeing then a brewery is certainly the best month to go to mykonos.
Mykonos.Best places to visit in Mykonos: The island of mykonos is a haven filled with sights and sceneries depicting culture, history, civilization, and the core earning of mankind to enjoy the life that we have been given. From tranquil beaches to archaeological museums and everything in between is a cultural representation of the evolution process that helps us truly appreciate the value of life. Here is a list of the best places to see in mykonos that you must add to your itinerary for your mykonos tour packages: Agios sostis beach One of the most spectacular, mykonos best places to visit is fondly named after the church in the neighbourhood. It is a secluded area with not a lot of foot traffic due to its dysconnectivity with the main town. However, it is the ideal place to lie on the beach and relax or even try the ultimate greek cuisine experience. Paradise beach The party hub of mykonos houses is one of the most popular nightclubs in Greece. It was quite a success with the Rastafari population back in 1970 but has quickly evolved to accommodate diverse people from every community to enjoy some vibrant music and beach activities. Panormos beach Another secluded beach lies along the bay in the northern regions of mykonos. Public transportations is not available in this region so you may have to drive yourself on a scooter or hire a private car. It is the perfect place to indulge in traditional beachside activities such as volleyball or serene walks. Platys Gialos beach The ultimate relaxation spot on the southern corners of mykonos is Platis Gialos beach. It is the paradise of mykonos trip packages where the beachside hotels meet beachside fun. Aside from the sunbeds and water activities, what makes Platys Gialos one of the highlights of the mykonos vacation package is the array of Italian, Greek, and Mediterranean eateries. You can also catch water taxis added to other regions on the island to check out the equally best places to go in mykonos. Super paradise beach If you love to party and especially enjoy the vibe at paradise beach, super paradise beach is your haven. The beach bars and the loud music creates quite the atmosphere for a beach party especially if your mykonos trip packages are booked for the best time to travel to mykonos. Enjoy fresh seafood as well as indulge in water sporting events or simply choose to relax on the sunbeds under the parasols. There is even a club dedicated to Jackie o’s. Armenistis lighthouse This beautiful octagonal lighthouse sits touring in the armenistis area overlooking one of the neighbouring islands of Greece called Tinos and the vast water body that encompasses it all. you can enjoy an exquisite sunset while bathing in the historic symbolism of Mykonos’s very own maritime hub, especially if you take a trip during the best time to visit mykonos. Cine manto mykonos If you truly want to immerse yourself in the culture of Greece, the Montreal cinema is the epitome of traditional representation. Much like the olden days, an amphitheatre welcomes locals and tourists to enjoy cinematic works alongside workshops dedicated to mosaic handiwork. You can also partake in garden yoga or enjoy a meal at the cine manto restaurant overlooking the scenic views of an era past. Archaeological museum of mykonos One of the best things to see in Mykonos is the museum depicting the history of not only the island but of Greece in general. Built at the turn of the 18th century, the archaeological museum of mykonos welcomes tourists to the statues of Aphrodite and Hercules leading the way towards 6 exhibition rooms. The museum consists of various historical and cultural objects including the pithos jar from the 7th century bc and a number of exquisite potteries from the 1st to 25th-century bc. It is also home to several burns and grave statues from rheneia as well as several portraits representing events from the trojan war. Folklore museum One of the best things to see in Mykonos is the whitewashed 18th-century building with matching red brick windows transformed into a museum dedicated to Greek folklore. Unlike the archaeological museum, this establishment is dedicated to tools and pieces of furniture from years ago as well as pictographic representation of historical moments. It also displays the original cannons from the war of independence fought in 1821. Additionally, the folklore museum has dedicated displays of bedrooms, living rooms, and other infrastructure from the 19th century as utilised by the middle-class population. Rarity gallery Built in 1995, the rarity gallery is an exhibition house supporting artists with contemporary ideologies. It is an ancient manor house that has been converted into an art gallery displaying photographs, paintings, and sculptures curated by dedicated art levels to introduce a new way to the world. Ano mera If you ever wondered how the locals of mykonos originally started inhabiting the land and finding the centre, ano mera is one of the best places to see in mykonos. You can take a water taxi to this remote village away from the vibrancy of the city and experience Greek life for yourself. The entire village built around the 16th-century Panagia tourliani monastery is a testament to early architectural structures including a fountain made of marble. You can also visit the monastery to view the display of ancient relics from the Byzantine era and pick up a few souvenirs to take home when you remember your mykonos vacation packages. Panagia paraportiani Another spectacular building with whitewashed walls is the church located on the seaside of the Kastro neighbourhood. This historic site represents five churches that were built and rebuilt on top of one another since the 14th century. You can spot a lot of archaeological anomalies that make this church one of the most beautiful places in Mykonos for tourists as well as locals. Little Venice quarter A true representation of the olden times, the little Venice quarter was erected by sea merchants back in the 16th and 17th centuries. located along the chora at alexandra beach, this area proudly presents an amalgamation between nature and civilization. The eateries and residential properties located around the beach are so close to the Aegean sea that it is practically like sitting in a houseboat. It is among the most beautiful places in mykonos.
Mykonos.What to eat in Mykonos: The Greeks are fond of healthy living but who says you cannot do that with an exquisite spice palette? Greek cuisine, especially the food found in mykonos, is rich in nutrition due to the explicit use of seafood and the uncanny combination of ingredients to create unique dishes like no other. here are some of the foods that you must try during your mykonos vacation package when you check out the best things to see in mykonos: Amygdalota : A pear-shaped sweet baked with almond flavours and rose essence and consumed mostly during the festivals in mykonos. Lazarakia : A handmade biscuit consisting of raisins, sugar and cloves was made on Lazarus Saturday to commemorate Lazarus. Melopita : A crispy pie made of the tirovolia cheese mixed with honey and cinnamon and wrapped in two sheets of phyllo pastry before frying to a crisp. The fried pie is then sprinkled with black sesame seeds and honey for extra sweetness. Rafioli : It is sweet or savoury depending on the topping used, rafioli are fried pieces of dough filled with orange, tirovolia cheese, and honey and sprinkled with cinnamon. Some places serve it with onions and herbs on top for added flavour. Tiganites Gries : Speaking of sweets, tiganites gries is a 2600-year-old recipe that is very simple to make yet oozes with flavour. They are recorded as the earliest pancakes ever made by mixing flour with water and salt. These pancakes are fried and stacked before topping them with fresh fruits, cinnamon, honey, or powdered sugar. As time went by, tiganites gries became a traditional food for the locals of mykonos, regardless of their social and economic conditions. Kremidopita : My Favourite for the locals of Mykonos, especially around Easter, is kremidopita. It is a pie made of onions mixed with tirovolia cream cheese, dill, and other herbs and spices filled between two sheets of phyllo pastry baked to a crisp. Louza : Mykonos’s very own version of prosciutto is made annually around autumn. since it is the shoulder season and the best time to visit mykonos, you might actually get to try this dish from the local vendors as street food. Kopanisti with mostra rusk : One of the best food items that originated in mykonos is the kopanisti cheese. It is either prepared from pure sheep's milk or a mixture of goat and sheep’s milk. The pinkish-yellow colour and unique texture make it beyond delectable. It is best enjoyed with mostra rusk but you can also eat it plainly as an appetiser. Mostra : A delightful appetiser made by spreading kopanisti cheese over rusk and topped with chopped tomatoes or grapes, depending on availability. It is sprinkled with olive oil, capers and oregano for an enhanced taste. Most of these dishes are available at the shacks located by the various beaches of mykonos. Some of the traditional food items are available in remote places such as the villages around the main city or the monastery town of Ano mera.
Mykonos.Things to do in Mykonos: When planning your mykonos tour packages, plan your itinerary ahead to participate in the following things to do: Enjoy the largest nightclub and numerous restaurants on a paradise beach Partaking in watersports such as wave boarding, jet skiing, etc. on a super paradise beach Take a walking tour of the solitary beaches of agios sostis and the panoramas. Take a water taxi to ano mera Enjoy the exquisite greek cuisine Learn the historical significance of Greece at the archaeological and folklore museum. Delve into the culture of Greece at the rarity gallery. Explore the wonders of nature versus architecture at Delos, Panagia paraportiani, and the armenistis lighthouse. Enjoy a cinema with your fellow tourists and the locals at cine manto. Go shopping for souvenirs to commemorate your mykonos tour packages. Regardless of the activities in which you choose to partake, if you plan your trip during the best time of year to visit Mykonos, the scenery itself is enough to make your jaw drop. couple that with the exquisite greek cuisine, and all your senses go on holiday when you book your mykonos travel packages.
Mykonos.Forex currency exchange for Mykonos: As part of Europe, Mykonos, which is located in Greece, accepts euros. You can check out the forex currency exchange rates as well as guidelines as to where you can convert your Indian rupees into euros here.
Mykonos.Visa application for Mykonos tour: When planning for your mykonos travel packages, one of the primary steps is to apply for a Schengen visa. A tourist visa for Greece is valid for 90 days from the day of issuance. you need to apply for your visa anywhere between three months to 15 days before your mykonos vacation package is effective. The cost of visa application for Indians travelling to mykonos is rs. 4,680 and an additional rs. 1,560 as a service fees approx. You can fill out the visa application online and print a copy to carry along to the visa application centre near you. you will need to submit biometrics and a digital photograph will be clicked at the centre therefore you need to apply for your visa personally. Along with the printed visa application and biometrics, you will also need to provide a passport that is valid for the next three months and was issued within 10 years. you must also have medical insurance with a minimum coverage of rs. 23,60,970 approx. Medical insurance is mandatory for a Schengen visa to visit any country in the European Union. you will also need to submit an official bank statement reflecting your account for the previous three months. You also need to provide the details of your mykonos holiday packages including your flight reservations, accommodation, and an itinerary if it is already planned to include some of the rare and most beautiful places in mykonos.

faq.question: How many days are ideal for a Kashmir trip?
faq.answer: An ideal Kashmir trip should be around 7-10 days, if you're looking forward to a comprehensive experience. However, for a shorter trip of 4 to 5 days, you can experience only a few tourist places and interests. Depending on your Kashmir trip plan, you'll need to consider the experiences and locations you want to enjoy, as well as the pace of travel.

faq.question: Is Kashmir safe for tourists?
faq.answer: Yes, Kashmir is relatively safe for tourists. The Kashmir tourist packages planned are relatively safe, especially for covering destinations such as Pahalgam, Gulmarg and Srinagar. In the past few months, there have been significant improvements across infrastructure and security, especially for the near-zero crime rate for tourists. Kashmir has a very welcoming environment for tourists as well.

faq.question: What is the best time to visit Kashmir for snow?
faq.answer: Winter is the best time to visit Kashmir if you wish to experience snow. Therefore, you can book winter Kashmir holiday packages, between December and February, especially to experience snow. During winter, Kashmir receives maximum rainfall, and the valley will turn into a winter wonderland. January brings heavy snowfall in Kashmir, which is the coldest month.

faq.question: What kind of clothes should I carry?
faq.answer: You must carry warm clothes to visit Kashmir. Woollen, fleece, and thermals should be good. It is advisable to wear your clothes in layers. You will need thermal trousers, a windproof jacket, and comfortable shoes. If you're planning to visit any cultural place, make sure to carry a headscarf to respect local customs. Other essential accessories include sunglasses, gloves and woollen socks.

faq.question: Can I use my mobile phone in Kashmir?
faq.answer: Yes, you can use your mobile phone in Kashmir. However, if you have a prepaid SIM card from any other state in India, you must buy a local SIM card. This is done in Jammu and Kashmir for security reasons. Postpaid SIM cards from other states will work. It is advisable to purchase a local tourist SIM card. These are available at the railway stations, airport and other shops. The different mobile network providers in Jammu and Kashmir include Jio, Airtel and BSNL.

faq.question: What are the must-try foods in Kashmir?
faq.answer: Some of the must-try foods in Kashmir include slow-cooked lamb curry, Rogan Josh, Tabakh Maaz, Goshtaba and a yoghurt-based mutton or chicken curry, Yakhni. Apart from its delicious range of non-vegetarian dishes, Kashmir also has some of the best vegetarian dishes for everyone to try, such as Dum Aalo and Lyader Tschaman (cheese cooked in turmeric gravy). During cold winters, you may just try the traditional Kahwa.
