Jammu and Kashmir.Why Kashmir is the Jewel in India's Crow: When booking your Kashmir tour packages, be sure to list all the top places you'd like to see. Being the Crown Jewel of India, Kashmir is home to some of the most beautiful places on Earth. For this reason, it is often referred to as Heaven on Earth. In fact, there's a famous couplet, "If there is paradise on the face of the earth, it is here, it is here, it is here," truly describing the beauty of Kashmir. Upon visiting Kashmir with your Kashmir travel packages, you would get to see the snow-capped mountains, vibrant culture of Kashmir, beautiful flowing streams, relaxed pristine lakes and lush green meadows. It is truly the destination for nature lovers. But even adventure seekers can visit this destination and enjoy a variety of activities. Some of the top scenic places that make Kashmir the Crown Jewel of India include Pahalgam, Sonmarg, Gulmarg and the Dal Lake of Srinagar. Apart from being such a beauty, Kashmir is often referred to as the Crown Jewel of India because of its rich history and deep cultural roots. The land of Kashmiri Pandits and Kashmir Hindus, the age-old traditions, customs and heritage are preserved to date here. Kashmir is also popular for its handicrafts such as Pashmina shawls, papier-mache art and designed carpets. If you plan to get some souvenirs from your Kashmir trip, you should definitely consider buying these. Kashmir is also a popular destination among pilgrims and spiritual people because it is home to some of the most essential temples, Sufi shrines and mosques. The Amarnath Cave is one of the top pilgrimages for Hindus. This temple is dedicated to Lord Shiva and is open only for a specific period (due to weather and safety concerns) for the pilgrims to set out on the Amarnath Yatra. Other places of religious importance in Kashmir include the Shankaracharya Temple and Hazratbal Shrine.
Jammu and Kashmir.Planning Your Kashmir Trip: Essential In: Before booking your Jammu Kashmir tour package, it is extremely crucial to plan your trip. For this reason, you should check all important information, such as the Kashmir tour package price, the best time to visit, and more. Below is all the information that you'll need to have a fun and exciting trip to Kashmir.
Jammu and Kashmir.Best Time to Visit Kashmir: Summer is the best season to book your Kashmir tour package. This Heaven on Earth has the best to offer during Spring as well. However, winter and autumn may not be the most favourable times to visit Kashmir due to the extremely cold weather. Booking your Kashmir trip plan during the summer ensures that you can visit the tourist places and enjoy the fun activities. Therefore, you book the best Kashmir tour packages between March and late August. The temperature ranges between 15°C and 30°C during summer, offering a comfortable experience. Moreover, it's during this time that you can truly soak in the natural beauty of Kashmir, with its vast blue skies and lush green meadows, as part of your Kashmir travel packages. If you want to experience snow, you may plan your visit between October and March. During winters, Kashmir experiences heavy snowfall with the temperature ranging between 2°C and 11°C. The Kashmir trip cost during this time is slightly lower because of off-season. If you're lucky enough, you may get to see snow and witness the winter wonderland of Kashmir in Gulmarg, where you can enjoy different winter sports.
Jammu and Kashmir.How to Reach Kashmir?: Booked your Kashmir trip package? Here's how you can reach Kashmir via different modes: By Air: For this, you'll have to choose the Kashmir tour package with flight. Srinagar Airport (SXR) is the primary airport of Kashmir, India, which is well-connected to major cities of India such as Delhi, Kolkata, Bangalore and Mumbai. All the major airlines operate to Srinagar. The main airport is located around 12 km from the city centre. By Train: If you want an affordable Kashmir trip cost, travelling via train may be a convenient option. The closest railway station to Kashmir is Jammu Tawi, situated 290 km from Srinagar. Trains operate to Jammu Tawi from major destinations of India such as Chennai, Kolkata, Mumbai and Delhi. After reaching Jammu Tawi by train, you can either take a bus or hire a taxi to reach other parts of Kashmir. By Road: The Kashmir tour packages usually don't include road trips to Kashmir from other parts of India. However, if you're planning one, it surely can be an unforgettable experience. Taking your own car or hiring one should help you enjoy a road trip to Kashmir. There are three popular routes that you can take to reach Kashmir by road, via Delhi, Chandigarh and Amritsar. You can plan your Kashmir travel packages and choose the best road option that fits your needs.
Jammu and Kashmir.Safety & Travel Tips: Before booking Kashmir holiday packages, it is important to check the safety and travel tips. While Kashmir is relatively safe for tourists, it is important to monitor the security situation. Here are some of the top safety and travel tips to follow on your Kashmir trip: You should book your Kashmir travel packages with only approved operators. Avoid visiting restricted or remote areas as part of your trip. Considering the high security of the region, always carry digital copies of your essential documents. The network in Kashmir may be slow, which means UPI payments won't work. Therefore, it is advisable to carry cash. If you don't have a postpaid mobile connection, you can purchase a local prepaid SIM card in Kashmir. Stick to the popular tourist spots that are declared safe and avoid travelling at night. You should carry essential items, especially medicines, if you're planning to visit higher altitudes on your Kashmir package. Pack your clothes according to the weather, and wear clothes in layers as the temperature can significantly change in the mountains. Follow local news before finalising the Kashmir tour packages. After visiting, stay connected to your tour operator. Wear modest clothes and respect the local culture. If you want a budget-friendly Kashmir tour package price, book your trip during discounts or around winter.
Jammu and Kashmir.Permits and Regulations: You don't need any general Inner Line Permit (ILP) with your Kashmir tour packages for visiting major destinations such as Gulmarg, Sonmarg or Pahalgam. However, if you're visiting any border areas near the Line of Control (LoC), you may need special permits. Foreigners will also need special permits to visit some regions in Kashmir. As a tourist, you must carry your valid photo ID. International tourists must have a visa for India and a valid passport. Prepare a proper Kashmir trip plan, and depending on the regions you'll be travelling to, apply for Border Areas Permits or Foreign Tourist Permits.
Jammu and Kashmir.Curated Kashmir Tour Packages by Thomas: Ready to explore the heavenly beauty of Kashmir? Let's discuss some of the best Jammu and Kashmir tour packages to choose from by Thomas Cook. Introduction to Packages Thomas Cook brings a wide range of Kashmir tour packages for everyone. You can book any of the Kashmir travel packages wherein you get to enjoy, relax, or set out on an adventure. Some of the top Kashmir tour packages to choose from by Thomas Cook are as follows: Diverse Categories of Kashmir Packages Kashmir has something to offer everyone. Therefore, the Kashmir tour packages are designed accordingly. Below are some of the popular Kashmir trip package categories to choose from are as follows: Honeymoon: The honeymoon Kashmir tour packages are designed for newlywed couples. It is ideal for couples who wish to witness the beauty of snow-clad mountains and cosy stays in shikara rides. Family packages: The family Kashmir holiday packages usually cover the major destinations. You can select the duration according to your preference, allowing you to discover hidden gems. Adventure packages: The adventure Kashmir travel packages are designed for thrill seekers. From trekking to rafting, you can explore the adventurous side of Kashmir. Whether you are a solo traveller or travelling with your friends, you can book these activities. Spiritual packages: The spiritual packages for Kashmir are designed for pilgrims who want to visit the mosques or temples. Kashmir has a rich cultural past and religious history. Therefore, you can book these Kashmir tour packages and explore these spiritual places.
Jammu and Kashmir.Top Attractions & Experiences in Kashmir: The Kashmir holiday packages take you across a wide range of attractions and experiences that you won't ever forget. Some of the top attractions that you can explore on your Kashmir tour packages include the following: Gulmarg Gulmarg is one of the top destinations that you must visit during your Kashmir tour packages. Gulmarg's gondola is the second-longest and second-highest cable car globally. A visit to the Apharwat Mountain on the gondola ride handles around 600 people per hour. Most of the winter sports activities are around this place. Srinagar Srinagar is the capital of Kashmir, which is a crucial part of Kashmir travel packages. Upon your visit to Kashmir, you'll explore the historic gardens, vibrant culture and Dal Lake. Do not visit the Mughal Gardens. The backdrop of the Zabarwan Mountains offers the perfect blend of nature and cultural visuals. You must also visit Shankaracharya Temple and Pari Mahal. Sonmarg Often referred to as the Meadow of Golden Light, Sonmarg is a popular destination that you must not miss out on. Most Kashmir tour packages consider Sonmarg as a major hill station, taking you across beautiful landscapes. The scenic beauty, green meadows, lovely streams and majestic mountains of Sonmarg are perfect for trekking. Adventure lovers can also try various activities such as river rafting, hiking, skiing and more. Betaab Valley Betaab Valley offers a perfect view of the free-flowing Lidder River. This place received its name from the Bollywood movie Betaab. This valley is located around 15km away from Pahalgam, in the Anantnag district of Jammu and Kashmir. Dachigam National Park Dachigam National Park is a protected area spread across an area of 141 square kilometres. This national park houses the endangered Hangul deer or Kashmir stag. Apart from this, you can also witness the local wildlife of the region, such as Himalayan black bears, leopards and other bird species. Kashmir Valley Kashmir Valley is one of the largest Himalayan Valleys in the shape of an elliptical saucer. It is surrounded by the Pir Panjal and Karakoram ranges. The Kashmir Valley offers a mesmerising view of the surrounding region. The valley stretches across 140kms and is one of the top places to visit for adventure seekers and mountaineers. You can book adventure Kashmir tour packages to experience this beauty. Gurez Valley Situated at an altitude of 2,400m above sea level, Gurez Valley is surrounded by snow-capped mountains. This valley offers an impeccable view of the natural beauty of Kashmir. It is known for offering a serene and peaceful environment. Upon your visit to the Gurez Valley, you can explore the lakes and dense forests. Yusmarg If you wish to explore lush green meadows as part of your Kashmir tour packages, you must explore Yusmarg. This beautiful valley is located in the Budgam district and often referred to as the Meadow of Jesus. The grassy pastures here have sheep, offering a stunning view of the landscape. You can also enjoy a pony ride. You will see nomads grazing their cattle at Tosa Maidan. Doodhpatri This underrated gem is something you must miss out on. Yet another beautiful destination in the Budgam district, Doodhpatri is located 43km from the main city of Srinagar. Apart from green meadows, the area is dotted with creeks and waterfalls. It is a true natural retreat for all the tourists.
Jammu and Kashmir.Things to Do in Kashmir Beyond Sightseei: From sightseeing to adventure activities, Kashmir tour packages offer you a wide range of experiences. Here are some of the top adventure activities you may indulge in upon your visit to Kashmir: Skiing in Gulmarg If you're a lover of winter sports, you must definitely book your Kashmir travel packages during winter. Visit Gulmarg to witness the beauty of a winter wonderland and enjoy the thrill of skiing. The snow-capped mountains are your grand stage where you make your descent along the snowy slopes. Shikara Ride in Dal Lake A shikara ride in Dal Lake is one of the must-try activities on your Kashmir tour packages. It feels very serene, peaceful and calm. Setting out on a shikara ride during the evening will offer a quiet atmosphere. The lavishly decorated shikaras float all around the Dal Lake offering a beautiful view of the snowy mountains. Enjoy a stay in the boathouse Do not miss out on the experience of staying in a boathouse. This is one of the best activities you can do in Kashmir. The houseboat stay offers a very dreamy experience, especially during the evening when the sun sets. Enjoy a kahwa on the boathouse and soak in the rich flavours of delicious Kashmiri cuisine. Take a Gondola Ride in Gulmarg Apart from skiing, you should also enjoy a gondola ride in Gulmarg. Book one of the Jammu and Kashmir tour packages that includes this activity as well. Step into the winter wonderland on your aerial chariot. As the gondola takes you to a height, you watch the true beauty of Kashmir unfold. The higher you go, the better the view of Gulmarg you get. Go for Boating at Mansar Lake On your Jammu Kashmir tour package, you must try boating on Mansar Lake. This lake offers an emerald view of Kashmir. As you said, across this aquatic haven, you'll get to learn about the ancient myths and legends of the place. Birds flying across the blue skies create a divine and peaceful view. Go for a Pony Ride in Pahalgam Although a very simple activity, a pony ride in Pahalgam is something you must not miss out on. As a part of your Jammu tour packages, this iconic experience is one of the best ones to indulge in. Along the way, make sure to enjoy the scenic charm of the surrounding region and witness the simple, yet innocent joy. The tour guide may take you across the Lidder River on your ride, explaining to you about Himalayan folklore. River Rafting One of the most thrilling activities to enjoy in Kashmir as a part of one of your Kashmir tour packages is river rafting. The activity is usually conducted around the Zanskar and Lidder rivers. You can raft and flow along the raging rhythm, enjoying the surrounding beauty. Trekking There are numerous trekking spots in Kashmir that you must visit. The Tarsar Marsar trek is one of the most popular ones that you can indulge in. Along the way, you'll witness the beauty of mighty shadows and quaint hamlets, especially the raw beauty of Kashmir. For trying these thrilling activities, you'll have to book adventurous Kashmir travel packages.
Jammu and Kashmir.Essential Tips for a Smooth Kashmir Trip: Once you've booked the Kashmir trip package, you will have to start planning early. This is to ensure that you don't miss out on anything during your trip. Here are some of the key tips to follow: Packing checklist: Irrespective of the season you've booked the Kashmir tour packages for, it is crucial to carry warm clothes. You should carry comfortable shoes, thermal warmers (because nights can be cold during summers too), sunglasses and caps. Light woollen clothes and jackets are a must. Connectivity: Prepaid SIM cards from other Indian states are not valid in Kashmir. Therefore, you may carry a postpaid SIM card. If not, you may purchase a local prepaid SIM card upon reaching Kashmir to stay connected. Mobile network coverage may not be strong or non-existent in high-altitude areas. Health and Safety: Always carry your personal medicines while on your Jammu and Kashmir tour packages. You should check the weather conditions in Kashmir and pack your clothes accordingly. After all, you wouldn't want to fall sick on your trip. Respect Local Culture: Kashmir is a primarily conservative region. Therefore, it is important to dress modestly. You should also respect the local customs to prevent any unwanted attention. Before entering any mosque, temple or other religious places, open your shoes outside the premises. Ask for permission before taking photographs. Responsible Tourism: Kashmir is one of the most beautiful destinations in India. Therefore, you wouldn't want to spoil the beauty by littering around. It is advisable to use eco-friendly materials and avoid plastic water bottles. If you're looking for a more affordable Kashmir tour package, consider staying in local homestays. As a part of boosting responsible tourism, support the local economy. Save all Emergency Contacts: You must stay informed about the security situation in Kashmir. For this reason, you'll need to save all emergency contacts, in case you need them. For that purpose, you'll have to save contacts for ambulances, tourist helplines, police and fire. If your Kashmir tour packages coincide with any political events, it is advisable to consult the experts. It is extremely important to book a Kashmir trip package after considering all the options. You must stay updated about all the security advisories and plan your trip responsibly. Following these tips mentioned above should help you enjoy your Kashmir package with complete peace of mind.
Jammu and Kashmir.Why Book a Kashmir Tour with Thomas Cook: Thomas Cook provides a comprehensive experience for all their Kashmir travel packages. They have a well-planned Kashmir package that helps you enjoy a smooth and comfortable experience. Booking the best Kashmir trip package ensures that you get to explore all the major tourist places and enjoy the heavenly beauty of Kashmir. With Thomas Cook, you wouldn't have to worry about anything on your trip. They will take care of most of the things for your Kashmir tour packages, ranging from local transport to guided tours and even accommodation. Therefore, all you need to do is pack your bags and visit Kashmir with Thomas Cook. Whether you're seeking adventure or are going on a honeymoon with your spouse, Kashmir is the best place to be. Thus, they work towards crafting the best experience for everyone on the Kashmir travel packages. Considering the availability of so many Kashmir tour packages at Thomas Cook, you can choose one that fits your needs the most. If required, you can also connect with the team to customise the package as per your requirements. Therefore, the Kashmir package will be tailored to your travel budget and preferences. If you want to explore Jammu too during your trip, you may book the detailed Jammu Kashmir package from Thomas Cook. The expert team will assist you in the process by providing details on the ideal time to travel to Europe, as well as necessary information on places to visit, adventurous experiences to try, and more. By choosing Thomas Cook as your booking partner for Kashmir holiday packages, you're choosing relaxation. As they take care of everything about your tour plan, you just need to bring your peace of mind so that you can relax and have a peaceful vacation.

faq.question: When is the best time to visit Cape Town for whale watching?
faq.answer: The period of June to November is considered the best time to visit Cape Town for whale watching.

faq.question: What are some nice places to go in Cape Town for taking pictures?
faq.answer: Some nice places to visit in Cape Town for photography are Table Mountain, Boulders Beach, and Robben Island.

faq.question: What is the currency of Cape Town?
faq.answer: The currency used in Cape Town is called South African Rand. You can check out Thomas Cook for foreign exchange services at competitive rates.

faq.question: What is the best transport option for getting around Cape Town?
faq.answer: The best transport option for getting around Cape Town safely and comfortably is via taxi or rental cabs.

faq.question: Is Cape Town a good holiday destination?
faq.answer: Yes, it is one of the most popular holiday destinations in South Africa. You can get great Cape Town honeymoon packages, family or group tour plans, and much more with Thomas Cook.

Cape Town.Introduction: An international holiday can satisfy your inner travel geek and offer you a great perspective on what the rest of the world has to offer. If you are interested in witnessing the marvellous scenic and cultural glory of South Africa, Cape Town is a must-visit destination. We at Thomas Cook offer some of the best Cape Town tour packages you can pick for a dream travel experience. You can choose from our multiple professionally-picked trip plans, with a starting price of INR 1,60,792 to INR 3,79,451 per adult. In addition, you can get both group tours and “customised holiday” options with our international Cape Town travel packages, alongside Premium, Standard and Deluxe tour type choices.&nbsp Cape Town packages by Thomas Cook take care of all the nitty-gritty of your travel, like transport, accommodation, meals, sightseeing, etc., so you can enjoy a smooth sailing vacation without worrying about the planning aspect. If you are travelling from the major cities of India, like Mumbai, Delhi, Bangalore, Kolkata, etc., you can set it as your departure city on our website and get refined results on the best Cape Town tour packages available to you. South Africa’s Cape Town is a major part of the country’s tourism sector. You can check our handy Cape Town tour guide for a swift overview.
Cape Town.Table of Content: Most Popular Cape Town Tour Packages Holidays in Cape Town How to reach Cape Town Travelling within Cape Town Best time to visit Cape Town Cape Town COVID-19 tourist guide Best Places to Visit in Cape Town What to eat in Cape Town Things to do in Cape Town People also ask about Cape Town Tour Packages
Cape Town.Most Popular Cape Town Tour Packages: If you want to witness the wonders of Cape Town solo or with friends and family in an all-inclusive trip, check out Thomas Cook’s most popular Cape Town tour packages: Tour Packages NameDurationPriceSouth African Extravaganza With Suncity - Winter9 Nights 10 DaysRs. ₹276 600South African Trio - Winter11 Nights 12 DaysRs. ₹459 300Classic Cape Town7 Nights 8 DaysRs. ₹113 500Cape To Coast Escape (Self Drive)9 Nights 10 DaysRs. ₹129 900Splendid South Africa - Private Journeys6 Nights 7 DaysRs. ₹149 300
Cape Town.Holidays in Cape Town: The magnificent Cape Town houses the legislative branch of the South African government. It is also one of the three capital cities of the country. Cape Town is the oldest city in South Africa, locally referred to as the “Mother City”, with its origin dating back to 1652. It is among the most popular tourist destinations in South Africa, known for yielding some of the best scenic visuals of nature and its landscapes. Not only it features the most mesmerising mountains of the region but also vineyards, beaches, gushing ocean water and much more. Besides its natural beauty, the rich local culture and more than 300 years old historical heritage also draw significant tourist attention and traffic throughout the year. It makes up for a fantastic holiday getaway with friends, family or even solo. You can include this gorgeous city as a part of your whole South Africa trip or take a dedicated short Cape Town vacation for a wonderful experience. If you are looking to spend some quality time with your significant other, Cape Town stands to be a great couple’s holiday destination. It features tons of adventurous activities, popular attractions, exuberant nightlife, fantastic accommodations, and much more. No matter the criteria, you can always find Cape Town holiday packages that suit your needs at Thomas Cook India.
Cape Town.How to reach Cape Town: As one of the major hubs for tourism in South Africa, Cape Town offers great connectivity within the country and other parts of the world. Here are some best ways to reach Cape Town that you can consider for your trip: Reaching Cape Town by air Reaching Cape Town by train Reaching Cape Town by road Reaching Cape Town by air Going on an aeroplane would be the best option for international travellers who are planning their Cape Town trip directly from their home country. Reaching Cape Town by air is the fastest and the most comfortable way to get to this trip destination. For instance, if you are travelling from India, you can find frequent flights from Mumbai, Bangalore and Delhi to Cape Town International Airport from reputable airlines. You can also take a flight to South Africa’s Johannesburg and then board a Cape Town-bound plane if it suits your trip plan. You can easily check flight availability and book plane tickets from here. Reaching Cape Town by train If you are travelling to Cape Town from major cities in South Africa, you can opt for a train journey. The primary railway station in Cape Town offers great connectivity with Pretoria and Johannesburg with the availability of daily trains. Reaching Cape Town by road If you are in for a memorable road trip, there are various ways to reach Cape Town via road from other parts of South Africa. For instance, if you are beginning your journey from Johannesburg, you can take the N1 national route to reach Cape Town. This road also connects Cape Town with Pretoria, Bloemfontein and Zimbabwe.
Cape Town.Travelling within Cape Town: Once you reach Cape Town, you can enjoy the beauty and cultural delight of the place at a pace that suits you best. While travelling within a holiday destination can be exciting, it is important to learn about the best possible routes and transport options so that you can enjoy the views without wasting significant time on the wheels. Here’s how you can get around in Cape Town: By Metrorail By local bus By taxi/rental cabs Other ways By Metrorail Metrorail is one of the key transport options available within Cape Town. They can cover great distances in less time and make your travel easy. However, most sources don’t recommend it as an ideal intra city transport option for tourists for various reasons. By local bus The ideal choice of commute within Cape Town for tourists is via bus. You can find both local, private and minibuses running across the city, serving as a shuttle for visitors who want to reach their desired location. By taxi/rental cabs Taxis and rental cabs are the best and most comfortable transport options you can pick to get around in Cape Town. They are also quite cheap, reliable and easily available across the city. Other ways Besides public transport, you can cover short distances within Cape Town by cycling on a rental bike or taking a lovely walk through the beautiful city streets.
Cape Town.Best time to visit Cape Town: Going on an international holiday is a big deal and requires detailed planning to ensure a memorable trip. You can get different travel experiences when backpacking in Cape Town, depending on the time of your visit. Generally, the month of January to April is considered the best time to visit Cape Town. It is the period of summer in the region which provides dry and sunny weather ideal for enjoying Cape Town’s beach experience. In addition, visiting Cape Town during this period is best for enjoying picturesque views, water sports and several land adventure activities. As January-February is the peak season, you can take a trip during March-April to experience comparatively lower crowds at Cape Town attractions and prices of the shoulder season. During this period, you will be able to witness famous local events like Cape Minstrel Carnival in January or Cape Town Carnival and Cape Town International Jazz Festival in March. For a sensational beach holiday in Cape Town during summer, check out this tour package. Besides this, many individuals consider the June-September period as the best time to travel to Cape Town for its whale-watching experience. It also hosts the Hermanus Whale Festival in late September. And if you want to witness the well-renowned regional wildflowers, you can visit Cape Town during the September-December period. You can find Cape Town vacation packages that fit your preferred time of visit with ease.
Cape Town.Cape Town COVID-19 tourist guide: Due to the recent COVID-19 outbreak, there are certain travel guidelines and protocols that are needed to be followed by all domestic and international tourists. This also applies to tourism in Cape Town and other South African countries. For a detailed Cape Town COVID-19 tourist guide, you can refer to our South Africa travel guidelines page. Alongside this, make sure to follow the general measures to ensure your safety. This includes maintaining personal hygiene, wearing a mask in public, keeping a safe distance, and so on. If you want to enjoy the famous tourist attractions of this city, now is the best time for a visit as all Cape Town sightseeing locations are allowing visitors.
Cape Town.Best Places to Visit in Cape Town: The fine coastline, sky-high landforms, and local heritage make up some of the greatest tourist attractions of Cape Town. During your next visit, check out these best places to visit in Cape Town: Cape Point Castle of Good Hope Victoria and Alfred Waterfront Kirstenbosch Botanical Garden Table Mountain Boulders Beach Robben Island Cape Point Cape Point ranks among the most popular places to visit in Cape Town. It is the go-to spot for visitors who are searching for the signature South African ocean-side view. Cape Point is surrounded by lush green valleys and hills, distinct local flora, beautiful ocean waters, mesmerising cliffs, and much more. In addition, you can also witness the diverse wildlife at this location, which is home to over 250 species of birds and different animals. It serves as a great spot for trekking, spending some romantic time, dining, and so on. It is also one of the best places to visit in Cape Town for couples. Castle of Good Hope The Castle of Good Hope in Cape Town is South Africa’s oldest surviving building. Built back in 1666 by the Dutch East India Company for the purpose of refilling ship supplies, the Castle of Good Hope now welcomes visitors who want to delve into the rich history and local heritage of the region. It is one of the best places to see in Cape Town for a historical deep dive. Victoria and Alfred Waterfront The Victoria and Alfred Waterfront is a location popular among both locals and travellers. You can find an array of pubs, quality restaurants, bars, and much more at this place, making it an ideal hub for experiencing the local nightlife. Some other famous things you can check out here are the shopping markets, art galleries, aquariums, entertainment shows, etc. It is one of the best tourist places in Cape Town. Kirstenbosch Botanical Garden The Kirstenbosch Botanical Garden is among the most famous places to visit in Cape Town. It was founded back in 1913 with the objective to preserve and showcase the vibrant South African regional flora. Besides witnessing the exuberant variety of local flora, you can choose this spot as a starting point for your hiking trail to Table Mountain. The visit time for this botanical garden is from 8 AM to 6 PM, and there’s an entry fee for both adults and children. Table Mountain The flat table mountain is among the major in-land Cape Town attractions for tourists. It is a must-visit site if you want to experience the spectacular views of Cape Town and its surrounding locations. This spot is ideal for individuals who want to capture the beauty of the region through their camera lens. In addition, nature lovers will also find Table Mountain interesting as it is home to various species of local plants and animals. You may take a hike or opt for a cable car ride to reach the Table Mountain top, which has an elevation of 1086 metres. For ideal holiday accommodation in Cape Town hotels, you can consider booking a villa in this part of the city. Boulders Beach When visiting Cape Town, make sure to put your waypoint at Boulders Beach for a unique experience. Alongside offering an eye-catching beach view, this location is the primary site for penguin watching. It is the nesting ground for over 2000 African Penguins, offering adorable views as they walk on the sand with their tiny feet. It is one of the major Cape Town tourist attractions. Other than penguin watching, Boulders Beach in Cape Town is also a good location for photography and swimming. If you visit this spot at a time of low tourist traffic, you will have a better chance of seeing large groups of penguins. Robben Island One of the most important places to visit in Cape Town during your tour. Apart from being a site of major historical importance in the region, it is also the place where Nelson Mandela spent 18 years of his life under imprisonment. Today, it is a UNESCO World Heritage Site, has a museum, and features more than hundreds of bird species.
Cape Town.What to eat in Cape Town: Besides visiting the famous places in Cape Town, your trip will remain incomplete without tasting the wide range of local cuisines. Here are some tasty must-try delights that you have to check in Cape Town: Bunny chow- As one of the most popular dishes offered in Cape Town, Bunny Chow is a must-try food item for visitors. It is made with a hollowed-out piece of white bread filled with rich curry. This tasty dish is usually served alongside salad and makes up for a good medium meal. Braai- This is the regional version of a backyard barbecue. Depending upon where you try it in Cape Town, you will find an array of food options that fall under the braai criteria. The list of must-try braais includes lamb chops, steak, pork chops, spice sausage, etc. Gatsby- The true essence of a delectable Cape Town delight comes in the form of Gatsby. It is a variant of a submarine sandwich stuffed with juicy meat, sauces, and chips. As you have expected, this flavourful item is largely available as street food at local shops and eateries. Sushi- Even though Sushi is not a traditional dish of the region, it is a must-try food item in Cape Town. The wonderful coast has made the region an exceptional place for trying seafood. You can find Cape Town’s variant of delicious sushi at various high-end restaurants across the city.
Cape Town.Things to do in Cape Town: Here are some exciting and adventurous things you can do during your Cape Town tour: Surfing at Muizenberg- The ocean surrounding the coast of Cape Town is an ideal place for tourists who want to experience water adventures. For example, you can surf the waves or learn the art of surfing at Muizenberg. It is a great location for beginner surfers because the waves there are perfect for learning. Penguin watching at Boulders Beach- Besides soaking in the beauty of natural landscapes, Cape Town is a great destination for wildlife watching. You can take a quick trip to Boulders Beach and see hundreds of African Penguins. It is one of the most iconic things to see in Cape town. Paragliding from Lion’s Head- Cape Town is filled with plenty of adventure activities that you can try out. For instance, make sure to try the paragliding experience from Lion’s Head to capture some of the best aerial views of Cape Town. Hiking on the Table Mountain trail- For a more relaxed and calming adventure, take on the hiking trail to Table Mountain solo or with a group and enjoy the scenic views from the summit. It is among the most popular places to visit in Cape Town. Exploring the nightlife at V&A Waterfront- When in Cape Town, make sure to catch a glimpse of the lively local nightlife at Victoria and Alfred Waterfront.
Cape Town.Forex/Currency for Cape Town: The South African Rand is the currency used in Cape Town, and you will require it to pay for your expenses during your Cape Town holidays. The best way to get foreign currency for your travel destination is to get it secured before your trip starts in order to avoid any inconveniences. We at Thomas Cook offer foreign exchange services to ensure your journey remains smooth sailing. You can easily opt for these services from the comfort of your home and buy forex at a competitive exchange rate for any currency, including South African Rand. It is a simple four-step process that allows popular payment methods, including net banking. You can either pick up your purchased forex from a local Thomas Cook branch or have it delivered to your doorstep.
Cape Town.Visa for Cape Town: If your country doesn’t have a visa-on-arrival policy with South Africa, you will need to apply for a tourist visa prior to your Cape Town tour. For instance, Indians will need to apply for a visa to visit any South African country, be that for travel, business, or other purposes. The validity of a SA tourist visa is 90 days, which is sufficient for your tour needs. As South Africa allows eVisa, passport holders of these 14 eligible countries can skip long queues and save a lot of time procuring their visa for the Cape Town tour. You can check out our quick guide on how to apply for a visa online in 4 simple steps.

Thiruvananthapuram.Introduction: Get ready to escape to wonders of Thiruvananthapuram with our Thiruvananthapuram packages. The scenic and spell-bound beauty of Thiruvananthapuram will keep you in awe all through your trip. From short stays at Thiruvananthapuram to long stay, we've everything covered for Thiruvananthapuram, including your health and safety measures with Thomas Cook Assured Program, in our Thiruvananthapuram Tour Packages. Just pick from the below list of Thiruvananthapuram package and let us do the rest for you. Nagpur.Introduction: The city of Nagpur came to existence in 18th Century by the leader of Gond Dynasty named Bakht Buland Shah. Over the time, multiple dynasties captured and modelled the art and architecture of the place in their own style. The city was plunder twice and razed to the ground. However, despite the attacks and wars, development of Nagpur continued. In 1817, The British annexed the city from the Marathas. Nagpur city is very fascinating and diverse in terms of travel and cultural experience. Food lovers and shopping enthusiasts would love to come here to enjoy the street side attractions and various famous restaurants. Apart from sightseeing attractions, one can also laze around picnic spots like Waki Woods, Adasa and Ramtek. The city takes pride in the history that spans back to 5000 years. There are evidences of life existing during megalithic era. The first written inscription about the settlement of Nagpur comes from a 10th century copper plate found in Wardha district. According to another inscription found in Ramtek, Nagpur and its surrounding regions formed the part of the thickly wooded country called Jhadimandala under Yadavas of Devagiri.

Portugal.Introduction: In the aftermath of the COVID-19 pandemic, it is natural that everyone would be looking forward to an exciting, fun vacation to a great exotic destination. The European nation of Portugal is one such place that is a must-visit. Portugal offers many beautiful locales that are bound to take your breath away.Thomas Cook offers a variety of holidays in Portugal. Whether it is a trip for your family (Portugal family holidays), a private holiday for a couple, or a solo vacation, Thomas Cook has many attractive options for Portugal holidays. Brace yourself for quaint towns, jaw-dropping coastlines and delicious cuisine that you can savour from your Portugal travel packages and Portugal vacation packages!
Portugal.PORTUGAL TRAVEL RESTRICTIONS FOR COVID: A few months ago, in December 2021, Portugal entered a state of calamity due to an increase in COVID-19 cases and the advent of the Omicron variant. However, Portugal is open for tourism, subject to certain restrictions as per the recent Portugal travel guidelines meaning that it is possible to plan your own Portugal holidays in 2022!
Portugal.HOLIDAYS IN PORTUGAL: A trip to Europe will not be complete without a holiday in Portugal. The Iberian nation has tons of options. From the iconic cities of Lisbon and Porto, river cruises , UNESCO heritage sites and beach destinations across the country, Portugal has almost everything. Situated between the Atlantic Ocean and Spain, Portugal's diverse culture, topography, and cuisines are in stark contrast to its small size. Blessed with a beautiful coastline and mountainous regions, Portugal is one of the oldest countries in the world and has a rich and varied history and heritage.Go to the Thomas Cook website to plan your own Portugal holidays with Portugal vacation packages! Numerous things ought to be on your essential to-do list while being in the nation of Portugal. From the hidden cave beaches in Algarve, canoeing in Furnas Lake, spotting flora and fauna at the Peneda-Geres National Park and dolphin sighting at the Sado Estuary, to savouring egg tarts and other such indigenous sweet indulgences, wine tasting in Porto, nightlife and heritage walks in Lisbon and enjoying the beautiful city of Sintra, Portugal truly has unlimited options.Thomas Cook offers Portugal holiday packages, including Portugal family holidays from many Indian cities like New Delhi, Mumbai, Bangalore, Chennai, Kolkata, Ahmedabad, Hyderabad etc.
Portugal.Most Popular Portugal Tour Packages: Portugal Holiday Packages No. of Days Price* Highlights of Spain and Portugal 9 Nights/ 10 Days Rs. 1 96 473 Head to Thomas Cook and choose your ideal Portugal vacation now from an assortment of Portugal travel packages! Book your holidays to Portugal. Explore more about Europe tour packages. Shopping in Portugal Traditional shops and markets are where people frequent their shopping needs during their Portugal vacation. Rates are mostly fixed in shops, even though negotiation is accepted to an extent while going for large souvenirs or buying stuff in bulk quantities. Mercado municipal or municipal market is your cliched go-to place for groceries like vegetables, fruits, bread, fish and meat. There are flea markets that will offer a variety of options at low prices. Supermarkets and malls are more common in larger cities like Lisbon and Porto , and they have multitudes of stores, cafes, restaurants, cinema halls and gaming arcades. Portugal is a sought-after destination for its ceramics, carpets, rugs, blankets and tapestries. It is also famous for its wine options, like port wine and table wine. The country also boasts of options like olive oil, cheese, ham, honey, herbs, teas etc. Clothes, bags, shoes, and other apparel are also available for the average or luxury buyer.Head to Thomas Cook soon to check out your tour packages to Portugal. Best Time to Visit Portugal The best time to visit Portugal is the spring season, i.e. March to May, right after the winter. Another good time to visit the country is autumn, sometime between September and October when the climate is warm. Summers can be scorching hot in Portugal. However, many tourists flock to the country's coastal towns to bathe in the sun and enjoy the warmer waters. Hence, the Portuguese summer will be a good time to indulge in water sports. Even winters have their fair share of sunny weather, and because of this, exploring the cities and inland areas of Portugal will not be an unpleasant experience whatsoever. Based on your preferences, you can choose the Portugal travel packages that suit you best. There are abundant Portugal tour packages to fit all of your needs. How to Reach Portugal By AirDefinitely the fastest way of travel, there are many options from India for an air travel trip to Portugal. Portugal has as many as five international airports that are fairly busy with year-round flight schedules from different locations: The Lisbon Portela Airport, Porto Francisco Sa Carneiro Airport, Faro Airport, Madeira Airport and the Joao Paulo II Airport in Ponta Delgada. Indians can travel to Portugal via flights from New Delhi, Mumbai, Bangalore, Chennai and Panjim. All these places have one-stop flights to Portuguese cities. However, there are no direct flights, and the duration to reach Portugal from India will be a bit more than 9 hours. Book your Portugal holiday travel fast!By TrainPortugal is fairly well-connected by rail from many European cities. Travellers can use the Eurail network to visit Portugal.By RoadRoad travel to Portugal will be challenging because it is covered by water from three sides. It shares its land border only with Spain, and hence, interested passengers will need to go to Spain first to undertake a road trip to Portugal.By SeaIt is a pity that there are no cruises between India and Portugal. However, if and when one is ready to travel to another country, Portugal will have cruise options. It has a beautiful coastal area along the Atlantic Ocean.
Portugal.Places to Visit in Portugal: Below are the best places to visit in Portugal: ● Lisbon ● Porto ● Braga ● Sintra ● Algarve ● Madeira ● Albufeira ● Lagos Lisbon : Be it to indulge in the sweetness of 'pasteis de nata', the delectable seafood or the beautiful mosaic walkways, Lisbon offers it all. Portugal's capital city is one of the calmest and most affordable tourist destinations in Europe. Tourists go on sightseeing tours that are in plenty throughout the day. The nighttime offers a lot of live music and restaurant options or pub crawls. Porto : Porto is the second-largest city in Portugal, and like Lisbon, it is situated next to a river and offers many attractive sightseeing options. The Sao Bento possesses many places with walls adorned with pretty azulejo ceramic tiles. Gardens, historic palaces and churches are aplenty in Porto. Porto has arguably one of the best food options in Portugal, and even the most basic stuff is incredibly delicious. As the name suggests, Porto is home to a port as well.Head to Thomas Cook to get the best tour packages for Portugal.Braga : The fourth biggest city in Portugal is a symbol of ancient and modern European culture and goes by the name of Braga. Often labelled as a youth capital, Braga is home to many students. The place is full of cafes, shops, restaurants and bars, making it really upbeat and vibrant. Those interested in exploring spiritual options can visit the Unique Cathedral and the Sanctuary of Bom Jesus do Monte.Sintra : Situated in the foothills of the mountain range that gave its name, Sintra is a paradise for tourists. With its hard-to-ignore coloured walls that are almost in-your-face, castles and gardens, the place is a favourite destination for children. It is only 30 kilometres away from Lisbon and is a day trip option from the capital. Sintra is famous for the 19th century Pena Palace and the old Castle of the Moors and has a reputation as a place straight out of a Disney colouring book.Algarve : As one of the most picturesque locations in Europe, Algarve is a potential option for hiking and having a relaxing beach outing. The country's southernmost region is home to many settlements, golf courses, and huge resorts.The warm weather allows you to escape towards a visit to fishing villages and coves where you will be greeted by pristine waters adorned with cliffs. Almost everyone speaks English, and it is possible to discover calmer and less crowded places as you go into Sagres, Tavira and Aljezur.Madeira : Madeira has a distinct place in Portuguese culture. The sub-tropical island is situated next to the Atlantic Ocean, more than 1000 kilometres away from the mainland. Madeira used to be home to Christopher Colombus and is also famed for being the native place of football icon Cristiano Ronaldo. The footballer's own museum in Madeira is one of the most popular places, along with the Colegio church, the Quinta das Cruzes mansion and the Lavradores market. The capital of Madeira, Funchal, is also known to be a party destination.Albufeira : Albufeira is a crowd-favourite destination in Portugal. Blessed with diverse topography and terrain that offers coves, cliffs and sandy beaches, Albufeira is known for its beaches and golf courses, where tourists throng during the summertime. However, the village becomes less crowded during the spring and autumn seasons and is a perfect spot for a relaxing holiday. Lagos : Unlike some of the places in Algarve, Lagos has kept its age-old charm intact and is a sought-after destination. Lagos is full of mediaeval townhouses and churches famed for their Baroque architecture. The many cafes, bars and restaurants give a quaint touch to Lagos and are loved by locals and tourists alike. Book your holidays to Portugal now! Best Hill Stations : There is more to Portugal than bustling cities and nightlife. Many hill stations in Portugal have historic and ancient castles full of their own heritage and culture.Favaios : Favaios goes back to the Roman era and is known for its fruity port wine and four-cornered bread. You go close to 10 kilometres up into the hills from the town of Pinhao near the Douro River, enjoy the terraced vineyards and scenery to reach Favaios.Trancoso : Trancoso is situated in the central part of Portugal and boasts of rich history. Jews used to live in this hill town, starting from ancient times. In the 1500s, many engraved crosses on their houses to show their faith. You might find a few of these markings even now.Marvao : Marvao is a quaint little village with whitewashed houses situated on rocky terrain. The place is not at all crowded and has one of Portugal's best-preserved mediaeval castles. It is not too far away from the capital city of Lisbon.Monsaraz : Like Marvao, Monsaraz is also situated to the east of Lisbon. Whitewashed houses with roofs made of red tiles are very common in Monsaraz. It is one of the most beautiful monument villages in the country. Best Beaches Portugal has as much as 1000 kilometres of coastline along the picturesque Atlantic Ocean. The temperate Mediterranean climate is ideal for a beach visit. Let us check out a few of the best beaches in the country.Camilo Beach : Camilo Beach is hands down one of the best beaches in the country. It is situated near Lagos in the western Algarve and is known for sandstone cliffs and rock formations. It is a white sand beach blessed with pristine turquoise waters. Falesia Beach : Falesia Beach is also located in Algarve and is lined with cliffs and sandstone structures. It is almost six kilometres long, and many cafes, resorts and restaurants flank the sandy stretch.Amado Beach : One of the country's most famous surfing spots, Amado Beach, is blessed with waves. Since this lies close to a national park, this beach might not be everyone's cup of tea and can be of interest to people keen on exploring the diverse flora and fauna. Quinta do Lago Beach : Situated at the centre of the famous Parque Natural da Ria Formosa, this beach has abundant birdlife. Many birds like flamingos and storks frequent the marshland.
Portugal.Best Honeymoon Destinations in Portugal: Portugal offers an assortment of honeymoon packages too. Let us check a few of those places out.Setubal : Setubal is not a clichéd destination in Portugal, even though it is one of the best places to visit. Situated between the Sado Estuary and the Arrabida Mountains, Setubal is where people can enjoy their honeymoon by hiking, swimming or enjoying a candlelit dinner. Evora : Evora is the largest city in the Alentejo province and has many rustic farms and delectable food options. Old buildings are the order of the day in Evora, where honeymooners can enjoy a relaxing vacation. Sintra : Located close to the capital city of Lisbon, Sintra is a designated UNESCO World Heritage Site and is one of the most beautiful destinations in Portugal. All sites in Sintra will seem to have come straight out of a fairy tale or a cartoon, with its vivid and resplendent coloured buildings. The place is guaranteed to make your honeymoon memorable.Lisbon : Last but not least, the capital city of Lisbon is a potential destination for couples. It has sightseeing options galore, where honeymooners can frequent walks along Alfama and Bairro Alto. There are ample restaurants, cafes, bars and clubs in Lisbon for a great honeymoon. Head to Thomas Cook soon to plan your honeymoon through the Portugal honeymoon packages. What to eat in Portugal An assortment of delectable culinary options ranging from seafood rice to sweet egg tarts are available at restaurants in Portugal . Whether it is fancy Michelin-starred restaurants, local taverns or roadside grills, the country truly has diverse food varieties. Let us take a look at some of the must-try delicacies in Portugal. Pastel de nata : Arguably the most popular dessert of Portugal, pastel de nata (custard tart) is a small egg tart that many deem a world-class sweet dish. The Egg custard filling goes exceedingly well with the flaky pastry. The powdered sugar or cinnamon sprinkled on the top is the icing on the cake. Bacalhau : Bacalhau is salted cod - a staple fish for the Portuguese and is a crowd favourite. It can be baked as a fillet, grilled, put in rice or shredded with eggs, onions and potatoes. Every restaurant in the country is guaranteed to have a Bacalhau dish. Arroz de pato : Arroz de pato means duck rice and is a really tasty delicacy. Boiled and shredded duck meat is put on a bed of rice, baked and then decorated with sausage and orange garnishings. It has a very homely taste and might not be readily available at every Portuguese restaurant. However, it is more than worth the effort to seek out the dish at places that serve it. Acorda : Acorda is bread soaked in broth. It is akin to Indian rice porridge. However, what sets it apart is the fact that it comes with seafood. Rock hard bread is rehydrated using water, garlic, olive oil and cilantro. Acorda is definitely a gourmet experience for ardent foodies. Queijo : Queijo is Portuguese cheese and is not as popular as iconic European cheeses. However, there are many varieties of cheese to go after on your visit to Portugal. Some of the options are Serra de Estrela, Azeitão and São Jorge. Cataplana de marisco : This seafood stew gets its name from the vessel in which it is cooked and served. The cataplana (cooking pot) is loaded with seafood like fish and shrimp and cooked with white wine, spices, vegetables and herbs. You should ideally try this dish from the Algarve region where it originates. Things to Do in Portugal Portugal is a country that offers numerous options to the average tourist, in the sense that there are many different things to do in Portugal . Let us explore a few of the things to do in Portugal. Hidden cave beaches in AlgarveThe southernmost region in Portugal, the Algarve region, is famous for its abundant beaches. Algarve is a brilliant blend of white sand, blue waters and good weather. It is home to many hidden caves along its beaches. The most famous is the Benagil Cave, and there are other such places like the Zorreira Cave, the Arches of Marinha, Captain's Cave and the Elephant Cave. Checking out these hidden cave beaches is a great activity while in Portugal. Visit the Jeronimos MonasteryJeronimos Monastery is a beautiful monastery situated in Lisbon. It has resplendent Gothic architecture and is hence a crowd favourite. The monastery is a UNESCO World Heritage site, and it is almost always in every tourist itinerary. Wine tasting in PortoAny vacation in Portugal does not get over without a quintessential wine tasting tour. Even though port wine is prevalent in the country, you will be able to discover your favourite wine choice by embarking on a detailed wine tour. The best place to do it might be Porto. Nightlife in LisbonLisbon is known for its nightlife. The city does not stop bustling well into the night and has numerous bars and clubs. There are great options for dining, clubbing, dancing and entertainment. The place is guaranteed to satisfy every partygoer. What to Shop In Portugal Portugal offers great shopping options to both the average customer and those who do not mind splurging on expensive stuff. Ranging from rugs, carpets, blankets to designer wear, jewellery, and souvenirs, many varieties of things are available across the country. Colombo Mall, LisbonColombo Mall is one of the biggest malls in Portugal and looks great both from outside and inside. It has more than 300 shops and is a great place for luxury shopping in Lisbon. The place is a one-stop solution for everything, and there is a play area and outdoor garden where kids can enjoy themselves. AlbufeiraThere are many trinket shops in Albufeira. There are a variety of dress options and handcrafted materials that are available at the World In store. There is an option to get your hands on cushions, toys, jewellery, rugs, blankets and carpets. It is guaranteed to be one of the best shopping experiences in Portugal.El Corte InglésIt is the largest department store in Europe and offers a variety of things, ranging from clothes to books to furniture. Anything and everything is available here and what sets it apart from other options is that it is one of the cheapest shopping places. INDIAN RESTAURANTS IN PORTUGAL There are a handful of Indian restaurants in Portugal, adding to the variety of cuisines available in the country. ● Tasca David’s Indian Tandoori Restaurant, R. Infante Dom Henrique 72, Ferragudo ● Leo Restaurant, Rua do Zaire 17C, 1170-397 Lisbon ● Masala, R. Frederico Arouca 288, 2750-355 Cascais ● The Raj Indian Restaurant, Av. Beira Mar 1, 8365-101 Armação de Pêra ● Indian Night’s Tandoori Restaurant, E R. da Igreja Nova 7, 8200-137 Albufeira ● Spicy, R. do Benformoso 203, 1100-085 Lisboa ● Gandhi Palace, Rua Douradores Loja 167-169-171, 173, 1100-205 Lisboa ● Clay Oven, Sítio Areias De São João Edif. Mira Vila, Lj. B, R/C, Albufeira, Faro ADVENTUROUS THINGS TO DO IN PORTUGAL The nation of Portugal is blessed with options that cater to all kinds of tourists, with plenty of options for the adventurous ones as well. The beaches in Algarve offer a wide array of water sports activities. From surfing and lounging to even diving, these activities are a great way of spending quality time in the Iberian country. Surfing at the beaches in Nazare is yet another option for adrenaline junkies. The place is frequented by adventure enthusiasts from all around the globe. October to March is a great time to visit Nazare. Canoeing at Furnas Lake is one of the breathtaking things to do while in Portugal. Please make sure that you walk or get on a bike on the track around the lake to see bamboo and redwood forests, meadows and vines. There are a lot of adventure options in Serra da Estrela. It is a crowd favourite as the place offers ample opportunities to do bird and animal watching. There is also the not-so-little thing of climbing up Torre, the highest location in the Estrella mountains. Other activities include skiing, horse riding, mountain biking and paragliding. INTERESTING FACTS ABOUT PORTUGAL ● Portugal is the oldest European country. ● The oldest bookstore in the world is in Lisbon. ● Renewable energy is of great importance in Portugal. ● It is one of the best surfing spots in Europe. ● Ferdinand Magellan, the first explorer to have circumnavigated the globe, was Portuguese. So was Vasco da Gama. ● Port wine is the national drink. ● Piri piri sauce originated in Portugal. ● It is the westernmost point of the European continent. ● Portugal boasts of as many as 17 UNESCO World Heritage Sites. THINGS TO KNOW BEFORE VISITING PORTUGAL ● Do not assume that the starters the waiters bring you while you read the menu are free. ● Portugal's road safety record is not impressive. Use trains preferably. ● Vegetarians need to be aware that the traditional vegetable soup or caldo verde will almost always contain sausage. ● Football tickets are cheap in Portugal. ● A lot of the places in Portugal are best explored on foot. Case in point, Alfama in Lisbon. PORTUGUESE CULTURE It is important to note that Portugal is a Roman Catholic nation with an intimate and close-knit family value structure. Lisbon and Porto are European Capitals of Culture, and the country is known for its art, literature, food, music and football, among other things. PORTUGAL VISA FOR INDIANSApplying for a visa used to be a painful process, but now you can apply for a visa for Portugal online. All you will need is your visa checklist of documents that need to be made ready at the time of your visa appointment.A Schengen visa is what you will need to travel to Portugal. Thomas Cook does not offer its own visa services for Portugal. However, the Thomas Cook online visa processing centre and customer care are more than equipped to handle queries and solve your issues, in case there are any. You can feel free to ask all your visa related questions to the online visa process centre of Thomas Cook.FOREIGN CURRENCY The currency in Portugal is the Euro (EUR). 1 EUR = approximately 84 Indian Rupees (INR) It is better to carry the local currency of Portugal in person to avoid the additional currency conversion fees abroad. You can also buy currency from your nearest Thomas Cook outlet. Thomas Cook prides itself in offering the best currency exchange rates and services worldwide. With Thomas Cook, you can afford to relax at home and go on to buy or sell your Forex online. You can get a Forex card delivered to your home very quickly. You can also opt for Forex pickup at any of our branches for you to collect at your convenience.So, what are you waiting for? Book your tour package to Portugal by heading to the Thomas Cook website. Choose your trip, sit back and enjoy as we guarantee a chill vacation to the westernmost point of Europe, full of fun and frolic!OTHER SUCH PLACES TO VISITPortugal is not the only attractive destination across the globe. Jaipur.Introduction: Jaipur tour packages are in high demand these days. This is because Jaipur is one of the best and most beautiful tourist destinations in India. Many people opt for a package trip to Jaipur and have a gala time with their loved ones. If you are looking for a nice holiday spot this year, consider going on a Jaipur tour. You can find some very attractive Jaipur tour packages that offer a wonderful vacation experience. The Thomas Cook Jaipur tour packages are excellently designed, have a lot of flexibility, are affordable, and can be customised as per your needs. These are some reasons that make the Thomas Cook Jaipur tour packages so popular among Indians. If you are looking to book one of the Jaipur tour packages, take a look at this article before you finalise your Jaipur trip package. Learn about the features of Jaipur, the best time to go on a Jaipur tour, the modes of transport and so on. Then, make an informed decision and buy the most suitable Jaipur trip package to have an unforgettable Jaipur tour.
Jaipur.Jaipur Tour Package Price and Trip Cost: Tour Packages NameDurationPriceRajasthan Saver 6 Nights 7 DaysRs. ₹28 000Best of Rajasthan 6N6 Nights 7 DaysRs. ₹47 000Blend Of Mughal and Rajput Culture3 Nights 4 DaysRs. ₹18 200Jaipur with Ranthambore4 Nights 5 DaysRs. ₹24 400Golden Triangle5 Nights 6 DaysRs. ₹27 400 Where to Stay and What to Eat in Jaipur? As mentioned above, Jaipur has some amazing accommodation options. This makes Jaipur a great tourist destination. The same is true for the food of Jaipur. You find the best of Rajasthani cuisine right here in Jaipur. Take a look: Food Highlights in Jaipur You will largely find vegetarian food in Jaipur, as a majority of the Rajasthani people are vegetarians. You will find non-vegetarian food too, but your options would be limited to big restaurants. When you take one of the Jaipur tour packages and reach the city, you see that there is a lot of traditional food, and also all kinds of global cuisine available. The Jaipur tour packages are also popular among global travellers, so the city caters to all types of tourists.
Jaipur.Why Choose a Jaipur Holiday?: Here are some reasons why Jaipur acts as the perfect holiday destination, and why you may consider taking a Jaipur trip package to reach the city: Rich history One of the main reasons for the popularity of the Jaipur tour packages is the history of Jaipur. Jaipur is the capital of Rajasthan and was once the seat of the Kingdom of Amber. It was established in 1727 by Maharaj Sawai Jai Singh II. You will find palaces and other historical monuments all around the city. These are factors that make the city so popular and give you some great reasons to buy a Jaipur travel package and visit the city. Culture and tradition Just like history, Jaipur also has a very rich culture and tradition. When you buy the Jaipur travel package and visit Jaipur, you will notice remnants of the culture and tradition at every step. You will not be disappointed at any time, as colourful markets, grand street corners, festivals and events will keep you amazed at all times. Extremely beautiful Apart from the magnificent manmade attractions of the city, Jaipur also has stunning natural beauty. When you opt for a Jaipur holiday package and go there, you get to see beautiful lakes, hills, greenery and many other natural attractions, which add to the experience of your Jaipur holiday package. Good food, accommodation and shopping Good food is always a part of any successful trip, right? When you go on a Jaipur tour, you get amazing food. The local Rajasthani cuisine, made up of wonderful dishes like dal,bati, churma and shrikhand, leaves you asking for more! The Thomas Cook Jaipur holiday packages mostly include the food, so you don't have to go looking for food at a particular restaurant. You will also have a lot of places to shop and some beautiful hotels to stay in. Accommodation is also included in most of the Jaipur holiday packages, so once you buy a good Jaipur holiday package, you do not have to worry about anything else. Affordable holiday spot And finally, Jaipur serves as one of the most affordable holiday destinations in India. faq.question: What is the duration of a typical Rajasthan tour?
faq.answer: A Rajasthan tour lasts between 6 and 10 days, depending on the Rajasthan trip package you choose and the places you visit. The main places that are usually part of Rajasthan vacation packages are Jaipur, Jodhpur, Udaipur, Jaisalmer, and sometimes Bikaner or Mount Abu.

faq.question: Is it safe to travel to Rajasthan?
faq.answer: Yes, local and foreign guests can feel safe on a Rajasthan tour. The state depends on tourism and makes it easy for people to visit by having good facilities, friendly people, and police officers who are visible.

faq.question: Are permits required for all tourist spots?
faq.answer: Most places that are part of Rajasthan tour packages in India are free to visit and don't need special permissions. Amer Fort, City Palace, Lake Pichola, and Mehrangarh Fort are just entry tickets, and these are typically included in Rajasthan travel packages.

At night, your Rajasthan vacation packages can include a local market trip or a traditional Rajasthani dinner. Day 2: Jaipur City Exploration After visiting Hawa Mahal and Jal Mahal, your Rajasthan holiday package will continue with a stop at Amer Fort. You can go on a hot air balloon ride or watch a traditional folk dance show in the evening. Day 3: Jaipur to Bikaner (330 km) Travel by road to Bikaner to begin your package trip to Rajasthan. Take a holy trip to Junagarh Fort and Karni Mata Temple (Rat Temple). Day 4: Bikaner to Jaisalmer To get to Jaisalmer, you have to drive through the desert. When you get to your hotel through our Rajasthan tour packages, check out Gadisar Lake and enjoy the golden colour of the sandstone buildings. Day 5: Jaisalmer Desert Adventures A camel ride across the Sam Sand Dunes will highlight your Rajasthan trip. After this, there was a night in a desert camp with traditional shows and food. Day 6: Jaisalmer to Jodhpur Get to Jodhpur, which is also known as the "Blue City." Mehrangarh Fort and Jaswant Thada are on your Rajasthan tour packages in India. Take a walk around Clock Tower Market in the evening. Day 7: Jodhpur to Udaipur via Ranakpur Visit the Ranakpur Jain Temples on your way to Udaipur. Enjoy a calm evening by Fatehsagar Lake when you get to the City of Lakes through our Rajasthan tour packages. Day 8: Udaipur – The Romantic City Check out Shilpgram, City Palace, Saheliyon Ki Bari, and Jagdish Temple. A boat at sunset on Lake Pichola and dinner with a lake view are usually part of Rajasthan vacation packages. Day 9: Udaipur to Pushkar Go to Pushkar, which is known for its peaceful lake and Brahma Temple. Take a walk down cute streets and eat tasty treats from the area. Traditional Rajasthani Dishes You can sample the region's rich culinary customs by eating these classic meals, which are frequently included in your Rajasthan holiday package: Dal Baati Churma: It comes with lentil curry, baked wheat balls, and crushed sweet wheat. Every Rajasthan holiday package should include this activity. Laal Maas: A hot meat curry made with butter and red chillies. Serves a lot of it in high-end Rajasthan vacation packages for foodies. Gatte Ki Sabzi: Dumplings made from chickpea flour and cooked in yoghurt gravy. Most Rajasthan tour packages for families include this traditional meatless treat. Ker Sangri: A sweet dish made with beans and berries that was originally made during famines. Now, a speciality in planned package trips to Rajasthan. Mawa Kachori: Ideal for adding a sweet note to your Rajasthan trip, this deep-fried sweet treat from Jodhpur is filled with dry fruits and dipped in sugar syrup.
