Since there are so many package categories for the Vrindavan tour, it is advisable to choose one that fits your needs. For example, whether you seek a comprehensive spiritual experience or are a first-time visitor, you can select a trip that suits your travel plans.
Vrindavan.Mathura–Vrindavan Tour Plans & Itinerari: The Mathura Vrindavan tour packages are designed to fit every traveller's needs. There are various types of itineraries and plans to choose from. While you can complete the Vrindavan package in one or two days, it may not provide a comprehensive experience. Therefore, during this short-term stay, you may not get to visit all the temples or the ghats. In fact, for a shorter duration, you can't even explore any of the activities apart from temple visits. Thus, you'll be missing out on a lot of spiritual experience, especially the aartis or ghat visits. If you're planning a longer itinerary for your tour packages, know that you'll be getting a comprehensive experience. So, you can plan to dedicate anywhere between 6 and 7 days to exploring the place. Apart from your temple visits, you'll also be taken to the hidden gems. The locals have many stories about these places. Therefore, you can interact with them and learn more about the temples. When setting out on a guided Vrindavan tour, you'll be taken across all the major highlights. The guide will provide you with information about these temples, the cultural sites you'll be visiting, and more. However, if you're travelling alone, without any guide, be very mindful of the scams. Some scamsters may be present around the temples trying to dupe the first-time visitors. It is best if you connect with only authorised sellers and vendors to avoid any unwanted costs. If you're taking Mathura Vrindavan tour packages along with Agra, you'll get a chance to visit some of the most popular temples along this circuit. Some of the major monuments and temples that you'll explore in the circuit include the following: Shouripur Jain Temple Goverdhan Temple Brahmand Ghat Nidhivan Bankey Bihari Temple Panch Mathi Gokul Mandir Dauji Mandir Agra Fort Radha Kund Shankha Dhwaj Mandir Kusum Sarovar ISKCON Vrindavan Prem Mandir Taj Mahal Bateshwar Dham Vaishno Devi Temple Girirajji Temple Radha Rani Temple Raman Reti If you're seeking additional safety on your Vrindavan tour packages, it is advisable to check if insurance is provided. A comprehensive package usually provides standard travel insurance cover for the entire trip. However, if you plan to extend beyond the number of days specified in your package, you may incur higher charges. On the other hand, the Vrindavan tour package price will also increase if you customise it for more exclusive experiences. It is best to consult with your tour operator about all costs to maintain transparency and avoid any unexpected expenses later on.
Vrindavan.Top Temples & Spiritual Experiences in V: Shri Banke Bihari Temple Dedicated to Lord Krishna, Shri Banke Bihari Temple is one of the most popular Hindu temples in Vrindavan and the Mathura District. The temple is open every day. However, you must follow the temple timings before visiting. It is one of the most important shrines in India, among the 7 Temples dedicated to Thakurji in Vrindavan. Govardhan Hill A visit to the Govardhan Hill is one of the best spiritual experiences to indulge in during your Mathura Vrindavan tour. Govardhan Hill is a sacred sandstone structure located near Vrindavan. The locals believe that this was the hill that Lord Krishna lifted with his little finger to save the people from the wrath of Indra. Prem Mandir The grandeur of Prem Mandir is a sight to behold upon your visit to this region. The Prem Mandir was established in 2001 by Jagadguru Shri Kripalu Maharaj. This temple is often referred to as the Temple of God's love. The temple houses the grand statues of Ram, Sita, and Radha Krishna. The serenity felt at this temple is unmatched. ISKCON Vrindavan ISKCON temples are located all around the world, but ISKCON Vrindavan is the one you must not miss out on during your Vrindavan tour packages. Swami Prabhupada built this temple dedicated to the brothers, Krishna and Balarama. Every day, Bhagavad Geeta classes are held at this temple, where you get to learn about the stories and teachings mentioned in the Geeta. You must visit during the early morning or evening to experience Aarti. Birla Mandir During your Mathura trip, you must also visit the Birla Mandir. The temple is dedicated to an incarnation of Lord Vishnu, Lord Lakshmi Narayan. The red sandstone structure has beautiful carvings. As sunlight falls on the temple, it turns the entire structure a mix of red and gold that will surprise you. Sri Ranganathan Temple Sri Ranganathan Temple is one of the most beautiful temples in the Land of God, also known as the town of temples. It is a prominent temple, probably one of the largest ones in the entire circuit. The temple is dedicated to Lord Vishnu and Goddess Lakshmi. The temple houses the statues of Lord Rama, Sita, and Lakshmana. Other statues at the temple include those of Lord Narsimha, Ramanujacharya, and Venugopala. Radha Gokulanada Temple You should definitely visit the Radha Gokulananda Temple when setting out on a Vrindavan tour package. The temple is located between Radha Raman Temple and Keshi Ghat. Lokanath Goswami established it. Although the temple lacks a magnificent or vast structure, it is essential in Hinduism. The devotees of Lord Krishna consider this temple to be of great importance. The temple houses the statues of Lord Chaitanya, Radha Gokulananda, and Radha Vinodha.
Vrindavan.Things to Do in Vrindavan Beyond Temple: Set Out on Vrindavan Parikrama Apart from temple visits on Vrindavan tour packages, you should also embark on a Vrindavan Parikrama. You must give at least 2-3 hours to complete the Parikrama. While there are no fixed hours, it is advisable to start it early in the morning. It is best to perform the Parikrama during the Ekadashi month, as per the Hindu calendar. On the Parikrama, you must cover temples such as Madana Mohana Temple, Kaliya Ghat, Madana Ter, Sringara Vat, and Keshi Ghat. Attend Brahmotsavam Festival You must attend the Brahmotsavam Festival during your Mathura Vrindavan tour held at Rangji Temple. This ten-day festival is one of the biggest ones in Vrindavan and is held just two days after Holi. The pilgrims and devotees come together to pull the giant chariot, on which the idols are placed. The temple authorities conduct yagnas and havanas daily. The joyous celebration fills the entire atmosphere. Plan your visit to prevent missing out on the experience. Enjoy Delicious Meals Being a temple town, Vrindavan and Mathura have some of the most delicious dishes. Whether it is savoury snacks or sweets, you'll be in food heaven here. The 'Malai Wali Lassi' is one of the best things to try on your trip to Vrindavan. Also, be sure to try the delicious jalebi dipped in rabri. You can also pack some pedas as souvenirs. Attend Evening Aarti at Prem Mandir You should make time to attend the evening aarti at Prem Mandir. Built across 54 acres, the temple authorities conduct aarti daily dedicated to Lord Krishna and Radha. The evening aartis are of utmost importance at the temple, held every evening. While the Parikrama starts at around 7 PM, it concludes with the evening aarti at approximately 8:10 PM. You must know that the aarti changes, so plan your trip accordingly. The temple closes at 8:30 PM. Take Part in Bhagavad Geeta Classes The Bhagavad Gita classes in ISKCON are a must-attend event on your Vrindavan tour packages. You don't have to pay any additional fees for the Vrindavan tour package to attend these classes. These classes are designed to help you easily learn the teachings of Lord Krishna. You can take the online assessments after attending the classes to test your knowledge. After completing the course, you can get a personal mentorship and certificate. Explore Kans Qila Take a deep dive into the history as you explore what is believed to be Kans Qila in Mathura. Situated in Ratankund, Mathura, the locals believe that this fort has existed since the time of the Mahabharata. Raja Man Singh of Amber restored the fort in the 16th century. The fort showcases the perfect blend of Hindu and Mughal architecture. The Kans Qila is often referred to as the Purana Quila of Mathura or the Old Fort. The Brahma Ghat and Vasudev Ghat are located just near this fort.
Vrindavan.Vrindavan Trip Cost & Budget Planning: A trip to Vrindavan is a spiritual journey you embark on. This place attracts thousands of devotees and tourists from India and international destinations alike every year. Depending on your preference, you can plan your budget accordingly. The Vrindavan trip cost can range from basic to luxurious, depending on the type of experience you choose. For example, if you opt for only Vrindavan tour packages, the package price might be slightly lower. On the other hand, if you select Vrindavan Mathura tour packages, with Agra, Bateshwar Dham, and other destinations, the cost would increase. The Vrindavan tour package price entirely depends on the type of experience you want. If your Vrindavan trip is only for a couple of days, typically 2-3 days, you wouldn't have to invest a lot. Here are some of the key ways to plan Vrindavan holiday packages on a budget: Travelling via air is likely to increase the Vrindavan tour cost, irrespective of the destination you're flying from. Thus, it is best to prefer a bus or train to travel to Vrindavan when you're coming from Delhi. You can find a wide range of accommodation options in Vrindavan, ranging from budget-friendly to mid-range and luxury hotels. If you're booking comprehensive tour packages, the accommodation is likely included, so you won't have to spend extra. If you're travelling alone, consider booking Dharamshalas or budget-friendly hotels to save money. Vrindavan is home to some of the most delicious vegetarian meals. Therefore, the food choices you make will have a significant impact on the Vrindavan trip cost. The street food for snacking is available at affordable rates. However, if you want a formal dining experience, you may try fine dining meals. The Mathura Vrindavan package usually offers meals. If meals aren't included, you can consume food from any of the hotels. You must choose local transport to get around in Vrindavan. Several budget-friendly travel options are available, such as e-rickshaws, private taxis and auto-rickshaws, to help you manage your Vrindavan tour cost. While some temples do not charge entry fees, if you wish to donate, you should plan accordingly. Temple donations, however, are miscellaneous expenses.
Vrindavan.Essential Tips for a Smooth Vrindavan To: Once you've booked your Vrindavan tour packages, you should prepare for it accordingly. Here are some of the key tips for planning a smooth Vrindavan tour: You should pack clothes depending on the time you're travelling to Vrindavan. For Summers, you must pack lightly, whereas for winters, you may carry your layering garments. However, many people avoid visiting during the monsoon as the showers prevent them from visiting temple to temple. On your Mathura Vrindavan tour, you'll be visiting a lot of temples, mostly. Therefore, it is advisable to dress modestly. Your knees and shoulders should be covered to show respect for the local culture. All the temples require barefoot entry. Therefore, you must remove your shoes/sandals before visiting any temple. It is best to carry easy-to-remove footwear. Summers in Vrindavan can get slightly difficult to handle. Therefore, it is advisable to stay hydrated during your summer Vrindavan trip. If you want an affordable Vrindavan trip cost, it is advisable to avoid visiting during festivals such as Krishna Janmashtami or Holi. These places are in high demand during this time. Therefore, the cost of the accommodations and travel will be slightly higher than usual. Follow the temple rules, especially regarding photography. While many temples put up restriction boards, some don't. Thus, if you want to take pictures of the temples during your Vrindavan trip, it is best to ask the authorities before doing so. Be very mindful of all your belongings, especially while travelling to temples. Most of the temples included in the Vrindavan package are surrounded by numerous monkeys. Thus, there's a risk that these monkeys may take away your precious belongings. You should learn a few local phrases to communicate effectively with the locals. While your guide will help you with the history of the temples, some of these locals can offer you hidden knowledge about the temples. Do not fall prey to any touts, as this would unnecessarily increase the cost of your Vrindavan tour. You must stick only to authorised guides and vendors who can help you through the process.

Comprehensive experience: Thomas Cook offers an extensive experience for Mathura Vrindavan tour packages. As a part of the package, they include sightseeing, meals, accommodation, and flights. However, the flights will be optional, so discuss with the team and book accordingly. Just grab your bags and be ready for the comprehensive experience. Flexible and Customised Itineraries: Thomas Cook offers the option to customise itineraries for Vrindavan tour packages. If you have any specific requirements for accommodation or meals on your trip, you can consult the Thomas Cook team. Transparent refund policies: Thomas Cook has exclusive transparent refund policies. If there are any last-minute changes to your plan, you can refer to their refund policies. Thus, you'll receive all the information about how much you'll receive if you cancel the plans. Thomas Cook is dedicated to providing an exclusive Vrindavan package wherein you can enjoy your best. With Thomas Cook, you get 24/7 support for your packages, so you don't face any challenges.

NYC has something or the other to offer to people and at every budget.

Never miss the chance to visit the Statue of Liberty & Times square when in New York City. Empire State Building is the second tallest building in the city (first being the World Trade Centre). It is a skyscraper in Manhattan and the first building to have 100 floors. The view from the 102nd floor is magical and bring back a lot of memories from Hollywood movies. The National 9/11 Memorial & Museum commemorates the attack on the World Trade Centre on 11th September has thousands of images, artefacts that is entirely dedicated to the innocent people who died due to the terrorist attack. table_of_content: The Best Time to Visit Gujarat

faq.question: 1. Which is the best time to visit Gujarat?
faq.answer: The winter season, which is between November and February, is the best time to plan a trip to Gujarat as you can expect the temperature to be pleasant for outdoor activities. If that is not possible, then October too is an enjoyable time for a Gujarat tour.

faq.question: 2. How far is Gujarat from Mumbai?
faq.answer: Gujarat is 613.4kms from Mumbai. The quickest flight is to Surat, which will roughly take you 1hr and 15mins.

faq.question: 3. Which airport serves as Gujarat's main airport?
faq.answer: 14 airports are in operation in Gujarat, the main one being Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel International Airport (AMD), 9kms from Ahmedabad.

faq.question: 4. Is Gujarat safe for female travellers?
faq.answer: Yes, Gujarat is very safe for female travellers.

faq.question: 5. What festivals are celebrated in Gujarat?
faq.answer: Gujarat is a land of festivities. Apart from the annual cultural festival, Rann Utsav in the Rann of Kutch, and the grand Navratri celebrations across the state, Gujarat also hosts several fairs throughout the year.

faq.question: 6. Can I go to Gujarat by a cruise or ferry?
faq.answer: No, as of now, there are no cruises connecting other parts of the country to Gujarat.

faq.question: 7. Are Daman and Diu a part of Gujarat?
faq.answer: No, Daman and Diu are a union territory in western India. Gujarat shares its border with the two regions.

faq.question: 8. Is drinking prohibited in Gujarat?
faq.answer: Yes, Gujarat is a dry state. Alcohol is banned here under The Bombay Prohibition Act, 1949.

faq.question: 9. How many days are required for a Gujarat trip?
faq.answer: An 8-day long vacation suits the splendour Gujarat has to offer. However, if you are crunched for time, there are Gujarat packages ranging from a quick 2 nights and 3 days to a moderate 5 nights and 6 days.

faq.question: 10. How much time does it take from Mumbai to Ahmedabad by flight?
faq.answer: It takes about 1 hour 5 minutes in flight from Mumbai to Ahmedabad via direct Mumbai Ahmedabad flight.

faq.question: 11. What is Gujarat well-known for?
faq.answer: Gujarat, the home state of Mahatma Gandhi, the Father of the Nation, stretches out into the Arabian Sea with a hint of the desert and a 1600-kilometer-long coastline. Beaches, temple towns, and historic capitals are must-visit places in Gujarat and should definitely be included in your Gujarat tour packages. Gujarat is known for its wildlife sanctuaries, hill resorts, and natural beauty.

faq.question: 12. Can I eat meat in Gujarat?
faq.answer: Gujarat is predominantly a vegetarian state. However, there are certain communities that prepare dishes with meat and seafood.

Gujarat.Introduction: At first glance Mahatma Gandhi and lions seem to have no apparent relation with each other. But there is one, Gujarat, the home of the Mahatma and the world’s only remaining Asiatic Lions have many more of such contrasts that give a distinctive flavour to your holidays. It is a land of bewitching natural beauty, idyllic beaches, stunning architectural feats, historical monuments and ancient civilizations. A Gujarat tour will take you on an exciting adventure that encompasses all these delights and much more. There are sights to see, food to savour and shopping to do. All you have to do is pick your holiday from a list of Gujarat tour packages. Experience the cultural charm of Gujarat this summer and grab exciting discounts of up to ₹6,000 on your trip. The catalogue of Gujarat tourism packages is comprehensive, offering you a wide range of choices. A holiday in Gujarat is not too costly either! Thomas Cook’s range of Gujarat holiday packages starts at a very reasonable Rs. 56,900/- for a quick outing of 2 night and 3 days, going up to Rs. 5,13,915/- for a leisurely vacation of 9 nights and 10 days. Booking a Gujarat tour with Thomas Cook is easy, your only dilemma will be choosing a holiday. Every one of our Gujarat packages offers an extraordinary holiday experience. But our holiday experts are always at hand to guide you through your options for Gujarat holidays. Gujarat welcomes everyone with open arms and fulfils their holiday wishes. It offers thrills by the bucketload for adventure seekers. It enchants history enthusiasts with stories of love, power, corruption and wars. Honeymooners are treated to a glorious honeymoon experience with the plushest of accommodations. Gujarat packages offer an amazing array of vacation delights to families that they can enjoy together and create memories of a lifetime. Animal lovers will love the teeming Gir forest with its multitude of wild inhabitants. Gujarat holidays do not disappoint anybody but delights its visitors with thoughtful touches that makes their holidays extra special. Thomas Cook’s Gujarat holiday packages are tailor-made by a team of well-travelled experts who understand and appreciate the ingredients of a memorable holiday. These Gujarat tour packages will make you a perfect brew of experiences to excite your holiday palate. The well-thought-out itinerary lets you explore the delights of a Gujarat tour at leisure without rushing you through a hurried holiday. You leave the planning to us and concentrate on enjoying your holidays. Thomas Cook lets you start your Gujarat tour from most of the major cities around the country like Mumbai, Delhi, Chennai, Kolkata, Hyderabad, Pune and Bengaluru. So, what are you waiting for? Go ahead, choose from a range of Gujarat tourism packages. With Gujarat holiday packages available from all major cities such as Mumbai, Kolkata, Hyderabad, Bangalore, Pune, Chennai, Delhi and Ahmedabad getting to Gujarat with Thomas Cook India is convenient and comfortable.
Gujarat.Most Popular Gujarat Tour Packages: Tour Packages NameDurationPriceGreat Rann Utsav 2N2 Nights 3 DaysRs. ₹17 300Great Rann Utsav 3N 3 Nights 4 DaysRs. ₹27 900 Gujarat And Madhya Pradesh - Char Jyotirlinga Tour7 Nights 8 DaysRs. ₹46 600Gujarat - Dwarka Somnath With Diu And Sasangir6 Nights 7 DaysRs. ₹34 400Wonderful Gujarat9 Nights 10 DaysRs. ₹57 300 An exciting adventure awaits you with our Gujarat tour packages. Gujarat is now allowing visitors with a few precautionary measures that are designed to ensure your good health. While thermal scanning will be mandatory for all, there is no quarantine for domestic travellers. Additionally, local administrations will have their own rules that you must follow to ensure safety. Most attractions will be open, but not all of them will be fully operational. Here’s the complete list of guidelines and travel documents for travelling to Gujarat. If you're looking for a perfect holiday getaway, you can opt for one of our Gujarat holiday packages. Our team has carefully crafted the Gujarat packages to offer you a complete experience of the state. We understand that every traveler is unique, and that's why we offer several Gujarat trip packages to choose from. Whether you're looking for an action-packed trip or a relaxed, leisurely holiday, we have the perfect Gujarat tour package that suit your needs. Here’s the complete list of guidelines and travel documents for travelling to Gujarat.
Gujarat.The Best Time to Visit Gujarat: The winter season is the best time to enjoy Gujarat tour packages. While it is hot through most of the year, the mercury dips appreciably during winter. The days are cool, evenings are cold, and nights are colder. Average temperatures hover between 12°C to 29°C and humidity is at its lowest. The period between November to February showcases the best that Gujarat tourism packages have to offer. All the attractions are open and wildlife sanctuaries are teeming at this time. Summers can get really hot, and temperatures hover between 28°C to 41°C. Sightseeing becomes difficult as stepping out in the heat is not enjoyable. Similarly, the rainy season may not be to everyone’s taste for a holiday. Temperatures range between 27°C to 35°C and are made bearable by the rains. But humidity can be as high as 62%. Gujarat looks refreshed and beautiful as the rains wash away the summer dust and heat. Just pick a time, pack your bags and go!
Gujarat.How to Reach Gujarat: Gujarat enjoys good connectivity with its neighbours and other major cities of India. It is internationally well-connected too. Here are the options that you get to start your Gujarat tour from different parts of the country. Reaching Gujarat By Air Flying is the fastest way to reach Gujarat from other major cities. It has 17 airports, which includes the highest number of domestic airports anywhere in India. The ones in Vadodara, Rajkot, Junagarh, Bhuj, Surat, Porbandar and Bhavnagar are some of the prominent ones. Ahmedabad’s Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel is the only international airport in the state. They receive flights from all major Indian cities like Mumbai, Delhi, Pune, Hyderabad, Bengaluru, Chennai and Kolkata. Reaching Gujarat By Rail Gujarat’s rail network is managed by Western Railways and is one of the busiest rail networks in the country. Vadodara Junction is Gujarat’s biggest and the busiest railway station. Since it falls on the Mumbai-Delhi main line, it receives many trains such as Rajdhani, Shatabdi and Duronto that pass through to the rest of the cities. Ahmedabad Junction, Rajkot, Surat and Junagarh are a few of Gujarat’s major rail stations. Reaching Gujarat By Road The roads in Gujarat are some of the best-maintained in the country, especially the highways connecting cities. Most of them have two-lane and four-lane carriageways. If you choose to drive yourself, taking a road trip into Gujarat is fun. The road network criss-crosses the state and provides excellent point-to-point connectivity. Gujarat State Road Transport Corporation (GSRTC) runs a good bus service connecting major cities and states around. Local Transport There are two ways to travel between cities, within the state; buses and taxis. Buses are the cheapest means of public transport, but a taxi is more comfortable, although more expensive. An autorickshaw is the most convenient way of travelling within a city. Local buses also make city travel easier if you’re on a tight budget.
Gujarat.Where to Go in Gujarat: Gujarat is a vast canvas with a veritable rainbow of hues to colour your holiday in different shades. From the remains of an ancient civilisation to a teeming wildlife sanctuary, and everything in between, Gujarat is a joy to explore. Here are our picks of places to go during your Gujarat tour. Dholavira - A must visit on your Gujarat tour package Dholavira is a slice of one of the most advanced ancient civilisations in the world. A part of the Indus Valley Civilisation, it was a prosperous Harappan city that existed between 2650 and 1450 BCE. Walk among the excavated ruins and listen to the echoes of a developed civilisation as its people went about their daily business almost 4500 years ago. The locals have their own name for the site, Kotada Timba. One of excavation’s most remarkable features is its extraordinary water conservation system. Mansar and Manhar were two of its most crucial water channels that carried water all across the city. You can spend hours here, admiring the architecture and getting a feel of how life must have been all those thousands of years ago. It is a must-visit on your trip to Gujarat. Gir National Park – Wildlife Sanctuary in Gujarat You don’t have to travel to Africa to see wild lions. The Gir National Park holds the distinction of being the last habitat of Asiatic Lions in the wild. Here you can observe these magnificent beasts in their natural habitat and enjoy face-to-face encounters with the king of the jungle. It is wholeheartedly dedicated to the preservation of these lions. The project was started by the Nawab of Junagarh to prevent Asiatic Lions from going extinct. Though the lions are the star residents of the national park, they are not the only ones. It is home to an impressive 2000 different insect species, 38 mammal species, 300 bird species and 37 species of reptiles. Some of the other animals you can see here include chinkara, sambar deer, chausinga, spotted deer, hyenas and leopards. Gir takes a place of pride on most Gujarat tour packages. Rann of Kutch – Visit this White Desert on Your Gujarat Tour The wide-open space at the Rann of Kutch is an impressive sight. The vast salty plains are flat as far as the eye can see. During the day, the salt shines and dances with the heat. The White Desert of India is one of the most beautiful sights in the world. Especially on a moon-lit night when it shines like a diamond. The moonlight gives it an ethereal and serene quality that soothes the senses. The best time to visit the Rann is during the ‘Rann Mahotsav’ which is held every year between December and February. It is a highlight of Gujarat packages. The silent plains come alive with dance, music and cultural performances. Enjoy a camel safari and shop for souvenirs to take back home. When you get tired, fefuel your enthusiasm with a taste of the local fare. Gandhinagar – The Capital City of Gujarat The understated capital city of Gujarat is one of the only few planned cities in India. It is included in most Gujarat packages. Sitting on the western banks of the iconic Sabarmati River, Gandhinagar is an enchanting city. It is home to the revered Akshardham temple, one of India’s most beautiful places of worship. Unlike the bustle of other major cities, Gandhinagar has a calm aura. The Adalaj Stepwell is an explorer’s delight. The Dinosaur and Fossil Park entertains children and adults alike. Check out the Khadi Gram and watch artisans weave their magic. You can even try your hand at weaving cloth out of Khadi. Gandhinagar is a green city with 54% tree cover. The city has numerous parks and gardens all over if you need a break from sightseeing. Bhuj – Explore this Historical City Bhuj has a history that goes as far back as the 16th century. It is home to many historical sites associated with the Mahabharata and Alexander’s march into India. Some of the most well-known attractions of Bhuj include the Cenotaphs Complex which holds the royal family’s tombs. The Prag Mahal, Aina Mahal, Sharadbaug Palace and Bhujia Fort are beautiful palaces and forts with stories to tell. A significant addition to many Gujarat packages. The Hamirsar Lake and Deshadsar Lakes are popular hangout spots that offer beautiful vistas of the greenery around. The Kutch Museum chronicles all the region’s history and lays it all out beautifully for you. If shopping is your idea of fun, you can shop to your heart’s content for meenakari jewellery, local handicrafts, spices and vibrant colourful clothes. Be sure to try the local cuisine for a taste of Bhuj. Mandvi - Golden-Brown Sprawl of Sand Mandvi is a bit off Gujarat’s usual tourist circuit. But that doesn’t make it any less beautiful. It has a tranquil quality that makes it a pleasant city to explore. Named after the sage Mandavya, it is mentioned in several Indian scriptures. The city has a great religious significance and has several religious sites, some of them quite ancient. But the city’s main attractions are its beach and a fort. The Mandvi Beach is a firm favourite of adventure seekers with its water sports facilities. It is also a great spot for lounging or spending some fun time with your family. The marvellous views of the Arabian Sea will hold you back with their vibrant hues. Save some energy for exploring the Vijay Vilas Palace. If wildlife fascinates you, head to Topansar Lake for birdwatching. With so much to offer, Madvi surely deserves to be part of your Kerala trip. Junagarh – Prominent Cities of Gujarat Junagarh sits at the foothills of the Girnar range. Located close to the Gir National Park, the former princely state is now a popular tourism destination. Apart from the national park, it has a number of attractions such as beaches, forts and amusement parks to liven up your holiday. The 150-year-old Sakkarbaug Zoo is a treat for children. It features a thriving population of animals including panthers, deer, antelopes, spotted deer and black bucks. The Uperkot Fort is an old guard that still stands tall. The Girnar hills are great for trekking. You can camp amidst the mountains if you feel adventurous enough. The Buddhist Caves offer a peaceful retreat, so take a break from your sightseeing and relax for a bit. The beaches of Junagarh also offer adventure in the form of thrilling water sports. Statue of Unity Standing peacefully on the banks of the Narmada River, the Statue of Unity commemorates the Iron Man of India, Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel and his vision of an independent India. The 790-foot-tall bronze sculpture is currently the tallest statue in the world. It is an awe-inspiring sight as the statue comes into view from afar and grows in stature as you draw nearer. You can buy a ticket and go up to the statue’s viewing gallery. The viewing gallery sits at 500 feet and offers spectacular views of the surrounding area. The museum at the base depicts Sardar Patel’s life in more than 2000 photographs. Do not miss the laser and light show which takes place every evening at 7:00pm. Pay a visit to the nearby Valley of Flowers for a spectacular display of natural beauty.
Gujarat.Food in Gujarat: Gujarat is known for its warm hospitality that involves a lot of food that’s rich in taste as well as calories. Gujarati cuisine has an unbelievable variety of vegetarian food that tantalises the tastebuds with sweet, savoury and tangy flavours. In addition to its scrumptious tastes, it offers a range of textures that vary from satisfyingly crunchy to melt-in-the-mouth soft. From the famous duo of jalebi-phaphda to the quintessential khaman-dhokla, the list of Gujarati delicacies seems endless. Here are five members of the Gujarati cuisine that will give you a glimpse into its diversity. Khandvi Khandvi is nothing but soft delicate strip of gram flour soaked in buttermilk rolled up and tempered with curry leaves and mustard seeds. It may sound simple but has a great flavour that, together with the fluffy texture, makes it a tasty any-time snack. A must try dish on your Gujarat trip. Gujarati Samosa The Gujarati-style samosa is a crunchier version of its Punjabi cousin. Wrapped in a thin flour strip or ‘Patti’, it takes on a crunchy texture that satisfies the senses with each bite. The filling may differ from city to city, but it mostly uses a mixture of cooked potatoes, cabbage, onions and green peas. Enjoy it with a variety of chutneys that enhance its taste by just the right amount. Undhiyu Undhiyu is a winter delicacy that warms your soul with its sweet and savoury flavour profile. There is no fixed recipe, and each region of Gujarat has its own version. Cooked to perfection in an inverted clay pot, it uses the freshest produce that’s available during winter. It combines several tastes and textures to create a unique culinary masterpiece that’s best enjoyed with piping hot puris. Try this delicacy on your next Gujarat trip. Dhokla If ever there’s a universally recognisable representative of the Gujarati cuisine, it has to be Dhokla. The spongy fluffiness is a flavour bomb which explodes as soon as you bite into one. You will find many variations of this humble snack that make it more interesting. It is tempered with curry leaves and mustard seeds that give a great kick between mouthfuls. Handvo Handvo is basically a savoury cake that’s made out of fermented rice and lentil batter. The traditional version is baked over coals, while the more modern method uses a pressure cooker to cook it. There’s also a pan-fried version which has a crispy golden cover. This is a very popular snack that’s usually served with a tangy green chutney and a piping hot cup of tea.
Gujarat.Things to Do in Gujarat: A Gujarat tour is rarely a quiet experience. With so much to see and do, your holiday is a procession of experiences. Here are some of our recommended things to do on Gujarat tour. Visit Sabarmati Ashram This is one of the must-visit places in our Gujarat packages. Sitting on the banks of the Sabarmati River, the Sabarmati Ashram is an island of tranquillity amidst Ahmedabad’s hustle and bustle. The museum on the premises is a treasure trove of historic memorabilia. Here you will walk through historic corridors that were once graced by some of the greatest freedom fighters of India. Step into a stepwell The ‘Rani ki Vav’ is a classic stepwell that features an astonishingly intricate design. Almost every wall, pillar and ceiling of this UNESCO World Heritage Site is covered with a carving or a sculpture. In fact, it has about 1500 sculptures spread throughout the structure! They all follow a mythological theme centred around the 10 incarnations of Lord Vishnu. It was constructed by Rani Udaymati of the Solanki dynasty. One of the highlights recommended place on your Gujarat holidays. Explore the ancient ruins at Lothal The ruins at Lothal date back to 2450 BCE and offer a glimpse into the life that existed back then. Lothal was an important part of the Harappa Civilisation and played an important role in connecting the region with western civilizations like Egypt and Mesopotamia. It offers a fascinating experience as you walk amidst structures that were built more than 4000 years ago. Visit the Milk Capital of India Anand is the home of the popular milk cooperative, Amul. This is the place where India’s white revolution began and transformed the lives of the people around. You can visit the Amul plant and see some of your favourite products being made. The museum inside documents the journey of Amul and offers an interesting insight into the project. Watch Flamingos at Kutch Sanctuary The mud flats at the Kutch Sanctuary turn pink every winter when the flamingos flock in for breeding every year. It is a fascinating sight to see ripples of contrasting colours run across a sea of pink plumages. It is one of the biggest attractions of the Kutch Rann Utsav and a must-visit place on your Gujarat trip. It is a spectacle that’s not to be missed.

Bahamas Cruise.Introduction: Balmy, Blissful, Brilliant – Bahamas. Where beaches are laden with pink-white sand, like fairy dust or tiny crystals of rose quartz. Where blue is not just blue, but a gateway into a world of turquoise, sapphire, teal, and cobalt. A culture brimming with European and African heritage, and a legacy of the great Christopher Columbus. Imagine being part of a fantastical world, where storytelling and folk magic hang in the air, and pirates, soldiers and slaves whisper from the past. Islands in galore, the Bahamas captures pockets of buttery sunshine all year round. All the while, tantalising your taste buds, with pineapple cocktails and fresh crab meat. Bahamas Tour Packages No. of Nights Price Best of East USA and East Canada 08 Nights 09 Days Rs. 2,43,230 American Explorer 12 Nights 13 Days Rs. 3,15,118 USA - Eastern Wonders 12 Nights 13 Days Rs. 3,15,118 USA - Western Wonders 13 Nights 14 Days Rs. 3,56,460 American Grandeur with Bahamas Cruise 18 Nights 19 Days Rs. 4,34,310 USA For All 19 Nights 20 Days Rs. 3,31,650 Eager to experience all this magic? At Thomas Cook, we strive to make your trip to the Bahamas, as beaming and breath taking as the place itself. Sights to see More than just a collection of islands, The Bahamas is a treasure chest of history, culture, and recreational wonders. It caters to every whim and fancy, be it a peaceful walk along a picturesque coast, or adrenaline pumping water sports. From the local markets, to the festivals, colourful streets, and old fashion bungalows – The Bahamas pulses with life. Here are some of the places that you should check out. Nassau Bahamas’ vivacious capital – Nassau, is a commercial hub and business district. Don’t worry though, it also offers its travellers an eclectic mix of history and culture. A walk along Bay Street will lead you to its quaint and colourful straw market. There, you will find woven hats, bags, mats, dolls, jewellery and other little souvenirs. Nestled in the heart of old town Nassau, is an interactive Pirate museum. Based on the 18th century golden age of piracy, this theme park commemorates great adventures at sea, grand pirate fleet ships, treasures, and more! A visit to the National Art Gallery of the Bahamas, will introduce you to the charming 1860’s Villa Doyle - a lovely colonial style mansion in creamy-yellow, that houses 4 galleries and a fascinating collection of traditional and modern Bahamian art. Paradise island True to its name, Paradise island offers its visitors a glimpse into the very heavens itself. An affluent kingdom of casinos, waterparks, and manmade villages, its only link to Nassau, is two little bridges that lead you to an alternative universe of splendour. You will discover fine dining restaurants, boutiques and emerald lawns, galore. The Atlantis resort consists of luxury accommodations built around a sprawling 154-acre waterscape. The focal point being the Mayan Temple slides, The Rapid River, Power Tower, Splashers, and the Dig. Exploring this island’s attractions, is akin to embarking on a magical underwater expedition. CocoCay island CocoCay island, also known as Little Stirrup Cay, glitters 55 miles north of Nassau. Exquisite turquoise beaches, electrifying rides, and a quintessential tropical vibe, renders it the perfect day island to traverse. It is as if the whole island was painted by a master, every detail heightened amidst a sea of watercolours. Home to the tallest waterslide, and largest wave pool - the possibilities are as endless as the shades of blue. Excited about all this Bahamian beauty? It doesn’t end here.The Bahamas – Salt in the air and sun-kissed hair Your Thomas Cook packages, will not only cater to your tropical fantasies, but will immerse you in the cultures of America and Canada too. In each package, you will be discovering both – land and water. We’ve put together a list of a few spots that are worth visiting in the North American continent. USA A burst of colour, cacophony of sounds, and composition of smells – New York is a bustling metropolis captured with a neon filter. Climb the Empire State Building, cycle through Central Park, and sail to Ellis Island. Sip a delicious hot chocolate, at Rockefeller centre, and savour each bite of a classic hot dog at Coney Island. Washington DC, with its historical charm and glistening architecture, speaks of political stories of yesteryear. Visit the Unites States Capitol, Lincoln memorial, and Washington Monument. Marvel at the majestic White house and comb through the Smithsonian Museum of Natural History. Canada From French colonial, to Gothic Revival – the city of Montreal is a magnificent aesthetic fusion. Notre-Dame Basilica of Montreal, along with Montreal Botanical gardens, Mount Royal, and Old Port of Montreal are only some of the city’s numerous treasures. Toronto is a city that juxtaposes a concrete jungle with vast spaces of natural greens. Some iconic spots to visit include – CN tower, Royal Ontario Museum, Distillery District, and Toronto Islands.

Las Vegas.Introduction: A glittering and dazzling city, Las Vegas has received several monikers such as ‘Sin City’, ‘City of Lights’ and ‘Entertainment Capital of the World’ due to its fabulous cityscape that includes opulent hotels, legendary casinos, exquisite fine dining options and electrifying nightclubs. Known for its vibrant nightlife, it is also a popular destination with honeymooners who wish for a thrilling and adventurous time. Embark on an unforgettable journey with the Las Vegas honeymoon packages and other tour packages to explore the vivacious city.

The highlight is the iconic ‘The Strip’ that exudes an exuberant air and comprises of the most luxuriant hotels, finest casinos in the world, high-end shopping centres, nightclubs, amusement parks and theatres. Synonymous to Las Vegas that allure tourists owing to its splendour and grandeur are the crème da la crème hotels such as The MGM Grand, Caesars Palace, The Palazzo, The Venetian, Bellagio and The Wynn. During the day visit the the Mob Museum that showcases the history of The Mob and narrates the tale of the most organised crime history of the 70s era, various art galleries, Titanic Artifact Exhibition, Red Rock Canyon National Conservation Area and many more. Add glitz and glamour to the nights as you witness the outstanding and impressive light and sound show that devours the entire street of downtown Fremont and embark on the world of gambling at the best casinos, attend the marvellous live entertainment shows and revel at the nightclubs till the wee hours of the morning. For adrenaline rush seekers, the desert land has plethora of outdoor adventures such as ziplining, hiking, car racing, abseiling, rock climbing, horse riding, skiing, snowboarding and many more that can further enrich the fun and memorable trip. Book from the various Las Vegas tour packages for an enthralling holiday between March-April when the weather is pleasurable averaging 25C or during the winter season between November-February to experience the peak tourist season and an exhilarating ambience.

faq.question: What is the ideal duration for a Kerala trip?
faq.answer: If you want to explore and enjoy the main attractions and experiences that Kerala has to offer, then you will need around 14-18 days. However, if you are short on time, you can cover some of its key tourist destinations in 5-7 days. You can find Kerala tour packages for both short and long vacations on Thomas Cook.

faq.question: Is Kerala safe for tourists?
faq.answer: Yes. Kerala is a very safe tourist destination in India. However, as a recommendation, tourists are advised to exercise general caution during their visit. For instance, make sure to keep your valuables in a safe location, stay hydrated when travelling during summer, check the weather forecast before heading out during monsoon in hilly areas, and so on. You can consider checking our Kerala group tour packages if you prefer travelling with fellow tourists.

faq.question: What are the must-try dishes in Kerala?
faq.answer: The cuisine of Kerala is truly delightful and is a great way to experience the unique local flavours and culture. On your Kerala trip, make sure to try some of its most popular dishes, such as Appam with stew, Idiyappam, Kerala Prawn Curry, Sadhya, Malabar Parota, Thalassery Biryani, Erachi Varutharacha Curry, etc.

faq.question: Are there any direct flights to Kerala from major cities?
faq.answer: Yes. There are tons of direct flights operating daily between Kerala and other major Indian cities. For instance, you can easily find direct flights from New Delhi to Kochi, Bengaluru to Thiruvananthapuram, Mumbai to Kozhikode, etc. For reference, a direct flight from Mumbai to Kozhikode takes around 2 hours.

faq.question: What are the best shopping items to buy in Kerala?
faq.answer: A major part of a Kerala vacation is to indulge in local shopping activities. There are tons of renowned markets in the state with tons of offerings. Some of the best things to shop for in Kerala include Kasavu Sarees, Nettipattam, Coir products, elephant figurines, Nilavilakku, etc.

Kerala.Why is Kerala the Ultimate Tropical Para: The state of Kerala is located on the southwestern edge of India, on the Malabar Coast. The amazing offerings presented by this region strongly justify its nickname, which is “God’s Own Country”. Kerala is a treasure trove for nature lovers, and here’s why it is the ultimate tropical paradise: Wildlife and nature: Nature’s majestic green foliage covers the state from top to bottom. A good portion of the iconic Western Ghats lies within the borders of Kerala. Additionally, tons of endangered plants and animals thrive in its ecosystem. The state is home to plenty of massive tea plantations and renowned wildlife sanctuaries. The incredible Thattekad Bird Sanctuary, Periyar National Park, Wayanad Wildlife Sanctuary and Eravikulam National Park are located in this state and commonly covered in the best tourism packages for Kerala. Beaches: An integral part of a tropical holiday destination is the availability of beaches, and Kerala has no shortage of these. Kerala’s beautiful landscape is nicely decorated by multiple pristine beaches. If you love the sun, sand, water and tranquil ambience, then you will love the Kerala beaches. Some of its most renowned beaches are Varkala Beach, Kovalam Beach, Cherai Beach, Alleppey Beach, and Marari Beach. Both South Kerala tour packages and Kerala packages for couples commonly cover these beaches. Backwaters: What sets Kerala apart from other tropical destinations is its wonderful backwaters. The state has a massive network of brackish lagoons, lakes and canals that run along the coast, parallel to the Arabian Sea. Exploring this labyrinth of narrow waterways while soaking in the surrounding natural beauty and peace is one of the best things to do in Kerala. The backwaters of Alleppey and Kumarakom are particularly famous. Get South Kerala tour packages featuring backwaters on our website. Hill stations: Kerala is a tropical paradise with some of India’s most iconic hill stations, such as Munnar, Wayanad, Idukki, etc. The flexibility of switching from beaches and backwaters to towering hills and soothing weather makes it a top pick for vacations, especially in summer. Kerala tour packages for couples and families, centred towards these hill stations, are easily available. Weather and unique offerings: The weather in Kerala favours all kinds of tourism around the year. It also has tons of unique things to offer, such as rural tourism, culinary tours, ayurvedic retreats, and traditional arts. What makes this better is that you can even experience all of these on a budget trip to Kerala.
Kerala.Planning Your Kerala Trip: Essential Inf: The most important thing to consider when considering a trip is proper planning. Thankfully, Thomas Cook offers the best tourism packages for Kerala, which takes care of the trip planning and management. For general use, here is some essential information for planning a Kerala trip:
Kerala.Best Time to Visit Kerala: Thanks to its diverse landscapes and availability of prominent tourist destinations, you can visit Kerala at any time of the year. However, there are certain advantages to visiting this destination at certain time frames. Here’s what you need to know about the best time to visit Kerala: Kerala in Summer Summer covers the months from March to May or mid-June, with an average temperature ranging between 28-38°C. Coastal regions stay quite warm, while hilly areas, like Wayanad, Thekkady, and Munnar, offer a more relaxed weather. Hence, summer is considered the best time to visit Kerala for hillside vacations, especially if you are focused on a budget trip to Kerala. Kerala in Monsoon Monsoon in Kerala takes over the months from mid-June to September. The heavy rainfall brings a whole new look to the region by enhancing its natural elegance. Exploring the rain-soaked landscapes of Kerala while the fragrance of wet soil lurks in the air can be a fascinating experience. Kerala packages for couples remain quite in demand during this season. Kerala in Winter The mercury starts to drop as October rolls in, marking the onset of winter. It extends till February and features an average temperature range of 18-28°C in low-lying regions and 10-25°C in hilly areas. Winter is the best time to visit Kerala for all kinds of tourism experiences. Get the best tourism packages for Kerala for any season from Thomas Cook.
Kerala.How to Reach Kerala?: Reaching Kerala for tourism purposes is very easy, especially with Kerala tour packages from Thomas Cook that feature optional flights. Here’s what you need to know about how to reach Kerala: By Air The main airports in the state are in Kochi, Thiruvananthapuram and Kozhikode. They handle tons of domestic and international flights daily. You can easily find flights to these airports from major Indian cities. Such as Pune, Bangalore, Hyderabad, Mumbai, Delhi, etc. For reference, a direct flight from New Delhi to Kochi takes about 3 hours. By Rail For a more economical travel option for your budget trip to Kerala, you can consider travelling via train. The main railway stations in Kerala are located in Kasaragod, Ernakulam, Kozhikode, Alappuzha, Thiruvananthapuram, etc., featuring daily trains to and from major Indian cities. By Road The NH 66 and 544 connect Kerala to its neighbouring states and other prime locations in the country. You can choose to drive to Kerala in your own car or take a rented taxi, or take an overnight luxury bus.
Kerala.Safety & Travel Tips: Here are some useful travel and safety tips for your Kerala trip: Emergency numbers for the Police are 100 and 112, Fire Station is 101, and Ambulance is 102 and 108. The primary language spoken in the state is Malayalam. However, English is also widely understood and spoken. If you are travelling to Kerala during the monsoon, do note that frequent rainfall can hinder travel plans. If you are travelling to Kerala during summer, be aware of the high heat and humidity. If opting for an Ayurveda centre in Kerala, make sure they are certified by the Department of Tourism. When visiting religious or cultural sites, make sure to dress modestly.
Kerala.Permits and Regulations: Generally, there are no such permits and regulations for domestic tourists to visit Kerala. However, do take note of the following: Foreign tourists will require a valid passport and visa to visit Kerala. Tourists will need a general entry ticket to visit national parks and wildlife sanctuaries. Those visiting Lakshadweep from Kochi, Kerala, will require a special entry permit for Lakshadweep.

With this plan, you will be accompanied by other fellow travellers and follow a pre-arranged trip itinerary with things like accommodations, meals, sightseeing, etc., included. Kerala.Top Attractions & Experiences in Kerala: Proper research helps make a fulfilling Kerala trip itinerary. With Kerala tour packages from Thomas Cook, you get expert-made trip itineraries offering a well-balanced vacation experience without any compromise. Here are some key tourist attractions and experiences that you can cover on your visit to Kerala: Alleppey Alappuzha, also known as Alleppey, is among the most serene and beautiful places to visit in Kerala. The place is mainly known for its intricate network of narrow canals and big lagoons. If you want an authentic Kerala backwaters experience, you must visit Alleppey. Alongside this, Alleppey is famous for its Ayurvedic resorts, Alleppey Beach, houseboat stays, etc. Check out Thomas Cook for South Kerala tour packages featuring Alleppey. Munnar A trip to Kerala is incomplete without spending a couple of days at Munnar. It is a widely popular hill station in South India, attracting tourists from all over the country, especially honeymooners. The place is dotted with winding roads, massive tea gardens, dense greenery, breathtaking viewpoints and towering hills. Popular Kerala packages for couples commonly feature this destination. Kochi The historic port city of Kochi in Kerala is a great tourist site that you can add to your Kerala trip itinerary. Featured in popular family trip packages to Kerala, the city is a melting pot of diverse cultures. Additionally, you can find tons of iconic landmarks in this city that showcase its rich historical past, such as the Jewish Synagogue, Jew Town, Fort Kochi, Mattancherry Palace, Santa Cruz Basilica, etc. Trivandrum Thiruvananthapuram or Trivandrum is the capital city of the state and a renowned tourist destination. It offers a unique blend of the region’s traditional charm and growing urbanisation. The city is mainly known for being the home of the famous Sree Padmanabhaswamy Temple, which is one of the 108 Divya Desams in Hinduism. Trivandrum is a major highlight of the most popular South Kerala tour packages. Varkala If you are interested in a laid-back experience accompanied by beachfront views, charming sunsets and ayurvedic retreats, Varkala can be a great choice. Located an hour and a half away from the capital city, Varkala caters to travellers seeking scenic views, serene vibes and good seafood. Its key attractions include Varkala Cliff, Varkala Beach, Janardhanswamy Temple, etc. Popular South Kerala tour packages usually feature Varkala. Thekkady Head to Thekkady in Kerala to explore the mesmerising regional wildlife and spice plantations. The famous Periyar Tiger Reserve is located here and is a great site to visit if you are interested in tiger sightings. It is also home to the magnificent Periyar Lake, which attracts tourists with its incredible views and fun boating opportunities. You can easily find family trip packages to Kerala featuring Thekkady on Thomas Cook. Kumarakom Kumarakom is another great place in Kerala where you can enjoy its iconic backwaters alongside lush greenery and a serene ambience. The quaint hamlets, budget resorts, fun houseboat stays, fishing opportunities, etc., are a few examples of things that the place features. Other notable tourist attractions of this place include the Kumarakom Bird Sanctuary, Vaikom Beach and Vembanad Lake. Kumarakom is a major inclusion in several romantic Kerala tour packages for couples.
Kerala.Unique Experiences Beyond Sightseeing: The spark of Kerala tourism doesn’t just end at sightseeing, as the state has tons of unique and exciting experiences to offer. Here are some of the most unique experiences you can have on your Kerala trip: Ayurvedic Wellness Retreats If you are heading to Kerala for a digital detox and get in sync with nature, do consider getting your mind, soul and body a refresh. Kerala has tons of Ayurvedic Wellness Retreats that attract visitors from all over the country as well as the world. These retreats utilise the ancient Ayurvedic approach to promote good health. The best tour plan for Kerala should include this experience. At these retreats, you will receive a whole package experience that includes accommodations, activities, meals, etc., primarily focused on promoting your well-being. At these retreats, you will get to try yoga, focus on mindful eating, connect with nature, get ayurvedic therapies, and more. For a streamlined experience, consider Kerala tour packages centred around Ayurvedic wellness retreats.
Kerala.Traditional Art Forms: Art, culture and tradition are some of the core elements that flow through the veins of Kerala. The state has many notable traditional art forms that tourists love to explore and experience on their visit. For example; Kathakali: Originating from Kerala, Kathakali is an Indian classical dance form that is famous around the globe. The performance is based on the themes of Hindu mythology, and involves a group of dancers with elaborate makeup and costumes, expressive hand and facial gestures and rhythmic music. Mohiniyattam: Another traditional dance form originating from Kerala. The name comes from Mohini, the female avatar of Lord Vishnu. It is a solo dance performance mainly done by women, involving white and gold traditional attire, graceful & unique movements, subtle expressions, and traditional music. Kalarippayattu: It is an ancient traditional martial art form originating from Kerala. Kalarippayattu was developed from the real battlefield combat techniques that are unique to the region. It emphasises body control, weapons and barehanded combat techniques.
Kerala.Culinary Tours: Exploring the culinary side of the region is an integral part of your Kerala tour. On your visit, make sure to try some of Kerala’s most iconic food items, such as: Appam with stew: It is a paper-thin and crunchy rice pancake that you can eat alongside creamy, coconut curry. Kerala prawn curry: It is an aromatic and spicy curry dish made with prawns, coconut milk, curry leaves, chilli, salt, and a handful of local spices. Sadhya: This is the traditional Kerala thali dish. It is served on a banana leaf and includes rice, papadam, pickle, moru and tons of other side dishes. Banana fritters: What better snack to bless your tea time in Kerala than Banana fritters? These are basically slices of ripe bananas with plain flour coating, which are then deep-fried in oil. While on this topic, do note that Kerala tour packages from Thomas Cook come with meals as standard inclusion. Village Life Experiences The soul of Kerala truly resides within its rustic village side. Exploring these rural areas and their myriad cultural and traditional offerings can fulfil the heart of every traveller. Far away from the hectic city life, the natural elegance and serene ambience of the village life can make anyone feel nostalgic. Some iconic experiences to have while on one of these visits include: Backwater cruises: Taking a cruise of the backwaters while witnessing the scenic beauty and daily village life is a surreal experience for tourists. To experience this, get the best tour plan for Kerala. Exploring paddy fields: There are many laid-back activities one can be a part of on their Kerala trip, such as paddy field walks, cycling tours, socialising with the locals, etc. Learning about local crafts: Kerala village side is noted for its practice of brilliant traditional crafts. You can learn more about things like fishing, coconut-based crafts, coir making and more.
Kerala.Essential Tips for a Smooth Kerala Trip: To ensure a smooth and hassle-free travel experience, here are some handy tips for your Kerala trip: Pick the right time: Travelling to Kerala during peak summer or monsoon can be a bit challenging due to the high heat and humidity and frequent rainfall. Hence, picking the right time for your vacation is very important. Ideally, the best time to visit Kerala for tourism is between October and March due to cooler temperatures and lower humidity levels. You can find Kerala group tour packages for any season on our website. Things to pack: Due to Kerala’s tropical climate, make sure to pack light and breathable clothes. If you are visiting during winter, especially the hill stations, then do add some warm clothes. Besides, do carry rain gear, comfy footwear, sunscreen, insect repellent, prescription medications and a powerbank. Get around locally: Navigating Kerala is very easy if you get the hang of the local transportation options. You can easily find taxis, auto-rickshaws, buses and rental bikes/cars at your disposal for intra-city and inter-city travel. Better accommodation selection: Expensive hotels and resorts can make your wallet thin. Proper budgeting is essential so you can focus on spending your trip budget on major sightseeing tours and activities. You can easily save money on accommodations by picking budget homestays or hotels. Accommodations are standard inclusions in our Kerala group tour packages. Dress code: When visiting religious and cultural sites, especially temples, make sure to dress in modest clothing. For example, there are strict dress codes for visitors of the Sree Padmanabhaswamy Temple and failing to adhere to it can result in no admission. Avoid littering: The beauty of Kerala needs to be preserved as it is. So, on your visit, make sure not to contribute to pollution. Avoid littering and ensure proper disposal of your garbage. Get a travel package: If you don’t want the hassle of planning and managing the trip from scratch, consider investing in the best tourism packages for Kerala. They come with unique itineraries and take care of trip essentials, so you can have a smooth vacation experience.

Monsoon (June to September) Monsoon transforms Kodaikanal into a lush green paradise, with the landscape coming alive with vibrant hues. The rainfall during this season adds to the scenic beauty of the hill station. While outdoor activities may be limited due to occasional rainfall, monsoon offers a unique charm for nature lovers. Summer (March to May) Summer is considered one of the best times to visit Kodaikanal, especially for those escaping the scorching heat of the plains. The weather during this season remains pleasant, with temperatures ranging between 20°C to 30°C. Enjoy leisurely strolls amidst the blooming flowers in the botanical gardens, or indulge in boating on serene lakes like Kodaikanal Lake and Berijam Lake. Our Kodaikanal travel packages offer the perfect escape from the summer heat, allowing you to explore the city's famous tourist places in Kodaikanal with ease. Winter (December to February) Winter is another popular time to visit Kodaikanal, especially for those who enjoy cooler temperatures and misty mornings. Kodaikanal.How to reach Kodaikanal?: Reaching Kodaikanal is an integral part of planning your Kodaikanal holiday packages to this picturesque hill station. Situated in the southern state of Tamil Nadu, India, Kodaikanal is well-connected to major cities by road, rail, and air, offering travellers various options to reach their desired destination with a well-curated Kodaikanal trip plan. By Road Kodaikanal is easily accessible by road, with well-maintained highways connecting it to major cities like Chennai, Bangalore, and Coimbatore. The journey by road offers breathtaking views of the Western Ghats and lush greenery, making it a memorable experience for travellers. Private taxis, buses, and self-driven cars are popular choices for reaching Kodaikanal by road, providing flexibility and convenience for tourists. By Air The nearest airport to Kodaikanal is Madurai Airport, situated around 120 kilometres away. From Madurai Airport, travellers can hire taxis or board buses to reach Kodaikanal. Madurai Airport is well-connected to major cities across India, including Chennai, Bangalore, Mumbai, and Delhi, making it a convenient option for travellers flying in from other parts of the country. By Rail: While Kodaikanal doesn't have a railway station of its own, the nearest railway station is Kodai Road, located approximately 80 kilometres away. From Kodai Road, travellers can hire taxis or board buses to reach Kodaikanal. Several trains connect Kodai Road to major cities like Chennai, Bangalore, and Coimbatore, offering an alternative mode of transportation for tourists. By Bus Kodaikanal is well-connected to nearby towns and cities by a network of state-run and private buses. Regular bus services operate from cities like Chennai, Bangalore, and Coimbatore to Kodaikanal, offering an affordable and comfortable mode of transportation for tourists. With our Kodaikanal travel packages, you can embark on a shopping spree and discover the best that the town has to offer, while also exploring the top places to visit in Kodaikanal.
Kodaikanal.Interesting Facts about the Kodaikanal p: Discover the fascinating allure of Kodaikanal with these 10 interesting facts that showcase the rich history, culture, and natural beauty of this enchanting hill station: Kodaikanal is renowned for its rare and exquisite Kurinji flowers, which bloom once every 12 years, covering the hillsides in a carpet of vibrant purple hues. These ephemeral blooms attract nature lovers and photographers from around the world, adding to the allure of the hill station. Kodaikanal is often referred to as the "Princess of Hill Stations" due to its picturesque landscapes, misty hills, and serene ambience. Nestled in the Palani Hills of the Western Ghats, this idyllic retreat has captivated travellers with its timeless beauty for centuries. The iconic Kodaikanal Lake, spanning over 60 acres, is a man-made marvel created by the British in 1863. Originally built to serve as a reservoir for irrigation purposes, the lake is now a popular recreational spot where visitors can enjoy boating, fishing, and leisurely walks along the promenade. Cascading from a height of 180 feet, the Silver Cascade Waterfall is a mesmerising sight to behold in Kodaikanal. Named for the shimmering silver sheen of the water as it cascades down the rocks, this natural wonder is a must-visit attraction for nature lovers and adventure seekers alike. The enchanting pine forests of Kodaikanal, with their towering trees and dappled sunlight, evoke a sense of tranquillity and serenity. These dense forests are perfect for leisurely walks, picnics, and nature trails, offering a refreshing escape from the hustle and bustle of city life. Located near the Pillar Rocks, the Devil's Kitchen is a series of deep crevices and caverns formed by natural erosion. According to local folklore, these mysterious caves were once the hiding place of the legendary demon princess, making them a popular spot for exploration and adventure The Shola forests of Kodaikanal are a unique ecosystem characterised by dense evergreen forests, rolling grasslands, and meandering streams. These biodiverse habitats support a wide variety of flora and fauna, including endangered species such as the Nilgiri tahr and the Indian elephant. Kodaikanal is home to one of the oldest astronomical observatories in India, established in 1899. Perched atop a hill, the Kodaikanal Solar Observatory conducts research on solar phenomena and atmospheric conditions, making it a significant centre for scientific study and exploration. Kodaikanal is home to a diverse population comprising people from various ethnicities and backgrounds. From the indigenous tribes of the region to settlers from different parts of India and the world, the hill station boasts a rich tapestry of cultures, traditions, and languages, adding to its vibrant and inclusive atmosphere. Surrounded by lush forests and rolling hills, Berijam Lake is a serene reservoir located in the heart of Kodaikanal. The crystal-clear waters of the lake offer breathtaking views and provide a peaceful retreat for visitors seeking solace amidst nature's beauty. With its scenic beauty, rich history, and cultural diversity, Kodaikanal offers a truly immersive and unforgettable experience for travellers. Explore the best that this charming hill station has to offer with our Kodaikanal travel packages, designed to showcase the top places to visit in Kodaikanal.

United Arab Emirates.Introduction: The United Arab Emirates located in the western part of the continent of Asia is a congregation of seven countries ruled by an elective monarch. The seven countries that constitute the UAE are Abu Dhabi, Ajman, Dubai, Fujairah, Ras Al Khaimah, Sharjah, and Umm Al-Quwain. It is hard to choose the best places to visit in the United Arab Emirates because every country under this federation has its charms. Abu Dhabi and Dubai in particular are popular holiday destinations and most frequently opted for United Arab Emirates store packages. United Arab Emirates.Best time to visit UAE: The Federation of UAE was built on a desert, which makes the climatic conditions warm to exceedingly hot, especially during summer. If you are looking to shop for luxury items at a discount and enjoy the charms of UAE at lower costs than usual, then the best time to visit the United Arab Emirates is during the months of March to July. There are multiple sales available during your United Arab Emirates vacation from major brands, so your trip is overall cost-effective although you will have to battle the excruciating heat. If you are looking for a pleasant experience on your holidays in UAE, then the ideal and best time to visit UAE is during winter or spring. The months of November to February are relatively cooler. This is the shoulder season to check out the best places to go in the UAE as well as explore open shopping destinations, walk through the cities for an immersive experience, and enjoy the natural vibe in the man-made country. It is also when the most exclusive parties take place in ras al Khaimah with international artists, so you may end up having the experience of a lifetime.
United Arab Emirates.Places to visit in UAE: If you book your UAE tour package during the best time to visit UAE, you can explore a lot of the top attractions that are famous for more than one reason. The most popular tourist spots are located in the best places to go in the UAE, such as Abu Dhabi, Dubai, Ras Al Khaimah, and Sharjah. Here is a list of places that you simply must add to your itinerary of UAE tour packages: Burj Khalifa The world's tallest architectural enigma, the Burj Khalifa, is an absolute delight to look at. You can see the structure from almost anywhere in Dubai, not to mention when you're flying above the city. for a mere 200 AED, you can take a trip to the top of the monumental building and view the entire city at a glance. Louvre Abu Dhabi The UAE counterpart of the Louvre in Abu Dhabi is a cultural and historical hub, and one of the best places to go in the UAE. The reflection of human history through artwork commemorating important events as well as celebrating the ingenious thoughts of artists' spectacular vision that you simply cannot miss. Beaches You can find a lot of entertaining activities at the beaches of UAE. They are the best places to go in the UAE for a panoramic view of the ocean splashing against white sandy beaches lined with exquisite restaurants and clubs. At Jumeirah beach, you can see the magnificence of the Burj Al Arab, while La Mer hosts some of the finest restaurants in the city. In Abu Dhabi, you can go shopping along the beachside whereas, in Ajman and ras al Khaimah, the beach resorts truly add to the essence, If you are looking for a pleasant experience on your holidays in UAE. Al Fahidi quarter If you truly want to witness the past of the federation, then the al Fahidi quarters are the most beautiful place in UAE that you will ever see. The remnant of the old city proudly displays the well-preserved old houses and small marketplaces from ancient times. you can walk through the quarters for an authentic feel of the eras gone by. Sheikh Zayed mosque A testament to the growth of Islamic architecture, sheikh zayed mosque located in Abu Dhabi sports some of the best craftsmanship that conjugates ancient traditions with modern designs. the 24-karat gold chandeliers mixed with crystal dressing however above golden tiles of mosaic and glass. This truly is one of the best places to visit in UAE. Sharjah art museum The city of Sharjah upholds several restrictions but that is only to preserve the integrity of what it has to offer. It is one of the best cities to visit in UAE if you want to indulge in art and history, especially at the Sharjah art museum. with an intricate infrastructure to truly illuminate the beauty of the artworks curated since the 18th century from European as well as Arab artists will leave you awestruck. Sharjah Museum of Islamic civilization This incredible museum is one of the most famous places in UAE for preserving the true cultural history of Islam. The building is dedicated to preserving artefacts that have impacted Islamic traditions in the past and display several pieces of documentation including manuscripts expressing the true purpose and teachings of the religion. Dubai mall One of the biggest malls in the continent is also one of the most famous places in UAE. Not only can you go shopping for luxury items and the finest Dubai craftsmanship, but you also walk through an aquarium over an underwater zoo. Dubai mall is an all-inclusive place where families can have a jolly good time while witnessing a spectacle only Dubai can offer. Dubai creek One of the most popular tourist destinations and the best place to visit UAE is Dubai creek. The water body that cuts through the city with scenic views of the metropolis on either side is marvellous in the making. Palm island As you know, the UAE was built on desert land but the more spectacular display of their architectural genius lies on palm island. This man-made island erected on the water in the shape of a palm tree is one of the most luxurious spots in Dubai and the most beautiful place in UAE. Hajar mountains One of nature's beloved gifts to the UAE is the Hajar mountain range. The spectacularly barren, brown peaks tower over the federation across Dubai, Fujairah, Ras Al Khaimah, and Sharjah. You can visit the forts and watchtowers that once served to protect the people of UAE and are now converted into preserved historic sites.
United Arab Emirates.What to eat in UAE: The cuisine of the Mediterranean has always appealed to foodies all around the globe. while on your UAE tour packages from India exploring the best places to visit in united arab emirates, pick some time to taste the authentic dishes from one of the healthiest cuisines around the world: Al machboos - The original recipe of biryani is made with long-grained rice cooked in the essence of a gravy made of seafood or red meat with a hint of dried lemon. It is served with salad and jeera raita. Chelo kebab - Chunks of meat, be it beef, chicken, or lamb are closely skewered together and grilled before serving with a side of grilled tomatoes and rice flavoured with saffron. Manakish - A cheese-filled Lebanese flatbread is baked and topped with your choice of meat, vegetables, herbs, and spices. Balaleet - One of the favoured breakfast foods for the Emirati population is sweetened vermicelli hooked with rose water, saffron, and cinnamon. For vegetarians, the dishes are served as is with a topping of pistachios, whereas for non-vegetarians it comes with a side of a tiny omelette. Shawarma - Shawarma is a perfect blend of meat grilled on a vertical rotisserie sliced thinly and mixed with veggies and mayonnaise, wrapped in a tortilla. Falafel - An incredible vegetarian delicacy made of ground chickpeas mixed with vegetables and rolled into balls before deep frying. falafels are served with a side of salad and hummus but you can also enjoy these supremely delicate vegetarian meatballs just like that Al Harees - A popular and traditional dish mostly served during high religious festivities such as Ramadan as well as birthdays or weddings is Al Harees. It looks like a porridge made of wheat and meat cooked with salted water over a period of time. Fattoush - For healthy vegetarians, you can try the fattoush salad made by combining cucumbers, tomatoes, onion, lettuce leaves, mint leaves, and garlic, and seasoned with lemon and olive oil. Shirin polo - Another popular traditional dish that you simply must try on your United Arab Emirates holidays is the sweet rice derived from a Persian recipe. It is made by mixing carrot strips and rice with a sweetened zest of citrus. Once the dish is cooked it is garnished with almonds, dates, and raisins. Knafeh - A popular sweet dish made of phyllo dough that is shredded to hold a chunk of sweet cheese with syrup on top. Fatteh - It is a Mediterranean lasagna that is made of three layers. stock-soaked bread lays the groundwork at the bottom, the second layer is filled with stuffed brindles and chickpeas, while the third layer is a combination of yoghurt mixed with tahini sauce. Just looking at this dish makes your mouth water. Mahalabiya - Another popular traditional dish served during Ramadan is the custard dessert made from milk that is boiled till thickened. Sugar and cornstarch are added to the milk along with rose water and cream to create the delectable pudding. Jellab - If the best time to visit UAE for you is the summer, then you will definitely need Jellab to get you through the heat of the day. The schooling drink is made of rose water blended with grapes and garnished with raisins and pine nuts. Arabian coffee - The infamous Arabian coffee beans are blended and simmered with cumin, cloves, cardamom, and a hint of saffron to create a thick concoction called Gahwa.

faq.question: What are the Cherry Blossom Festival dates in Japan for 2025?
faq.answer: In 2025, the Cherry Blossom Festival in Japan is expected to bloom from March 20 to April 13, showcasing the iconic sakura across popular spots like Tokyo, Kyoto, and Osaka.
