Japan.Best time to visit Japan: Considering a trip to Japan? Here is a guide on seasons, weather, and experiences to determine the best time to visit Japan. Spring (March to May): The best season to visit Japan is definitely in the spring, if you're planning a trip there. With temperatures ranging from 10°C to 20°C, the weather is pleasant and revitalising. It's cherry blossom season, and witnessing the softly falling pink petals in parks and on streets will be a lifelong memory. The majority of Japan trip packages are most popular during this time of year, particularly in April. Some of the greatest places to visit in Japan at this moment include popular Japan tourist spots like Kyoto, Tokyo, and Nara, which are bursting with life. Summer (June to August): If you can tolerate a little heat and humidity, summer has its own special pleasures. Japan's festival season is the best time to go to Japan to travel to destinations like Hokkaido or hike Mount Fuji. Summertime Japan tourist packages from India are typically less expensive, making them ideal for travellers on a tight budget. Autumn (September to November): Another popular season for travellers is autumn. The best time to go Japan for sightseeing is because of the clear skies and temperatures, which range from 12°C to 22°C. There are a ton of things to do in Japan, such as visiting temples, taking walks in the mountains, and attending local festivals, and the country is painted in shades of crimson and gold by the fall foliage. This is one of the best times to visit Japan with Japan holiday packages for those seeking serene yet colourful scenery. Winter (December to February): Do you want to explore hot springs, ski resorts, and snow-capped mountains? In Japan, winter is enchanting. Although the Japan tour cost from India varies, it is generally less expensive in some places, particularly for tourists who enjoy snow sports. During this time of year, Northern Japan and Hokkaido are popular places to see in Japan, and there are plenty of cosy, reasonably priced Japan trip packages available from India. Travelling to Japan is always a great experience, regardless of the time of year. Simply choose the best month to visit Japan based on your preferred travel style, and you'll have an amazing vacation!
Japan.How to reach Japan?: Organising your ideal Japan packages from India? Here are the best ways to travel from India to this stunning island nation for a comfortable and easy trip around the best places to visit in Japan. By train The answer to the question of whether it is possible to go from India to Japan by train is no. There is no train connection between India and Japan because Japan is an island republic divided by the ocean. On the other hand, train travel within Japan is a true pleasure once you get there. The nation is home to one of the most sophisticated railway networks in the world, namely the Shinkansen bullet trains. Many Japan trip packages include these high-speed trains, which provide a comfortable and easy way to travel between popular Japan places to visit. Consider purchasing the Japan Rail Pass for your local travel, particularly if your Japan tours from India include visits to several cities. By Road The ocean barrier makes it impossible to travel by car from India to Japan. The two nations are not connected by an overland route. However, there is a first-rate road system in Japan, so don't worry. All of the places to see in Japan have buses, taxis, and private shuttles, so it's simple to go at your own speed. Internal road transfers are typically included in Japan packages from India. Road travel throughout Japan enhances the convenience and comfort of any journey, whether it's a family vacation or a romantic honeymoon package. By Air Flying is the most convenient and quickest way to get from India to Japan. Major Japanese airports, including Tokyo Narita, Haneda, and Kansai (Osaka), have direct and connecting flights to major Indian cities like Delhi, Mumbai, Chennai, Bengaluru, and Hyderabad. Direct flights typically last between six and eight hours. Flying is an effective and time-saving way to get to Japan, with affordable options available. To make your vacation to Japan hassle-free, you may choose from a variety of Japan tour packages, including Japan packages from India that cover everything from airfare to lodging and sightseeing.
Japan.Best Places to Visit in Japan: With a variety of famous sites, serene temples, serene beaches, and verdant hill retreats, this list of top Japan attractions is expertly put together for your Japan tour packages or any specially designed Japan packages from India. These are must-sees if you're organising a trip to Japan in order to fully experience its allure. Famous Places to Visit Himeji Castle: Himeji Castle, a magnificent UNESCO World Heritage Site, is frequently included in high-end Japan holiday packages. This castle, which is well-known for its historic significance and immaculate white façade, displays traditional Japanese architecture and warrior history. Mount Fuji: Mount Fuji, Japan's highest peak and a sacred and picturesque volcano, is one of the country's most famous Japan attractions. For hikers and photographers both, it's an ideal spot, particularly around dawn. A necessity for your tour of Japan. Nara Park: Nara Park, a park in the centre of Nara, is well-known for its open green areas, amiable deer, and nearby historical landmarks. One of the most serene locations to visit in Japan, it's ideal for a calm afternoon on your Japan tours from India. Shirakawa-go: This picture-perfect mountain community is renowned for its peaceful rural setting and steep, thatched-roof homes. It's a rare opportunity to see old Japan in its most pristine state and a treasure in many India Japan tour packages. Jigokudani Monkey Park: This park, which is well-known for its snow monkeys that take wintertime baths in hot springs, is a favourite among tourists seeking out unusual Japanese attractions. This location enhances your Japan tour packages if you're looking for an unforgettable wildlife experience. Beaches/ Hill Stations to Visit Okinawa's Nishihama Beach: This beach on Hateruma Island, which is well-known for its "Hateruma Blue" waters, is included in many leisurely Japan trip packages. Excellent for sunbathing, swimming, or simply taking in the island atmosphere when visiting Japan. The Japanese Alps, or Kamikochi: With walking trails, views of the mountains, and wooden bridges, Kamikochi is a tranquil retreat tucked away in the Alps. One of the popular Japan things to see for hikers and environmental enthusiasts looking for peaceful getaways in Japan. Yonaha Maehama Beach (Okinawa): One of Japan's most stunning beaches is Yonaha Maehama Beach in Okinawa, a 7 km expanse of fine white sand that contrasts with the deep blue sea. If you enjoy serene, picturesque beaches, include this in your Japan travel packages. Hakone: Hakone, which offers hot springs, a picturesque lake, and the opportunity to see Mount Fuji, is a popular stop on many India-Japan travels. Many India-to-Japan travel packages include this popular mountainous area. Nikko: Nestled in the hills, Nikko is the ideal fusion of spirituality and nature. It is one of the best places to visit in Japan and offers a magical experience with waterfalls, shrines, and serene hikes. Temples to Visit Tokyo's Sensō-ji: Sensō-ji, the oldest Buddhist temple in Tokyo, is a vibrant, busy area teeming with local small businesses, prayers, and culture. a lively stop on the majority of trip itineraries to Japan. Kyoto's Kinkaku-ji (Golden Pavilion): One of the most remarkable sights in Japan is this shining golden temple that is mirrored in a serene pond. For a serene yet famous spiritual stop, include it in your India-to-Japan tour packages. Tōdai-ji, Nara: This temple, which is surrounded by a serene environment and deer, is home to one of the biggest bronze Buddha statues in Japan. This ought to be at the top of your list of must-see Japan tourist places if you're organising your trip. Tokyo's Meiji Jingu: Nestled within a verdant woodland in the heart of Tokyo, this Shinto temple offers a peaceful respite from the bustle of the city. For those seeking inner serenity, it fits quite well with the majority of India-to-Japan tour packages. Kyoto's Fushimi Inari Taisha: One of the most photographed locations in Japan is this shrine, which has thousands of distinctive red torii gates twisting along a forest trail. It is distinctively Japanese, picturesque, and spiritual.

Japan.Japan Currency: Knowing the local currency is crucial when making travel plans to Japan. The Japanese Yen (JPY), represented by the sign ¥, is used in Japan. Banknotes come in quantities of 1,000, 2,000, 5,000, and 10,000 yen, while coins are available in denominations of 1, 5, 10, 50, 100, and 500 yen. ₹1 is currently worth about ¥1.69; therefore, while organising your trip to Japan, always check the most recent exchange rates. Being aware of your financial situation aids in better planning for the Japan trip cost, and everyday costs such as local travel, souvenirs, and Japanese food. There are plenty of locations in Japan that take cash rather than credit or debit cards, from the bustling markets of Tokyo to the cultural treasures of Kyoto. It makes sense to bring yen when visiting both well-known and lesser-known things to see in Japan. It's particularly helpful when going to neighbourhood restaurants or food stands selling authentic Japanese cuisine. Nubra Valley.Introduction: Former trading route that connected the eastern side of Tibet with Turkistan through the Karakoram Pass is the Nubra Valley. There are several Nubra Valley tour packages that take you on a gaiety ride over this unearthly place. Here is a list of few of them curated specially by our travel experts at Thomas Cook:
Nubra Valley.Most Popular Nubra Valley Packages: Tour Packages NameDurationPriceRemarkable Ladakh5 Nights 6 DaysRs. ₹36 600Marvellous Ladakh6 Nights 7 DaysRs. ₹42 100Ladakh - Buy 1 Get 1 Free6 Nights 7 DaysRs. ₹45 000 The Nubra Valley generally consists of both the Shyok and Nubra Valleys, their rivers rise from within the remote glaciers of these valleys and flow through the Karakoram Range. The Nubra Valley, hence, is the most beautiful sighting that you will come across in the entire region of Leh and Ladakh. There are a lot of locales you might want to view in this beautiful valley. Here are a few of them: Places to Visit Known for More Details Diskit Gompa Buddha Statue Situated around 120 km from Leh, this massive statue if Buddha is a part of the Buddhist Monastery Diskit Gompa Buddha Statue Magnetic Hill Though the science behind this area is unknown, it is home for the most magical phenomenon which is believed to take your vehicles uphill without any efforts from your side Magnetic Hill Ensa Gompa Constructed near the Panamik Village of Leh, the Ensa Gompa will be reached after a six-hour long trek from the village. It is 250 years old, and is a display of gorgeous Buddhist murals. Ensa Gompa White Sand Dunes Yet another wonderful place to explore in the Nubra Valley, the White Sand Dunes are a collection of white sand which makes it a perfect spot for clicking pictures White Sand Dunes Samstemling Gompa Located approximately one km away from the village of Sumur, this gompa is an abode of some valuable Buddhist idols and scrolls that have been preserved from a long time Samstemling Gompa Zimskang Museum This unpopular museum located in the alleys of Nubra is a home for the valuable memorabilia that are related to the ancient Silk Route Zimskang Museum Diskit Monastery Dating back to the 17th century, the Diskit Monastery is one of the largest and the oldest monasteries that exists in Nubra Diskit Monastery Tsolding Buddha Park This ‘no vehicle’ Buddha Park maintained by the Buddha Image Construction Project Committee, is a natural wonder. You can get some alluring views of the beautiful snow-capped mountains alongside some green landscapes that make the entire place beautiful. Tsolding Buddha Park Khardungla Pass Lying 80 km away from Hunder, this beautifully scenic place is a valley filled with raw-rocks and some narrow roads that pave way to the Shyok Valley. You can witness some gorgeous waterfalls on your way alongside rich wheat and barley fields. Khardungla Pass Disclaimer- The family discount mentioned for this destination is defined basis 2 adults & 1 child combination.

The city is home to several stunning attractions, like the open-air Hippodrome dating back to the Roman age. Also, while in Istanbul, don’t miss the Byzantine Hagia Sophia, a tall sixth-century dome, a significant landmark for every Turkey tour. It is advisable to select all-inclusive Turkey tour packages to ensure you don’t miss any of the exciting sights in Turkey. Goreme Goreme is one of the most popular Turkey tourist places you can visit. Located in the Cappadocia region of Central Turkey, it is a quaint town famous for the Goreme Open Air Museum. A must-see sight on your Turkey trip, Goreme has exotic cave churches and frescoes dating back to the 10th and 12th centuries. There’s also a quaint old castle, Uchisar, a fortification carved into a massive rock that offers stunning views from the peak. You may also want to check out the Pigeon Valley, an exciting trail dotted with pristine scenery, during your Turkey tour. The town boasts several exotic restaurants offering local cuisine, and the bartenders can always customise your drinks the way you like them. Find Turkey tour packages featuring Goreme on Thomas Cook. Kas Kas is one of the most beautiful Turkey tourist places, known for being a quiet and peaceful seaside attraction. Tucked away in the Antalya region, it dots the Mediterranean coastline of the country. Kas is one of the best places to visit in Turkey if you fancy snorkelling and watching marine life. This quaint town also has several diving schools with expert instructors who will teach you the basics of underwater diving and take you on guided deepwater trips. If this site piques your interest, book Turkey tour packages featuring Kas. Ephesus Ephesus, a UNESCO World Heritage Centre, is one of the best Turkey places to visit for historical tours. History lovers will enjoy visiting Ephesus, an hour’s drive from the city of Izmir, which hosts several cultural shows. Although Ephesus is in ruins today, it was once a centre of trade with the Grecians. You may also want to visit the Temple of Artemis and the Temple of Hadrian, besides the Basilica of St. John. A visit to Ephesus is a common inclusion in many Turkey holiday packages. Konya Konya is one of the most beautiful tourist destinations in Turkey that you can consider visiting. Once the capital of the Seljuk Turks, the city still boasts numerous mosques showcasing the brilliance of Seljuk architecture and their intricate tomb designs. Konya, commonly found in many popular Turkey tour packages from India, is also one of the best places in the country where you can experience the ceremony of Whirling Dervishes. Some major Turkey sightseeing places in Konya include the Mevlana Museum, Karatay Madrasa, Aziziye Mosque, Alauddin Kayqubad Mosque, Aladdin Hill Park, etc. If you can’t find Turkey holiday packages that include Konya, make sure to add the city to your customisable tour package plan. Pamukkale Pamukkale in Turkish means “cotton castle”, and the nickname relates to the whitewashed natural site that has hot springs. The spas used to attract people even during the 2nd century B.C., and the area is a UNESCO World Heritage Site. The mineral forests, waterfalls and terraced basins at this location are some of the most popular Turkey places tourists like to check out. It is recommended to hire a Turkey tour guide if you want to learn all about this location from a local’s perspective.
Turkey.Best hill stations/ best beaches in Turk: Here are some famous hill stations and beaches in Turkey that you should find included in most Turkey tour packages; Hill stations Trabzon Located beside the Black Sea, Trabzon is one of the best tourist places in Turkey. It is a fascinating hill station that should be a part of your Turkey tour. The Pontic Mountains pass through Trabzon, and trekkers and hikers love visiting this beautiful hill station in Turkey. The compelling silence prevailing throughout this area also attracts many tourists looking for a tranquil getaway in Turkey. The only place of habitation in this area is probably the Sumela Monastery, and a Greco-Roman Orthodox Ministry built way back in 360 AD. Also, check out the Ataturk Kosku Museum, built in memory of the founding fathers of Turkey, during your stay in Trabzon. You can easily find Turkey tour packages that include a short stay at this location. Bursa Bursa, also known as Yesil Bursa (green Bursa), is one of the most beautiful Turkey famous places you can visit. It has many green parks and lies surrounded by magnificent mountains. This mountainous terrain is an exciting tourist attraction. Tons of Turkey tour packages from India have this site included in their itinerary. If you visit Bursa in winter and are interested in skiing, see Mount Uludag, the famous ski resort. The place is also popular in the summer when the snow melts, and tourists can go up by the winch service. While in Bursa, ensure you don’t miss Cumalikizik, a village with remarkable cobblestone streets that will enchant you. Make sure to look for Turkey packages that include this site if you are hoping for a hill station visit. Beaches Kabak Beach It is one of the most verdant beaches with sparkling turquoise blue water, perfect for a soothing dip. The long stretches of white sand filled with pebbles of all sizes and shapes at this beach give it a unique charm. Besides its tranquil ambience, Kabak Beach is also one of the most sought-after Turkey famous places for a beachside vacation. Known as the hidden gem, the tourist crowd at Kabak Beach is quite slim, making it ideal for tourists looking for a secluded beachside location. You can find Kabak Beach as a standard inclusion in many Turkey packages. Kleopatra Beach You can enjoy a quiet afternoon at Kleopatra Beach, which is clean and has gentle waves lapping the shores at regular intervals. It is located in the Antalya region, roughly 120 kilometres from the main city centre of Antalya and just 2.5 kilometres from the Alanya settlement. The beach derives its name from the legendary queen Cleopatra, believed to have been bewitched by its beauty while sailing past. The stunning bay can captivate you and attract domestic and international tourists. If you can’t find Turkey packages that include a visit to Kleopatra Beach, try opting for customisable tour plans.
Turkey.Honeymoon destinations in Turkey: Here are some romantic places that are often featured in popular honeymoon special Turkey packages; Antalya Antalya is a vibrant resort city with plenty to offer, such as luxury resorts, romantic dinners, exuberant nightlife, colourful markets, charming old town, pristine beaches, fun cable car rides, and much more. You can easily find Turkey tour packages featuring Antalya. If not, you can surely add it to your trip itinerary with customisable Turkey tour packages for couples. Ankara Ankara, the capital of Turkey, is noted for its cafes, pubs, restaurants and cultural immersion. From spending quality time at Kugulu Park to enjoying a traditional Turkish Hammam experience, the city offers a lot. Ankara is also seen as a popular trip starting point in some Turkey packages. You’ll find plenty of Turkey honeymoon packages online that include this city. Bodrum Bodrum has gained a lot of popularity due to its stunning beaches and marinas. Honeymooners can easily find luxury accommodations in this city and explore its wonders on a rental scooter. The sunrise and sunset viewpoints in Bodrum are praiseworthy. The city also offers scopes for wine tours and water sports. If you are interested in Bodrum, make sure to look for honeymoon special Turkey tour packages that include this location.
Turkey.Interesting things to do in Turkey: If you are looking for some interesting things to do in Turkey during your visit, here are some popular options you can consider; Explore Ephesus Ephesus is an ancient 10th century BC built Greek city that now lies in Selcuk in Izmir Province, Turkey. The site houses well-preserved charming streets, grand buildings and remnants of schools and shops, shrines and more. The site is renowned for housing the Temple of Artemis, the Great Theatre and the Library of Celsus. Make sure to go for Turkey tour packages that feature a visit to this site. Enjoy a Turkish bath Experience the traditional Turkish bath at a hammam. Consider getting scrub and massage services before indulging in a calm, rejuvenating steam bath. You can find hammams in Istanbul and Bursa. Witness the architectural marvels in Istanbul The architectural brilliance and historical significance of structures in Istanbul, such as the grand Aqueduct of Valens and the magnificent Basilica Cistern, not only captivates the curious minds but also make for a great backdrop for photos. Other wonders across the city include the Hagia Sophia, Topkapi Palace, Blue Mosque, etc. Experience shopping at the local bazaars Besides being a visual treat, the colourful and maze-like bazaars around Turkey offer all kinds of goods for sale at reasonable rates, including spices, handmade crafts, leather items, food products, souvenirs and lots more. Book Turkey tour packages that give you sufficient time to experience these types of activities. Watch the sunrise from the top of Mt. Nemrut For more soothing experiences, don’t forget to witness the beautiful sunrise from the top of Mt. Nemrut. The glistening scenery as the golden sun rays reflect on the rocky surface of the mountain and its surrounding region is truly spellbinding. Most popular Turkey tour packages include Mt. Nemrut sightseeing.
Turkey.Adventurous things to do in Turkey: For thrillseekers, there are some wonderful things to do in Turkey, such as; Skiing adventures If you are visiting Turkey during the winter season, consider skiing adventures to enjoy its snow-covered landscapes in the best way possible. The gentle slopes and surrounding landscapes make skiing one of the most popular things to do in Turkey on a trip. Erciyes is the most notable place for this adventure. Some Turkey tour packages may feature this experience as a standard inclusion. Hot air balloon ride Many tourists visiting Turkey have this activity on their bucket list. It is a breathtaking experience to witness the majestic Turkish landscapes from the sky in a hot air balloon. Cappadocia is the most notable place where you can try hot air balloon adventures. Consider looking for Turkey holiday packages that include this activity first before you start to plan for it yourself. Surfing Individuals more inclined towards water adventures can try surfing in Turkey. The beautiful coastline, availability of both gentle and challenging waves and clear blue water make for an incredible surfing experience. You can try surfing activities in Gökçeada, Turkey. If you are interested in this activity, you can enquire in advance which Turkey packages feature it. Gület cruise On this cruise, you ride a Gület, a traditional wooden yacht, and explore the charming blue waters of the country’s southwestern coastline. While on this cruise, you can enjoy charming views, eat fresh-caught fish, enjoy on-deck music and sleep under the starry sky. You may or may not find this cruise as a standard inclusion in some Turkey packages. Hiking If you are looking for more on-land adventures, consider hiking a part of the Lycian Way. It is a popular and beautiful trekking path to Antalya from Fethiye, with charming villages, ancient ruins, beach towns, etc., along the way.
Turkey.What to eat in Turkey?: Apart from checking out the best available Turkey tour packages from India, make sure to research the local Turkish delicacies. Some popular choices are; Piyaz Piyaz is a unique salad available in Antalya that is made with Candir, a smaller version of the old butter bean grown in the region by the same name. Exotic scent Candir is cooked with tahini, a little water, salt, vinegar, lime juice, and garlic. Olive oil is added to get the perfect finish. During your Turkey tour, make sure to look for and try the traditional version of Piyaz, where chopped soft-boiled egg is mixed into the dish prior to serving. Make your Turkey visit a wonderful experience by opting for Turkey tour packages by Thomas Cook. Ezo Gelin Corba This delicious dish has a very interesting legend tied to its origin and is a highlight of Turkey tours and travels. It is a savoury soup containing domato salca (tomato puree), red lentils, finely chopped onions, and tomatoes and served with a sprinkle of chilli flakes and dried mint to add flavour. Believed to be originating from a small village near Turkey’s Gaziantep City, Ezogelin Corba has become a favourite of both locals and tourists. Shakshuka Shakshuka is a traditional Turkish side dish with zucchini, eggplant, chilli, and tomatoes. Most of the vegetarian dishes in Turkey are in the Mediterranean style, cooked in olive oil, known as zeytinyagli yemegi in local parlance. Depending on where you try Shakshuka in Turkey, you will find some variations in its seasoning and use of different chillies. Shakshuka is a very delectable dish that you shouldn’t miss out on during your Turkey tour. Kisir Kisir is a famous Turkish salad made from tomatoes, parsley, garlic, mint, and a generous touch of broken bulgur wheat. Each region in Turkey has its version of Kisir. Still, the one available in Antalya tops the list because of the additional ingredients of nar eksisi (fermented pomegranate molasses) and pul biber (red chilli flakes) that give it a hot and spicy twist which you can enjoy during your Turkey tours and travels. Mercimek Kofte Mercimek Kofte is a vegetarian delicacy that is also known as belluh. It is made from broken bulgur wheat, red lentils, salt, finely chopped tomatoes and onions, scallions, red pepper paste (aci biber salca), and cilantro. Mercimek Kofte is served as small, easy-to-bite pieces and makes a great afternoon snack. It is best eaten with a twist of lemon and rolled onto a fresh lettuce leaf. Even if you don’t find Mercimek Kofte included under meals in the popular Turkey tour packages from India, make sure to try it out by yourself. Yaprak Dolma Yapkrak Dolma comes in both vegetarian and non-vegetarian versions. The Isparta, vegetarian version is made with rice cooked with ripe tomatoes and parsley, finely chopped onions, garlic, tomato puree, black pepper, salt, water, and olive oil. The heady mixture is a filler rolled onto a vine leaf. The non-vegetarian version has minced meat added to the above ingredients before cooking.
Turkey.Best Indian Restaurants in Turkey: Besides browsing through the available Turkey holiday packages, individuals should also note down the following Indian restaurants in the country they can consider visiting; Dubb Indian Restaurant While on your Turkey tours and travels, consider checking out the Dubb Indian Restaurant, located at a minute’s walking distance from the heart of Istanbul. It is one of the best places in Turkey where you can try delicious Indian dishes. The place remains operational from 12 AM to 10:30 PM, serving a mouthwatering range of Indian dishes, such as Biryani, Paranthas, Butter Chicken, etc. Besides its good food quality, the restaurant is also quite affordable, with an approximate dining cost for two individuals being TRY 100 to 300. Musafir Indian Restaurant The Musafir Indian Restaurant in Istanbul is another well-known restaurant you can check out during your Turkey tour. The elegant indoor and outdoor seating, mixed with great service and warm ambience, makes it a fine choice in the city. Serving customers since 2004, the Musafir Indian Restaurant is also a very budget-friendly option with an approximate cost of TRY 50 to 100 per person. Masalaa Magiic Indian Restaurant The Masalaa Magiic Indian Restaurant is another great restaurant that you will find in Istanbul, Turkey. Located at a 10-minute drive from the city centre, the place is known for its traditional home-cooked style meal, pleasant ambience and prompt service. It is the perfect place to go to for all your Indian food cravings at a reasonable price. You can eat at this restaurant for as low as TRY 10 to TRY 50. Make sure to try their kebab and butter chicken during your visit.
Turkey.Shopping in Turkey: If you are confused about what gifts and souvenirs to purchase when shopping in Turkey, check out the following options; Turkish Blue Evil Eye Amulet Locally called Nazar Boncuk, the Turkish Blue Evil Eye Amulet is a popular market item that you can purchase during your Turkey tours and travels. It is known for bringing good luck and protecting one from mishaps. Besides, its beautiful blue colour looks great as pendants, bracelets or casual home/office decor. Where to find: Some of Turkey's best places to visit for buying Nazar Boncuk are the Grand Bazaar, Diva Bazaar, and Beyoglu El Sanatlari Evi. Rugs and carpets The traditional Turkish handmade rugs and carpets can also be an interesting purchase as gifts or for personal use. Made with high-quality materials like bamboo fibre, cotton, silk, etc., these vibrant-coloured rugs and carpets are also offered with shipping options in case you are unable to carry them with you. Where to find: The local markets and shops in Istanbul are a great location for buying Turkish rugs and carpets during your Turkey tour. Turkish mosaic lamps If you are looking for home decor items, nothing can beat the beautiful and intricately designed Turkish mosaic lamps. These lamps can be ideal as both gifts or souvenirs and will surely be the centre of attraction in any room where they are placed. Where to find: The Kapali Carsi marketplace in Istanbul is a well-known place where you can buy high-quality mosaic lamps while on your Turkey tours and travels. Coffee and tea sets Tea and coffee sets are available all across the markets and stores in Turkish cities. These are some of the most frequently bought items by tourists during their Turkey tours and travels. These intricately designed sets will surely highlight your tea/coffee time back at home and can be an elegant way to welcome guests. Where to find: One of the best places in Turkey where you can buy tea and coffee sets is the Kemeralti Bazaar in Izmir.
Turkey.Interesting facts about Turkey: Here are some fun and interesting facts about Turkey that you should know about prior to your trip; Istanbul extends to two continents: The residents of Istanbul can claim that they are Asians by night and Europeans during the day or vice versa. The city is divided by the Bosphorus Straits, putting part of the city in Asia and part of it in Europe. However, most of Istanbul (95%) is in Asia, and the rest (5%) is in Europe. The capital of Turkey is Ankara, not Istanbul: Istanbul used to be the capital city of three empires, Byzantine, Rome and Ottoman. However, contrary to popular belief, it is not the current capital of Turkey as that title is held by Ankara. Istanbul was earlier known as Byzantium: You may have heard of the Byzantine Empire, originating from the Greek word Byzantium. The term refers to Byzas, the founder of Istanbul, which was known as Byzantium when it was established. The name later changed to Constantinople. In 1930, the city got its current name – Istanbul. Turks Love Tea More than Coffee: You may have heard of Turkish brew too frequently, which generally refers to coffee. However, the truth is that over 95% of the population prefers to drink tea. Don’t forget to try it yourself during your Turkey tours and travels. Turkey is the primary source of Hazelnuts: An interesting fact about Turkey is that it produces around 75% of the total hazelnuts in the world. Turkey has 82,693 Mosques: Although Saudi Arabia and Pakistan top the list with the most significant number of mosques, Turkey also boasts 82,693 mosques at last count. One of the biggest and best is the Sultanahmet Mosque, popularly known as the Blue Mosque of Istanbul. Serving Turkish Tea: As mentioned earlier, the Turkish drink more tea than coffee. Serving tea is steeped in tradition and is followed more like a ritual that shows the host’s hospitality. Don’t be surprised if a Turkish family welcomes you with a hot cup of tea, which means they like you and are trying to be friendly. Oil Wrestling: One of Turkey's most exciting and action-packed traditions is oil wrestling, which takes place in every corner of the country. This traditional sport can be dated back to Mesopotamia, where it originated. History has it that the sport started in 1362 as the exciting Kirkpinar oil wrestling championship in Erdine. The wrestlers soak themselves in oil and try to grapple with each other, and the many slips and falls add to the fun element. The end of June is the best time to go Turkey to witness this traditional sport.
Turkey.Turkey Culture: The culture of Turkey is very diverse and includes various elements derived from other cultures like Central Asia, the Middle East, Eastern Europe, etc. A normal handshake is often given when meeting someone new in Turkey, while a kiss or two on the cheek is the common way to greet close family members and friends. Food in Turkey is a crucial aspect of the local culture, and its wastage is highly frowned upon. The residents of Turkey are very hospitable, and frequent get-togethers are very normal in the local culture. Reading coffee cups after finishing the beverage is a well-known practice in Turkey. The dressing style in Turkey is a very interesting and stylish one. The local men usually go for clothes with sombre colours, with popular styles being baggy trousers and waistcoats. The local women, especially in the village areas, prefer the traditional attire. Weddings in Turkey are grand occasions marked by elaborate ceremonies, rituals and celebrations. The Turkish population observes and celebrates a wide number of traditional events and festivals, including the holy month of Ramadan.
Turkey.Turkey visa for Indian tourists: Indian tourists will need to apply for and acquire a visa for their Turkey tours and travels. Though Indian passport holders are not eligible for the Turkey visa on arrival option, they can easily opt for the eVisa feature. The validity of this visa is up to 30 days, which is sufficient for more trip plans. You can easily get your Turkey visa from the online visa services offered by Thomas Cook. Just visit the official website, choose the destination country, type of visa, and branch and provide the communication details. You will then get the quote, after which you can proceed with the online payment and document submission process. After this, you will receive your Turkey visa very soon.
Turkey.Foreign currency for Turkey visit: The official Turkey currency is called the Turkish Lira, denoted by the code TRY and sign ₺. For your Turkey trip, you will need the local Turkey currency for your expenditures in the country. As of now, the 1 Turkish Lira is equivalent to 2.22 INR. You can easily buy forex for your Turkey trip via the online forex services offered by Thomas Cook. Just go to the official website, choose the currency type, check rate (Forex card or cash), and forex amount and provide your contact details to get a quote. After that, choose a delivery method and complete the online payment. Once complete, you will obtain your Forex order receipt along with your foreign currency within the specified time.

Adventurous things to do on your Munnar trip
Munnar.Introduction: When planning a vacation, it is pivotal to choose a location that befits your holiday needs. If you are looking for a vacation in a relatively cold place in the southern parts of India with diverse culture and tons of activities to do, Munnar in Kerala is the place for you. Munnar is a quintessential hill station lined with velvety green tea plantations on either side of winding elevated roads. The hills of Munnar look like manicured gardens straight out of a picture book. There are endless reasons to visit Munnar, and the picturesque hill station is one of the top tourist destinations in south India. A plethora of budget to luxurious hotels waits to greet tourists from all around the world. The hill station is perched at an elevation of 1600 m above sea level with an exquisite view all around no matter which way you turn. Munnar is one of the première producers of tea and spices in India and the heavenly scent wafting in the air is yet another major attraction of the hillside holiday destination in Kerala. If you are booking a Munnar holiday, you'll be happy to know that the base price of Munnar tour packages is from as little as ₹13,300 per head. And for that, you get to have 3 nights and 4 days of fun in Munnar with a hotel, meals, and sightseeing all conveniently packed in. For a slightly different experience, Hills of Kerala is a 4-day tour of both Munnar and Thekkady for ₹14,000. You will adore the flexibility of these Munnar packages, be it a short visit or a long holiday, there's something that matches your mood and pocket. Now, if you are planning a slightly longer Munnar holiday, the Kerala Delight (5 nights, 6 days including Munnar, Thekkady, Alleppey and Kochi) begins at ₹36,300 post discount. If you want something more opulent, Marvellous Kerala has you experience Munnar, Alleppey and Kovalam over 6 days, starting at ₹26,200. What you will enjoy is the fact that most of these Munnar tour packages provide EMI as low as ₹834 a month. In addition, you gain loyalty points on all your bookings, so your subsequent trip becomes even more delightful. If you prefer a mix of hills, backwaters and beaches, options such as Vibrant Kerala and Enchanting Kerala (6 days) begin at about ₹20,000. Frankly, you'll have a choice to select from for your ideal Munnar tour. Where would you like to go first? Browsing through Munnar tour packages can be quite a daunting task as there is so much to see and do. But don’t worry as we have got you covered in our Munnar holiday package.
Munnar.Most Popular Munnar Tour Packages 2025: Below are the most popular Munnar Tour Packages: Tour Packages NameDurationPriceKerala - Munnar Calling3 Nights 4 DaysRs. ₹17 300Hills Of Kerala3 Nights 4 DaysRs. ₹17 900Kerala Delight5 Nights 6 DaysRs. ₹32 000Simply Kerala3 Nights 4 DaysRs. ₹18 200Vibrant Kerala5 Nights 6 DaysRs. ₹20 000
Munnar.Best time to visit Munnar: A holiday destination is at its finest during the peak seasons. When planning a trip to the hills of Kerala, it is important to know which season makes it the best time to visit the tourist places in Munnar. Before you book your Munnar trip package, here is a detailed weather guide for you to choose the best time to visit this scenic and breathtaking hill station. Thanks to its idyllic location and pleasant climate all year round, you can visit Munnar anytime you have a vacation. You can book your Munnar holiday packages based on your weather and temperature preferences as well as the availability of your vacation days. All you need to do is take into account the following climatic details and pack accordingly. December to February: winter is hands down the best time to visit Munnar . The temperatures range between 10 and 15 degrees Celsius during this time of the year. The cold weather of the hills coupled with the chill of winter in a relatively hot southern state adds immensely to the thrills of your vacation. You can wake up to a gorgeous sunrise on the horizon and a thick layer of mist covering the streets and tea plantations. The entire experience makes the air feel so fresh that it is a treat to the senses. A hot cup of Munnar tea or coffee while gazing at the spectacular view outside rounds up the trip with a sense of tranquility. Winter is also the best time to enjoy the lush green outdoors of Munnar. The beauty of the scenery around the hill stations grows tenfold when the sun is peeping to illuminate the healthy foliage. You can go for a hike or take a walk around town, explore the cuisine in the restaurants in Munnar, and go sightseeing. No matter what you choose to do, the favorable climate in winter coupled with the exquisite scenery makes the Munnar trip all the more delightful. March to May: when winter passes and spring has bid adieu shortly after, it is time to reign in the summers in Munnar. Halfway through March the temperatures start to rise in Munnar and may vary between 19 and 35 degrees Celsius. If you do not like chilly weather and are planning a honeymoon in Munnar, this might be the best time to visit. There are no school schedules or religious holidays during this time, as a result of which the hill station is less crowded due to tourists and locals being busy with their daily routines. It is the ideal time to score great discounts on flights and accommodations as well as take trips to the best places to see in Munnar. Adventure activities in Munnar are also available for reasonable prices during the summer months. Carry some light clothes and explore activities in Munnar without thick crowds. The cafes in Munnar are also pretty vacant so you can enjoy quick service and personalized attention from March to May, which makes it the best time to visit Munnar. June to September: Monsoons in hill stations are a special type of experience that is extremely enjoyable but rare. Luckily, June to September is monsoon season in Munnar and the region experiences frequent and unexpected showers. So, if you plan your Munnar trip during this time, you will be greeted by surprising rain showers that make the vacation feel like a movie. While it may be hard to get around on foot at this time, you can always wait out the rains in the restaurants or cafes in Munnar, sipping on hot beverages and cherishing rainy day Munnar foods. The indigenous cuisine of Munnar includes bajjis, which are variants of fritters that are perfect for rainy days. The rain brings out the true beauty of Munnar's landscape. If you are someone who enjoys photography, peace, and serenity, then this is the best time to visit Munnar. You can capture the exquisite glamour of Munnar tourist spots and create valuable memories with your loved ones. This is also a good time to plan Munnar tourism packages for your honeymoon. The cosiness in the air and the lush scenery, the warmth of the indoors, and the invaluable company make it such a treat for couples. You could plan your Munnar trip packages multiple times a year to truly appreciate the hillside destination for everything it has to offer. The idyllic setting of the places to visit in Munnar is the perfect backdrop for solo holidays or a vacation with your loved ones.
Munnar.How to reach Munnar?: Munnar is one of the primary holiday destinations in the southern parts of India. However, reaching the peak of the hill station only allows one means of transport; the bus. Of course, you can take a flight or train to Kerala and plan your travel from the airport or railway station to your destination on the rolling hills. By air The closest airport to Munnar is the Cochin International Airport which facilitates flights not only from around India but also from other countries. It is located approximately 110 kilometres from the hill station, a journey that can be covered by bus or cab. The Madurai international airport is also located close by at a distance of 140 kilometers from Munnar. The rest of the distance to reach the destination must be covered by road. By railway Although reaching Munnar directly via train is not an option, you can travel to the nearest railway station which is Aluva. These railway lines connect different states to the city of Kerala. From there you can catch a bus to the Munnar bus station directly or choose a more comfortable journey via private cabs. By road No matter which form of transportation you choose, road travel is the best way to reach Munnar comfortably and promptly. Not only is it a cost-saving option but you also experience the complete panoramic views of the hill station before you reach the summit. Travelling within Munnar Munnar does not have a well-connected public transport facility. Hence most people stick to cabs, tour buses, or self-drive options during their visit to Munnar. You may find a few auto rickshaws here and there, but they are scarce and travel to only limited places. By now we are sure you must be eager to visit Munnar. And why not, this idyllic hill station hidden away in Kerela is nothing less than a fantasy. To help you book a memorable holiday, we have crafted some exciting Munnar packages. You can choose any based-on inclusions, budget, itinerary, etc. And if you like, we would be happy to customize a package based on your personal preferences
Munnar.Best honeymoon destinations and packages: As soon as you think of Kerala, the lush green and sprawling landscape of Munnar conjures up in your mind. The beauty and scintillating weather of this charming hill station in Southern India is very impressive, making it the most loved destination in Kerala and one of the finest in the entire country. A highly famous place and for very good reason too, Munnar is a breathtaking town located in Kerala, which is known for its stunning cardamom, tea, and coffee plantations. Preferred by tourists from across the globe, it is an all-year-round travel destination, thanks to its rosy climate and delightful setting. Taking just a single look at its serene environs can tell you what a popular honeymoon destination in India it is. The romantic backdrop of rolling hills speckled with numerous enchanting woodlots and delightful wildlife that grace them spells magic on you. The place is also well-known for lovely treehouses, several national parks, myriad vantage viewpoints, and dreamy boat rides that you can enjoy here profusely. For the more adventurous too, Munnar has a lot in tow, with the likes of many scavenger hunts, trekking, rope climbing, and camping opportunities to name a few. Here are some of the top romantic places that you must include in your itinerary when planning your Munnar packages for couples: The blossom hydel park Eravikulam national park Echo point Photo point Lockhart gap Mattupetty dam Attukal waterfalls Kolukkumalai Tea Estate The Blossom Hydel Park - if your Munnar travel packages are intended for a honeymoon, then you cannot go wrong with the blossom hydel park. Taking a walk along the serene pathways lined by carefully manicured shrubs and bushes is almost a spiritual experience. It is also a destination for adventure activities in Munnar. You can take a boat ride along the Muthirapuzha River or simply explore the banks of the water body in its finesse. Eravikulam National Park - a thrilling adventure for couples on their honeymoon in Munnis to explore the national park to come across several indigenous species in their natural habitat. A unique experience is spotting the Nilgiri Tahr, but that's not all. The Eravikulam national park is also home to several rare varieties of butterflies and home to rolling tea gardens, crystal waterfalls, and mysterious forests. Attracting through the national park hand in hand with your spouse comes with a promise to strengthen your body found in adventure. Echo Point - who doesn't enjoy hearing their own words reflected back by natural phenomena? It is just as exciting as an adult as it was when we were children. Echo Point is among the most spectacular places to visit in Munnar with scenic views of the panoramic western ghats. Have a picnic lunch surrounded by nature or hike up to the top of the hill for a unique view that you will never forget. Photo Point - if you are looking for opportunities to capture memories from your Munnar trip, don't miss the Photo Point in your Munnar travel package itinerary. Aside from the beautiful weather and scenic views, it is a perfect place to unwind for solo trips, honeymoons, or Munnar packages for family. Take a few selfies or capture the essence of the surroundings for a viral post on social media. Lockhart Gap - another spectacular destination that you cannot miss when planning your Munnar travel packages for a honeymoon is Lockhart Gap. It is the perfect place to witness the glory of sunrise or sunset while being surrounded by mist and mystery. If you are planning a solo trip to Munnar, or traveling with your friends, family, and even a spouse, hiking along the Lockhart Gap is an enlightening experience for all. Mattupetty Dam - one of the major tourist attractions in Munnar is the Mattupetty Dam. The proximity to tracking grounds across Shola forests and connectivity to the indo swiss farm project are some of the popular reasons that make the Mattupetty Dam a must-visit place for Munnar I could just for the family. If you are looking for adventure activities in Munnar, consider speed boating at Mattupetty Lake or just sit by the water and listen to the serenity of the natural sound. The rolling hills that surround the dam also add to the aesthetic appeal of this famous tourist place to visit in Munnar. Attukal Waterfalls - on your honeymoon trip to Munnar, don't forget to visit Attukal waterfalls. Not only will the natural scenery take your breath away but splashing around in the water or enjoying a picnic by the side can be an extremely romantic experience. The first mountain path was also carved around the Attukal waterfalls. Therefore, a trek can be an especially enlightening, fun, and adventurous activity in Munnar with your spouse. Kolukkumalai Tea Estate – you cannot make a list of places to visit in Munnar and not add the tea estate. The lush rolling hills are divine with the scent of fresh tea leaves. The long stretches of tea gardens surrounded by all things green are some of the perfect tourist places in Munnar. The Kolukkumalai tea estate is one of the highly sought-after places to visit in Munnar as it is located at the highest altitude. The tea gardens provide a spectacular view of the surroundings around the hill station, making it a coveted spot for photography and making memories. These world-class tea plantations are one of the most popular on the list of places to visit in Munnar. Hence, it is recommended to ensure that the Munnar packages you have shortlisted include a visit to these tea plantations.
Munnar.Best places to visit Munnar: The hill station is blessed with 360-degree panoramic views, a gorgeous valley that is home to many explored and unexplored treasures. You can either choose from one of our existing Munnar tour packages or get a customizable one, depending on what places you would like to visit. You can start your tour by visiting the architectural wonder aka the Mattupetty Dam. Here you can enjoy some thrilling water sports. You can then head towards Kundala Lake for a relaxing boat ride or visit the picturesque echo point and enjoy spectacular views of Munnar. You cannot miss out on a visit to the famous Tata Tea Museum, where you can sample hundreds of varieties of tea and spices and stock up on the ones you love. If you are a fan of wildlife, you could enjoy an adventurous safari at the Eravikulam National Park, or elephant rides at the elephant sanctuary. Munnar has so much to offer to its visitors; each place will leave you mesmerized and wanting more. Eravikulam National Park: This one you cannot miss if you're an animal and nature lover. This place offers you trekking routes, serene nature walks, and opportunities to sight wildlife such as the Nilgiri Tahr. Do not forget to take your camera along; photography here is pure joy. This park is a must-have in most good Munnar packages because it's indispensable. Echo Point: You'll enjoy this if you're a fan of playful outdoor locations. You can hike, go boating, or simply camp here with buddies. When you yell, your voice comes back echoing. Ideal for a peaceful picnic too, so don't forget to add it to your Munnar tour packages. Mattupetty Dam: A picturesque location where you can attempt bird watching and boating on tranquil waters. You will adore sitting beside the reservoir, soaking in the peace. If you opt for appropriate Munnar packages, you will have the opportunity to explore nearby dairy farms too. Elephant Lake: Go for a trek around here, and you may find elephants moving in their natural habitat. It's peaceful and perfect for a picnic. All who visit with Munnar tour packages describe this as one of their most relaxed halts. Tata Tea Museum: You will be able to walk amidst tea gardens, attempt your hand at tea plucking and experience various fresh tea varieties. For you who like local culture, it is a nice addition to any Munnar packages. Kundala Lake: You'll experience a serene shikara ride and boating on the transparent waters with surrounding green hills. This place offers the most romantic atmosphere in your Munnar packages. Top Station: You can't possibly leave Munnar without visiting here. You get a rare perspective where cottony clouds kiss a bluish valley below. If you time your Munnar tour packages just right, you might even spot the once-in-12-years Neelakurinji flowers! Camping and photography here are unbeatable. Devikulam: If you prefer peaceful adventures, this one's for you. Hike, fish or even rock climb here. And don't miss the ancient Ganesh temple hidden within. Perfect place to include in customised Munnar tour packages. Lakkam Waterfalls: A hidden gem with waterfalls set amidst dense forests and tea gardens. If trekking is on your Munnar packages, this is ideal. Wonder Valley Adventure & Amusement Park: If you need some enjoyment and excitement, this park is full of adventure activities just right for a respite from sightseeing. Attukad Waterfalls: Hidden away in the forest, this is a great photography spot as well as a light trekking location. Be sure to bring a small picnic, you'll find yourself spending hours just sitting at the falls enjoying your food and watching the water. Anamudi Peak: This is the highest point in South India. If trekking is in your wheelhouse, you’ll be rewarded with views of rivers, spice plantations and tea gardens below. The best time to visit is in the morning when the mist is still lingering.
Munnar.Best hill stations to visit on your Munn: Apart from the spectacular green hills of Munnar, you can also plan trips to nearby hill stations when planning your Munnar tourist packages. Here are some of the best hill stations apart from Munnar tourist spots that you don’t want to miss: Wayanad Malampuzha Ooty Vagamon Peerumedu Thekkady
Munnar.The Best Foods to Eat in Munnar: As a state with diverse religions and cultures, Kerala sports multi-cuisine, and as a holiday destination that hosts tourists from all over the world, the best restaurants in Munnar serve different kinds of delicacies that will leave your mouth watering. There are veg restaurants in Munnar where you can experience Munnar food at its finest as well as nonvegetarian cafes and restaurants that serve the most delicate meat preparations you can think of. Here are some of the must-have foods in Munnar: Kerala biryani India has the most delectable preparations of biryanis, but each state has its own recipe. When planning your Munnar holiday packages, make sure to add Kerala biryani to your itinerary. We know it is not a location but the search for the delicious concoction of rice and me or vegetables is an adventure that you will love. Appam with vegetable or meat stew Kerala's special variation of fluffy dosas is best enjoyed with a stew or gravy of your choice. If you like meat, opt for muttons or beef stew for a perfect balance of spices. You can also visit veg restaurants in Munnar, especially during the rainy season, to treat your senses with appam and vegetable stew. Idiyappam with mutton gravy Kerala also has its very own variation of pasta. Idiyappam, which is made from rice flour dough carefully prepared into noodle-like strands, is a mouthwatering delicacy best served with mutton curry or mixed vegetables. Malabar parottas with choice of curry Go to any cafe in Munnar and try the Malabar parottas with Kerala chicken curry or vegetable curry. This particular Munnar food is enjoyable during any season of the year. Prawn curry Munnar as a hill station surrounded by seaside villages on the Malabar coast offers a fresh supply of seafood, especially prawns. The unique preparation that involves coconut milk, curry leaves, jaggery, and a handful of locally grown spices will tease your tongue with the prawn curry. Beef curry Munnar food is big on using beef as one of the primary ingredients. The tender pieces of meat cooked in a delicious combination of local spices emanates a heavenly smell that will leave you wanting more. Ari Pathri with chicken masala Another indigenous variety of chapati available in Munnar made of rice flour is Ari Pathri. These thin Indian flatbreads served with chicken curry or chicken masala are one of the coveted things to eat when enjoying your Munnar travel packages. Puttu and Kadala Curry: This one's filling. You'll receive steamed coconut layers of rice cake (puttu) accompanied by mildly spiced black chickpea curry (kadala). Whatever your itinerary includes, visiting waterfalls or simply meandering through tea plantations on your Munnar trip, this will keep you energised for good. Parotta and Beef Fry: If you're a fan of strong flavours, you'll enjoy this flaky parotta with hot beef fry. It's got that 'once more' ingredient, something that is always advised to tourists on a Munnar tour package to at least attempt. Banana Fritters (Pazham Pori): Sweet, crunchy and soft within, with ripe bananas coated in rice flour batter. Ideal snack for those damp Munnar nights when you're unwinding after checking off places from your Munnar tour packages. Kappa and Meen Curry: While you're in Munnar, you'll see Kappa and Meen Curry all over, consisting of soft-boiled tapioca (kappa) with spicy and tangy fish curry (meen curry). It's comfort food for the locals, and a flavour bomb for you. It makes the perfect meal after a long trek or nature walk, which are both a part of many Munnar tour packages. Ela Ada: You will love this sweet snack, rice dough, stuffed with coconut and jaggery, and steam-cooked in banana leaves. It smells heavenly and is savoured nicely with an evening cup of tea after a long day of wonder during a Munnar tour package. Unniyappam: These deep-fried balls of rice, jaggery and banana are sweet, soft within and crisp outside. Push a few into your tea breaks in Munnar's hill cafes.
Munnar.What to shop in Munnar?: Shopping in Munnar is just as much an adventure as strolling along the trekking trails. You can go shopping in Munnar while exploring the tea gardens, the town, or even the restaurants in Munnar. Here are some of the things that you simply must acquire during your Munnar trip: Fresh tea leaves from the tea estates in our local bazaar. Coffee grounds Homemade chocolates Locally curated peppercorns, cloves, cinnamon sticks, and cardamom. Aromatic essential oils Handcrafted home décor Handmade greeting cards and other paper-based products Traditional Kerala clothing is dyed with natural colors. There are several plantations and local shops where you can procure these items for an authentic experience of shopping in Munnar. However, these icons are also available at the local mall and shopping centre where you can find everything in one location.
Munnar.Things to do in Munnar: Munnar offers its eager visitors a host of outdoor, indoor, unique, and memorable activities. Those who love the outdoors and want to explore the natural landscape of the region can go on treks and hikes through the rugged yet scenic terrain of Munnar or spend a night camping under a starlit sky, trading stories and sharing meals by a cosy bonfire. For a truly unique experience, you can spend a night at a luxurious yet rustic tree house. You can enjoy the gorgeous sunsets and have a romantic moonlit evening in complete isolation with your loved one. And in the morning wake up as the first ray of light hits your face and enjoy uninterrupted views of the morning skies. And if that's not enough, you can enjoy a much-needed spa day at an authentic Ayurvedic spa centre, which will leave you refreshed and rejuvenated. While you are in Munnar, do not forget to try the mouth-watering home-cooked meal serving delicious local cuisine. All given above also qualifies Munnar as a complete family holiday destination. We have crafted some fascinating Munnar family tour packages to make your family tour to Munnar memorable.
Munnar.Adventurous things to do on your Munnar: Aside from spending time at some of the top Munnar resorts for couples or going on quiet picnics, there are still tons of adventure activities in Munnar. Here are some of the activities in Munnar that appeal to thrill seekers: Mountain biking along the hairpin bends of the Western Ghats ATV biking in the forests and sandy terrains Rock climbing with a professional spotter Rappelling along the rocky mountainsides River crossing with ropeways Speed boating on the calm waters Bamboo rafting across the riverbeds Jeep safaris through the forestlands Trekking along the beautiful trails surrounded by lush forests. Elephant safari through the National park There are several other mind-blowing activities in Munnar that will raise your adrenaline and take your breath away. If you like a bit of rush in your veins, make sure to add these activities in Munnar to your itinerary when planning your exquisite Munnar travel packages.
Munnar.Interesting facts about Munnar: This beautiful hill station which served as a summer retreat in the colonial era still serves as a wonderful destination to escape the heat proactively. Although the best time to visit this great Keralite destination is from September to May, it is apt for being visited any time of the year to enjoy the sightseeing of naturally alluring sites like Nilgiritahr, the many waterfalls, pick up great souvenirs like cardamom-flavoured green tea and enjoy the authentic Malabar cuisine. Here are some interesting facts about Munnar that will help you plan your itinerary for travel packages to Munnar with more consideration: Munnar was once inhabited by several varieties of flora and diverse classes of fauna. However, with the growing tourism and expanding civilization, that's from indigenous animals and birds have migrated away. Some of the native flora has also started to regress in the area. One of the rare varieties of orchids known as the Neelakurinchi that blooms every 12 years is found in the green hills of Munnar. The hill station is located approximately 1600 meters above sea level, therefore the temperature is always regulating itself from warm to chilly. The hills of Munnar sprout where three rivers namely the Nallathanni, the Mudhirazupha, and the Kundaly come together. This is where the name of the hill station is derived from. Munnar is often called the Kashmir of southern India.

Interesting things to do in Phuket
Phuket.Introduction: Soak up the sun in Phuket this winter with our exclusive Buy 1 Get 1 Free offer for your perfect escape. Phuket is one of Thailand’s southern provinces, comprising the island of Phuket and around 32 other small islands off its coast. Phuket Island is the largest island in the country, featuring exceptional sites of tourist attractions and unique experiences that draw large tourist crowds from all over the world. In addition to the island's unscathed natural beauty, it is known around the globe for its vibrant culture, exuberant nightlife scene, bustling markets, availability of fun activities and adventure and more. One of the most iconic parts of Phuket tourism is that it is home to over 30 pristine beaches in Thailand. Whether it's about enjoying a party-like environment for cheap at Patong Beach or spending some quality time while enjoying the seafront views with the family at Kata Beach, Phuket is well-equipped to cater to all kinds of tourism needs. Besides, urban exploration on foot is one of the key tourism elements that tourists enjoy in Phuket. Exploring and experiencing the thriving nightlife and busy street markets on this Thai island is bound to put a smile on your face. Also, due to its geographical location, Phuket has become a major hub for day trips in Thailand, a few popular ones being a trip to Phi Phi Islands, a boat trip to Phang Nga Bay, and so on. Apart from Phuket sightseeing, Phuket is popular for hosting some of the most iconic events and festivals in Thailand, like Loi Krathong, Vegetarian Festival, Songkran, etc. Attending such events can be ideal for tourists who want to experience the local culture and traditions up close.
Phuket.Most Popular Phuket Tour Packages: Tour Packages NameDurationPricePhuket - Buy 1 Get 1 Free4 Nights 5 DaysRs. ₹30 300Wonderful Thailand - Honeymoon Special5 Nights 6 DaysRs. ₹52 400Simply Krabi And Phuket5 Nights 6 DaysRs. ₹60 600Dazzling Thailand7 Nights 8 DaysRs. ₹51 100Blissful Thailand - Honeymoon Special7 Nights 8 DaysRs. ₹61 300
Phuket.Best Time to visit Phuket: Season Months Weather Pros Cons Peak Season December to March Average temperature: 24-32°C, low humidity. Clear blue sky. Pleasant weather, clear skies, calm sea, ideal for outdoor activities High prices, crowded tourist spots. Shoulder Season April to May, August, November April-May: Hot (27-36°C); August, November: moderate rain. Less crowded, discounts on accommodations and flights. Variable weather conditions; hot temperatures in April and May. Low Season June, July, September, October Heavy rainfall. Lowest prices, fewer tourists. Heavy rain limits outdoor activities and watersports.
Phuket.How to reach Phuket: Mode of Transport Details Pros Cons By Air The most comfortable way to travel to Phuket from India. Direct and multi-stop flights available from major Indian cities like Delhi, Mumbai, and Hyderabad. Quick (e.g., 4 hours 35 minutes from New Delhi), widely available, convenient. Can be expensive depending on the season; additional travel time from the airport to Phuket Town. By Road Not viable from India. However, it is possible from other Thai cities like Bangkok and Pattaya via taxi, bus, or rental car. BKS bus services take around 13 hours from Bangkok. Affordable, flexible schedule for buses, opportunity to see scenic routes. Long travel time (e.g., 13 hours from Bangkok), less comfortable than flying. By Waterway Accessible if you're on a Thailand Cruise or from nearby islands like Phi Phi via ferries or speedboats. Ferry from Phi Phi to Phuket takes 2-2.5 hours. Unique travel experience, scenic views, variety of options (ferries, speedboats). Limited availability based on weather and schedule; can be slower and less predictable. This table provides a summary of different transportation options to reach Phuket from India or nearby locations, including their pros and con.
Phuket.Places to visit in Phuket: When it comes to sightseeing, Phuket has tons of renowned tourist places and attractions to offer. So, if you are visiting Phuket on your next international vacation, consider checking out these famous tourist places; Big Buddha: The Big Buddha is probably one of the most well-known and fascinating tourist places to see in Phuket. Located on top of the famous Nakkerd Hills, it is a massive 45-metre-tall statue of Maravijaya Buddha in a seated posture. Also known as Ming Mongkol, the Big Buddha is the tallest statue and the most famous landmark in Phuket. To reach the site of the statue, you will need to cover a 6-kilometre uphill path, but the scenic view and serene charm from the top make everything worth it. You can find a visit to this landmark as a common part of most popular Phuket travel packages. Phuket Old Town: Situated on the eastern side of the island, the Old Town is considered one of the most sought-after Phuket tourist places. It is a place where history and culture thrive in their top gear, offering an overall alluring tourism experience for anyone who explores this part of the island. The Phuket Old Town is marked by several quaint cafes, popular museums, beautiful shrines, attractive age-old architecture, souvenir stores and much more. It is no surprise that most popular Phuket sightseeing package options feature it in their itinerary as a standard inclusion. Andamanda Water Park: When talking about famous tourist places to see in Phuket, one can’t miss mentioning the local favourite, Andamanda Water Park. Covering an area of approximately 9.8 hectares, the water park features over 21 attractions and 32 water slides. During your visit, you will notice that the park is divided into 5 zones with specific themes, each offering something unique and more exciting than the other. A visit to this park will not only cheer you up but also offer you a much-needed break during your Phuket travel. The water parks also feature restaurants where you can grab a bite and relax until you are ready for the next adventure. Enjoy more attractions like this on your trip with Phuket travel packages by Thomas Cook. Wat Chalong: There are many religious shrines and temples across Phuket and Wat Chalong is one of the most popular ones. It is a historic Buddhist temple and is considered among the most important Phuket places to visit for religious trips. The astounding traditional architecture of this temple, paired with around three dozen carved Buddhas, lotus ponds, detailed gold carvings and a stone serpent bannister, makes it popular across the boundaries. Every day, local devotees and tourists flock to the temple to offer prayers and learn about Buddhism. So, if you are in Phuket and are interested in exploring all these, make sure to drop by Wat Chalong. Phuket Bird Park: For your tour to Phuket, don’t forget to add a small visit to the famous Phuket Bird Park. It is a place where nature enthusiasts and individuals with a keen interest in birds can spend hours exploring without being bored during their Phuket travel. The park is home to over a hundred distinct bird species from all across the globe, including Asia, South America, Africa, etc. The lush green surroundings, natural waterfall and chirping of birds create a wonderful vibe that remains unmatched. The park stays open from 9 AM to 5 PM and hosts live bird shows thrice a day for guests. So, try looking for Phuket trip packages featuring a visit to this park.
Phuket.Best hill stations and best beaches in P: Most individuals planning a trip to Phuket already know that it is home to numerous beautiful beaches. However, one thing many forget is that Phuket is also quite hilly, featuring some of the most fascinating hill climbs and viewpoints that one can cover. Here are some famous examples that you can add to the Phuket itinerary Hills in Phuket Monkey Hill: Toh Sae Hill or Monkey Hill is one of the most famous natural landmarks and the best tourist places in Phuket. It is a place that attracts hundreds of tourists every day, thanks to the presence of several monkeys in the area who always stay engaged in mischievous antics, offering a sense of fun and entertainment for visitors. You can find trained monkeys performing fun tricks to wild ones stealing your food from hand, and everything in between. The hill is also home to beautiful macaques and being Phuket’s tallest hill, it has one of the best hill viewpoints on the island. There are several Phuket trip packages featuring a visit to this hill. Khao Rang: Khao Rang or Rang Hill is Phuket’s most famous and stunning viewpoint. Located close to the city centre, it becomes a charming getaway for tourists and locals during early morning and afternoon. Enjoying the natural beauty, serenity and remarkable panoramic views during a visit to Khao Rang are a bucket list item for many. There are playgrounds, restaurants, parks and panoramic terraces on the top of this hill. So, consider adding it to your Phuket city tour itinerary. Beaches in Phuket Patong Beach: Stretching around 2.2 miles in length, Patong Beach is one of the top places to visit in Phuket for its picturesque beach vibes. Known for hosting upbeat parties, the pristine shoreline, availability of fun watersports and exuberant nightlife make it a site worth visiting. You can explore this beach as a part of the Phuket city tour. However, don’t expect much serene vibes at Patong Beach as it welcomes massive tourist crowds around the year. Kata Beach: The magnificent Kata Beach, on the western coast, is also one of the top places to visit in Phuket for beach vacations. It is a beautiful beach that is known for offering stunning seafront views and billowing waves ideal for surfers and paddlers. Tourists from all over the world come to Kata Beach in the morning for sunbathing experiences and after dark for fun parties. There are tons of snack vendors and massage parlours near the beach that you can visit on your Phuket travel. Karon Beach: Karon Beach is yet another renowned beach in Phuket that you can visit to enjoy secluded vibes and a tranquil ambience. Despite not being overly crowded, Karon Beach has all the amenities one can hope for, including fun watersports, availability of food and snacks, a vibrant nightlife, shops and markets, and more. Get deals on Phuket trip packages featuring Karon Beach on our website.
Phuket.Best honeymoon destinations in Phuket: Freedom Beach: Freedom Beach is a secluded beach that attracts couples and honeymooners from all over the globe. It features a 300-metre-long stretch of white sandy beach which is bordered by lush greenery and towering hills on one side and turquoise water on the other. You and your spouse can easily reach this beach via longtail boats, which makes for a thrilling yet romantic experience. Some romantic things newly married couples can do at Freedom Beach include renting and chilling on the sun lounger under an umbrella, enjoying soothing swim sessions, exploring the corals and marine life during snorkelling, etc. Get honeymoon-themed Phuket trip packages featuring Freedom Beach on Thomas Cook. Phi Phi Islands: When it comes to romantic getaways for honeymooners, Phi Phi Islands come to mind. It is a group of 6 stunning islands, just 45 km from Phuket. It is a place where couples can witness the unblemished beauty of nature, characterised by colossal cliffs, pristine beaches, dense greenery and clear blue water, paired with fun parties. You can easily create a Phuket tour plan including a short trip to Phi Phi Islands as it can be reached in under 45 minutes via speedboats. You can easily find honeymoon special Phuket trip packages that feature Phi Phi Islands on our website. Phang Nga Bay: Located between the mainland of the Kra Isthmus of Southern Thailand and Phuket, Phang Nga Bay is 400 sq. km. bay in the Andaman Sea. It is considered one of the most breathtaking tourist places in Thailand, thanks to its natural beauty and attractions composed of seagrass beds, limestone caves, towering cliffs, mangrove forests, diverse flora and fauna and more. It has become a major honeymoon destination in the country, only a 1 to 1.5-hour drive away from Phuket. Its main tourist attractions are James Bond Island, Koh Panyi, Cave of Reclining Buddha, Samet Nangshe Viewpoint, etc. Looking for Phuket trip packages for honeymooners featuring a boat trip to Phang Nga Bay? Check out the available planned trip options on our page. Here’s a Phuket tour guide if you want more details on popular tourist places.
Phuket.Interesting things to do in Phuket: To have the most fun on your trip, your Phuket itinerary needs to have a proper balance of sightseeing and fun activities. Here are some fun and interesting things you can try on your Phuket trip; Try out Phuket street food Explore Bangla Road Opt for a relaxing Thai Massage Attend the Simon Cabaret Show
Phuket.Interesting facts about Phuket: The more one learns about Phuket, the more they get eager to visit this fascinating destination. So, here are some intriguing facts about the place that you should know prior to the visit; Phuket, which we know and love, is a name derived from a Malay word called “Bukit”, which means hill. The previous name of the island was Thalang and Junk Ceylon (as per Western sources). Phuket is Thailand’s largest island and covers a total area of around 576 square kilometres. It is both a province and an island and is marginally smaller in comparison to Singapore. Remember the famous 1974 James Bond movie called “The Man with the Golden Gun”, starring Roger Moore and Christopher Lee? Well, the magnificent limestone cliffs featured in the film are shot from this region. Despite being renowned for housing some of the most pristine beaches in Thailand, the majority of the land area in Phuket is actually covered by mountains and hilly terrain. As a result of the devastating effects caused by the December 26th, 2004 Tsunami, Phuket was one of the first places in the world to introduce tsunami-detection buoys produced by the USA. One of the biggest statues of Buddha is located in Phuket. It is a 45-metre tall, white marble statue, nestled on top of Mount Nagakerd. You can hire a local Phuket travel guide to learn more about this landmark and the local history. Phuket hosts a massive annual vegetarian festival, which lasts for roughly 8 days. Phuket is a well-known choice as a vacation destination for beach lovers as it is home to over 30 stunning beaches. Roughly 60% of the total island of Phuket is covered in plantations of palm oil, pineapple and rubber. Due to its favourable geographical condition, you can go for the popular Phuket one day tour to Phi Phi Islands and the Phuket one day tour to Phang Nga Bay without making any major changes to your itinerary.
