faq.question: How many days are ideal for a Manali trip?
faq.answer: For first-timers, a 4- to 5-day Manali trip is ideal. You don't have to rush when you visit popular spots like Solang Valley, Rohtang Pass, and Old Manali. If you choose one of the more expensive Manali India tour packages, you'll also have time to relax at your hotel or do things in the area, like walk through a town or get a spa treatment. Some Manali tour & travel packages even include an extra day trip to Kasol or Naggar, so if you have time, you might want to stay for 6 days.

faq.question: Is it safe to visit Manali during the winter?
faq.answer: Yes, Manali is a safe place to visit in the winter. Ensure you book an approved Manali tour and packages that include safe accommodations with heating, knowledgeable guides, and emergency support. But if you're going during the snowiest months (December through February), make sure that your Manali travel package includes suggestions for winter gear and good car arrangements for snowy landscapes. You can be sure you're prepared by choosing a tour for Manali that includes real-time weather reports and information on the condition of the Rohtang road.

faq.question: What is the best time to visit Manali for adventure activities?
faq.answer: From March to June, adventure sports like flying, river rafting, and zorbing are best. This is the time of year when most Manali vacation packages include things to do in Solang Valley or along the river near Raison. From December to February is the best time to have fun in the snow. Ensure that your Manali package includes all the permits and trained activity organisers you need. Our Manali trip tour packages for winter include skiing, snowboarding, and rides on snowmobiles, which are great for people who like to be scared.

faq.question: Do I need a permit for Rohtang Pass?
faq.answer: There is a cap on the number of cars visiting Rohtang Pass each day, so you need a permit. This pass is included in all of our Manali India tour packages, including the Rohtang Pass. You won't have to worry about getting a ticket or getting your car cleared if you book a trip to Manali package with Thomas Cook. faq.question: What should I wear while visiting Manali?

faq.question: Can I get snow in Manali during the summer?
faq.answer: Yes, but not in the middle of town. Up higher, like at Rohtang Pass and sometimes in Solang Valley, it still snows in April and May. faq.question: What is the best time to visit Canada?
faq.answer: Summer is the best time to plan a Canada trip because sunny days and pleasant temperatures are the norm. Despite its size, Canada's weather patterns are largely consistent throughout the country, with distinct seasons similar to those found in Europe.

faq.question: Are winters harsh in Canada?
faq.answer: In most Canadian regions, except for British Columbia coast (where winters are moderate), winters are harsh, accompanied with freezing winds and extreme snowfall.

faq.question: Which are the best cities to visit in Canada ?
faq.answer: Montreal, Quebec City, Vancouver, Ontario, Calgary, Toronto, and Victoria are some of many cities you should not miss out on your Canada tour

faq.question: Which destinations in Canada are family friendly?
faq.answer: Canada offers many destinations where one could unwind and have a relaxing bonding session with their family. Some of these places are Niagara Falls, Prince Edward Island, Whistler, Quebec City, Jasper National Park, Ottawa, Toronto, Victoria and Vancouver Island, Halifax and Montreal.

faq.question: Which language is highly spoken in Canada?
faq.answer: The official languages of Canada are French and English. Many other unofficial languages are spoken in restaurants, buses, and schools across Canada. In fact, there are more than 200 languages spoken here from all over the world.

faq.question: What is the official currency of Canada?
faq.answer: Canadian Dollar is the official currency of Canada.

faq.question: How many days do I need to wait for a Canada visa?
faq.answer: A tourist visa needs 15 days for processing.

faq.question: What is the duration of a flight to Canada from India?
faq.answer: A direct flight from India to Canada takes a minimum of 14 hours and 18 minutes.

faq.question: Can I use a Canada visa to visit the USA?
faq.answer: No, one cannot visit the USA with a Canadian visa. You must have a valid US visa.

faq.question: How long should a Canada tour last?
faq.answer: If you want to soak up the bright lights of a bustling city while also venturing out into a more remote region, such as Banff or Jasper National Park, for a few days in the wilderness, you'll need at least ten days in this magnificent country.

faq.question: Is Canada an expensive country?
faq.answer: Yes, Canada is an expensive country.

faq.question: Can I use my Indian driver's license in Canada?
faq.answer: No. One would need to apply for an International Driving Permit to be able to drive in Canada.

Canada.How to reach Canada?: The first crucial step in organising your trip to Canada is figuring out how to reach Canada. Here is straightforward advice on how to reach Canada easily and comfortably, regardless of whether you're travelling by plane from India, crossing from the US, or envisioning a picturesque train journey through its countryside. By train Although there are no direct trains from India, you can enter Canada by Amtrak train if you fly to the US first. Popular routes include New York to Montreal and Seattle to Vancouver. Once inside, the VIA Rail system provides a stunning means of national transportation. Many Canada tour packages from india include the must-try experience of gliding past the Rockies, lakes, and woods. By Road Although it is not possible to drive straight from India to Canada, numerous border crossings make it simple to get there if you are already in the US. For people who value independence and flexibility, this area offers beautiful road excursions and well-maintained roadways. A lot of Canada travel packages also include guided road tours along beautiful roads like the Cabot Trail or the Icefields Parkway, which is ideal for making your dream trip to Canada an unforgettable experience. By Air Air travel is the simplest and most convenient option to start your Canada visit. Major Indian cities like Delhi, Mumbai, ,Bengaluru and Chennai have several direct and connecting flights to popular Canadian cities like Toronto, Vancouver, and Montreal. Air Canada, British Airways, Lufthansa, and Air India are some of the popular carriers. Depending on the itinerary, an average nonstop flight lasts between 15 and 17 hours. Once you land, there are well-connected taxis, local trains, and airport shuttles that make it easy to go to your accommodation or Canada tourist attractions. For added comfort, airport transfers are included in the majority of Canada travel packages.
Canada.Best Places to Visit in Canada: Planning a trip to Canada involves finding the country's best natural beauties, urban cities, and cultural experiences. Whether you're purchasing a Canada package from India or looking for personalised Canada travel packages, here's a fast introduction to the best places to visit in Canada, including famous monuments, tranquil beaches, and spiritual temples.
Canada.Famous Places to Visit: Ontario's Niagara Falls: A trip to Canada wouldn't be complete without seeing this renowned marvel. Enjoy a wonderful boat trip near the thundering falls or take in the nighttime illumination. Alberta's Banff National Park: Banff, a sanctuary of turquoise lakes, glaciers, and snow-capped hills, is a highlight of most Canadian itineraries. You can take leisurely drives, see Lake Louise, or unwind in the Banff Upper Hot Springs. British Columbia's Vancouver: One of Canada's most popular tourist destinations is Vancouver, a city where mountains and the sea meet. Shop in Gastown, see Stanley Park, and savour delectable Canada food at its eclectic eateries. Quebec City: Quebec City is reminiscent of Europe with its French flair and cobbled streets. It's among the best destinations in Canada for both romantic and history buffs.
Canada.Beaches/ Hill Stations to Visit: Prince Edward Island: Prince Edward Island is well-known for its fresh seafood, undulating hills, and red sand beaches. A serene destination for those reserving Canada trip packages. Quebec's Mont-Tremblant: A quaint hill station with streets reminiscent of Europe, ideal for hiking and skiing. Cabot Trail: The Cabot Trail in Nova Scotia is a picturesque route that provides cosy coastal accommodations, views of the ocean, and whale viewing. British Columbia's Whistler: A popular destination on most Canada holiday packages, this resort town is renowned for its hiking, skiing, and adventure sports.
Canada.Temples to Visit: Richmond Hill's Vishnu Mandir: One of the biggest Hindu temples, Richmond Hill's Vishnu Mandir is renowned for its colourful festivals and architectural design, a must add to your Canada trip packages. Toronto's BAPS Shri Swaminarayan Mandir: This temple is a serene spiritual haven and a masterwork of carved stone. Sri Siva Sathyanarayana Swamy Temple: The province's first South Indian-style temple, Sri Siva Sathyanarayana Swamy Temple in Alberta, features traditional Dravidian art, making it a must-visit in many Canada trip packages. Hindu Sabha Mandir: Hindu Sabha Mandir in Brampton is a bustling centre for cultural events, community gatherings, and prayers. Cities to Cover To truly appreciate Canada's charm, you just must include a few cities in your list of places to visit in Canada . Toronto, a vibrant city renowned for its skyline, CN Tower, and amazing Canadian cuisine, is a great place to start your Canada tour. Travel west to Vancouver, which offers countless Canadian activities like biking through Stanley Park and exploring Granville Island, where mountains meet the sea. With their vibrant festivals, historic streets, and French culture, Montreal and Quebec City offer a little bit of Europe. Don't miss Calgary or Banff for stunning mountain views, or Ottawa, the capital, with its museums and Parliament Hill. Best Time to Visit London When planning a London tour, it is essential to first figure out a favourable time for the visit. Doing so can also help in finding the ideal London tour packages. Here is a detailed look at the season-wise best time to visit London; Summer in London Summer, which spans from June to August, is considered the best time for a London trip. The temperature ranges between 18-30°C, and the daylight hours are pretty long. It offers an overall warm and pleasant weather, ideal for outdoor tourist activities. You can expect some rain and high humidity during these months. As it is a peak season for a London tour, you will have to deal with large tourist crowds and steep prices. A London tour during summer is centred around visits to museums and galleries. Thomas Cook offers multiple summer special London packages. Spring in London Spring in London covers the months from March to May. The temperature usually ranges between 8-18°C, which is quite favourable for outdoor tourist activities. The weather bounces from cold and rainy to warm and sunny. It is a good time for a London trip, especially if you want to avoid peak-season crowds and the off-season cold. Winter in London Winter, from December to February, in London is quite chilly. You can expect temperatures between 2°C and 7°C. Hence, some tourists tend to avoid it. However, major celebrations like Christmas and the New Year also attract a big tourist crowd during December. Then again, the tourist crowd starts to disperse as mid-January and February roll in. Some consider January and February to be ideal for budget travellers going on a London tour. If you like festivities, book London packages for December from Thomas Cook. Autumn in London London welcomes the autumn season in September, and it lasts as long as November. The city sees around 2 inches of precipitation each month and an average temperature range of 11 and 15°C. However, the cosy ambience, mild weather and fiery autumn foliage make it a great time for a London tour. Get great deals on London packages for any season on our website. How to reach London? London is well-connected to major cities in India and the rest of the world. So, for your London tour, you can choose from a wide array of travel options. Here’s how to reach London from India; By air The metropolitan area of London is served by six international airports. Among them, the London Heathrow Airport is considered the main one. It is located roughly 23 km to the west of Central London and is well-connected to various corners of the globe. For reference, a direct flight to London Heathrow Airport from New Delhi takes about 9 hours and 45 minutes. The other five airports you can consider for your London trip are City Airport, Gatwick Airport, Luton Airport, Southend Airport and Stansted Airport. Popular airlines like Ryanair, Qatar Airways, Emirates, Air India, IndiGo, etc., operate regular flights on this route. Find and book London packages featuring round-trip flights on Thomas Cook. By rail For your London tour from India, you can’t opt to travel by train. It is because there is no direct train route between Indian cities and London. However, once you are in the U.K., you can choose to travel to London by train. International rail also connects London to other cities in Europe. For example, you can consider the Eurostar high-speed train which links London to European cities like Amsterdam, Paris and Brussels. By road For a London tour from India, travelling by road is not a very feasible option. However, if you are craving a road trip, you can consider it. It can take around 6-10 weeks, and you will have to cross 15 nations or more. The route starts in India and goes through Myanmar, China, Kyrgyzstan, Uzbekistan, Kazakhstan, Russia, Belarus, Poland, Germany, the Netherlands, Belgium, France and the U.K. As for local transfers, the London packages by Thomas Cook usually include them under the plan price. Here are some of the best places to visit in London that are sure to leave a lasting impression: The British Museum On your London tour, step into the world of antiquity and marvel at the vast collection of art and artefacts from around the globe. From the Rosetta Stone to the Elgin Marbles, the British Museum houses treasures spanning thousands of years of human history making it one of the famous tourist places in London. This site is covered in popular London packages. Buckingham Palace Witness the Changing of the Guard ceremony and admire the grandeur of the official residence of the British monarch with the London trip packages. During the summer months, explore the State Rooms and Gardens for a glimpse into royal life. The Tower of London Immerse yourself in centuries of royal history at this UNESCO World Heritage Site with London trip packages. Explore the imposing fortress, discover the Crown Jewels, and hear tales of intrigue and betrayal on a London tour guide led by the Yeoman Warders. Make the most out of your London tour with London packages from Thomas Cook. The London Eye Soar above the city skyline on this iconic Ferris wheel with London trip packages for breathtaking views of London's landmarks, including Big Ben, the Houses of Parliament, and St. Paul's Cathedral. Opt for a champagne experience for a truly memorable ride. The Tate Modern Delve into the world of contemporary art at this renowned gallery housed in a converted power station on the banks of the Thames. Explore works by Picasso, Warhol, and Hockney, among others, and enjoy panoramic views of the city from the viewing platform with London trip packages. The West End Experience the magic of London's theatre district with a visit to the West End with London trip packages. Catch a world-class musical, play, or ballet at historic venues such as the Royal Opera House or the Theatre Royal Drury Lane. St. Paul's Cathedral Climb to the top of Sir Christopher Wren's masterpiece for panoramic views of London tourist places and explore the cathedral's stunning interior, including the Whispering Gallery and the crypt. Check out more sites like St. Paul’s Cathedral during sightseeing with London packages. Covent Garden Wander through the bustling streets of this vibrant neighbourhood on your London tour. The place is known for its street performers, boutique shops, and lively atmosphere. Stop for a meal at one of the many restaurants and cafes or browse the stalls of the famous market when travelling with London trip packages. These are just a few of the best places to visit in London, each offering a unique glimpse into the city's rich heritage and contemporary culture. Whether you're exploring on your own or with the guidance of a knowledgeable London tour guide, London's diverse attractions are sure to captivate and inspire. Explore London tour packages to make the most of your visit to this dynamic and captivating city. Best Hill Stations/ Best Beaches in London London, being a bustling metropolis, is not typically associated with hill stations or beaches. However, there are a few nearby destinations that offer a refreshing escape from the city's hustle and bustle. Here are some of the places to visit in London hill stations and beaches, that are worth exploring: Best Hill Stations Near London: The Chiltern Hills Stretching across Buckinghamshire, Oxfordshire, Hertfordshire, and Bedfordshire, the Chiltern Hills are renowned for their picturesque landscapes, quaint villages, and ancient woodlands. Visitors can enjoy leisurely walks, wildlife spotting, and visits to historic sites such as the Hellfire Caves when travelling with London trip packages. Box Hill Located in Surrey, Box Hill offers stunning panoramic views of the surrounding countryside and the River Mole making it one of the ideal places to visit in London. It is a popular destination for hiking, cycling, and picnicking, with several scenic trails and viewpoints to explore while on a London tour. Get the best deals on London packages on our website. South Downs National Park Just a short drive from London, South Downs National Park is a haven for outdoor enthusiasts. With rolling hills, chalk cliffs, and charming villages, it offers endless opportunities for hiking, cycling, and nature-watching. Don't miss the iconic white cliffs of Beachy Head and the picturesque village of Alfriston when travelling with London trip packages. Best Beaches Near London: Camber Sands Nestled in East Sussex, Camber Sands is a picturesque beach renowned for its golden sands and dunes. It offers ample opportunities for sunbathing, beachcombing, and kite surfing, making it a popular destination for families and outdoor enthusiasts travelling with London trip packages. Brighton Beach Located on the south coast of England, Brighton Beach is a vibrant seaside destination known for its colourful beach huts, bustling promenade, and iconic pier. Visitors can enjoy sunbathing, swimming, and water sports, as well as exploring the quirky shops and cafes along the seafront. Some London tour packages with comprehensive itineraries may feature such locations. Botany Bay Situated in Kent, Botany Bay is a hidden gem along the Thanet coastline. With its dramatic chalk cliffs, rock pools, and sandy shores, it provides a serene escape from the crowds. Visitors can enjoy leisurely walks, fossil hunting, and picnicking against the backdrop of stunning natural beauty with London trip packages. While London may not have its own hill stations or beaches, these nearby destinations offer the perfect opportunity to unwind and reconnect with nature. Whether you're seeking panoramic views, outdoor adventures, or simply a day of relaxation by the sea, these hill stations and beaches near London have something to offer every traveller. Explore London tour packages to combine your city break with a refreshing excursion to these scenic London places to visit. Best Honeymoon Destinations to explore in London For couples seeking a romantic getaway, London offers an array of enchanting destinations perfect for a memorable honeymoon experience. Here are six of the best honeymoon places to see in London, along with honeymoon London packages from India: Hyde Park Escape the hustle and bustle of the city and retreat to the tranquillity of Hyde Park. Rent a rowboat on the Serpentine Lake, take a leisurely walk through the lush greenery, or enjoy a romantic picnic amidst the scenic surroundings. It is a common feature on popular honeymoon special London tour packages. Covent Garden This charming neighbourhood boasts cobblestone streets, historic architecture, and a vibrant atmosphere making it one of the best places to see in London. On a visit to this place during a London tour, couples can stroll hand in hand through the bustling market, enjoy a romantic meal at one of the many restaurants, or catch a live performance at the Royal Opera House. Thames River Cruise Embark on a romantic cruise along the River Thames and take in the iconic sights of London from a unique vantage point. Choose from a variety of cruise options, including sunset cruises, dinner cruises, and private charters for an unforgettable experience. Notting Hill Explore the picturesque streets of Notting Hill, famous for its colourful houses, quaint cafes, and eclectic boutiques. Wander hand in hand through the charming neighbourhood, stopping to admire the vibrant street art and soak up the romantic ambience. The Shard Treat your loved one to panoramic views of the city from the observation deck of The Shard, London's tallest building. Sip champagne as you gaze out over the skyline and capture memories that will last a lifetime. Kew Gardens Lose yourselves in the beauty of nature at Kew Gardens, one of the world's most renowned botanical gardens. Discover exotic plants, tranquil ponds, and secluded pathways as you explore this enchanting oasis together. For couples chalking out their London itinerary for a honeymoon from India, various London tour packages cater to romantic getaways, offering seamless travel arrangements and curated experiences to make your London trip plan unforgettable. Whether you're seeking a luxury escape, cultural immersion, or outdoor adventure, London has something to offer every honeymooning couple. Interesting Things to do in London & Adventurous Things to do in London London is one of the most happening places in the U.K. So, to enhance your London tour experience, add these fun and adventurous things to the trip itinerary; Explore the British Museum One of the most popular things to do on a London tour is to take a guided excursion of the British Museum. It boasts an exceptional collection of antiques collected from all over the globe. It is a testament to human history, with around 8 million exhibits. From Egyptian mummies to the Amaravati Marbles, it houses it all. Take a River Thames Cruise Go on the iconic Thames River cruise to enjoy the beautiful sights and daily sounds of London’s bustling life from the comfort of the boat. As you enjoy the gentle breeze, you will be able to witness many historic landmarks, stunning gardens, incredible modern architecture, and so on. Stroll through Hyde Park Covering an area of 350 acres, Hyde Park is one of the most beautiful royal parks in London. Tourists love to visit this park on their London tour to enjoy the beautiful views complemented by a large lake, several trees, well-kept flower gardens, etc. Activities like skating, cycling, boating, tennis, etc., are also available here. Indoor Skydiving Enjoy the fun and thrill of skydiving without the risk. Visit iFly London, where you will be placed inside a vertical wind tunnel and guided by an instructor. The upward airflow will assist you in enjoying this free-falling experience. Kayaking on the River Thames Explore London from the iconic River Thames and its canals. Rent kayaks with safety gear and embark on this exciting journey through the historic London waterways. On your journey, you will also be able to spot famous landmarks, like the London Eye, Tower Bridge, etc. High Ropes Course On your London tour, give your adrenaline a pump by checking out Go Ape at Battersea Park. Try the high ropes course there, which includes zip lines, rope bridges, Tarzan swings and much more. What to Eat in London? Exploring London’s cuisine is a great way to get in sync with the region’s diverse culture. Here are some top foods to try on your London tour; Afternoon Tea: Grab some afternoon tea at a local cafe on your London tour. It comes with a cup of warm tea with finger sandwiches, cakes, and bread & jam on the side. Fish and Chips: This dish is made of battered white fish and chunky potato fries with hints of salt and malt vinegar. Bangers and Mash: Gravy-rich juicy sausages with buttery mashed potatoes and peas on the side. Scotch Egg: Try this on your London tour. It is hard-boiled hen eggs covered in sausage meat and coated in breadcrumbs. What to Shop in London? On your London tour, you can consider shopping for these popular items; British chocolates: Grab some flavourful and creamy English chocolates when shopping in London. They are available in colourful packaging and taste amazing. Traditional biscuits: Buy some traditional biscuits for your tea time. There are many bakeries in the city that you can visit to shop for biscuits on your London tour. Miniature landmarks: Bring some of London’s iconic landmarks home with you. Shop for miniature figurines of the Big Ben, London black taxi, red telephone booth, etc. Books: London is a haven for literature enthusiasts. Shop for all kinds of rare and antique books at the vintage bookstores during your London tour. Interesting Facts about the London London is a city steeped in history and culture, with a wealth of fascinating facts and lesser-known tidbits waiting to be discovered by visitors. Here are a few interesting facts about London that every traveller should know before embarking on their journey: The London Underground Commonly known as the Tube, the London Underground is the oldest underground railway system in the world, dating back to 1863. It is also one of the largest, with over 250 miles of track and 270 stations serving millions of passengers each day. Make sure to experience the Tube on your London tour. The City of London The historic heart of London, known as the City of London, is actually a separate entity from Greater London. It is home to the capital's financial district and is governed by its own unique set of laws and traditions, dating back to Roman times. Big Ben Contrary to popular belief, Big Ben is not actually the name of the iconic clock tower at the north end of the Palace of Westminster. Big Ben refers specifically to the 13.5-tonne bell housed within the tower, while the tower itself is officially known as the Elizabeth Tower. The Royal Parks London is home to eight royal parks, covering over 5,000 acres of green space within the city. From the vast expanses of Hyde Park and Richmond Park to the serene gardens of Kensington Palace and St. James's Park, these parks offer a peaceful retreat from the hustle and bustle of city life. The Great Fire of London In 1666, a devastating fire swept through the city of London, destroying much of the medieval city within the old Roman walls. The fire raged for four days, consuming over 13,000 houses, 87 churches, and numerous public buildings, but ultimately led to the modernisation and rebuilding of the city. The Shard Standing at a height of 310 meters (1,016 feet), the Shard is the tallest building in the United Kingdom and the fifth tallest in Europe. Its distinctive glass façade and towering spire dominate the skyline of London, offering panoramic views of the city from its observation deck and restaurants. The Thames Barrier Stretching across the River Thames in southeast London, the Thames Barrier is one of the largest movable flood barriers in the world. Designed to protect the city from tidal surges and flooding, the barrier has been operational since 1982 and plays a vital role in safeguarding London's low-lying areas. These fascinating facts offer a glimpse into the rich history and cultural heritage of London, making it a truly unique and captivating destination for travellers from around the world. Whether you're exploring top attractions or uncovering hidden gems, London promises an unforgettable experience for visitors of all ages. Explore London tour packages to make the most of your visit to this dynamic and diverse city. London Culture London's cultural landscape is as diverse and dynamic as the city itself, with a myriad of unique customs and traditions that reflect its rich history and heritage. Here are six lesser-known cultural habits and traditions followed in London: Queuing Brits are renowned for their love of queuing, and Londoners take this cultural habit very seriously. Whether waiting for a bus, entering a museum, or ordering a pint at the pub, queuing is seen as a symbol of politeness and respect for others' time. Keep that in mind when travelling with London travel packages. Pantomime During the festive season, Londoners flock to theatres across the city to enjoy the time-honoured tradition of pantomime. These family-friendly musical comedies feature exaggerated characters, slapstick humour, and audience participation, making them a beloved cultural staple for generations. Afternoon Tea A quintessentially British tradition, afternoon tea is a cultural institution in London. Typically served between 3 pm and 5 pm, afternoon tea consists of a selection of finger sandwiches, scones with clotted cream and jam, and delicate pastries, all accompanied by a pot of freshly brewed tea. Don't forget to indulge in it when travelling with London travel packages. Pub Culture Pubs are an integral part of London's social fabric, serving as communal gathering spaces where friends and neighbours come together to relax, unwind, and socialise. From historic taverns dating back centuries to trendy gastropubs serving craft beers and artisanal cocktails, London's pub culture is diverse and vibrant. Loyalty to Local Football Clubs Football is more than just a sport in London; it's a way of life. Londoners are fiercely loyal to their local football clubs, with rivalries between teams such as Arsenal, Chelsea, Tottenham Hotspur, and West Ham United running deep. Attending a match at one of the city’s iconic stadiums is a cultural experience not to be missed for sports enthusiasts on their London tour. Experience the city’s glorious football culture with London tour packages from Thomas Cook. Royal Ceremonies London is home to a wealth of royal ceremonies and traditions that offer a glimpse into the pomp and pageantry of the British monarchy. From the Changing of the Guard at Buckingham Palace to the Trooping the Colour ceremony on the Queen's official birthday, these events are steeped in centuries of tradition and heritage. These unique cultural habits and traditions offer a glimpse into the diverse and vibrant tapestry of London's cultural identity. Whether enjoying afternoon tea, cheering on a local football team, or queuing patiently in line, these customs and traditions are integral to the fabric of life in the capital city. Explore London tour packages to immerse yourself in the rich cultural heritage of this dynamic metropolis with an affordable London trip cost. London Visa for Indians & Foreign Currency For your London tour, you will need to obtain the UK standard visitor visa, which allows stays for up to 6 months. You have to apply for it via the official channel prior to the visit. Applicants will need to submit personal details and documents like passports, a detailed London tour plan, proof of financial means, etc. Applicants may also have to go through a visa interview. After the final review, approved visas will be shared with the successful applicants. Some London tour packages may include assistance with visa applications and services. Besides a visa, tourists will also require sufficient foreign currency to fulfil payments during the London tour. The official currency of London is Sterling, which is denoted by the code “GBP” and the sign “£”. Its main unit is “pound” and one pound is further divided into 100 pence. Banknote denominations of £5, £10, £20 and £50 and coin denominations of £1, £2, and then 1, 2, 5, 10, 20 and 50 pence are in circulation. The exchange rate as of 30th June 2025 is 1 GBP = 117.54 INR.

London.Interesting Things to do in London & Adv: London is one of the most happening places in the U.K. So, to enhance your London tour experience, add these fun and adventurous things to the trip itinerary; Explore the British Museum One of the most popular things to do on a London tour is to take a guided excursion of the British Museum. It boasts an exceptional collection of antiques collected from all over the globe. It is a testament to human history, with around 8 million exhibits. From Egyptian mummies to the Amaravati Marbles, it houses it all. Take a River Thames Cruise Go on the iconic Thames River cruise to enjoy the beautiful sights and daily sounds of London’s bustling life from the comfort of the boat. As you enjoy the gentle breeze, you will be able to witness many historic landmarks, stunning gardens, incredible modern architecture, and so on. Stroll through Hyde Park Covering an area of 350 acres, Hyde Park is one of the most beautiful royal parks in London. Tourists love to visit this park on their London tour to enjoy the beautiful views complemented by a large lake, several trees, well-kept flower gardens, etc. Activities like skating, cycling, boating, tennis, etc., are also available here. Indoor Skydiving Enjoy the fun and thrill of skydiving without the risk. Visit iFly London, where you will be placed inside a vertical wind tunnel and guided by an instructor. The upward airflow will assist you in enjoying this free-falling experience. Kayaking on the River Thames Explore London from the iconic River Thames and its canals. Rent kayaks with safety gear and embark on this exciting journey through the historic London waterways. On your journey, you will also be able to spot famous landmarks, like the London Eye, Tower Bridge, etc. High Ropes Course On your London tour, give your adrenaline a pump by checking out Go Ape at Battersea Park. Try the high ropes course there, which includes zip lines, rope bridges, Tarzan swings and much more.
London.What to Eat in London?: Exploring London’s cuisine is a great way to get in sync with the region’s diverse culture. Here are some top foods to try on your London tour; Afternoon Tea: Grab some afternoon tea at a local cafe on your London tour. It comes with a cup of warm tea with finger sandwiches, cakes, and bread & jam on the side. Fish and Chips: This dish is made of battered white fish and chunky potato fries with hints of salt and malt vinegar. Bangers and Mash: Gravy-rich juicy sausages with buttery mashed potatoes and peas on the side. Scotch Egg: Try this on your London tour. It is hard-boiled hen eggs covered in sausage meat and coated in breadcrumbs. What to Shop in London? On your London tour, you can consider shopping for these popular items; British chocolates: Grab some flavourful and creamy English chocolates when shopping in London. They are available in colourful packaging and taste amazing. Traditional biscuits: Buy some traditional biscuits for your tea time. There are many bakeries in the city that you can visit to shop for biscuits on your London tour. Miniature landmarks: Bring some of London’s iconic landmarks home with you. Shop for miniature figurines of the Big Ben, London black taxi, red telephone booth, etc. Books: London is a haven for literature enthusiasts. Shop for all kinds of rare and antique books at the vintage bookstores during your London tour.
London.Interesting Facts about the London: London is a city steeped in history and culture, with a wealth of fascinating facts and lesser-known tidbits waiting to be discovered by visitors. Here are a few interesting facts about London that every traveller should know before embarking on their journey: The London Underground Commonly known as the Tube, the London Underground is the oldest underground railway system in the world, dating back to 1863. It is also one of the largest, with over 250 miles of track and 270 stations serving millions of passengers each day. Make sure to experience the Tube on your London tour. The City of London The historic heart of London, known as the City of London, is actually a separate entity from Greater London. It is home to the capital's financial district and is governed by its own unique set of laws and traditions, dating back to Roman times. Big Ben Contrary to popular belief, Big Ben is not actually the name of the iconic clock tower at the north end of the Palace of Westminster. Big Ben refers specifically to the 13.5-tonne bell housed within the tower, while the tower itself is officially known as the Elizabeth Tower. The Royal Parks London is home to eight royal parks, covering over 5,000 acres of green space within the city. From the vast expanses of Hyde Park and Richmond Park to the serene gardens of Kensington Palace and St. James's Park, these parks offer a peaceful retreat from the hustle and bustle of city life. The Great Fire of London In 1666, a devastating fire swept through the city of London, destroying much of the medieval city within the old Roman walls. The fire raged for four days, consuming over 13,000 houses, 87 churches, and numerous public buildings, but ultimately led to the modernisation and rebuilding of the city. The Shard Standing at a height of 310 meters (1,016 feet), the Shard is the tallest building in the United Kingdom and the fifth tallest in Europe. Its distinctive glass façade and towering spire dominate the skyline of London, offering panoramic views of the city from its observation deck and restaurants. The Thames Barrier Stretching across the River Thames in southeast London, the Thames Barrier is one of the largest movable flood barriers in the world. Designed to protect the city from tidal surges and flooding, the barrier has been operational since 1982 and plays a vital role in safeguarding London's low-lying areas. These fascinating facts offer a glimpse into the rich history and cultural heritage of London, making it a truly unique and captivating destination for travellers from around the world. Whether you're exploring top attractions or uncovering hidden gems, London promises an unforgettable experience for visitors of all ages. Explore London tour packages to make the most of your visit to this dynamic and diverse city.
London.London Culture: London's cultural landscape is as diverse and dynamic as the city itself, with a myriad of unique customs and traditions that reflect its rich history and heritage. Here are six lesser-known cultural habits and traditions followed in London: Queuing Brits are renowned for their love of queuing, and Londoners take this cultural habit very seriously. Whether waiting for a bus, entering a museum, or ordering a pint at the pub, queuing is seen as a symbol of politeness and respect for others' time. Keep that in mind when travelling with London travel packages. Pantomime During the festive season, Londoners flock to theatres across the city to enjoy the time-honoured tradition of pantomime. These family-friendly musical comedies feature exaggerated characters, slapstick humour, and audience participation, making them a beloved cultural staple for generations. Afternoon Tea A quintessentially British tradition, afternoon tea is a cultural institution in London. Typically served between 3 pm and 5 pm, afternoon tea consists of a selection of finger sandwiches, scones with clotted cream and jam, and delicate pastries, all accompanied by a pot of freshly brewed tea. Don't forget to indulge in it when travelling with London travel packages. Pub Culture Pubs are an integral part of London's social fabric, serving as communal gathering spaces where friends and neighbours come together to relax, unwind, and socialise. From historic taverns dating back centuries to trendy gastropubs serving craft beers and artisanal cocktails, London's pub culture is diverse and vibrant. Loyalty to Local Football Clubs Football is more than just a sport in London; it's a way of life. Londoners are fiercely loyal to their local football clubs, with rivalries between teams such as Arsenal, Chelsea, Tottenham Hotspur, and West Ham United running deep. Attending a match at one of the city’s iconic stadiums is a cultural experience not to be missed for sports enthusiasts on their London tour. Experience the city’s glorious football culture with London tour packages from Thomas Cook. Royal Ceremonies London is home to a wealth of royal ceremonies and traditions that offer a glimpse into the pomp and pageantry of the British monarchy. From the Changing of the Guard at Buckingham Palace to the Trooping the Colour ceremony on the Queen's official birthday, these events are steeped in centuries of tradition and heritage. These unique cultural habits and traditions offer a glimpse into the diverse and vibrant tapestry of London's cultural identity. Whether enjoying afternoon tea, cheering on a local football team, or queuing patiently in line, these customs and traditions are integral to the fabric of life in the capital city. Explore London tour packages to immerse yourself in the rich cultural heritage of this dynamic metropolis with an affordable London trip cost.
London.London Visa for Indians & Foreign Curren: For your London tour, you will need to obtain the UK standard visitor visa, which allows stays for up to 6 months. You have to apply for it via the official channel prior to the visit. Applicants will need to submit personal details and documents like passports, a detailed London tour plan, proof of financial means, etc. Applicants may also have to go through a visa interview. After the final review, approved visas will be shared with the successful applicants. Some London tour packages may include assistance with visa applications and services. Besides a visa, tourists will also require sufficient foreign currency to fulfil payments during the London tour. The official currency of London is Sterling, which is denoted by the code “GBP” and the sign “£”. Its main unit is “pound” and one pound is further divided into 100 pence. Banknote denominations of £5, £10, £20 and £50 and coin denominations of £1, £2, and then 1, 2, 5, 10, 20 and 50 pence are in circulation. The exchange rate as of 30th June 2025 is 1 GBP = 117.54 INR.

africa.African Food: Alongside researching Africa packages, make sure to take a peek at its local food culture. From the desert land of the North to the alluring coasts of South Africa, each region dishes up a delicacy of their own which are bound to give you a delectable experience. Here are different ailments one should not miss when in Africa. Piri Chicken This is a luscious esculent of smoky grilled chicken which is cooked with pepper, garlic, coconut milk, piri sauce and lime. It tastes best when served with vatapa, which is the traditional sauce made of cassava leaves, cooked in peanut sauce. This scrumptious delicacy is a part of the Mozambique region of Africa. Enjoy more of Africa’s local food on your trip with Africa packages from Thomas Cook. Jollof Rice and Egusi Soup One of the major sins you can commit in Nigeria is not enjoying the highly pleasing feast called Jollof Rice. It is called as the National favourite dish of Nigeria, and is also, quite famous across the west of Africa. It is a basic yet appetizing one pot dish whichconsists of rice, tomatoes, onions and pepper. It can be savoured well with Egusi Soup asit is traditionally served in African festive gatherings. Bunny Chow Brought to South Africa by the Indian labourers who visited this land to work on sugar-cane fields, this is one of the most prominent street foods of South Africa. It is a quarter loaf of bread which is hollowed out and filled with sizzling hot curry. It is available in all the major cities of South Africa, but it is said that the best ones are found in Durban. Note that most Africa tour packages by Thomas Cook come with included meals.

faq.question: When is the best time for a Himachal Pradesh Trip?
faq.answer: The best time for the Himachal Pradesh trip will vary significantly depending on the type of experience you want. Both summers and winters are often considered to be the ideal time to visit Himachal. If you wish to explore pleasant weather and enjoy sightseeing, you must book your Himachal Pradesh tourism tour packages between March and June. However, if you want to enjoy winter sports and activities, it is best to book the winter Himachal Pradesh holiday packages. October to February is the best time to experience the Himachal winter. The temperature is extremely low, but with snowfall, you can enjoy skiing. However, it is advisable not to visit Himachal during the monsoon, as there's an increased risk of landslides.

During the Himachal Pradesh tour, you must visit Dharamshala and McLeodganj. It is here that you'll get to witness the vibrant Tibetan culture in India. Both the Hindu and Buddhist festivals are celebrated on a wide scale here, showing the rich tradition of the place. Unlike many other places, in Himachal Pradesh, no matter what time you visit, you're always up for an experience. This would give you a brief idea of what would be covered in your trip. Typically, a comprehensive Himachal Pradesh tour package lasts six days and seven nights. During this duration, you'll be covering some of the major destinations of Himachal Pradesh. However, some of these destinations may vary depending on the season you're travelling in. Usually, the Himachal Pradesh tour package should cover flights, accommodation, breakfast and dinner. The tour managers will be provided if you're booking group tours to Himachal Pradesh. Here's a brief sample itinerary for your Himachal trip package: Day 1: Arrival You will be arriving at your scheduled destination. Considering the proximity of Himachal to Chandigarh, the arrival is usually here. Your first day is booked to explore Chandigarh, where you'll cover major destinations. Then, you can go souvenir shopping or relax in your hotel. Day 2: Road trip to Manali Your second day begins with your road trip to Manali, with all the inclusions mentioned in the package. The road trips for Himachal Pradesh tourism packages are usually longer, so by the time you reach Manali, it will be later in the evening. Since you're tired, you can just check into your hotel and relax. Day 3: Sightseeing The third day is dedicated to full-day sightseeing. You'll be visiting some of the top destinations like Hidimba Devi Temple, Solang Valley, and more. If you want, you can opt for extra activities like hiking, trekking, paragliding, and more for additional charges. You'll be treated to a delicious Himachali lunch, and then in the evening you're free to explore on your own. Day 4: Manali Sightseeing After breakfast at the hotel, as part of your Himachal Pradesh tour package, you'll set out to explore other major destinations of Manali. You'll be served tea/coffee and snacks en route. After a day trip, the evening is at your leisure, so you can take your time and enjoy. Your overnight stay is booked at the hotel. Day 5: Manali to Shimla The next day, you'll be driving from Manali to Shimla, which will take around 9 hours. So, wake up early, have breakfast at the hotel, and then head out. The drive is pretty scenic, allowing you to enjoy the views. During this part of the Himachal Pradesh tour package, you'll take a halt mid-way to enjoy lunch at a local restaurant. Arrive by evening, check in at the hotel, and then rest. Day 6: Shimla City Tour Your day begins early with a morning breakfast, followed by exploring all the major destinations in and around Shimla. Some of the destinations that you may cover in the itinerary include Indira Bangalow, a visit to Kufri, exploring the Himalayan Nature Park, and then visiting the Himalayan Zoo. After all this, your leisure time is booked by taking you around Christ Church, Scandal Point, and The Ridge. This is for your leisure time, so you can plan it however you want. Day 7: Departure After your morning breakfast at the resort, you'll be checking out. You can proceed to the airport and continue with the rest of your journey. Himachal Pradesh.Essential Travel Information for Himacha: Here’s all the important information to know about the Himachal Pradesh tours:
Himachal Pradesh.Best Time to Visit Himachal Pradesh: The best time to book your Himachal Pradesh tour package depends on the type of experience you want to enjoy. Both summers and winters are great in Himachal. If you book a Himachal trip package during the summer months (March to June), you can go sightseeing and enjoy the pleasant weather. On the other hand, booking the package during winter months, mostly from December to February, allows you to witness snowfall and try out winter activities like hiking and biking. Although it is not advisable to visit Himachal during the monsoon, you may prefer to do so if you want to witness lush green mountains and beautiful waterfalls. However, some activities during this time may be limited because of the risk of landslides or floods.
Himachal Pradesh.Best places to visit in Himachal Pradesh: Uncover the best of Himachal with the popular Himachal trip packages by Thomas Cook. From charming hill stations to majestic valleys, here are some of the best places to visit in Himachal Pradesh; Famous places to visit in Himachal Pradesh Kasol Kasol is a quaint hamlet nestled on the banks of the Parvati River in the Parvati Valley. It is one of the best places to see in Himachal Pradesh, especially if you like charming cafes and a laid-back tourism experience. In addition, Kasol’s beautiful trekking trails attract thrill seekers around the year, making it a well-known Himachal tour spot. Some of its main attractions are Manikaran Sahib, Parvati River, hot springs, Nature Park Kasol, etc. Kasol is a common inclusion in many Himachal tour packages. Khajjiar Popularly called the ‘Mini-Switzerland of India’, Khajjiar is one of the most beautiful places to visit in Himachal Pradesh. The place features vast grassy meadows surrounded by dense forest and mighty hills, which create some outstanding scenery. Its main attractions, which are also covered in Himachal travel packages, include the Khajjiar Lake, Kalatop Wildlife Sanctuary, Khajji Nag Temple, etc. It is also a major adventure hub, offering activities like zorbing, paragliding, horse-riding, etc. You can find Khajjiar featured in almost all Himachal trip packages. Rohtang Pass Linking Lahaul and Spiti Valleys with Kullu Valley, Rohtang La is one of the most alluring Himachal tourist attractions that you can check out. It is a high mountain pass with an elevation of 3,980 metres and is open to tourists between May and November every year. Tourists in thousands visit this mountain pass for its breathtaking snow-covered landscapes and thrilling adventures. Popular Himachal tour packages often feature the Rohtang Pass. Make arrangements for the Rohtang La permit prior to the Himachal tour. Also, avoid carrying non-biodegradable plastic bags or waste, tobacco products, and styrofoam products when visiting the pass. Also, when booking Himachal trip packages, check whether it includes the pass for Rohtang. Hill stations to visit in Himachal Pradesh Shimla Shimla, the state’s summer capital and a common stop of Himachal trip packages, is one of the most visited hill stations in India. This hill town boasts a diverse culture and past. To this day, the essence of the bygone colonial era is evident through its old buildings and city streets. On your Himachal tour, check out Shimla’s popular spots like Jakhu Temple, Kalka-Shimla Toy Train, Mall Road, Christ Church, Green Valley, Viceregal Lodge, etc. Find Himachal packages with Shimla as a standard inclusion on Thomas Cook. Dharamshala The alluring hill town of Dharamshala serves as the state’s winter capital. It is home to Tibetan communities and is the holy abode of the 14th Dalai Lama. Exploring Dharamshala and McLeodganj is like exploring a part of Tibet and Tibetan Buddhism. You can book Himachal packages featuring Dharamshala from our website. During your Himachal trip, consider checking out Dharamshala’s main attractions like the Dharamshala Cricket Stadium, St. John in the Wilderness Church, Gyuto Monastery, Namgyal Monastery, Bhagsu Falls, etc., most of which are featured under sightseeing in popular Himachal packages. For more attractive deals on Himachal holiday packages featuring popular tourist places, check out our page. Dalhousie Dalhousie is a well-known hill station in Himachal Pradesh. Situated on five hills with an elevation of 1,970 metres, this hill town was established in 1854 and was named after Lord Dalhousie, the then British Governor-General in India. It is among the most popular places to visit in Himachal, thanks to its alluring landscapes and rich colonial heritage. When visiting Dalhousie on your Himachal trip, check out Panchpula, Dainkund Peak, Satdhara Falls, St. John’s Church, etc. Find Himachal tour packages featuring a stay in Dalhousie on our website.
Himachal Pradesh.How to Reach Himachal?: By Air: Your Himachal tour package price will increase if you're travelling by air. The airports in Himachal, Shimla, Kullu-Manali and Dharamsala (Kangra airport) are small, so you'll have to take a flight to Delhi. Upon your arrival at the Delhi airport, you can either take a connecting flight or set out on the road trip. Shimla airport is located 40km away from the main city. By Road: A road trip to Himachal offers breathtaking views, but it's also a long journey. It has eight national highways and 19 State highways, ensuring great connectivity to other parks of India. If you're taking a road trip, you can also explore the remote destinations. However, you'll have to increase the duration of your Himachal Pradesh tour package for road trips. By Train: Pathankot to Shimla and Kalka to Shimla are the two prominent train routes available. If you're taking this route, you can get a great view of the hilly regions. If you book Himachal holiday packages during the summer months, you get two extra special holiday train options on the Kalka-Shimla route.
Himachal Pradesh.Travel Tips and Safety Guidelines: Here are some travel tips and safety guidelines for a trip to Himachal Pradesh: Always carry your identity proof documents while visiting Himachal. Monitor the weather conditions before finalising your Himachal Pradesh tour package. You must carry your personal medicines and avoid consuming street food. Before going sightseeing, you must keep your valuables and luggage in your hotel. When travelling during the monsoon, ensure that you choose safe destinations and avoid areas prone to landslides and flooding.
Himachal Pradesh.What to Pack for Your Himachal Trip?: Always pack all your essentials for your Himachal trip. Here are some things you must have: Bring comfortable clothes, but also include layers and outerwear to protect you from the winter. If you visit high-altitude regions, carry medicines and stay hydrated. A sunscreen with high SPF and lip balm is a must. For emergencies, keep an extra charger, a powerbank, and a small torch in your backpack. Organise your toiletries, makeup items and accessories in ziplock bags. If you're carrying a camera and its accessories, ensure their safety to prevent the risk of being lost.
Himachal Pradesh.Himachal Tour Package Price and Trip Cos: The Himachal tour package price completely depends on how you plan it. There are affordable packages as well as luxury packages. These affordable packages come with luxury accommodations and more. However, if you want a simple Himachal trip package with fewer inclusions, especially in terms of activities, the cost would be significantly lower. The Himachal Pradesh trip also varies depending on the number of people travelling. For example, group tours to Himachal Pradesh are usually cheaper than the regular ones. You may explore the wide range of packages available and select one that suits your needs. Always keep in mind that affordable tour packages may not include all the elements you wish to enjoy. On the other hand, adding extra activities and customising your trip packages would significantly increase the cost. A trip to Himachal can surely be one of the best things to relish, whether you're visiting during winter or summer. You can book your Himachal Pradesh tour package and view a list of activities you can enjoy. Some of the factors affecting the Himachal tour package price are as follows: Duration The longer the duration, the higher the cost. Shorter Himachal Pradesh holiday packages are usually cheaper than the longer ones. The shorter ones typically have a duration of 4-5 days, wherein you can explore the main sightseeing spots. On the other hand, the longer trips, which are usually 6-7 days, allow you to go sightseeing and enjoy various activities. Inclusions You may want to consider the inclusions to determine the Himachal tour package price. Usually, the packages with inclusions such as accommodation, local transport and meals are slightly affordable. However, if you want luxury accommodation or extra amenities, your Himachal trip cost would eventually increase. Time of Travel Travelling to Himachal during the peak tourist season often results in higher costs. Since the place is so much in demand during this time, everything gets sold out quickly. This eventually increases the cost of amenities, especially for last-minute bookings. If you are planning during peak season, try to book early to secure good deals and discounts. Activities The activities you plan on trying will have a significant impact on the cost of the Himachal Pradesh package. If you just plan to try regular activities like sightseeing, the cost wouldn't be high. On the other hand, if you wish to go paragliding, trekking, river rafting and other such adventure activities, you'll have to shell out some extra money.
Himachal Pradesh.Where to Stay and What to Eat in Himacha: The Himachal tour packages offer a wide range of experiences, from delicious food to great accommodations. Let's explore some that you can try: Food Highlights in Himachal On your trip to Himachal Pradesh, you'll likely find yourself in a food heaven with all the delicious options available. Here are some of the top food options to try during your Himachal Pradesh travel packages: Madra: This is one of the most popular delicacies served in Himachal Pradesh, made of chickpeas. It is a representation of the Himachali food culture. Madra tastes the best with chapati, rice and babru. You can find Madra in almost all the restaurants around Chamba and Kangra. Tudkiya Bhath: You can never go wrong with the delicious Tudkiya Bhath in Himachal Pradesh. This is a unique way through which the Pahadi people cook their food. It contains various ingredients like yoghurt, potatoes and lentils that make the overall dish tasty. Spicy Lotus Stems: You might be surprised, but this is a dish you won't want to miss. Flavoured with some extra garlic, tomato and cinnamon powder, it goes really well with the Tudkiya Bhath. Siddu: You must consume Siddu when in Himachal. In fact, it is one of the best foods often served on the Himachal Pradesh trip packages. It's a side dish made of wheat flour. The fermented wheat dough is cooked to make delicious servings. You can find it in any of the local restaurants in Himachal Pradesh.
Himachal Pradesh.Accommodation Options in Himachal Prades: The Himachal Pradesh tour packages offer a wide range of accommodation options that you must try. You can choose any of the accommodation types for your needs. While the Himachal tour packages usually offer budget-friendly guesthouses, hostels and homestays, you can select cottages, villas and bungalows too. For more luxury and unique experiences, you can also try tented lodges and farmstays. Himachal Pradesh has numerous boutique hotels as well that you must not miss out on.
Himachal Pradesh.Activities to Explore in Himachal Beyond: Apart from sightseeing, you can try a lot of other things on your Himachal Pradesh tourism tour packages. These usually include trying out a wide range of fun activities, shopping, and so much more. So, the best Himachal tour packages are the ones that offer a complete experience. Adventure Activities Himachal Pradesh is not only known for its ethereal beauty, but also for the wide range of activities you can try. Some of the top adventure activities you can try during your Himachal Pradesh trip packages include: Trekking: Himachal Pradesh offers several trekking destinations, with Hampta Pass being one of the most popular. You can also go regular trekking around Spiti, Garhwal and Kinnaur. Winter sports: During winter, Himachal turns into a winter wonderland. With Manali and other places being covered in snow, you get a chance to indulge in a wide range of winter sports and activities. For example, you can also go heli-skiing in Solang Valley. Water sports: The fiercely flowing rivers of Himachal Pradesh are something you must not miss out on if you're an adventure buff. You can try rafting on the Beas River or go angling around the Tirthan River. It gives you a complete adrenaline rush, but that's totally worth it. If you wish to explore more adventure activities in Himachal Pradesh, you would have to customise your Himachal Pradesh tour package. Therefore, you can include a variety of activities like camping, rock climbing, mountain biking and more.
Himachal Pradesh.Local Culture and Villages: Himachal Pradesh is deeply rooted in local culture and tradition. As you embark on your journey with Himachal Pradesh trip packages, you'll discover the answers on your own. After exploring the main spots, it's the offbeat and remote destinations that will give you clarity. If you wish to explore the local culture of Himachal, it's best to stay in homestays. You can also take some time to explore the Gaddi community. Being friendly with the people and connecting with them can be one of the best ways to know the local culture.
Himachal Pradesh.Shopping and Souvenirs in Himachal: The rich cultural heritage of Himachal ensures that there are numerous items available for purchase. While the best experience is crafted in Himachal trip packages, it's also advisable to look for some souvenirs and keepsakes from the region. Here's what you can shop for as souvenirs in Himachal: Kullu Shawls: These shawls are extremely popular for their intricate patterns and vibrant colours. Made out of high-quality wool, the Kullu Shawls are very warm and have traditional designs. Himachali topi: This is one of the best souvenirs that you can buy. They're also made of wool and have very beautiful, colourful designs. It's a very prominent symbol of Himachal's culture. Kangra tea: This is tea grown in Kangra, known mostly for its flavour and aroma. It is available in two varieties: green and black, perfect for tea enthusiasts. Kangra paintings: If you're an art lover, consider adding some Kangra paintings to your home collection. These paintings usually cover stories about natural beauty, royal courts and more.
Himachal Pradesh.Why Book Group Tours to Himachal Pradesh: Planning to go on a Himachal Pradesh trip? You need the best booking partner. That's where Thomas Cook steps in. Thomas Cook has been associated with planning trips for years, ensuring everyone gets a fun and memorable experience. Whether it's solo tours or group tours, Thomas Cook caters to the needs of all. End-to-end trip planning: From travel to accommodation to meals, Thomas Cook takes care of it all.
