Varanasi.Places to Visit in Varanasi: Varanasi, often hailed as the spiritual capital of India, boasts an array of captivating attractions that offer a glimpse into its rich cultural heritage and religious significance. Let's delve deeper into the top places to visit in Varanasi: Dashashwamedh Ghat At the heart of Varanasi's spiritual fervour lies Dashashwamedh Ghat, where the iconic Ganga Aarti ceremony unfolds each evening. Here, priests clad in traditional attire perform intricate rituals, accompanied by the rhythmic chants of hymns and the flickering glow of oil lamps. Witnessing this mesmerising spectacle against the backdrop of the Ganges during your Varanasi city tour is an experience that resonates deeply with visitors. Kashi Vishwanath Temple Dedicated to Lord Shiva, the Kashi Vishwanath Temple stands as a testament to Varanasi's spiritual legacy and is one of the iconic places to visit in Varanasi. Devotees flock to this sacred site to seek blessings and offer prayers amidst the divine atmosphere. The temple's towering spire and intricate architecture leave visitors awestruck, while the devotees engage in rituals and ceremonies that date back centuries. A must add to your Varanasi itinerary, when planning your tour to Varanasi. Sarnath Venture beyond the city limits to explore the ancient Buddhist site of Sarnath, where Lord Buddha delivered his first sermon. Amidst the tranquil surroundings, visitors can admire the majestic Dhamek Stupa, which marks the spot of the Buddha's teachings. The Mulagandha Kuti Vihar offers a serene retreat, housing exquisite frescoes and relics that depict the life of the Enlightened One, making it one of the iconic places to see in Varanasi. Manikarnika Ghat Steeped in centuries-old traditions, Manikarnika Ghat offers a glimpse into Varanasi's profound relationship with death and rebirth, making it one of the most serene and best places to visit in Varanasi. Cremation ceremonies take place here around the clock, as families bid farewell to their departed loved ones amidst the crackling flames and billowing smoke. Witnessing these rituals, while solemn, provides a deeper understanding of the cycle of life and death in Hindu culture. Assi Ghat As the southernmost ghat in Varanasi, Assi Ghat exudes a serene charm that captivates visitors on their journey with Varanasi travel packages. Early risers gather here to witness the ethereal beauty of the sunrise, casting a golden glow over the tranquil waters of the Ganges. A leisurely boat ride along the river provides a unique perspective of the city's skyline, dotted with ancient temples and bustling ghats. Ramnagar Fort Perched on the eastern bank of the Ganges, Ramnagar Fort exudes regal splendour and historical significance. Built-in the 18th century by the Maharaja of Varanasi, the fort boasts an impressive museum that showcases a diverse array of exhibits, from vintage cars and royal artefacts to a unique armoury collection. Exploring the fort's labyrinthine halls and courtyards offers a glimpse into Varanasi's royal past, making it a must-visit with Varanasi travel packages. Banaras Hindu University (BHU) Explore the sprawling campus of Banaras Hindu University, a renowned seat of learning and cultural heritage. The university's iconic Bharat Kala Bhavan museum houses a vast collection of art and artefacts, including ancient sculptures, textiles, and manuscripts. Visitors can immerse themselves in the rich tapestry of Indian history and culture showcased within its walls. Bharat Mata Temple Pay homage to the spirit of Mother India at the Bharat Mata Temple, where a unique marble relief map of India serves as the focal point of worship on your trip with Varanasi travel packages. Symbolising national unity and diversity, the temple celebrates India's rich cultural heritage and geographical diversity. Visitors can marvel at the intricately carved map, depicting the country's varied landscapes and historical landmarks. Durga Temple (Monkey Temple) Dedicated to Goddess Durga, the Durga Temple, fondly known as the Monkey Temple, is a vibrant testament to Varanasi's religious diversity. Visitors are greeted by playful monkeys that roam the temple's premises, adding to its lively atmosphere. The temple's colourful facades and intricate carvings draw worshippers and tourists alike, who come to seek blessings and marvel at its architectural beauty. Tulsi Manas Temple Immerse yourself in the serene ambience of the Tulsi Manas Temple, dedicated to Lord Rama and adorned with verses from the Hindu epic Ramayana. The temple's white marble facade and ornate carvings create a tranquil oasis amidst the bustling city. Visitors can partake in prayer and meditation, soaking in the spiritual energy that permeates the surroundings. Exploring these Varanasi tourist places is made convenient with Varanasi sightseeing tour, offering Varanasi tour guide and transportation arrangements. Whether you're seeking spiritual enlightenment, cultural immersion, or historical insights, Varanasi has something to offer every traveller. Plan your Varanasi tour itinerary today and embark on a journey of discovery in this ancient city along the sacred Ganges.
Varanasi.Best Hill Stations/ Best Beaches in Vara: Varanasi, nestled along the banks of the sacred Ganges River, is renowned for its spiritual significance and cultural heritage. While it may not boast hill stations or beaches within its vicinity, Varanasi serves as a gateway to nearby destinations that offer scenic hill retreats and serene beach getaways. Nearest Hill Stations: Rajgir Located approximately 240 kilometres from Varanasi, Rajgir is a picturesque hill station nestled in the verdant hills of Bihar. Surrounded by lush forests and tranquil lakes, Rajgir is a popular pilgrimage site for Buddhists and Jains. Visitors can take a cable car ride to the summit of Ratnagiri Hill for panoramic views of the surrounding countryside. Sarnath Although not a traditional hill station, Sarnath holds historical and spiritual significance as the place where Lord Buddha delivered his first sermon. Situated just a few kilometres from Varanasi, Sarnath offers a peaceful retreat amidst lush greenery and ancient ruins. Visitors can explore the Dhamek Stupa, Chaukhandi Stupa, and other Buddhist monuments that dot the landscape. Bodh Gaya Renowned as the site where Gautama Buddha achieved enlightenment, Bodh Gaya is located approximately 250 kilometres from Varanasi. Set amidst lush hills and picturesque landscapes, Bodh Gaya attracts spiritual seekers and nature enthusiasts alike. The Mahabodhi Temple, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, is a must-visit destination, radiating a sense of peace and serenity. Nearest Beaches: Varanasi Ghats While Varanasi is not known for its beaches in the traditional sense, the ghats along the Ganges River offer a unique waterfront experience in the Varanasi itinerary. Visitors can stroll along the riverbanks, take a boat ride on the Ganges, or simply soak in the serene ambience of the ghats. The Dasaswamedh Ghat and Assi Ghat are popular spots to witness sunrise and sunset, respectively. Patna Situated around 250 kilometres from Varanasi, Patna is the capital city of Bihar and boasts a scenic riverfront along the Ganges. While the city does not have sandy beaches, the riverbanks offer tranquil spots for picnics, boat rides, and leisurely walks. Visitors can explore historic landmarks such as the Golghar, Patna Museum, and Mahatma Gandhi Setu Bridge. Allahabad Located approximately 125 kilometres from Varanasi, Allahabad is renowned for its confluence of three rivers – the Ganges, Yamuna, and the mythical Saraswati. While not a beach destination per se, the riverbanks in Allahabad offer picturesque views and serene surroundings. Visitors can explore the historic Kumbh Mela grounds and enjoy leisurely walks along the riverfront. Varanasi.Adventurous things to do in Varanasi: While Varanasi is renowned for its spiritual and cultural offerings, it also presents opportunities for adventure enthusiasts seeking adrenaline-pumping experiences amidst its ancient charm. Here are a few adventurous things to do on your Varanasi one day tour: Parasailing over Varanasi Soar high above the cityscape of Varanasi with an adrenaline-fuelled parasailing adventure. Strap into a harness attached to a parachute and take to the skies, as you are towed by a speedboat along the Ganges River. Enjoy panoramic views of Varanasi's ghats, temples, and bustling streets from a bird's-eye perspective, creating memories that last a lifetime. River Rafting on the Ganges Experience the thrill of navigating the rapids of the Ganges River with an exhilarating river rafting expedition. Join a Varanasi tour guide that takes you through the turbulent waters, offering stunning views of Varanasi's picturesque landscapes and historic landmarks along the riverbanks. Feel the rush of adrenaline as you conquer the rapids and navigate the twists and turns of this iconic river. Trekking in the Surrounding Hills Escape the hustle and bustle of the city and explore the scenic hills surrounding Varanasi with a thrilling trekking expedition. Venture into the lush forests and rugged terrain of the nearby hills, accompanied by an experienced Varanasi tour guide who leads you through hidden trails and picturesque landscapes. Immerse yourself in the tranquillity of nature and witness breathtaking vistas of Varanasi's skyline from elevated viewpoints. Hot Air Ballooning Embark on a breathtaking hot air balloon ride over Varanasi, offering a unique vantage point to admire the city's beauty from above with a well-curated Varanasi travel plan. Drift serenely across the skyline as the sun rises or sets, casting a golden glow over the ancient cityscape and the meandering Ganges River below. Capture stunning aerial photographs of Varanasi's iconic landmarks and vibrant street scenes from your lofty vantage point. Cycling Tours Embark on an adventurous cycling tour through the streets and alleys of Varanasi, exploring its vibrant markets, ancient temples, and bustling ghats. Pedal your way through the city's winding lanes and narrow bylanes, discovering hidden gems and off-the-beaten-path attractions along the way. Feel the pulse of Varanasi's vibrant culture as you interact with locals and immerse yourself in the city's bustling street life with our Varanasi travel plan. These adventurous activities offer a unique perspective on the Varanasi itinerary, allowing travellers to experience the city's rich heritage and natural beauty in an exciting and exhilarating way. Whether you're navigating the rapids of the Ganges or soaring high above the city skyline, Varanasi promises unforgettable adventures for those seeking thrills and excitement. Plan your Varanasi holiday packages today and embark on a journey of adrenaline-fuelled exploration in this ancient city. Plan your dream vacation to Varanasi with affordable Varanasi trip cost options available.
Varanasi.What to Eat in Varanasi?: When it comes to culinary delights, Varanasi offers a delectable array of dishes that tantalise the taste buds and leave a lasting impression on food enthusiasts. Here are five must-try delicacies that capture the essence of Varanasi's vibrant food culture: Kachori Sabzi: Breakfast in Varanasi isn't complete without a serving of crunchy kachoris filled with lentils seasoned with spices, accompanied by a steaming-hot, spicy potato curry. The most recommended places? Ram Bhandar near Thatheri Bazar and Chachi Kachori Shop in Lanka. Don't miss trying this during your visit to Varanasi! Banarasi Lassi: Thick and creamy, flavoured with rabri and dry fruits and served in kulhads, Banarasi Lassi is a must-try. Blue Lassi Shop is renowned for serving it! Baati Chokha: A country treat, baati chokha is wheat balls filled with sattu, grilled on charcoal, and accompanied by a spicy brinjal and potato mash. The best ones are available at Baati Chokha Restaurant near Telibagh. Malaiyyo: During your winter visit, do not miss Malaiyyo, a light milk foam dessert that is saffron and cardamom flavoured. The elusive sweet can be had only in the morning at Thatheri Bazar. Ensure your Varanasi tour takes in this one! Tamatar Chaat: A fusion like no other, this smashed tomato-based appetizer is fiery, sour, and fantastic. Try it only at Kashi Chaat Bhandar. Aloo Kachori: These are different types of kachoris, filled with spicy mashed potatoes and served along with tangy chutneys. You can find these at every street corner, but try your best at Deena Chat Bhandar in Thatheri Bazar. Ideal for a quick meal while on your Varanasi tour. Rabri Jalebi: This is a traditional sweet dish, hot jalebis soaked in sugar syrup, served with thick, creamy rabri—heaven on earth! Om Shree Ram Bhandar is the place to go for it. Dahi Chutney Golgappe: These crunchy goodies are filled with spicy potato, curd, and tangy chutneys—a burst of flavours in each bite. Go to Kashi Chaat Bhandar for the best ones! No matter what’s on your itinerary, make sure your Varanasi packages include a food walk. This city is a paradise for food lovers, and every dish tells a story of its rich heritage!
Varanasi.Must Visit Restaurants/Resorts in Varana: When it comes to dining and accommodation in Varanasi, there are several exceptional options that cater to diverse preferences and budgets. Here are four must-visit restaurants and resorts that promise an unforgettable experience on your Varanasi trip plan: Restaurants: Baati Chokha Immerse yourself in the rustic charm of traditional Bihari cuisine at Baati Chokha, a renowned restaurant that celebrates the culinary heritage of the region. Located on the outskirts of Varanasi, this quaint eatery offers a rustic ambience and authentic flavours, with dishes like baati (baked wheat flour dumplings), chokha (spicy mashed potato and roasted eggplant curry), and litti (stuffed wheat balls) delighting the palate. Savour the hearty flavours of North Indian cuisine while soaking in the serene countryside surroundings on your Varanasi trip plan. Brown Bread Bakery Nestled in the heart of Varanasi's bustling streets, Brown Bread Bakery is a popular haunt for travellers seeking delicious food and a relaxed ambience. Known for its delectable baked goods, hearty breakfast options, and flavourful international cuisine, this charming eatery is a favourite among locals and tourists alike. Enjoy a leisurely meal amidst rustic decor and vibrant artwork, making it a must-visit dining destination on your Varanasi travel plan. Zaaika Restaurant: Step into the elegant surroundings of Zaaika Restaurant and embark on a culinary journey through the rich flavours of Indian cuisine. Located in a heritage property in the heart of Varanasi, this upscale restaurant exudes old-world charm and sophistication. Delight your taste buds with a diverse menu featuring traditional North Indian delicacies, Mughlai specialities, and innovative fusion dishes, all expertly prepared using the finest ingredients and authentic spices on your Varanasi trip plan. Open Hand Cafe Perched atop a rooftop terrace overlooking the bustling streets of Varanasi, Open Hand Cafe offers a tranquil escape from the chaos below. Known for its serene ambience, friendly service, and delicious vegetarian fare, this rooftop eatery is a favourite among discerning travellers. Indulge in a variety of healthy and flavourful dishes, including fresh salads, sandwiches, and Indian classics, while enjoying panoramic views of the cityscape on your Varanasi trip plan. Resorts: Ganges Grand Situated on the banks of the Ganges River, Ganges Grand offers a serene and tranquil retreat amidst the hustle and bustle of Varanasi. This luxurious resort boasts spacious rooms and suites, modern amenities, and panoramic views of the river and city skyline. Guests can relax and unwind in the resort's lush gardens, indulge in rejuvenating spa treatments, or enjoy a leisurely swim in the outdoor pool on their Varanasi travel plan. Brijrama Palace Experience the epitome of luxury and heritage hospitality at Brijrama Palace, a majestic riverside retreat located along the banks of the Ganges River. Housed within a restored 18th-century palace, this opulent resort offers lavish accommodations, exquisite dining options, and unparalleled views of the river and surrounding ghats. Immerse yourself in the regal ambience of the palace, complete with intricately carved architecture, lush gardens, and impeccable service on your Varanasi travel plan. Ramada Plaza JHV Varanasi Experience unparalleled luxury and comfort at Ramada Plaza JHV Varanasi, a premier resort located in the heart of the city. Boasting spacious rooms, elegant decor, and modern amenities, this resort offers a perfect blend of sophistication and relaxation. Guests can dine at the resort's fine dining restaurants, unwind at the spa and wellness centre, or enjoy a leisurely stroll along the beautifully landscaped gardens. The Gateway Hotel Ganges Escape to The Gateway Hotel Ganges for a serene and idyllic getaway in Varanasi. Nestled amidst verdant gardens and overlooking the Ganges River, this upscale resort offers luxurious accommodations, world-class amenities, and warm hospitality. Guests can dine at the hotel's signature restaurant, relax by the poolside, or explore nearby attractions with the assistance of the resort's knowledgeable concierge team. These restaurants and resorts exemplify the diverse culinary and hospitality offerings of Varanasi, ensuring a memorable and enjoyable experience for travellers seeking a taste of luxury and comfort. Whether indulging in delectable cuisine or unwinding amidst scenic surroundings, these establishments promise an unforgettable stay in the spiritual capital of India. Discover Varanasi's charm without breaking the budget with our unbeatable Varanasi trip cost.
Varanasi.What to Shop in Varanasi?: Varanasi, renowned for its rich cultural heritage and vibrant markets, offers a delightful shopping experience for visitors. Here are a few must-shop items and areas to explore while in Varanasi: Handwoven Brocades Varanasi is famous for its hand-woven brocades, known for their intricate patterns and luxurious textures. Explore the narrow lanes of the city's old quarter to discover traditional workshops and boutique stores showcasing a stunning collection of brocade fabrics, shawls, and stoles. From classic floral designs to contemporary motifs, these handcrafted creations are perfect for adding a touch of elegance to your wardrobe. Banarasi Silk Sarees No Varanasi trip packages are complete without indulging in the timeless elegance of Banarasi silk sarees. Renowned for their exquisite craftsmanship and intricate designs, these luxurious sarees are a symbol of tradition and opulence. Head to the bustling markets of Vishwanath Gali, Thatheri Bazar, and Chowk to browse through a dazzling array of Banarasi silk sarees, each adorned with intricate zari work and motifs. Wooden Toys and Handicrafts Discover the charm of Varanasi's traditional wooden toys and handicrafts, crafted with precision and attention to detail. Wander through the lanes of the city's handicraft markets to find an eclectic mix of hand-carved wooden toys, lacquerware items, and decorative artefacts. These vibrant creations showcase the rich cultural heritage of Varanasi and make for perfect gifts and mementoes. Brassware and Metalwork Immerse yourself in Varanasi's rich artisanal heritage by exploring its vibrant markets brimming with exquisite brassware and metalwork on your journey with Varanasi trip packages. From ornate puja items and temple bells to intricately carved utensils and home decor pieces, Varanasi offers a treasure trove of traditional metal crafts. Visit the bustling markets of Thatheri Bazar and Godowlia to find unique souvenirs and keepsakes crafted by skilled artisans. Street Food and Local Delicacies Indulge your taste buds in Varanasi's delectable street food and local delicacies, which are a blend of flavours from across India. Sample mouth-watering treats such as kachori sabzi, Chaat, litti chokha, and malaiyyo from the city's bustling street food stalls and markets on your journey with Varanasi trip packages. Don't miss the opportunity to savour traditional sweets like rabri, jalebi, and lassi, which are sure to tantalise your taste buds. Ganga Jal and Spiritual Artefacts Experience the spiritual essence of Varanasi by purchasing sacred items such as Ganga jal (holy water from the Ganges), rudraksha beads, yantras, and idols of Hindu deities. Explore the bustling markets near the ghats, such as Dashashwamedh Ghat and Assi Ghat, to find an array of spiritual artefacts and religious souvenirs. These items serve as reminders of your spiritual journey and offer blessings from the holy city of Varanasi. Varanasi's vibrant markets and bustling lanes offer a delightful shopping experience, where you can immerse yourself in the city's rich cultural heritage and discover unique treasures to cherish. Whether you're exploring the traditional silk markets or sampling the local street food delights, Varanasi promises a memorable shopping excursion for visitors of all tastes and preferences on their journey with Varanasi trip packages. Explore Varanasi's rich culture and heritage without worrying about Varanasi trip cost.
Varanasi.Interesting Facts about the Varanasi pla: Varanasi, also known as Kashi or Banaras, is one of the oldest continuously inhabited cities in the world and holds immense cultural and spiritual significance. Here are ten interesting facts about this enchanting city: Did you know that Varanasi is one of the world's oldest continuously inhabited cities? It's more than 3,000 years old and is said to have been founded by Lord Shiva himself. When you take a Varanasi tour, you're walking through history literally. Death is liberation in Varanasi. Elderly individuals from all over the country come to the city, believing that they will be saved if they die here. Even some packages for Varanasi include stops at Mukti Bhavan, where patients wait for their last ride. There is a sunken Shiva temple at Scindia Ghat, which tilts towards the river. It's said to have sunken hundreds of years ago and still stands partially underwater, providing a mysterious twist to your visit to Varanasi. Varanasi is home to approximately 88 ghats, each with its own tale. While Dashashwamedh Ghat stands popular for the majestic Ganga Aarti, Manikarnika Ghat is renowned for having its perpetual funeral pyres. If you go for one of the tour packages of Varanasi, you will be able to see these opposing yet captivating places. To be adventurous, head to the Baba Keenaram Sthal, a shrine devoted to the Aghori sect and their unorthodox rituals. A one-of-a-kind inclusion in many Varanasi tour packages! Banaras Hindu University (BHU) is Asia's largest residential university. Even if you are not a student, it is a must-visit during your tour of Varanasi! The 18th-century Ramnagar Fort features a clock that doesn't only indicate time—it indicates the astronomical position of the sun, moon, planets, and zodiac constellations. Constructed ages ago when there was no modern technology, it still works! A visit to this fort on your Varanasi tour gives a dash of royal history to your travel schedule. A trip to Varanasi is not all about temples and rituals—it's a journey of history, mystery, and spirituality. So, when are you booking yours? These fascinating facts about Varanasi highlight the city's rich history, cultural heritage, and spiritual significance, making it a must-visit destination for travellers seeking an authentic Indian experience while exploring the best places in Varanasi. Explore Varanasi holiday packages today and embark on a journey of discovery in this timeless city. Unlock the secrets of Varanasi's spirituality at an affordable Varanasi trip cost.

Brazil.Brazil Tour Packages: Brazil, the largest country in Latin America and the world's fifth-largest, is a popular tourist destination. Visit some of the most beautiful and jaw-dropping places in Brazil by booking one of the many Brazil Tours. These vacation packages include tours to ancient colonial towns, sandy beaches, and the Amazon's thick jungles. Diversity in Brazil packages makes it a one-of-a-kind travel destination for many travelers. Brazil is famous for its clear blue lagoons, vibrant cosmopolitan cities, samba, and endless stretches of warm sand. Brazil tour packages from India offer many options to help tourists remember their time in this beautiful country. Select your ideal vacation package and be amazed by the wonders of Brazil, its distinct cuisine, rich culture, beautiful beaches, waterfalls, and adventure. Take advantage of discounts and special offers on tour packages to experience thrilling Brazilian adventures and enjoy lazy afternoons on tropical beaches. Don't forget the fantastic nightlife! Here is a detailed guide to all the popular destinations and best things to do in Brazil's vast and brilliant country
Brazil.Brazil travel restrictions for Covid: With Brazil Travel Guidelines stay up to date with local regulations throughout your trip.
Brazil.About Brazil Holidays: Brazil is an exciting and vibrant country. It is an eclectic mix of people, geography, culture, and landscapes that spans a large area and has five different time zones. Beaches, the Amazon rainforest, the Rio festival, a sinful cuisine, legendary soccer players, friendly locals, and the world-famous Brazilian coffee bean are all here. Ecosystems found in this South American country are home to many plant and animal species. Planning a vacation to Brazil can be difficult, with many things to see and do.
Brazil.Most Popular Brazil Tour Packages: Brazil Holiday Packages No. of Days Price* South American Delights 10 Nights/ 11 Days Rs. 3 50 615 South American Delights with Amazon 13 Nights/ 14 Days Rs. 4 32 378 South American Delights with Add On Ushuaia and Patagonia 15 Nights/ 16 Days Rs. 4 99 549 South American Delights with Amazon, Ushuaia and Patagonia 18 Nights/ 19 days Rs. 5 81 312
Brazil.Popular things in Brazil: Brazil, South America's largest country, has Portuguese as its official language. Brazil is known for its fantastic festivals, the locals' swing, friendliness, natural wonders, delectable food and beverages. It hosts a famous carnival and is home to talented soccer players. It is also known for its beautiful waterfalls, tropical beaches, and rainforest. Here are some well-known things from Brazil: Caipirinha: It is a Brazilian alcoholic drink made with cachaça, lemon, sugar, and ice. It originated in São Paulo. It has two variations—Caipiroska, made with vodka instead of cachaça, and Caipirissima, made with rum. Caipirinha is an ideal drink for a hot summer night and the perfect fuel to help you dance all night. Carnival: This is one of the most well-known carnival festivals in the world. You can enjoy Samba dance performances, a diverse menu of food and beverages, brightly coloured costumes, and a festive atmosphere. The Carnival began in the 1830s as a Portuguese tradition to commemorate Lent. Over time, street musicians and dancing were introduced. The Rio Carnival features over 300 bands and 100 parades. Soccer: Soccer is Brazil's most popular sport, with the national team consistently ranking among the best in the world. They have won five World Cups! If you're a soccer fan, you must be aware that Brazil is home to some of the world's greatest soccer legends, including Ronaldinho Gacho, Neymar, Ronaldo, and Pelé. For Brazilians, football is a passion, and they frequently refer to their country as “o Pas do Futebol” (the country of football). Coffee: Brazil produces one-third of the world's coffee, making it the world's largest producer. It has held this position for 150 years. Most coffee plantations are in the southeastern states of Minas Gerais, So Paulo, and Paraná, where the environment and climate are ideal for this plant.
Brazil.Souvenirs to Shop for in Brazil: Cachaça: If you want authentic Cachaça, you must buy it from Brazil. This sugarcane-based drink is widely available, thanks to many local distilleries. Paraty, a small coastal town in Rio de Janeiro with several excellent distilleries, is the best place to buy cachaça. Gemstones: Minas Gerais is well-known for its gems, particularly emeralds. It is the best place in Brazil to find exquisite necklaces, bracelets, or rings set with local emeralds. Brazil produces roughly 65% of the world's coloured gemstones, and all major cities have specialised shops where you can buy genuine precious and semi-precious stones. Cangas: These vibrant and colourful beach towels are another famous Brazilian gift. They can be found almost everywhere in Brazil, including nearly every local store. Cangas and beachwear come in a variety of styles and colours. Brazilian flip-flops: Havaianas, a well-known flip-flops brand, is also based in Brazil. These slippers are widely available in Brazil at a low cost. They come in various sizes and shapes and are one of the best souvenirs you can buy. Divino Espirito Santo: In some parts of the country, particularly Minas Gerais, dove carvings are extremely common. They come in various sizes ranging from a few inches to several feet. These carvings depict the Holy Spirit and are placed over doorways to provide protection and good luck to those who enter. They are affordable and an easy find.
Brazil.Best time to visit Brazil: The best time to visit Brazil is between November and March. These are the warmest months in the country, with temperatures ranging from 27°C to 33°C. It is ideal for New Year's and Carnival celebrations. Brazil can be visited any time of the year and it enjoys several climatic extremes, none of which are severe enough to prevent travel. In June and July, the northeastern coast gets some rain, but it's usually in the form of afternoon showers.
Brazil.How to Reach Brazil from India?: When flying to Brazil, book flights to Sao Paulo or Rio de Janeiro, which have the country's largest airports. These airports have direct flights to North America , Europe , South America , and cities in Asia and Africa. However, all major cities have airports, though many are domestic. Note that there are no direct flights from India to Brazil. You'll need to get a connecting flight in Abu Dhabi or Dubai to reach Sao Paulo. There are flights from Mumbai, Delhi, Kochi, Chennai, and Thiruvananthapuram. GOL Linhas Aereas, Etihad Airways, and LATAM Airlines Brazil are among the airlines that fly to Brazil. Most international flights land at Guarulhos International Airport in Sao Paulo . From the airport, there are many options to reach the city. You can take a bus or book a prepaid taxi or a metered taxi, which are relatively affordable. To get to downtown Sao Paulo from the airport, you can also try the available train options, including the Airport Express, Connect (including stops), and Line 13 + Line 12, which involves changing trains. To get to the train station, you will have to take a 10-minute taxi ride from the airport. If you haven't budgeted for taxis, you can opt for the Airport Bus Service, which is economical and convenient. The trip to Brazil’s downtown Sao Paulo takes about one and a half hours.
Brazil.Most Popular and Must-visit Destinations: Brazil has over 70 national parks and dozens of monuments, including Christ the Redeemer in Rio de Janeiro, one of the Seven New Wonders of the World. These attractions make the country an exciting destination. Here are some places in Brazil that you must see: São Paulo: Also known as the concrete jungle, São Paulo, a city of over 12 million people, is considered Brazil's business capital. However, that tag doesn't do justice to this exciting city. It has some of the country's best art galleries and museums. You can try delicious dishes at one of the many international restaurants known for their haute cuisine. Head to Praça Roosevelt for hipster bars and a change of pace. And remember to save some stamina so that you can enjoy São Paulo's nightlife. After all, it is known as a city that never sleeps. Rio de Janeiro: Rio de Janeiro is known for its stark contrasts, it perfectly blends golden shores and lush forests with a bustling urban life. It is home to 6 million people and an exotic holiday destination for millions of tourists each year. Aside from prominent sights such as Christ the Redeemer, Sugarloaf Mountain, and Copacabana check out the unforgettable TransCarioca hike that stretches 180 kilometres (112 miles) and you can experience a mesmerizing sunset at Arpoador. Fernando de Noronha: You can take a break from all the travelling by spending time on this island. Fernando de Noronha is a volcanic archipelago 350 kilometres (217 miles) off the coast of Brazil that can only be reached by plane from Recife. This protected area provides several opportunities to relax on golden beaches and get close to wildlife. It has jagged coastlines, untouched scenery, and shallow seas teeming with tropical fish, sharks, turtles, and dolphins. The ecological sanctuary limits the number of visitors per year, which helps to preserve some of Brazil's natural landscapes. Pantanal: If you are looking for an adventure that will take you into the heart of the wilderness, a visit to the Pantanal is a must. The world's largest wetland, located in the centre of Brazil, has spectacular natural landscapes and is home to one of the world's most unique and complex ecosystems. Explore this preserved natural habitat on a guided tour, keep an eye out for caimans, capybaras, hundreds of bird species, and even the occasional shy jaguar.
Brazil.Best Beaches in Brazil: Copacabana: It is a crowded crescent-shaped beach in Rio de Janeiro that stretches over 4 kilometres. It is located in the heart of the city. Here you can participate in watersports such as surfing. You can spend time at cafés, restaurants, hotels, and even visit two historical forts, one of which houses a military museum. These are all located along the boardwalk. Buzios resort town: Armaço dos Buzios, locally known as Buzios, is loved by many international personalities. It is located north of Rio de Janeiro. Ferradura Beach and Geribá Beach are two of the most popular beaches among the town's many beaches. Ferradura Beach is great for snorkelling. And if you want a great surfing experience, head to Geribá Beach, which also has a host of restaurants. Situated around the town's south end, Praia de Tucuns is quite windy, making it ideal for people seeking a picturesque spot with fewer people. Jericoacoara: If you're looking for a private slice of paradise, look no further. The area around Jericoacoara did not have electricity until 1998. Even now, there are no street lights around here. Tourists flock here searching for virgin sands and quiet windsurfing spots. You will be able to see a sky full of stars once the sun sets. Praia de Pipa: Locals who want to rest in a peaceful atmosphere frequent this quiet place. Since this area falls under a protected region, there are laws preventing any major structures from being built here. Here you can sit on the golden sand and enjoy the view of surrounding pink cliffs. You might even spot a dolphin if you're lucky. The Praia do Madeiro beach is just a few minutes away, and you can do many activities there such as kayaking, surfing, swimming, etc.
Brazil.Best Honeymoon Destinations in Brazil: Brazil's landscape and culture form a perfect background for any honeymoon. From the beautiful beaches to samba and the rainforests of the Amazon, Brazil has it all. If you're looking for some honeymoon spots in Brazil, here are the best places for newlyweds to visit: The Amazon: If you are planning a honeymoon in the southern part of the country, you must go to the Amazon. The name itself denotes a remote location, and going on a luxury cruise allows you to have a good time on your honeymoon. You can spend some alone time with your loved ones in the Amazon river by paddleboarding or canoeing. Couples can hire a guide and go on a short trek through the forest to spot endangered animals. Paraty: Another popular honeymoon destination, Paraty is located a few hours from Rio de Janeiro. Paraty is a seaside colonial town and is famous as one of Brazil's most romantic places to visit. You’ll get to experience a blend of culture and architecture here. You can see many national parks and go island hopping. The bay is full of countless spectacular islands and untouched beaches. You can explore these in a single day or a week, depending on the pace of your holiday. If you're feeling particularly adventurous, you can trek through parks and nature reserves as well. If not, you can go for romantic walks along the cobblestone streets lined with well-preserved architectural structures and boutique shops. Florianopolis: Florianopolis, also known as Floripa, is one of the best honeymoon destinations in Brazil. It is also known as Brazil with a European flavour. This location is perfect for couples who want to spend time together on a beach. Couples who enjoy water sports can sail or windsurf in the sea. Couples looking for a romantic getaway will appreciate the city's romantic fine dining options, shopping malls, and resorts. Iguazu Falls: Iguazu Falls, located on the border of Brazil and Argentina, is one of the world's most beautiful natural wonders. Couples can take in breathtaking views of over 240 waterfalls. It is sure to be an unforgettable experience for you. Couples can take a helicopter ride over the waterfall or get wet in a boat. They can even go on a short hike to a nearby forest and spend some quality time there.
Brazil.Top 7 Must-visit Places in Brazil: Christ the Redeemer: Christ the Redeemer is the most famous statue of Jesus Christ in Rio de Janeiro. It is located on the 700-metre (2,300-foot) Corcovado mountain peak and offers a panoramic view of Guanabara Bay's interior to the north and Lagoa Rodrigo de Freitas to the south. The Christ the Redeemer statue, which stands 39.6 metres (130 feet) tall, including its 9.5-meter (31-foot) pedestal, has become an icon of Rio de Janeiro and Brazil. Lencois Maranhenses National Park: When people think of Brazil, an image that immediately pops into their minds is beautiful beaches and lush jungles, not sand dunes. Massive dunes can be found in Lencois Maranhenses, one of the country's most impressive national parks. However, this is not a desert. During July, torrential rains create beautiful clear lagoons that can reach depths of 3 metres (10 feet) in some areas. Interestingly, although these pools are only temporary and disappear during the dry season, you can find fish swimming in them. Bonito: Bonito is the starting point for ecotourists who want to visit some of Brazil's most unique natural attractions, such as spectacular waterfalls and caves. Its most remarkable feature is its crystal-clear waterways, where you can swim with the fish. Abismo Anhumas, which caters to the more daring, offers visitors the opportunity to rappel to the bottom of a cavern and walk among stalagmites and other cave formations. Snorkelling or diving in the cavern's waters, which contain even more strange structures, is also an option. Curitiba-Paranagua Train Ride: The Curitiba-Paranagua Train Ride, also known as the Serra Verde Express, is one of Brazil's most scenic train journeys. It cuts through the Atlantic rainforest and crosses 30 bridges on its 84-kilometre (52-mile) route connecting modern Curitiba with the historic Paranagua in Parana state. Cathedral of Brasilia: Brasilia's Cathedral is unlike any other in the world. It comprises 16 concrete columns weighing 90 tons each, and it looks more like an Indian teepee than a church. The Four Evangelists statues, by Dante Croce, grace the front of the church, which was completed in 1970. Museu de Arte Contemporânea: Museu de Arte Contemporânea (Modern Art Museum) appears to be an upside-down light fixture, a UFO, or an elegant serving bowl, depending on one's perspective. The museum, designed by Oscar Niemeyer, opened in 1996 in the Niteroi suburb city of Rio, and it overlooks Rio and the bay. Sugarloaf, Rio de Janeiro: Sugarloaf is a well-known symbol of Rio de Janeiro. It is a rounded rock peak that rises 394 metres above the beaches and city. It juts out of a tree-covered promontory. Tourists flock to its summit for views of Rio de Janeiro and the harbour, as well as the thrill of riding in a cable car suspended between Sugarloaf and the Morro da Urca, a lower peak from which a second cableway connects to the city. One of three early forts that can be visited below these peaks, near the long Praia da Urca beach, is the star-shaped Fort São João.
Brazil.Five Must-try Foods in Brazil: Moqueca: More than just a fish stew, moqueca is served with a theatrical flourish. Clouds of fragrant steam rise from the piping hot clay pot as it is uncovered on the table. Both Baianos and Capixabas claim to be the inventors of the dish and serve equally delicious variations. Fish and other seafood are made into a stew with diced tomatoes, onions, and coriander. The Capixabas use annatto seeds for natural red food colouring, while the Baianos use palm oil, peppers, and coconut milk in their version. Moqueca is served with rice, farofa (toasted cassava flour), and Piro. Feijoada: Feijoada is a well-known Brazilian dish and is eaten all over the country. This hearty stew is made with black beans and various cuts of pork. Tomatoes, cabbage, and carrots are added to enhance the flavour. Traditionally, slow-cooked offal such as trotters and ears have been used. Brazil's national dish consists of fried kale with bacon bits, rice, farofa, and an orange slice. Vatapá: Vatapá is a thick Bahian stew with shrimp as its core ingredient. It is mixed with bread, herbs, ground peanuts and coconut milk. It goes well with rice as well as acarajé. Instead of shrimp, tuna can also be used to prepare this dish. Sometimes you’ll find alternatives like chicken or vegetables being used to prepare variations of this popular dish. Acarajé: Acarajé, a well-known street food, is another Bahia favourite. This crispy, deep-fried fritter is made from mashed black-eyed peas with chopped onions. Empadão: It's a flaky pie filled with chicken casserole and vegetables such as corn, hearts of palm, and peas. Chicken is occasionally replaced with beef or shrimp. It is served for family lunches and dinners on weekends and public holidays in Brazil.
Brazil.Top 3 Things to do in Brazil: Wildlife watching in the Pantanal: It is a one-of-a-kind destination because it is a massive swamp. Not only can you see beautiful macaws here, but also piranhas, jaguars, caimans, and other animals. Snorkel in Bonito: Due to its striking natural beauty and unique wildlife, Bonito is one of the best ecotourism destinations in Brazil, if not the world. Tourists come here to snorkel with colourful fish in crystal clear waters. You might even spot alligators and snakes here! Various locations in Bonito are suitable for eco-snorkelling, but the Prata River and the Sucuri River are the favourite choices. Sail in the Poti river canyons: According to experts, Poti Canyon is about 400 million years old. Its spectacular fauna and flora, as well as prehistoric rock carvings, make it a must-see. The canyon, which is about 197 feet (60 metres) high and hidden in a desert wilderness, creates an oasis in the region where visitors come to kayak or sail in a tour boat.
Brazil.Best Places for Shopping in Brazil: Rua Oscar Freire: Many national and international boutiques fill the streets of Jardin. Here you can find various fashionable items. You can even eat something at a number of gourmet food shops while shopping. The restaurants specialise in international and traditional cuisines and are a wonderful treat. If you're interested in purchasing accessories, you can visit the many high-end jewellery stores here. Visconde de Pirajá: This commercial area in Ipanema is one of Brazil's most well-known markets, with plenty of places to shop. Forum Ipanema, Galeria Astor, Esquina de Ipanema, and Galeria Beco de Ipanema are some of the shopping malls in the area. Here you will find fantastic designer collections, and it is a must-visit if you enjoy all things fashionable. Feira do Rio Antigo: Feira do Rio Antigo is a must-see for anyone obsessed with antique collections, crafts or music. Every first Saturday of the month, there is an antique fair, and if you plan a trip to Brazil, you should definitely stop by. Rua 25 de Março: It is a famous market in Brazil if you want to buy things at low prices. There are many small markets and shops in the Centro neighborhood of São Paulo that sell toys, textiles, and other small trinkets. However, be careful and keep an eye on your belongings when shopping here because the streets are pretty packed.
Brazil.Hotels to Stay in Brazil: Quality Paulista (São Paulo, Jardins): Quality São Paulo Jardins is located on the charming Alameda Lorena in the Jardins district of São Paulo. This area is known for its high-end shops and restaurants. The hotel offers an outdoor swimming pool, a well-equipped fitness centre, and free Wi-Fi in public places. Address: 360 Alameda Lorena, Jardim Paulista, São Paulo, Brazil Hotel Fioreze Centro: Hotel Fioreze Centro is located in the Gramado City Center and offers accommodations and recreational amenities such as a fitness centre. Saint Peter's Church, Festivals Palace, and Mini Mundo are all within walking distance of this property. You'll even get free Wi-Fi here. Address: Rua São Pedro, 438 - Centro, Gramado City Centre, Gramado, Brazil Pousada Quinta da Lagoa Azul: Pousada Quinta da Lagoa Azul offers a restaurant and free Wi-Fi in Jijoca de Jericoacoara, 24 kilometres from Jericoacoara. Address: Rua da Lagoa Azul, S/N Cruz, Jijoca de Jericoacoara, Brazil
Brazil.Indian Restaurants in Brazil: Tandoor: With its simple interiors and quiet atmosphere, Tandoor is the place to go if you want to satisfy your cravings for chicken tikka, butter chicken, and chicken biryani. And you can pair everything with rotis (Indian flatbreads grilled on the fire)! Casa Prema: It is a vegetarian Indian restaurant in the Brazilian city of Sao Paulo. Aside from their grilled tofu and onion skewers, they also serve a royal banana lasagna with various salads. They have a peaceful resting area which can be a relaxing experience in the company of their brews, a cup of good old chai or a ginger and mint tea. What's best is that these beverages are available at no extra cost! Bombay Grill: Bombay Grill is an Indian restaurant in Brazil that will remind you of home away from home. Their street food selection is out of this world. You will find it very difficult to choose between their pani puri, bhel puri, and chole bhature. Their butter chicken is as sweet, rich, and creamy as you'd expect.
Brazil.Adventurous Activities to Do in Brazil: Hang gliding in Rio: Running off the edge of a platform with nothing between you and the ground 800 metres (2,624 feet) below takes a leap of faith. However, the view of the rainforest and beaches is very enthralling. You might just forget about the ground until your toes touch the sand at So Conrado beach. Kite-surfing in Jericoacoara: From July to November, kite-surf enthusiasts from all over the world flock to this tiny village to enjoy some of the best and most consistent kite-surfing conditions in Brazil. It is suitable for both beginners and advanced surfers. Diving in Fernando de Noronha: This small archipelago off the coast of Pernambuco has some of the best diving spots in Brazil. You can see dolphins, turtles, manta rays, and numerous underwater caves. Crowds are kept to a minimum because only 420 visitors are permitted on the island at any given time.
Brazil.Interesting Facts About Brazil: Rio de Janeiro was previously the capital of Portugal, making it the only European capital outside the continent. Brazil is one of the world's most biodiverse countries. It is home to approximately 4 million plant and animal species. Except for Ecuador and Chile, Brazil shares a border with every South American country. Brazilian inmates can reduce their sentences by four days for each book they read.
Brazil.Brazilian Traditions: Bumba-meu-boi and regional festivals: Many regional festivals are held in Brazil throughout the year. The Bumba-meu-boi festival in Sao Luis involves the townspeople acting out a folk story about the killing and resurrection of a bull. The festivities last for several months. Ceremonial washing of the steps of the Bonfim Church, which has an audience of 800,000 people, takes place at the end of January in Salvador. Women dressed in traditional costumes wash the steps with scented water. In the run-up to Easter, the citizens of Nova Jerusalem put on the massive passion play in South America. New Year’s celebrations: Rio is home to Reveillon, a boisterous New Year's Eve celebration. Many local restaurants serve special buffet lunches early in the day. By the evening, multitudes of people gather along the city's beaches to watch the midnight fireworks display. Candomble adherents celebrate New Year's Eve by donning an all-white garb and lighting candles. They even launch small boats loaded with trinkets into the ocean as an offering to the sea goddess Yemenja. Festa Junina: To commemorate the feast days of Santo Antônio, So Joo, and So Pedro, Brazilians began a tradition known as the Festa Junina. It is a party hosted in June, July, or even August. Everyone dresses up as cowboys and cowgirls and dances in a coordinated group choreography known as Quadrilha.
Brazil.Brazilian Visa for Indians: You must apply for a tourist visa if you want to travel to Brazil. You can now apply for your visa online. To obtain your visa through Thomas Cook you must follow four simple steps. Fill out the necessary visa information to receive the payment details. You can pay for your visa with a credit card, a debit card, or net banking. Thomas Cook gives you the option of submitting your documents online or dropping them off at the nearest branch. The company keeps you informed throughout the entire process until you receive your visa.
Brazil.Foreign Currency: If you are travelling to Brazil, you need the local currency. Brazil's currency is the Brazilian Real. One Brazilian Real is approximately equal to 15 Indian Rupees. One of the first things you should do if you want to enjoy a hassle-free trip is to obtain the local currency. Thomas Cook can help you purchase foreign currency for travel purposes. Follow three simple steps to purchase forex from Thomas Cook: Select the currency and denominations you require. Enter your information as well as the preferred method of delivery. You can have your order delivered to your home, or you can pick it up from a branch. Make your payment with a debit card, a credit card, or net banking.
Brazil.Similar Countries to Visit: Colombia: Colombia is similar to Brazil, although people speak Spanish instead of Portuguese. The demographics and development levels of both countries are comparable. Furthermore, rainforest covers a large portion of both countries' territory. Colombia, on the other hand, is much more mountainous than Brazil. Peru: Peru is similar to Brazil, but Native Americans have a dominant influence on its demographics and culture. Despite this, both have tropical climates, with Peru being significantly more mountainous. The Amazon river originates in Peru's Andes Mountains. Paraguay: Because Paraguay is located southwest of Brazil, its climate and landscape are similar to southern Brazil. Agriculture is very similar in both countries. Corn and soybeans are grown extensively in both countries. However, it is landlocked, unlike the other countries on this list. Hydropower provides the majority of electricity in both countries. They share a large dam on the Paraná River, which runs through their shared border.

The museum of New Zeeland or commonly known as Te Papa in the local language, offers the amazing galleries to explore. This gallery is an incredible part of New Zeeland’s history and culture. It is a major part of Wellington packages. There are 11 galleries of artworks that showcase the amazing works of New Zealand and Pacific Island artists. The Beehive is New Zeeland’s most iconic building. It is site of New Zeeland’s parliament. It was built in 1907 and follows a Neoclassical Edwardian style of architecture. You can also explore Wellington Botanic Gardens and Zeelandia to enjoy the pristine sights of nature. Hiking and cycling to Mount Victoria, shopping on Cuba street, surfing at Houghton Bay beach are some of the favourite things to do in Wellington. faq.question: What is a Schengen visa?
faq.answer: The Schengen visa is a short-stay visa which allows you to travel to any of the Schengen areas for as long as 90 days. If you have a Schengen visa, you can travel freely within any of the Schengen member countries. Some Europe tour packages from India may handle the aspect of a visa as required for your trip.

faq.question: What is the biggest city in Europe?
faq.answer: The biggest city in Europe is Istanbul although population wise it’s not counted as the most populated city in Europe because it straddles the border between Europe and Asia. You can easily find Europe packages for Turkey if you want to spend a few days in Istanbul.

faq.question: Are Euros acceptable currency all over Europe?
faq.answer: It’s possible to use Euro in 20 of the EU countries. These include countries such as Austria, Belgium, Cyprus, Estonia, Finland, France, Germany, Greece, Ireland, Italy, Latvia, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Malta, the Netherlands, Portugal, Slovakia, Croatia, Slovenia and Spain.

faq.question: Can I drive across Europe?
faq.answer: Yes, it’s possible to drive across Europe, and you get to cross multiple country borders. However, do make sure that you have the appropriate driving license and that your rental company allows you to take the car across borders.

faq.answer: The best time to visit Europe is during the summer. The weather is perfect and there are a host of festivals and events which are planned around this time specifically. You can find the best deals on Europe packages for summer on our website.

Summer (July to August) If you love vibrant cities, open-air events, and long, bright days, this is the best month to visit Europe. During this time, nations like France, Italy, and Spain were vibrant and energetic. But be prepared for larger crowds and more expensive travel to Europe. There are still some excellent Europe packages tours available if you plan and make your reservations early. Spring (April to June) Spring is the best time to go Europe if you want nice weather, beautiful gardens, and fewer tourists. You'll appreciate taking in the festivities in Italy and Spain or walking through tulip fields in the Netherlands. It's also a great time for beautiful walks and photography. Additionally, compared to the summer, Europe packages tours are typically less expensive during this time. It's undoubtedly among the best time to go Europe. Winter (November to March) Winter is the best season for a low Europe trip cost. Christmas markets and lights adorn cities, particularly in Germany, Austria, and the Czech Republic. It's also the greatest place to go skiing, and several Europe tour packages include snowy excursions. Just wear warm clothing because it can be very chilly here in the winter. But it's a hidden gem for off-season Europe tourism because of its modest cost and understated appeal. Autumn (September to October) Desire vivid colours, warm temps, and fewer crowds? If you like wine tours, local festivals, and quiet sightseeing, now is the best time to visit Europe. During this time of year, a lot of highly regarded Europe travel packages are offered at affordable Europe tour prices. Train travel is one of the simplest and most picturesque methods to see Europe, even though it is not possible to go straight from India to any European city. Some of the best high-speed train networks are found in nations like France, Germany, Switzerland, and Italy. During your journey to Europe, you can visit several locations by using passes like Eurail. It's affordable, cosy, and incredibly effective, making it the perfect choice for anyone organising a flexible and well-connected trip around Europe with Europe honeymoon packages. Europe.Best Places to Visit in Europe: There are many stunning places to visit in Europe, whether you're planning your first vacation there or want to see more of this lovely continent. A wealth of culture, nature, and history can be found throughout Europe, from iconic sites to serene hill stations and historic temples. This is a short list of the best attractions in Europe attractions that you should include on your itinerary. Famous Places to Visit Here are the best places to visit in Europe: Italy's Colosseum: This historic amphitheatre in the centre of Rome serves as an intriguing reminder of the Roman Empire. It's one of those European destinations for history buffs. French Eiffel Tower: A trip to Paris wouldn't be complete without taking in this famous site. It's undoubtedly a highlight of any vacation to Europe, whether you see it from the ground or ascend for sweeping vistas. Czech Republic's Charles Bridge: It is one of the best things to see in Europe in Prague because of its quaint statues and cobblestone promenade. Germany's Neuschwanstein Castle: One of the most picturesque things to see in Europe, this Bavarian castle feels enchanting and like something from a fairy tale. England’s Stonehenge: This is a must-see for anyone searching for unusual travel experiences in Europe. It is a primordial mystery and a significant cultural icon. Beaches/ Hill Stations to Visit A combination of mountains and coastlines should be included in your Europe tour packages if you're more of a nature lover. Here are the best places to visit in Europe when it comes to beaches or hill stations: Italy's Amalfi Coast: This is one of the most romantic places to see in Europe because of its vibrant cities, glistening ocean, and sheer cliffs. This is included in most Europe honeymoon packages. Greece's Santorini: This island, which is well-known for its sunsets and white-washed houses, is ideal for beach lovers and couples travelling through Europe. Switzerland's Swiss Alps: This is one of the top hill stations and one of the best destinations to visit in Europe all year round, whether you want to ski or just take in the scenery. Lake Bled, Slovenia: A picturesque lake with a little church on an island, excellent for tranquil getaways while travelling through Europe. France's French Riviera: Savour the finest Europe cuisine, fashion, and beaches along this glitzy coastline from Nice to Cannes. Temples to Visit Unexpectedly, there are also spiritual locations in the best country to visit in Europe that are worth seeing. Apollo Temple at Delphi: This mythological location combines history with breathtaking mountain vistas. Athens' Acropolis: It is one of the most popular Europe tourist places in Ancient Europe and is home to the Parthenon, a UNESCO World Heritage site. Rome's Pantheon: It features Roman architecture, is still standing after two millennia, and is frequently featured in Europe packages. St. Peter's Basilica, Vatican City: Although it is officially in the Vatican, St. Peter's Basilica in Vatican City is a must-see on any tour and is frequently included in it when travelling with Europe trip packages from India. Greece's Meteora Monasteries: These revered monasteries, which are unique spiritual destinations in Europe, are erected on granite pillars.

Explore Vatican City, the Colosseum, and savour European fare like gelato and real pasta. France's Paris: Paris, a popular travel destination, is known for its romance, art, and cuisine. England's London: An ideal fusion of the modern and the ancient. London is one of the best destinations in Europe because of Big Ben, Buckingham Palace, and the West End.

Somnath.Most Popular Somnath Tour Packages: Tour Packages NameDurationPriceGujarat - Dwarka Somnath With Diu And Sasangir6 Nights 7 DaysRs. ₹34 400 Gujarat And Madhya Pradesh - Char Jyotirlinga Tour7 Nights 8 DaysRs. ₹46 600Dekho Gujarat4 Nights 5 DaysRs. ₹24 000Gujarat - Dwarka Somnath With Sasangir6 Nights 7 DaysRs. ₹32 800Dwarka Somnath Darshan Yatra 7 Nights 8 DaysRs. ₹52 000 Somnath is the first jyotirlinga of Lord Shiva located on the shoreline of Arabian Sea. It has been believed that Somnath was built in four phases: Gold, Silver, Wood & Stone. The Moon of God, Lord Soma was cursed by his father in law Daksha Prajapati because of which Moon lost its lustre and light. The Moon of God prayed Lord Shiva. Lord Shiva was very pleased with the bhakti of Lord Soma & granted him light, curing his curse! Therefore, Lord Soma built a Golden temple for Lord Shiva. It was built by Ravana in silver; Lord Krishna built it in sandalwood & Bhimadev built the temple with stone. Apart from being a pilgrimage for Hindus, Somnath is a perfect beach destination for the tourist and has many other attractions. Somnath is very near to Veraval and gate built to enter Somnath from Veraval is a triple gate which is stunning. Take out some leisure time & explore the architectural beauty of this monument. Prabhas Pata Museum of Somnath is an ancient museum containing many sculptures of Hindu Gods & Goddess. It also has the remains of the old Somnath temple which was built in an ancient century. For all the historic lovers, this museum just cannot be missed! The location of the Somnath on the shoreline of the Arabian sea makes it very unique as there is no land in a straight line from Somnath shoreline to Antarctica. Somnath temple is a pure reflection of the stonemasons of Gujarat who beautifully craved the seven-storied spacious temples.

It leads to Antarctica. This temple is around 5kms away from the Somnath temple. South Korea.Best Time to Visit South Korea: When is the best time to visit South Korea? It depends on what you want to see: cherry blossoms in bloom, colourful leaves in fall or holiday lights in the winter. South Korea is one of the most adaptable places due to its four distinct seasons, each of which offers its own charm. Because of the warm weather and beautiful scenery, spring and fall are typically regarded as the best times to go South Korea. These months' temperatures, which range from 10°C to 22°C, are ideal for touring and visiting well-known South Korea tourist places outside. Here is a quick yearly breakdown to help you plan your South Korea tour packages from India: Spring (March-May): For picture-perfect beauty, April is the best month to visit South Korea because that's when the cherry blossoms are in full bloom in Seoul and Gyeongju. Summer (June-August): It's warm and hot, but it's a great time to visit coastal places to see in South Korea, like Busan and Jeju Island. Autumn (September-November): With golden leaves and cool air, autumn is the best time to visit South Korea for photography. Winter (December-February): With off-season prices on South Korea packages, this is a great time to visit for snow fans and ski areas. A South Korea trip is always amazing, whether it's for culture, nature, or events.
South Korea.How to Reach South Korea?: How to go South Korea easily is a common question from travellers. There are many ways to travel in the country, so there is something for every income and plan. Knowing how to reach South Korea helps you plan your trip effectively and get the most out of your South Korea tour packages, whether you're travelling abroad or just exploring the country. By Train The peninsula doesn't have an international rail link, but once you get there, the high-speed train system makes local South Korea travel quick and easy. Major South Korea attractions like Seoul, Busan, and Gyeongju can be reached in hours thanks to the Korea Train Express (KTX). While visiting South Korea, travellers on South Korea trip packages can enjoy beautiful rides through the countryside easily. KTX is the best way to see the local South Korea things to see if you're on a tight budget. By Road South Korea does not have any direct roads from other countries, but its own highway system is very good. It's a great idea to rent a car or take a long-distance bus to see some of the lesser-known South Korea tour spots. You can visit a variety of South Korea tour attractions thanks to the motorways that link Seoul, Busan, Incheon, and Jeonju. People looking for exciting things to do in South Korea should take road trips through hilly areas and along the coast, which have beautiful views. Additionally, luxury South Korea holiday packages include guided coach trips to take you to different places. By Air If you're thinking about how to reach South Korea from abroad, flying is the most practical option. The largest airport is Incheon International Airport, which is close to Seoul and has direct flights from many Indian cities. With flexible South Korea trip cost choices from India, the majority of South Korea tour packages include flights. Airports in Busan and Jeju handle local and regional lines for South Korea travel packages in addition to Incheon. It is simple to explore South Korea tour places and enjoy every aspect of your South Korea trip with the help of airlines like Korean Air and Asiana Airlines, which guarantee comfortable flights.

One of the main South Korea tour attractions is Gyeongbokgung Palace, so don't miss it. Busan: It is one of the most famous tourist places people book in South Korea travel packages, and is known for its beaches and temples. South Korea packages include many things to do here, including shopping and trying different kinds of fish. Gyeongju: One of the most historically significant places to see in South Korea is the old city, which is known as a "museum without walls" Incheon: People often include this city in the best South Korea tour packages because of how beautiful the coast is and how modern the buildings are. South Korea.Beaches/ Hill Stations to Visit: Jeju Island: It is one of the most beautiful places to go in South Korea, with waterfalls, beaches, and rocky landscapes.
