faq.question: Do they speak English in Norway?
faq.answer: While Norwegian is the primary language spoken in Norway, being a popular holiday destination has also motivated the locals to speak English as a second language. over 88% of the Norwegian population speaks English almost fluently. However, if the travel itinerary for your Norway tour package from India includes taking a trip to some of the unique places to visit in Norway in remote locations, it is best to pick up a few Norwegian phrases to ease the conversation.

faq.question: How much does a Norway trip cost from India?
faq.answer: Apart from the visa and service fee required when booking your Norway holiday package, you will need approximately rs. 60,000 to cover the Norway trip cost for five to seven days. This is of course, excluding the cost of your Norway tour package.

faq.question: Why is Norway famous?
faq.answer: Norway is famous for many things including the shorter working hours and high wages, the northern lights, the fjords, glaciers, and a plethora of museums. However, the most stunning fact about Norway is the rising and setting of the sun. Norway experiences three months of complete darkness during winter and complete days of light due to the midnight sun. This uncanny phenomenon is enough to draw tourists from all around the world to experience 24-hour days and 24-hour nights as nature's many miracles.

faq.question: How many days is enough in Norway?
faq.answer: Norway may not be a huge country but it has a lot to offer when it comes to the top places to visit in Norway. It takes a considerable amount of time to explore all the fjords, museums, villages, islands, and glaciers for the best experience out of your Norway tour package from India. It also takes patience to come across the aurora borealis, which is a site you simply cannot miss if you plan for a Norway trip. It is best to book Norway vacation packages for five to seven days at least to ensure that you get the most out of your trip to the Scandinavian land of Vikings.

faq.question: Why is Norway so costly?
faq.answer: Norway is one of the few countries where labour laws are strictly designed to benefit the residents. People who work in Norway have limited office hours as well as high income to compensate for the hard work they put in to meet the country's beauty and resplendent. In order to do so, the government pays the people a healthy salary which is derived from the massive tourism of Norway. It is an ethical practice that ensures that any money we pay towards Norway tour packages goes to improving the lives of those who make Norway vacations great and adventurous.

faq.question: How much cash can I bring into Norway?
faq.answer: There are certain limits when it comes to carrying currency to and from Norway. in order to maintain the high value of the Norwegian krone, you can only carry up to 25,000 nok which is equivalent to rs. 2,02,900 approximately.

faq.question: Can you drink alcohol in Norway?
faq.answer: The Scandinavian country of Norway has strict rules when it comes to alcohol. softer alcoholic beverages such as beer and wine are only available to people who are 18 years or older. harder spirits such as scotch, vodka, rum, etc. are only available to individuals above the age of 20.

Norway.Introduction: One of the most popular holiday destinations these days is the Scandinavian haven of Norway. The perfect destination to encounter glacial mountain ranges, as well as coastal fjords, Norway, is the ultimate vacation spot for snowy activities as well as traipsing through green fields. Thomas Cook offers Norway packages made by travel experts to help you bring your dream vacation to life.The Viking capital of the world has a long history of hosting tourists. The joyful, vibrant, and friendly atmosphere of Norway is in drastic contrast to its past, which attracts those who wish to explore life outside their comfort zone. Norway honeymoon packages are particularly popular for those planning a Norway trip from India.
Norway.Most Popular Norway Tour Packages: Norway Tour Packages No. of Nigts / Days Price* Highlights of Norway and Denmark 7 Nights/ 8 Days Rs. 2 69 664 Scandinavian Extravaganza 9 Nights/ 10 Days Rs. 3 18 607 Focus on Scandinavia - Cosmos 11 Nights/ 12 Days Rs. 2 09 848
Norway.About Norway: The Nordic nation of Norway is nestled in Northern Europe’s Scandinavian Peninsula. Officially called the Kingdom of Norway, the charm of this country is highlighted by its breathtaking scenic beauty. From colossal mountains to stunning fjords to a glacier-shaped coastline, the landscape of Norway is very interesting. Besides these, tons of tourists embark on a Norway tour to witness the majestic midnight sun and the Northern Lights. There are even special Norway tour packages based on this particular experience. Being the land of Vikings, the cultural and historical heritage of Norway has earned recognition around the globe. Oslo, the capital of Norway, is one of the most popular tourist destinations in Europe. Then, there are the more picturesque locations like Geiranger, Flam, Tromso & Bergen that tourists adore. You can easily find Norway tour packages by Thomas Cook that feature these notable destinations. If you are a thrill-seeker, note that Norway offers tons of opportunities for adventure tourism. Activities like hiking, cycling, alpine skiing, dog sleighing, ocean rafting, etc., are available on a Norway tour. On Thomas Cook, you can find Norway packages starting from only INR 1,30,400. You can also get up to 9% off and earn Loyalty Bonus with Edge Rewards on select Norway tour packages.
Norway.How to reach Norway from India: Even though Norway is a long way from India, there are multiple travel options available. For your Norway tour, you can use any of these methods; By airThe best way to reach Norway from India is by flight. However, direct flights from India are not available, so you will have to rely on connecting flights for your Norway tour. Oslo Airport in Norway is the main hub, dealing with regular international flights from and to over 100 destinations. For reference, a one-stop flight from Mumbai to Oslo via Dubai takes around 12 hours. Other major international airports in Norway are in Bergen, Tromso, Trondheim, Sandefjord, Kristiansand and Stavanger. Thomas Cook offers Norway tour packages that are inclusive of flights. By roadTravelling to Norway from India by road is not a viable option. However, road connectivity between Norway and places like Finland, Russia or Sweden is great. If you are visiting from Sweden, take the E6 motorway, and if you are coming from Finland, take the E8. You can also consider buses to Oslo from Copenhagen, Gothenburg, and Stockholm for the Norway tour. By railThere is no direct rail link between Norway and India. However, this Nordic nation has an extensive rail network that connects it to other places in Scandinavia and Europe. For instance, regular trains to Oslo are available from major European cities like Copenhagen, Gothenburg, Stockholm, etc. So, if you are going on a European trip from India that features a Norway tour, you can opt for this method. By seaDirect travel to Norway from India by sea is not widely available. However, ferries from Germany, Denmark and Sweden are regularly operated. If you opt for cruise liners bound for Norway, they usually dock at Bergen, Oslo, Stavanger, Trondheim, etc. As for local transfers, the Norway packages by Thomas Cook include them under the plan price.
Norway.Travelling within Norway: If you are planning a trip to Norway from India, you will definitely want to check out the most beautiful places in Norway. you will want to travel within the country to various cities, smaller towns, and villages for an immersive Norwegian experience. there are four major means of transportation to get around the country, which are as follows: Railway Boats and ferries Public buses Private cars or taxi service
Norway.Best time to visit in Norway: A Norway tour is the gateway to witnessing the divine power of god in nature. When planning your Norway trip, you need to consider the best time to visit Norway. Doing so helps with picking the most suitable Norway packages for the journey. However, The problem is, there is no particular best month to visit Norway as you can visit any time. Each season has its own charm, so it is not about the best time to travel to Norway. Summers in Norway are particularly warm and green, affording the best opportunities to take a cruise across the fjord. You can explore the arctic and Atlantic oceans on these luxury cruises as well as disembark at some of the best places to visit in Norway. You can also go hiking or take a road trip across the country for an authentic nordic experience during the summer. If you are looking for summertime fun surrounded by nature, then April is probably the best month to visit Norway for you. In fact, a Norway tour anytime during summer can be great for those who love walking through tall grasses and the fresh air of the countryside. Get exclusive deals on Norway packages for summer on our website. If you love the cold season and wish to experience the enigma of the northern lights, popularly known as the aurora borealis, then the best time to visit Norway for you is during the winter. During the cold season, the mountain ranges are covered in snow. It is the perfect time to go skiing through the white glaciers or take a trip across the arctic circle to catch a glimpse of the northern lights. The aurora borealis is best observed from remote locations, especially by the side of water bodies. This is particularly ideal for Norway honeymoon packages as they create the perfect backdrop for romance.
Norway.Best Places to visit in Norway: Norway vacations are nothing without the most beautiful places in Norway. when planning your travel itinerary as per your Norway tour package, you must include the following best places to visit in Norway on your list: Akershus fortress Oslo is one of the best cities to visit in Norway for many reasons, only for the Akershus fortress of king Haakon v back in 1299. In the 17th century, king christian iv converted it into the royal residence for the renaissance. Now, it houses the Norwegian resistance museum, and the armed forces museum, and offers walking tours to explore the history of Scandinavian civilization. Several Norway packages feature this site in the itinerary. Bryggen hanseatic wharf Bergen is one of the best places to visit in Norway, and the Bryggen hanseatic wharf is one of the many tourist destinations that make your Norway trip package completely worthwhile. Lined with a number of buildings with a deep history and lovely artistry, the Bryggen hanseatic wharf is home to several restaurants, and boutiques, and the infamous Bryggen museum depicts the culture of the middle ages. Bygdoy peninsula Located in one of the best cities to visit in Norway, the bygdoy peninsula in Oslo is among the top attractions of the country. It is the perfect place to enjoy everything that the region has to offer on a Norway tour. This includes the stretches of rocky beaches for relaxation, forests where you can go hiking for the authentic nordic experience, and parks designed for wholesome family fun with the picnic or games. Geirangerfjord Among the famous places in Norway known for the fjords is Geirangerfjord, a UNESCO world heritage site popular for its photographic scenery. It features 11 sharp hairpin bends along the mountain road, which presents a thrilling experience for adventure seekers. Explore more places like Geirangerfjord with Norway packages by Thomas Cook. Jotunheimen One of the best places to go in Norway where nature dominates everything in the surrounding is Jotunheimen. The acres of alpine stretches feature the highest mountain ranges and glaciers along with an array of waterfalls, lakes, and rivers that provides sustenance to the indigenous wildlife. It is one of the few national parks in the world where you can catch a glimpse of large herds of reindeer on the Norway tour. Lillehammer One of the top places to visit in Norway, Lillehammer is a hub of several landmarks. located in the gudbrandsdal valley overlooking lake mjosa, Lillehammer hosted the winter Olympics in 1994 and has been a popular holiday destination for the variety of historic structures dating back centuries. Lofoten islands One of the best places to visit in Norway, especially for a Norway honeymoon package, is the Lofoten islands an archipelago that is not only in compliance to watch the dancing northern lights but also home to many species of wildlife, marine life, and the most exquisite birds. You can easily book Norway tour packages featuring the Lofoten Islands from Thomas Cook.You can ferry around the different islands to check out the war memorial museum, the magic ice, or even the history of fishery in Norway. It is truly one of the most beautiful places in Norway to spend an afternoon or a whole day learning the cultural significance of the gorgeous count Preikestolen One of the most beautiful places in Norway is Pulpit rock, commonly known as preikestolen. It is the perfect place to take a hike up the mountainside only to gaze upon the panoramic view of lysefjord. After the rewarding trek, you can also explore the region for the canary from the world war two period converted into a museum dedicated to sardine fishing and canning. Find the best Norway packages featuring sites like Preikestolen on our page. Sognefjord Norway is known for its entrapment between glacial mountains and fjords. Sognefjord is the largest fjord in the Scandinavian country that cuts more than 200 kilometres inland. you can take a cruise to the king fjord or if you wish to keep your Norway trip cost to a minimum, you can take a boat ride or a ferry across the 5-kilometre expanse of the waterbody. Apart from the fjord itself, the region of Sognefjord offers some of the unique places to visit in Norway including an exquisite museum featuring the Norwegian glaciers. Tromso The fishing village of Tromso is the ultimate destination to catch a glimpse of the aurora borealis on a Norway tour.. Located in the arctic, the region is popular for its polar museum dedicated to displaying marine life in its natural habitat from the northern hemisphere. The arctic museum it's one of the most unique places to visit in Norway as it astutely reflects the current situation in the arctic region influenced by climatic conditions. While in Tromso, take some time to walk around the arctic-alpine botanic garden, which is one of the best places to visit in Norway. These stunning attractions make Tromso one of the best cities to visit in Norway.. Tromso is a major inclusion in popular Norway packages. Vigeland sculpture park One of the most unique places to visit in Norway is the vigeland sculpture park which houses 650 of the best artwork designed by gustav vigeland. These iconic figures are sculpted from granite, wrought iron, and bronze and arranged in a thematic representation of different aspects of life.
Norway.What to eat in Norway: The food in Norway is just as unique and interesting as the people who make it and the region that inspires the cuisine. To truly immerse yourself in Norway’s culture, you must try the local cuisine. Here are some popular dishes to try on your Norway tour; ReinsdyrstekOne of the top dishes to try in Norway is Reinsdyrstek. It is a non-veg dish made of roasted reindeer meat. This tasty red meat will surely make your taste buds happy, especially if you are trying it for the first time. Reunsdyrstek is typically served alongside peas, mashed potatoes and lingonberry jam. Rokt LaksTry Rokt Laks on your Norway tour. It is the Norwegian smoked salmon that is known for its flavourful taste. Salmon is mainly used for this preparation in fillet form, which has been fermented and air-dried. It is then hot or cold smoked, offering a savoury final dish. FiskesuppeAvoid letting Norway’s cold overwhelm you by trying some Fiskesuppe. It is a delicious and hearty fish soup that contains pieces of shrimp, salmon, cod, cream and root vegetables. Fiskesuppe is served warm, alongside some freshly buttered, crusty white bread. BrunostBrunost, also called brown cheese, is one of Norway’s most loved foods. It has a fudge-like texture and a sweet yet tangy caramel flavour. It is made using cream, whey and cow’s/goat’s milk. Try some Brunost on your toast, waffle or bread during breakfast on your Norway tour. MultebærCloudberries are a delicacy in Norway. You can try it in dessert form with Multekrem or in jam form with some vanilla ice cream. You can naturally get some cloudberries along the Norwegian hiking trails. On a side note, know that Norway packages by Thomas Cook include meals as standard.
Norway.Things to do in Norway: To have some fun and amusement on your Norway tour, consider adding fun activities to the trip itinerary. Here are some of the most fun things to do in Norway; Take your best Instagram photo at Preikestolen or TrolltungaEnough seeing others taking breathtaking tabletop cliff-edge photos and uploading them to the gram. On your Norway tour, it is your time to shine. Preikestolen or Pulpit Rock near Stavanger is a prime spot for this. The scenic view and scope for photography here make it worth the 1.5-hour trek. You can also visit Trolltunga for a similar scenic background for your photos. Go on the scenic Besseggen Ridge hikeNorway has a plethora of hiking trails. Hiking is the perfect way to explore and experience the dramatic landscapes in the region. The Besseggen Ridge hike is a notable one to consider. The narrow ridge, rugged trail, and views of emerald lakes make it an exciting experience. Rent a motorboat and explore AurlandsfjordSeeing the Aurlandsfjord feels grander from up close. You can rent a motorboat and explore this serene and scenic fjord with your loved ones or co-travellers on the Norway tour. The towering cliffs and crystal blue water offer a nice contrast. If you are lucky, you may also get to see sea otters and dolphins. Witness the grandeur of the Northern LightsPeople from all around the globe visit Norway to witness the heavenly sight of the Northern Lights. It is the dance of lights in the night sky, best seen between October and March. If the weather is on your side, you can catch it at Tromsø, the Lofoten Islands, Bodø, Svalbard, etc. You can book Norway tour packages exclusively centred around this experience. Check out the midnight sunWhen does the sun shine? Sometimes, at midnight in Norway. If you are going on a Norway tour in the Summer, you can experience the midnight sun. To catch this incredible phenomenon, visit the places beyond the Arctic Circle in Norway.
Norway.Forex currency exchange for Norway tour: Even though it is a European country, Norway has its own currency, the Norwegian krone. When planning for your Norway vacation packages, you need to convert some of your money into local currency for emergencies. you can check out the guidelines for forex currency exchange for Norway tour packages from India here
Norway.Visa application for a trip to Norway fr: An important part of your Norway trip package is the visa application. As a European country, there are two types of visas for which you can apply when planning your Norway trip. The traditional Norway visa is only applicable if you plan on visiting Norway and no other place in Europe. However, if you are planning an extended vacation with the Norway tour as part of Schengen tourism, then you need to apply for a Schengen tourist visa at least six to nine months before your Norway tour package from India is booked. You can register online by filling out the application and sending in the subsequent documents to the Norway embassy in India afterward.

faq.question: What is the best time to visit Bhutan?
faq.answer: The best time to visit Bhutan is from October to December, when the weather is sunny and the air is clear. Although it gets colder in January and February, the weather is dry and pleasant from then until April. In late spring, the famed rhododendrons blossom gloriously, filling the valleys with colour.

faq.question: How many days are required in Bhutan tour?
faq.answer: In order to really experience Bhutan's natural beauty and treasured culture, you should spend 7 to 10 days there. You can thoroughly explore the best of Bhutan's natural wonders and cultural highlights with this seven-day Bhutan tour through the country's culture and outdoors.

faq.question: Which is currency commonly used in Bhutan?
faq.answer: The ngultrum is used locally in Bhutan, it is the country's official Bhutan currency. It is used for all financial transactions on a professional level as well as locally.

faq.question: Is Bhutan a friendly place for tourists?
faq.answer: Bhutan is a peaceful, safe, and well-kept nation that is gradually embracing a more contemporary way of life. The majority of the land is made up of steep, tall mountains that are carved through by a system of rivers that create deep valleys perfect for your Bhutan trip.

faq.question: How to reach Bhutan from India?
faq.answer: You can reach Bhutan from India by air or land. From important Indian cities including Delhi, Mumbai, Kolkata, and Bagdogra, you may fly directly to Paro International Airport in Bhutan. On this route, Druk Air and Bhutan Airlines run frequent flights. The border towns of Phuentsholing, Samdrup Jongkhar, or Gelephu are another option for entering Bhutan by land. These entry points have set entry/exit times, and a travel permit is required. The most popular land access port, Phuentsholing, lies in the southwest and connected to the Indian town of Jaigaon. To get to Thimphu, the capital of Bhutan, from there, you can either take a taxi or a bus. Please make sure you check the most recent requirements and restrictions for travel before you depart.

faq.question: How much does a trip to Bhutan cost from India?
faq.answer: How long you intend to remain in Bhutan will determine this. The total cost of a 5-day trip to Bhutan, including housing, meals, and touring, comes to roughly INR 27,000 per person.

faq.question: Do I need a visa to visit Bhutan?
faq.answer: No, a visa is not necessary to visit Bhutan if you are a citizen of India. When visiting Bhutan, you must, however, have one of the following two documents with you: Valid Indian Passport having validity of minimum 6 months Voter Identity Card, issued by the Election Commission of India You must apply for a visa in advance through your travel agent if you are not a citizen of India, Bangladesh, or the Maldives.

faq.question: How much does a Bhutan trip from India cost for a week?
faq.answer: A 6-7day Bhutan trip from India generally costs ₹35,000–₹70,000 per person, depending on travel mode, hotel category, and activities.

faq.question: Do Indians need a visa to visit Bhutan?
faq.answer: No visa is required for Indian citizens, but you must carry a valid passport or voter ID and get an entry permit at the border or airport.

faq.question: What is the best time to visit Bhutan?
faq.answer: The best time to visit Bhutan is in spring (March–May) & autumn (September–November).

faq.question: How many days are enough for Bhutan?
faq.answer: 5–7 days are ideal to cover popular destinations like Thimphu, Paro, and Punakha.

faq.question: Which places should I visit in Bhutan?
faq.answer: Must-visit spots include Tiger’s Nest Monastery in Paro, Punakha Dzong, Buddha Dordenma in Thimphu, and Dochula Pass.

faq.question: Is Bhutan an expensive destination for Indians?
faq.answer: Bhutan is budget-friendly for Indians as there’s no daily Sustainable Development Fee (SDF) like for other nationalities, but costs depend on hotel and travel choices.

faq.question: What currency is used in Bhutan?
faq.answer: The Bhutanese Ngultrum (BTN) is the official currency, but Indian Rupees (₹) are also widely accepted.

faq.question: Is Bhutan good for a family vacation?
faq.answer: Yes, Bhutan is family-friendly with monasteries, scenic valleys, and peaceful towns that are safe and enjoyable for all ages.

faq.question: Will language be a problem in Bhutan?
faq.answer: No, English and Hindi are commonly understood in most towns, so communication is easy for Indian travellers.

Bhutan.Introduction: Bhutan is one of India’s prettiest neighbours. The Bhutan trips, therefore, are very popular among Indians, and if you are planning your own Bhutan trip, you are surely in for an excellent adventure. From stunning views of the Himalayas to cleanliness and safety, the country offers everything you need for a nice and peaceful holiday. Bhutan.Best time to visit Bhutan: Though a Bhutan trip is possible all around the year, visiting the country at certain times enhances the experience. Here’s a detailed look at the best time to go Bhutan: Spring Spring, which sets in by March and lasts till May, is considered to be the best time to go Bhutan. The weather is perfect, with cool, sunny days and pleasant nights. There is minimal rainfall, and the views are great. This is also the peak Bhutan travel season, so you can easily find suitable Bhutan packages. Find the best Bhutan tour packages and start your Bhutanese adventures right away! Summer Summer in Bhutan brings a lot of rainfall to the places to go in Bhutan. The summer months last from June to August. However, with the rains come beautiful greenery, gushing waterfalls and foggy hills, all of which make for a picturesque Bhutan trip. The crowds are also less, so you can easily access the best places to visit in Bhutan. Explore the Bhutan tour packages and plan your summer Bhutan tour today. Autumn After spring, autumn is considered to be the best time to visit Bhutan. From September to November is autumn time in Bhutan, and the place comes alive with its beauty. So you can choose any one of these months as the best month to visit Bhutan. You also get to experience many events and festivals at this time of year, all the best places to visit in Bhutan. Enjoy the culture, eat the local Bhutan food, and soak it all up! Book the best Bhutan trip packages and have a great Bhutan trip in autumn. Winter Find the best December Bhutan travel packages if you wish to visit a winter wonderland close to home. Plan a Bhutan trip in winter. From snowfall to misty mornings, you will find a truly wonderful holiday destination when you buy the Bhutan trip packages and go to the country in winter. A lot of tourists don't consider winter to be the best season to visit Bhutan, so you will find fewer crowds, which is an added benefit. Different tourists have different preferences about the weather, so choose the best Bhutan tour packages as per your preferences and start your trip to Bhutan from India without any further delay.
Bhutan.How to reach Bhutan?: When you book one of the Bhutan trip packages, you get the travel included. However, if you book an accommodation-only package, you will have to arrange for the travel yourself. Here are some options on how to reach Bhutan: By train If you wish to know how to reach Bhutan by train, you will be happy to know that trains go up to Hasimara in West Bengal, and the Bhutan travel packages also take you there. The Bhutanese border is a short distance away, and you can drive down from Hasimara to start your glorious Bhutan tour. You can also see some of the best Bhutan tourist places when you drive down. You can find some Thomas Cook Bhutan tour packages that include train travel. By Road How to reach Bhutan by road? Well, it is very easy. You can take a bus or your own vehicle to Hasimara or New Jalpaiguri in West Bengal. From there, you can take a taxi or another bus to cross the border and start your Bhutan trip. You can also start exploring the best places to visit in Bhutan and eat the yummy Bhutan food from local eateries when you drive into the country. Book the best Bhutan tour packages, and your taxi rides will be taken care of. By Air One of the most common ways of reaching Bhutan is by air. Many people who ask how to go Bhutan end up travelling by air, which is also a feature on most of the Thomas Cook Bhutan travel packages. You have direct flights to Paro airport in Bhutan. Most of the Thomas Cook Bhutan trip packages from India include airfares, so it becomes very convenient for travellers. Since Paro has many Bhutan tourist places, you can start exploring as soon as you land. Choose any one of these travel options or the best Thomas Cook Bhutan tour packages and make your Bhutan tour a grand success!
Bhutan.Best Places to Visit in Bhutan: The best places to visit in Bhutan include hills, monasteries and beautiful hill stations. Some of the top places to visit in Bhutan are: Famous Places to Visit One of the most famous places to see in Bhutan is the Tiger Nest Monastery in Paro. According to many, visiting this place is one of the highlights of going on a Bhutan tour. It is one of the oldest monasteries in the country and is beautifully nestled on a cliff. Another one of the most famous Bhutan tourist spots is the Chele La Pass, which is situated between Paro and Haa Valley. It offers stunning views of the Himalayas and is one of the most visited Bhutan tourist spots. A visit to these famous Bhutan attractions is included in most Bhutan packages. Hill Stations to Visit A Bhutan trip takes you to some of the best hill stations in Asia. The country has stunning hill stations like Punakha and Phobjikha Valley. You will be mesmerised to see the beauty of these towns, which are undoubtedly some of the Bhutan places to visit. Opt for the best Bhutan holiday packages and have a memorable Bhutan tour. Temples to Visit Some of the most iconic temples in Bhutan include the Paro Taktsang, the Kyichu Lhakhang and the Jambay Lhakhang, among others, which are the most revered Bhutan things to see. These are deeply religious places that form an essential part of Bhutan’s culture. When you choose one of the Bhutan travel packages, you get to visit most of the famous cities, like: Paro All the Bhutan packages, which include the best-selling family tour packages from India, take you to Paro. table_of_content: Things to Know While Visiting Ujjain
Ujjain.Introduction: If you're planning a spiritual getaway, Ujjain is the perfect destination. It is the one place that houses everything under its scintillating umbrella, right from coveted spiritual getaways to visually classic cities encompassed under its ancient buildings and sacred temples. Let the Thomas Cook Ujjain tour packages, starting from just ₹8,999, take you through the bygone era, rich in its holy temples and worship. Ujjain sightseeing will be accompanied by a visit to some of the most famous temples. In our Ujjain holiday packages, we include a full city tour of Ujjain, with time for a deep exploration of its beauty as a historical place. Our specially crafted itinerary on Ujjain tourism enables you to experience local culture and delicious Ujjain famous food.
Ujjain.Mos Popular Ujjain Tour packages: Tour Packages NameDurationPrice Gujarat And Madhya Pradesh - Char Jyotirlinga Tour7 Nights 8 DaysRs. ₹46 600Jyotirlingas Darshan - Mahakaleshwar Omkareshwar Darshan2 Nights 3 DaysRs. ₹23 700Narmada Jyotirlinga Darshan Yatra 4 Nights 5 DaysRs. ₹32 400Malwa Darshan Yatra - Indore Maheshwar Mandu Ujjain4 Nights 5 DaysRs. ₹33 800Malwa Jyotirling Darshan Yatra4 Nights 5 DaysRs. ₹37 900
Ujjain.Cultural Significance: Ujjain, one of the seven holy cities of India, holds a deep cultural and historical significance. When you plan your Ujjain trip, you're not just visiting another city; you're stepping into a place filled with traditions that have been passed down through millennia. Here's what makes Ujjain holiday packages so culturally rich and why you should consider it for your next holiday: The Spiritual Heartbeat: Ujjain is a symbol of spirituality. During your tour to Ujjain, you will find that the city's identity is inextricably linked with its temples. Mahakaleshwar Temple is one of the 12 jyotirlingas and a must on any Ujjain tour. If you're making an early morning visit, then I suggest you include the Bhasma Aarti—an important ritual that involves offering sacred ash to Lord Shiva. This is a must-include in your Ujjain tour itinerary for a complete experience. Historical Landmarks:During your Ujjain city tour, you will discover ancient sites that have seen centuries of history. Kal Bhairav Temple is one of the more interesting stops on any Ujjain tour, as liquor is offered here as prasad. The other place carrying both historical and religious significance is Ram Mandir Ghat. With a Ujjain travel guide, you can discover more about what you are seeing during your Ujjain tour. Rich Traditions and Rituals:When you choose Ujjain travel packages, you’ll be stepping into a world of vibrant traditions. The Kumbh Mela, held every 12 years in Ujjain, is one of the largest religious gatherings in the world. If you're visiting around this time, your Ujjain trip plan should accommodate this event, as it’s a once-in-a-lifetime experience. Various other rituals like Mangal Aarti at the temples or the Narmada Aarti along the river will give you a taste of Ujjain's spiritual essence. Ujjain's Unique Cuisine:No trip to Ujjain would be complete without relishing the delicious food that Ujjain offers. Right from crispy poha and jalebi to Bhutte ka Kees, the city's food will leave you wanting more. Apna Sweets and Modak Restaurant are a must add to any Ujjain trip packages. In conclusion, your Ujjain travel will offer an enriching blend of history, culture, and faith. Whether you're booking Ujjain tour packages or customised Ujjain holiday packages, the city promises a fulfilling journey. Let your Ujjain tour guide lead you to the top places to visit in Ujjain, ensuring your Ujjain sightseeing is packed with memorable experiences.
Ujjain.Places to visit in Ujjain: When you're planning your trip with Ujjain packages, you'll be amazed to discover that the city offers more than just temples. There are several other religious and natural sites worth exploring, adding a unique flavour to your experience. Let me guide you through some places to visit in Ujjain that will make your trip even more memorable: Ram Mandir Ghat:It is one of the ghats located on the banks of the Shipra River and is considered highly religious. Quite a several devotees perform sacred rituals here, especially during Kumbh Mela celebrations. You would feel the spiritual energy just by walking on the ghat. This is not something you should miss in your Ujjain tour guide-sun down. Bhartrihari Caves: These caves are located on the banks of the Shipra River, and because of this, they are quite an interesting addition to your Ujjain travel packages. They bear the name of the famous poet Bhartrihari who is said to have meditated there. The caves are a serene escape from the bustle of the city, and allow you to reflect while soaking up history. Sandipani Ashram: This is where Lord Krishna and Sudama were groomed as learners. Sandipani Ashram is a spiritual learning centre. It is an otherwise quiet place, a fair distance from the hustle-bustle of the city. Do not miss adding it to your Ujjain tour packages for a more diverse experience. Kalidasa Academy: Kalidasa Academy is one of the most unique places to visit in Ujjain that is interesting for literature and culture lovers. Dedicated to the legendary poet, Kalidasa, this cultural centre hosts exhibitions and events where an individual gets a glimpse of ancient Indian literature and performing arts. The Sacred Trees of Siddhavat: Siddhavat is a sacred banyan tree located along the Shipra River. It is believed to fulfil wishes, and people often put threads on its trunk as part of their devotions. Adding this to your Ujjain tour packages will offer you a special, nature-centric aspect of Ujjain tourism. With so many spiritual and natural spots, your Ujjain tour packages will go beyond just the temples at Ujjain. Including these places to see in Ujjain ensures your trip is diverse and fulfilling!
Ujjain.Temples to Visit in Ujjain: When you're planning a trip to Ujjain, visiting its famous temples is a must. The temples at Ujjain are not just sacred spots but are integral to the spiritual and cultural heart of the city. Let me walk you through some of the best places to visit in Ujjain when it comes to its magnificent temples. Mahakaleshwar Temple:You may have visited several destinations, but the Mahakaleshwar Temple is something you should not miss. This is one of the 12 jyotirlingas that make it a very essential place to visit in Ujjain. The temple is famous for the Bhasma Aarti, which is performed early in the morning. You should include this in your list of tours to Ujjain for a complete experience. Harsiddhi Temple:Another iconic location is the Harsiddhi Temple, especially for the devotees of Goddess Durga. The temple is illuminated by large oil lamps on Navratri, making it one of the most visually captivating Ujjain tourist places. The unique combination of ancient architecture and spiritual significance makes it one of the best places in Ujjain to visit. Kal Bhairav Temple: Another fascinating stop on your Ujjain sightseeing list is the Kal Bhairav Temple. What's unique about this temple is its ritual of offering liquor to the deity. It’s definitely one of the more intriguing temples in Ujjain and should be part of your things to do in Ujjain. Chintaman Ganesh Temple: This temple is considered one of the oldest temples dedicated to Lord Ganesha in Ujjain. The temple is especially important to get peace and mental relief. Ancient architecture and a tranquil atmosphere make the temple an excellent place to add to your Ujjain trip plan. Gadkalika Temple:It is believed to be the temple where the great poet Kalidasa worshipped, Gadkalika Temple is a blend of light spirituality and history. It is not one of the popular Ujjain tourist places, but you should feature it in the itinerary of your sightseeing tour Ujjain. Mangalnath Temple: Known as the birthplace of Mars (Mangal), this temple is one of the most revered places for getting astrological doshas cured. A visit to this temple adds a profound experience for travellers making a Ujjain tour. The best time to visit Ujjain for temple-hopping is during festival seasons like Mahashivratri or Navratri. These are the best places to see in Ujjain, and they offer not only spiritual fulfilment but also a deeper connection to the city's heritage. Your Ujjain trip plan will be incomplete without exploring these temples at Ujjain, each offering its unique charm! Here's a timetable for visiting the temples in Ujjain, including the best festivals and opening hours: Temple Name Opening Hours Best Month (Festival) Festival Mahakaleshwar Temple 4 AM to 11 PM February-March Mahashivratri Kal Bhairav Temple 4 AM to 11 PM November-December Bhairav Ashtami Harsiddhi Temple 5 AM to 9 PM September-October Navratri Chintaman Ganesh Temple 5 AM to 9 PM August-September Ganesh Chaturthi Mangalnath Temple 5 AM to 9 PM July-August Mangala Gowri Vrat Gadkalika Temple 5 AM to 9 PM September-October Navratri
Ujjain.Things to Know While Visiting Ujjain: When you're planning your trip with Ujjain packages, there are a few important things to keep in mind: Dress modestly, traditional attire is always preferred, especially while visiting the top places to visit in Ujjain like temples. Pre-book your Ujjain tour packages during peak festivals like Mahashivratri for a smooth experience. Follow the local customs, especially at the Ujjain tourist places and temples and always remove your shoes before entering. Avoid loud conversations and photography inside temples, unless allowed. For first-time visitors, hiring a Ujjain tour guide or using Ujjain travel packages can enhance your experience of Ujjain tourist attractions. Make sure your Ujjain itinerary includes trying local Ujjain food at popular spots. Ujjain is a holy city, and there are strict rules against littering, especially near sacred sites so always carry a reusable bag and water bottle.
Ujjain.Best time to visit Ujjain: If you're planning a tour to Ujjain, the best time to explore this holy city is between October and March. The pleasant weather during these months makes it perfect for your Ujjain city tour. October to March (Winter Season): It is believed to be the best time to travel to Ujjain. The cool, dry climate will make it easy for you to visit the best attractions in Ujjain, including the famous Mahakaleshwar Temple. It is also an ideal time to enjoy roadside famous food in Ujjain. Mahashivratri (February-March): If you love religious festivals, this is the time when Ujjain wakes up to its strength. Your Ujjain tour guide will recommend including this festival in your Ujjain trip plan. The feeling of the spiritual vibe in the city during these days cannot be explained. July to September (Monsoon Season): While the monsoon season can bring heavy rains, it’s still a good time to visit the famous temples in Rameswaram and Ujjain. If you're comfortable with a little rain, you’ll find fewer crowds and enjoy the serene atmosphere. No matter when you go, pre-booking your Ujjain trip packages can make your experience smoother. Your Ujjain travel will be rich with cultural experiences, and with a well-planned Ujjain itinerary, you’ll cover all the highlights.
Ujjain.Quotes and Inspirations: Here are some meaningful quotes and personal affirmations inspired by my visit to Ujjain, which can enrich your Ujjain trip experience: “The mind is everything. What you think you become.” — Buddha. This quote reminds you to stay positive and focused during your Ujjain tour, helping to elevate your spiritual journey. “You must be the change you wish to see in the world.” — Mahatma Gandhi. This resonates deeply as you visit the temples at Ujjain, encouraging you to embrace personal growth and transformation. “In the silence of the heart, you will find the answer.” This quote inspires reflection as you explore the Ujjain tourist attractions. As you plan your Ujjain itinerary, let these words guide you toward deeper insights and experiences that will enrich your journey. Whether you’re enjoying the famous food in Ujjain or wandering through its ancient streets, keep these inspirations close to your heart.
Ujjain.What to Eat in Ujjain?: When you’re in Ujjain, trying the local cuisine is a must. The city offers a delightful mix of traditional flavours that perfectly complement your Ujjain trip plan. Dal Bafla: The most prominent local cuisine found here is Dal Bafla. It tastes like Rajasthan's Dal Baati but softer. You can have it at Meghdoot Punjabi Restaurant, a local favourite. Sabudana Khichdi: It is a light snack you can enjoy during Ujjain tourism that is equally filling, particularly during fasting. For that, you need to go to Vikram Tea Stall which is just next to the Mahakaleshwar Temple. Poha Jalebi: A popular breakfast, Poha or flattened rice served together with crunchy and sweet Jalebi is great to begin your Ujjain sightseeing. You can try this at Modak Restaurant. Bhutte ka Kees: The unique dish made of grated corn with a burst of flavour is something you won't find everywhere. You can head to Apna Sweets for this. Malpua: For those with a sweet tooth, Malpua, a deep-fried sweet pancake, is a must-have. Shipra Bhawan serves some of the best. Your Ujjain tour itinerary will feel incomplete without tasting these local delights. Make sure your Ujjain travel guide points out the best eateries as you explore the top places to visit in Ujjain. After all, indulging in Ujjain famous food is an experience that adds flavour to your entire journey!
Ujjain.Shopping in Ujjain: When it comes to shopping in Ujjain, you’ll find a treasure trove of unique items that capture the essence of this holy city. Here are some tips for your Ujjain trip: Local Handicrafts:Visit the local markets like Chowk and Gopal Mandir Market, and get beautiful handcrafted items. You can look for wooden artefacts, brassware, and so on. These make unique souvenirs and must not miss your list of Ujjain tours. Traditional Attire:If you are interested in wearing traditional dress, then head for Vishal Garments for some exquisite ethnic wear. It's a great way to mingle with the local crowd while visiting the Ujjain tourist attractions. Religious Offerings: Being an important city, you would find some excellent shops selling religious articles. There would be plenty of puja thalis and lots of intricate idols. Visiting the shops is one of the top things to do in Ujjain. Handmade Jewelry:Seek out unique, handcrafted jewellery from local artisans. Look for shops around Fatehgarh Fort that sell intricate designs at reasonable prices, perfect for gifts or personal keepsakes. Local Spices and Sweets: Do not forget to pick up some local spices or sweets from Apna Sweets or Madhuram Sweets. These are ideal to distribute among friends and family back home.

Jordan.Introduction: About Jordan Holiday: Have you ever dreamt of wandering through Jordan? Have you later worried about how time and money could be major constraints to this wish of yours? Well, we say give us your worries and relax as we curate the ultimate Jordan Holiday for you! Jordan.Most Popular Jordan Tour Packages: Jordan Tour Packages No. of Nigts / Days Price* Best Of Jordan 5 Nights/ 6 Days Rs. 1 43 070 Egyptian Extravaganza With Jordan 11 Nights/ 12 Days Rs. 2 67 195 About JordanLocated on the eastern bank of the river Jordan, the Hashemite Kingdom of Jordan is an Arab country with the most vibrant of bazaars, desert castles that put everything else to shame, and food that is finger-licking good. From taking a panoramic tour of its capital city on a horse and going on safari through the storms of Wadi Rum, to looking for exquisite corals in the Red Sea and dipping yourself in the shallowest water body in the world, it wouldn't be wrong to say that Jordan is an enlightening trip. With stories engraved all across its rocks, a Nabatean city as old as time, and Roman structures that take you back at least a few centuries, Jordan is a captivating destination that fills you up with magnificent stories which will stay with you forever and more. Home to a UNESCO World Heritage Site and one of the new 7 Wonders of the World, Jordan offers an elegant and impeccable retreat that a traveller will never forget.To get the ultimate taste of Jordan, hurry up and book your holiday package today. If you want to dig a little deeper about this impressive country to plan your holiday accordingly, then please keep reading. Best Time to Visit JordanThe peak time to plan a trip to the Hashemite Kingdom of Jordan is between the months of March and May. Visiting Jordan and making the most of it involves being outdoors throughout, and so, the Spring season serves the perfect temperatures for you to loop a sling bag around your neck, wear a pair of the most comfortable shoes you own and hop between Jordanian attractions. If, however, Spring does not suit your travel plans, then the next season to visit Jordan would definitely be Autumn, which stops over at Jordan between the months of September and November. People who have been plotting for years to take the plunge at Aqaba, and literally so, these months are the best ones. This is not only because the water is soothing during the season of Autumn, but also because you will see marine life of Jordan in its magnificence and abundance during this time of the year. Though considered an all-year travel destination, Jordan can be pretty hot during summers, preventing you from floating in the Dead Sea or gallivanting through the capital city of Amman. Visiting the Jordan valley during sweltering months of Summer may be a bad idea, and therefore isn't recommended. How to Reach JordanBy AirOnce you have decided to visit Jordan, every way, easy and hard, opens up to make the dream a reality for you! The two major travel choices, that is by air and by road, are all available to reach Jordan. There are five airports in the country, of which the Queen Alia International Airport, 35kms from Amman, serves as the main airport. In Amman, this international airport welcomes several airlines from around the globe, such as Emirates, British Airways, Air France, Qatar Airways, and Lufthansa, to name a few. Royal Jordanian Airlines, which is the national carrier, brings people from 43 destinations into Jordan. By RoadFor those travelling to Jordan from other middle eastern places like Jerusalem or Syria, availing the bus service to transverse the picturesque landscapes by road is a good option. If you are travelling from the borders of Syria, Saudi Arabia, or Israel, then you can even go for cab services available there. The railways have been suspended in the area since 2006, due to their dysfunctionality. Where to Go in JordanIf you are planning to head off to Jordan for your next vacation, then it is clear to us that you are someone who likes exploring places off the beaten road. Keeping that in mind, here's our list of extraordinary places in Jordan that your travel boots should make it to: AmmanReady to live out your very own version of Arabian Nights? The place in Jordan from where you should start this incredible journey, would definitely be Amman. The capital city of Jordan, Amman is the perfect cocktail with ancient history as its base, and smooth hints of modernity as the ultimate flavours. For those who are stirred most by history, Jebel al-Qala, the highest hill in the city, has ruins waiting to bare stories carved into their stones. On top of this hill lies the Citadel that will hold your imagination by its hand and walk you down time with its 8th century Umayyad Palace and the historic Roman structure, the Temple of Hercules. While enjoying the history and architecture scene in Amman, you should also take out time to be a part of its blossoming art space. If you find yourself in the city in Summer, then missing out on the Amman Summer Festival would be a big mistake. The art fiesta is a burst of energy, of epic dance and music filling the entire King Hussein Park, transforming it into a festival goer's nirvana. And for those who are fans of the original retreat, one of the most ancient homes of mankind, Iraq al-Amir, with olive groves separating its caves, is the place to be when in Amman, Jordan. Dead SeaImagine floating in the saltiest and the lowest ocean of the world while surrounded by wise old mountains and sand dunes the colour of earth. You wouldn't be wrong to assume that you have reached paradise, but to be more accurate, you would have landed in the Dead Sea soaking up the Arabian sun. Jordan nestles the Dead Sea on its east while Palestine does the same in the west. When in the Dead Sea, floating in its often deemed magical waters, is an activity that you must participate in. While some believe the water can heal arthritis and osteoporosis, others marvel at how the mud by the sea is a potion for skin allergies. Whether these beliefs are true or not can only be settled by testing the waters for yourself. A pro-tip for this activity: The Dead Sea is ten times saltier than the other natural water bodies of the world, and therefore, do not shave any part of your body at least 48 hours before jumping in. PetraIt is often said that a traveller looks back on her journeys with rose-tinted glasses, but once you visit Petra, the Rose City of Jordan, your memories will literally be coloured pink. The multi-coloured rocks and the Nabatean architecture of this city make it an archaeological treasure that people from around the globe flock to see. If Petra is the gem of Jordan, the Egyptian-art-inspired Obelisk Tomb is Petra's jewel. Situated between the Petra Visitor Centre and Siq, you will see four obelisks and a weathered human figure in the middle. It is believed five people were buried here and each obelisk and the central figure represent their funeral. This architecturally-gorgeous monument will lead you to the door of the Siq, welcoming you to Petra. A 1200-metre journey and stories galore await your arrival. Wadi RumWhile Petra is the showstopper of your Jordan delight, Wadi Rum is unmissable for its rather unexplored beauty. With sun setting every evening behind it red-sand desert, Wadi Rum is nature's ultimate gift to a photographer who dreams in landscapes. If the skies allow it and you are lucky, then you can even see the Red Sea from up above the Jabal Umm ad Dami, the highest peak in Wadi Rum. The Nabateans have inscribed their history as rock drawings and carvings all across Wadi Rum. The most famous of these imprints is the Khazali Canyon, named after a criminal called Khazal who ran through the chunk of Jabal Khazali mountain right up to summit while being chased. When he reached the top, he had nowhere to go, and so jumped off the cliff, but landed unharmed. Apart from this marvellous story, this canyon is also home to some of the most unique reptiles and desert mammals that you will ever lay your eyes on. Mount NeboThe Holy Land of Mount Nebo, where Christians believe Moses saw the Promised Land, is mostly visited by archaeologists, cattle, and someone who has lost their way. But that is exactly what adds to the beauty of this place. For those with a pictorial imagination that can infuse history into the ruins to make them magically come alive, Mount Nebo is a hidden treasure on a road not taken by too many travellers. Climbing up Mount Nebo would mean making it to the place from where you can see panoramic views of Moses' Promised Land. If that was not enough, on a clear day, you can even see breath-taking sights of the Jordan Valley as well as the Dead Sea from up here. Imagine being at the summit of this historic mountain, away from the worries of the world looking at some miraculous sights, now isn't that a holiday in the true sense of the word! JerashWould you like to see some astonishing glimpses of Rome almost 4000km away from Rome? If yes, then Jerash has to make it to your list of Jordanian enchantments. A day's trip from the capital city, Amman, Jerash is one of most well-preserved Roman ruins located out of Italy. If arches and temples evoking memories of Rome is your idea of beauty, then you are not alone. Admirers of distinctive architecture make it a point to travel to Jerash when in Jordan to explore the bygone era of exuberance of the Middle East. Food in JordanLet the Levantine aromas and scents of candied fruits take over your senses when walking along the streets of Jordan. Amman, the capital city, is a treat with its sweet and spicy delicacies leaving you drooling and wanting for more. Whether it is the crumbling deliciousness of baklawa, or the cheesy gooey pastries locally known as kanafeh, Amman knows perfectly well how to tease a connoisseur's taste buds. A traditional Jordanian cuisine involves the tastiest falafels your tongue will ever taste. It also includes a large platter of Mansaf, meat coated with flatbread that is as thin as paper served along with a mound of fragrant rice. Here's a fun food fact that foodies might already be aware of: Mansaf is the national dish of Jordan and is a regular in a Bedouin kitchen. Food does not have a language, it transcends every boundary, and that is why Mansaf is cooked in a variety of ways in Jordan, some recipes coming out of Israel and some even from Iraq. Have you ever experienced the elements of drama in food? If not, then you must try the Maqluba when in Jordan. Best defined as a comfort food, Maqluba is a cake of upturned rice with meat chunks buried in it. Each spoon of Maqluba will tell you how the rice not only hides lumps of meat in it, but also the feeling of home. Once you have relished the Maqluba, be sure to taste the Ottoman import, one of the tastiest lamb preparations in the world, the Shawarma. Sweep its richness with a piece of moist pita bread and close your eyes as you savour its sizzling taste for a long time. Things to Do in JordanTouring Jordan with Thomas Cook India means being a part of a whole lot of activities, both cultural and adrenalin-thumping fun. From visiting the most spectacular archaeological artefact, the Roman Theatre, to sleeping in Bedouin camps under a starry night, here are some of the most fascinating things to do in Jordan: Adventures of the desertGet ready for an adventure of a lifetime by going on a desert safari in Wadi Rum. Chasing a sandstorm in a jeep isn't all that this desert has to offer; here, you can even experience life on Mars by staying overnight in a dome that is built like a spaceship. Another life changing experience that you ought to add to your bucket list is camping in woollen yurts under stars in Wadi Rum with the Nomadic tribe, Bedouin. A horseback ride through mountainsTo get the most stunning views of the Rose City of Petra, get on top of a horse and let the animal escort you through this ancient city. Travellers who have been to Petra exclaim in joy about the experience of seeing the rugged and gorgeous terrain of Petra while riding a horse. This is definitely unmissable! The ultimate Seagasm at AqabaVisiting Aqaba and not scuba diving is like visiting Jordan and skipping Petra! The only Jordanian coastal city, Aqaba boasts of a vast marine life, with more than 500 coral species calling its waters home. Apart from the corals, you are also likely to stumble by a tank at the bottom of the Red Sea. A Lebanese freighter is another treasure you will catch sight of. See the Roman TheatreBuilt under the rule of Antonius Pius, one of the most peace-loving Roman Emperors, the Roman Theatre was built between 138-161 CE. The theatre is not only spectacular-looking, but is also designed in such a way that the sun never meets or hurts the spectators. You must not miss the chance of being one of the 6000 people that the steep yet comfortable stairs of this theatre can house. Lose your way while admiring the beauty of Little PetraWhile the antique city of Petra takes up most of the limelight, Siq Al Barid or Little Petra doesn't fail to impress when given a chance. From some of the most ancient settlements ever to have existed to landscapes that leave you breathless, Little Petra on a camel is a must when on a Jordanian journey with family.
