faq.question: How many days are ideal for a Goa trip?
faq.answer: Usually, 4–7 days is the sweet spot. This period enables you to strike a balance between the calm of the south and the lively energy of the north if you have reserved a Goa holiday package or a comprehensive Goa trip package. Longer visits allow you to explore at your own pace and immerse yourself in cultural experiences, while shorter stays may feel hurried, particularly if you're attempting to see both areas.

faq.question: North Goa vs. South Goa: Which is better for me?
faq.answer: Your travel preferences will determine this. If you're looking for markets, nightlife, and bustling beaches like Baga and Calangute, a North Goa tour package is ideal. However, for peaceful beaches like Palolem or Agonda, yoga retreats, and peaceful evenings, a South Goa tour package is perfect. To get the best of both worlds, many tourists combine the two in their Goa travel packages.

faq.question: Is Goa safe for solo female travellers/families?
faq.answer: Although Goa is thought to be safe, as is the case everywhere, a little prudence goes a long way. Select reputable accommodations that are part of your Goa packages, stay away from remote areas late at night, and limit your travel to well-known clubs or beachside locations if you are a single woman. Goa's kid-friendly attractions, lifeguard-patrolled beaches, and well-run resorts will appeal to families. Just keep basic guidelines in mind, such as not swimming after dark and having emergency contacts on hand.

faq.question: What are the must-try Goan dishes?
faq.answer: One of the highlights of any trip to Goa is the food. Sorpotel, Pork Vindaloo, Chicken Xacuti, and Goan Fish Curry with steamed rice are must-try dishes. The renowned layered dessert bebinca is a must-try for anyone with a sweet tooth, while street food enthusiasts should sample ros omelette and choris pao. You can get a genuine taste of Goa's distinctive fusion of Portuguese and Indian cuisine by dining at neighbourhood shacks or restaurants, which are frequently featured in travel packages.

faq.question: Can I rent a scooter in Goa?
faq.answer: Of course. It's the most practical and economical way to travel, frequently resulting in a cheaper trip to Goa than taking a taxi. Helmets are required, and a valid driver's license is required. Finding a rental is easy, whether you're in Panjim, Calangute, or Palolem, because they are available in all of the popular areas.

faq.question: What is the best way to enjoy Goa's nightlife?
faq.answer: You can find famous clubs like Tito's or Hammerzz if you reserve a Goa tour package with flight and lodging in North Goa. Beach shacks in Anjuna or Vagator provide live music and bonfire nights for a more relaxed atmosphere. For a distinctive experience in South Goa, attend a silent noise party at Palolem.

faq.question: What is included in Goa tour packages?
faq.answer: When you make a Goa tour package booking, you usually receive accommodation, food, sightseeing tours, and travel. Adventure activities, nightlife tours, and cultural explorations are also included in certain Goa holiday packages. You can pick a honeymoon package trip to Goa, a Goa trip package with friends, or a Goa family tour package depending on what type of traveler you are.

faq.question: What is the Goa tour cost?
faq.answer: The Goa tour cost depends on accommodation class, length, and activities. Budget Goa travel packages begin at ₹7,000, but luxury ones rise above that. For a 5-day Goa travel package, the budget can vary between ₹10,000 and ₹30,000, including important Goa tourist points.

faq.question: Are Goa tour packages suitable for families?
faq.answer: Yes! Many Goa tour packages for families incorporate beaches suitable for children, wildlife sanctuaries, and river cruises. These packages usually include church visits, spice plantations, and dolphin-watching tours, making Goa an excellent destination for people of all ages.

faq.question: How many days are sufficient for a Goa trip?
faq.answer: A 5-day Goa tour package is best suited for visiting both North and South Goa. In case you don't have much time, a 3-day Goa trip package allows you to visit major Goa tourist spots, beaches, and nightlife. But if you want to relax, a week is the best duration!

faq.question: What are the must-haves in Goa food?
faq.answer: If you're a foodie, you shouldn't miss Goan Prawn Curry, Bebinca, and Vindaloo. Seafood enthusiasts will relish the real Goa cuisine at beach shacks. Eating Goa's local cuisine is one of the things to do in Goa!

Goa.Introduction: Imagine entering a location where turquoise waves meet golden sands and where music, spice, and historical tales permeate the air. That is precisely how a trip to Goa feels. Goa always manages to surprise you, whether you're planning your first vacation or your fifth. The vibrant energy of Baga or Calangute may entice you, or you may prefer the more subdued charm of South Goa with its tranquil beaches and historic sites that are best seen with a South Goa tour package. What makes Goa so appealing is its mix of everything you love in a holiday - from the Portuguese cathedrals that whisper history, to floating casinos on the Mandovi that shine at night. A Goa vacation is not just about beaches; it’s also colourful festivals, soulful music, football-crazy residents, and food that will make you want to stay longer. That's where carefully chosen Goa travel & vacation packages come in useful, allowing you to explore stress-free. This destination never lets you down, whether you're looking for a family-friendly Goa package, a leisurely Goa holiday package, or a Goa travel packages for fun with friends. Every sunset seems to be reserved for you here.
Goa.Most Popular Tour Packages of Goa: Tour Packages NameDurationPriceSimply North Goa3 Nights 4 DaysRs. ₹8 900Escape to North Goa3 Nights 4 DaysRs. ₹13 000Exceptional South Goa3 Nights 4 DaysRs. ₹15 600Amazing Goa4 Nights 5 DaysRs. ₹18 600Incredible Goa4 Nights 5 DaysRs. ₹14 500
Goa.Why Goa is the Perfect Getaway for Every: Nothing compares to the allure of a Goa tour when you're looking for a getaway that combines beaches, history, nightlife, and culture. Goa offers something that feels like it was made especially for you, regardless of the type of traveller you are. Beaches for All Feelings The North Goa tour package is ideal if you want to experience the vibrant, busy atmosphere of Baga and Calangute. A South Goa tour package, however, will take you to serene areas like Palolem and Colva if you're yearning for some peace. You won't ever feel out of place with Goa packages made for both moods. Nightlife and Adventure Goa is known for its thrilling activities, which include scuba diving, parasailing, and dancing the night away at clubs and shacks. Water sports or admission to night cruises are frequently included in Goa travel packages, which ensure a flawless experience. Heritage and Culture There is more to a Goa tour than just surf and sunsets. Every area is rich in history, from the Basilica of Bom Jesus to the vibrant festivals and colourful Portuguese homes. You can fully immerse yourself in this vibrant culture with Goa travel packages. Adaptable Options There are countless options, ranging from romantic retreats and solo excursions to family-friendly Goa packages. There is always a Goa travel packages that suits your needs, whether your Goals are leisure, adventure, or culture. Affordable Choices Concerned about the Goa trip cost? You can find something that suits your budget among the many Goa tour packages and Goa trip packages available. To save money and effort, you can even choose a Goa tour package with flight.
Goa.Planning Your Goa Trip: Essential Inform: The excitement of organising a trip to Goa starts long before you even set foot on the golden sands. With so many different Goa tour and travel package options, it's helpful to know the basics so you can plan a trip that suits your travel preferences and budget. Here is everything you need to know before purchasing your Goa holiday package, regardless of whether you're thinking about a North Goa tour package that offers vibrant nightlife or a South Goa tour package that offers tranquillity and charm.
Goa.Best Time to Visit Goa: Goa is welcoming throughout the year, but the time of year you choose will determine how you experience it. Peak Season (November to February): The most popular time to visit Goa is during the peak season, which runs from November to February. The weather is consistently pleasant and cool, making it ideal for water sports, beach walks, and celebrations of holidays like Christmas and New Year's. The only drawback is that Goa becomes crowded and more expensive during this time of year. A wise way to save money is to book Goa tour packages or a Goa tour package with flight. Monsoon Season (June to October): This is the time of year for you if you enjoy peace and greenery. You can find fantastic savings on Goa packages and Goa trip packages, and waterfalls like Dudhsagar look their best. Heavy rains, however, may limit water activities. Summer Season (March to May): Although it can get hot, the summer months of March through May are a good time to take advantage of cheaper flights and lodging. Budget travellers can truly stretch their money here, and adventurers frequently choose this time of year for water sports.
Goa.How to Reach Goa?: Depending on your location and financial constraints, you can choose to travel to Goa by air, train, or road. By Air: Manohar International (Mopa) in the north and Dabolim in the south are Goa's two airports. Choosing a Goa tour package with flight facilitates travel, particularly during busy times. By Road: Travelling to Goa by car is an adventure in and of itself. Beautiful coastal stretches can be found along National Highway 66. Goa is also connected to cities like Pune, Bangalore, and Mumbai by regular buses. By Train: From Delhi, Mumbai, and other major cities, there are trains on the picturesque Konkan Railway. The principal stations are Madgaon, Vasco-da-Gama, and Thivim.
Goa.Getting Around in Goa: Once you're there, exploring is where the real fun starts. Scooter or Bike Rentals: Renting a bike or scooter is the most common option for travellers. It keeps the Goa trip cost down, gives you freedom, and is reasonably priced. Taxis: Taxis are widely available, but it's advisable to negotiate prices in advance. You can make reservations using the Goa Miles app for transparency. Car Rentals: Rental cars are ideal for groups or families. More comfortable but a little more expensive. Local Buses and Ferries: Local buses and ferries are inexpensive and ideal for experiencing local culture. Ferries are particularly charming when traversing rivers and islands. Motorcycle Taxis (Pilots): A distinctive Goan feature, motorbike taxis (pilots) are excellent for short distances.
Goa.Accommodation Options: Your entire trip to Goa can be influenced by where you choose to stay. Luxury Resorts & Hotels: Located mostly in South Goa, luxury resorts and hotels provide world-class service, private beaches, and swimming pools. Perfect if you reserve through the upscale Goa trip package. Beach Huts & Cottages: If you want to wake up to the sound of waves, beach huts and cottages are ideal. Ideal for couples and low-budget travellers. Boutique Hotels & Guesthouses: Located throughout the state, boutique hotels and guesthouses offer individualised stays that strike a balance between comfort and regional flair. Homestays & Service Apartments: Homestays and service apartments are perfect for families and extended stays because they provide a homey atmosphere with kitchens. Hostels: Hostels are social, reasonably priced, and perfect for backpackers. A great way to reduce the Goa trip cost.
Goa.Curated Goa tour packages by Thomas Cook: You most likely picture sandy beaches, a bustling nightlife, mouthwatering seafood, and countless adventures when you think of a trip to Goa. However, it can occasionally feel overwhelming to plan everything yourself. Thomas Cook's carefully chosen Goa tour packages, which are intended to make your vacation stress-free and unforgettable, can help with that. These Goa packages, with their thoughtfully designed itineraries, handle your travel, lodging, sightseeing, and activities, leaving you free to savour every second. There is a tour package that is ideal for your travel style, whether you are looking for a North Goa tour package that is full of energy and nightlife or a South Goa tour package that offers romance and peace. Package Categories: Family Packages: Comfort and family-friendly activities are crucial when organising a vacation. Family-friendly resorts with pools, kid-friendly areas, and entertainment are included in Goa trip package. Anticipate dolphin-watching excursions, spice plantation visits, and sightseeing tours to Old Goa's historic churches, activities that satisfy everyone. Honeymoon Packages: A Goa Honeymoon package can become the most romantic getaway if you're travelling as a couple. Imagine spa treatments for two, sunset cruises on the Mandovi River, and candlelit dinners by the beach. A lot of these Goa trip package focus on opulent stays in South Goa, which provide the seclusion and tranquilly you would desire for a honeymoon. Solo and Group Packages: A Goa trip package is an exciting and safe way to meet other adventurous travellers while still enjoying your independence if you're travelling alone. Customised itineraries combine beach hopping, sightseeing, and nightlife for larger groups of friends. These group travel packages to Goa strike a balance between enjoyment and freedom, ensuring that everyone makes the most of their time there. Adventure Packages: Adventure-focused itineraries are also included in Goa tour packages for those who enjoy thrills. Imagine hiking through the verdant Western Ghats, taking a jeep safari to the Dudhsagar Falls, and participating in a variety of water sports like jet skiing, parasailing, and snorkelling. These Goa packages are designed to really raise your heart rate. Party & Nightlife Packages: These are for you if you envision a Goa tour that involves dancing until dawn. The most well-known clubs, beach festivals, and party spots in North Goa are the focus of specially designed nightlife packages. With everything planned ahead of time, you can party at Anjuna, Baga, or even on a river cruise. Additional Tailored Bundles: For those looking for luxury, wellness, or spirituality, there are also Goa trip package available. You could choose luxury getaways in upscale resorts that redefine indulgence, or you could take part in contemplative retreats, touring Goa's churches and serene villages.
Goa.Top Attractions & Experiences in Goa (No: Choosing where to go and what to do is one of the most exciting aspects of planning a trip to Goa. Goa is like two places in one: South Goa, which is quiet, serene, and ideal for a leisurely getaway, and North Goa, which is vibrant, energetic, and lively. There is a lot to discover, whether you reserve your Goa trip package for a tranquil South Goa tour package or for exciting northern days. The best part is that most Goa tour packages give you a taste of both by combining them. North Goa Baga Beach Beach shacks, water sports, and nightlife abound on Baga, one of the most well-known beaches. This is the place to be if you enjoy parasailing or just relaxing by the waves. It is nearly always included in a North Goa tour package. Aguada Fort A must-see during your Goa tour is this Portuguese fort from the 17th century. It is ideal for history buffs and photographers, and it has a view of the Arabian Sea. Anjuna Beach and Flea Market Renowned for its bohemian atmosphere, Anjuna hosts trance parties and the well-known Wednesday Flea Market, where you can purchase clothing, jewellery, and handicrafts. The Basilica of Bom Jesus The relics of St. Francis Xavier are kept in this church, which is a UNESCO World Heritage Site. Many Goa tour packages include this significant cultural destination. Fort Chapora Chapora is well-known for Bollywood films and for its breathtaking views of Vagator Beach. It is a popular destination on the majority of Goa holiday packages. South Goa Butterfly Beach Only reachable by boat or hiking, this undiscovered treasure feels serene and unspoiled. Make sure to request that this be included if you have a customised Goa holiday package. Palolem Beach Palolem, a crescent-shaped island famous for dolphin-spotting boat rides, is ideal for a peaceful getaway. It is the focal point of many tour packages to South Goa. Dudhsagar Waterfalls One of the most striking sights in Goa is this magnificent four-tiered waterfall. A jeep safari through the verdant forest that precedes it is a common feature of adventure-focused Goa packages. Yoga Retreats & Spice Plantations South Goa is also well-known for its spice plantations, where you can take part in traditional Goan cuisine and farm tours. Your Goa holiday package becomes a restorative getaway when you include yoga and meditation retreats. Fort Cabo de Rama A historic fort with expansive views of the coast and a quaint old church. It's perfect if you want to combine nature and history while visiting Goa.
Goa.Things to Do in Goa Beyond the Beaches (: Golden beaches and vibrant shacks are typically the first images that spring to mind when you think of a trip to Goa. Goa, however, is so much more than that. You can discover a world of adventures, culture, cuisine, and shopping that extends well beyond the coastline with the correct Goa tour packages. The experiences that await you are limitless, regardless of whether you choose a South Goa tour package for peace and history or a North Goa tour package for energy and nightlife. Let's look at a few must-do activities for your upcoming trip to Goa. Hike to the Dudhsagar Waterfalls A jeep safari to the stunning Dudhsagar Waterfalls is a must-do for nature lovers. The four-tiered waterfall, which is surrounded by lush forests, is particularly magical during the monsoon season. It's one of those things that completes a tour of Goa. Adventure Thrills and Water Sports Goa is an adventurer's dream come true. There are water sports for every level of excitement, from flyboarding in Chapora to parasailing and jet skiing at Calangute. While kayaking through mangroves offers a more subdued form of adventure, scuba diving excursions to Grand Island allow you to discover a variety of marine life. These options are now included in the majority of Goa travel packages, so you don't have to worry about additional Goa trip expenses. Exploration of Culture and History Your trip to Goa is about more than just having fun; it's also about learning about the local culture. Explore Old Goa and be in awe of the UNESCO-designated Basilica of Bom Jesus or Se Cathedral. Explore the vibrant Portuguese homes of Fontainhas, Panjim's Latin Quarter. These locations are frequently covered if you have a Goa holiday package that includes guided tours. Explore Goa’s Forts There are numerous historic forts in Goa, all of which have stunning views. While Cabo de Rama in the south adds a more subdued charm, Fort Aguada in the north is recognisable. You can gain a better understanding of Goa's history by visiting these locations while on vacation in Goa. Shopping The flea markets in Goa are renowned. You can find clothing, jewellery, and souvenirs at the Saturday Night Bazaar in Arpora and the Anjuna Flea Market. There are many options at Porvorim's Mall de Goa if you like contemporary shopping. An excellent Goa tour package with flight frequently gives you more time and energy to shop until you drop. Culinary exploration Goa is a foodie's paradise. You can take a cooking class to learn how to make traditional dishes like vindaloo or xacuti, or you can just eat seafood at well-known eateries like Martin's Corner. Local gaddos offer choris pao and ros omelette for those who enjoy street food. For a true culinary adventure, many carefully planned Goa packages even include food trails. Wellness retreats South Goa offers ayurvedic treatments, yoga retreats, and meditation centres for those looking for tranquilly. If you want both sightseeing and rejuvenation, booking a South Goa tour package is ideal. River Cruises on the Mandovi An unusual way to spend your evenings is to take a dinner or sunset cruise down the Mandovi River. With live music, dancing, and Goan vibes, it's an experience that makes your trip to Goa more glamorous without breaking the bank.
Goa.Essential Tips for a Smooth Goa Trip (Pa: It's always exciting to plan a trip to Goa, but a little planning can make your vacation less stressful and more pleasurable. Whether you're planning your own Goa travel packages or taking one of the many available, it makes a big difference to know what to pack, how to stay in touch, how to handle your finances, and to keep emergency contacts close at hand.
Goa.Checklist for Packing: Think tropical, lightweight, and useful when packing. Swimwear, sarongs, and cover-ups are essential if you're going from beach to shack, while cotton and linen clothing are your best bets for the humid climate. Bring comfortable walking shoes for exploring markets or forts, and flip-flops for the sand. Bring a light raincoat or umbrella if your Goa travel packages fall during the monsoon season. Remember to apply insect repellent, sunscreen, and sunglasses. To reduce plastic waste, it's also a good idea to bring along a reusable water bottle and a small first-aid kit. A waterproof phone pouch will be useful because Goa holiday packages frequently include adventurous activities. ATMs and banking With so many ATMs in Goa's towns and popular tourist locations, taking out cash is rarely an issue. However, it's a good idea to have some cash on hand for smaller sellers at beach shacks or flea markets. Notifying your bank about your Goa tour dates helps prevent needless card blocks, but the majority of hotels and restaurants accept cards. Don't forget to budget for local transport, shopping and spontaneous street food treats when planning your trip to Goa. Connectivity It's simple to stay connected, but you'll need to prepare. Prepaid local SIM cards from Jio or Airtel are dependable and reasonably priced; they can be purchased at local stores or at the airport. A copy of your passport and visa is needed for activation, so keep them with you. Although you might not receive a local number, an eSIM can be an option if you'd prefer to avoid paperwork. Your North or South Goa tour package will go smoothly, and you won't get lost in new places if you have dependable connectivity. Contacts for Emergencies Having numbers on hand is always comforting. Dial 112. Ambulance services are available at 108, and the police can also be contacted at 100. Travellers can contact the helpline at 1363, and if a woman needs immediate assistance, she should dial 1091. It's also a good idea to have your hotel contacts and airline helpline close at hand if you have selected a Goa tour package that includes transport. Responsible Travel Making a positive impact is one of the most crucial parts of travel. Say no to needless plastic, carry a reusable water bottle, and properly dispose of waste. When visiting temples or churches, wear modest clothing and always be mindful of local traditions. One excellent way to add meaning to your Goa travel packages and give back to the community is to support regional craftspeople, spice plantations, and family-run restaurants.
Goa.Why Book Your Goa Tour with Thomas Cook?: Golden beaches, vibrant shacks, and heartwarming sunsets are what come to mind when you think of Goa. Imagine going through all of this without having to worry about preparation. That's why it's so important to book your Goa tour with Thomas Cook. You enter a skilfully designed experience rather than merely purchasing a ticket. You can select the ideal Goa tour package for your mood from a variety of options, such as a North Goa tour package that is teeming with adventure sports and nightlife, or a South Goa tour package that is full of peaceful churches and quiet beaches. If you're concerned about the Goa trip cost, there are options to suit every budget, ranging from luxurious resorts to more affordable lodging. To save you the trouble of making separate reservations, you can find well-thought-out Goa packages, such as a full Goa holiday package or even a Goa tour package with flight. Everything is set up for you, whether you're looking for a quick weekend getaway or a weeklong comprehensive Goa travel package. Thus, your trip to Goa turns into more than just a vacation. It turns into a journey where everything else is taken care of and you just unwind, explore, and make memories.

Vaishno Devi.Introduction: Shri Mata Vaishno Devi temple is one of the most important and revered shrines in India. Devotees from all over the country like to go on a holy pilgrimage to this shrine, which is located in Trikuta Hills, roughly 13 km from Katra in Jammu and Kashmir. Find essential information regarding the Shri Vaishno Devi tourism and the popular Vaishno Devi tour packages on this page.
Vaishno Devi.Most Popular Vaishno Devi Tour Packages: Tour Packages NameDurationPriceMata Vaishno Devi Temple Darshan Yatra2 Nights 3 DaysRs. ₹16 300
Vaishno Devi.Historical and cultural importance of Va: There are many legends associated with the origin of the temple. One such tale talks about a demon named Bhairon Nath, who was infatuated with the beauty of Vaishno Devi and sought to marry her. However, in order to commit to her penance, she fled from him to escape his approach. But Bhairon Nath was persistent and kept his pursuit on and followed her through the rugged Trikuta Mountains. In desperation, Vaishno Devi reached a cave in the mountains where she transformed into a powerful goddess. It is believed that outside this cave she beheaded the demon in her divine form. After this, Bhairon Nath had time to reflect on his mistake and sought forgiveness from the goddess. He attained salvation after merging his soul with the goddess. Devotees in millions visit this shrine every year. As for the historical background, there isn’t much information however the existence of the temple can be traced back several hundred years. The first mentions of the holy cave can be found in the ancient epic, Mahabharata. Popular belief is that the first ones to build the Kol Kandoli Temple and the main Bhawan were the Pandavas to show their gratitude to the mother goddess. Over time, the temple gained popularity and soon became a major site for pilgrimage in the country. The present structure of the temple is quite modern, considering it has undergone several renovations, additions and other changes. To enjoy all of these experiences on your trip, opt for the Vaishno Devi tour packages by Thomas Cook.
Vaishno Devi.Traditions and rituals practised in Vais: Throughout the year, millions of devotees and tourists visit Shri Vaishno Devi Temple. The occasion of Navarati is the most popular time for a spiritual Vaishno Devi tour. You can find Maa Vaishno Devi in Katra tour packages for the festive season on our website. The temple complex includes the holy cave dedicated to Vaishno Devi and other shrines dedicated to other deities. The main rituals at the Vaishno Devi temple are the traditional aarti (prayer) and the distribution of holy offerings (Prasad). The atmosphere of the shrine is divine and very soothing, with Bhajans (devotional songs) and “Jai Mata Di” chants reverberating through the air. On a visit to Shri Vaishno Devi Mandir, devotees make traditional offerings to the goddess of sarees, “chunri”, Cholas, gold or silver ornaments, flowers, etc.
Vaishno Devi.Places to Visit in Vaishno Devi: On your Vaishno Devi tour, make sure to check out the nearby places of religious importance, such as; Other religious places to visit in Vaishno Devi Dera Baba Banda Dera Baba Banda is one of the best places to see in Vaishno Devi for Sikhs. Located in Katra’s Reasi district, this holy gurudwara is over 300 years old and is dedicated to a sikh military officer named Baba Banda Bahadur, who was a follower and one of the favourites of Guru Govind Singh. It is roughly 60 km from Shri Vaishno Devi. It is sometimes included in Vaishno Devi tour packages. Ban Ganga Ban Ganga is located at a distance of 6 km from Katra Railway Station. It falls along the route of the holy yatra, making it one of the most important places to see in Vaishno Devi for pilgrims. It is a sacred river where pilgrims like to take a holy dip before continuing their journey to Shri Mata Vaishno Devi. The place has two ghats for holy dips and is associated with many interesting legends. It is a common part of the itinerary of popular Vaishno Devi holiday packages. Baba Dhansar Only 14 km from Katra Railway Station is the holy site of Baba Dhansar. Placed on the side of the majestic Karua Jheel, it is one of the most important temples in Vaishno Devi. As per legend, in ancient times, Baba Dhansar with the help of Lord Shiva was able to defeat a demon that caused havoc in the area. After that, the villages constructed a cave temple and a Nag temple in honour of Lord Shiva and Baba Dhansar. You can look for Vaishno Devi holiday packages featuring this site on our website. Unique features of Vaishno Devi Alongside the revered temples in Vaishno Devi, the landscape surrounding the region is quite breathtaking. While covering the trail, you will come across dense forests, majestic green hills, blue lakes and sacred cave temples. The revered temple of Shri Mata Vaishno Devi is located on the lap of Trikuta Hills. In addition, the sacred Ban Ganga river flowing through the region adds a scenic charm to the landscape. Our Vaishno Devi holiday packages are designed to offer you the ideal experience on your spiritual journey.
Vaishno Devi.Temples to Visit in Vaishno Devi: Here are the details of the main temples in Vaishno Devi; Shri Mata Vaishno Devi Temple The Shri Mata Vaishno Devi Temple is the main temple in the region and a standard part of all Vaishno Devi holiday packages. It is one of the most visited Vaishno Devi tourist places, drawing millions of devotees from all corners of the country every year. Situated on the lap of the beautiful Trikuta Hills in Katra, J&K, the shrine is dedicated to Mata Vaishno Devi or the Mother Goddess. It is believed that visiting this shrine will lead to one’s wishes being fulfilled. Inside this cave temple, there is the idol of the goddess in the form of a five and a half feet tall rock with three heads (Pindies), which signifies her three forms, Maha Kali, Maha Lakshmi and Maha Saraswati. The shrine is a standard inclusion of a Vaishno Devi trip plan. Ardhkuwari Just half an hour from Vaishno Devi Temple is the holy site of Ardhkuwari Gufa. This 52-foot-long cave is also known as Garbajoon cave and has a shape that resembles a mother’s womb. It lies on the route of the yatra, making it one of the most crucial Vaishno Devi places to visit. According to the legend, it is believed that Mata Vaishno Devi took shelter and meditated for nine months in this cave before proceeding towards the Holy Cave of Vaishno Devi. Get Vaishno Devi holiday packages featuring this shrine on our website. Bhairavnath Temple The Bhairavnath Temple is among the most noteworthy Vaishno Devi places to visit. Located on the Bhairavnath hilltop, it lies only a few minutes from the Holy Cave temple, making it a common inclusion in popular Vaishno Devi travel packages. Bhairavnath was a tantric who pursued Vaishno Devi for marriage. When she fled from him he followed her to the cave where he was beheaded by the goddess in her divine form. According to the locals, the site of the temple is believed to be the place where he took his last breath and it was later constructed into a temple dedicated to him. Devotees on their Vaishno Devi pilgrimage often add this shrine to their Vaishno Devi itinerary. Deva Mai Temple The Deva Mai Temple is an important temple in the region. Just 9 km from the Katra Railway Station, it is a major inclusion of a spiritual Vaishno Devi itinerary. There are many legends associated with this place, one of which suggests that it is a place where Vaishno Devi meditated during the early Kalyuga phase in order to complete her life as a human. Get Vaishno Devi travel packages featuring prominent tourist attractions on Thomas Cook. Temple Name Opening Time & Closing Time Distance from Katra Railway Station Shri Mata Vaishno Devi Temple 6 AM to 12 PM & 4:30 PM to 9:30 PM 16 km Ardhkuwari 5 AM to 9 PM 10 km Bhairavnath Temple 5 AM to 12 PM and 4 PM to 9 PM 17 km Deva Mai Temple 8 AM to 10 PM 9 km
Vaishno Devi.Things to Know While Visiting Vaishno De: Here are some things that you should know prior to the Vaishno Devi trip; You will need an authentic yatra slip for the visit, which you can only obtain from the Tourist Reception Centre at the Katra Bus Stand. During the climb, walk on the paved track instead of the stairs in order to avoid over-straining yourself. Playing cards, chewing betel or smoking are prohibited on the route and at the Bhawan. Liquor, egg, fish and meat are banned in Katra Town. Video cameras are prohibited beyond Ban Ganga. Respect the sentiments of co-pilgrims. Dress modestly on the route to the yatra and when visiting the Vaishno Devi Temple. To explore the many hidden gems in the region, consider hiring a Vaishno Devi tour guide.
Vaishno Devi.Best Time to Visit Vaishno Devi: Before searching for the best Vaishno Devi tour packages, make sure to look into the ideal time for the visit first. Here’s what you need to know; Summer in Vaishno Devi Summer stretches from March to June and the region experiences clear weather with warm temperatures, ranging between 15 to 30°C. It is a good time for the holy pilgrimage to Vaishno Devi, however, do note that the weather can get quite hot during the daytime. It is advised to pack light and comfortable clothes for your tour to Vaishno Devi in summer. Browse Vaishno Devi travel packages for summer on Thomas Cook. Monsoon in Vaishno Devi The months from July to September cover the monsoon season in Vaishno Devi. The region experiences moderate to warm temperatures with heavy rainfall, which leads to slippery and muddy paths, making the trek more challenging and hazardous. If you have chosen this season for your tour to Vaishno Devi, make sure to proceed with caution. Get attractive deals on Vaishno Devi travel packages on our page. Autumn in Vaishno Devi Autumn in the region reigns from October to November, offering overall pleasant weather conditions. You can expect an average temperature range of 10-25°C, ideal for pilgrimage, Vaishno Devi sightseeing and other outdoor activities. The natural beauty of the region also takes on a majestic look during these months. Many consider it the best time to visit Vaishno Devi. If you are visiting during these months, make sure to book accommodation, train/flight tickets, Vaishno Devi packages, etc., in advance. Winter in Vaishno Devi Winter, which extends from December to February, presents the region with cold weather with an average temperature range of 0-15°C. The picturesque snow-covered landscape makes the journey to Vaishno Devi more beautiful. If you are visiting during these months, be mindful of the chilly weather and snow-covered slippery roads. Purchase Vaishno Devi travel packages for any season on Thomas Cook.
Vaishno Devi.Quotes and Inspirations: There are many reasons why millions of people, just like us, are drawn towards the holy shrine of Shri Mata Vaishno Devi. It is a renowned temple for Hindus and the region has many other notable religious landmarks that fall on its route. Many devotees have said that they get a calling from the holy site itself, termed as “Bulawa”. This is when the goddess calls to her children and upon receiving the call, they must visit the shrine. We also heard that whoever visits the shrine to pay their respects to the goddess, has their wishes fulfilled by her. All of these combined have led us to the doors of the goddess in the lap of the Trikuta Hills. Along the way, we visited other notable spiritual sites like the Dera Baba Banda, Ban Ganga, Baba Dhansar and so on. For a better tourism experience, you can hire a local Vaishno Devi travel guide. As our hearts slowly filled with the serene ambience, scenic landscapes and spiritual gain, we took the main path leading to Shri Mata Vaishno Devi Mandir, which took us about a few hours. However, once we were at the temple, all of the effort felt worth it. Seeing like-minded devotees drawn to the temple to catch a glimpse of the goddess, we felt just at home. The whole ambience was filled with a serene and divine aura that can not be properly translated into words. It is one of those experiences that you can feel by being there in person. After our darshan and offerings, we stayed and enjoyed the views of the region and then started our journey back. While returning, we remembered a quote from Sri Ramakrishna, where he said “The winds of God’s grace are always blowing, it is for us to raise our sails”. Maybe taking up the holy journey to Vaishno Devi was a major step towards our divine voyage. For Vaishno Devi spiritual tours with interesting itineraries, opt for the Vaishno Devi packages by Thomas Cook.
Vaishno Devi.What to Eat in Vaishno Devi?: To satisfy your hunger on your Vaishno Devi trip, make sure to try these food options; Rajma Chawal This classic North Indian food is one of the most popular vegetarian dishes available on the route to Vaishno Devi. This famous Vaishno Devi food both tastes good and fills your hunger. It consists of spicy, gravy-rich kidney beans served with steamed rice. Chole Bhature Chole Bhature is a vegetarian Vaishno Devi famous food item that locals and pilgrims like to eat when in Katra. It is a classic combination of deep-fried bread, called Bhature, and a side of spicy, gravy-rich chickpeas, called Chole. It is a simple yet mouthwatering dish that you can try on a budget. Most of the Vaishno Devi packages by Thomas Cook come with meals as a standard inclusion. Kashmiri Pulao No matter where you are in Jammu and Kashmir, you will always find eateries offering aromatic Kashmiri Pulao. It is a traditional Kashmiri sweet and savoury rice dish that is prepared with basmati rice, spices, saffron, and a mix of fruits and nuts. Paneer Butter Masala Paneer Butter Masala is a famous food in Vaishno Devi. It is often described as the king of vegetarian dishes in the region. Paneer Butter Masala is a rich and creamy dish made of Indian cottage cheese (paneer) and other ingredients like spices, tomatoes, butter, cashews, etc. Aloo Dam Aloo Dam is a beloved vegetarian Vaishno Devi food item that is consumed on the side of rice, roti, puri or bhature. It is a tangy and spicy potato curry dish made with flavourful masala gravy and yoghurt. You can find it at most vegetarian eateries in the region on your Vaishno Devi visit. Get the best deals on popular Vaishno Devi trip packages on Thomas Cook.
Vaishno Devi.Shopping in Vaishno Devi: Shopping is one of the best things to do in Vaishno Devi. Here are some markets you can consider checking out; Lavanya Shopping Complex One of the most popular places to visit in Katra for shopping is the Lavanya Shopping Complex. The complex is home to many shops and vendors, selling all kinds of traditional Kashmiri products, ranging from clothing to souvenirs. The place is most popular for buying pashmina shawls and stoles in the region. Look for Vaishno Devi trip packages that offer sufficient time to explore these markets. Katra Main Bazaar Katra Main Bazaar is the main shopping place that falls on the route to the holy Vaishno Devi yatra. In this market, you can find all kinds of products, including general items as well as regional works. Some examples of things you can buy at this market include clothes, shawls, food items, walking sticks, baskets, etc. The best thing to buy from this market is locally grown, high-quality dry fruits, like dried apples, almonds, walnuts, etc. Ardhkuwari Market Another well-known market that visitors can check out on their Vaishno Devi travel is the Ardhkuwari Market. The place is notable for its many shops selling all kinds of colourful traditional clothes. It is also a good place for buying winter apparel. Other things available for purchase at this market are accessories, like hairpins, bangles, brooches, etc., fruit baskets, souvenir items, etc.

Perth.Introduction: Perth is a charming little destination in Western Australia. The capital of Western Australia is 3300 km from Sydney. Known for its world class beaches, perfect weather and the pristine Swan River, Perth gives enough reasons to travellers to travel all the way from the East Coast and visit this lovely city.
Perth.Most Popular Perth tour packages: Tour Packages NameDurationPriceDiscover Perth3 Nights 4 DaysRs. ₹53 700Paradise In Perth5 Nights 6 DaysRs. ₹84 100Perth With Margaret River5 Nights 6 DaysRs. ₹98 700 For a traveller, Perth tour package presents with a lot of options to explore. Perth’s inner-city park is the largest in the world. Perth’s prodigious park is spread in an area of 400 hectares. Filled with untamed bushland and cultivated gardens, the park is the home to about 70 bird species along with more than 300 plant species. The park is filled with trees that are more than 75-year-old. This park is an essential part of every Perth Package and a must visit for families and children Perth is also the home to the cute Quokka. This catlike animal is indigenous to Perth and is friendly to tourists. One can also take a cruise on Swan River or visit the Rottnest Island on a day trip. Perth provides a splendid travel experience and Best things to do in Perth Perth tour package offers an array of activities and day trip. The stunning Swan River provides cruising options from where travellers get to enjoy the lovely views of Perth’s skyline. The cruises connect the inner city of Perth and historic city of Fremantle. One can enjoy the sights of modern glass Bell Tower, the Kings Park and the famous Fremantle harbour during the cruise. Those looking for a day trip can also visit The Rottnest Island. Located 18 kms from the coast of Perth, the island is the home to more than 50 gorgeous beaches and bays. Rottnest is a fine addition in Perth package. The locals call this place Rotto and it is best explored on bicycle. Visitors can enjoy activities like tennis, golf, fishing, swimming, snorkeling, kayaking, and diving on their trip to this island. Perth tourism boasts of some of the most scenic beaches including Scarborough, Cottesloe, City Beach, Trigg, Sorento, and Leighton. table_of_content: Facts you need to know about Singapore
Best Places to Visit in Singapore

faq.question: Which is the best time to travel to Singapore?
faq.answer: The best time to travel to Singapore is between December and February when the entire city is lit up for Christmas, New Year and Chinese New Year.

faq.question: Is visa on arrival available for Indian nationals in Singapore?
faq.answer: Indian nationals do not have the option for visa on arrival in Singapore. They need to obtain a Singapore entry visa before they arrive.

faq.question: Which is the most widely spoken language in Singapore?
faq.answer: Singapore has many languages and recognizes Mandarin, Malay, English, and Tamil as the major languages due to its multi-ethnic and multi-cultural society. However, Malay is the most widely spoken language in Singapore, as the Malaysian community is considered indigenous. So when on Singapore trip, tourists have no communication problems because English is widely spoken and understood.

faq.question: How many days are required for a vacation to Singapore?
faq.answer: You will need at least 3 days to have a satisfactory vacation in Singapore as places like Sentosa and Jurong Bird Park are liable to take an entire day each. A day to walk around and see the sights and do some shopping should be factored in as well.

faq.question: Is Singapore an expensive holiday destination?
faq.answer: Yes, it is one of the priciest holiday destinations but if you book ahead and choose accommodation wisely and opt for public transport often, you will find it to be quite reasonable.

faq.question: Is my Indian driver's license valid in Singapore?
faq.answer: Yes, it’s valid in Singapore but you also need to have an International Driving Permit that has been issued by an authorised body in India.

faq.question: Are there Indian restaurants in Singapore?
faq.answer: Yes, since Indian cuisine is very popular in Singapore and there’s a vibrant Indian community living here, there are numerous Indian restaurants in Singapore. From the high-end Colony in Ritz Carlton to RAS in Clarke Quay, or the Tiffin Room in the iconic Raffles Hotel, Indian restaurants form an important part of Singapore’s international cuisine.

faq.question: How far is Changi Airport from the city?
faq.answer: Changi Airport is approximately 24km from the centre of the city.

faq.question: How far is Sentosa Island from the airport?
faq.answer: Sentosa Island is around 26.5km from Changi Airport. Taxi is the fastest way to reach Sentosa from Changi.

faq.question: Which is the most widely spoken language in Singapore?
faq.answer: Singapore has many languages and recognizes Mandarin, Malay, English, and Tamil as the major languages due to its multi-ethnic and multi-cultural society. However, Malay is the most widely spoken language in Singapore, as the Malaysian community is considered indigenous. So when on Singapore trip, tourists have no communication problems because English is widely spoken and understood.

faq.question: When is the Great Singapore Sale held?
faq.answer: The Great Singapore Sale is held every year in the month of June and July.

faq.question: Which is the best time to visit Singapore?
faq.answer: The best time to visit Singapore is between December and February when the entire city is lit up for Christmas, New Year and Chinese New Year.
