Singapore.About Singapore: Singapore has been always been a popular vacation destination for people since decades. One of the reasons why it is so famed is because of its ability to keep reinventing itself every few years. Whether it’s in terms of iconic landmarks or beautiful sights and fantastic things to do, Singapore has become a melting pot of culture and is well-known among people for its delicious street food and high-end shopping malls. Singapore is the upper crust when it comes to holidaying in Asia as it offers a cosmopolitan experience unmatched by any other Asian destination. Singapore has everything going for it – a superb transport system, glitzy nightlife, awe-inspiring architecture, and entertainment options that begin right at the Changi Airport which has consistently been chosen as one of the top airports in the world and for good reason too. Singapore tourists are sure to find themselves spoiled for choice as they come across the many different facets of this city and all that there is to explore.
Singapore.Facts you need to know about Singapore: Singapore is the only island city-state in the world & has a population of approximately 5.5 million people It is the 4th largest financial center in the world; the 2nd busiest port in the world; one of the biggest tourist attractions in the world; one of the highest per capita incomes and standard of living in the world. The tallest indoor waterfall in the world is located in Singapore at the Gardens by the Bay, and it stands at a height of 35 meters Singapore was home to the world’s first night zoo Singapore pioneered the first F1 night race Military service is mandatory for all men in Singapore for two years after finishing high school
Singapore.Places to Visit in Singapore: Singapore is one of the most electric cities that you can find yourself in, a city that is vibrant and alive with culture, technology and the most amazing food you can find. As a city that constantly keeps changing to keep up with the times, it’s also a city that never stays suspended in time. It’s always evolving and yet, there are some places to visit in Singapore that are perennial favourites. Here are some of them. Sentosa Island Universal Studios Botanic Gardens Gardens by the Bay Chinatown Jurong Bird Park Sentosa Island For the best possible entertainment and amusement, Sentosa Island is one of the places to see in Singapore. An island very close to the main city, Sentosa offers a full day’s worth of excitement and enjoyment with its many attractions such as a butterfly garden, a thrilling underwater world, and the humongous Merlion statue whose summit can be reached by elevators inside the actual statue. Sentosa is one of the places you must see when in Singapore as your trip would be incomplete otherwise. Universal Studios A trip to Singapore simply must include a visit to Universal Studios in Resorts World Sentosa, which has thrilling and exciting rides as well as some spectacular shows. A 49-acre park with seven fun zones, Universal Studios offers the kind of enjoyment that makes adults wish they were back in their childhood. A perfect family outing that includes some hair-raising rides featuring some of the most famous pop culture icons such as Transformers, Jurassic Park, and Sesame Street. Some of the shows here are also extremely thrilling. Botanic Gardens Even if you’re not much of a nature enthusiast, you’ll be stunned by the gorgeous greenery of the Singapore Botanic Gardens that is spread out over 82 hectares and has more than 10,000 species of plants and trees. The National Orchid Garden and the Jacob Ballas Children’s Garden are wonderful, but the real treat is the interactive rainforest exhibit which is an unforgettable experience with its museums, statues, fountains and a beautiful lake to top it all. Gardens by the Bay Right in the middle of Singapore, you’ll come across these stunning structures that look like something out a futuristic movie. These Supertree structures which are a part of the Gardens by the Bay are simply fantastical and they are connected by a skyway that allows people to go on a skywalk that looks out over the gardens. The greenhouse is also shaped like a seashell and it is called the Flower Dome and is currently the largest glass greenhouse in the world. The Super trees, the gardens and the conservatories are spectacular. Chinatown If you are looking for a bustling look at contemporary Chinese culture in Singapore, then look no further than Chinatown with its heritage red lanterns, authentic and delicious Chinese food, traditional shops and temples and much more. It’s easy to lose yourself in the middle of this cultivated chaos filled with ceremonies and cultural relics that it’s quite easy to forget that you’re in modern Singapore. Food is also one of the big draws here, especially delicious street food that’s cheap and has a wide variety. Jurong Bird Park One of the most famous landmarks of Singapore is the Jurong Bird Park which is known to have around 5000 birds from 400 different species. There are several interactive shows and exhibits that will keep you enthralled. But the most spectacular sight here is the Waterfall Aviary which is a delight to the senses. The 30m high waterfall is the world’s largest walk-in aviary. Other things to see here include the Penguin Coast, the Pelican Cove and the Flamingo Lake. Marina Bay Sands & SkyPark One of the most recognized landmarks in Singapore, Marina Bay Sands is a dream destination. SkyPark has views of the skyline that are awe-inspiring, particularly during sunset. If you happen to be on a Singapore package, you simply cannot miss the light show along the waterfront promenade. The infinity pool atop (for hotel guests) is another attraction. Jewel Changi Airport Even if you’re just passing through, Jewel Changi Airport is an attraction in itself! This world-class airport is home to the Rain Vortex, the world’s tallest indoor waterfall, surrounded by lush greenery. With unique shops, a canopy park, and stunning light shows, it's the perfect place to relax before or after your Singapore tour packages. Singapore Flyer Want to see Singapore from a completely different angle? The Singapore Flyer, the world's tallest observation wheel, provides panoramic views of the city and Marina Bay. The 30-minute air-conditioned capsule ride is ideal for taking stunning photos, particularly at sunset. Most Singapore packages include this as a highlight, so it's an excellent addition to your tour. Merlion Park A visit to Singapore would not be complete without taking a peek at the iconic Merlion statue. Situated in Marina Bay, it is the ideal place for pictures with the cityscape as the background. Whether you're travelling solo with a Singapore package or with the family, it's a must-see for that quintessential Singaporean experience. Singapore Zoo & Night Safari For nature enthusiasts, the Singapore Zoo is a heaven. Unlike other zoos, the animals here have open enclosures that mimic their natural environment. For a one-of-a-kind experience, the Night Safari offers an evening Singapore trip to see nocturnal animals. This exhilarating attraction is included in most Singapore packages.
Singapore.Languages Spoken in Singapore: Singapore has four major languages – English, Malay, Tamil and Mandarin, apart from this, the locals also speak “Singlish” which is a very peculiar language spoken by mixing English with various local dialects and not bound by rules of grammar To-long! – Help! Apa kabar – Hello Se la mat jalan – Good bye Terima kasih – Thank you Ya – Yes Teedak – No Ta-hoo-kah ber -da ha sa Ingris? – Do you speak English? Sa-ya tee dak fa-ham – I don’t understand Sama sama – Same
Singapore.Best Places to Visit in Singapore: If you want to venture beyond the common tourist spots on your Singapore package, here are five offbeat destinations that will make your Singapore package special. Whether you are an adventure junkie or a nature enthusiast, these offbeat places will provide you with an unconventional glimpse into Singapore's charm. Lazarus Island: Looking for a serene retreat from the city? Lazarus Island is a secret haven of white sandy shores and clear blue waters. Unlike the populated Sentosa beaches, this beach is ideal for a quiet retreat. Take a picnic, have a swim, or simply bask under the sun. Island-hopping has become part of many Singapore tour packages, so do not miss this treasure! Kranji Countryside: For a contrasting Singapore package, visit Kranji Countryside. This countryside getaway is all about lush farms, fresh fruits and vegetables, and escaping the city. Ideal for a relaxed Singapore vacation, you can go to goat farms, organic gardens, and even farm-to-table dining. Haw Par Villa: Quirky and underrated, Haw Par Villa is an open-air museum. With its repertoire of colorful statues, vibrant dioramas, and Chinese folklore, it offers an interesting glimpse into mythology and history. It is worth a visit during your cultural Singapore package. The Projector: If indie movies and offbeat experiences are your thing, The Projector is Singapore's hippest cinema. Based in a retro theatre, it plays independent movies, documentaries, and cult classics—ideal for a snug night out during your Singapore packages. The Southern Islands: A boat trip away are the Southern Islands—such as St. John's and Sisters' Islands—ideal for an unconventional excursion. The unspoiled islands offer wonderful hiking trails and snorkelling. It's easy to experience island hopping with many Singapore packages that offer island tours.
Singapore.Best Time to Visit Singapore: Although Singapore is a year-round destination, the months from November to January, which are the winter months can be the best time to visit Singapore. The weather is not as hot and humid as it is throughout the rest of the year. Christmas and New Year are celebrated with great gusto here and it’s a complete treat for the senses if you happen to visit during this time. The downside is that this is also peak time and the crowds can be quite a deterrent especially if you were looking for a quiet vacation. Also, since the Chinese New Year falls during this time, it’s very likely that you will find ticket and accommodation prices soaring if you haven’t booked ahead of time. Another great time to visit Singapore is during the month of July. This is especially if you love shopping or food because Singapore has the Great Singapore Sale and Singapore Food Festival during this time. April is also a good time to visit if you’re looking to save money on flights and bookings while the offseason in Singapore is between August and October. Since Singapore hosts the Singapore Grand Prix in mid-September, you can however expect an influx of tourists. But apart from that, hotel rates during this time are quite manageable. Average Climate in Singapore Singapore’s climate is characterised by two monsoon seasons separated by inter-monsoonal periods (see table below). The Northeast Monsoon occurs from December to early March, and the Southwest Monsoon from June to September. The average temperature is between 25 degrees Celsius and 31 degrees Celsius. April is the warmest month, January is the coolest month and November is the wettest month.
Singapore.How to Reach Singapore: The best way to reach Singapore is by flight. Singapore is well-connected to India by several direct flights from various cities. There are numerous major airlines that connect India with Singapore and some of these include Air India, Singapore Airlines, Thai Airlines and Tiger Airways to name a few. Some of the cities from where you can fly directly to Singapore include New Delhi, Chennai, Mumbai, and Bangalore. Singapore’s Changi Airport is stunning and offers several facilities like a swimming pool, gym and gorgeous gardens, and even movie theatres, offering passengers an unforgettable experience. If you’re traveling from Malaysia, then you can reach Singapore by road too. There’s a causeway between Singapore and Johor Bahru and you can rent a car and drive between both countries yourself. You can also opt to hire a taxi or simply take a bus if you wish to travel from Malaysia to Singapore. You can also travel to Singapore by train, and there are two lines. One connects Singapore with Malaysia and the other connects it with Thailand. Local transport in Singapore is efficient and organized. You can opt for the MRT for which you can take the EZ-Link Card or a Singapore Tourist Pass if you’re staying for more than a week. The buses are also well-connected and alternatively, you can try the trishaws, but you need to have decent bargaining skills, or you’ll end up spending a lot of money.
Singapore.Food in Singapore: Would you believe it that some people make a trip to Singapore, just to enjoy the food? Yes, it’s true. Singapore is rightly considered a feast for the taste buds and a huge number of choices – Malay, Chinese, Japanese, Indian and even continental. There’s something for everyone here. While the range of food in Singapore is quite mind-boggling, here are a few dishes that you must try while you are here. BakChorMee Dim Sum Satay BakChorMee One of the most popular dishes that you should certainly try when in Singapore is BakChorMee which translates to minced meat and noodles. However, the dish itself is not as mundane as the translation, because it’s a glorious combination of minced meat layered with broth and flat egg noodles, spiced with chilies and a tangy aftertaste. Dim Sum It’s quite possible that you will have the best dim sums of your life in Singapore. While available all over China and other Asian countries, the dim sum here melts in the mouth and is stuffed with curried chicken, vegetables, pork, beef or seafood. Dim sum in Singapore can often veer into the main course category as people just can’t stop eating them! Satay This is street food at its best. Strips of meat, be it beef, chicken or pork are skewered and basted with sauce to create a chewy and delicious delight which is often served with peanut sauce and onion rings. This tasty treat is found in most street food outlets and is often sold out very fast. Drinking Water Singapore tap water is safe & potable to drink. The water in Singapore undergoes a multi-step purification process, which includes coagulation, sedimentation, filtration, and disinfection. The water is then stored in covered reservoirs and distributed through a network of pipes to homes and businesses throughout the country.
Singapore.Popular Local Cuisines in Singapore: Singapore's multi-ethnic culture and heritage presents a multitude of colourful cuisines, each with its own unique flavor. From Michelin-starred delicacies and international cuisines to scrumptious creations by homegrown chefs and timeless hawker favorite’s, this is where you can taste it all. Savour new flavours, new atmospheres, and discover the obsession that goes into crafting every dish. Chwee kueh: It may not be the prettiest belle at the ball, but we think chwee kueh is in a league of its own. It’s definitely one of our top picks for breakfast foods around the island. Nothing beats the simplicity of chewy rice cakes. And when you slather them in salty chye poh and tangy chilli, you’ve got the perfect bite. Wanton mee: A perfect bowl of wanton noodles should be a medley of wildly contrasting textures. Firm, springy noodles drenched in sauce, soft dumplings stuffed with flavourful meat, and thick char siew (barbecued pork) slices with a good bit of charring. Some of our top picks include Ji Ji Wanton Noodle Specialist and Fei Fei Roasted Noodle. Char kway teow: This fried rice noodle dish may not score major points for presentation, but we guarantee you it’s darned tasty. What you’ll get is a plate of flat rice noodles, stir-fried in sweet dark soy sauce with fishcake, egg, bean sprouts, prawns and cockles. It’s no secret that this doesn’t register high on the health-o-meter, but we just can’t resist digging into a plate at the much-lauded Armenian Street Char Kway Teow.
Singapore.Best Indian Restaurants in Singapore: Mustard. 32 Race Course Road, Singapore 218552. +65 6297 8422.Mon-Fri, Sun 11.30am-3pm, 6-10.45pm; Sat 11.30am-4pm, 6-10.45pm. Jaggi’s Northern Indian Cuisine. 37/39 Chander Road, Singapore 219541. +65 6296 6141.Mon-Fri 11am-3pm, 5.30-10.30pm; Sat & Sun 11am-10.30pm. Banana Leaf Apolo. 54 Race Course Road, Singapore 218564. +65 6293 8682.Daily 10.30am-10.30pm. Komala Vilas. 76-78 Serangoon Road, Singapore 217981. +65 6293 6980. Daily 7am-10.30pm. Khansama Tandoori Restaurant. 166 Serangoon Road, Singapore 218050. +65 6299 0300.Daily 11am-11.30pm. Gokul Vegetarian Restaurant. 19 Upper Dickson Road, Singapore 207478. +65 6396 7769.Daily 10.30am-10pm.
Singapore.Things to Do in Singapore: An eclectic city by day or night, Singapore wins the hearts of people of all ages. From its gorgeously lit up skyline to its wide range of activities, there are innumerable fun things to do in Singapore and there’s not a single moment where you could feel bored. Here are some of the top things to do in Singapore. Night Safari: Take a thrilling Night Safari through the world's first nocturnal wildlife park. Ride on a tram and explore various habitats, catching glimpses of lions, tigers, and elephants in their naturalistic habitats. For a special experience, reserve a walking trail for an up-close experience with the nocturnal animals. This is typically part of many Singapore tour packages. Skydive Indoors at iFly Singapore: Always dreamed of skydiving but not quite ready for the real deal? At iFly Singapore, you can feel the rush of free-falling without ever leaving the ground, in a huge indoor wind tunnel. It's adventure combined with safety at its finest, making it an absolute must-do for thrill-lovers on a Singapore tour packages. Kayak Through the Mangroves at Pulau Ubin: For a quirky experience, kayak through the peaceful mangroves of Pulau Ubin. This island is ideal for nature-lovers away from the cityscape. Cycling around the island and trekking through the Chek Jawa wetlands are also wonderful ideas to give your Singapore trip an adrenaline rush. AJ Hackett Sentosa for Bungee Jumping & Giant Swing: Adrenaline seekers, rejoice! Jump off a 47m-tall bungee tower or experience the Giant Swing, which propels you flying through space at breathtaking velocity. This is a hands-down top contender of the most adventurous activities you can include in your Singapore packages. Experience Indoor Surfing at Wave House Sentosa: Miss the beach culture? Wave House Sentosa allows you to surf artificial waves, so it's a cool and fun way to surf without going to the beach. Either you're a pro or just starting out, it's a good way to boost your Singapore vacation. Trek to Henderson Waves: Skip the typical tourist attractions and hike up to Henderson Waves, Singapore's tallest pedestrian bridge. The panoramic views of the city from this 274m-long bridge are justifiable, particularly during sunrise or sundown. If you are nature-loving, include this in your Singapore tour packages. Scuba Dive at Sisters' Islands Marine Park: Yes, it is possible to scuba dive in Singapore! The Sisters' Islands Marine Park boasts colorful coral reefs and sea life. Alternatively, you could try snorkeling or go on a guided marine biodiversity tour if diving is not your preference.
Singapore.Entertainment & Nightlife in Singapore: Buzzed and ready to rumble? As a city that never sleeps, Singapore has some of the best late-night spots and nightclubs for party animals to revel in until the early morning Zouk Club: Where celebs, Asia’s A-listers, and the life of the party hangout, is the booziest, wildest nightclub on the island! In your best party clothes, get ready to drop to electrifying DJ beats on the large dancefloor! And if you’ve had your fill of fun at Zouk, time to head to its sister club, Phuture, a more laidback venue that’s famous for its jazzy and R&B vibes. CE LA VI Restaurant & Skybar: Time to take the party to the skies and level up the luxury with a fabulous dinner and cocktail at . This opulent fine dining restaurant spoils you with fancy plates of Key Lime Pearls, Rice-Flake-Crusted Lobster, and other savoury treats, as well as 360-degree views of the Singapore skyline at night from the Skybar Marquee Singapore: Known as the megaclub of Singapore, has hosted well-known international EDM DJs such as Tiesto, Zedd and Steve Aoki. Famous for ‘The Big Q’ Ferris Wheel, Marquee boasts an immersive clubbing experience with its design and state-of-the-art sound system. And if you need an extra thrill on top of the booze and thumping tunes, then the three-storey spiralling slides will be open to delivering you right to the dancefloor! Cuba Libre Café & Bar: Is a favourite amongst Latin music fans. Dance the night away with music by resident live bands and DJs like Adrenalina and DJ Boi, and of course, don't forget to try signature cocktails like the Caipirinha and the Cuba Libre.
Singapore.Shopping in Singapore: If you are a shopaholic who is traveling to Singapore, rush to these places to have the best shopping experience in Singapore. This Singapore shopping guide will come in handy for you. Get all of your answers to where to shop in Singapore right here! Orchard Road – Vibrant Shopping Marina Bay – Shopping Spree Exemplified Chinatown – Steal Prices Little India – Sari, Spice, And Everything Nice VivoCity – Gallery For Lifestyle Haji Lane – Boutiques Street Clarke Quay – Shopping For The Fashionistas IMM – Home And Clothing Bugis Street – Flea Market Holland Village – One Stop Shop City Plaza – Fine Shopping Experience Lucky Plaza – Shopping Experience Redefined Scape Underground – Centre For Youth Anchorpoint Shopping Centre – Affordable Shopping Tanglin Mall Bazaar – Vintage Accessories And Retro Clothing Mustafa Centre – Lowest Prices Ever Far East Plaza – All The Fashion Trends Pasarbella – Best Farmer’s Market The Editor’s Market – Brands On A Budget
Singapore.Local Cabs App in Singapore: Here are some of the best taxi apps in Singapore: Grab: Grab is one of the most widely used ride-hailing apps in Southeast Asia, including Singapore. It offers various ride options, including taxis, private cars, and more. ComfortDelGro Taxi Booking App: This app is developed by comfortDelGro, one of the largest taxi operators in Singapore. It allows users to book ComfortDelGro taxis directly. Ryde: Ryde offers both carpooling and taxi booking options. It's known for its carpooling features, which allow users to share rides with others heading in the same direction. TADA: TADA is a ride-hailing app that operates in Singapore and other countries. It offers various vehicle options, including taxis. Gojek: While known for its wide range of services beyond just taxis, Gojek also provides taxi booking options in Singapore. Hailo: Hailo is a taxi app that provides an easy way to book and pay for taxi rides in Singapore. moobiTaxi: This app connects users with available taxis and allows for convenient booking and payment. SMRT Book a Taxi: Developed by SMRT, another major taxi operator in Singapore, this app enables users to book SMRT taxis. Electric Plug Details For Singapore there are three associated plug types C, G and M. Plug type C is the plug which has two round pins, plug type G has three rectangular pins in a triangular pattern and type M is the plug that has three round pins. Singapore operates on a 230V supply voltage and 50Hz.

table_of_content: Best hill stations/ best beaches in Mexico
Mexico.Introduction: Mexico is a beautiful country in the southern portion of North America, bordered by the United States on the north and Guatemala and Belize on the southeast. It is a vast country filled with tons of cultural, historical and geographical diversity that one can witness during their stay. From dense green forests and rugged desert terrain to ruins of ancient civilisations and traces of modern history, there is a plethora of renowned Mexico tourist attractions for you to witness and explore. Hence, if you are interested in gaining more in-depth knowledge about foreign culture and history, Mexico can be an ideal option. Whether it's a short adventure trip with friends or a long vacation with family, you can find the right Mexico tourism packages that fit your travel needs on our website. Thomas Cook offers multiple Mexico tour packages within most budget ranges to assist tourists in getting the best travel experience. From an 8 Nights/ 9 Days trip to all the way up to a 19 Nights/ 20 Days trip, these professionally organised Mexico tour packages offer the best value for your buck. In addition, these Mexico tour packages take care of most of the crucial aspects of setting up a foreign vacation, such as Mexico sightseeing, hotels, meals, etc., so you don’t have to deal with them yourself. As of now, the lowest available Mexico tour package price from India is INR 2,47,000.
Mexico.Table of Contents: Popular Mexico Tour Packages About Mexico Holidays Best time to visit Mexico How to reach Mexico Best Places to Visit in Mexico Best Hill Stations/Beaches in Mexico Best honeymoon destinations in Mexico Things to do in Mexico What to Eat in Mexico What to Shop in Mexico Interesting Facts about Mexico Mexico Culture Mexico Visa for Indians Foreign currency for Mexico
Mexico.Most Popular Mexico Tour Packages 2023: Mexico Tour Packages No. of Nigts / Days Price* Grand Bargain America with Add on 3 Nights Cancun 14 Nights/ 15 Days Rs. 3 50 218 Mexican Fiesta 8 Nights/ 9 Days Rs. 2 48 100 Grand Bargain Tour of America with Mexico 19 Nights/ 20 Days Rs. 5 06 905
Mexico.About Mexico holiday: While looking for Mexico package holidays, research and learn about the destination first. Mexico, officially called the United Mexican States, is a country in North America covering a total area of 1,972,550 square kilometres. This page aims to offer interested travellers key details about the destination so they can choose from the most suitable Mexico tourism packages from India. For instance, there are tons of places to visit in Mexico for history buffs, such as the jaw-dropping ruins of the ancient civilisation of the Aztecs and Maya. Its vibrant and diverse culture also plays a massive role in promoting regional tourism, with things like Fiestas, Carnivals, Mariachi Bands, Jarabe Tapito, etc. The country is also a key adventure hub, offering opportunities for kayaking, ziplining, whitewater rafting, hiking, etc., which you may or may not find included in popular Mexico tourism packages. The actual Mexico trip cost, however, depends on a lot of factors, including trip duration, time of visit, total travellers, etc. If you wish to experience all these in style, check out the Mexico tour packages available on our page. You can find Mexico package holidays from major Indian cities, including Delhi, Mumbai, Kolkata, Chennai, Hyderabad, Ahmedabad, Bangalore, etc.
Mexico.Best time to visit Mexico: While you can find Mexico tour packages for every season, select a timeline that fits your trip expectations. These are the season-wise Mexico tour experiences; Mexico in winter The winter season in Mexico stretches from December to February. The weather condition stays quite pleasant with an average temperature of around 20°C. You can expect to witness snowfall in the highlands of northern Mexico. Due to all these, winter is considered the best time to visit Mexico. The best month to visit Mexico to witness snow in some northern regions is December. You will have no problem finding Mexico package holidays for the winter season. Mexico in spring Spring in Mexico starts around March and extends till May. The average temperature range stays around the mid-20s and only slightly increases towards May. You can expect rainfall from April, which highlights the region’s natural beauty. Spring is actually one of the shoulder seasons for Mexico tour but you can still find Mexico package holidays for this season. Besides winter, it is the best season to visit Mexico for holidays. Mexico in summer Summer is the off-season for tourism in Mexico. It stretches from June to August and features extreme temperatures around 30°C to 40°C. It is also known as the hurricane season, which is why tourists generally avoid making Mexico travel plans around these months. You can easily find Mexico tour packages for summer on our page. Summer is generally termed as the best season for Mexico tourism on a budget. Mexico in autumn Autumn, from September to November, is the other shoulder season for the Mexico tour. The temperature hovers around the 30°C mark in the southern parts and the 20°C mark around the northern regions. You can find Mexico packages for the autumn season without much worry.
Mexico.How to reach Mexico: Indian travellers on their Mexico tour can use the following modes of transport to reach the country; By air The easiest and most popular way to travel to Mexico from India is via flight. Mexico has multiple international airports that help it to stay connected to major parts of the world. While there are no direct flights operating from India to Mexico, individuals can easily find connecting flights from major Indian cities, including Delhi, Mumbai, Bangalore, etc. Some popular Mexico package holidays may include optional flights. As winter is the best time to go to Mexico, consider booking flights in advance to avoid unavailability. By Road/Rail There is no direct road or rail system that connects India to Mexico. One can alternatively travel to the USA from India and then continue their journey to Mexico by road. It is not recommended because it requires a lot of paperwork and time, so look for Mexico packages that include flights. By water There are no direct cruise ships that sail from India to Mexico. However, tourists can first reach the USA and then board a cruise from one of its ports to reach Mexico. Some Mexico tour packages may or may not include transportation.
Mexico.Places to visit in Mexico: Here are some of the best places to visit in Mexico: Mexico City Mexico City is the country’s capital and a standard inclusion in most Mexico tour packages. It is one of the best places in Mexico where you can experience and witness the authentic regional cuisine, culture and traditions alongside major historical sites and architectural masterpieces. Some of its highlights include the Historic Centre of Mexico City, Teotihuacan, Xochimilco, Coyacan, Chapultepec, etc., which are often included under sightseeing in most Mexico package holidays. Copper Canyon The Copper Canyon is among the top Mexico tourist attractions. Comprised of a series of six separate canyons, it is a site of immense natural beauty and historical importance. This UNESCO World Heritage Site is sometimes also called the “Grand Canyon of Mexico” and is seen in the majority of Mexico packages. Chichen Itza The archaeological site of Chichen Itza is one of the best places in Mexico for history buffs. This UNESCO World Heritage Site is one of the finest restored evidences of the Mayan Civilisation. Its main attractions include the giant El Castillo and the almost 1000-year-old Caracol. Make sure to add this site to your customisable Mexico package holidays. Oaxaca Oaxaca is also among the most well-known tourist places to see in Mexico. Individuals interested in experiencing the real cultural side of the country will find this city interesting. Some of its main attractions are the Oaxaca Cathedral, Monte Alban, the Cultural Centre, etc. If you can’t find Oaxaca in Mexico packages, add it by contacting your trip operator.
Mexico.Best hill stations/ best beaches in Mexi: Following are some renowned beaches and hillside destinations in Mexico that you can check out; Hill stations Ajusco Ajusco is one of the best tourist places in Mexico for a hillside vacation. It is basically a 3,930-metre-high lava dome, making it the highest point in the city. Tourists love to have a short stay in this area and check out its surrounding beauty during their Mexico travel. It is often included in some Mexico tour packages. Cuetzalan This small mountain town, 177 km from Puebla, is one of the most beautiful places to visit in Mexico. The rustic town buildings surrounded by densely covered green hills offer some of the best picturesque views. Consider adding Cuetzalan to your Mexico package holidays. Pico de Orizaba Pico de Orizaba is among the most famous Mexico tourist places that you can check out. This 5636-metre-tall, inactive stratovolcano is the highest peak in the country. Found in major Mexico packages, tourists love to visit Pico de Orizaba for its breathtaking scenic beauty and hiking adventures. Beaches Playa Cerritos Playa Cerritos in Baja California Sur, Mexico, has been a major spot for surfing adventures. It is among the few Pacific beaches in Baja where you can swim safely. It is included in many Mexico package holidays and remains filled with tons of tourists around the year. Playa Carrizalillo The Playa Carrizalillo is a secluded beach in Mexico’s Puerto Escondido. One has to descend 160 steps to reach this beach, which is a small but beautiful sandy stretch surrounded by swaying palm trees on one side and clear blue seawater on the other. You can find this beach in most Mexico package holidays. Playa del Amor Playa del Amor, also known as the Lover’s Beach, is a very popular beach in Mexico. Located in Cabo San Lucas, the golden sand, turquoise sea water, and the backdrop of giant arid rock formations make it one of the most eye-catching tourist attractions in Mexico. You can find Playa del Amor in popular Mexico tour packages.
Mexico.Best honeymoon destinations in Mexico: Check out the following places to find a romantic destination for your honeymoon in Mexico; Playa del Carmen This resort city in Quintana Roo is a renowned destination for a honeymoon in Mexico. Playa del Carmen is known for its major tourist attractions, including vibrant nature reserves and beautiful white-sand beaches. Also, the availability of luxury accommodations for married couples and an exuberant nightlife scene makes it a prominent choice for honeymooners. You won’t have a problem finding a Mexico honeymoon package that includes this location. Cancun Besides being known for Mexico family tours, Cancun is a wildly popular honeymoon destination. This city is a complete vacation spot featuring all the honeymoon essentials, from luxury resorts and restaurants to beautiful beaches and vibrant nightlife. You can customise a Mexico honeymoon package that includes Cancun. Puebla Newly married couples can easily add Puebla to their honeymoon Mexico travel plan. It is also one of the best Mexico places to visit to try out delicious regional dishes, especially desserts, during a Mexico trip.
Mexico.Things to do in Mexico: If you are looking for some fun things to do during your Mexico trip, here are some options to consider; Horseback ride to Chamula During your Mexico tour, take a horseback ride to San Juan Chamula and enjoy the journey while soaking in the surrounding natural beauty and daily rural life in the region. Ride a hot air balloon over the Ruins of Teotihuacan Exploring the remarkable ancient ruins of Teotihuacan in Mexico on a hot air balloon is one of the best things to do in Mexico. Enjoy the breathtaking, top-view perspective of this archaeological site to spice up your Mexico tour experience. Ride a train through Copper Canyon Exploring the immense natural beauty of the renowned Copper Canyon by travelling on the El Chepe, otherwise called the Copper Canyon Train, is one of the best things to do in Mexico. Make sure to add it to your Mexico travel plan. Learn surfing in Sayulita If you keep an interest in surfing or learning the art of surfing, there’s no better place to try it than Sayulita Village during your Mexico tour. Note that you may or may not find surfing as a standard inclusion in most India to Mexico tour package options.
Mexico.What to eat in Mexico: Alongside looking for Mexico tours all-inclusive plans, don’t forget to research the authentic regional dishes. Some well-known options are; Menudo Menudo, also called Pancita, is a popular dish in Mexico. It is a seasoned soup prepared with ingredients like beef tripe and corn kernels over a chilli pepper base. Don’t forget to add this to your Mexico travel plan. Quesadilla Quesadillas are either flour or corn tortillas that are folded in half and have a filling made of cheese and a few other ingredients. It is then grilled or deep-fried and consumed while hot. It is not usually included under meals in most Mexico packages tour plans. Flan Mexican Flan is a flavourful regional sweet dish you can try during your visit. It is a baked custard made with whole eggs and evaporated and condensed milk, finally topped with light sugar syrup. Tacos Do not forget to try the authentic tacos during your Mexico trip. It is a folded tortilla with a filling in the middle made of offal, steak, chorizo, onions, etc. Mexico City is one of the best places to visit in Mexico for delicious Tacos. Burritos Burritos are another signature Mexican food that you can find everywhere. It is a rolled flour-made tortilla with a stuffing made of meat, cheese, refried beans, chopped vegetables, etc.
Mexico.What to shop in Mexico: Here are some things you should look for when shopping in Mexico; Mayan Wooden Mask These finely carved wooden masks depict various natural symbols, ancient deities and jungle animals tied closely to the Mayan culture. These beautiful art pieces are perfect home decor items that you buy in Mexico. Where to buy: These masks are available all-around major cities included in Mexico trip packages, especially the open markets. Lucha Libre Mask If you are a wrestling fan, you will love the Lucha Libre Masks sold all around Mexico. These colourful masks help symbolise the native form of freestyle wrestling in Mexico and are very affordable. Where to buy: The street market in San Miguel de Allende, Guanajuato, is a great place to buy Lucha libre masks. So, consider looking for Mexico tourist packages that include Guanajuato. Mexican Chocolate Authentic Mexican chocolate is a delicious and compact gift idea that you can consider. It is perfect for cooking and making hot chocolates and can also help satisfy your sweet tooth as a quick snack. Where to buy: Some of the best places to buy Mexican chocolate are from the local shops in Oaxaca, Chiapas and Tabasco. It is best to go with Mexico tourist packages that include a short stay at these locations. Mexican Coffee Beans Mexican coffee beans are another affordable regional product you can buy as a gift or for self-consumption during your trip. Where to buy: Consider buying Mexican coffee beans from the local markets in the country’s southern states, which you can find in some Mexico trip packages.
Mexico.Interesting facts about Mexico: While searching for Mexico travel all-inclusive packages, check out some lesser-known facts about the country that every tourist should know about; Mexico has a total of 35 UNESCO World Heritage Sites within its territory, including the likes of Chichen Itza, Teotihuacan, Uxmal, Queretaro, Guanajuato, etc. The world’s smallest volcano is found in Mexico, called Cuexcomate. It is located on the outskirts of Puebla City and has a height of just 43 feet. Thlachihualtepetl, or the Great Pyramid of Cholula in Mexico, is known as the world’s largest pyramid by volume known to ever exist. You can look for customisable Mexico trip packages where you can add this site. The first printing press in North America was set up in modern-day Mexico City by Juan Cromberger in 1539. Mexico is home to jaguars, with an estimated population of around four to five thousand of them living in the wild. The capital city of Mexico is slowly sinking. Built on a lake, Mexico City has sunken a total of 30 feet over the years, at a rate of 20 inches per year.
Mexico.Mexico culture: Here are some popular culture habits and traditions followed in Mexico; Mexicans love to hold fiestas, where they celebrate various occasions like birthdays, anniversaries, weddings, etc., in a grand manner. Laughter, dance and music, good food and decorations are some of its major features. Mexico celebrates a special event called Dia del Nino on 30th April, where children receive presents and candies from their parents and teachers. Fiestas, games, competitions, etc., are held at schools instead of classes. To honour the ones who have died, Mexican families observe the famous Dia de los Muertos on November 1st and 2nd. Calaveras, marigold flowers, home altars (Ofrendas), visiting graves with gifts, etc., are some highlights of this event. Consider Mexico trip packages around this time to witness this popular event.
Mexico.Mexico visa for Indians: In order to enter Mexico for the purpose of tourism, Indian nationals will need to acquire a Mexico tourist visa. This visa allows a stay of up to 180 days in the country, and you must apply for it prior to your travel. Do note that some Mexico trip packages may or may not include visas. However, you can easily get a visa for your Mexico trip yourself through the online visa services offered by Thomas Cook. Just visit the official site, enter the country name, visa type and preferred branch and submit your communication details to get a quote. You can then proceed to pay online through any popular medium (credit card, debit card, net banking, etc.), submit necessary documents online or at a nearby branch and receive your visa without any hassle.
Mexico.Foreign currency for Mexico visit: The official currency of Mexico is the Mexican Peso (Code: MXN). The approximate value of 1 Mexican Peso to Indian Rupee is 1 MXN = 4.82 INR. You will need to acquire sufficient Mexican pesos for your Mexico trip so you can make payments there without any hassle. You can get foreign exchange through the online forex services offered by Thomas Cook in just a few clicks. Just visit the official website, select the check rate (cash or forex card), pick the currency type and forex amount, fill in the required traveller details and delivery type, complete the online payment and you will soon receive the forex order completion receipt.

With the breathtaking valleys paired with alluring landscapes that are sure to take your breath away, Sonmarg is a beauty of its kind. Our travel connoisseurs at Thomas Cook have curated some special packages for your next Sonmarg trip. More Details About Avantipur Temple Buddhist Temples Located in Narang, these Buddhist worship places form a great part of the Sonmarg culture, More Details About Buddhist Temples Amarnath Temple One of the most prominent places of worship among the Hindus, the Amarnath Temple attracts a great part of pilgrims from different parts of the country More Details About Amarnath Temple Island Retreat Park Located approximately 4 km away from the beautiful Sonmarg, this park lies amidst the charming River Sindh More Details About Island Retreat Park Thajiwas Glacier One of the most accessible glaciers in the stunning Sonmarg, this glacier offers some stunning picturesque views, making it a perfect place for all the camera lovers More Details About Thajiwas Glacier Baltal This beautiful valley in the outskirts of Sonmarg, is a base camp for all the tourists who wish to go on the Amarnath Yatra More Details About Baltal Raja Dinkar Kelkar Museum Since Sonmarg is comparatively small; there are no museums in this area. Jodhpur.Introduction: About Jodhpur Holidays - Wish to witness the beautiful sunrise in this regal land? All you have to do is book one of Thomas Cook India’s Jodhpur tour packages and soak in the colours and cultures of this sunny city. When you visit the Thomas Cook India website, you will find a plethora of Jodhpur holiday packages. Based on the specific places you wish to explore and the particular form of recreation you wish to participate in, Thomas Cook India will curate your itinerary and put together a holiday for the history books. Thomas Cook India’s Jodhpur tours give you an opportunity to choose from a brief Jodhpur holiday lasting 4 nights and 5 days to a lavish vacation to Jodhpur spread over 12 nights and 13 days. Whether you are looking for a budget-friendly holiday starting from INR 16,899 to a premium vacation that prices about INR 5,08,130 you will find the perfect match according to your needs and requirements. Thomas Cook India’s Jodhpur tour packages have been crafted very carefully by travel experts, taking in mind the diverse interests, unique requirements and comfort of every individual. Right from sightseeing choices to leisure time and shuttle services to accommodation, every aspect of your Jodhpur trip is planned with utmost care. The best part about your Jodhpur holiday is that you can board a flight or get into a train from all major cities like Pune, Mumbai, Cochin, Hyderabad, Chennai, Ahmedabad, Delhi, Kolkata, Udaipur and Bangalore to Jodhpur.
Jodhpur.Most Popular Jodhpur Tour Packages: Tour Packages NameDurationPriceRajasthan Saver 6 Nights 7 DaysRs. ₹28 000 Exquisite Rajasthan6 Nights 7 DaysRs. ₹32 800Best of Rajasthan 6N6 Nights 7 DaysRs. ₹47 000Rajasthan - Desert Adventure4 Nights 5 DaysRs. ₹24 500Ancient Rajasthan5 Nights 6 DaysRs. ₹30 100 Also, if you are looking to understand more about Jodhpur then visit our Jodhpur tourism section which covers everything about Jodhpur like the Things to Do in Jodhpur, Best Times to Visit Jodhpur, Places to Visit in Jodhpur and much more.Jodhpur, famously known as the ‘Blue City’, is placed gracefully in the stark land of the Thar Desert. Nestled near the center of Rajasthan, this mesmerising city surrounds the fort and is bounded by a wall with various gates. Although, over the past decades, Jodhpur has developed outside its ancient walls. The Mehrangarh fort stands tall, watching over the city from its perch on a rocky mountain top. Blessed with amazing cultures, heritage, history and royalty, this place will leave an impact on you and will make you feel like a King. The whole city brightens up as the sun rises and you can witness the beautiful night sky full of stars once the sun sets. The history of this place is seen through their astonishing architecture which includes beautiful blue coloured buildings, massive forts, ancient monuments, magnificent temples and rich, cultured havelis. With its mouthwatering delicacies and its fascinating markets, you will fall in love with this place. Warm people and diverse ethnicities fill up the land of Jodhpur.
Jodhpur.Best Time to Visit Jodhpur: The best time to visit Jodhpur is from the month of October to March. The weather during these months is pleasant. The cool breeze with warm sunshine during the day is perfect for sightseeing and exploring the desert. The month of October brings in some major celebrations like the Navratri, Dussehra, Diwali and the local Marwar festival. During the month of January, you can witness the famous Desert Kite festival where the whole sky is filled with beautiful and colourful kites. Another grand festival in Jodhpur which lies in the months of January and February is the Jodhpur Nagaur Fair. Celebrations in Jodhpur are vibrant and colourful which give a glimpse of its culture and history. The summer season comes in with sizzling hot days which begins from the month of April and lasts till June. It is advisable to stay indoors during summer afternoons, but evenings are quite pleasant and comparatively cooler. The monsoon season brings in humidity which is slightly better than the summer season.
Jodhpur.How to reach Jodhpur: Traveling to Jodhpur is an easy affair as it is comfortably accessible to air, rail and road. The following ways can be considered while travelling to Jodhpur. By Air: The Jodhpur Domestic Airport is about 5 kilometers away from the main city. Jodhpur is connected by all other main cities like Pune, Cochin, Chennai, Mumbai, Ahmedabad, Hyderabad, Kolkata, Bangalore and Delhi. Being a prime tourist spot, Jodhpur is well connected by most air carriers. Indira Gandhi International airport in New Delhi is the nearest International airport from Jodhpur. By Train: Traveling to Jodhpur by rail system is just perfect compared to other options of mobility as there is a railhead located right in the city. Jodhpur is well connected with other parts of the country due to an extensive rail network. Popular rail carriers that run from Jodhpur Railway Station are Howrah Jodhpur SF Express, Jaisalmer Delhi Express, Chennai Jodhpur Express, Rajasthan Sampark Kranti Express, Suryanagari Express and Bangalore Jodhpur Express. By Road: Jodhpur is well connected by major cities like Jodhpur, Delhi, Jaisalmer, Bikaner, Agra and Ahmedabad by road. One can travel to Jodhpur by boarding luxury buses or hiring a private taxi from major cities in India and important towns in Rajasthan.
Jodhpur.Places to visit in Jodhpur: Despite being nestled in a desert, there’s many other things to do in Jodhpur ranging from visiting beautiful historical forts to adventure activities, retail therapy, culinary classes and much more. Here’s a comprehensive list. Mehrangarh Fort One of the largest forts in India with an astonishing history, Mehrangarh stands tall over a mountain top overviewing the beautiful city of Jodhpur. This majestic fort is enclosed with thick walls and has seven gates namely Victory Gate, Gopal Gate, Bhairon Gate, Marti Gate, Fateh Gate, Dedh Kamra Gate and the strong Loha Gate. Each gate in the list was built to serve different purposes. The fort is home to some opulent palaces known for their baroque carvings and sprawling courtyards, gracious balconies, enormous galleries and beautifully decorated residences. The Sheesh Mahal and Phool Mahal are a piece of royalty and beauty. This ancient fort is also famous for its gigantic museum and galleries which exhibit the heritage of the royalty and sing songs of its history through its paintings, armours and sculptures. Umaid Bhawan Palace A beautiful blend of an interesting past and a lavish present, Umaid Bhawan Palace is home to the royal family of Jodhpur. An example of culture, royalty and history, this palace was the last palace that was built in India. This mesmerising palace consists of a heritage hotel, an enormous museum and a luxurious mansion of the royals. Housing some rare artifacts, vintage cars and brilliant paintings, this Palace is a must visit for every traveler during their Jodhpur holiday. To add to the beauty of this structure, it is surrounded by a lush green garden which is elegantly decorated with a variety of colourful flowers.Kaylana Lake If you wish to step away from the bustling city and relax at a serene where you are greeted with cool breeze and stunning beauty, Kaylana Lake is the place you should head. This is a manmade lake which was constructed by Pratap Singh in 1872 and till date, it serves as a primary source of drinking water for the residents in Jodhpur. An amazing view can be witnessed during the sunset as the golden rays of the sun spreads all over the blue lake and as the night sky approaches, the waters glitter as a reflection of the million stars shining up high.Surrounded by pristine trees and mountains, this lake is like a magical painting on a canvas. Jaswant Thada Jaswant Thada is a mesmerising building constructed in memoriam of Maharaja Jaswant Singh II by his son. The alluring white marble monument offers a peaceful backdrop away from the hustle of the city. The architecture of this cenotaph tells a story about the past through its carvings and paintings. The entire structure resembles a temple like construction with an enormous garden by its side. The sunrays bring out the vibrant colours of this marble monument and adds to the elegance of this place. At the entrance, one can encounter local musicians and fold dancers that entertain everyone with their culture and amazing skills. Jantar Mantar Jantar Mantar is an array of enormous unique sculptures that are instruments used to gauge astronomy built by Maharaja Sawai Jai Singh II in the 18th century. If you are curious about celestial objects, this is the place to be during your trip to Jodhpur. As symmetrical as the inside of a geometry box, this observatory also houses the tallest sundial in the world. The Vrihat Samrat Yantra helps measure, calculate and interpret the local time. Dilwara Jain Temple Wish to soak in the culture and understand a bit more about the religious teachings of the Jains? Head to this divine pilgrimage site, the Dilwara Jain Temple. This monument is located in the beautiful city of Mount Abu and is approximately 5 hours away from Jodhpur by road. You can hire a private taxi or hop in a bus from Jodhpur to this architectural wonder. This monument is made of white marbles and it tells a story about the Jain preaching through its carved pillars. This place is a must visit during your Jodhpur holiday due to its divine grace and astonishing pieces of work.
Jodhpur.What to eat in Jodhpur: Feast like the royals in the land of the Maharajas. Jodhpur is a place for sweet tooth, namkeens and globs of ghee. With its delicacies, Jodhpur raises the bar for other cuisines. From traditional shops to A-class restaurants, here are a few irresistible dishes one must try during their trip to Jodhpur. Mawa Kachori If you wish to indulge in a sweet treat, this Jodhpuri delicacy is just the thing for you. Stuffed with mawa and fried in oil, this is a perfect dessert if you have a sweet tooth. Mirchi Vada Want something to eat while sipping some hot tea? Try this crispy sider that is a bit salty and spicy which makes a perfect evening snack. This delicacy is made of spicy potato stuffed in green chilli peppers which is batter fried. It tastes best with tamarind chutney or green chutney. Dal bati churma This lip smacking Jodhpuri dish is loaded with the goodness of spiced lentils, ghee and are best had with little, deep-fried dough balls. This dish is a treat to your taste buds and stomach. The dal is cooked with lentils and spices, which is garnished with cumin and coriander. Pyaaz Kachori Please your taste buds by munching on some pyaaz kachori. This delicacy is a crispy and flaky deep-fried snack which is made of plain flour and spicy onion stuffing. Makhaniya lassi Who doesn’t love a chilled lassi? It is an incredibly luscious dessert made of curd and is flavoured with cardamom, rose and saffron water topped with dry fruits and saffron. Your Jodhpur holiday is incomplete if you miss to try out this lip-smacking delicacy.
Jodhpur.Things to do in Jodhpur: Jodhpur is brimming with rich culture, heritage and enlightening activities. Our Jodhpur packages make sure that they encase every kind of experience. Here are some things to do while you are in Jodhpur. Make sure you add them to your shortlisted Jodhpur tour package. Sail the desert sea Take a ride on the colourfully dressed camels amidst golden sand dunes of the Thar desert. Spend the night camping in the middle of the desert sea and witness the beautiful night sky full of stars glistening and brightening up the dark desert. Experience the beauty of dusk and dawn and evoke the photographer in you by clicking some ravishing pictures of the scenic desert. Soak in the culture and enjoy some mesmerising folk dance and music performances by the locals. If you wish to seek some adventure and thrill, go dune bashing over the broad heaps of sand. Either drive the SUV or hire the car for a perfect escapade. Get a bird’s eye view parasailing in the clear blue sky and experience an amazing activity. Shop for souvenirs in the City market Walk down the street of Jodhpur and come across the most colourful and lively market, Tripola market, with shops beautifully arranged. Hued souvenirs, Rajasthani jootis, authentic handicrafts, colourful carpets and wall hangings, silver trinkets, Bandhej apparel and traditional Rajasthani jewellery are some things you will want to take back home after your Jodhpur trip. And how can you forget to try on some local authentic delicacies while taking a stroll in the market? Walking through the market, you will be distracted by the ambrosial fragrances of the famous Mawa kachori, delicious Gulab jamuns and jalebis, spicy samosas, mirchi vada and kachoris, the lip smacking lasun chutney and lassi and the grand Rajasthani thali. Sounds delicious right? Don’t forget to try these scrumptious items on your Jodhpur holiday. Be royal at the Mehrangarh Fort Take a stroll through the beautiful Mehrangarh Museum and learn more about the cultures of Jodhpur. Walk down the history lane of this beautiful fort through its paintings and artifacts. Seek some blessings at the Chamundi Mataji Temple and the Nagnechiji Temple which are nestled within the Mehrangarh fort. Climb on the top of the fort and get an opportunity to feed wild kites and eagles, a ritual done by the Maharajas during their era. For an adrenaline rush, one can try the thrilling Flying Fox zipline activity at the Mehrangarh Fort.

Montreal.Introduction: Travelling in Canada is incomplete without including Montreal in your holiday plan. This cosmopolitan hub of Canadian culture is known for its rich colonial history. Montreal boasts a blend of European charm and North American dynamism. Enthusiastic travellers who want to experience the seamless fusion of the past and present opt for Montreal tour packages. Whether you're admiring the rich history or lots of urban charm, your Montreal holiday package will cover your needs. These days, Montreal tour packages highlight an unforgettable visit to the picturesque Old Montreal Neighbourhood, along with iconic landmarks like the Notre-Dame Basilica of Montreal and Bonsecours Market. Montreal is the second-largest French-speaking city in the world. On your Montreal tour, you will see how its native people are fiercely proud of the French language, rich heritage, and cultural traditions. And yes, they welcome non-French-speaking travellers with utmost happiness and pride. Some of the primary Montreal attractions are museums, nightclubs, parks, shopping districts and galleries. Anyone who wishes to experience a relaxing and rejuvenating trip during their vacation must consider Montreal travel packages. There are many things to do in Montreal that will make you experience a thrilling city that effortlessly blends rich history, urban charm, culinary diversity, and mesmerising creativity. Montreal.Best Time to Visit Montreal: Thanks to its ideal climate and array of festivals, the best time to go to Montreal is all year round. However, most people think the best time to visit Montreal is from March to May and September to November, when hotel accommodation, good food, and transportation are available at lower prices. And yes, the temperature also remains mild in these months. Read on the following discussion to understand the best time to go to Montreal in a simplified manner: March to May is the Best Time to Lose in the Vibes of Springtime Flowers As the temperature rises in March, hotel accommodation prices begin to fall. April is the best month to visit Montreal, when the city welcomes the spring season with an open heart, when the first blue petals of the Siberian Squill bloom. Things to do in Montreal during the springtime are strolling leisurely in the mild, warm weather, visiting outdoor pubs, heading to museums to understand the city’s past, and witnessing the iconic festivals. Key Events Blue Metropolis Literary Festival Montreal St. Patrick’s Day Parade Montreal Museums Month Pouzza Fest Festival Vue Sur la Relève June to August is the Peak Season for Numerous Festivals Is your budget allowing you to spend big chunks of money on your Montreal holiday package? Consider June to August the best season to visit Montreal because global tourists flock to the destination to witness outdoor concerts and other mega events. During this summer season, you can easily explore the city on foot. However, it gets a bit cold after dark. You can spend your evening watching the sunset at popular rooftop bars and your day hopping between famous cafes. So, if you have chosen the best season to visit Montreal, book your flights, hotel accommodations, etc., in advance for a hassle-free experience. Key Events Formula One: Grand Prix of Canada Montreal Pride Les Francos de Montreal MURAL TEST Montreal International Jazz Festival MUTEK Osheaga September to November Is the Best Time to Experience Autumn Colours Fall is the best time to go to Montreal for tourists who want to explore the city when summery throngs free the space. You can expect the temperature to steadily drop in September and enjoy the cinematic drop of autumn leaves. The wilderness of Parc du Mont-Royal, Oratoire St. Joseph, Carré St. Louis, and more will provide an unforgettable experience with their vibrant orange, crimson, and brown leaves. Key Events Montreal International Film Festival POP Montreal Montreal Burlesque Festival December to February Is the Best Time to Enjoy the Underground City When the temperature plummets to a single digit, the whole city is covered in snow and ice. To feel warm, most tourists go to RESO, an Underground City, where they explore numerous shopping malls, restaurants, hotels, and movie theatres. So, enjoy an indoor Montreal trip without facing harsh weather conditions. Key Events Christmas in the Park Montreal’s Christmas Village Igloofest Montreal en Lumiere
Montreal.How to Reach Montreal City: These days, Montreal tourism has emerged as a favourite international destination pick for worldwide travellers that can be easily accessed by land, water, and air. Check out the details to know how to reach Montreal City: By Plane Check out which Indian airlines are offering a Montreal trip at affordable prices. You will be landed at YUL Montreal Trudeau International Airport, which is located just 20 minutes from downtown. Upon arrival, you will get an array of services, including car rentals, limousines, and airport shuttles to make your way to the city. You can choose the most popular 747 Express Bus, which features 24/7/365 service, taking you to one of the nine downtown stops in around 40 to 60 minutes. It costs $7. On choosing a taxi, the Montreal trip will cost you a flat $38. By Train If you are on a Canada tour, consider the VIA Rail Network, which makes the Montreal trip of thousands of Indian tourists highly accessible every year. It is the easiest way to reach downtown. Besides this, you can head to Gare Centrale to choose one of 17 departures that will make your Montreal tour seamless. With it, you can access the Bonaventure metro station, the Underground Pedestrian Network, BIXI bike rentals, a taxi, and more. Are you coming from the USA? Make sure to choose Amtrak trains for your Montreal tour, as they offer a direct route from many American cities to the downtown area. By Bus Check out which American and Canadian operators are offering a Montreal trip at affordable prices. By bus, you will arrive at the Montreal Bus Central Station which is connected to the Underground Pedestrian Network. By Car Are you exploring Canada by car? Plan your Montreal tour. Depending on where you are this time, you may choose one of the popular bridges—Champlain, Jacques-Cartier or Victoria—or the Louis-Hippolyte-La Fontaine bridge-tunnel to reach downtown directly.
Montreal.Best Places to Visit in Montreal City: The top attractions of Montreal tourism include many amazing and serene places to see in Montreal. Check out these most-visited Montreal tourist places. Famous Places to Visit in Montreal City Olympic Park: The most popular attraction of Montreal tour packages is the Olympic Park. With its beautiful landscape, rich history, and stunning rocks, the Olympic Park is a must-visit destination. Old Montreal: Old Montreal is famous for its vintage buildings, which date back to the 17th, 18th, and 19th centuries. It resembles an old Persian-style quarter filled with historic sites, streets, and landmarks. Jarding Botanique: Jardin Botanique is the home to diverse plants, which are grown in 30 themed gardens and 10 exhibition greenhouses. Notre Dame Basilica: Notre Dame Basilica is another highlight of Montreal tour packages. It was founded in 1656 and is the oldest church in the city. The Orange Julep: The Orange Julep is one of the favourite Montreal tourist attractions for history lovers. The Mont-Royal is nicknamed the green lung of the city. This park is a perfect thing to see in Montreal. So, take a stroll here. Worthwhile Monuments: Appreciate the tranquillity in the monuments to Jacques Cartier and King George VI. Parc Jean Drapeau: Parc Jean Drapeau is an artificial island and is popular for many family-friendly attractions highlighted by Montreal packages. Biosphere Museum : As a remnant of the 1967 World Fair, the Biosphere is now a museum dedicated to ecological issues. Musée des Beaux Arts: Musée des Beaux Arts is the oldest museum in Canada and houses vast collections of paintings, sculptures, and new media. With so many things to do in Montreal, your Montreal tour will be an amazing experience. Beaches to Visit in Montreal The best things to see in Montreal are beyond your imagination. On your Montreal trip, let your inner water baby come outside on sun-kissed beaches. Besides lying back on the warm sand, there are many activities to do in Montreal beaches, like pedalling a paddleboat, swimming, kayaking, sailing, and more. Plage-Saint-Zotique: Located in the town of Saint-Zotique, Plage Saint-Zotique is a mesmerising beach, offering a perfect weekend getaway that you should not miss. Here, there are many things to enjoy, like a volleyball court, a playground, and paddleboats, along with lying on warm sand to relax. Pointe Calumet Beach: Are you a beach party freak? Consider Pointe Calumet Beach to enjoy fun, frolic, and beach club parties. Secluded from all the hustle and bustle, this beach is an ideal destination to relax at Lake Deux Montagnes. Saint Timothee Beach: Located at Ile-Papineau in Valleyfield, Saint Timothee Beach is in front of the St. Lawrence River, offering travellers a peaceful space. Saint Gabriel Beach: Spread across a vast region of 10.5 km and an average depth of 28 meters, Sainte Gabriel Beach is a perfect place for trekkers. There are many adventurous activities to enjoy, such as kayaking, swimming, sailing, stand-up paddleboarding, and more. Fortunately, here you will get a perfect escape in the summer season. Jean Dore Beach: Jean Dore Beach is another excellent beach located near downtown, making it easily accessible by bike or public transit. So, add this fascinating beach to your must-visit Montreal tourist places. And yes, enjoy the crystal-clear water and beautiful golden sand on the ground.
Montreal.Foods to Eat in Montreal City: Montreal cuisine has a distinctive flavour, focusing on local and seasonal ingredients. Check out the following discussion to learn why it is a first-of-its-kind gastronomic destination: Tantalise Your Tastebuds with Poutine: Also known as the canvas of creativity, Poutine is a favourite Montreal food that you should never avoid. Find your own poutine spot in La Banquise, Ma Poule Mouillee, and more. Delicious Bagels: If you are a true foodie, you would never like to miss the best bagels. So, head to the Mile End and grab your favourite hot bagel from the St. Viateur and Fairmount shops. Explore Your Favourite Ice Cream: Although there are numerous ice cream shops in Montreal, you must visit KEM COMBA, known for its traditional, rich flavours. Here, you will not only savour the soft-serve taste that changes with the seasons but also enjoy engaging conversations with fellow ice cream enthusiasts. Never Miss Sweet Spots: Do you want to relish the unique taste of Paris in Montreal? Savour the best breads and pastries. And yes, explore elegant croissants, cookies, hearty breads, and more to enjoy the smack of the real Montreal cuisine. Get Caffeinated and Refresh Yourself: Are you a true coffee amateur? Montreal has found itself as a world-class coffee town that will make you explore the best cafes, just like in Portland, Melbourne, and New York.
Montreal.Montreal Currency: There are many places to visit and many things to do in Montreal. All you should have is the Montreal Currency. Remember, the currency in Montreal is the Canadian dollar . Its banknotes come in the denominations of $5, $10, $20, $50, and $100, while its coins are available in 5 cents (nickel), 10 cents (dime), 25 cents (quarter), $1 (loonie), and $2 (toonie). Presently, 62.45 Indian rupees carry the value of one Canadian dollar. You should have Canadian currency while making purchases in Montreal. Knowing about the top currency exchange options can help you make an informed decision: Banks: Explore Canadian banks to convert your Indian rupees to Canadian dollars. However, the exchange rate may vary. Canada Currency Exchange Centres: Discover the Canada Currency Exchange Centre in Canadian airports and cities. Take advantage of their money exchange services. ATMs: Find out which international ATMs offer Canadian dollars using an Indian debit card. Online Exchange Services: Check out which online services allow you to exchange money for your Montreal trip.

Udaipur.Introduction: The city of Udaipur, discovered by Maharana Udai Singh in 1553, is located in the southern region of the State of Rajasthan. Nestled amongst the Aravalli Mountains, it is also called the “city of lakes“ and “Venice of the East”. If you truly want to explore Udaipur, book any Udaipur Travel Packages from Thomas Cook for an immersive experience. You can also opt for a specific Udaipur city tour package while travelling to Rajasthan. This “White City” used to be the capital of the Mewar Empire ruled by the Sisodia clan. The proximity to other cities of Gujarat made it a hub for salt and fabric traders bringing in the wealth for the Mewar empire. If the traveller in you enjoys these snippets from history, the Udaipur tour should make a place in your travel list. Udaipur tour packages can be customised to be luxurious as well as budget-friendly. Udaipur holiday packages of Thomas Cook are well tailored to quench your traveller's thirst within your budget. The beauty of the city lies in the Aravalli mountains, serene lakes, majestic palaces, and lush green gardens. Book Udaipur vacation packages in winter when the weather is pleasant with bright sunshine and comfortable evenings. Summers are difficult with temperatures hovering around 45 degrees and the absence of rain. These extreme temperatures are experienced as Udaipur is close to the Thar desert. The city is rich in culture and traditions and fairs and festivals are celebrated with a lot of enthusiasm. Thomas Cook’s Udaipur city tour package can give you a glimpse inside the local celebrations. The locals are known for their Art and architecture. Carry back some brightly coloured puppets, blue potteries, and mirror works as memories from your Udaipur tour.

Accessible by flight, rail, and road, the Udaipur trip attracts tourists from domestic and international places as well.
