Udaipur.Best time to visit Udaipur: Being close to the Thar desert, Udaipur experiences an extreme climate. The winter though is the best time to visit Udaipur, monsoon and summer has a different charm. Winter (October to March): The winter season is best for the Udaipur city tour. The days are cool and nights are colder with temperatures ranging from 10-25 degrees Celsius. You can rest assured that the Udaipur itinerary will be filled with camel rides, trekking, boating, bird watching and so much more. Udaipur sightseeing package will be full of fort and palace visits along with colourful and cultural festivals like the World Music Festival, Kumbhalgarh Festival, and World Living Heritage Festival. The warm royal cuisine of Rajasthan can be best enjoyed in this weather. Since this is peak time for tourists, you can expect the Udaipur trip cost to be a little steeper. However, the Udaipur holiday packages of Thomas Cook are tailored to fit your pocket as well as comfort standards. Make sure to carry sturdy shoes, layered clothing, a good camera, and a good mosquito repellent. Summer (March to June) Udaipur travel in the summer season is not for the faint-hearted. With temperatures soaring from 38 to 40 degrees, heat waves make it extremely uncomfortable and dangerous to move out for any sightseeing. The evenings are pleasant and are the only time for any outings. Although the entrance fees and hotel accommodations will be friendlier and luxury will be affordable, most palaces, forts, rides, and lakes will be out of bounds. The usual Udaipur packages will have fewer activities and more luxurious feasts around the hotel pools and restaurants. The Mewar festival is the only thing that can be enjoyed in the sweltering heat. Remember to carry light, cotton clothes with open sandals. Hats, sunglasses, and sunscreens are a must to prevent sunburns, and drink water frequently to fight dehydration. Monsoon (July to October) With moderate to high rainfall, monsoon is the second-best season for budget travellers to book Udaipur trip packages. Boat rides on lakes and rooftop dining with the lush green view around will be highlights of your Udaipur trip in Monsoon. You can visit the magnificent Monsoon Palace, City Palace, or Bagore ki Haveli for a magical environment amongst the raindrops. Book Udaipur Trip Packages without delay if you want to discover the best of the city during the monsoon season in a cost-effective way. The budgeted flight options, accommodation, and intra-city commutes will all be included in the Udaipur vacation packages. All you need to carry are a raincoat, windcheater, and camera cover to stay dry and enjoy your stay.
Udaipur.How to reach Udaipur?: Udaipur is well connected by air, water, and roads from all parts of India. So you need not worry about how to reach Udaipur. Thomas Cook’s Udaipur trip cost is all-inclusive giving you the option to choose from flights, trains, and private car services. By Air Dabok Airport or Maharana Pratap Airport, 24 km away from the city centre, connects the other parts of India to the city. Udaipur vacation packages will come with various options of flights from Delhi, Mumbai, Jaipur, Hyderabad, and Jodhpur. International travellers can enter India via Delhi or Mumbai International Airport as the options of flights are much more numerous than the nearest Jaipur International Airport. The Jaipur airport only has connecting flights to a few South Asian countries like Kuala Lumpur and Bangkok. From the airport, you can avail of welcome and transport as included in your Thomas Cook Udaipur travel packages or book a prepaid taxi or cab to reach your destination. By Rail The two railway stations of Udaipur are Udaipur City railway station and Rana Pratap Nagar railway station, located a stone’s throw from the main city. Both these stations are well connected to major cities of the country like Kolkata, Bangalore, New Delhi, Mumbai, Jaipur, Ajmer, Kota, Agra, etc. Udaipur tour is an integral part of Rajasthan Tourism, the luxurious trains like Palace on Wheels, Maharaja Express, and The Royal Rajasthan on Wheels are also exclusive ways to travel to Udaipur after reaching Rajasthan. But the more common and regular ones are Mewar Express, Chetak Express, and Gwalior- Udaipur Express which can be used to reach Udaipur. The stations are near the main bus stand and hence internal commute is taken care of by public transport. Most of the Thomas Cook Udaipur packages will arrange for your transit from the station as well. By Road Udaipur travel by road is a unique experience as the travel is comfortable and picturesque. If you want to soak in the vibes around, choose Udaipur holiday packages that give you the freedom to commute via private car deluxe buses or AC coaches. The NH8 highway connects both Delhi and Mumbai to Udaipur. It lies exactly at the intersection of NH8, Golden Quadrilateral, East-West Corridor, and NH76. Udaipur tour can be taken by road or bus from Delhi, Mumbai, Ahmedabad, Indore, Kota etc. You can ask your agent while booking Udaipur Packages to experience a self-driven journey with your family. Hiring a cab becomes expensive, especially during peak seasons, if you have a car or love to drive it saves you a load of money for the transit.
Udaipur.Places to visit in Udaipur:: The best places to visit in Udaipur City depend on the type of traveller you are. If you love history, the palaces and museums will be the places to explore. If you love nature, the sanctuaries, lakes, and mountains will be your ideal spots. Some of the best tourist places in Udaipur which are popular are listed here. Your Udaipur trip packages can be custom-made with places you would like to explore. Some of the places that are a must in your Udaipur itinerary are: City Palace This is an architectural wonder and its courtyards, pavilions, terraces, corridors, and rooms all come alive in the light and sound show held in “Manak Chowk”. The main entrance is adorned with a triple arch gate called “Tripoli” under which Maharanas were weighed in Gold and the same gold was distributed amongst the commoners. These make the palace one of the top places to visit in Udaipur. Lake Pichola The name is derived from a nearby village called Picholi. The erstwhile Jagniwas which was a Summer palace is now converted to a hotel named Lake Palace. The palace is beautifully decorated with white and black marble, well-carved niches, and gardens. In the Udaipur city tour, you can almost spend a day here exploring the Lake Palace, the Jagmandir, another summer palace, Bagore ki Havel, and Fateh Sagar Lake, which is connected to Pichola by a canal. Monsoon Palace Named Sajjangarh Palace is placed on a hilltop and gives a magnificent view of Fateh Sagar Lake and Sajjangarh Wildlife Sanctuary. This unique hilltop view makes it one of the favourite places to visit in Udaipur. In your Udaipur sightseeing package, you can have a mix and match of nature and history that will be a refreshing change. Ahar Museum While out on a Udaipur city tour, you can check out this small museum which is located close to several cenotaphs or tombs of Maharanas. The museum has a collection of earthen pottery and sculptures. A 10th-century metal Buddha figure is a well-known attraction here. Bird Park GulabBagh It has 28 species of birds and spreads over a 5.11-hectare area. Your Udaipur tour guide will help you go through the 12 enclosures including the Parrots House, Macaw and Cockatoo Courtyard, The Flightless Birds Section, and the Aquatic Avifauna Section, etc. Udai Sagar Lake One of the most famous tourist places in Udaipur, this was initially built as a dam on River Berach to supply water to the kingdom of Maharana Udai Singh. Jaisamand Lake The 2nd largest man-made sweet water lake in Asia was built to stop the water flow from the Ruparel River. Now amongst other Udaipur tourist places, this is a place for relaxation and family picnics.
Udaipur.Best Hill Stations in Udaipur:: Surrounded by the Aravalli range, Udaipur travel can be made more exciting by visiting the nearby hill stations with friends and family. You can ask your Udaipur Tour guide to add any one of these to your Udaipur vacation packages. Mount Abu The only hill station in Rajasthan is 163 km away from Udaipur. It can be a part of your Udaipur tour in winter. You can go boat riding in Nakki Lake, trek to Guru Shikhar Peak, and enjoy spotting wildlife in Mount Abu Wildlife Sanctuary. Nestled amongst the Aravalli range it is at the border of Gujarat and Rajasthan. The Dilwara Temple built on the hills is a Jain temple made of white marble. Do ask your Udaipur tour guide for further details of accommodation and transport. Toranmal Though not in the state of Rajasthan, this hill station of Maharashtra is quite accessible from Udaipur at a distance of 530 km. If you want to go beyond the typical Udaipur tourist places, then make the road to this quaint and pristine hill station via the Mandsaur-Ratlam-Dhar highway. You will not regret paying a little more over your Udaipur trip cost. Girnar Hill In Junagarh district of Gujarat, is a well-known religious destination and is visited by lakhs of pilgrims annually. If you want to visit a religious place outside Udaipur places to visit, you can ride a train or bus for 6 hours to get there.
Udaipur.Best Honeymoon Destinations in Udaipur:: Udaipur is the best honeymoon destination for a regal post-marriage experience. With so much beauty, serenity, and luxury, your tour of Udaipur might be the most romantic beginning of your happily ever after. The Udaipur trip packages for couples can be made exclusive by choosing the right places that will help you cherish every moment of your honeymoon. There are many palaces converted to 5-star hotels which have become the best places in Udaipur for honeymooners looking for some quiet time away from the hustle. Lake Palace The exquisite and pristine Lake Palace also offers the finest accommodation with spa facilities for newlyweds. Such Udaipur packages for honeymooners can be arranged to have a luxurious experience away from the common crowds. Sajjangarh Palace Again surrounded by lakes, the monsoon palace offers a panoramic view for clicking your honeymoon memories in Instagrammable settings. If you and your better half enjoy the countryside atmosphere, this might be one of the best places to visit in Udaipur or the best palace to stay in during your honeymoon. Saheliyon Ki Bari For the honeymoon couple’s tour to Udaipur, the celebration of their love will be incomplete without a visit to this place. The Garden of Maidens was built by Maharana Sangram Singh for his Queen and her 48 maidens as proof of his deep love for his wife. Under the Sun Aquarium If sea creatures fascinate both of you, a trip to this underwater world on your tour of Udaipur will be a spectacular time together. In this 125-meter-long aquarium, you can explore almost 150 species of sea creatures, like albino frogs and scampis. Vintage Car Museum A perfect place for your clicks, this museum should be amongst the top Udaipur tourist attractions in your Udaipur tour packages for your honeymoon.
Udaipur.Interesting Things to Do in Udaipur:: There are many interesting things to do in Udaipur other than just sightseeing. Sunset Cruise on Lake Pichola You must have a sunset boat cruise in Lake Pichola with your best half. Nothing can be more romantic than the sparkling waters of the lake surrounded by golden hues on the ghats, havelis, and temples surrounding it. Free up one evening in your Udaipur Itinerary to experience this lovely activity. Saheliyon ki Bari Take back memories of Udaipur sightseeing, with Instagrammable photos clicked in the picture-perfect garden of “ Saheliyon ki Bari” with lotus ponds, intricately carved fountains, and marble elephants. Pray in Eklingji and Jagdish Temple Amongst innumerable religious places to see in Udaipur, you can offer prayers to Lord Shiva in Eklingji Temple and Lord Jagannath in Jagdish Temple. Lord Shiva was the ruling God of Mewar and his abode was built in the 8th century. Jagdish temple displays an Indo Aryan style shrine with mandapa and hand-crafted pillars. Udaipur tour guide can help you take part in the puja ceremony. Ropeway You will be surprised to find a ropeway experience in Udaipur tour packages. The Mansapurna Karni Mata ropeway gives you a birds-eye view of Udaipur and its surroundings when you ride the little red cabins from Deen Dayal Park to Karni Mata temple at Machhala Hilltop. Horse Riding One of the sports the Royals used to indulge in and still do is Horse riding. This can be included on special requests in your Udaipur vacation packages. It will take you to the trails in Aravalli Hills. Your Udaipur travel will not be the same without this. Nature Trekking Choose your Udaipur tour guide who is enthusiastic about nature. The trekking paths in the Aravallis start from Ubeshwar temple and end at Morvania village. You can meet the locals and have a wonderful journey with your co-trekkers. Puppet Show If you have specifically opted for Udaipur tour packages for family, your children will love to catch a colourful puppet show in Bharatiya Lok Kala Mandal which is a cultural institution showcasing local art and talents. Rajasthani Cuisine Enjoy authentic, spicy, and delicious Rajasthani cuisine in a rooftop restaurant of Jagat Niwas on the Udaipur tour. The natural ambience of lakes and hilltops makes it an interesting lunch with your loved ones. Sajjangarh WildLife Safari If you are looking for something beyond the Udaipur city tour, you can get your quota of workouts while trekking through this sanctuary. You can spot deer, tigers, wild boars, and hyenas around Bari Lake located inside the sanctuary. Shopping in Hathi Pol Bazaar An integral part of Udaipur tourism is exquisite embroidery, Handicrafts, and miniature paintings. The Pichwai and Phad paintings sold in Hathi Pol Bazaar are the perfect souvenirs to take back home.
Udaipur.Adventurous things to do in Udaipur:: You will be excited to learn about the various Adventurous things to do in Udaipur besides taking pictures, having a city tour, and shopping for souvenirs. Along with accommodation and transport, you can book these rides and experiences to be included in Udaipur packages. Parasailing If you like the adrenaline rush Thomas Cook Udaipur travel packages can arrange for parasailing at Fateh Sagar Lake. This offers a breathtaking view of the lake and the Aravalli hills around. Hot Air Balloon Hot air Ballooning at Lake Pichola will take you above the entire city giving an enriching experience of the architectural wonders and natural beauty of the place. This can be your aerial Udaipur trip. Camel Safari Being near the desert area, Udaipur travel packages may include a camel safari. This can also be arranged locally by the Udaipur tour guides via local agents or organisers. Ropeway Udaipur tour packages can be personalised with adventure activities while preparing the daily itinerary. The Karni Mata Ropeway is one such activity that you can take part in if you are not afraid of heights. While on the journey you can view the entire city with lakes, palaces, and mountain ranges. Zip lining In case you want some speed in your tranquil Udaipur tour plan, zip lining in Neemach Hills is perfect for you. You can enjoy a thrilling skyride by gliding along a steel cable with lush green landscapes below. Night Camping Along with the camel ride, you can add night camping in the desert in your Udaipur holiday packages. The hotels or resorts are also well-equipped to provide you with the amenities for camping in extreme desert temperatures. Additionally, you can enjoy dessert, food, music, and performances by locals. Boating and Kayaking Both the artificial lakes. Pichola and Fateh Sagar are excellent places for relaxed boating and kayaking. This is just right for newlyweds who are looking for a romantic escape in Udaipur holiday packages for a honeymoon. Rappelling and Rock Climbing You can take a break from the monotony of the Udaipur city tour by engaging in rock climbing and rappelling in and around the rocky cliffs, and cascading waterfalls.
Udaipur.What to eat in Udaipur?: Though most Udaipur trip packages will include lunch and dinners, you can still refer to the below-mentioned list of must-haves, to understand what to eat in Udaipur. Food tours are also great ways to try out local street delicacies. Dal Baati Churma An iconic Rajasthani dish, well served in Udaipur. If you feel hungry while on a Udaipur city tour and crave something healthy yet fulfilling, try this simple and flavorful dish. The Daal is a lentil soup, the Bati are small wheat flour balls roasted and dunked in ghee, and the Churma is made of grounded Baatis mixed with sweet jaggery. Machli Jaisamandi It is believed to have derived its name from Lake Jaisamand, created by Mewar ruler Jai Singh who was served freshwater fish curry by the local tribes to please him. You can refer to your Udaipur travel guide to find places that serve this dish unique to Udaipur. Kachori A typical, crispy, deep-fried Rajasthani snack or breakfast dish served with chutneys or curd dip. There are various types depending on the variety of stuffings put in it, like Pyaz (Onion Kachori), Daal (Lentil Kachori), and Curd (Dahi Kachori). You can find Kachori shops on every corner during the Udaipur city tour. Mirchi Vada Very popular with locals, these deep-fried chilli fritters are spicy and tangy. The spice comes from the balance of spices mixed with potato and stuffed inside them. Then these are dipped into chickpea batter and deep-fried. You must make a stop at Dhabology for these delicious bites even if you are on a Udaipur one day tour. Mewari Thali One lunch in your Udaipur itinerary should consist of this Mewari Thali as lunch at the Nataraj Hotel. This came to the forefront when Anthony Bourdain, a famous chef and traveller visited this place and fell in love with the food. Thali consists of simple yet flavorful local dishes like Kachori, a dry seasonal vegetable, a potato curry, a paneer dish, and a flatbread along with rice, and ends with soft and warm gulab jamun. Methi Ki Gotey It works as the confluence of Gujarati Gotey, fried fenugreek balls dipped in yoghurt sauce, or Rajasthani kadhi. A one-of-a-kind street food found in selected places of Udaipur. Doodh Jalebi and Rabdi Malpua Do end your tour of Udaipur with a sweet dish. Amongst many unique “mithai”, Jalebi with warm milk is a combination that will leave you craving for more. Rabdi Malpua is another pancake-like sweet made from “Rabdi” or thickened milk, mixed with sugar and flour and fried in pure clarified butter. Save some money from your Udaipur trip cost and try these specialities from local street side stalls and restaurants and you will never forget the experience.
Udaipur.Must visit Restaurants/ Resorts in Udaip: As mentioned above in the must-have food list, there are popular local restaurants that serve these dishes and have maintained their authentic make and taste. These are some of the must-visit restaurants or food shops that you can incorporate into your Udaipur trip plan: For breakfast or snacking on Hing kachori or dahi kachori, stop near Jagdish Temple and try some in Jagdish Shri Restaurant nearby. But foodies who want to have the best ones need to head out to Pratapgarh Chaatwala just outside Central Jail. The best way is to check your Udaipur sightseeing package and find the local markets near the monuments you are visiting, to try out fresh and local favourites. You can try the affordable Manoj Prakash Centre, near Gulab Bagh for a bowl full of Daal Baati Churma. Another famous place is Santosh Dal Bati Restaurant which serves this authentic dish. If you love your meat and fish, try a tribal dish of Machli Jaisamandi, served in Paantya Restaurant and Laal Maas (or red mutton curry ) served in Upre or Ambrai restaurants just across Lake Pichola. A Udaipur city tour will become more enjoyable with these little food breaks. A little sweet after all these savoury delights will only enhance your satisfaction with the Udaipur tour. Dunk your sweet and crispy jalebi in warm saffron milk at Trivedi Upahar Grah, an age-old sweet shop at Surajpole Circle. The Rabdi Malpua at Shyam Misthan Bhandar will satisfy non-sweet lovers as well. The crispy and flaky pancakes are made of thickened milk and dipped in sugar syrup. The must-visit resorts in Udaipur are The resorts chosen for accommodation in Udaipur holiday packages depend on your budget and the number of persons visiting. However, if you love luxurious places and want to feel like royalty, some resorts can be hand-picked to give you a regal experience. Adjacent to Lake Pichola and City Palace is Oberoi Udayvilas which offers a luxurious stay with all modern amenities. The Lalit Laxmi Vilas Palace, located beside Lake Fateh Sagar is a heritage palace that has now turned into a 5-star hotel but retained its architectural beauty with domes, frescos, and archways. Udaipur travel packages sometimes can be curated along with packages offered by these resorts, especially for newlyweds or Honeymooners. Fateh Prakash Palace Resort, named after Maharana Fateh Singh was a royal residence converted to a commercial lodging for tourists. However, they have retained the lighting and the inimitable turrets and vaults. If you are a nature lover, include The Aodhi Resort Kumbhalgarh resort in the Udaipur vacation packages. Hidden amidst the greeneries of the Aravalli Range, you can take a leisure walk exploring the nearby first flora and fauna.
Udaipur.What to shop in Udaipur?: The markets of Udaipur are sure to make the women in your group very happy. They will be spoilt for choices. The Udaipur trip packages will have a day for visiting the marketplaces. It is ideal to be prepared with your list to maximise your time. Here is a list of the most coveted souvenirs from Udaipur: Miniature paintings give you a glimpse into the history of Rajputs of Rajasthan. These are small, highly detailed paintings, with bold colours and contemporary styles. The history of these paintings dates back to the 16th century when this form of art flourished under the reign of Maharana Jagat Singh I. You will get the best of these in Mewar Art Gallery and Vinayak Arts and Crafts. You can add these galleries to the list of sightseeing in the Thomas Cook Udaipur itinerary. Leather Diaries are perfect and exclusive souvenirs, these leather diaries are durable and water resistant. The leather is processed by local craftsmen using ancient techniques of tanning and stitching leather. The natural fabric and intricate crafting on the leather make these the choicest souvenirs for any tourist. You can get the finest ones in Craft Shades Inc. and Leather Panache. Block Print Fabrics with the art of Dabu or Akola is exclusive to Udaipur. These patterns are derived from natural forms. You can find these in Hathipole Bazaar or Bada Bazaar which are any ways among the famous Udaipur places to visit for shopping. The typical Rajasthani puppets are called “Kathputlis”. The puppeteers called “Nats” and “Bhats” are local tribal nomads who believe that the art of manoeuvring puppets to convey traditional folklore has a rich history dating back to the 1st century. You will find these puppets wearing colourful Rajasthani costumes and headgear, extensively in Hathipole Bazaar. Intricately designed pottery in Shilpagram captures the city's art heritage. Detailed ornate vases, charming figurines, and well-crafted platters are displayed for the tourists to choose from. You can drop in during the Udaipur city tour and pick up some quality souvenirs to take back home. The bazaar near Ghanta Ghar has several local shops offering jewellery with Meenakari, Polki, and Kundan works. You can find antique silver pieces decorated with souvenirs at affordable prices. Pick up some exclusively designed neckpieces, earrings, and bracelets for the women back home. The shopping list in your Udaipur itinerary will leave you quite satisfied with its variety and affordability.
Udaipur.Interesting facts about Udaipur:: Before booking any Udaipur city tour package, you must know a few interesting and unknown facts about the place that will make every penny you spend on the trip worthwhile. Most of these places related to these fun facts will be naturally included in Thomas Cook Udaipur packages, but you may like to customise them to suit your travel needs. Jaisamand Lake This is Asia's second-largest artificial lake constructed by Rana Jai Singh, with 7 islands, a dam, and a nature trail. It is a complete destination in itself with Jaisamand Wildlife Sanctuary and Jaisamand Island Resort. Kumbhalgarh Fort This fort built by Maharana Pratap has a special significance in the rich history of Udaipur. This spreads across 36 km and is the second longest wall after the Great Wall of China. It is built strategically amongst the hills of Aravalli and houses over three hundred and sixty Brahmanical and Jain temples. An iconic monument that forms an integral part of Udaipur holiday packages. Jagmandir Palace A floating marble palace on Lake Pichola which owns the top spot in the list of Udaipur sightseeing packages, was once home to Mughal Emperor, Shah Jahan. The emperor was so impressed by its marvellous structure and beauty that he said he was inspired to build the Taj Mahal. Blue Houses The blue-coloured houses have historical and geographical significance in Udaipur.Historically Brahmins used to reside in these houses. And the colour is believed to keep houses cool during the scorching summer heat of Rajasthan. Udaipur Solar Observatory It is believed to be the second-best one in the world. Located on the island of Fateh Sagar Lake, it is an astronomer's paradise as the water around reduces air turbulence helping in clarity. A hidden gem and a must-visit place in Udaipur trip packages. Sajjangarh Palace or the Monsoon Palace This was built perched on the hilltop to serve as an astronomical centre but later turned into a weather-watching centre due to its location. And hence the name. The Crystal Gallery A secluded gallery of all things crystal stands in Fateh Prakash Palace as proof of royal whims and fancies. The crystal artefacts, furniture, dinner sets, and even beds were ordered by Maharana Sajjan Singh in 1877, from F&C Osler and Company an English Firm. Book your Udaipur travel packages now so you do not lose out on any of the experiences.

table_of_content: Important Tips For Your Cruise Experience

faq.question: What are the health and safety measures being taken on the ship?
faq.answer: Proficient health and safety measures are in place for guests to have a worry-free experience. It is mandatory for guests to have temperature checks and provide signed declarations. The ship crew also undergo regular checks. Sanitisation protocols are impeccably followed across cabins and common areas, air filters, coils and water supplies are checked regularly. There is also a 24x7 medical centre with qualified personnel and an isolation room.

faq.question: What destinations do Cordelia Cruise tour packages cover?
faq.answer: Cordelia Cruises currently offer journeys to stunning destinations around India, Sri Lanka and the Maldives. These include short 2 night 3 day trips as well as longer 5 night 6 day cruises. Choose from a selection of Thomas Cook’s Cordelia Cruise tour packages to Mumbai, Goa, Chennai, Jaffna, Colombo, Galle and Trincomalee.

faq.question: Is the ship wheelchair friendly?
faq.answer: Yes! The Empress provides wheelchair access and facilities to guests.

faq.question: What food considerations have been made for guests following specific diets?
faq.answer: Cordelia Cruises are considerate of the varied cultural values and needs of the Indian palate. No pork or beef is served on board, only halal meat and seafood. There is also a dedicated Jain cuisine service that guests can avail for all meals. All meals must be consumed at designated areas and not inside the cabins. No outside food or drinks are permitted on board.

faq.question: What are the inclusions and exclusions of the Cordelia Cruises tour package?
faq.answer: Packages do not include air fare and transfers to and from ship terminals, visa fare or travel insurance. Surcharges, shore excursions or on board purchases are not included either. Your package includes cabin fare, port taxes, meals and some of the entertainment and activities.

faq.question: Is there internet access on the cruise?
faq.answer: Wi-Fi packages are available for purchase once you book your cruise.

faq.question: What are the available payment options for on-board offerings?
faq.answer: Indian Rupees and US Dollar are the two accepted currencies on board. Once you embark, you will be given a key-card, that is a rechargeable card that you can put money into and swipe for all your spends. Any remaining value at the end of the cruise will be refunded back to you.

faq.question: What are some of the top recreational facilities offered on the cruise?
faq.answer: Cordelia Cruises offer a packed itinerary of recreation, leisure and entertainment. The spa is the number one spot to relax and unwind. Two swimming pools, one dedicated for kids, offer a chance to splash about while on tranquil sea waves. The Marquee Theatre always puts on a show, be it magic or live music, stand-up comedy or dramatic play. Other popular activities and experiences include the Casino, DJ Nights and Outdoor Movie Nights as well as activities like dance, Zumba, yoga and rock climbing.

Cordelia Cruise.Introduction: Embark on your very own adventure on the high seas with Cordelia Cruises, India’s only premium cruise liner. Providing luxurious, indulgent, rejuvenating, entertaining and thoroughly captivating journeys to some of the most spectacular shores around the country. A cruise vacation is all your favourite holiday experiences rolled into one, with everything from accommodation to dining and activities looked after as well! Once you hop aboard The Empress, the royal treatment begins. The iconic ship is an experienced seafarer, having previously sailed across the Bahamas and Caribbean. And now, she is here to take you around the stunning seas, ports and islands around the country, under the stewardship of Cordelia Cruises. Take your pick of the wondrous destinations currently on offer by Cordelia Cruises. Let your hair down on a sea-bound weekend off the shores of Mumbai, or all the way to Goa. Sail towards Diu or Kochi, for culturally enlightening cruise holidays. Go from boat to beach, on cruises that take you to the spectacular Lakshadweep Islands and Male, Maldives. While the cruise tours around Sri Lanka’s Colombo, Galle and Trincomalee offer the perfect blend of tropical bliss and urban buzz. Thomas Cook brings you the best Cordelia Cruises Tour Packages for the cruise vacation of your choice, starting at just INR 25,794 per person. Our travel experts highly recommend Cordelia Cruises for your next escapade and have curated all-encompassing packages that ensure you have the most amazing time. Whether you’re looking to plan a big family trip or a girls trip, a romantic holiday for two or a company offsite. Whatever be your holiday type – girls trip, fam-jam, school reunion etc., one thing is for sure: you’ll get to experience travel in a completely unique way. Yet, in a way that travellers have been for centuries of history. This is how the world was explored, new places were discovered, cultures came to be. Now, you can sample this feeling as you roam aboard the cruise liner, the marvellous Empress. Where a journey of a lifetime awaits! One that you can now have as often as you’d like, with Thomas Cook’s amazing Cordelia Cruise tour packages. Cruises are available throughout the year, to select destinations based on the climate and seasonal possibilities. So whenever you decide that it’s time to pack up and go on a holiday, you should really consider a cruise holiday. Something extraordinary and memorable for you to experience and cherish, as you sail through life.
Cordelia Cruise.Highlights of Cordelia Cruise: Waking up to new, spectacular views as you glide across the vast expanse of the ocean is the first of many highlight experiences. Discover a ship that’s full of comfort, excitement, pleasure, relaxation and all-round engagement. You’ll never have a dull moment! In between sunrises and sunsets, you can enjoy it all. A magnificent stay in cosy, comfortable accommodation. Gourmet on-board dining including global fare as well as various Indian specialties and diets. Exceptional entertainment and activities for all ages. Book a Cordelia Cruise packages and it’s anchor-free enjoyment all the way. Accommodation Your home on the sea promises land comforts. Cordelia Cruises offer five cabin options, from budget-friendly interior rooms to lovely ocean view rooms and wonderful balcony rooms that open out onto your own private lookout point. As well as gorgeous suites and the special Chairman’s Suite, with luxurious state-of-the-art facilities as well as premium access to in-room dining and the Chef’s table experience. Whether you spend two nights on board or five, your cabin offers the perfect cosy bit of the ship to call your own. Where you can relax, spend time with only the view, enjoy the world-class amenities and feel totally refreshed for your next adventure out on the decks. Dining Dining aboard the Empress is always a delight. The restaurants and menus have been specially curated for the variable Indian palate, with cultural and dietary considerations taken into account. Anytime is a good time to visit the All Day Dining food court, where buffet food stations called Essence of India, Far Eastern Kadhai, Sugar & Spice, The International Grill, Hot Clay Tandoor, Kettle & Bun, Jain Haven, Vegetarian Bang Street Food and The Cafe, offer all imaginable global and local favourites. While the two specialty restaurants — The Waterfront and Chopstix — offer unique dining experiences. Perfect for families, groups and couples too. The Waterfront for its charming decor and gourmet servings, and Chopstix for a grand Pan-Asian affair that one always has the appetite for. For those seeking a more exclusive affair, make advanced reservations for a seat at the Chef’s Table, where you will be served a specially crafted menu. Entertainment The Empress knows how to entertain. Day or night, the fun never stops on a cruise vacation. With the wind in your sails, explore every avenue of excitement and thrill that Cordelia Cruises has on board. Make your way to the fabulous casino and play your luck. Soak in a sensational performance at The Marquee theatre. Broadway or Burlesque take your pick. Other nights, music performances turn the ocean into the hippest dance floor, and outdoor movie nights let you enjoy cinematic moments under starry skies. Meanwhile, spend your days rejuvenating at the spa, salon or fitness centre. Splash about in the swimming pool or dive into the quiet of a book at the reading zone. Go shopping on board the ship or plan your shore excursions so that you can make the most of your time on land. Cordelia Cruises also offer special activities for children, like a kids pool and rock climbing wall. Then there is the Cordelia Academy, where they are looked after with plenty of care, engaging activities and games.
Cordelia Cruise.Cordelia Cruise Destinations: Mumbai Nothing shines brighter on the coastline of the Arabian Sea, than India’s biggest metropolis, Mumbai. Cordelia Cruise tour packages take you to and from this vibrant cultural city, giving you the chance to explore everything from Colonial monuments to the historic street food. And then there’s everything from promenades and beaches to shopping centres, temples, galleries and museums, to visit too. The maximum city is always a total blast. Goa Cruise to the country’s favourite coastal destination, Goa. Where your state of mind switches to holiday mode instantly, and instinctively. Spend your shore time exploring the old town and its Colonial heritage. Visit beach after beach, they all have a charm of their own. Eat! Soak in as much of the coastal flavours as you like. Celebrate and party, the way only Goa can make you. This is where your sea escapade comes full circle. Lakshadweep A pleasure filled sail aboard Cordelia Cruises, that takes you all the way to the breathtaking Lakshadweep Islands off the coast of Kerala. Where white sand beaches welcome you, crystal blue waters beckon you, the local folk culture will charm you and the natural serenity will leave you enchanted and inspired. Don’t miss out on activities like kayaking, snorkelling and scuba diving, explore the underwater life just as much as you are life up on sea. An amazing experience for families and couples alike. Chennai Experience South Indian culture at its finest, in the beautiful blend of old and new that is Chennai. Sample its traditional filter coffee, marvel at its historic St. George Fort. Catch a sunset on the beach, you have a few to choose from. Or make your way for a pilgrimage to one of the bigger temples in the city. Sri Lanka Cordelia Cruise packages are a great way to visit Sri Lanka. Witness its beautiful port cities. The picturesque beach town of Galle has everything from monuments to museums, surfing to the stunning Japanese Peace Pagoda. The capital city of Colombo is a colourful cosmopolitan, where you can get a refill on your favourite urban pleasures, from shopping to fancy dining. Jaffna and Trincomalee are the other two ports you could visit, both offering a different perspective of Sri Lanka. One that’s bustling with culture treasures, and the other that’s full of natural wonders.
Cordelia Cruise.Important Tips For Your Cruise Experienc: Thanks for booking your Cordelia Cruise holiday with us. May we update you that this is Cordelia Cruise’s first sailing in Indian waters. This India sailing is a value for money product. Since cruising is a truly different holiday experience, it is important that we brief you on a few important points: 1. Arrival at Boarding Point: All guests should arrive at the Green Gate Ballard Pier by 2pm on the boarding day. As there is no seating area at the Green Gate, please that you will be required to queue up for embarkation process. Pl keep handy your RT-PCR negative certificate (taken within 72 hours of boarding) along with your Cruise Voucher Mandatory is also the original government identification card that you had provided during booking process Carry your debit/credit cards as cash payments are not permitted on the cruise Outside food, beverages and alcohol is not allowed and must not be carried into the ship We recommend that you also carry your sea-sickness medication/patches Hence please carry your international adaptor (American plug 110V) Please do not carry kettle, cooking equipment on-board as this is not permitted Don’t forget to bring with you your personal toiletries * Note: Vaccine certificate is not required for boarding the cruise 2. Onboard your cruise: You will be sailing in international waters and hence your sim card will not be operational. Hence we recommend that you purchase a wifi card that is available onboard. Shore excursions (eg in Goa and Lakshadweep) can only be purchased through the cruise shore excursion desk – onboard Passengers who do not purchase the shore excursions mentioned above will not be allowed to disembark To ensure your health and safety, please maintain social distancing at all times, especially at the reception, dining and entertainments areas. In case of any assistance onboard, please reach out to the Cruise Help Desk at Reception. Do be assured that we have a special Thomas Cook-SOTC Tour Manger onboard. May we request you to take care while use of toilet facilities on the cruise. Please avoid disposing items like your mask, etc. in the toilets as this would result in drainage concerns 3. Disembarkation: Please clear all your pending bills the day before disembarkation Please have your breakfast and be in readiness for disembarkation by 10am on the last date of your cruise. Please ensure you do not take back any of the ship’s property when you disembark. A check is done and the cost will be debited to you.

Kumarakom.Introduction: Kumarakom is unlike any other city in Kerala and has its own unique charm and essence. It is without a doubt one of the most visited cities in Gods own Country aka Kerala. In addition to its gorgeous and unmatched backwaters, the city is known for its divine ambience, serene atmosphere and pristine outdoors. This travel guide will help you book the ultimate Kumarakom tour package which you can customize, or book as offered. Kumarakom Tour PackagesTour Packages NameDurationPriceHoliday ThemeVibrant Kerala5 Nights 6 DaysRs. ₹20 000Family, SterlingPleasant Kerala 5 Nights 6 DaysRs. ₹22 800FamilyScenic Kerala4 Nights 5 DaysRs. ₹24 600FamilyColours Of Kerala8 Nights 9 DaysRs. ₹41 800Sterling, FamilyGlimpses of Kerala with Taj Hotels4 Nights 5 DaysRs. ₹45 000Family, Luxury, Taj HotelThe highlight of your time in Kumarakom will be your stay in one of the luxurious houseboats houses on the breathtaking backwater lagoons that surround this gorgeous city. Houseboats are fun for the whole family and if you are on a romantic getaway, you can even the book the entire thing for yourself. In addition to its endless and timeless beauty, Kumarakom has immense historical and religious significance. In fact, the origins of its name can be traced back to a deity known as Kumaran’s Akam who resides in the city’s oldest temple known as Kumaran. The city is also blessed with an abundance of fertile land which is used to grow staples such as rice and delicious coconuts that we all love and crave on a hot summers afternoon. The year 1874 saw the arrival of an English farmers knows as Alfred George Baker who claimed to stagger 500 acres of land surrounding the Vembanad Lake and unleashed the true agriculture potential of the region. Thus, marking a significant landmark in the history of this humble city. Vembanad lake is the largest lakes in the Kerala and Kumarakom is made up of little islands perched alongside the lake. The lake is the life source of the city and centre of all activities. It is surrounded by lush green forests and beautiful landscapes. The endless rows on coconut trees give the city a tropical touch, and diverse fauna and flora just add that something extra to this already great city. Kumarakom bird sanctuary is a haven for bird watchers and each year welcomes numerous migratory birds from all over the world. The 15-acre sanctuary is home to numerous species including but not limited to the Siberian Crane, Kingfishers, Moorhens, Ducks, Waterfowls, Heron, Egret etc. But if w bird watching is not your thing, then you can always head out to the stunning Aruvikkuzhi waterfall which is surrounded by many rubber plantations. This is an ideal place to unleash your inner photographer as there are many beautiful landscapes just waiting to be captured. The museums offer great insights into the history of the city and is home to some great exhibits and displays.

Pondicherry.Introduction: Are you wondering where to visit for your next holiday? If yes, then you may consider getting one of the Pondicherry packages and heading to this amazing colonial town in South India. Pondicherry is one of the most beautiful holiday destinations in India. It has some gorgeous beaches, beautiful monuments and quaint resorts that allow you to have the perfect vacation. Steeped in colonial history, Pondicherry has its own charm and charisma. It is a popular destination for global tourists as well. Hence, when you go on a Pondicherry tour, you get to have a unique holiday, which is quite different from other Indian holidays. Many exciting Pondicherry tour packages help you to explore the place wholesomely. You should book one of the best Pondicherry vacation packages and head to the city for the best vacation ever! The packages are all-inclusive and make it very easy for you to focus on the Pondicherry tourist attractions, while the Pondicherry holiday packages take care of all the reservations and bookings. You can book one of the many Pondicherry tour packages based on your preferences, budget and time frame. The various Pondicherry travel packages are flexible and can be customised per your needs. Pondicherry.Most Popular Pondicherry Tour Packages: Tour Packages NameDurationPriceBest of Tamil Nadu and Karnataka Tour9 Nights 10 DaysRs. ₹50 700Chennai, Tirupati, Kanchipuram, Pondicherry with Mahabalipuram- Tamil Nadu4 Nights 5 DaysRs. ₹24 700Pondicherry Mahabalipuram with Trichy and Madurai - Tamil Nadu5 Nights 6 DaysRs. ₹29 000Madurai Trichy Tanjore Pondicherry and Tirupati Tour - Tamil Nadu7 Nights 8 DaysRs. ₹46 100Dakshin Bharat Darshan Yatra9 Nights 10 DaysRs. ₹69 300
Pondicherry.Best Time to Visit in Pondicherry: Planning your Pondicherry trip and wondering when to go? The best time to visit Pondicherry is during the winter months, though each season offers a unique experience. To help you decide, here’s a seasonal breakdown to make the most of your Pondicherry tour packages. Winter (December to March) Winter is the ideal season to book your Pondicherry tour packages. The weather is pleasant, with mild temperatures and low humidity, making it perfect for sightseeing and exploring the city's beaches and attractions. As this is peak season, be sure to book your Pondicherry trip packages early to secure the best deals. Summer (April to June) Summer in Pondicherry is hot and humid, with temperatures often exceeding 40°C. While it's not the most popular time for tourists, you can find Pondicherry packages at discounted rates. If you're planning a quick getaway or a one-day tour, this off-season can be an affordable option. Monsoon (June to September) Monsoon brings heavy rainfall and high tides, making some areas of Pondicherry difficult to access. However, if you enjoy the charm of a rain-soaked city, this is a unique time to visit. Monsoon Pondicherry tours can be budget-friendly, offering you a different perspective of the city's vibrant culture. Pre-Winter (October to November) As the rains recede, Pondicherry becomes more inviting. The pre-winter period is a great time to explore the city’s attractions, enjoy local cuisine, and soak in the serene atmosphere. Consider booking your Pondicherry vacation packages during this period for a peaceful and memorable experience.
Pondicherry.How to reach Pondicherry?: There are multiple ways to reach Pondicherry. You can opt to fly in, take a train or come by road. When you book the Pondicherry vacation packages, the travel options may be included. Opt for a package that has a suitable travel option. Here are more details about how to reach Pondicherry: By Air: For international or domestic travelers, the nearest major airport is in Chennai, just 4 hours away from Pondicherry. While Pondicherry has a domestic airport, flights may not be frequent, making Chennai a convenient choice. Consider Pondicherry tour packages that include air travel from Chennai, or nearby airports like Bangalore and Salem. By Rail: Pondicherry’s railway station connects the city to major Indian towns, ensuring easy access for all. Many Pondicherry packages include train travel, allowing you to enjoy scenic views while saving money. It's a great way to experience Pondicherry tourism. By Road: Traveling by road is another popular option, with well-maintained highways and a robust bus network from cities like Chennai, Coimbatore, and Bangalore. Whether by bus or your own vehicle, road travel offers flexibility during your Pondicherry tours.
Pondicherry.Top Places to Visit in Pondicherry: There are some very nice places to visit in Pondicherry. Ensure you get one of the best Pondicherry holiday packages that take you to the most iconic places in the city, some of which are: Auroville Auroville is a landmark of the city and also one of the best places in Pondicherry. It is the beacon of Pondicherry tourism and one of the main reasons why a majority of the visitors come on their Pondicherry tours. The mini township has a community that welcomes people from all races, religions and castes to live in harmony. Regular meditation sessions happen and it is an important centre for spirituality. Most of the Pondicherry trip packages include a tour of Auroville, so don't miss out on it. Shri Aurobindo Ghosh Ashram Next on the list of Pondicherry tourist attractions is the Shri Aurobindo Ashram. It used to be the house of Aurobindo Ghosh, who was an Indian freedom fighter turned spiritual leader. He founded this ashram over a century ago and lived in it with his disciples. It is a place of worship, meditation and spirituality. If you are on a Pondicherry tour, ensure you visit this tranquil place that is one of the best places to visit in Pondicherry. The Pondicherry Museum If you are fascinated by arts and history, the Pondicherry Museum is your go-to place. After you get one of the best trip to pondy and reach the city, head over to this amazing museum that has artefacts dating back to the Pallava and Chola Dynasties. It is a very significant place and should be a definite addition to your Pondicherry sightseeing package. La Maison Rose After getting the Pondicherry packages and reaching the city, you will notice a very European vibe in there. This is because Pondicherry was a French colony for many years. You will still find a very strong French connection in the town. If you wish to experience the true flavours of French architecture, head over to La Maison Rose. It is a classic French-style house that has now been transformed into a shopping arena. Book the best Pondicherry holiday packages and experience unique structures like these on your Pondicherry tour and come back with a refreshed mind. Eglise De Notre Dame Des Anges If you book the Pondicherry family holiday packages and reach the city, you have to visit one of the most beautiful churches in the area, the Eglise De Notre Dame Des Anges. It is quaint, aesthetic and extremely peaceful. It is one of the prettiest Pondicherry places to visit, so don't skip it.
Pondicherry.Best Beaches in Pondicherry: Pondicherry is known for its beautiful beaches. Here are the most popular ones: Paradise Beach Paradise Beach is one of the most famous beaches of Pondicherry. It is also one of the cleanest beaches with crystal clear water. You can spend a whole day here and feel rejuvenated. Rock Beach Rock Beach is another mesmerising beach in the heart of the city. Many tourists agree that it is among the best places in Pondicherry. You can enjoy some majestic sunsets at this beach and make some great memories with your loved ones. Serenity Beach As the name suggests, this beach is serene and extremely beautiful. It has soft, white sand and gushing waves, which make it the ideal beach destination. Book the Pondicherry trip packages and reach this beach to have the best beach experience. Book the best Pondicherry trip packages and visit these amazing beaches to have a wholesome and soul-enriching experience.
Pondicherry.Interesting Things to do in Pondicherry: There are many interesting things to do in Pondicherry. When you book the best Pondicherry trip packages and reach the city, make sure you try out the following: Take a walking tour The real way to experience the charm of a city like Pondicherry is to take a walking tour. There are many interesting walking tours available that are presided by local experts, often citizens who have lived there for years. You can meet like-minded tourists from around the world and explore the Pondicherry tourist places like never before. Make this a part of your Pondicherry travel plan and have the most unique experience. See the famous places After booking one of the Pondicherry trip packages and arriving at the city, you must go for a Pondicherry city tour. This may sound basic, but Pondicherry is a place that needs to be seen! While you can relax at your resort, going out and exploring the Pondicherry tourist attractions will add a lot of charm to your trip. Make a proper Pondicherry itinerary that includes a wholesome Pondicherry sightseeing package so that you can see a whole lot of cool things in the city. Speak to the locals Do you know what makes Pondicherry such a special place? It is the friendly attitude of the locals. This is why people from all across the globe book the Pondicherry trip packages and come here. When you arrive in the city for your Pondicherry trip, start speaking to the locals. You will pick up some very interesting anecdotes about the history and culture of the place and it will be very rewarding. Spend time at Auroville As mentioned above, Auroville is the beacon of Pondicherry. Hence, after getting the Pondicherry trip packages and reaching the city, head over to the beautiful township of Auroville. Mingle with the other people staying there and soak in the spiritual air around. You can sit and meditate or get mesmerised by the architecture. Try the authentic French food Pondicherry was a French colony till the 1950s. Hence, there is a very strong French presence in the city. Get the best Pondicherry tour packages and visit the place to eat some of the best French food in India. There are cafes and restaurants set up by French people who continue to live here. Hence, you will get authentic French cuisine that is absolutely delicious and a very important part of the life of Pondicherry. As you can see, a Pondicherry trip can be very wholesome, thanks to the variety of things to do as part of the Pondicherry travel. Book one of the many Pondicherry trip packages, reach the city and experience its rich and glorious heritage.
Pondicherry.What to Eat in Pondicherry?: There are many options when it comes to the food of Pondicherry. Many of the Pondicherry travel packages include meals, but some don't. Whether you are eating at the hotel as part of the or you want to explore the local cuisine, you will find ample chances to eat delicious food when you are on your Pondicherry tour. Here’s a closer look at the famous foods to try on your tour of Pondicherry: French cuisine When you go on a Pondicherry tour, you will find many French restaurants, owing to the colonial past of the town. There is a significant French settlement here and many of them run authentic French restaurants. Try out the awesome, and most authentic French dishes and come back with a happy heart and stomach. Cafe meals and drinks Another interesting thing to note on your Pondicherry tour is that there are many cafes all around the city. You will find them scattered around the best tourist places in Pondicherry. The cafe culture here is quite strong and these little cafes serve some of the best meals and beverages. Whether you want to make a quick coffee stop or want to enjoy a relaxing meal, you won't be disappointed when you visit one of the many beautiful cafes of Pondicherry. Traditional, local dishes India is a vast nation with a diverse culture. This shows in its food too. So, when you go on a Pondicherry tour, ensure you try the local cuisine. There are lovely seafood markets near the fishing villages from where you can buy fresh seafood. Hire a Pondicherry tour guide and ask him to take you to such a place. Alternatively, you can also find the best local food in numerous restaurants and small eateries. From the freshest fish curries to steaming hot idlis, the local food won't disappoint you in Pondicherry. Even if your Pondicherry travel packages have food provisions at the hotels, step out to have a few local meals and savour the deliciousness. Global Cuisine Since the Pondicherry travel packages are also popular among foreign tourists, a lot of global travellers come to this part of India every day. Keeping their preferences in mind, there are a lot of global food options available in Pondicherry. From traditional continental dishes to the best of Asian cuisine, you will find a huge selection of food to choose from when you go on your tour to Pondicherry. Food is a very essential part of any holiday and you must not compromise on it when you go on your Pondicherry trip. Book the best Pondicherry travel packages to not only see the best places to visit in Pondicherry but also to savour some of the best food in the country.
Pondicherry.What to Shop in Pondicherry?: There are many interesting things to shop for in Pondicherry. Some of the best items include: Items from Auroville Auroville is one of the most famous tourist places in Pondicherry. Apart from its spiritual appeal, the place also has a wonderful shopping unit where you can find a variety of unique items. From handmade soaps to incense sticks, from handloom yarns to organic food packets, you will have a lot to choose from. Pottery The pottery of Pondicherry is very famous. You can get some beautiful pottery items in the local bazaars as well as the big boutiques. Check them out and be amazed! Silk sarees The silk of South India is very famous and so you get some of the best silk sarees in Pondicherry. Many markets and shops are selling some of the most gorgeous silk sarees at very reasonable prices. Pick them up and bring them back for yourself or as gifts for your loved ones. Remember to bargain for the sarees, especially at the local markets, as you can get some great deals there. There is a large variety of things that you can buy from the stores in Pondicherry. Keep picking up interesting items when you visit the best places to visit in Pondicherry and have a fun adventure.
Pondicherry.Interesting Facts about Pondicherry: Listed below are some of the most interesting facts about Pondicherry. Take a look and you will be all the more excited about travelling to this amazing travel destination which is very accessible and easy to reach: Was a French settlement Very interestingly, Pondicherry was a French colony till as late as 1954, after which it became an official part of India. Today, it is a Union Territory and a very integral part of the Republic of India. There are two parts of the town The French culture and influence is still very strongly present in Pondicherry. The city is divided into two parts, the French Quarter and the Indian Quarter. The former has awesome quaint, European architecture and the latter has a more local tone and flavour. Rich in wildlife attractions You will find many wildlife sanctuaries and parks in Pondicherry. It is also home to many migratory birds, rare animal and insect species and beautiful butterflies. Extremely spiritual And last but not least, Pondicherry is steeped in Spirituality. The city has many meditation centres, ashrams, temples, churches and spiritual spots where you can connect with the universe and find peace and happiness. Make a good Pondicherry tour itinerary and head over to this amazing tourist destination in South India. You will find many exciting Pondicherry trip packages to choose from, so get the best one and start your vacation without any delay.

Yas Island.Introduction: Yas Island is an island in U.A.E. It is a multi-purpose, leisure destination that is a must visit in U.A.E. Yas Island packages will make a perfect a tourist guide for you. Yas Islands has many attractions that are a must visit. All the travellers visit Abu Dhabi. Yas Island has Yas Marina Circuit that hosts the final formula F1 race and it also hosted Abu Dhabi Grand Pix. Ferrari World Abu Dhabi, Yas Water world, Yas Beach, Musical Fountain, Warner Bros World Abu Dhabi. Ferrari World Abu Dhabi is a great amusement park which is the first ever branded theme park. Formula Rossa is the world’s fastest roller coaster running at a speed of 149mph. It is an award-winning amusement park. Yas Waterworld is the world’s second-best waterpark in the world. It has more than forty water rides and attraction, some of them being Rush Rider, Bubbles ride. Bandit Bomber is the longest roller coaster which is about 550 meters long. This water park receives many awards and recognition. It has been built on the story of a brave girl called Dana who went out to search for a pearl that she believed would bring prosperity to her village. Warner Bros World Abu Dhabi will be opened in the year 2018 near Ferrari world and Waterpark. It is going to have 6 theme lands. These different theme lands will be based on the DC super heroes Superman, Batman, Looney Tunes and Hanna-Barbera. Warner Bros in Yas Island is going to be the 3rd Warner Bros themed park. SeaWorld Abu Dhabi is going to be the first sea world throughout the globe without orcas. It is yet to open in the year 2020. It is a marine life theme park and this park is going to the first one to be without killer whales. Yas Marina Circuit held formula F1 final races. The centre of the circuit has Marina hotel that gives a view of the circuit. There are many packages available to get the adrenaline rush: Driver Experience, Passenger experience, private driving tuition and racing license course. Yas Marina is the first hotel constructed on the F1 circuit. Yas Beach is a long beach on the Yas Island. It has yoga sessions on the beach. Many sport activities like kayaking, banana rides, jet skis. It is great place to enjoy the sun, the beach, drinks. faq.question: What is the best time to visit Bali?
faq.answer: The best time to visit Bali is between April and October. Since this is the dry season, you will be able to explore more places and many more activities will be available for your perusal.

faq.question: How many days are required for a Bali trip?
faq.answer: If this is your first Bali trip, you must plan at least 6 nights and 7 days of vacation.

faq.question: When is the tourist off-season in Bali?
faq.answer: The tourist off-season is during the monsoon months of July, August, December, and early January.

faq.question: What is the currency of Bali?
faq.answer: The currency of Bali is the Indonesian Rupiah.

faq.question: Are Indian nationals granted a visa on arrival in Bali?
faq.answer: Yes, Indian nationals are granted a visa on arrival in Bali.

faq.question: Can I ride a scooter with my Indian driver's license in Bali?
faq.answer: Along with an Indian driver's license, one would also need an International Driver's Permit to be able to ride a scooter in Bali.

faq.question: How far is Ubud from the airport?
faq.answer: Ubud is 40 km away from the Bali airport, also known as the Ngurah Rai International Airport. By road, this should take you around 40 minutes.

faq.question: What is the distance between Ubud and Genting?
faq.answer: The distance between Ubud, Bali, and Genting, Malaysia is 2,900 km.

faq.question: How far is Seminyak from the airport?
faq.answer: Seminyak is around 10 km (or 30 minutes) away from the Ngurah Rai International Airport.

faq.question: How far is Mount Batur from the airport?
faq.answer: By road, Mount Batur is around 75 km away from the Denpasar airport.

faq.question: Is it safe to travel to Bali?
faq.answer: Even for female travelers planning a Bali trip, it is a relatively safe vacation destination. Bali has a low rate of violent crime. As a visitor, you should exercise caution when crossing the street because there are many motorbikes there. Swimmers should exercise caution because riptides make some beaches unsafe for swimming. Avoid being a victim of fraud and pickpockets. Although earthquakes, tsunamis, and volcanoes are always a possibility, Bali has not yet experienced any severe destruction. In this popular tourist destination, everything continues as usual.

faq.question: What should you carry with you on a Bali trip?
faq.answer: The following is a list of necessities you need to carry on your Bali trip: For the beach and popular party places, wear comfortable, light attire. When visiting religious sites, wear covered, slightly more conservative attire. In Bali, sandals or flip-flops are both suitable footwear options. You'll need hiking boots for the hike up Mount Batur and other outings. Basic medications should be included in a first aid pack. Do not forget to bring your passport, visa, and copies of them, as well as your travel insurance documents and hotel reservation confirmations. If you want to take advantage of Bali's widespread free Wi-Fi in hotels and cafés, bring your devices, such as tablets, e-readers, or laptops.

faq.answer: Bali is the ideal vacation spot if you want to save money while still having a good time. Yes, you can arrange a budget-friendly Bali tour packages!
