Bali.About Bali: Enchanting mornings and beautiful sunrises that would make you want to get up during the early hours of the morning... Treks through the lush green forests that would make you explore more and more of nature... Clear blue waters that would make you take just one more dip in the sea... And, mesmerizing sunsets that would make you want to stay back for just another evening... just another day... Well, this is Bali Tourism for you, and for all of us! If you're a backpacker, surfing enthusiast, or diver- we have something of interest for you! If you're simply looking for a relaxing holiday with your friends, family or loved ones- well, Thomas Cook India has something for you, too! We may be yet to discover the kind of Bali trip package that would suit you, but we already have discovered one thing- Bali is an island you will like, for both its hippy vibe and spiritual charm. You can spend your evenings peacefully in one (or some) of the many temples or monasteries there or can rejuvenate your spirits by spending time at the many beachside shacks and cafes. So, in order to enjoy your vacation to the fullest, make sure to look for Bali packages that cover all your vacation requirements.
Bali.Best time to visit Bali: The ideal time for your trip to Bali depends on what you're seeking—either colorful festivals, good weather, or economical travel. For a perfect combination of sunshine, adventure, and cultural exposure, organise your Bali tour between April and October, which is the dry season. During this time, the sky is clear, there is minimal humidity, and you can surf, trek, and explore Bali's beautiful beaches. Also, it's the ideal time for sightseeing without sudden rain spoiling your plans. If you want fewer crowds and cheaper Bali tour packages, go during the shoulder season: May, June, September, and early December. The weather is still excellent, but the hotels and airfares are cheaper, and you won't have to experience the extreme tourist rush. This is perfect if you desire to see Bali in a less crowded environment. Conversely, if you want significant discounts on Bali packages, the wet season (November to March) could be a possibility. Although you might have to endure regular rain showers, Bali's green landscapes appear even more beautiful. It's an ideal time for spa retreats, temple explorations, and indulging in Bali's hospitality without shelling out too much money. For an unforgettable vacation with Bali packages, select the season that best fits your budget and travel style!
Bali.How to Reach Bali: The easiest way to reach Bali, Indonesia is by flight. Since Bali is the most popular tourist destination of Indonesia, its airport is connected to several major airports of the world. Indian cities like Delhi, Mumbai, Kolkata, Chennai, Hyderabad and Bangalore. In what will be a sigh of relief for all our customers, Indian tourists do not need to apply for a visa to travel to Bali. Indonesia provides visa on arrival for Indian tourists for a duration of up to 30 days from the date of travel. However, it is mandatory to carry your passport which must be valid for at least 6 months from the day of your arrival in Bali. Do note that some Bali packages may offer the feature of included or optional flights. However, if you don’t want the inclusion of transportation, you can opt for Bali packages that don’t feature transport.
Bali.Best Places to Visit in Bali: Sacred Monkey Forest:The Sacred Monkey Forest is a monkey sanctuary in Ubud, Balinese. It is home to the grey long-tailed macaques, and this forest is one of the most popular tourist spots in the country. This forest serves multiple purposes- visitors can experience playful, carefree monkeys in their natural habitat while simultaneously enjoying the beauty of nature along paths lined with trees. Our offer on Bali tour packages features the top places, attractions, sightseeing and other activities.Here are some of the best places to visit in Bali. Bali Safari Park Tegenungan Waterfall Nusa Penida Island Tanah Lot Temple Bali Swing Besakih Temple Ulun Danu Beratan Temple Bali Safari Park: Bali Safari Park is a great spot for families to visit and enjoy with their children. The 40 hectare- safari park is one of the biggest animal theme parks in Bali. This open zoo is different, and a must-visit for children as well as adults because this experience is as real as it gets. Animals- like orangutans, hippos, lions, zebras and white tigers- can be spotted roaming freely in large open spaces, without any cages in sight. You can enjoy this site along with several other prominent tourist attractions during your trip by going with the Bali packages offered on our website. Tegenungan Waterfall: This mesmerizing waterfall is located amidst luscious green forests, not too far from Ubud. The best part about the waterfall is that at the bottom of it is a natural pool where visitors can take a dip. The Tegenungan Waterfall is best suited for visits during the wet season, from October to March. Look for Bali tour packages that include a visit to this site under sightseeing. Nusa Penida Island: Located in south-eastern Bali, the island of Nusa Penida is a place that will take your breath away. Oh, and after you manage to catch it again, it just wouldn’t let you let go of your camera- it is that photogenic. This place does not have a lot of options for overnight stays, so most tourists prefer to make a day trip. The Nusa Penida Island is also a famous spot for snorkeling and scuba diving. Some Bali tour packages may feature a short trip to this island as a standard inclusion. Tanah Lot Temple: This 15th century temple is located on a rocky top, surrounded with crashing waves. The highly revered Tanah Lot is a Hindu Temple, popular not just for the calm one is sure to experience in the temple grounds, but also for the sunset views it has to offer. This, dear friends, is just the perfect place to rejuvenate your spirit and soul. Bali Swing: Remember the tiny FOMO feeling when you saw your college friend get that perfect picture at that photogenic swing surrounded by just the sea? Well, here’s your chance to do that and more! Head over to the Bali Swing near Ubud rice terraces, and just strike a pose! Not once, not twice but several times as there are three different levels of swings available for you to just strap on and swing away! Not just the Bali Swing, visit all the popular tourist attractions in this Indonesian province with our specially designed Bali travel packages. Besakih Temple: The ‘Mother Temple’ of Bali, officially called the Besaikh or Pura Besakih temple is the most famous temple complex in Bali. This temple is exquisite because of its vibe and its location- it rests on the slopes of Mount Agung. Surrounded by picturesque landscape of hills, streams and endless rice fields, the Mother Temple has shrines dedicated to multiple Hindu deities. Ulun Danu Beratan Temple: The 17th century Ulun Danu Beratan Temple is dedicated to the Hindu Trinity of Brahma, Vishnu and Shiva, as well as the Lake Goddess, Dewi Danu. The Gates of Heaven at Lempuyang Temple When you're looking for that Instagram-worthy shot on your Bali vacation, Lempuyang Temple's Gates of Heaven is a must-see. With the stunning Mount Agung in the background, this holy place is among Bali's most stunning locations. It's not all about the photo op—the temple compound is steeped in spirituality and is worth a visit on most Bali tour packages. Tukad Cepung Waterfall For a mystical experience, go to Tukad Cepung Waterfall, a beautiful secret gem hidden inside a cave. Sunlight passing through the rock formations provides an ethereal glow, making this a sight not to miss on your Bali tour. A brief jungle trek brings you to this hypnotic waterfall, where you can have a cool dip on your trip with Bali packages. Bamboo Forest, Penglipuran Walk into a fantasy realm at the Bamboo Forest in Penglipuran Village. Famous for its tranquil loveliness, this secluded forest is a refuge from the normal tourist trails. It's ideal for a quiet walk and an absolute must on any cultural Bali packages. Sidemen Valley If you prefer to get away from the throngs, go to Sidemen Valley, a serene sanctuary of rice fields, green hills, and typical Balinese villages. It's a good destination to witness genuine Balinese culture amid scenic strolls. This idyllic location is usually part of alternative Bali tour packages. Handara Gate Handara Gate is a true hidden gem tucked away in Bedugul. This stunning entrance to a golf resort has transformed into one of Bali’s most beloved photo spots. With misty mountains and vibrant greenery all around, the gate perfectly captures the essence of Balinese architecture. It’s no wonder that many Bali packages make a point to include a visit here, especially for those travellers eager to discover the quieter, picturesque side of Bali.
Bali.Foods to Eat in Bali: Alongside browsing through available Bali packages, make sure to also look into the exciting regional cuisine. One of the best experiences about visiting exotic places like Bali is the experience related to trying the local cuisines there. The amazing flavour made from blending local spices used through generations is all the reason to try local dishes in Balinese cafes and restaurants, leaving behind (at least for a while) the conventional food we all are so used to eating! Here’s what you must try dishesh in Bali: Fried/Smoked Duck Local Ice cream Barbeque Food Jimbaran Seafood Balinese Kopi Fried/Smoked Duck: Most menu cards will have Bebek Goreng (Fried Duck) or BebekBetutu (Smoked Duck) as their specialties. If you spot them, well, we are telling you, you must try them! Fried Duck is the conventional seasoned, boiled or steamed deep fried duck. Smoked Duck, on the other hand, is smoked for over 24 hours after being seasoned with local spices. Both are traditional Balinese dishes served with rice, vegetables and dips. Local Ice cream: If you thought Bali was just about beaches and swimming and temples, you thought wrong. Bali is also about food- particularly about ice cream! Get ready to spot several small gelatos or cafes that produce their very own, very Balinese ice cream. Once there, make sure you try their local flavours like the red dragon fruit, lemongrass or lychee! There’ll be no regrets, we assure you. Enjoy your dream vacation in Bali with your spouse by opting for the honeymoon special Bali trip packages on our website. Barbeque Food: Do you remember reading novels or watching movies where a group of friends just sit at the beach, around the fire and munch of barbequed snacks? Well, this can now very well be a scene out of your life, for the smell of charcoal-grilled meat is so tempting- something we must not ignore and indulge in! What makes Balinese barbeque food stand out from others is its seamless blend of sweet, savoury and slightly smoky taste with the perfect balance of meat and spices. So, make sure to try the Balinese barbecue food during your Bali tour. Jimbaran Seafood: This category of food is available at all the shacks along the Jimbaran beach. Cafes and shacks that specialize in Jimbaran seafood let their customers pick the sea food either from the a la carte menu or offer pre-set meals for more than two people. Balinese Kopi: What is even a vacation without some relaxing coffee? The Balinese Kopi, or coffee, is famous and for all the right reasons- it blends coffee grown in fertile volcanic lands. The stalls on main roads, or on beaches offer varieties of coffees- hot and cold. Let us handle the aspect of meals alongside accommodations, sightseeing, flights, etc., while you enjoy your dream vacation by going with Bali travel packages offered by Thomas Cook.
Bali.Things to Do in Bali: Bali, often referred to as the "Island of the Gods," is a mesmerizing destination known for its stunning landscapes, vibrant culture, and diverse range of activities. Whether you're seeking adventure, relaxation, or cultural immersion, Bali has something for everyone. Here's a list of things to do in Bali to make the most of your visit: White Water Rafting in Ayung River Surfing in Padang Padang and Kuta Join a Balinese cooking class Sunrise Trek to Mount Batur Bathe in the Pura TirtaEmpul Temple Museum hopping Watch and enjoy a traditional dance Explore the Artisans Village White Water Rafting in Ayung River: The Ayung River near Ubud is Bali’s longest and largest rapids. Just thinking about rafting in Ayung River is giving us the thrills! To add a big and red cherry on the cake, let us also tell you that the Ayung River is set in a beautiful backdrop of wild, unspoiled jungle, scenic rice fields and towering gorges. What you must expect from White Water Rafting in Ayung River is refreshing waterfalls, swirling foamy water, sudden drops and lots and lots of adrenaline rush! For the best adventure experience, opt for Bali packages that include these activities as standard attributes. Surfing in Padang Padang and Kuta: Can we really talk about beaches without talking about surfing? What an injustice it would be! Padang Padang Beach, in Bukit Peninsula (north-west coast) is one of the most famous surf spots of Bali and is visited by surfers from around the world. What’s more, Padang Padang is also host to international surfing events like Rip Curl Cup Padang. For beginners, however, heading to the Kuta beach will be a safer and more enjoyable surfing experience. This beach has several surfing schools, too. Join a Balinese cooking class: Yes, this is as unusual for us as it would seem to you! But, believe it or not, as part of a Bali Tour Package, you can join a home cooking class where you will be taught by locals the secret of authentic Balinese cooking. These cooking classes are coupled with market tours to find fresh vegetables, aromatic spices and other ingredients. Sunrise Trek to Mount Batur: The sunrise view from Mount Batur is one of the most exquisite views in the entire region of Bali. An early morning trek up Mount Batur is famous just for the views. Since Mount Batur is one of the active volcanoes in Bali, your visit needs to be accompanied by a certified local guide. Bali tours are incomplete without going for this mesmerising trek, so make sure to go for this experience. Bathe in the Pura TirtaEmpul Temple: A national heritage site, the TirtaEmpul Temple in Manukaya Village is a temple dating back to 960 AD. This legendary temple has a sacred water spring which is said to have healing properties. The Pura TirtaEmpul Temple is frequented by tourists from across the world who come to take a bath in the blessed water. Get to enjoy similar delightful experiences by opting for Bali travel packages from our website. Museum hopping: Bali is not entirely about beaches. It is also about cultural sights like museums and galleries. The Agung Rai Museum of Art (ARMA) is the place to go to explore the creative arts of Bali- it features Balinese architecture. Its creativity is heightened by its location- it is set amidst tropical gardens. The museum also hosts gamelan orchestras, woodcarvings, dance performances, history and indigenous fabrics. Museum visits are often included as a part of popular Bali packages. Watch and enjoy a traditional dance: One of the things to do in Bali is watch performances of Balinese dance forms such as Legong, Topeng, Kecak and Barong. These performances happen in multiple venues, one of which is the Ubud Palace where Balinese girls, dressed in traditional attire, dance to recite Hindu epics like Ramayana and Mahabharata. Explore the Artisans Village: It is often said that the best way to explore a region is to explore its culture through its people. We agree with this, completely. And, what better way to explore a place’s culture than through its artists? Bali offers you trips and visits to its artisans’ villages where you can interact with and learn from the artisan’s crafts of great varieties. Celuk village is known for its silversmiths- here, you can not only shop for high-quality silver jewellery, but can also learn how to craft them. Batuan village in Ubud is another artisan village. This one is famous for traditional Balinese paintings. Here, you can get hands-on lessons on traditional Balinese art, wood carving and dancing. Step out of your geographical boundaries and into this diverse land of relaxation, exploration and fun! Book your trip to Bali with offer through Thomas Cook India now, and give us the chance to make this vacation the vacation of your dreams!
Bali.Best Beaches in Bali: Bali houses some of the world’s most beautiful and renowned beaches. So, when visiting this province of Indonesia, make sure to spend some quality time by the beachside with your partner, family members or friends. Following is a list of some of the most popular beaches you can add to your Bali tour itinerary; Jimbaran Beach Jimbaran Beach is one of the most well-known beaches you can check out during your Bali tour. It is a beautiful tourist landmark that features a wide number of luxurious beachfront resorts and restaurants. Stretching a total of 4 kilometres in length, this pristine white sand beach is located in South Bali, on the west end of the narrow stretch of land that connects Bukit Peninsula with mainland Bali. The serene ambience mixed with the sound of the gushing waves and the panoramic view of the ocean makes it a great spot to spend time during both the day and the evening. Most Bali tour packages have Jimbaran Beach included in their trip itinerary. Kuta Beach During your Bali tour, make sure to check out the renowned Kuta Beach. Located just a few kilometres from the Bali Airport, this beach is known for its remarkable beauty and also for offering some of the best sunset views in the region. It is also one of the busiest beaches in Bali, thanks to the availability of numerous budget-friendly beach clubs, food and accommodation options nearby. You can easily find Kuta Beach in the tour itinerary of most Bali trip packages. In addition to spending some time by the golden sand and appreciating the beauty of the surroundings, tourists can participate in a number of fun activities at Kuta Beach, which includes things like scuba diving, shopping at the night market, surfing, checking out nearby tourist landmarks, kite flying, experiencing the lively nightlife at the place, etc. Nusa Dua Beach Nusa Dua Beach in Bali is another prominent beach site that remains filled with tourists around the year. It is also sometimes referred to as an “all-inclusive beach” due to the presence of several amenities close by, such as hotels, markets and shopping malls, an 18-hole golf course, museums and other tourist attractions, etc. As for the beach itself, the white sand and the blue water make up for an astounding scenic view. It is also a great site for scuba diving due to its clear ocean water and the presence of beautiful corals and marine life nearby. Other fun things one can do at Nusa Dua Beach during your Bali tour include aquatic parasailing, boating, scuba diving, swimming, jet skiing, snorkelling, getting a traditional massage, etc. Consider looking for Bali tour packages that include this beach under its planned itinerary. Seminyak Beach Seminyak Beach is among the most notable beaches you can visit during your Bali tour. The beach is mainly known for its laid-back ambience, scenic beauty, and the availability of top luxury waterfront resorts/villas. Seminyak Beach is also a must-visit site for individuals who want to enjoy watersports, witness the beautiful sunset and engage in the vibrant nightlife of Bali. Fun activities that you can be a part of at Seminyak Beach in Bali include swimming in the bubbling cold water, exploring the shoreline on horseback, surfing on gigantic waves, enjoying a relaxing sunbathing session, etc. Have a wonderful time by the beachside with your loved one by going with our professionally designed Bali trip packages.
Bali.Temples in Bali: Popularly known as the “Land of Thousand Temples”, it is no wonder that this island is home to some of the most important and well-known religious shrines in the country. Here are some of the most popular temples that you can include in your Bali tour itinerary; Uluwatu Temple The Uluwatu temple is one of the best places to visit in Bali for religious tours. Nestled on top of a cliff, roughly 70 metres above sea level, this temple welcomes thousands of devotees and tourists on their Bali tours and travels. Locally known as “Pura Luhur Uluwatu”, the shrine is dedicated to the deity Sang Hyang Widhi Wasa as Rudra. Besides its religious significance and alluring surrounding beauty, one of the main highlights of this temple is the Kecak Dance Performance, which is based on the Ramayana and takes place every day between 6-7 P.M. on an adjacent cliff-top stage. You can easily find Bali tour packages that include a visit to this temple. Pura Bratan Temple Pura Bratan, or the Ulun Danu Beratan Temple, is one of the most beautiful tourist places you can check out during your Bali tour. Located on the west of Lake Bratan in Bedugul, the Pura Ulun Danu Beratan is affiliated with Hinduism and features three shrines dedicated to Brahma-Vishnu-Shiva. The Pagoda-style temple architecture, a “floating” appearance due to the surrounding lake and the backdrop of the Bedugul mountains make this site one of the most beautiful religious landmarks in Bali. It is one of the most-visited tourist landmarks in the region, commonly found in popular Bali tour packages. Goa Gajah Located approximately 5 kilometres from the centre of Ubud, Goa Gajah is one of the most popular cultural and spiritual places to visit in Bali. Also called the “Elephant Cave”, Goa Gajah is a temple cave that features several intricate carvings and relics belonging to Buddhism and Shaivism. The temple complex is home to several attractions, such as the detailed cave entrance, an ancient pool, intricate stonework and relics, etc., all of which will leave you captivated. It is best to go for Bali tour packages that feature this site as a standard inclusion. Taman Saraswati Temple The Pura Taman Saraswati, also known as Ubud Water Palace, is one of the most significant religious landmarks you can visit during your Bali tour. Located at the heart of the Ubud town centre, it is a Balinese Hindu temple dedicated to the goddess Saraswati. The Pura Taman Saraswati is known for its exceptional temple architecture. The massive temple complex houses a beautiful lotus pond and water garden, which are two main highlights of the site. Tourists can also check out the Cafe Lotus for some delicious snacks and witness traditional dance performances at this location.
Bali.Bali Currency: As this province is a part of Indonesia, the official Bali currency is also the Indonesian Rupiah. It is denoted by the symbol “Rp” and the currency code “IDR”. A single rupiah can be divided into 100 cents (locally called “sen”). In terms of coins, the denominations available are Rp50, Rp100, Rp200, Rp500 and Rp1000. As for banknotes, the denominations available are Rp1,000, Rp2,000, Rp5,000, Rp10,000, Rp20,000, Rp50,000 and Rp100,000. Currently, the value of 1 Indonesian Rupiah is equal to 0.0054 INR. As the Indonesian Rupiah is the only accepted currency in Bali, tourists visiting this Indonesian Province will need to acquire its official currency to make legitimate payments towards their purchases and used services during their stay. You can easily acquire foreign currency for your Bali tour (in both cash and forex travel card forms) by opting for the online foreign exchange services offered by Thomas Cook. To do so, you will need to visit the official forex page, choose the correct details (like preferred check rate, currency type and forex amount) and provide your contact details to get a quote. After that, you can proceed with the online payment option and select your preferred delivery type. Finally, after payment confirmation, you will receive your order receipt and the foreign currency for your Bali tour within the appointed time.

faq.question: For what duration should I plan my Gangtok travel packages?
faq.answer: The capital city of Sikkim offers a variety of things to do, places to visit, food to try, and shop till you drop. Ideally, if you are in a time crunch, then a weekend trip of 2-3 days is enough to relax in Gangtok. However, if you truly wish to immerse yourself in your Gangtok trip, insider booking your Gangtok trip package for at least 4-6 days.

faq.question: Is Gangtok better than Darjeeling?
faq.answer: Darjeeling is one of the closest locations to Gangtok and is often favorably compared to Sikkim’s capital holiday destination. Both places offer chilly weather and exquisite scenic views. If you have to choose between Gangtok travel packages and Darjeeling trip packages, you need to consider your weather preferences and the time of year for your visit. Both places are equally cold during the winter season, however, there is a better chance of snowfall in Gangtok. The summer months are pleasant in both destinations but Darjeeling may experience more warmth than Gangtok.

faq.question: What makes Gangtok so famous?
faq.answer: As one of the seven sisters of the northeast regions in the Himalayan range, Gangtok is famous for its scenic views, thrilling adventure, and lip-smacking food, much like Siliguri, Darjeeling, and other cold holiday destinations nearby. However, what makes Gangtok so famous is the uninterrupted view of the Kanchenjunga peaks which are the third highest across the globe.

faq.question: When can I watch Snowfall in Gangtok?
faq.answer: During the winter months of late November to February, the temperature in Gangtok can drop below freezing, making snow a real possibility. There may not always be heavy snowfall, but the proximity of Sikkim's capital with the snow-capped Himalayas encourages at least a little amount of snow or sleet during winter. So if you want to have snow fights or make snow angels, you can plan your giant clock trip packages anytime from December to early February.

faq.question: What should I pack to wear for my Gangtok tourism package?
faq.answer: During most times of the year, Gangtok is relatively cold. Therefore, back some warm clothes such as sweaters shawls, heavy jackets, and scarves for your Gangtok trip. During the daytime, especially in summer, you can wear shorts, jeans, dresses, half-sleeved T-shirts and shirts, or just about anything else you like. However, at night time the temperature drops significantly so keep your warm clothes handy.

faq.question: Which language is widely spoken in Gangtok?
faq.answer: As a popular tourist destination, several languages are spoken in Gangtok. While the locals mostly communicate in Nepali, Lepcha, and Sikkimese, English is also widely spoken in Gangtok.

faq.question: What restrictions do I need to know about when planning my Gangtok travel package?
faq.answer: Sikkim is proudly one of the smoke-free states in India. Therefore, planning your Gangtok tourism package, know that you will not be allowed to smoke in public places. As a tourist destination, locals are also very inclined on keeping the locality safe, which is why alcohol is not available along the highway and drinking in public places is not particularly encouraged.

faq.question: Is it safe for women to plan a Gangtok tourism package solo?
faq.answer: Gangtok is one of the safest places for women to plan a solo trip. The welcoming and friendly nature of the locals combined with religious and cultural factors that are levied on locals and tourists alike makes it a very safe place for women. There is no rowdy behavior allowed in public places, and the crime rate is relatively low.

Gangtok.Introduction: Nestled in the lap of the Himalayas, Gangtok, the capital of Sikkim, beckons travelers with its breathtaking landscapes, vibrant culture, and serene atmosphere. To make the most of your visit to this enchanting destination, Gangtok tour packages offer a curated experience, blending adventure, culture, and relaxation. Let's delve into the enticing offerings of Gangtok's tour packages that promise an unforgettable getaway.
Gangtok.Table of Contents: Popular Gangtok Tour Packages Best time to visit Gangtok How to reach Gangtok Best honeymoon destinations for Gangtok travel Best hill station places to visit near Gangtok Experiencing the magic of Gangtok food Shopping in Gangtok Things to do in Gangtok Adventurous Things to do in Gangtok Interesting Facts about Gangtok People also ask about Gangtok Tour Packages
Gangtok.Most Popular Gangtok Tour Packages 2025: Tour Packages NameDurationPriceNorth East Delight With Pelling6 Nights 7 DaysRs. ₹39 000Fascinating Sikkim and Darjeeling6 Nights 7 DaysRs. ₹45 100Darjeeling and Gangtok with Pelling7 Nights 8 DaysRs. ₹50 400Sikkim - Awesome Gangtok3 Nights 4 DaysRs. ₹24 300Glimpses Of Eastern Himalayas4 Nights 5 DaysRs. ₹32 300
Gangtok.Best time to visit Gangtok:: Located in the eastern Himalayan range, Gangtok, the capital of Sikkim is one of the coldest places in the Indian subcontinent. It is the perfect location for a holiday with friends, to trek the Himalayan mountains solo, or to plan a honeymoon. However, if you do not prefer extreme chills, then you may want to consider planning your Gangtok tourism packages for more optimal seasons. Summer The best time to visit Gangtok is during the summer months of March to June. During this time the temperature during both day and night is warm enough to explore the hillsides and towns. You can taste delicious spicy mountain food and cherish the warmth of the sun against the picturesque view of the yellow and orange colors dancing on the lush greenery all around. You will still need a few warm clothes for when the weather changes dramatically and unpredictably. However, it is not as cold as Gangtok winters. Spring If you are planning your trip from a hot location, you can also design your personalized Darjeeling and Gangtok tourist packages for springtime. After the chills of winter have passed and summer is just about to make an appearance, the hills of Gangtok experience the most divine sight. The beauty of the place is nothing short of opulence at its finest. The local markets offer an array of Gangtok goods at reasonable prices. You can acquire a lot of handcrafted materials such as rugs, sweaters, scarves, blankets, and whatnot for a fraction of the price. Additionally, the weather and the scenery around are a treat to the senses. Winter If you do enjoy temperatures below freezing, then Gangtok in winter can be quite pleasant. This is the perfect time to plan your Gangtok trip for a honeymoon. The frigid temperature outside and the warmth indoors are the perfect opportunities to cuddle with your spouse and enjoy the scenic view with a cup of locally grown tea or hot chocolate. In fact, Gangtok holidays in winter are quite coveted for the chance of snow that allows you to indulge in snow fights, an activity that is rather rare in India. Monsoon However, if you wish for a Gangtok tour on a small budget, perhaps the monsoon months are well-suited for your Gangtok holiday package. The frequent rain makes the lush greenery shine brighter but keeps away thick crowds. The hillsides ooze beauty and if you don’t mind getting a little wet in the rain, then the off-season is perfect for your Gangtok trip.
Gangtok.How to reach Gangtok?: Although a hill station located significantly away from most airports and train ports, Gangtok is among the few well-connected hillside holiday destinations. There are a number of ways that you can reach Gangtok from your residential city. If you wish to travel by air: The closest airport located near Gangtok is Bagdogra Airport in Siliguri, West Bengal. This particular airport caters to flights from different parts of India, however, most of them may be connecting flights or have a layover in Kolkata. The journey is relatively faster and more pleasant. Not to mention, you will be landing at yet another beautiful holiday destination in India. After landing, you can take a public bus or opt for a private cab service to drive you to Gangtok. The journey takes approximately 4 hours which is the perfect opportunity to explore the surrounding beauty of your holiday destination. There is another exciting way to travel by air to Gangtok after you land at Bagdogra airport. There are TSA helicopters available to reach your Gangtok travel package destination within 20-30 minutes. While this is not a popular mode of transportation, if you truly want to admire the scenic beauty of Gangtok, consider taking the TSA helicopter to reach the hill station. It is not entirely pocket friendly but it is an experience that you will carry throughout your lifetime. If you wish to travel by train: If you prefer budget-friendly travel plans, the closest train station to Gangtok is located in New Jalpaiguri. You will need to book a private vehicle or bus from the train station to the hill station, however, the journey is relatively comfortable and easy on the pockets. If you wish to drive down: If you are planning your Gangtok holiday packages from nearby locations, the hill station is fairly accessible by road. Not only are there numerous buses that taxi people from nearby destinations to the capital city of Sikkim, but you can also opt for private transport. Apart from the affluent cab services, you can choose to drive to Gangtok with your friends and family in your private vehicle. If you like riding bikes on sharp bends and wiry roads, making a two-wheeler trip up the mountainside can also be quite enlightening. Be careful though, especially during the monsoon and winter season when the roads are likely to be sleek. During these times it is best to opt for taxi service or take the TSA helicopter all the way to Gangtok.
Gangtok.Best honeymoon destinations for Gangtok: If you are visiting Gangtok for the first time, especially with your spouse on a Gangtok trip package for honeymooners there are several areas that will create the perfect backdrop for romance. Although the entirety of Gangtok is beautiful and worthy of capturing memories, it is the serenity of these honeymoon destinations that amp up feelings of love and intimacy. Here are some of the best honeymoon locations that you simply must visit on your Gangtok package for couples: Tsomgo Lake - Mountain regions are often popular for their teal and emerald lakes reflecting upon the beauty that surrounds the region. The Tsomgo Lake located 3753 meters above sea level is a spectacle that can only be witnessed in the scenic beauty of Gangtok. It is an ideal place for honeymooners to visit during their Gangtok tour for the tranquility in the area. It's perfect for a picnic lunch or to spend some time holding hands and gazing upon the emerald green waters. Nathu La Pass - Among the many attractions of Gangtok is the Nathu La Pass. This romantic destination that connects the mountains of Sikkim and Tibet to each other is like a beautiful metaphor for marriage itself. Rumtek monastery - A honeymoon being the beginning of an auspicious milestone in life, it is only fitting to take a trip to one of the most around monasteries, especially if you are on your Gangtok tour packages. The Rumtek monastery dates back to the 16th century featuring the similar architecture of the holy institutions in Tibet. You could take a trip to receive blessings for your new life with your spouse. Ranka Monastery - Another spiritually enlightening monastery in your Gangtok tourism package is the Ranka monastery. Not only is it a spectacular location to get the blessings of lord buddha but the perfect placement of the monastery allows panoramic views of the hills and the valleys around Gangtok. It is also one of the must-visit places in Gangtok if you are on a solo trip or enjoying a Gangtok family tour package. Bhanjhakri Falls - The seven sisters waterfall is one of the coveted places to visit in Gangtok. The closest second is the Banjhakri waterfalls situated in proximity to the Ranka monastery. The 100 feet tall waterfall sports a footbridge for tourists to walk around while admiring the indigenous flora in the area. Yumthang Valley - There lies in the heart of Sikkim a part of Gangtok tourism packages, in the valley known for its flora. The Yumthang Valley is often dubbed the Valley of Flowers. It is nothing short of a picture-perfect moment derived from a poem with crystal lakes, green pastures, and a sprinkling of hot springs making it the most romantic setting for a honeymoon. Shingba Rhododendron Sanctuary - Another insanely beautiful place to visit for honeymooners is the Shingba Rhododendron Sanctuary. 43 square kilometers covered by indigenous flora that includes Aconites, Potentillas, Gentias, and Saxifrages, all varieties of Rhododendrons is the ideal place to not only bask in nature but also to live romantic moments from movies. Must visit places in Gangtok for your trip Gangtok is yet another masterpiece that exists within the seven jewels of India. Located in the quaint town of Sikkim, Gangtok features beautiful alleys filled with Tibetan architecture alongside some stunning views that are sure to take your breath away. The stupas and architectural sites of Gangtok are the places that you must visit at least once in your lifetime. Here is a list of places that you might like to visit in Gangtok: Places to Visit in Gangtok No. of Days Know More About Roomtek Monastery and Dharma Chakra Centre One of the most prominent places of the Kagyu lineage, this monastery is located outside the area of Tibet. It is the headquarters of the monasteries in the entire region. Know More About Roomtek Monastery and Dharma Chakra Centre Tsomgo Lake Most enjoyable place among both the adults and children, the Tsomgo Lake features a wide range of fun-filled activities for people of all age groups Know More About Tsomgo Lake Shiva Temple Located amidst the most picturesque locales, this Shiva Temple in the area of Gangtok holds great importance according to the Hindu epic of Mahabharata Know More About Shiva Temple Phesang Monastery 7 Days / 6 NightsLocated about 9-10 km north of Gangtok, this monastery is yet another beautiful construction that was rebuilt in the year of 1948 due to some issues Know More About Phesang Monastery Solpok Chardam A unique tourist attraction in Gangtok that covers both beauty and religion is the SolopokChardam. It is home for an 87 feet tall statue of Lord Shiva, which is the most important statue of this area Know More About Solpok Chardam Sikkim Research Institute of Tibetology This institute was set up in the year of 1958 and was known as the Namgyal Institute of Tibetology after the name of the late PaldenThondupNamgyal. Its main aim is to research about the Mahayana part of the Tibet Know More About Sikkim Research Institute of Tibetology Flower Exhibition Centre Covering sprawls of areas that are filled with the tropical and beautiful greenhouses, this exhibition centre is the best place to view a great range of flowers and plants Know More About Flower Exhibition Centre Temi Tea Garden Located in the beautiful Ravangla, this tea garden is the only one in the entire state of Sikkim. It produces some of the most aromatic teas that are sold for good prices across the state Know More About Temi Tea Garden Tasi View Point Situated around 4-5 km away from the northeast of Sikkim, the TashiViewPoint is the perfect place to view the sunrise at the point of Kanchenjunga Know More About Tasi View Point Chorten Stupa Built in the year of 1945 by Trulshik Rinpoche, this Buddhist stupa is an epitome of the Tibetan Buddhism that prevails in the area of Gangtok in Sikkim. Know More About Chorten Stupa
Gangtok.Best hill station places to visit near G: There are a number of places to visit in Gangtok that will simply take your breath away. However, there are also individual hill stations around town that are so exquisite that you need to spend at least a day admiring the beauty of nature. When planning your Gangtok trip packages, consider adding the following nearby hillsides to your itinerary: Yumthang valley Lachen La Chung Ravangla Yuksom Aritar Pelling Geysing Legship Mangan These places to visit near Gangtok add to the adventure of the eastern Himalayas with a touch of spirituality and divine intervention.
Gangtok.Experiencing the magic of Gangtok food:: Hillside regions are popular destinations for their exquisite dishes. Restaurants in Gangtok have the potential to tickle your taste buds with the most delectable concoctions of vegetables and meat. Aside from the heavenly fragrance of local tea, a number of delightful dishes are must-have Gangtok foods that you cannot miss. Momos (veg and meat) - one of the most crave-worthy foods in mountain regions especially places where Tibetan culture is prevalent are momos. These steamed dumplings come with a variety of fillings at restaurants in Gangtok. While the most common variety consumed by the locals, as well as tourists, are chicken and beef momos, you can also visit your veg restaurants in Gangtok and taste the heavenly combination of carrots and cabbage seared and filled into a soft dough that is steamed to perfection. Sel roti and Pahadi chicken curry – A Nepalese traditional form of flatbread that is served with a spicy, dry gravy of chicken is the ideal meal for meat lovers on a Gangtok tour. Sha Phaley – as a Tibetan variation of calzones, Sha Phaleys are deep-fried bread that is stuffed with meat to mouth-watering perfection. You can opt for a vegetable variety of this popular Gangtok food as well. Chocolate momos – if you thought the vegetable or meat momos were outstanding, the chocolate momo will double your joy. The gooey chocolate filling in the steamed dumpling is the perfect way to end a meal with the best of both worlds. Phagshapa – A pork delicacy for meat lovers that comes with meat and radish served with a side of rice. This healthy meal is a must-have for those who love pork as it is a local recipe commonly consumed by the people of Gangtok and Sikkim in general. Thukpa - in the chilly weather of Gangtok, Thukpa is a much-needed delicacy that can immediately warm up the body. The combination of noodles, vegetables, and soup the topping of any meat of your choice be it chicken, beef, or pork can brighten up your Gangtok trip in minutes.
Gangtok.Shopping in Gangtok: Going shopping in the local markets of Gangtok is an extravagant activity. Even though the prices are reasonable, the quality of the products makes them incredible purchases. Here is a list of the top items that you must purchase in Gangtok for a wholesome experience on your Gangtok holiday: Thangkas – traditional Buddhist paintings on fabric depicting stories from ancient times. Buddhist cultural artifacts – figurines carved from wood, metal, and clay to represent lord Buddha and the history of the peaceful region. Sikkim masks - colorful masks replicating the appearance of legendary creatures as well as deities. Wall décor - A variety of artwork on fabric, wood, and paper to hang on walls and reminisce about your Gangtok holidays. Clothing – jackets, shawls, sweaters, scarves, and other items woven and designed by the locals from scratch. Carpets and rugs – traditional carpet designs hail all the way from Tibet to brighten up your home design. Cups – traditional handiwork and artwork give birth to these exquisite ceramic beauties that will add character to your home décor and your china cabinet. Prayer flags – another Buddhist artwork that can be hung at home to invite tranquility. Woodwork – handcrafted pieces of furniture and décor made intricately with wood. Black cardamom seeds – a locally grown spice that you simply must take home to add a special touch to your food. Tea – from the region’s own plantation, a variety of tea leaves with organic fragrance and taste. Bamboo shoots – strengthen your teeth and enjoy the juicy sugary flavor fresh from the farms. Alpine cheese – Sikkim’s personal take on Swiss gouda cheese that melts and dances in your mouth with richness. Liqueur – flavorful bottles of exquisite Sikkimese beverages that pack a punch to battle the cold.
Gangtok.Things to do on your Gangtok tourist pac: Gangtok holidays offer an opportunity to relax and let go of the hustle and bustle of everyday life. It is endearing to think about sitting indoors to explore Gangtok food brought to your room. You can gaze upon the scenery outside the window, especially during monsoon and winters, there are a number of things to do in Gangtok that you cannot miss. While restaurants and cafes in Gangtok provide a thrilling and immersive experience for your Gangtok trip, it is absolutely fantastic to have a picnic on the hillsides overlooking the valleys. Consider packing a few of your desirable items from the restaurants if necessary and trek into the mountains or stroll to the popular sunset points for a picnic. It's the perfect activity for friends as well as honeymooners in Gangtok. Experience divinity when the sun rises or sets from the Tashi viewpoint. It is one of the coveted locations to take in the beauty of your surroundings in a quiet and serene setting. Witness the Seven Sisters Waterfall in its absolute glory. You have to crossover 7 stages in order to see the waterfall streaming down as four individual streaks. It is almost like being in a ‘Lord of the Rings movie with the lush bushes surrounding the sparkling water falling from such great heights. Take a spiritual trip to a monastery while on your Gangtok tourist packages. Not only is it a divine experience but if you are a history buff, you may love the collection of sculptures and architecture that dates back many centuries. If you truly want to immerse yourself in the local cultures of Gangtok, consider an excursion around town. You can talk to the locals who are very friendly and welcoming to learn a little more about the history of Gangtok and the different social and religious practices in the area. The villages in particular are plethoras of knowledge and wisdom. The residents you meet on your Gangtok trip can enlighten you about their ancestors and how they came about designing the serene living that predominantly exists in Gangtok. Another means to a spiritual experience is to travel to Namchi. The religious setting located on a mountain top 5500 feet above the average sea level is considered to be the capital of culture in Sikkim. It sports one of the most beautiful and tallest statues of lord shiva but that's not all. Namchi is also home to rose gardens, temples, and the infamous ropeways of Gangtok. Regardless of the duration of your Gangtok tour, consider adding at least some if not all of these things to do in one of the most beautiful holiday destinations in India.
Gangtok.Adventurous things to do in Gangtok:: Apart from the tremendously beautiful places to visit in Gangtok, there are a lot of adventurous activities to keep you occupied on your Gangtok trip. Here are some of the most famous activities that are well-suited for thrill seekers as well as those who want to dip their toe in the adventure pool: Trekking – as one of the few locations that allow the opportunity to explore the eastern Himalayan range, trekking is a coveted activity in Gangtok. Even if you are not up for an extensive trip through the mountains, consider a short trek to explore the natural beauty of the region. Paragliding - while the surrounding views of Gangtok are awe-striking, the aerial view will make you feel alive. Take a gander at the terraced expanses of rice fields, the lush greenery on the rolling hills, and the famous monasteries that certainly should be a part of your gang dog trip packages. Cable car rides - if hanging by a harness does not appeal to you, don’t worry. You can still enjoy the aerial view of Gangtok from inside cable cars. You won’t get the entire panoramic view since the cable cars are situated between hills. Nevertheless, you will see most parts of the capital city of Sikkim in its absolute glory. Yak safari – it is a rare sight apart from the mountain ranges to even catch a glimpse of a yak. If it doesn’t make you squeamish, consider taking a yak safari around the lakes and beautiful towns of Gangtok for a truly immersive experience.
Gangtok.Interesting facts about the Gangtok:: Gangtok is undoubtedly one of the most fascinating holiday destinations in India. The proximity to the snow-capped Himalayas and the serene beauty of the land itself makes it a popular location for holidays and honeymoons alike. But did you know that Gangtok also overlooks the third-highest mountain range in the world? Here are some more interesting facts that will make your Gangtok travel package all the more exciting: We all admire the litter-free streets abroad but Gangtok has brought the concept to India. Not only are the streets devoid of trash but spitting is also disallowed in not only yang talk but the entire state of Sikkim. There are a number of monasteries around the Himalayan range in different parts of India as well as its neighboring countries. However, the Rumtek Monastery is home to an ostentatious ‘black cat’ adorned with precious jewel stones including diamonds. Sikkim’s state animal is among the cutest creatures popularly found in China. The red pandas spotted in the sanctuaries around Gangtok are not only adorable but also highly regarded. Speaking of unique creatures in the vicinity, the Tsomgo Lake in Gangtok invites several varieties of migratory birds including the evasive Brahminy Duck. Once a year, the hills of Gangtok present the international flower festival. If you plan your Gangtok travel packages during this time, you may come across tourists from around the globe crowding to witness the fragrance and beauty of the exhibition. Apart from these entertaining aspects of Gangtok tourism, the hill station is also home to one of the most prestigious research institutions in the entire world. The Namgyal research institute of Tibetology securely houses ancient manuscripts from Tibetan, Sanskrit, and Lepcha cultures.

Berlin.Introduction: The capital city of Germany, and its largest city, Berlin is a major hub for all the media, culture, science and politics of the country. The Berlin tour packages are designed in such a way that you get to experience the must-do things in the city in a comparatively lesser time. Home to the most vivid cultural flair, Berlin is sure to keep you engaged with the kind of content that it has to offer. Here is a list of some packages curated by our travel experts at Thomascook: Berlin Tour Packages No. of Days Price* Berlin and Prague - By Europamundo (Flights Not Included) 4 Nights / 5 Days Rs. 30,692 Amsterdam and Berlin - By Europamundo (Flights Not Included 4 Nights / 5 Days Rs. 32,765 Three Capitals – By Europamundo (Flights Not Included) 7 Nights / 8 Days Rs. 59,309 Scandic East Europe 20 Nights / 21 Days Rs. 3,80,505 Scandic East Europe with Russia 24 Nights / 25 Days Rs. 4,43,160 Despite the horrible destruction of the city followed by the World War II, Berlin eventually gathered all its pieces to become the beauty that it is today. Known as the home for the infamous Berlin Wall along with many other monuments, Berlin is a wonderful place to experience medieval Germany. Here is a list of places that you might like to visit in Berlin: Places to Visit Known for More Details Berlin Wall Sprawling in the eastern part of Berlin, this iconic wall is one of the wonder that you can witness in the city. Looking like a stunning gallery, the Berlin Wall captures the true essence of Berlin More About Berlin Wall The Reichstag One of the most iconic structures in Berlin, the Reichstag is the official house to the German Parliament, the Bundestag More About The Reichstag Archenhold Observatory Your trip to Berlin would be incomplete without visiting the Archenhold Observatory. Featuring the world’s longest telescope, it is a great place to explore the galaxy. More About Archenhold Observatory Berlin Zoo and Aquarium One of the most favourite places among both kids and adults, this German zoo is a place where the rare species of the country lie. It homes many lions, kangaroos, bears, giraffes, ostriches, and others More About Berlin Zoo and Aquarium Memorial for Jews Representing one of the darkest phases in the German history, this Memorial holds an important place in the city. This is an underground museum that shows the wrath suffered by the Jews due to Nazi Germany. More About Memorial for Jews Pergamon Museum One of the most profound museums in Berlin, this contemporary construction takes you into the ancient mystical world of the Middle East, Greece, Rome, and Babylon. More About Pergamon Museum Kaiser Wilhelm Gedachtniskirche Homing the wonderful ‘floating’ Jesus, this landmark church of Berlin was bombed out earlier following which it was restricted later on. More About Kaiser Wilhelm Gedachtniskirche Nueu Synagogue Built during the 19th century, this synagogue was destroyed during the World War II. It was later reconstructed after the destruction of the Berlin Wall. Russia.Russia COVID 19 Tourist Guide: Guidelines: Russia has opened its doors and have resumed accepting all visa applications, including tourist visas. However, all tourists are required to present a printed version of a negative PCR test, no older than 72 hours. If you are travelling on student visa, COVID testing after the arrival will take place and will have to be in self-isolation for a few days. Most of the tourist attraction places are now functional, of course with all precautions and social distancing protocols. Visitors are requested to adhere to all measures. For medical treatment, visitors must carry a proof of travel on hand and special visa issued for this reason.
Russia.About Russia Holidays: The largest country in the world, Russia has so much variety to offer and incredible things to do that makes travelling to this majestic country a one of a kind of experience. The charms of this country vary from gorgeous mountains, splendid deserts, impeccable monuments, golden white beaches, natural geysers, palaces, stunning churches and a lot more. This rich cultural history and geography of this country make it a travel destination worth visiting.

Russia.Why visit Russia: Russia is a very friendly tourist destination. With luxurious resorts, villas and bungalows, Russia ensures you of a great time during your vacation. The friendly locals happily host tourists and love to introduce them to Russian hospitality. Russia has been a home to many artists, poets, political figures, and writers. Culture lovers would surely enjoy their time here. Every city in Russia has multiple museums and the towns boast of lovely architecture. Cities like Moscow and Saint Petersburg have a number of art galleries where you can spend your time enjoying remarkable works spanning decades. Apart from Moscow and Saint Petersburg, other attractive places to visit in Russia include the imposing Mount Elbrus, breathtaking Valley of Geysers, fascinating Kizhi Island, and beautiful Lake Baikal. Among these places, Lake Baikal is one of the favourites because of its scenic charms. This lake is the deepest and the oldest on the earth and is known to hold 20% of water on Earth. Surrounded by mountain ranges, Lake Baikal has a picturesque view and stunningly clear water with abundant marine life that is visible. If you are travelling on your honeymoon or to seek luxury then you can easily spend a few days here at any of the quaint resorts. Best time to visit Russia Russia is an all year destination. Still the winters are very harsh and not everyone’s cup of coffee or hot chocolate. January is the best month to witness snow and around this time, Saint Petersburg also experiences long nights with day time spanning just a few hours. Planning your vacation to Russia in the summers will assure you of an amazing time. The days are pleasant and the nights are perfect to take strolls in the city alleys. This season will disappoint you in a way because all the peaks are devoid of snow but on a plus side, the roads are open and water transport is completely operational. As the snow melts from the peaks and around Russian towns, beautiful flowers start blooming everywhere. Birds chirp around and green lawns make every corner of the country gorgeous. During mid-summer in the northern city of St Petersburg there is no real darkness. This unique phenomenon is known as ‘the white nights’. faq.question: What is the best time to visit Vietnam from India?
faq.answer: The best time to visit Vietnam from India could be from March to April, when rainfall is low and weather conditions are milder. The weather in Vietnam varies greatly from north to south, from temperate to tropical.

faq.question: Is Visa required for Vietnam tour?
faq.answer: To enter Vietnam, you must have a valid passport and a visa (or pre-approval for a visa on arrival). Your passport must be valid for at least six months beyond your intended stay and contain at least one blank visa page.

faq.question: Is Vietnam safe to travel?
faq.answer: Although Vietnam is one of the safest countries in the world for tourists, it is always a good idea to be prepared. These health and safety tips will make your Vietnam trip more enjoyable

faq.question: Which is currency commonly used in Vietnam?
faq.answer: Vietnamese Dong is the currency used for the majority of transactions, certain hotels, restaurants and other services also accept payment in US dollars. On your Vietnam tour, you might notice that all of the prices for food, beverages, and souvenirs in the airport of Ho Chi Minh City are listed in US dollars.

faq.question: Why Vietnam is so popular?
faq.answer: Vietnam Tour is known for being a popular tourist attraction due of its stunning beaches, rich culture, incredible food (like pho), and welcoming people.

faq.question: Do most people in Vietnam speak English?
faq.answer: Many Vietnamese will speak some English in tourist areas, but many will not. English speakers can be rare in more rural places. Vietnamese seniors occasionally speak more French than English.

faq.question: What should I carry for my Vietnam trip?
faq.answer: With the help of our Vietnam tour packages, you can carefully arrange your eagerly anticipated trip there. As soon as you are prepared for the holiday, write a list of everything you need to bring. The following items are essential for you to bring on your Vietnam trip: a reliable rucksack that can hold some basic clothing, sunscreen, snacks, drinks, and any items you buy locally. Pillows for travel are available for persons who travel widely frequently. earplugs for a restful night's sleep. towels that are light and quick to dry. medications for diarrhea or disturbed stomach. When visiting temples, pagodas, and other revered locations, bring covert attire. sturdy sandals, flip-flops and hiking or trekking shoes. Chargers, sunscreen, and hats are advised along with travel adapters.

faq.question: How many days are enough for a Vietnam trip?
faq.answer: Various Vietnam tour packages are available that will allow you to visit the country's top attractions in roughly ten days. The minimal amount of time needed to experience the cultural joie de vivre and visit significant historical sites is this. Although there is always more to do and see, you need at least a week to experience Vietnam in its entirety. Many travel experts agree that twelve days is the optimal amount of time because it allows for time spent in at least three major cities as well as excursions into the countryside. You will have plenty of time to participate in pursuits like village visits, riding, and kayaking.

faq.question: Is Vietnam worth visiting for Indians?
faq.answer: Yes, Vietnam is definitely worth visiting for Indians. Vietnam is a beautiful Southeast Asian country known for its rich history, diverse culture, stunning landscapes, and delicious cuisine. Vietnam.Vietnam Tour: In spite of its turbulent history, Vietnam tourism has rebounded to become a major vacation destination in South-East Asia. From gorgeous golden paddy fields and white-sand beaches to venerated pagodas and limestone islands dotting the sea, from the labyrinthine cave networks to the rugged mountains, Vietnam’s varied landscapes and the friendly folk– with their beautiful tribal costumes and traditions will welcome you with open arms and a sense of wonder. Despite an economic boom, Hanoi, the country's capital, has managed to retain its old-world charm. Its Old Quarter is a veritable feast of delicious food vendors, markets, and winding lanes. Ho Chi Minh City has a more modern vibe, whereas Hoi An, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, has managed to retain its charm despite the influx of tourists. From Ha Long Bay to the Mekong Delta, via the beaches of Da Nang, Vietnam will stimulate all of your senses and pull you in from all sides with its interesting history, beautiful scenery, delicious food, and lively energy. Vietnam trip is like taking a trip you've never been on before; the unique experiences, the varied geography and the mouth-watering food, all of these combined, give us something worth remembering.
