Vietnam.About Vietnam: Vietnam, officially the Socialist Republic of Vietnam, is a South-East Asian country. It is located on the eastern edge of continental South-East Asia with Hanoi as its capital city. It is a highly populated country with a population of approximately 99 million. Vietnam shares land boundaries with China to the North, Laos, and Cambodia to the West, and shares sea borders with the Philippines, Indonesia, and Malaysia via the South China Sea and with Thailand via the Gulf of Thailand. Vietnam has a coastline that stretches for 3,444 km or 2,140 miles, not including the numerous islands. Spread over 311,699 square kilometers, Vietnam is around the same size as New Mexico in the United States and about half the size of France. The distance between India and Vietnam is around 3194 km (1,985 miles), and the flight time is approximately 4 hours. Hanoi is the country’s capital city, and Ho Chi Minh City is its largest metropolis. However, despite the growth of cities like Ho Chi Minh City and Hanoi, only 36% of the population lives in the country's metropolitan areas. The Red River Delta in the north of the country and the Mekong Delta and Ho Chi Minh City in the south are home to the bulk of the population.
Vietnam.Vietnam Culture: The culture of Vietnam is believed to be one of the world’s oldest cultures, which has experienced massive changes throughout the span of thousands of years. As parts of northern Vietnam were under Chinese rule for some years, its influences are quite evident in the country’s culture and traditions. After its independence, the local rulers went on to conquer parts of the Khmer regime, from where the Cambodian influence surged into Vietnam’s art, music, cuisine and culture in general. The region also absorbed aspects of European culture, especially with the adoption of the Latin alphabet, religion (Catholicism) and architecture, when it was under French colonial rule. Following the exit of France, Vietnamese culture went through further changes with various influences from European, Asian and American cultures. While some aspects of Vietnamese culture kept changing over the years, others remained the same. The concept of family and clan are very strong in the culture, where it is valued way over individualism. It is not uncommon to see two or three generations of the family living together in a single household. Weddings are grand occasions in the country and include detailed rituals and ceremonies. Food in Vietnam focuses on fresh and healthy ingredients with the inclusion of various types of meats and locally-grown spices. There are well over fifty distinct ethnic groups in Vietnam and among them, the Kinh (Viet) have the largest numbers, around 85.32%. Every ethnic group has their own traditional costumes, with some examples being the Áo Tứ Thân, Áo Cánh, Áo Giao Lĩnh and the Áo Bà Ba. Religion and religious events play a massive role in Vietnamese culture, and the three major religions in the country are Buddhism, Taoism and Confucianism.
Vietnam.Best Time to Visit in Vietnam: It's easy to become overwhelmed while planning a Vietnam trip. There are so many sites to see (and foods to try) in Vietnam that figuring out the perfect time to visit might make your head spin. We've all been there, but fret not, let’s look at the best time to visit Vietnam and the best time to avoid it. The climate of Vietnam is a typical tropical monsoon climate, with significant humidity all year round. However, due to variances in latitude and topography, the country is affected by three major weather systems. So, the climate tends to vary significantly from one place to the next. Hence, the ideal time to visit Northern Vietnam differs from the perfect time to visit the country's southern regions. Temperatures in northern cities like Hanoi and the mountainous region of Sapa can be very mild. In contrast, in the south, in places like Ho Chi Minh City and the Mekong Delta, temperatures are almost always hot and humid. So, in general, to get the best of both the northern and the southern parts of the country, travelling to Vietnam in February, March, and April offers the best possibility of dry weather all across, with temperatures not as high as they are during peak dry season. In a gist: Season Month Weather Temperature Airfare Winter Dec, Jan, Feb Cold and Rainy 20°C - 26°C High Spring Mar, Apr Comfortable (dry sunny days punctuated with cloudy, misty mornings.) 20°C - 30°C Moderate Summer May, Jun, Jul, Aug Wettest and Hottest weather in Vietnam 24°C - 30°C Low Autumn Sep, Oct, Nov pleasant breezes, sunny days & cooler temperatures 20°C - 25°C High
Vietnam.How to Reach Vietnam: Most visitors arrive in Vietnam by air or by bus. However, there are also train connections from China and Mekong riverboat connections from Cambodia. Paperwork at the international airports in Vietnam is often less formal than at the land borders. Overland travel between Cambodia and China is now generally stress-free. It can take a long time to cross the border between Vietnam and Laos. Best way to reach Vietnam from India generally prefer to fly into the country by utilising the services of airline carriers that shuttle between India and Vietnam. The distance between India and Vietnam is 3,194 kilometres, and direct flight durations vary between 4 hours to 6 hours depending on the airport in India from where you are taking off. By Air By Sea By Road By Air Airline carriers like VietJet and Vistara operate non-stop flights from all the major cities in India to the international airports in Vietnam. Indigo airlines have also started non-stop flights from Kolkata to Hanoi. Other carriers also operate direct flights from Delhi and Bangalore to Ho Chi Minh City. The average flight time from New Delhi to Hanoi is 4 hours and 20 minutes. In Vietnam, there are three international airports; depending on which section of the country most interests you, the port of entry can be chosen. By Sea Despite not being directly accessible by sea, travellers from India can reach the magnificent country of Vietnam via the waterways that connect Cambodia, China, and Thailand. Indian tourists can reach Vietnam by crossing the Mekong Delta via Cambodia or Thailand. By Road Vietnam is only accessible from Laos and Cambodia because the other side of the country faces the sea. You may fly into either of these nations and then take a bus across their borders. However, we propose that you travel from Cambodia because it is much more convenient.
Vietnam.Tourist Places to Visit in Vietnam: Let us take a look at the main tourist destinations and different places to visit in Vietnam. Hanoi Northern Vietnam Central Vietnam The South-Eastern Coast of Vietnam The South-West Highlands Hanoi Vietnam's capital Hanoi is racing to make up for lost time due to the scars of war and a regime that kept the outside world at bay until the 1990s. Its streets overflowing with scooters competing for the right of way amid a cacophony of screaming horns, and layers of history, reflect eras of French and Chinese occupation all around, revealing the tenacity of the ambitious and proud Hanoians.This is one of the most popular tourist attractions in Vietnam and must be a part of your Vietnam tour packages from India. Some famous places to visit in Hanoi are: The Old Quarter Vietnam Museum of Ethnology Temple of Literature Vietnamese Women’s Museum Ho Tay Lake Long Bien Bridge Lotte Tower Observation Deck Northern Vietnam This is Vietnam's big-sky country, with undulating mountains, tumbling rice terraces, and the region's famous winnowed-out karst geology. Northern Vietnam's cultural kaleidoscope is just as diverse as its scenery. Villages situated between paddy-field patchworks outside of Sapa, in this heartland of hill-tribe culture, give dazzling color to hectic highland markets. The meandering ribbon roads north of Hanoi depict a pastoral world far apart from Vietnam's horn-blaring big-city streets. This is the place to go if you want to go road-tripping in Vietnam. Some must-see places in Northern Vietnam during your Vietnam trip: Halong Bay Sapa Ba Be National Park Cat Ba Island Ha Jiang Province Bac Ha Mai Chau Central Vietnam Central Vietnam is the country's geographic center, rich in historical and cultural sites, beautiful beaches, and world-class national parks. A few not-to-be-missed sights here include Danang Hoi An Hue Demilitarized Zone (DMZ) Bach Ma National Park Ninh Binh Province Phong Nha-Ke Bang National Park The South-Eastern Coast of Vietnam This glittering coastline of beautiful white sand, formerly the heartland of the Cham civilization, is now a one-stop destination for Vietnam's best beach vacation resorts. Some unmissable attractions here include Long Hai Vung Tau Phan Thiet Mui Ne Quy Nhon Quang Ngai Cha Ban Cham Con Dao Islands The South-West Highlands The highlands of Vietnam are one of the few places in the country that entice visitors with the promise of adventure. The Ho Chi Minh Highway travels through coffee plantations, pine-studded mountains, rice terraces, massive reed-covered lakes, and quiet hill-tribe settlements. Some major attractions in this part of the country are: Cat Tien National Park Dalat Kon Tum Lak Lake Buon Ma Thuot Ngoan Muc Pass
Vietnam.The Best Hill Stations in Vietnam:: Below are the most visited hill stations in Vietnam during your Vietnam tours: Dalat Ngoan Muc Pass Cat Tien National Park Lak Lake Buon Ma Thuot Dalat Dalat is Vietnam's alter ego—the weather is cold rather than tropically hot (as is true in the case of a hill station), and the town is filled with lovely French-colonial homes rather than the glass and concrete structures of the big cities, and the surrounding farms are lush with strawberries and flowers rather than rice. Ngoan Muc Pass The breathtaking Ngoan Muc Pass is located 43 kilometers south of Dalat. On a clear day, the ocean, which is 55 kilometers away, can be seen from here. The roadway winds its way down the mountain, passing beneath two massive water pipes that connect the lake to the hydroelectric power facility at the pass's foot. Cat Tien National Park It consists of an incredibly bio-diverse lowland tropical rainforest. The 72,000-hectare park is one of Vietnam's most astonishing natural assets and is known for hiking, mountain biking, and birdwatching. Lak Lake Popularly called Ho Lak, this natural body of water in the central highlands is surrounded by idyllic rural surroundings. Buon Ma Thuot This hilly region produces some of Vietnam's greatest coffee, marketed and consumed in plenty throughout town. In March, Buon Ma Thuot has an annual Coffee Festival, during which gallons of the elixir get consumed.
Vietnam.Best beaches in Vietnam: The beaches are one of the highlights of a Vietnam tour package. Here are the most popular beaches in Vietnam to explore. 1. Con Dao Islands: Finding gorgeous beaches, snorkeling coral reefs, and riding motorcycles along empty coastal roads are what Con Dao is all about. 2. Ho Tram/Ho Coc: Going on a long drive between Phan Thiet and Long Hai, passing by a magnificent lighthouse and limitless sands, makes these beaches a highlight of touring in Vietnam. 3. Tam Hai Island: Searching for driftwood on white-sand beaches and looking for a whale graveyard are some of the offbeat activities that this stretch of sandy paradise is known for. 4. Nha Trang: In this brash town, soak in the unique beach atmosphere or go boating to the offshore islands.5. Vung Tau: A seaside paradise is quintessentially known for its surf, fresh seafood, and gastronomic variety.
Vietnam.Honeymoon in Vietnam: The lovers’ paradise in Vietnam, Dalat is a town that is known best destination for honeymoon in Vietnam. As with everything romantic, the French were the first to arrive on the scene, fleeing the sweltering heat of Saigon (Now, Ho Chi Minh City). They left their vacation houses and the atmosphere of a European town behind. The Vietnamese couldn't help enhance Dalat's inherent beauty by adding a few finishing touches to the romantic portrait painted by the French arrivals. Whether it's the Eiffel Tower-shaped radio tower, horse-drawn carriages, or the Valley of Love's obsessively colorful heart-shaped cut-outs, this town takes romance seriously. Dalat is a popular destination for domestic visitors. It's a combination of Le Petit Paris, the honeymoon capital, and the City of Eternal Spring (daytime temperatures range from 15°C to 24°C). For visitors, the mild climate makes it an ideal location for various adrenaline-pumping sports. A few unmissable spots for honeymoon in Vietnam include: King Palace Hang Nga Crazy House Cremaillere Railway Station Xuan Huong Lake Linh Quang Pagoda Truc Lam Pagoda and Cable Car Elephant Falls King Palace: A trip to the King Palace in Da Lat is like walking into a fairy tale. This beautiful colonial-style mansion, flanked by gardens, provides a glimpse into Vietnam's royal past. Ideal for couples celebrating their honeymoon who want a blend of romance and history. Hang Nga Crazy House: This unique architectural wonder is a crazy retreat that's perfect for couples seeking something out of the ordinary. Venturing through its dream-like corridors is an experience like no other and, thus, a must-visit on your Vietnam travels. Cremaillere Railway Station: One of the oldest railway stations in Vietnam, and there is something of an old-world charm to it. Ride a vintage train through mountain mists and enjoy stunning views together. Most Vietnam packages feature this jewel for a magical experience. Xuan Huong Lake: No place is as romantic as this sunset walk along this peaceful lake. You may also take a swan boat ride and drink in the charm of Da Lat, making it a must-visit on your Vietnam tour. Linh Quang Pagoda: If spirituality and serenity are your draw, this pagoda is a serene retreat with its ancient charms. It is the ideal setting to achieve inner peace and forge a stronger relationship. Most Vietnam tour packages also feature visits to ancient temples such as this one. Truc Lam Pagoda and Cable Car: Ride the cable car to Truc Lam Pagoda for a panoramic view that is simply breathtaking. Nestled amidst pine woods, it is a serene and dreamy spot that every honeymooning couple should visit in their Vietnam tour. Elephant Falls: If both of you love adventure, this magnificent waterfall will be a sight to behold. Situated just outside Da Lat, it's a breathtaking natural spectacle where you can relish the misty air and click enchanting honeymoon photos.
Vietnam.Best Places to Visit in Vietnam: A Vietnam trip is incomplete without visiting these beautiful places: Hanoi Halong Bay Haiphong Ba Be National Park Dalat Dien Bien Phu Sapa Lao Cai Hue DMZ Quang Ngai Nha Trang Danang Lak Lake Hanoi: The capital is the confluence of tradition and modernity. Walk through the Old Quarter, take a visit to Hoan Kiem Lake, and see the Temple of Literature. A tour of Vietnam would not be complete without sampling the street food of Hanoi, particularly Pho and Bun Cha. Halong Bay: A trip to Vietnam is not complete without a visit to Halong Bay, which is famous for its jade water and giant karst limestone towers. Cruise among the islands, kayak through them, or trek to the secluded caves. Most Vietnam tour packages include overnight cruises for an otherworldly experience. Haiphong: A relaxed port city, Haiphong is the entry point to Cat Ba Island and lesser-visited beaches. Ideal for travelers seeking a peaceful retreat from the typical Vietnam tour circuit. Ba Be National Park: Located in northern Vietnam, Ba Be is a peaceful paradise of beautiful lakes, waterfalls, and caves. Ideal for the adventure-seeker with Vietnam packages, this place has boat rides, trekking, and contact with ethnic minorities. Dalat: This hill town of cool climate, rolling hills, and flower gardens is a popular destination on Vietnam tour packages. Tour the Crazy House, visit waterfalls, and ride a cable car over pine forests. Dien Bien Phu: Popular for the battle which marked the end of French colonialism, this town has war relics and museums to offer. A Vietnam packages here is apt for history enthusiasts. Sapa: Draped in foggy mountains, Sapa is perfect for trekking and experiencing ethnic tribes' culture. The Fansipan Mountain cable car experience is a must on any Vietnam vacation. Lao Cai: Lao Cai is your gateway to Sapa and also provides offbeat Vietnam tour experiences with its busy markets and beautiful scenery. Hue: The old imperial capital boasts of the renowned Citadel, royal mausoleums, and temples. A trip on the Perfume River on a boat ride is a compulsory experience while venturing through Vietnam tour packages. DMZ: A must-tour for any history enthusiast, the Demilitarised Zone (DMZ) provides the tunnels, the war remnants, and the combat sites of the Vietnam War. Quang Ngai: Tragedy surrounds this province in the form of the infamous My Lai Massacre site, now a tranquil landscape of stunning beaches and rolling countryside. Nha Trang: Nha Trang is a beach lover's paradise with blue waters and a vibrant nightlife. Scuba diving and island-hopping excursions are included in many Vietnam packages. Danang: Dedicated to having golden beaches, Marble Mountains, and the famed Dragon Bridge, Danang deserves a place in every Vietnam packages. Don't forget to take in the spectacular Ba Na Hills and Golden Bridge. Lak Lake: For a not-so-touristy Vietnam packages experience, go to Lak Lake. Take on traditional canoe excursions, view elephants, and lodge in a lakeside residence for a classic experience. The local flavors and historical gems of Central Vietnam, the empty coastlines and ancient temples of the South-East Coast, the lovelorn artistic towns and the adventure-filled national parks of the South-West Highlands, the urban tours and night-life of Ho Chi Minh City, the white sands and the floating markets of the Mekong Delta, all of these destinations have something for visitors of all age groups. Vietnam never ceases to amaze people with the variety of destinations and experiences it has to offer.
Vietnam.Foods to Eat in Vietnam: The cuisine in Vietnam is as diverse as the rich highlands, flooded rice paddies, forested mountains, and the sandy coasts. While a long history of contact with outsiders adds complexity, locals have adapted the Chinese, Indian, French, and Japanese techniques and specialities to suit their own palates over the ages. The incredible array of excellent delicacies available in the country encourages one to experiment. Though the well-known Vietnamese classics – pho, spring rolls, and shrimp paste barbecued on sugar cane – are all delicious, it's worth venturing into the backstreets and markets to eat with the locals, as here is where you'll typically find the most authentic food. Being an Indian traveler though, one might wonder about the dining options available in a primarily carnivorous country. The good news is that when it comes to vegetarian dining, there are now more options than ever before. The bad news is that you haven't arrived in Vegetarian Heaven, as the Vietnamese are omnivorous eaters. They appreciate vegetables, but they also adore everything from insects to sea dwellers to air-borne critters. Vegetarian (com chay) restaurants may be found in most towns, mainly around Buddhist temples. These are frequently small, basic places frequented by devout Buddhists. Many people use 'mock meat’, tofu, and gluten to make meat-like recipes that are surprisingly tasty. Many vendors and diners follow Buddhist precepts by becoming vegetarian on the first and 15th day of each lunar month. This is an excellent opportunity to visit the marketplaces and try dishes that would otherwise be prohibited. Vietnam offers a diverse range of dining and drinking options. Fresh and vibrant flavours, superb street food, and fine-dining restaurants that are housed in renovated colonial structures allow visitors to sample the country's unrivalled culinary offerings. If one wants to uncover the culinary past of Vietnam, one can do so by joining cooking workshops, market visits, and strolling tours, often curated by the locals. Some of the dishes to try when traveling to Vietnam Banh Cuon: Originating from Northern Vietnam, these Vietnamese Steamed Rice Rolls are made with thin steamed rice batter sheets filled with ground pork and minced wood ear mushrooms. Bun Cha: A grilled pork and noodle Vietnamese meal claimed to have originated in Hanoi, Vietnam. Bun cha is a dish that consists of grilled fatty pork (cha) over a bed of white rice noodles (bun) and herbs, with a dipping sauce on the side. Pho Bo: Soft slippery noodles in a substantial, meaty yet fragrant broth make up a bowl of beef pho. Banh Khoai: Rice-flour batter is used to make these hefty, dessert-plate-sized crepes, which are then fried in special long-handled pans with oil. They're filled with shrimp, pork, egg, and bean sprouts, then wrapped in lettuce with fresh herbs and dunked in a sauce made from earthy fermented soybeans. Bun Bo Hue: A Vietnamese meal that originated in Hue. Hue beef noodle soup is the name of the dish, although it also contains pork. The broth in this dish is created from beef and pork bones.
Vietnam.Best Indian Restaurants in Vietnam: Vietnamese cuisine is heavily inspired by Indian flavours and cuisines, making Indian cuisine extremely popular among locals. Indian restaurants may be found in every nook and cranny of the country's major cities. There are simply too many Indian eateries in Vietnam for an Indian traveller to be far from sapid Indian flavours if hunger strikes unexpectedly. Dalcheeni, Hanoi: This is without a doubt one of the best Indian restaurants in Hanoi, Vietnam. The restaurant offers a huge selection of Indian meals, particularly vegetarian alternatives, making it a haven for Indian vegetarian food lovers in Vietnam. The environment is colourful, and the interior decorations are enticing, making it one of Vietnam's greatest Indian restaurants.Located at: 100 Xuan Dieu, Quang An, Tay Ho, Qung An Tay Ho Hanoi 100000, Vietnam Shri Natraj Indian Cuisine, Ho Chi Minh City: This venue will make you fall in love with its vibrant, highly lit, and very warm friendly ambience. Shri Natraj is owned and operated by an Indian man named Rahul, who is extremely friendly, pleasant, and hospitable to tourists. The lavish food is really amazing and pure authentic Indian, and Rahul has done his best to maintain the interiors appealing with a nice Indian touch.Located at: 41 Bui Thi Xuan, Phuong Pham Ngu Lao, Quan 1, Ho Chi Minh Namaste India, Ho Chi Minh City: In the centre of Bui Vien Boulevard, a wonderful and pleasant fine dining Indian restaurant. Its delectable delights will have just the appropriate quantity of spices and oil to make you feel like you're eating something homemade. Pakoras are wonderful appetisers that are hot, spicy and served with aromatic chutneys. Weekends in this city are vivid and colourful, and there is typically a live band performing fantastic music.Located at: 189 Bui Vien, Phuong Pham Ngu Lao, Quan 1, Ho Chi Minh City Namaste Ha Noi: This is without a doubt one of the top Indian restaurants in Hanoi. Amazingly lovely environment with fascinating décor, delivering superb authentic Indian cuisine. The restaurant's extensive menu, which includes cuisines from both North and South India, will make you ponder your options. Mr Gopi, the owner, is Indian, and he will frequently come down to your table to make your dining experience as pleasant as possible.Located at: 46 Tho Nhuom, Tran Hung Dao, Hoan Kiem, Hanoi Bollywood Indian Cuisine: This is a no-frills eatery with lightning-fast service and shockingly low prices. One of the top places in the Sky Garden neighbourhood for north and south Indian cuisines, this restaurant delivers fresh, flavorful Indian foods. This restaurant specialises in delectable Indian cuisine. Bollywood Indian Cuisine is extremely cost-effective.Located at: SA 26-2, Sky Garden 2, Quan 7, Vietnam
Vietnam.Things to Do in Vietnam: Here are the top 5 things to do in Vietnam during Vietnam tours. Cave trips: Hang Toi (Dark Cave) and the subterranean treasures of Hang Va are accessible via an exhilarating zipline, swimming, and kayaking combo. A visit to the Lotte Tower, Hanoi: The observation deck of this beautiful new monument, which also houses a plethora of cafes and restaurants, is the greatest site to evaluate Hanoi's growth in relation to the Old Quarter. Experience the cafe scene at Con Dao: New establishments such as Bar200 Con Dao and Infinity Cafe & Lounge have breathed new life into quiet ancient Con Son town. Visit a local brewery: Traveling hopheads will find additional local brews at BiaCraft in Saigon's developing District 2 neighbourhood, while Pasteur Street Brewing Company blends local ingredients like lemongrass and Dalat coffee. Vespa Adventures: There's no better way to explore the rural lanes, craft villages, and riverfront landscapes near Hoi An, than on the back of a vintage scooter. Rental agencies provide package deals on the zipping two-wheelers too.
Vietnam.Adventurous Things to Do in Vietnam: Though Vietnam took a long time to realise its enormous potential as an outdoor adventure destination, things have changed dramatically in recent years. Apart from trekking in the hilly north, visitors can now engage in activities such as rock climbing, canyoning, sea kayaking, and kitesurfing. Although some activities, such as mountain biking, may be done throughout the country, Da Lat has emerged as Vietnam's adventure sports centre and Mui Ne as its surf city. The northwest highlands of Sa Pa and, to a lesser extent, Mai Chau, are the simplest and most popular trekking destinations. Sa Pa is also the starting point for ascents of Fan Si Pan, the country's highest peak, which should only be attempted by experienced hikers. In Vietnam, mountain biking is another activity which is growing increasingly popular. The most popular route is from Hanoi to Ho Chi Minh City, which takes two to three weeks. Previously, this would have meant battling trucks and buses along Highway 1, but now, the most forward-thinking tour operators are arranging excursions down the Ho Chi Minh Highway, which travels along the western Truong Son mountain chain and is pleasantly free of traffic. For people with an affinity for water, Vietnam's three thousand kilometre coastline should make it a watersports paradise, but the alternatives are now limited for a variety of reasons. One is simply an issue of access: the necessary infrastructure is lacking. The existence of potentially dangerous undercurrents along much of the coast, along with strong winds at certain times of the year, is even more critical. During the summer, many of the major beach resorts employ lifeguards or place flags indicating where it is safe to swim. If you're going somewhere else, double-check before you go. Here are a few places to look out for on your Vietnam tour packages: Canyoning in Dalat: Dalat is not only a picturesque hill station; it's also an adventure destination! Book a canyoning trip, and you'll rappel down falls, jump into deep rock pools, and canoe through jungle rivers. If you enjoy adrenaline-laden activities, canyoning in Dalat should top your Vietnam packages. Off-road Biking in Sapa: Dreaming of experiencing Vietnam's stunning mountains most excitingly? Get on an off-road bicycle and challenge Sapa's bumpy roads. While cycling through terraced rice paddies and isolated villages, you'll discover a Vietnam not many tourists encounter. Tailor your trail according to your expertise to give your Vietnam packages an unforgettable touch. Kitesurfing in Mui Ne: If you are hankering for adventure on the water, go to Mui Ne. There are strong, reliable winds that make this coastal town Vietnam's kitesurfing haven. Whether you want to be an amateur or a professional, you can learn and navigate the waves like a pro. It's the ideal add-on to any Vietnam packages for water sports enthusiasts. Caving in Phong Nha: Phong Nha is a caver’s paradise! Home to some of the largest caves in the world, including Hang Son Doong, this region is a must-visit for thrill-seekers. If you’re planning your Vietnam tour packages, make sure to include an expedition to the Phong Nha caves. Whether it’s a quick half-day tour of Paradise Cave or a multi-day trek into Hang En, you’re in for an unforgettable experience.
Vietnam.Interesting Facts about Vietnam: Vietnam is the top exporter of black pepper in the world. Vietnam's coastline stretches for almost 2,000 miles. Vietnam is home to roughly 16% of the world's species, indicating a high level of biodiversity Vietnam is the world's largest cashew nut exporter Fansipan is the tallest mountain in Vietnam, and indeed all of Indochina. It is known as "the roof of Indochina" because of its height of 3,143 metres In Vietnamese culture, the turtle is a significant emblem. The turtle is said to represent intellect, strength, and longevity, and Vietnamese people associate it with wins and independence following invasions at various points in history
Vietnam.Shopping in Vietnam: Hanoi: Visit the shops along P Hang Gai, P To Tich, P Hang Khai, and P Cau Go for Vietnamese handicrafts like textiles and lacquerware. Embroidered tablecloths, T-shirts, and wall hangings can be found in P Hang Gai and its continuation, P Hang Bong. P Hang Gai is also a great destination to get silk and custom-made clothing.Clothing, cosmetics, imitation sunglasses, bootleg DVDs and software, T-shirts, musical instruments, plumbing supplies, herbal treatments, jewellery, religious offerings, spices, coffee, and much more can be found in the ancient Old Quarter. Assume that practically all of the branded items you encounter are counterfeit. Mekong Delta: The night market in Duong Dong and the pearl farm in Long Beach's center are your best bets for souvenirs. Phu Quoc Pearls is a must-visit if you're looking for black, yellow, white, pink, or any other color of pearls. Various pieces of jewellery produced from the pearls are on display. Although avid pearl searchers can discover cheaper products in the village of Ham Ninh's kiosks, you can be certain of authenticity here. Ho Chi Minh City: Tourists are aggressively sold junk on the city's crowded streets, but there are lots of fantastic buys at bustling markets, antique stores, silk boutiques, and speciality shops selling ceramics, ethnic fabrics, lacquered bamboo, and custom-made apparel. Any shopping trip should begin on Dong Khoi and its intersecting streets, which are lined with galleries and boutiques and offer high-quality handicrafts and souvenirs. Ben Thanh and its neighboring streets are one of Saigon's liveliest neighborhoods due to its central location. Everything that the Saigonese eat, wear, or use is stacked high, and souvenir items are plentiful. Vendors are determined, and prices are normally higher than elsewhere, so haggle hard and ignore any signs that say "Fixed Price." Kon Tum Highlands: Kon Tum and the surrounding villages are ideal places to shop if you collect indigenous handicrafts. Although some items are cheaper in the villages, Kon Tum has a superior overall range. Buon Ma Thuot: Stock up on coffee here because it is cheaper and of greater quality here than in Ho Chi Minh City or Hanoi. Before you buy, have a look at the coffee shop strip on Ly Thuong Kiet. Nha Trang: In the blocks near the corner of Tran Quang Khai and Hung Vuong in Nha Trang, there are some good arts and crafts shops. Nguyen Thi Minh Khai is lined with fashion businesses selling anything from sling bags to sunglasses.
Vietnam.Best Places to Stay in Vietnam: In general, the quality of lodging in Vietnam is great. The range caters to all budgets in the main tourist locations, and while prices are a little high by Southeast Asian standards, the quality is generally also high. With the construction boom still going on, new rooms are being built all the time, which is fantastic for travelers because it keeps costs down and service standards high. Along the coast, there has been major growth in the number of luxury resorts (mostly oriented at the Asian package tour industry), although budget travelers and those travelling off the beaten path will find good cheap lodging all across the country. Staff at tourist information booths in Ho Chi Minh City and Hanoi will call to reserve a room for you, and booking online is becoming more convenient. When asking cyclo or taxi drivers for recommendations, be wary of being informed that the hotel of your choice is full or closed. It's also worth noting that Vietnam is rife with imitators; to prevent being transported to a hotel with a similar name, write down the street name and show it to your driver. When you've chosen a hotel, have a look at a few different rooms before deciding, as standards might vary greatly even within the same business. You should also double-check the bed configuration, as there are numerous variations throughout Vietnam. A "single" room may contain a single or twin bed, whereas a "double" room may contain two, three, or four single beds, a double, a single and a double, and so on. Check out for hotels in different categories and different budgets.
Vietnam.Visa Process for Indians: Indian passport holders considering a vacation to Vietnam must apply for a visa for the duration of their stay in the country. Yes, Indian passport holders are required to obtain a visa in order to enter the nation. However, it is now easier than ever for Indian citizens to obtain a Vietnam visa, thanks to online applications for visas on arrival. Indians visiting Vietnam can acquire their visa on arrival at one of the country's participating international airports, Ha Noi, Da Nang, Ho Chi Minh City, and Nha Trang. To apply for a visa to visit Vietnam, you must submit the following papers: Passport from India, valid for at least six months beyond the date of travel to Vietnam. For the visa stamp, you'll need at least two blank pages in your passport. Two passport-sized photos.
Vietnam.Currency in Vietnam: The official currency of Vietnam is the Vietnamese Dong. One Vietnamese Dong equals 0.0034 INR (as of 1st Jan 2025). Before you travel to Vietnam, make sure you purchase enough Dong to last you the entire duration of the Vietnam trip. Thomas Cook makes it easy for you to buy Forex online by just in one click. It is suggested that you buy forex in the form of a multi-currency card which can be used all across Vietnam as most of the establishments accept digital forms of payment, even the small mom and pop stores. However, the saying that “Cash is King” cannot be ignored completely and hence, it is advisable to keep a certain amount of Dong in cash as well, mostly in the form of small change, for use in cases of emergency.

faq.question: What is the best time to visit Odisha other than peak season?
faq.answer: Besides the peak winter season, you can visit Odisha during the monsoon (June to September). Check out our Odisha tour packages to find an ideal deal for your trip.

faq.question: What adventure activities does Odisha offer?
faq.answer: In terms of adventure activities, Odisha offers camping, trekking, wildlife safari and a wide range of water sports.

faq.question: What are some must-visit waterfalls in Odisha?
faq.answer: Some must-visit waterfalls in Odisha include Barehipani falls, Khandadhar falls, Duduma falls, and Harishankar falls.

faq.question: Which locations are best for shopping in Odisha?
faq.answer: If you want an ideal shopping experience in Odisha, make sure to check out the malls and local markets at Puri, Bhubaneswar, and Rourkela. The historical places of Orissa, like Cuttack and Konark, are also great sites for shopping for souvenirs.

faq.question: What are the best Odisha hill stations for a honeymoon trip?
faq.answer: The best hill stations for honeymoon in Odisha are Daringbadi, Mahendragiri, Tatapani and Chandragiri. They also feature some of the greatest tourist attractions in Odisha.

faq.question: How far is Puri from Bhubaneswar?
faq.answer: Puri is located about 65 kilometres from Bhubaneswar, which is a 1-and-a-half-hour drive by road. You can find great Puri and Bhubaneswar Orissa tourism packages on our website.

faq.question: When can we visit Shree Jagannath Temple in Puri?
faq.answer: The darshan timings for Shri Jagannath Temple in Puri are usually from 5 AM to 10 PM.

faq.question: What are some must-try desserts in Odisha?
faq.answer: During your Odisha trip, make sure to taste the must-try local sweet dishes like Chhena Poda, Chenna Gaja, Rasabali and Malpua.

faq.question: What are some famous tourist places near Odisha?
faq.answer: Some famous tourist places near Odisha are Kolkata, Digha, Srikakulam, Vizag and so on.

faq.question: What is the best picnic spot in Odisha?
faq.answer: Among the various options, the most popular picnic spot in Odisha is considered to be the Nandankanan Zoological Park. This zoological park is also the best place to visit in Odisha for seeing white tigers.

Orissa.Introduction: The mystical land of Odisha, formerly called Orissa, is a state that lies in the eastern region of India. Besides ranking as one of the largest states by area, Odisha is also a massively popular tourist destination in the country. It is home to some of the most impressive locations, scenic beauty, religious and historical sites of importance, rich cultural heritage, and much more. In addition, this Indian state is blessed with a beautiful coastline that stretches more than 450 kilometers along the mesmerising Bay of Bengal. Odisha’s jaw-dropping beauty and well-established tourism infrastructure make it a perfect holiday destination. If you are looking to experience the rich alluring beauty of nature, witness India’s vibrant wildlife, delve into the regional culture or want a spiritual getaway, you can add Odisha as your holiday destination. If you are looking for a solo-short break to unwind amidst the calming waves of the Bay of Bengal or the whole exuberant Odisha experience with your family, check out the great Odisha tour packages that Thomas Cook has to offer. These plans are handpicked by professionals and aim to deliver an exquisite travel experience by taking care of the essential aspects, including transport, accommodation, food, sightseeing, and so on. Considering your start location and budget, you can pick an ideal package that makes sense. You can also check out our quick guide on Odisha tourism to get a brief idea of the whole thing, including a detailed must-visit Odisha tourism place list.
Orissa.Table of Contents: Most Popular Odisha Tour Packages Best time to visit Odisha How to reach Odisha Best honeymoon destinations in Odisha Best places to visit in Odisha Best hill stations and Beaches in Odisha What to Eat in Odisha Shopping in Odisha Things to do in Odisha Adventurous things to Do in Odisha Interesting facts about Odisha People also ask about Odisha Tour Packages
Orissa.Most Popular Odisha Tour Packages: Odisha packs a series of different iconic experiences and locations that you can choose from. On Thomas Cook’s website, you can find more than 10 exciting Odisha tour packages offered at different budget ranges and with varying inclusions. Here are some popular choices:
Orissa.Best time to visit Odisha: Tour Packages NameDurationPricePuri Getway2 Nights 3 DaysRs. ₹14 000Bhubaneswar And Puri Darshan Yatra 3N3 Nights 4 DaysRs. ₹17 400Scenic Odisha5 Nights 6 DaysRs. ₹31 500Bhubaneshwar Bhitarkanika And Puri6 Nights 7 DaysRs. ₹33 200Puri Bhubaneswar Darshan Yatra 4 Nights 5 DaysRs. ₹36 000Some people take a trip to Odisha for its mesmerising beaches and scenic visuals, while others visit the location to witness its religious, cultural, and historical aspects. There are three main seasons to tour Odisha, each of which can offer a completely different travel experience during your visit. Among them, winter (October to February) is widely preferred as the best time to visit Odisha due to its delightful weather. The temperature usually remains within the range of 16 to 28°C, with an average of 22°C. However, it can drop in the range of 8 to 14°C during the months of December and January. The weather stands clear through the day with mistful early morning and late night cold winds. It is also the peak Odisha tourism season, which attracts visitors from around the nation and the globe. Some of the highlights of visiting Odisha this season include the Durga Puja festival, hill stations, trekking and adventure activities, the Konark Dance festival, and much more. Make sure to pack some light warm clothes as it can get a bit chilly during winter in Odisha. Other than that, as winter is the peak tourism season, it is recommended that you make your hotel reservations, flight bookings, and other plans in advance in order to avoid unavailability problems. Take a look at our well-planned and affordable Orissa tour packages with customisation options for an ideal trip. Besides winter, many tourists prefer visiting Odisha in the months of June to September, which is the time of monsoon. The temperature remains pretty bearable throughout, with a range of 25 to 32°C. Days are quite humid, while the nights paint a soothing picture surrounded by cool breezes. Monsoon in Odisha is generally the shoulder season for tourism. Places remain comparatively less crowded during this period, while random drizzles and showers multiply the natural greenery of the region. Some key highlights of Odisha tourism in monsoon include the divine scenic beauty of nature, the Raja Parba festival, the Nuakhai festival and so on. When planning your trip to Odisha during the monsoon, check the weather forecast in advance and keep in mind that sudden rain showers may obstruct your sightseeing experience. Summer, on the other hand, is the low season for Odisha tourism. It usually stretches from March to May, with an average temperature range of 26 to 41°C. The hot weather and humidity remain prevalent through this period, making it quite troublesome to travel during the day. The key highlight of this season is the Rath Yatra, which is a famous festival in the region. If you are ready to deal with the annoying summer heat, you can tour Odisha during this time and get some great deals on transport and accommodations, alongside low crowd hindrance. Here is a detailed guide on the best season-wise time to visit Odisha.
Orissa.How to reach Odisha?: One of the best parts about Odisha tourism is that it is well-connected to the popular parts of India. It features air, rail and road transport options if you wish to travel to this beautiful Indian state. In addition, the intrastate connectivity of Odisha is very accessible. Here are the best ways to reach Odisha: Reaching Odisha by air Reaching Odisha by railways Reaching Odisha by road Reaching Odisha by air Travelling within Odisha Reaching Odisha by air Travelling by air is usually the most comfortable and fastest way to reach Odisha. Its main airport is located in the capital city of Bhubaneshwar, called the Biju Patnaik International Airport. It lies just 3 kilometres from the centre of the city and offers great connectivity to visitors who are flying in from major Indian cities, including Chennai, Kolkata, Mumbai, Delhi and so on. It also offers a great starting point for Odisha tourism as the famous tourist site Puri lies about 65 kilometres from it. You will be able to find daily domestic and international flights from reputable airlines at Biju Patnaik International Airport. Make sure to check and book flights to Bhubaneshwar a few days before your trip. Reaching Odisha by railways Another popular transport option to reach Odisha is by train. It is well-connected to India’s major cities by its railways, with daily running trains to and from Odisha. Usually, the two main railway stations of the state are Bhubaneshwar and Puri, which have direct trains from and to Kolkata, Mumbai, Hyderabad, Chennai, Tirupati and so on. Coromandel Express, Rajdhani Express and Konark express are popular trains running through the Bhubaneswar railway. If you are travelling from Kolkata to Puri, you can take the Shri Jagannath Express special train, which is about an 8-9 hour journey. Reaching Odisha by road If you are travelling to Odisha from a nearby location or looking for an exciting road trip experience, you can choose from the various routes that connect to this Indian state. You can opt for a private car or a rental cab for this purpose and hop on a road that offers easy access to Odisha from your starting point. For instance, individuals travelling from Andhra Pradesh or West Bengal can take up NH 16 to reach Odisha. Interstate buses are also a great option, especially if you are travelling from a neighbouring state. Travelling within Odisha In terms of transportation within Odisha, by road is the best alternative. This includes buses, cabs, autorickshaws, rental cars and so on. Local trains can also come in handy for travelling within Odisha.
Orissa.Best honeymoon destinations in Odisha: Amidst the immense natural beauty, local culture and traditions, and regional heritage, there are many romantic places in Orissa. This is why it ranks among the top honeymoon destinations in the country. For instance, Puri is considered the best Orissa tourist spot for newlyweds who want to soak in the rich culture of the region and witness popular sightseeing locations. In addition, the Puri beach works as a perfect location for romantic strolls along the wet sand and watching beautiful visuals painted by the rising and setting sun with your partner. Other than Puri, the best places to visit in Orissa for a honeymoon include Chilika Gopalpur Chandragiri Chilika The Chilika lake is the largest brackish water lagoon in all of Asia and a massive tourist attraction in Odisha. It spreads over a massive area across the district of Ganjam, Khorda and Puri. It is a popular honeymoon destination for couples, with key attractions being the Honeymoon island, Nalabana island and Bird sanctuary, Rambha Bay, a romantic boat drive and lots more. It also features some fantastic resorts and local Odisha cuisine. Chilika is also one of the ideal Odisha places to visit in general. Gopalpur Besides being one of the most beautiful places in Odisha, Gopalpur is also an excellent choice for a honeymoon destination. Its impressive beach and accommodations add to a serene holiday experience with your loved one. The Gopalpur lighthouse and the availability of water sports are some of the major attractions of this place. For ideal Gopalpur Orissa tourism packages, you can check out our Scenic Odisha trip plan. Chandragiri If you are planning your trip to Odisha to experience its vivid culture with your partner, don’t forget to check out Jirang (Chandragiri). It is a small village located within the district of Gajapati in Odisha, featuring a glimpse of the beautiful Tibetan culture and population. It is among the most famous Orissa places to visit. The major couple's attractions in Chandragiri are its breathtaking valley and hills, Khasada waterfall, Buddhist Monastery and Tibetian food. You can go through our exciting honeymoon tour package range to plan a memorable trip with your better half.
Orissa.Best places to visit in Odisha:: The imposing land of Odisha is the centre for many religious, sightseeing and adventure activities. Besides its stunning national parks, beaches and hill stations, it offers a vivid insight into the local arts, culture and tradition. Here are some best places to visit in Odisha: Puri Bhubaneswar Cuttack Konark Rourkela Berhampur Puri Puri is one of the main tourist places in Odisha. It is perfect for a calming vacation by the beach with family or friends. There are also numerous sightseeing locations in and near Puri, including the famous Puri Jagannath temple, Beach sand art, Chilika lake and Bird sanctuary, Raghurajpur Craft village and Gundicha Ghar temple. Don’t forget to check out the 5-day Puri Beach festival if you are visiting in November. Bhubaneswar Referred to as the city of temples and the land of iconic architecture, Bhubaneswar is one of the major tourist places in Orissa. It is the perfect location for individuals who are in the state’s historical and cultural heritage. Some of the main tourist attractions in and near Bhubaneswar include Udayagiri and Khandagiri Caves, Nandan Kanan Zoological Park, Lingaraj Temple, Parasurameswara Temple, Ratnagiri Buddhist Excavations and Satkosia Tiger Reserve. Our exciting range of Orissa tour packages includes all major tourist places of the region, like Bhubaneswar and Puri and so on. Cuttack Among the other important places to visit in Orissa, Cuttack holds a significant position in the state’s historical and cultural heritage. It piques the interest of many tourists as it still resembles the rich beauty of the region under a vintage filter. Some excellent attractions in and near Cuttack include the Fort of Barabati, Ansupa Lake, Singanatha Temple, Mahanadi Barrage, and Bhitarkanika Wildlife Sanctuary. Konark Located about 65 kilometres by road from Bhubaneswar, Konark is home to several sculptures dating back to ancient times and ranks among the top Orissa famous places. The Sun Temple in Konark is a UNESCO World Heritage site, which is an ancient architectural marvel of India. Besides this, some other exciting tourist attractions in and near Konark include Chandrabhnaga beach, Archeological Museum, and Konark Annual Dance Festival. Consider our Konark Orissa Tour Packages for an exciting holiday. Rourkela The planned city of Rourkela within the Sundargarh district is one of the best tourist places in Odisha. Besides being famous for its blooming industrial sector, it is home to several of the state’s key tourist attractions. Hence during your next Odisha trip, don’t forget to visit Rourkela for its waterfall, trekking, and birdwatching experience at Khandadhar Nature Camp, Hanuman Vatika, Nehru Traffic Park and so on. Berhampur Berhampur is called Odisha’s “silk city” and is located in the Ganjam district. It is one of the famous places in Odisha for its silk saree shopping and street food. The must-visit sites in and near this location include Biju Patnaik Park Brahmapur, Sri Sri Sidhi Binayak Pitha, Potagarh, Aryapali Beach, Taratarini temple, Panchama, etc.
Orissa.Best Hill stations and Beaches in Odisha: If you are visiting Odisha for your next trip, you will be in for a versatile experience. Amidst the famous places in Odisha lie some of the most iconic beaches and hill stations. Hill stations in Odisha Daringbadi- Daringbadi, a small town with lush greenery and spellbinding hills, is popularly known as the “Kashmir of Odisha''. It is one of the best places to visit in Odisha if you want to capture the charming views of nature and its landscapes. You can also partake in the exciting activities Daringbadi has to offer, which include birdwatching, trekking and jungle safari. Mahendragiri hills- Located about 40 kilometres from Paralakhemundi, Mahendragiri is yet another popular hill station in Odisha. It is the second-highest mountain peak in the state and is famous for its mesmerising natural beauty. The Mahendratanaya river flowing down in this location is a key attraction of this hill station in Orissa. Other hill stations- You can also check out other hill stations in Odisha, like Dumuriput, Tensa Hill, Taptapani, Deuli Hill, Devagiri Range, Malayagiri Hills, and so on. Beaches in Odisha Puri beach Puri is the main Odisha tourist spot for beach vacations. The fine beach of Puri is renowned for its serene yet gushing waves and peaceful sunrise/sunset views. It also hosts many water adventures and beach activities that you can participate in. You can take a peaceful walk on the sand, go for camel rides or conquer the waves on a jet ski at the Puri beach. Looking for a quick trip? Check out our Short Break to Puri package in the summer. Talasari beach The Talasari beach in the district of Baleswar is another popular beach site in Odisha. It is a super-clean, low-rush area, perfect for a calming swim or immersive boat ride during your trip. The local cuisine and street food are a must-try while you are there. Others You can also check out other popular beaches in Orissa, like Chandipur beach, Gopalpur beach, Aryapalli beach, Astaranga beach, Beleswar beach, Paradeep beach and so on.
Orissa.What to Eat in Odisha?: While you tour Odisha and experience its grand aspects, consider diving deep into the excellent local cuisine that the region has to offer. Here are some must-try Odia delicacies: Khichdi Macha ghanta Rasabali Chakuli pitha Kanika Khichdi This special khichdi-variant is an immensely important dish in the land and culture of Odisha. This finger-licking nutritious delight is prepared with ghee, rice, light spices, lentil and chopped vegetables. Besides being a popular household meal, Khicede is also served as the primary “bhog” of the Shree Jagannath Temple in Puri. Macha ghanta Having a magnificent coastline makes Odisha a place with tasty seafood dishes and recipes. Macha Ghanta is a prime example of such a dish, which is a fish curry prepared with chilli powder, chana dal, garlic, onion, tomatoes, cinnamon sticks and potatoes. Its delectable taste extends when paired with a salad and steamed rice. Rasabali This is a major Odia dessert, served as a part of Shree Jagannath Temple’s Chappan Bhog. It includes small vada-shaped tikkis made with wheat flour, paneer, sugar, and cardamom powder, which are deep-fried and soaked in flavoured milk. Chakuli pitha Pitha is a popular dish in India’s eastern region. The Chakuli pitha is one of its special variants made in Odisha with ingredients like urad dal and rice batter. The final product looks similar to tortillas and is served with curry, ghugni, pickles or aloo bhaja. Kanika Kanika is a local favourite, often prepared during auspicious or special occasions. It is also a part of Shree Jagannath Temple’s Chappan Bhog and is prepared with basmati rice, cashew nuts, ghee, cinnamon, sugar and cardamom as the main ingredients. This sweet dish can be enjoyed as a dessert or a main course alongside veg and non-veg curry.
Orissa.Shopping in Odisha?: Odisha has some of the greatest local craftworks and markets for ideal souvenir shopping. If you are wondering what to shop in Odisha, here are some options you can consider: Jewellery work Wooden mask Konark wheel Sarees Jewellery work Odisha is home to some of the finest works of jewellery craftsmanship in the country. These handmade pieces, which include jewellery boxes, earrings, necklaces and so on, can be an excellent gifts for yourself or a loved one. Look for Filigree work for the finest silver carved ornaments available in the major cities of the state. Wooden mask The hand-painted wooden masks in Odisha are great examples of the immaculate skills of the local artisans. These souvenirs help pack the essence of the region’s tradition with you to your home. These wooden masks of Hindu deities can be ideal home decor items, and the best place to buy them is at Raghurajpur. It is also one of the best places to see in Orissa. Konark wheel The Konark wheel is a beautiful and must-have souvenir you can buy during your visit to the Sun Temple. This souvenir resembles the wheels of the Sun Temple in a miniature format, usually carved in stone or wood. Sarees The handlooms in Odisha produce some of the most beautiful sarees in the eastern region of India. This is why shopping for many tourists largely includes checking out and buying famous sarees from well-known places. For saree shopping, you can check out Pasapalli sarees (Bargarh district), Bomkai sarees (Ganjam district), Kotpad sarees (Koraput), and so on. If you are shopping in Bhubaneswar, you can check out Ekamra Haat, OSHC on Rajpath road, Pipli, BMC Keshari Mall, and so on. For shopping in Puri, you can visit Swargadwarda market, Chandan bazaar, Red Cross road, and other local markets.
Orissa.Things to do in Odisha:: There are several amazing things that you can do solo or in a group in Odisha. Some examples include Enjoying the beach experience Exploring important historical sites Witnessing the religious marvels Spending time with nature- Enjoying the beach experience The beach experience in Odisha is an outstanding part of the whole tourism aspect. There are tons of beautiful beaches throughout its massive coastline, and you can spend some quality time there while watching the sunset or sunrise. You can also consider doing water sports, taking strolls on the sand, trying out seafood, and more at the various Odisha beaches. Exploring important historical sites Along with famous cultural and religious tourist attractions, there are many historical places in Odisha. Exploring these locations and learning about their mysteries is one of the most exciting things you can do during your trip. Barabait fort and Khandagiri caves are prime examples of such historical monuments of Odisha. Witnessing the religious marvels The numerous temples across Odisha provide a sense of divinity and offer an insight into the ancient architectural beauty of the place. So when visiting Odisha, visit these religious marvels, including the Sun Temple, Sir Jagannath Puri Temple, Chausath Yogini Temple and so on. Spending time with nature Most famous tourist places in Odisha are surrounded by nature’s captivating beauty. So when visiting Odisha, spend some time amidst the hill stations, waterfalls and valleys.
Orissa.Adventurous Things to Do in Odisha:: If you are looking for some adventurous things to do in Odisha, you can consider: Jet-skiing- If you are into boat rides and water sports, you will love the adrenaline-pumping jet-skiing experience Odisha has to offer. The Puri Konark Marine Drive, Tampara Lake and Chilika are some of the best places in Odisha where you can partake in this thrilling activity. You can also find scuba diving, snorkelling, paddle surfing, rafting and other water sports experiences at Ramachandi beach. Trekking- Camping and trekking go hand in hand, and you can avail of both across various nature camps in Odisha. For this adventurous activity, you can check out the nature camps at Simlipal, Satkosia, Debigarh and Bhitarkanika. Camel/horseback riding- For some light-hearted thrilling rides through the sandy beach of Odisha, you can consider going for horse or camelback riding. You can take on this activity at any popular beach in Odisha, including Puri beach. Wildlife safari- If you want to witness the regional wildlife up close in an exciting safari experience, the Odisha Wildlife Tour is a must-try. During this tour, you will be able to see animals like Gharial crocodiles, elephants, white tigers, a variety of birds, deer and much more. Popular Odisha wildlife tours and safari sites include Nandankanan, Simlipal, Ratnagiri, Satkosia, and so on. Many popular Orissa tourism packages come with wildlife sightseeing as a standard inclusion.
Orissa.Interesting facts about Odisha: If you are in need of some more convincing for scheduling your next trip to Odisha, take a look at these interesting facts: Odisha’s Bhubaneswar, the “Temple City” of India, is home to over 600 gorgeous temples and has a history that spans over 3000 years. Odisha’s Konark Sun Temple, built in the 13th century, features 24 spoked wheels that can accurately represent current time by casting a shadow. It is among the most famous monuments of Odisha. The Chilika Lake in Odisha is the nesting ground for several types of migratory birds coming from Siberia and a place for endangered Irrawaddy Dolphins. The grand open-air kitchen inside Puri’s Shri Jagannath Temple cooks food for over 1 lakh devotees on a daily basis, in 15,000 clay pots which are for one-time use only. Odisha is home to over 60 unique tribal groups with well-defined traditions and cultures. The famous Daringbad hill station is the only place to experience snowfall during winter in Odisha. Odisha’s massive 485 kilometres long coastline features some of the most beautiful and unexplored beaches in the country.

Germany.Introduction: Germany, officially the Federal Republic of Germany, is a captivating nation tucked away in Central Europe. With the Alps to the South and the North Sea and Baltic Sea to the North, it has a very diverse and interesting topography that tourists love to explore on their visit. Germany is one of the most visited destinations in Europe, as well as the world, and rightfully so. With medieval castles peeking through the horizon, historic cities boasting their rich heritage and lush greenery blanketing the colossal hills, Germany brings fairytale stories and landscapes to real life. To explore and experience the best of this European nation, consider booking your Germany trip with us. Thomas Cook offers professionally curated and affordable Germany tour packages for all kinds of themed vacations. From adventure-focused journeys and cultural trips to well-balanced family vacations and romantic honeymoon getaways, you can find Germany travel packages that fit your eclectic travel needs on our website. We also provide customisable Germany holiday packages for travellers who want their own choice of flair to their vacation plan. From checking out the best of German cities to exploring its spellbinding natural and cultural heritage sites, the itinerary of each of our Germany tour packages provides a unique and unforgettable experience. We offer a streamlined vacation experience on a budget, where aspects like hotels, meals, sightseeing, and flights are all taken care of under the price of the Germany tour packages from India. You can find 7N/8D Germany travel packages starting from only INR 1,51,300/adult up to more comprehensive 16N/17D plans priced at INR 4,93,800/adult. Also, when booking Germany tour packages from Thomas Cook, you can avail of our exclusive offers and discounts, such as up to INR 50,000 off on a Germany trip, a free American Tourister Bag on a per-room basis, etc.
Germany.Most Popular Germany Tour Packages: If you are interested in a Germany tour, you may want to check out our top-performing Germany tour packages first. Here is a list of the popular Germany packages: Tour Packages NameDurationPriceEuropean Express - Summer 202612 Nights 13 DaysRs. ₹340 200European Express With 4 Nights Mediterranean Cruise - Summer 202616 Nights 17 DaysRs. ₹376 700European Express With 3 Nights Mediterranean Cruise - Summer 202615 Nights 16 DaysRs. ₹376 700Sensational Europe - Summer 202614 Nights 15 DaysRs. ₹412 000Sensational Europe With 3 Nights Mediterranean Cruise - Summer 202617 Nights 18 DaysRs. ₹448 500
Germany.Best time to visit Germany: Before you start looking for Germany tour packages, consider learning about the tourism experience it offers in different seasons. In general, the best time to visit Germany is between March and September. So, when planning a Germany trip, consider scheduling it during this period. Here are details of the season-wise best time to visit Germany: October-February Autumn fades out, and winter chimes in during these months. You can expect cold and wet weather with an average temperature range of 0-12°C. It is the off-season for Germany tour and travel as strong winds, storms and shorter daylight hours hinder tourist activities. Some consider it a good time for a budget Germany trip and attending local festivities. Also, skiing is one of the best things to do in Germany, and January is considered the best month to visit Germany for skiing adventures. Get the best deals on Germany trip packages for winter on Thomas Cook. July-September Summer mainly prevails between July and August. It is the peak season for Germany tour and travel due to its gentle weather and longer daylight hours. You can expect an average temperature range of 18-30°C. It is the best time to visit Germany to attend key local festivals, engage in sightseeing, attempt hiking, road trips, and other fun outdoor activities etc. September, on the other hand, is the best month to visit Germany for witnessing its beautiful autumn charm without needing to deal with heavy rainfall. Due to peak season demand, it is recommended that you book Germany trip packages for the summer in advance. March-June It is springtime from March to June in Germany. It is shoulder season for the Germany trip, with overall pleasant weather and occasional rainfall and chilly mornings. You can expect an average temperature range of 8-20°C. It is considered the best time to go Germany for sightseeing and hiking activities. You can enjoy slight shoulder season discounts on hotels, flights and activities. Find and book Germany trip packages for any season on our website.
Germany.How to reach Germany?: For a convenient vacation experience, book Germany tour packages featuring round-trip flights from Thomas Cook. Here are the options available for how to go to Germany: By train If you are thinking “How to reach Germany by rail?”, note that there are no direct trains between India and Germany. However, Germany is well-connected to its European neighbours via an extensive railway network. Most trains are operated under Deutsche Bahn (DB) and they link Germany to Poland, Denmark, Austria, the Czech Republic, Switzerland and Italy. Several EuroCity (EC) trains also operate on this route. High-speed InterCity Express (ICE) and the Thalys trains are also popular options to consider on the Germany trip. By Road For your Germany trip, note that there are no direct road links between India and Germany. If you are travelling from a neighbouring European country, you can reach Germany by road. There are many bus companies that link major German cities to their neighbouring countries. Some notable names are Eurolines, MeinFernBus, FlixBus and ADAC Postbus. By Air The best answer to “How to reach Germany from India?” is by flight. There are several international airports in prominent German cities that are linked to major cities in India by regular flights. Some of the major international airports that you can consider booking a flight to for your Germany trip are Frankfurt Airport (FRA), Düsseldorf International Airport (DUS), Munich International Airport (MUC) and Berlin Brandenburg Airport (BER). For travel reference, a direct flight from New Delhi, India, to Frankfurt, Germany, takes around 9 hours. You can book Germany travel packages featuring flights from Thomas Cook.
Germany.Best Places to Visit in Germany: There are tons of popular places to visit in Germany, many of which are featured in the itineraries of Germany tour packages by Thomas Cook. Here are some examples of notable Germany tourist places:
Germany.Famous Places to Visit: Brandenburg Gate: Situated in Berlin, the Brandenburg Gate is an architectural masterpiece, modelled after the Propylaea of the Acropolis of Athens. The site is one of the best places to see in Germany. Neuschwanstein Castle: Neuschwanstein Castle is a very important castle and one of the most popular German tourist places. Tucked in the foothills of the Alps, its fascinating architecture is a blend of Gothic and Romanesque revival styles. Middle Rhine Valley: It is a notable UNESCO World Heritage Site, considered one of the top places to visit in Germany. Cliff top castles, historic towns and rolling forested hills by the side of the Rhine River create a captivating scenery. It is a notable inclusion in several Germany trip packages. Cologne Cathedral: Embrace the brilliance and grandeur of Cologne Cathedral’s Gothic architecture on your Germany tour. Its towering spires, intricate carvings and stained-glass windows will leave you awe-struck. Also, while in Cologne, do try some of the fine dishes of German cuisine. Saxon Switzerland National Park: Located along the Elbe River in Saxony, it is a massive national park covering an area of 93 square kilometres. The area is dotted with scenic valleys, hidden caves, lush forests and rugged sandstone formations, making it one of the best places to visit in Germany. Black Forest: It is one of the best places to see in Germany, especially for nature lovers. Covered in thick woods and dotted with timbered houses and thermal springs, the beauty of its landscape has been winning the hearts of tourists for many years. Some consider spring to be the best season to visit Germany for touring the Black Forest.
Germany.Beaches & Hill Stations to Visit: Amrumer Strand: It is a captivating wild beach on Amrum Island that you can visit on your Germany trip. Enjoy a serene walk along the wide open sandy shore and click postcard-worthy photographs. Hiddensee Beach: Located on Hiddensee Island, the beauty of this beach is unparalleled and is one of the best things to see in Germany. You can reach Hiddensee Beach by ferry from Stralsund between April and October. Garmisch-Partenkirchen: This hill station is one of the most beautiful places to go in Germany. On your visit, you can witness and experience its vast green meadows, lush greenery, serene ambience, pristine lakes and towering snow-capped mountains. Mittenwald: Cosy accommodations, lush greenery and colossal mountains, these pretty much define Mittenwald in Germany. Besides enjoying its peace and beauty, you can check out Mittenwald’s yoga retreats, museums, cathedrals, etc., on your Germany trip.
Germany.Temples to Visit: Sri Muthumariamman Temple: Located in Hannover, it is the largest Hindu Temple in North Germany. The shrine is dedicated to Muthumariamman, the goddess of rain and fertility, who is a manifestation of Devi Parvati. Visiting this shrine is one of the best things to do in Germany for religious travellers. Sri Kamadchi Ampal Temple: On your Germany trip, visit the Sri Kamadchi Ampal Temple in Hamm. It is the largest Hindu shrine in Germany. The temple is modelled after the Kanchi Kamakshi Temple in India and houses the idol of the goddess Kamakshi and several other intricately carved sculptures.
Germany.Cities to Cover: On your Germany tour, make sure to visit the key cities. The itineraries of our Germany travel packages feature several prominent German cities, such as: Berlin: The largest city and capital of Germany, Berlin is noted for its vibrant culture, layered history, delectable German food culture and array of notable German tourist attractions. Most of the prominent German things to see are in this city, such as the Reichstag, Berlin Cathedral, and Brandenburg Gate. Frankfurt: Located in Western Germany, it is among the most popular Germany tourist spots. Frankfurt has an intricate history and a pretty skyline for tourists to explore and enjoy. The key German attractions in this city are Romerberg, Museum Embankment, Frankfurt Cathedral, etc. Munich: Munich is where you can explore the fine cultural and historical heritage aspect of Germany tourism. Also, on your visit, do try the best German food in Munich. Some of its key attractions are Nymphenburg Palace, the English Garden, and Marineplatz. Stuttgart: Add Stuttgart to your “Germany what to do and what to visit list”, especially if you are a car enthusiast. Its main tourist attractions include the Mercedes-Benz Museum, Porsche Museum, Palace Square, etc. Cologne: Home to the UNESCO World Heritage Site of Cologne Cathedral, this city is an integral part of Germany tourism. On your visit, you can explore and experience the city’s stunning architecture, exuberant nightlife, popular dishes of German cuisine and annual carnival.
Germany.How to Choose the Best Germany Tour Pack: While browsing the list of available Germany tour packages, make sure to do your due diligence to find the most suitable plan. Here’s what you need to consider: Price: When choosing Germany travel packages, make sure to consider the average Germany trip cost from India. The best Germany tour packages from India are those that provide your ideal vacation experience within your budget. Trip theme: Evaluate your trip needs and then look for Germany holiday packages online. For example, if you are a thrillseeker, you may want to go for adventure-focused Germany packages. Those travelling with families will find the family tour packages from India a better deal. Similarly, newlyweds planning a honeymoon may find pre-planned romantic Germany trip packages more suitable. Inclusions: If you are looking for a streamlined vacation experience, go for Germany tour packages that come with the essential inclusions, such as hotels, meals, flights, sightseeing, activities, etc. Duration: Consider the trip duration when choosing Germany packages. Make sure the trip itinerary is well-paced, offering sufficient time for you to enjoy the key attractions and experiences that Germany has to offer. Customisation: If you want to make adjustments to your trip itinerary, you may want to go for customisable Germany packages. With these plans, you can make changes to the trip itinerary, hotels, activities, etc.
Germany.Germany Currency: The official currency used in Germany is the Euro. It has been the sole currency in the country since 2002. The Euro is denoted by the symbol “€” and code “EUR” and is the official currency of 20 of the 27 EU member states. The Euro is further divided into cents, where 1 Euro is equivalent to 100 Euro Cents. Here are the details of its banknotes and coins: Banknotes: Frequently used: €5, €10, €20, €50 and €100. Rarely used: €200 and €500. Coins: 1c, 2c, 5c, 10c, 20c, 50c and €1, €2. If you are planning a Germany tour, make sure to research the currency exchange. It will help you get the right forex amount for your vacation. Here’s what you need to know about the current currency exchange rate of the Euro: Exchange rate (Euro to other currencies): Indian Rupees (INR) 1 Euro = 101.90 Indian Rupees U.S. Dollar (USD) 1 Euro = 1.16 U.S. Dollar Note: Exchange rate as of 11:58 UTC, 24th October, 2025 If you want to acquire Euros for your upcoming Germany tour, consider getting them in a mixture of cash and a pre-paid travel card from Thomas Cook’s online forex services. Forex travel cards ensure fast, hassle-free and secure payments on your international holidays. It is well known for the skyscrapers, international airport and considered as the financial capital of Europe. Getting around I quite simple because of the contemporary and well-organized public transport system.
