faq.question: What is the best time to go to Hanoi besides Autumn?
faq.answer: Apart from Autumn (late September to November), Spring (February to April) is often preferred as Hanoi's best time to visit. And if you are looking for an off-season discount, winter (December to January) is the best time to travel to Hanoi.

faq.question: What is the most reliable way to travel within Hanoi?
faq.answer: For short distances, opting for a Cyclo is the best way to get around in Hanoi. Otherwise, rental cars and taxi services are ideal for covering longer routes.

faq.question: What is the currency used in Hanoi?
faq.answer: The official currency of Hanoi is the Vietnamese Dong (VND), and you will have a better time using it for covering your expenses there.

faq.question: What are some must-visit museums in Hanoi?
faq.answer: The Hoa Lo Prison Museum, Vietnamese Women’s Museum, Army Museum and Vietnam Fine Arts Museum are some of the most important museums and best places to visit in Hanoi.

faq.question: What are some must-try local desserts in Hanoi?
faq.answer: Some must-try desserts in Hanoi include S’Patisserie, Bo Bia Ngot, Sua Chua Nep Cam, Che Chuoi, and Kem Xoi.

Hanoi.Introduction: Going on a Vietnam tour is a fine choice for individuals who want to witness the natural, cultural and historical heritage of that part of Asia. One such example of a destination within Vietnam that makes up for an outstanding holiday getaway is Hanoi. To ensure that your holiday planning remains simple without making any compromises on your Hanoi trip, you can go with the wide range of holiday plans offered by Thomas Cook. There are more than 15 Hanoi tour packages you can pick from, available at various price points. Each of these travel plans comes with different inclusions, and you can browse through the available options to find something that matches your expectations. At Thomas Cook India, we understand the importance of an international holiday vacation, and that's why we offer only the best Hanoi holiday packages. The best part about these tour packages is that it takes care of all the core aspects of the trip, including accommodations, transport, sightseeing, meals, etc., so you can enjoy the ultimate Hanoi experience without any issues. As for the destination itself, Hanoi is an incredible place full of interesting tourist attractions, and you can learn more about it on our handy Hanoi tourism guide. Continue reading further to find more details on popular Hanoi tour packages, the best visit time, must-try cuisines and other essential things.
Hanoi.Table of Content: Most Popular Hanoi Tour Packages Holidays in Hanoi How to reach Hanoi Travelling within Hanoi Best time to visit Hanoi Hanoi COVID-19 tourist guide Best Places to Visit in Hanoi What to eat in Hanoi Things to do in Hanoi People also ask about Hanoi Tour Packages
Hanoi.Most popular Hanoi tour packages: No matter the travel criteria, you can always find something suitable from our wide range of Hanoi vacation packages. Here are the most popular Hanoi tour packages you can consider: Hanoi Tour Packages No. of Nigts / Days Price* Vietnam Extravaganza 5 Nights/ 6 Days Rs. 89 637 Vibrant Vietnam And Cambodia 9 Nights/ 10 Days Rs. 1 68 933 Hanoi Free & Easy 3 Nights/ 4 Days Rs. 19 452 Discover Vietnam 4 Nights/ 5 Days Rs. 40 151
Hanoi.Holidays in Hanoi: The city of Hanoi (Ha Noi) is located in the northern region of Vietnam. Besides being the second-largest city in terms of population, Hanoi is also the official capital of Vietnam. It covers a total area of about 3,359.82 square kilometres, and its historical roots go back to the 3rd century BCE. If you are going on a Vietnam trip or looking to spend your holidays in an Asian country with immense cultural and historical significance, Hanoi stands to be a perfect choice. It is one of Vietnam’s main tourism centres, attracting travellers from around the globe with its historical landmarks, religious sites, and natural beauty. The wide range of experiences Hanoi has to offer makes it an ideal site for an exclusive short vacation. This is why Hanoi ranks among the most popular places to visit in all of Asia for an exceptional tourism experience. You can consider our Hanoi Free & Easy tour plan if you are looking for a dedicated Hanoi travel package for a holiday getaway.
Hanoi.How to reach Hanoi: One of the crucial parts of planning your Hanoi trip is to figure out the best possible transport method to reach the destination. Here are the available best ways to reach Hanoi in Vietnam: Reaching Hanoi by air Reaching Hanoi by train Reaching Hanoi by road Reaching Hanoi by air Regardless of your departure location, the best way to reach Hanoi is by air. The international air connectivity of the capital city of Vietnam is handled at the Noi Bai International Airport, which is one of the largest and busiest airports in the country. For instance, if you are starting your journey from Delhi, India, you can get on a one-stop flight to Hanoi, which runs on this route on a frequent basis. You can also get flights to Hanoi from other major cities in India, as well as other parts of the world. To ensure a hassle-free trip, make sure to secure your flight tickets ahead of your Hanoi journey. You can click here to check and book flight tickets on the go. Reaching Hanoi by train Visitors travelling to Hanoi from other parts of Vietnam can opt for a train journey. There are daily train services to Hanoi Railway Station from major Vietnamese cities, including Ho Chi Minh City. In addition, there is an international train which offers connectivity between China and Vietnam. This train runs on the Beijing to Hanoi route and involves a 36 hours journey. Reaching Hanoi by road Making a cross-country road trip to reach Hanoi from a neighbouring region is a possibility but is not considered an ideal option. It's because it can take a lot of time, involves a lot of paperwork and so on. In the case of travelling to Hanoi by road from other parts of Vietnam, both bus and rental cab services can be good transport options.
Hanoi.Travelling within Hanoi: After you reach Hanoi, you will need to sort out the best available transport methods for travelling within the city. Here are the best available options for getting around in Hanoi: By bus By taxi/rental car By motorbike taxi Cyclo Other ways By bus One of the main intra city transport options in Hanoi is via bus. However, it can be a bit challenging for visitors who are unfamiliar with the route or the Vietnamese language. It is recommended to ask for help from the locals if you are planning on using a bus to travel within Hanoi. By taxi/rental car One of the best ways to get around in the city of Hanoi is in a rental car or a local taxi. They are reasonably priced and make up for a quick and comfortable journey through the city streets. By motorbike taxi One of the most unique options for travelling within Hanoi is via a motorbike taxi. In this method, you will just have to sit on the backseat of a motorbike while the rider glides through the traffic to help you reach your desired location. Cyclo Another intra city transport service that you can avail of in Hanoi is a Cyclo. It closely resembles a “rickshaw” and is ideal for travelling short distances and local sightseeing. Riding a cyclo is also one of the best things to do in Hanoi. Other ways Some other popular options available for intra city travel in Hanoi are cycling and walking. They are ideal commute options for short distances and offer a leisurely travel experience.
Hanoi.Best time to visit Hanoi: If you want to make the most out of your holiday trip, make sure to learn about the best time to visit Hanoi. Generally, Hanoi tourism can be classified into four categories based on seasons and among them, Autumn is widely considered to be the best time to visit Hanoi. This period stretches from either late September or October to November and is the peak season for tourism in Hanoi. The weather throughout Autumn remains clear with an average temperature range of 24 to 27°C, offering a perfect window for sightseeing and experiencing the regional culture. You may have a slight chance of experiencing rainfall, which only enhances the natural glory of the place. This is also the time for some of Hanoi’s major events, including the Liberation Day and Keo Pagoda Festival. Some tourists also consider the Spring season as the best time to visit Hanoi. It usually lasts from February to April and has a slight chance of rainfall. The temperature tends to remain cooler in the beginning and rises towards the end. This is a key time for travellers who want to take a peek at the region’s culture and tradition. Spring in Hanoi is the time for various festivals, including the Tet Festival, Co Loa Festival, and Le Mat Festival. The other notable seasons for Hanoi tourism are winter (December to January) and summer (May to September). These are usually the off-seasons, but you can consider them if you are looking for cheaper deals on accommodations and a relatively lower crowd.
Hanoi.Hanoi COVID-19 tourist guide: The global pandemic situation had a great impact on both domestic and international tourism sectors. It introduced various rules and regulations on travel that are still widely followed. If you are going on an international trip to Hanoi, don’t forget to get details of the local COVID-19 travel safety guidelines. You can find more information on the latest COVID-19 tourist guide for Hanoi and other regions on our International Travel Guidelines page. Other than that, make sure to follow the general health and safety measures for travelling. It includes avoiding travel if you have a cough or fever, keeping social distance, wearing a face mask, etc.
Hanoi.Best Places to Visit in Hanoi: The vast land of Hanoi is filled with important sites of tourist attractions, located very close to the city centre. Here are some examples of Hanoi top attractions that you should not miss: Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum Museum of Vietnamese History Lake of the Restored Sword Hoa Lo Prison Museum Temple of Literature Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum The Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum, located at the heart of the city, is one of the most famous places in Hanoi. It is the resting place of one of Vietnam’s most influential leaders, Ho Chi Minh, also known as Uncle Ho. The Mausoleum is built on Ba Dinh Square, which is the same site where the Declaration of Independence was read by Uncle Ho back on 2nd September 1945. Tourists are allowed inside the Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum for a short time to look at the glass coffin where Uncle Ho rests. In addition, the architectural magnificence of the building, based on Lenin’s Mausoleum, is one of the best attractions in Hanoi. Museum of Vietnamese History The Museum of Vietnamese History in Hanoi is the go-to site for history buffs and tourists who want to learn more about the region’s historical background. This establishment was built during the French colonial era, and you can find its traces in the building’s architecture. It holds over 200,000 exhibits that range from the modern era to the Bronze Age. Hence, the Museum of Vietnamese History ranks on the Hanoi best places to visit list. Lake of the Restored Sword Locally known as Hoan Kiem Lake, the Lake of Restored Sword is one of the best places to visit in Hanoi. It is a calming spot that offers great scenic visuals, ideal for taking pictures or spending a lovely evening. The key attractions of this place are the Huc Bridge, Thap Rua Tower and the Ngoc Son Temple. This place also has a very interesting legend behind it, known as the “Legend of Sword Lake”, which dates back to the 15th century. During your visit, make sure to check out the light-up view of the Huc bridge, local open-air restaurants and everything else that this place has to offer. For a short break, you may find this Hanoi trip package to be a suitable choice. Hoa Lo Prison Museum The Hoa Lo Prison Museum is one of the main sites of tourist attractions in Hanoi. This site is all that remains of the Hoa Lo Prison, which was built in 1896. The prison complex was intended to capture and hold political prisoners during the colonial era. Some of its display items include John McCain’s suit, interrogation room, guillotine, and Cachot, which act as a silent reminder of the horrifying colonial period in Vietnam. Besides this, the history of how the prison operated and its failure are also among the interesting things you can learn during your visit there. Temple of Literature This beautiful site in Hanoi was established back in 1070 by the then Emperor Ly Thanh Tong. The Temple of Literature is a monument dedicated to Confucius and is a massive point of tourist attraction in the city. Apart from being surrounded by well-maintained gardens and serene ponds, the sights of halls, statues, carved walls, courtyards and altars are some of the best things to see in Hanoi.
Hanoi.What to Eat in Hanoi: Vietnam has some of the best Asian cuisines that you can try out during your visit to Hanoi. Here are some of the best foods to eat in Hanoi during your holiday vacation: Bun Cha- Bun Cha is one of the many specialities originating from Hanoi. This mouth-watering dish is basically a type of noodle with the main ingredients being grilled pork and rice noodles. This heavenly delight is best served with a tasty dipping sauce, and your best chance of trying it out in Vietnam is in Hanoi. Cha Ca- This is a regional favourite and a must-try dish during your visit to Hanoi. Cha Ca is prepared by marinating fish, ideally catfish or cod, in turmeric and then cooking it with other herbs and ingredients, like green onion, dill, galangal, etc. It is mostly served as a side dish and paired with steamed rice or rice noodles. Bun Rieu- Hanoi offers a variety of delectable soups that you can try, including Bun Rieu. It is a type of noodle soup that uses crab as one of its main ingredients, which gives it a very strong and flavourful twist. The other important ingredients used in making Bun Rieu are meat, rice noodles, tofu, annatto seed oil, and tomato. Kem Xoi- When tasting local cuisines in Hanoi, don’t forget about the dessert. One of the most popular Hanoi desserts among locals and tourists is the Kem Xoi. It is a dish that uses sticky rice, ideally pandan, as its base and has ice cream and toasted coconut flakes as its toppings. It is a perfect conclusion to your Hanoi meal or can be a handy choice for a snack.
Hanoi.Things to do in Hanoi: Besides visiting the array of iconic locations and tasting delicious cuisines, there are lots of interesting activities that you can do in Hanoi during your trip. Here are some examples of the best things to do in Hanoi: Spend some time by the Hoan Kiem Lakeside- Hanoi’s Hoan Kiem lake is the go-to point where you can unwind and spend some quality time surrounded by beautiful nature and sceneries. At the lakeside, you can catch some fresh morning air or conclude your outing for the day with the setting sun. It also makes for a great place to hang out, take photos and dine with friends or your partner. This is one of the top things to do in Hanoi. Find out about Vietnam’s history- From important establishments to a number of museums, Hanoi is the centre for history buffs who want a glimpse into the history and heritage of the city and Vietnam as a whole. Make sure to reserve a day or two for learning about Hanoi’s history. Watch a puppet show at Thang Long Water Puppet Theatre- Watching a puppet show at the famous Thang Long Water Puppet Theatre is a signature entertainment activity and one of the best things to do in Hanoi. These shows are usually hour-long live performances, featuring puppets on a water stage operated by skilled puppeteers, traditional music, and great storytelling. Stroll through the Old Quarter- The Old Quarter is an essential part of the city, and exploring it on foot is one of the top things to do in Hanoi. This area also has the famous Train Street and features various restaurants, shops, beautiful sights, and so on. Also, the nightlife in the Old Quarter makes it a must-visit place in Hanoi.
Hanoi.Forex/Currency for Hanoi: Acquiring the foreign currency of the place you are going to visit is one of the essential parts of planning an international trip. For example, Hanoi accepts Vietnam Dong (VND), and you will require it to pay for the expenses you make there. For this purpose, you can either opt for currency notes or a forex travel card, both of which can help you make payments in a foreign country. You can get this settled in a few simple steps by going with the foreign exchange services offered by Thomas Cook. It comes with tons of currency options you can choose from and also offers the choice of getting your forex delivered to your doorstep.
Hanoi.Visa for Hanoi: You will require a Vietnamese tourist visa in order to travel to Hanoi unless you are a passport holder of a nation that falls on the Vietnam Visa Exemption list. For example, Japan is on the Vietnam Visa Exemption list, so Japanese nationals can visit Hanoi or other Vietnamese cities without a visa if they plan to stay for 15 days or less. Travellers from other countries, for example, Indians, will require a visa for their Hanoi, Vietnam tour. The best way to approach this is by applying for a Vietnamese e-Visa, which has a validity of 30 days and will be sufficient for your Hanoi trip.

faq.question: What is the best time to visit Paris?
faq.answer: The months of June to August and September to October are ideal for a Paris trip. Summer and autumn both have their ups and downs. From June to August, Paris' weather is nearly perfect. The average high temperature is in the high 70s, with long days of sunshine.

faq.question: How many days are enough for a Paris tour?
faq.answer: Plan at least 7 days in Paris to get a good sense of the city, see some of the major sights, and stroll through the main neighborhoods. A week in Paris is a good place to start, especially if it's somewhere you've wanted to go for a long time.

faq.question: In Paris, can you speak in English?
faq.answer: Although English is not widely spoken in France as a whole, it is widely spoken in Paris' tourist areas, particularly at well-known attractions and at restaurants and hotels.

faq.question: What is Paris famous for?
faq.answer: Paris is one of the world's most beautiful cities. The Louvre Museum, Notre-Dame Cathedral, and the Eiffel Tower are all world-famous places you should definitely visit on your Paris trip. It is known for being a romantic and culturally rich city. The city's high-quality gastronomy and café terraces are also well-known.

faq.question: What is the currency used in Paris?
faq.answer: Euro is currency used in Paris.

Paris.Best Time to Visit Paris: Visiting Paris is an experience that, according to most people, doesn’t depend on choosing an appropriate month. However, to make your vacation feel comfortable and ensure that you get to experience Paris at its best and find appropriate Paris packages, here’s what you should know about the best time to visit Paris: Weather The best time to visit Paris in terms of weather is the summer season that typically starts from April and lasts till early October. You get to enjoy warm sunny days: just what you need for your walks around Paris. Peak Season Peak season in Paris usually starts towards the end of May and starts to fade away by the middle of September. During these months, Paris becomes a home to citizens from all around the globe, and the vibe is immaculate. You can look for Paris tour packages during the peak season. Months The best month to visit Paris starts from May and goes till the end of September if you want a bright, sunny and warm vacation.
Paris.How to Reach Paris?: An integral part of planning your trip to Paris includes finding the right commute option. Here’s how to reach Paris in different ways: By Train Travelling to Paris by train is a convenient method as there are multiple railway stations all around the city, including Gare Saint-Lazare, Gare de Lyon, Gare du Nord, etc. You can use the railways to travel between different European countries as well. By Road Travelling to Paris by road depends on the country or city you are travelling from. Remember that travelling by road includes paying toll taxes to enter Paris. If you wish to explore Paris by road, you can look for Paris tour packages to get details of Road transport rules and regulations. By Air Paris has two international airports: Charles de Gaulle and Orly, which you can choose depending on the country you are travelling from. If you’re travelling from India, several airlines are operating from cities like Bangalore, Mumbai and Delhi to Paris. Paris trip packages can help you find affordable and flexible flights.
Paris.Best Places to Visit in Paris: Take a look at this table of places to visit in Paris that you must not miss out on while exploring Paris tour packages: Famous Places to Visit The Eiffel Tower: A list of Paris sites to see has to start with the iconic Eiffel Tower. Built in 1887, the Eiffel Tower is one of the most visited sites around the globe. Over the years, it has become a symbol of love and spectacular engineering achievement. The Louvre Museum: Whether you are a fan of art and culture or not, a visit to this museum is a must. It is a home to one of the most iconic paintings of Mona Lisa and numerous other masterpieces that will sweep you off your feet. Disneyland Paris: For a fun day, make sure you keep a day aside to enjoy the iconic Disneyland in Paris. Disneyland spreads across 4800 acres, offering several resorts, theme parks, dining experiences, souvenir shops and rides that are truly fun-filled, making it one of the most loved Paris tourist places. Musee d’Orsay: Is a trip to Paris even complete without visiting some of the most iconic museums? The Musée d’Orsay is where you can find the legendary works of Van Gogh and many other artists. Arch of Triumph: The iconic Arch of Triumph, or as the French say: Arc de Triomphe de l’Etoile, is another must-visit place that you can find in Paris Tour packages. It is dedicated to the patrons of the French Revolution. Palas de la Cite: Consider yourself a history geek? You must not miss out on visiting the Palais de la Cité: A royal palace with history written on all its walls.
Paris.Beaches/Hill Station to Visit: Seine River A cruise or boat ride around this gorgeous lake is something you must not miss out on your trip to Paris. The ride takes you around the beautiful bridges of Paris and several picnic spots. Paris Plage The Paris Plage is a beautiful artificial Beach stretching over 3.5 km located at the Parc Rives. It has become the perfect spot for a relaxed day and fun picnic activities. Loire Valley and Normandy For an authentic countryside life experience, head straight to the and Normandy. A trip to these places will make your Paris trip a wholesome package. You can explore exquisite and serene beaches here.
Paris.Chapels and Cathedrals to Visit: Sainte-Chapelle If you are a fan of medieval architecture, then make sure to visit the Sainte-Chapelle, which is also known as the heart of Paris. This gorgeous Imperial Chapelle has architectural beauty in every corner. Notre Dame de Paris Cathedral Boasting one of the finest French Gothic architecture, this 12th-century Cathedral is one of the most iconic places to go in Paris.
Paris.Cities to Cover: Once you are done with your Paris sightseeing and visiting Paris attractions, you can also opt for small trips to cities near Paris through your Paris tour packages. Begin your journey in Versailles to immerse yourself in the finest French culture and art. Stroll through the grand halls and manicured gardens that once hosted royalty. Next, head to Chantilly, which is home to possibly the most beautiful French architecture. Finally, wrap up your getaway with a visit to Fontainebleau. It is a historic town rich with imperial charm and forested beauty, boasting one of France’s largest royal châteaux. These nearby cities promise a perfect blend of heritage, elegance, and tranquillity.

faq.question: How many countries make up Asia?
faq.answer: A total of 48 countries (China, India, Indonesia, Pakistan, Bangladesh, Japan, Philippines, Vietnam, Turkey, Iran, Thailand, Myanmar, South Korea, Iraq, Afghanistan, Saudi Arabia, Uzbekistan, Malaysia, Yemen, Nepal, North Korea, Sri Lanka, Kazakhstan, Syria, Cambodia, Jordan, Azerbaijan, United Arab Emirates, Tajikistan, Israel, Laos, Lebanon, Kyrgyzstan, Turkmenistan, Singapore, Oman, State of Palestine, Kuwait, Georgia, Mongolia, Armenia, Qatar, Bahrain, Timor-Leste, Cyprus, Bhutan, Maldives, Brunei)make up the Asian continent, encapsulating 3/5ths of the world’s population!

faq.question: Can I drive through Asia?
faq.answer: Driving through certain parts of Asia is possible to an extent. Asia is divided into several regions – Western Asia, Central Asia, Southern Asia, Southeastern Asia and Eastern Asia. Driving between countries of a specific region is often possible, as long as they share a land border.

faq.question: Which is the most populated country in Asia?
faq.answer: China is the most populated Asian country, with a capacity of almost 1.5 billion!

faq.question: Which are the most budget friendly Asian countries?
faq.answer: Vietnam, Laos, Nepal, Bhutan, Sri Lanka, India, and (parts of) Thailand are some of the more budget friendly Asian countries to visit.

faq.question: Which countries in Asia are the most expensive for a holiday?
faq.answer: China, Dubai, Israel, South Korea, Japan, Hong Kong, and Singapore make up some of the most expensive travel destinations in Asia.

faq.question: Can I see Asia with just one visa?
faq.answer: No, each Asian country has distinct Immigration rules and regulations that visitors must abide by.

faq.question: Which Asian countries should I visit for a beach vacation?
faq.answer: Thailand, Indonesia, Maldives, Philippines, Sri Lanka, and Vietnam possess some of Asia’s most serene, beautiful beaches.

faq.question: Which country in Asia is the best for solo female travellers?
faq.answer: Singapore, Cambodia, Thailand, Vietnam, Japan, and Indonesia are destinations safe for solo female travellers.

faq.question: Which Asian countries have visa on arrival of Indians?
faq.answer: A pleasantly surprising number of countries permit a visa on arrival for Indians. They include – Thailand, Bhutan, Cambodia, Maldives, Macau, Indonesia, Nepal, Laos, Sri Lanka, Hong Kong and Jordan.

Asia.Best Time to Visit: Summer is the best time to visit Asia. Summer usually stretches from May to August and is the peak travel season. This season also coincides with the monsoon in parts of Asia such as India, Sri Lanka, southern China, Japan, Korea and the Philippines. This is when you will find almost every holiday destination in the continent packed with a throng of tourists. The pleasant weather combined with a host of activities to do mean that as Asian summer is one you will not easily forget.
Asia.How to Reach Asia: The best way to reach Asia is by flight, especially if you’re travelling from India. There are direct flights from several major Indian cities such as Delhi, Mumbai, Hyderabad to popular destinations in Asia such as Bangkok, Malaysia, Tokyo, Singapore and Shanghai to name a few. Since summer is the peak season in Asia, it’s a good idea to book your flight tickets well in advance.
Asia.Places to Visit in Asia: With forty-eight countries in Asia, each having something fantastic to offer visitors, it can get quite difficult to choose where you want to go. Nevertheless, here are some of the places to visit in Asia, countries that you must visit if you want to have the full Asian experience. India Sri Lanka Nepal Bhutan Thailand Indonesia Vietnam Singapore India In a subcontinent amassing over a billion residents, treasures lie far and wide. Explore India tourism with the Himalayas in the North to the Backwaters in the South, an array of experiences await you. Sri Lanka The island of Sri Lanka, as though a chunk of Southern India were merely chipped of the subcontinent, emerges beautifully from the Indian Ocean waters. It’s hard to imagine how such a small mass of land has so much to offer! Boasting a diverse wildlife, Sri Lanka’s Uda Walawe and Bundala National Parks almost replicate the experience of an African safari. Numerous Land and water bound species are protected within the borders of Lanka. The boutiques, malls, and restaurants of Colombo perfectly complement the surf culture at Arugam Bay, both displaying healthy competition at its finest! Nepal Nepal Tourism, home of the Himalayas, calls upon many trekkers and adventure enthusiasts for the Everest Base camp trek, the Annapurna Circuit trek, or the Langtang Valley trek. Although these endeavours require a fair bit of experience, altitude training, and physical vigour, the effort rewards in more ways than one. Activities like white water rafting and kayaking are among Nepal’s highlights Bhutan Bhutan Tourism relays an array of Himalayan treks facilitated by expert, local guides; the country’s Snowman trek and Jhomolhari trek are among the few globally eminent walks! Ascending the peaks offer as much delight as descending into the valleys. Bhutan’s Haa Valley, fairly close to Paro, is yet another trekking hub prioritizing intentional travel through homestays and personalized hostels. Meanwhile, the Thimphu valley’s museums, markets, restaurants, nightlife, and boutiques render it a tourist’s paradise. Thailand The land of coconut curries and thrift shops, Thailand has a variety of experiences to offer its visitors. You can begin with an indulgent spree in the urban Bangkok, drawing shopaholics from far and wide. Street food vendors, late-night food, exquisite rooftop bars, and underground clubs are much of the city’s charm. Skyscrapers coexist with ancient temples, lending to a wholesome capital. Indonesia The Gili Islands, only a boat ride away from Bali, Indonesia, welcome all to their glistening, turquoise waters. Jump in for a swim, scuba dive, snorkel, and explore the diverse marine life of this region. And then for some more enjoyment, experience fresh, local seafood amidst the pleasant vibes at Gili Trawangan. While Jakarta is where the party is – always – at, the Baliem Valley and Kerinci Valley boast challenge and adventure. Carefully hike through the jungle trails of Baliem Valley or explore the flora and fauna of the Kerinci. No matter where your Indonesian adventures take you, its Sate, its Nasi goreng, and peanut sauces will be sure to keep up! Vietnam It has the monsoons and the aridity, the spring blossoms and the autumn discolouration, the summer humidity and the winterly chills, Vietnam is a heaven on Earth. Hoi An is the urban metropolis of Vietnam, flowering in restaurants, cafes, bars and shopping, while its sister city, Ho chi Mihn provides the same vibrancy amidst its fervent motorcyclists and street vendors! Hike through Bac Ha or wander through evergreen forests and their inhabitants at Ba Be National Park. One does not depart Vietnam without a cup – or more – of the local Vietnamese coffee! Whether hot or iced, the flawless combination of espresso, condensed milk, and sugar will embed Vietnam in your memories for years to come! Singapore Singapore, a land of pride and pleasure, is the blueprint of an urban sanctuary in today’s times. What does it not have? A seamless, public transportation system, functional healthcare system, astounding cuisines, blend of rich cultures, environmental sustainability, endless shopping and only more! Explore Singapore Tourism with the Garden’s by the Bay surrounding the popular Marina Bay Sands hotel, and the Singapore Botanical Gardens are the literal lungs of the country. Singapore is perhaps the only place in the world where food stalls reside high in the Michelin records. Take full advantage of your time at the Changi International Airport, the world’s number one airport. A little city of its own, the amenities, delicacies, and accessibility within three extensive terminals easily enable prolonged, restful layovers!
Asia.Famous Food in Asia:: Asian food is a riot of flavours that is nutritious and wholesome as well. Thanks to its primarily agrarian economy, vegetables, nuts and cereals are prominent, with rice being a staple across the continent. Meat and fish are add-ons, used to highlight a dish rather than serve as the mainstay. The Asian culinary repertoire is vast and delightful, but here are a few dishes you must eat while in Asia. Thali, India Ramen, Japan Halo, Philippines Xiaolongbao, China Hainanese Chicken and Rice, Singapore Thali, India The thali gets its name from the large platter it is served in. It is a melange of vegetables in various forms such as curries, stir-fries and crisp fried vegetables served with rice, rotis, pickles, crisp popadums and dessert. Guests are encouraged to vary their selection with each bite in this choose-your-own-adventure style meal where no two bites taste the same. Traditionally all vegetarian, meat based alternatives to the thali also exist and are just as delightful. Ramen, Japan A far cry from the instant ramen that is popular the world over, ramen in Japan is an art, science and religion one large bowl. Served piping hot with a variety of toppings, ramen is the perfect example of Japanese attention to detail. The hot broth is the perfect balance savoury and comforting, the noodles are slippery, chewy and slightly springy and the chashu pork that sits atop the bowl is tender enough to melt in your mouth. Almost every region in Japan has its own take on ramen from the light shoyu broth to the heavy, gelatinous tonkotsu. Halo, Philippines Halo is a Philipine frozen dessert which is best described as larger than life. Imagine a bowl piled high with shaved ice, topped with evaporated milk, sweetened coconut, stewed fruit, nuts, candy and all sorts of sweet treats. Streaked with sweetened flavoured syrups in a technicolour, neon palette that will leave your own palate asking for more, Halo is a dish you shouldn’t miss in the Philippines Xiaolongbao, China If you’re ever in China and can’t decide between ordering the dumplings or the soup, try the xiaolongbao instead. These steamed buns hold a savory surprise in the form of soup. The come with a straw that you use to pierce the dumpling in order to slurp the soup before devouring the rest of the bao. These are a wholesome filling meal in themselves that will leave you wanting more. Hainanese Chicken and Rice, Singapore The simplicity and flavour of this dish is astounding. Poached chicken, with glistening velvety skin is served with aromatic sticky rice, pickled cucumber and dipping sauces to create an iconic Singaporean comfort food. Usually served at street side hawker centers, this dish has now entered the world-famous Michelin Guide with a list of 8 hawker stalls serving what could be the cheapest Michelin starred meal you can buy.
Asia.Things to Do in Asia: Roar at the Lion’s Rock in Sri Lanka Feel like royalty in India Enter the Tiger’s Nest in Bhutan Fly to Singapore Step into the Emperors shoes in China Conduct some fishy business at Tsukiji, Japan Roar at the Lion’s Rock in Sri Lanka The ancient royal compound of Sigiriya, or Lion’s Rock is the star attraction in Sri Lanka's Cultural Triangle. It is best visited in the morning, before the day heats up and takes roughly 45 minutes to reach the summit, but you’ll be treated to frescoes, rock gardens, and other splendid sights with every step. Feel like royalty in India India stereotypically conjures up images of palaces and elephants. Jaipur's old city plays to this stereotype. The City Palace, built by the last surviving maharaja, is a complex of mini-palaces arranged in a series of unfolding boxes—so the deeper the realm, the more private and less accessible it becomes. Despite the fact that the princess and her family still live in the palace, it serves very much as a public living room—you’ll see locals chatting in its squares and relaxing in its open-air pavilions—and isn’t at all forbidding. Enter the Tiger’s Nest in Bhutan Taktsang Palphug, commonly known as Tiger’s Nest, is a 17th-century structure built into a sheer rock-face 10,200 feet above Paro Valley. It is called the ‘Tiger’s Nest’ because, legend has it, in the eight century Guru Rinpoche flew to this spot on the back of a tiger. Construction began in the 1400s, with expansions being carried out until the late 1600’s. In 1998, a fire swept through the buildings, but the temples were restored to their original shape. There are 15 beautiful temples in Tiger’s Nest complex whose wall paintings have been painstakingly restored by UNESCO. Fly to Singapore The wonder of Singapore seizes you as soon as you deplane at Singapore's Changi International Airport. Constantly voted, the best-run airport in the world, Changi International is not only clean and efficient, it's packed with perks you wouldn't expect to find in an airport. It has a butterfly garden, a swimming pool, a gym, a spa, a movie theater, an arcade, a playground, and free tours of the city for those with a layover of five or more hours. Tired of taking an escalator? Changi International Airport has slides travellers can zip down to save time and have tons of fun. This airport is constantly changing with fun new elements being added every year. Step into the Emperors shoes in China To best experience the Forbidden City skip the museum within the complex's grounds and head to the North Gate, going backward toward the main entrance at Tiananmen. This is where every emperor and his family lived when the 7.8-million-square-foot, 980-building compound was finished, until 1912, when the Qing dynasty finally came to an end. Conduct some fishy business at Tsukiji, Japan Nearly 3,000 tons of seafood moves through Tokyo’s Tsikiji market everyday to retailers and restaurants in Japan and abroad. Have a textbook on marine biology open as you wander the maze of stalls selling everything that could be pulled from the sea. Make way and watch your step in this busy market as things get hectic in the mornings. By afternoon, the market is all but empty as the vendors set up for the next day, this is when you can grab some superbly fresh sushi and sashimi, made from fish from the market or even grab a bowl of ramen, rubbing shoulders with fisherman and traders.

Hungary.Holidays in Hungary: The splendid country of Hungary is a part of Central Europe and spans a total area of about 93,030 square kilometres. As a landlocked country, it shares its border with Slovakia, Ukraine, Romania, Croatia, Serbia, Austria and Slovenia. Hungary’s largest city in terms of population is Budapest, which is also the country’s official capital. Due to its geographical location, Hungary has always been a crosspoint for people travelling to and from various European nations. Besides acting as a hub for Europe tourism, Hungary is also one of the most popular vacation spots in the world. The extremely unique local culture and traditions, mixed with a modern-day European touch, have created a perfect blend for tourism. In addition, Hungary features many UNESCO Biosphere Reserves, natural landforms, historical landmarks, famous World Heritage Sites, etc., which massively contribute towards enhancing its tourism aspect. When you add all of the things that Hungary has to offer for tourists, it becomes one of the must-visit destinations in Europe. It works as an ideal getaway for individuals looking to spend their holidays at a location that has an array of different experiences to offer. You may find this Hungary trip package interesting if you are looking for a week-long vacation.
Hungary.How to reach Hungary: One of the best parts about travelling to any European country is that you get a lot of options in terms of transport. Here are the best ways to reach Hungary: Reaching Hungary by air Reaching Hungary by rail Reaching Hungary by road Reaching Hungary by air Reaching Hungary by plane is the best available option due to the country’s extensive network of air connectivity. There are multiple international airports serving the major Hungarian cities with the availability of frequent flights from and to different parts of the world. The Budapest Ferenc Liszt International Airport is the ideal option for tourists as it is located close to the capital city and works as the starting point for the Hungary tour. If you are travelling to Hungary from any major city in India, you can get a one or two-stop flight that commonly runs on this route. Besides Budapest, the other two major international airports in the country are Debrecen Airport and Heviz-Balaton Airport. Don’t forget to book your flight tickets in advance to avoid any issues during the trip. Reaching Hungary by rail If you are going to Hungary from a neighbouring country, travelling by rail is an option you can consider. The country’s capital city, Budapest, acts as the hub for East-European railway transportation routes and offers extensive domestic rail connectivity. You can find international trains from the major cities of the neighbouring European countries, like Croatia, Austria, Slovakia, Serbia, etc., serving this station. Reaching Hungary by road The option of reaching Hungary by road can also be a decent pick for individuals travelling from neighbouring countries. The best way to approach this is by opting for an international bus service that operates on the route between the main Hungarian cities and major cities of neighbouring European countries.
Hungary.Travelling within Hungary: Among the various available choices, here are the best ways you can travel within Hungary without any issues: By train By bus By cars By metro Other ways By train The central hub for the railway network in Hungary is in the capital city of Budapest. It offers great intercity connectivity and features three main types of trains, namely IC trains, Express trains and Passenger trains. If you are planning on travelling longer routes within Hungary, opting for a train can be handy. By bus The national bus network of Hungary is widely spread out, connecting major cities. They are a comfortable and reasonably priced transport option that tourists can pick for travelling within Hungary. By cars A popular way to reach various destinations within the country is by rental cars. Besides being reliable, it makes up for a comfortable journey. Cab services are also available, especially in Budapest, and are ideal for travelling within the city or nearby locations. By metro Tourists can also opt for underground metros to travel locally within Budapest. It has all the major stops and is the most sensible way to get around Hungary’s capital city. Make sure to learn about how the four lines of the Budapest metro work to avoid any confusion. Other ways The other popular ways for covering intracity routes in Hungary include hiking, cycling and renting a scooter.
Hungary.Best time to visit Hungary: Getting a rough idea about the best time to visit Hungary can help make your trip experience more special. Generally, the period of spring (March to May) is considered the best time to visit Hungary. The temperature usually ranges between 6 to 25°C, with cold breezes as a daily feature. Tourists from all over the globe travel to Hungary during this time to witness the cultural side of the region that comes with its major events, including the Holloko Easter Festival and Paprika Festival. Since it is a semi-shoulder season for Hungary tourism, the tourist crowd and cost of accommodations remain comparatively lower. The peak season for Hungary tourism is in Summer (June to August). The average temperature during this time remains around 23°C, offering great weather for local travel and sightseeing. Some notable events during this period are the Balaton Festival, Sziget Festival, Summerfest International Folklore Festival and Budapest Festival of Folk. Many consider this period the best time to visit Hungary because of the pleasant weather, lakeside activities and natural biosphere reserves. Similar to Spring, Autumn (September to October) is a shoulder season for Hungary tourism. The days are typically warmer, and the orange/yellow leaves of the trees give the region a picturesque look. Many tourists prefer visiting Hungary during this time, ideally for its lower crowd, outdoor activities, and some notable regional events. Tourism in Hungary during winter (November to February) remains low. It features extremely cold weather with snowfall and fog. You may visit Hungary during this time to witness beautiful snow-capped landscapes, Budapest Christmas Fair, Winter Festival, winter activities and so on.
Hungary.Hungary COVID-19 tourist guide: The COVID-19 pandemic forced the world into a state of lockdown and caused various countries to close their borders. However, Hungary is once again accepting tourists from around the globe now that the pandemic situation is under control. But there are certain rules and regulations that travellers have to follow when visiting the country. You can find more details on this subject on our Hungary Travel Guidelines page. Also, in order to ensure your safety, make sure to wear a mask and follow other general COVID-19 health and safety measures.
Hungary.Best Places to Visit in Hungary: Before starting your Hungary trip, make sure to learn about the most popular landmarks and sites so that you don’t miss out on anything. Here are some of the best places to visit in Hungary: Budapest Eger Holloko Aggtelek National Park and Caves Esztergom Lake Balaton Budapest Apart from being the capital city, Budapest is one of the most popular places to visit in Hungary as well as Central Europe. It offers a unique sightseeing experience that remains unmatched. The city is filled with grand structures, both from the pages of history and modern times. It also features multiple UNESCO World Heritage Sites and delectable Hungarian cuisines. Exploring the city by foot can offer some great picture-perfect moments during your Hungary trip. Some of the main attractions in Budapest are the Buda Castle, the Parliament building, the House of Terror Museum, the Basilica of St. Stephen, and Memento Park. Eger Eger is one of the most interesting places to visit in Hungary. It is popular across the region for its numerous tourist attractions and rich history. Eger has seen various historical moments unfold over several decades, including the reign of St. Stephen, the Mongol invasion, the siege of Eger, Ottoman rule, and so on. The neo-classicist city architecture also attracts quite the tourism crowd throughout the year. Some of its main attractions include Eger Castle, County Hall, Magic Tower, Vineyards of Eger, Cathedral Basilica of Eger, etc. It is about 4 kilometres from Valley of the Beautiful Women, where the country-famous Bikaver is made. Holloko Next on the list of top attractions in Hungary is the old village of Holloko. This site perfectly illustrates rural life in Hungary prior to the agricultural revolution. The old village of Holloko is well-preserved and gives a close look into the historic architectural aspect of the region. The notable sites within this location include St. Martin Church, Hollokoi Falamuzeum, Paloc Doll Museum, Holloko Castle, and so on. Leisurely exploring this place and its surroundings can feel like you have travelled back in time. Aggtelek National Park and Caves Aggtelek ranks on the list of the most interesting places to visit in Hungary. It houses the Aggtelek National Park, which spreads about 200 square kilometres in area. The Baradla Cave, with a length of over 25 kilometres, is protected within the bounds of this park. This naturally-formed cave is home to some of the most spellbinding stalagmites and stalactites, often having a peculiar colour and form. Tourists commonly visit this site for an adventurous guided tour, hiking, or just to appreciate the natural beauty of the caves. Esztergom Another destination that secures its place on the list of top tourist attractions in Hungary is Esztergom. It is one of the oldest towns in the country and has major historical significance. One of the main attractions of this destination is the Esztergom Basilica, which is the largest church in the country. On top of that, its Christian Museum holds some of the most unique exhibits related to Christian history. The overall visuals Esztergom and its surroundings offer can take you back to a vintage era. The Danube river flowing along the town is another famous attraction of this place. Lake Balaton Lake Balaton, located in the Transdanubian region, is a freshwater lake and one of the major tourist attractions in Hungary. Besides being one of the largest lakes in Europe, the various resort towns, lush green forests, calming atmosphere and scenic visuals surrounding this place make it a perfect holiday getaway within the country. It also features a beautiful trail that tourists can cover on foot or on a bicycle. Some major attractions in and near Lake Balaton are Festetics Palace, Balaton sound festival, Szigliget Fortress, etc.
Hungary.What to Eat in Hungary: The taste of delicious, and authentic Hungarian cuisine can be found all over the country during your visit. Here are some of the must-try dishes in Hungary that you can consider: Goulash- Goulash is a massively popular Hungarian cuisine that many are familiar with. It is available at both local eateries and premium restaurants. Goulash is ideally a steamy bowl of stew made with potatoes, beef, onions, carrots, pork and paprika. You can have this affordable and tasty bowl of goodness as a light meal. Chicken paprikash- This is another popular Hungarian dish found across various parts of the country. It both looks and tastes phenomenal and is ideally a gravy-heavy stew-like dish served alongside steamed rice. The main ingredients used in Chicken paprikash are chicken, Hungarian paprika, sweet peppers, sour cream, onions and tomatoes. Meat stew- Regionally known as Porkolt, this meat stew is an iconic dish in every Hungarian household. It is made with a variety of meat choices, vinegar, tomatoes, Hungarian paprika, potatoes and vegetables. This meat stew can be a delicious relief when exploring through cold weather, and you can get it at any major restaurant. Stuffed cabbage leaves- Just as the name suggests, this dish is made using cabbage leaves with various tasty fillings. Ideally, the stuffings are a blend of chopped cabbage, bacon, rice, tomato sauce and ground beef. It is then covered in a sauce of choice and slow-baked over a few hours. You can easily try it at any restaurant in Budapest.
Hungary.Things to do in Hungary: During your international trip to Hungary, make sure to do these exciting activities: Take a cave bath- The Miskolc-Tapolca natural cave system is one of the most popular attractions in Hungary. You can enjoy a relaxing natural thermal spa deep inside the cave, which has a water temperature of 30°C. The cave bath at this site has been well-used since the 17th century. Go on a Danube river cruise- When visiting Budapest, consider going on a grand cruise on the Danube river. It can offer a whole new perspective to your Hungary trip, and you can catch some appealing views of the river and the setting sun. Explore the streets of Sopron- The Hungarian city of Sopron has some of the most aesthetically pleasing architecture in the region. When exploring the city streets on foot, the old buildings will make you feel like you are part of a classic film. Go for a hike at Lillafured- Lillafured has some of the most beautiful hiking trails in the country. Going on an exciting hike at this location can help you get some fantastic scenic visuals of the region. You can also opt for a Lillafured forest train ride, which can add a majestic twist to your trip. Explore the caves of Aggtelek Karst- While travelling across Hungary, don’t forget to check out the caves of Aggtelek Karst. It features some of the greatest creations by nature in the form of stalactites and stalagmites.
Hungary.Forex/Currency for Hungary: The official currency used in Hungary is called Hungarian Forint (HUF). Even though some places or stores in the country may accept Euro, it is recommended that you obtain and use Hungarian Forint during your trip for an overall hassle-free experience. For this, you can consider using the foreign exchange services offered by Thomas Cook India and get your forex at competitive exchange rates. It is a simple process and you can choose from multiple currency options depending on your travel needs. It is best to carry Hungarian Forint in the form of cash and a forex card for your trip.
Hungary.Visa for Hungary: Tourists from non-Schengen countries visiting Hungary or any other European countries within the Schengen agreement will be required to apply for a short-term tourist visa in advance. For example, Indians travelling to Hungary will require a Schengen Visa, which will allow them to enter Hungary and other countries that are a part of the Schengen agreement. This type of short-stay visa has a validity of 90 days within a period of 180 days and is ideal for tourists who are visiting Hungary or any other Schengen country for vacation. Make sure to apply for it in advance to avoid any issues during your trip.

faq.question: How much does a Georgia trip from India cost for a week?
faq.answer: A 6-7days Georgia trip from India generally costs ₹70,000–₹1.2 lakh per person, depending on flights, hotels, and activities.

faq.question: Do Indians need a visa to visit Georgia?
faq.answer: Yes, Indians need an e-visa for Georgia, which costs around ₹2,000–₹2,500. However, Indians holding a valid US/UK/Schengen visa can get visa-on-arrival.

faq.question: What is the best time to visit Georgia?
faq.answer: The best time to visit Georgia is in spring (April–June) and autumn (September–October) for mild weather, blooming landscapes, and fewer crowds

faq.question: How many days are enough for Georgia?
faq.answer: A 6–8 day trip is sufficient to explore Tbilisi, Batumi, Kazbegi, and Kakheti wine region.

faq.question: Which are the must-visit places in Georgia?
faq.answer: Don’t miss Tbilisi Old Town, Narikala Fortress, Batumi Boulevard, Gergeti Trinity Church in Kazbegi, and Kakheti vineyards.

faq.question: Is Georgia an expensive destination for Indians?
faq.answer: Georgia is relatively affordable compared to Western Europe, with budget-friendly food, stays, and public transport.

faq.question: What currency is used in Georgia?
faq.answer: Georgia uses the Georgian Lari (GEL). Indian travellers can easily exchange USD or EUR, and cards are accepted in most cities.

faq.question: Is Georgia good for a family vacation?
faq.answer: Yes, Georgia is family-friendly with historical sites, scenic mountains, beaches, and fun activities for kids.

faq.question: Will language be a problem in Georgia?
faq.answer: English is spoken in tourist areas, but in rural regions, knowing basic phrases or hiring a guide is helpful.

faq.question: Is Georgia safe for Indian tourists?
faq.answer: Yes, Georgia is considered very safe for travellers, with friendly locals and low crime rates.

faq.question: What food should I try in Georgia?
faq.answer: Try dishes in Georgia like khachapuri, khinkali, mtsvadi, chakapuli, churchkhela, along with Georgia’s world-famous wines.

Georgia.Introduction: Georgia tour packages by Thomas Cook bring you closer to making your dream of experiencing the wonders of the world a reality. Georgia is a country in West Asia and Eastern Europe, part of the Caucasus region. While many may overlook Georgia when setting up their international trip itinerary, tourists who want to explore something beyond the commercialised holiday places pick it as a priority. It has been inhabited since the era of prehistory, hence, it is no wonder that the land boasts an incredible cultural and historical heritage that piques the interest of curious minds around the globe. Moving past the nation’s rich history and culture, the natural beauty found in the forms of caves, lakes, national parks, mountains, waterfalls, etc., are the other highlights of the tours to Georgia. Georgia’s ancient cave cities, such as the Vardzia and Uplistsikhe, attract tons of tourists annually. On top of this, there is the exemplary Georgian architecture that acts as one of the main highlights of the Georgia tour. Its countless heritage sites, ancient monuments, sulfur baths and major vineyards also deserve special mention. Thrill-seekers on their tours to Georgia consider activities like skiing, hiking, river rafting, off-roading, paragliding and much more. If you want a first-hand experience of these, consider opting for the Georgia tour packages by Thomas Cook. You can find Georgia tour packages for all types of themed vacations on our website, including adventure trips, family getaways and romantic honeymoons. The starting price of Georgia tour packages offered by Thomas Cook is only INR 35,500/adult. Each Georgia travel package is equipped with a unique itinerary to offer you the vacation experience you deserve. Alongside hotels and sightseeing, these Georgia travel package options also take care of things like meals, flights, etc., to offer a more hassle-free trip experience. Learn more about popular Georgia travel package options and tourism on this page.
Georgia.Most Popular Georgia Tour Packages: Tour Packages NameDurationPriceHighlights Of Azerbaijan With Georgia8 Nights 9 DaysRs. ₹153 300Georgia - Buy 1 Get 1 Free4 Nights 5 DaysRs. ₹62 200Classic Georgia4 Nights 5 DaysRs. ₹35 800Georgia VAN Tours - 04 Nights / 05 Days4 Nights 5 DaysRs. ₹45 600Fascinating Georgia5 Nights 6 DaysRs. ₹57 700
Georgia.Best time to visit Georgia: If you have some context about the best time to visit Georgia country, it can make choosing Georgia tour packages for your holidays a much easier task. Here’s what you need to know about the best time to visit Georgia country for vacations; Georgia in May-June & September-October For touring the lowlands around Tbilisi, the period from May to June or September to October can be a good time to visit Georgia. The gentle weather during these months offers the ideal window for tours to Georgia as it helps to avoid the intense summer heat and chilly winter cold. While you can expect some rain in May and June, it settles towards the end, offering ideal conditions for hiking. Furthermore, what makes these months a good time to visit Georgia are the wildflowers in the Caucasus in June, Georgia food & wine festivals in September, and the shade of Autumn and the harvest festival in October. Get Georgia holiday packages for these months on Thomas Cook. Georgia in April, July & August If you are interested in witnessing Rhododendrons, participating in water rafting and going on bird-watching trips, April can be the best time to visit Georgia. On the other hand, July is the best time to visit Georgia for beach trips; however, do account for possible afternoon showers. The temperature and weather conditions in August are similar to those of July. While beach visits are still a thing in August, going to snow-free lower mountain regions for camping, horse riding, caving, cycling, fishing, etc., is more preferred. August is the best month to visit Georgia if you want to experience the folk festival of the Tusheti region. If you are interested in these experiences, consider booking your package trip to Georgia accordingly. Georgia in November-March November to March is considered the low season for the Georgia tour. The temperature starts to drop, reaching freezing conditions inland by December. The cold and snowfall make tours to Georgia difficult. The highlight of this season is the Christmas markets and celebrations of Christmas and New Year. March can be considered the best month to visit Georgia for the low prices. You can get good deals on Georgia holiday packages for the off-season on our website.
Georgia.How to reach Georgia?: One of the most essential aspects of the Georgia tour is to decide the mode of transfer. For a streamlined vacation experience, you can opt for Georgia tour packages featuring optional flights. Here are the main ways to travel to Georgia from India; By train There is no direct railway link between India and Georgia. However, if you are travelling from its neighbouring countries, you can opt for this mode of transport. The Georgian Railway network links Azerbaijan and Armenia and offers a relaxing and picturesque train route. Overnight trains from Baku (Azerbaijan) and Yerevan (Armenia) are available to Tbilisi. The train journey is not usually included in Georgia holiday packages from India. By road Road transfer is not feasible for tours to Georgia from India. However, travellers heading towards Georgia from neighbouring nations like Azerbaijan, Turkey, Armenia, etc., can consider this option. For this, note that the roads are great and having international driving permits and the necessary visa and other documents is highly advised. Make your dream vacation a reality with Georgia packages by Thomas Cook. By air Air travel is the most preferred mode of transport for tours to Georgia from India. It is convenient, comfortable and widely available. There are many international airports in the country of Georgia, with the three main ones being the Tbilisi International Airport, Batumi International Airport and Kutaisi International Airport. Tbilisi International Airport is the choice that is most opted for, while the Batumi Airport and Kutaisi Airport serve the southwestern and central-western parts of the country. Tbilisi International Airport is around 17 km from the capital city. It is well-connected to the rest of the world, including major Indian cities. For travel reference, a direct flight from New Delhi to Tbilisi takes around 5 hours and 20 minutes to complete. Consider the Georgia holiday packages by Thomas Cook for a hassle-free vacation experience.
Georgia.Best places to visit in Georgia: There are tons of famous Georgia places to visit. So, you need to create the perfect Georgia tour itinerary that lets you experience the best of what the country offers without any compromise. You can pick from our selection of Georgia holiday packages with interesting itineraries on Thomas Cook. Here are some of the most famous places to see in Georgia; Famous places to visit Tbilisi Georgia’s capital city, Tbilisi, is one of the best places to go in Georgia. It is a city that offers tourists a vivid insight into the region’s age-old history and modern hexes. Lying along the banks of the River Kura, the city is dotted with traditional buildings and surrounded by mountains, giving it a heavenly visual appeal. On a visit, you can check out its attractions like the Chronicle of Georgia, Old Tbilisi, Narikala Fortress, Sioni Cathedral, etc. You can find this as a standard inclusion in your package trip to Georgia. Kutaisi Kutaisi is known for being the home of the Gelati Monastery Complex and the Motsameta Monastery. Tourists visit Kutaisi to explore and embrace the cultural and historical heritage of Georgia from up close. Its other attractions include Katskhi Pillar, Bagrati Cathedral, Martvili Canyon, Sataplia Nature Reserve, etc. Book a package trip to Georgia featuring Kutaisi from Thomas Cook. Batumi Batumi is one of the most scenic places to see in Georgia for urban tourism. Known for its panoramic coastal scenery, the city offers various noteworthy sites of historical importance alongside cafes, restaurants, gardens and bars. Tourists also visit Batumi for fun adventure activities, like horse riding, water sports, etc. Batumi’s key tourist sites are the Europe Square, Gonio Fortress, Botanical Garden, Miracle Park, Batumi Boulevard, etc. Explore the best of Batumi with a professionally designed package trip to Georgia. Uplistsikhe Uplistsikhe is one of the best Georgia tourist places to visit for history buffs. It is an ancient rock-hewn town that will leave you speechless. This ancient site offers a close look into a historical settlement in the region, with remains of squares, streets, fates, sewer systems, tunnels and so on. The key attractions of Uplistsikhe are a 1st or 2nd century Temple Theatre, Tamaris Darbazi, a massive pre-Christian era Makviliani Temple, etc. To find sites like Uplistsikhe in the itinerary of your Georgia travel package, book plans from Thomas Cook. Hill stations in Georgia Gudauri Gudauri, noted for its heavenly landscape, is a beautiful mountain resort town in Georgia’s Caucasus Range. Located at an elevation of 2,200 metres, Gudauri has attracted many notable personalities, including the likes of Alexandre Dumas. Tourists visit Gudauri for its scenic beauty, peaceful ambience and skiing and other snow adventures. It is often included in popular Georgia travel package options. Svaneti A short trip to Svaneti on your Georgia tour should be enough to give you a taste of what unblemished natural beauty amidst the mountains looks and feels like. It is a place covered by acres of vast green pastures with small stone-built homes and quaint villages with a backdrop of towering, snow-capped mountains in the back. It is centred around Mestia, a popular hiking town in Georgia with available cosy accommodation and restaurants. For a more tailored trip experience, consider the customizable Georgia packages by Thomas Cook. Beaches stations in Georgia Batumi Beach If beach visits intrigue you, consider checking out Batumi Beach in Georgia. It is the city’s most popular beach, offering scenic views of the Black Sea and space for swimming and sunbathing. There are food vendors and quaint cafes along the beach, so you can grab a bite afterwards. The beach is very close to Batumi Boulevard, where all the fun activities take place as the evening sets in. It is usually included in Georgia travel package options that feature Batumi. Green Cape Beach The Green Cape Beach is one of the most preferred Georgia tourist places for enjoying the scenic coastal vibes away from the bustling city side. With aquamarine water on one side and lush green on the other, it is no wonder that this beach is a fan favourite. You can spend hours enjoying the vibes at this site or consider engaging in activities like swimming, sunbathing, water sports, and more. Religious places to visit in Georgia Gelati Monastery The Gelati Monastery in Kutaisi is a UNESCO World Heritage Site. Built in the 12th century by Georgian King David IV, the monastery complex has churches of St. Nicholas and St. George, with an academy where various subjects were once taught and a bell tower. Included in the itinerary of popular Georgia packages, the highlight of the site is the Church of the Virgin, which is the main cathedral on the complex. The place holds immense importance in terms of religion and history in Georgia. Bagrati Cathedral The Bagrati Cathedral in Kutaisi is yet another site of religious, cultural, and historical importance in Georgia. Located on Ukimerioni Hill, this 9th-century cathedral boasts its exemplary Georgian architecture. The cathedral is noted for its grand dome, intricate stone carvings, mosaic tiles, ancient inscriptions and so on. Witness more wonders like Bagrati Cathedral by opting for a Georgia travel package from Thomas Cook. Holy Mother of God Church of Bethlehem This church, also called Upper Bethlehem Church, is one of the key religious sites in Georgia. Located in Tbilisi, the church was originally built as an Armenian church back in the 18th century. It now acts as a Georgian Orthodox Church, and its history dates back to the late 5th century. Georgia packages featuring Tbilisi often include a visit to this site.
Georgia.Food to eat in Georgia: You can’t really explore and enjoy the wonders of Georgia on an empty stomach. So, while you are on your Georgia tour, why not spend time trying and tasting the delicious and unique Georgian cuisine? Here are some of the most popular Georgia food items to try; Khachapuri It is a hearty delight in the form of bread filled with cheese, somewhat resembling the popular dish, pizza. If you are not lactose intolerant, this might just be your new favourite dish on the Georgia tour. Different types of this famous Georgia food item are offered. Starting with Imeruli Khachapuri, which is the basic one. There is Megruli Khachapuri, which has added cheese on top, and Adjaruli Khachapuri, which has melted cheese in the middle of hot, puffy bread and chunks of eggs and butter on top. Batumi is one of the best places to go in Georgia for Adjaruli Khachapuri. Churchkhela Imagine elongated sweet potatoes that somewhat resemble colourful kebabs, well, that’s how an Indian would imagine Churchkhela to be at first glance. However, in reality, it is a type of local Georgian candy made with ingredients like hazelnuts, walnuts, almonds, raisins, etc. These ingredients are incorporated onto a string, which is then immersed in a mixture/juice of fresh grapes and then dried out. If you have a sweet tooth, a bite of churchkhela should be your perfect match. The markets of Batumi or Tbilisi are some of the best Georgia places to visit if you want to grab a churchkhela. Khinkali It is a traditional Georgian dish, shaped like twisted modak-shaped dumplings and filled with meat (lamb or pork) and a handful of local spices. Khinkali are either steamed or boiled and are known for its soft texture and juicy and delicious insides. If you are a vegetarian, you can consider trying the veg version that comes with a filling of cheese, mushroom or potato. Try khinkali in Tbilisi at Tsiskvili restaurant on your Georgia tour. Lobio Lobio is a simple yet delicious Georgian food item you can try as a comfort dish. It is basically a soup made with kidney beans with things like onion, coriander, garlic, walnuts, etc., added per taste. For Lobio, try eateries at Salobie, near Mtskheta. Most Georgia travel package options on Thomas Cook feature meals. Badrijani Nigvzit Whether you are a vegetarian or a non-vegetarian, Indians and their love for eggplant dishes go hand in hand. The dish Badrijani Nigvzit gives you a similar but different taste. While the name of this Georgia food item might be a mouthful, its taste is surely mouthwatering. It is basically a stuffed eggplant dish made using eggplants, lemon, garlic, walnuts, etc., and fried in oil. You can find this dish at any major restaurant in Georgia, especially in Tbilisi. Get the best deals on Georgia packages on Thomas Cook.
Georgia.Georgia currency: On your Georgia tour, you will need the local currency of Georgia in order to pay for services and commodities there. Like every country, Georgia has its own currency system that it accepts. The official currency of Georgia is called the Lari, denoted by code: GEL and symbol: ₾. 1 Georgian Lari is further divided into 100 tetri. In terms of coins, the Georgian Lari is available in denominations of 1, 2, 5, 10, 20 and 50 tetri and then 1 and 2 Lari. In terms of banknotes, the denominations that are currently available for the Georgian Lari are 5, 10, 20, 50, and 100. Tourists need to learn about the exchange rate of the Georgian Lari so they can make the right purchase of foreign currency for the Georgia trip. As of now, that is 6:25 PM UTC, April 8th 2025, the value of 1 Georgian Lari is equivalent to INR 31.42. For the trip, you can get Georgian Lari foreign currency in the form of cash and/or a forex card. You can use this to pay for services and purchases you use/make in Georgia during the Georgia tour. You can consider getting Georgian Lari foreign currency from your bank or an authorised online or offline forex dealer. So, alongside looking for the ideal Georgia travel package, make sure to acquire the necessary foreign currency for your Georgia travel purposes.
