Tawang.Things to Know While Visiting Tawang: Being well-informed about the destination can come in handy during your visit. Alongside looking for Tawang tour packages, make sure to consider the following pointers; On your tour to Tawang, make sure to carry enough cash as ATMs and card payments are scarce in the region. Though there are no specific bans on any food items in Tawang, there are certain restrictions that tourists should respect and follow. For example, many local restaurants don’t serve non-veg on Wednesdays, so make preparations accordingly on your tour to Tawang. Tawang is in Arunachal Pradesh, and all visitors, except the native people of Arunachal, need an Inner Line Permit (ILP) to enter the state. Tawang has some unique local customs and practices and tourists must respect and follow them during their visit. Buddhist pilgrims walk around the monastery and monuments in a clockwise way, which visitors are advised to follow. Visitors are advised to wear modest and non-revealing clothes when visiting monasteries and other religious sites in Tawang.
Tawang.Best Time to Visit Tawang: Planning a spiritual trip centred around the best time to visit Tawang can be really helpful. Here is a brief explanation of the season-wise Tawang tourism experience to help you find the ideal time for your Tawang travel; Summer in Tawang Summer in Tawang starts in March and ends in August. Despite being the hot season, you don’t have to worry about unbearable heat because the region experiences a moderate climate with an average temperature range of 10-25°C. The overall weather stays calm and pleasant, making it the best time to visit Tawang for spiritual tourism and Tawang sightseeing. Summer is also a good time to take part in adventure activities and attend local festivals like Losar. Find Tawang packages for summer on our page. Monsoon in Tawang Monsoon which lasts from July to September, brings quite the rainfall to the region. Despite having a nice average temperature range of 15-25°C, the frequent rain increases the risk of slippery roads, roadblocks and landslides, which give rise to safety hazards and affect tourism. If you are visiting during this time, make sure to exercise caution. In general, it is a good time for a Tawang trip to witness its incredible natural beauty. Winter in Tawang Winter in Tawang lasts from November to February. These are the coldest months in the region, with an average temperature range of -10 to 12°C. Those who are not a fan of the harsh cold may avoid visiting during this season. Otherwise, winter is the best time for a Tawang trip, especially if you want to witness the majestic snowfall and stunning snow-covered landscapes. Quotes and InspirationsOur soul-searching expeditions often take us to some of the most beautiful places in the world. Tawang is one such place, except it is both naturally and spiritually beautiful. When one steps into Tawang, the scenic topography and unscathed natural beauty start some sort of healing that is much appreciated coming from busy city life. Free from chaos and polluted streets, exploring the majestic town and its surrounding region has been very impactful. Then comes the revered Buddhist monasteries that are not only visited by people of Buddhist faith but also people of diverse beliefs and backgrounds. Talking to the resident monks, and taking a sneak peek into their lifestyle and the Buddhist teachings have helped our minds to expand in a divine way. As the Dalai Lama once said, “The purpose of our lives is to be happy”, which is something we find and re-discover as we progress in life. It is a feeling of true happiness that we achieved while exploring the incredible architecture of the gompas and learning about their immense historical, cultural and spiritual heritage. Happiness, inner peace and sacred silence, maybe that’s all we need to live life in a better way, which is something only spiritual places like Tawang can truly reflect. To make the most out of your visit, carefully pick the right Tawang tour itinerary.
Tawang.What to Eat in Tawang?: The local delights are perfect for satisfying your hunger and taste buds during Tawang sightseeing. Here are some popular Tawang local food to try; Wungwut Ngam: Wungwut Ngam is a popular chicken with rice powder dish. It uses a variety of local herbs and other fresh ingredients, which are slow-cooked to give the dish its signature taste, texture and aroma. It is a famous non-veg local dish that you can try on your Tawang trip. Chura Sabzi: Chura Sabzi is a signature dish in the region that is popularly consumed by the natives of Tawang. It is a curry-type dish that is made using fermented cheese and a wide range of local greens. It is usually topped with some spice pepper and paired with steamed rice. Make sure to give it a try on your Tawang trip. Lukter: Lukter is one of the most popular Tawang food items that you shouldn’t miss. It is a non-veg side dish in the region, made by combining cooked dry meat and chilli flakes. Lukter is mainly known for its spice level due to the use of Bhut Jolokia or ghost pepper and is usually served on the side of steamed rice. Find the best Tawang packages with optional flights on our website. Koat Pitha: Koat Pitha is a fried snack item that is loved by locals and tourists alike. It is a small, ball-shaped snack made using mashed banana, rice flour and jaggery, which is then deep-fried until it turns golden brown. Ngatok: Ngatok is a very popular, non-veg Tawang food that you can try. It is a soothing fish curry dish, made using locally caught fresh fish and a series of regional herbs and spices. Most of the Tawang tour packages offered by Thomas Cook feature meals as a standard inclusion.
Tawang.Shopping in Tawang: If you like shopping for gifts and souvenirs, make sure to leave an appropriate gap for it on your Tawang travel plan. As for local things to buy, consider these products; Thangka paintings: These are beautiful, hand-painted traditional Tibetan artworks on silk, cotton or canvas. They include vivid imagery depicting Buddhist deities, scenes, or symbols. You can buy them as scrolls or wall hangings. Check out the local souvenir and art galleries. Get deals on Tawang packages for any season on our website. Tibetan carpets: The handcrafted Tibetan carpets are a must-buy in Tawang. They feature intricate weaving and colourful patterns and are often of very good quality. If you're confused about what to do, you should try snorkelling at Blue Bay Marine Park or visit temples and numerous sugar plantations. Mauritius.Best Time to Visit Mauritius: December to April is the best time to visit Mauritius. This is the peak season, with pleasant weather and temperatures ranging from 20-28°C. Most of the Mauritius tourist places will be overcrowded with tourists. You can indulge in various things to do in Mauritius, like water sports, wildlife adventure and more. October to November is usually the moderate season. The influx of tourists to places to visit in Mauritius is lower. This is indeed the best time to visit Mauritius if you wish to enjoy sightseeing while relaxing near the beaches. The climate is warm and humid, with temperatures ranging from 20 to 28 °C. Chinese New Year is celebrated with immense grandeur. May to September is summer, so it is the off-season. However, this is the best time to go to Mauritius if you want affordable deals and great offers. The island is significantly less crowded, which makes it an ideal time for sightseeing and exploration. Surfing is one of the best things to do in Mauritius during this time. You need to figure out the best time to go to Mauritius if you're planning this trip, depending on the activity you want to try. If you book Mauritius tour packages during the summer or off-season, you'll get great discounts on accommodation and flight tickets. However, it is advisable to determine what's best for your Mauritius trip. Choose a package that best suits your travel goals.
Mauritius.How to Reach Mauritius?: Once you've booked Mauritius travel packages, you must understand how to visit. Flight is the most convenient option to travel for your Mauritius packages from India. By Train No training services are available from India to Mauritius. For this reason, most Mauritius packages offer flights as an option. Being an island, no railway system has been introduced yet. However, the government plans to introduce a light rail system to facilitate fast, easy transport. Car rental and taxis are the best way to explore Mauritius tourist attractions. Once you've figured out how to reach Mauritius via air, try out some sightseeing destinations. It is advisable to use a reputable car rental or taxi service for sightseeing. Guided tours can be among the best ways to learn about the island's history and culture. By Road Travelling via road for Mauritius tour packages from India isn't feasible. If you've been looking for how to reach Mauritius by road, it's not possible, as it is connected to the mainland only by air. However, you can rent a car to visit the things to see in Mauritius. Highways in Mauritius connect major cities such as Grand Baie and Port Louis. It is advisable to hire a Mauritius tour guide if you're taking the bus. Some guided tours also include private transportation. You can enjoy a hassle-free service and your holiday. By Air Sir Seewoodagur Ramgoolam International Airport (MRU) is the official airport of Mauritius. Flights are available from major cities such as Delhi, Chennai, Mumbai, and Bangalore. The easiest way to understand how to go to Mauritius from India would be to board a direct flight by Air India, Qatar Airways, Emirates and Air Mauritius. The cost of Mauritius tour packages from India reduces if you take connecting flights. Although the travel time will increase due to a layover, the price will be reduced. Booking Mauritius packages in advance can also help to avoid unnecessary high charges.
Mauritius.Famous Places to Visit: Chamarel Seven Coloured Earth: This visually stunning wonder is a landmark for Mauritius tourism. The geopark has vibrant layers of coloured dunes formed due to volcanic activity. Black River Gorges National Park: If you're an adventure seeker, you'll love trekking across the Black River Gorges National Park. The scenic trail offers a great view of the birds and waterfalls. Le Morne Brabant: This place offers panoramic views of the Indian Ocean from its summit. Owing to its beauty and its status as a symbol of freedom, this place is a prominent UNESCO World Heritage Site. Grand Baie: Mauritius, is more than just natural beauty and beaches. Grand Baie is a living proof of that. The nightlife and entertainment at Grand Baie are unmatched in Mauritius. There are numerous cafes, bars and boutique shops around.
Mauritius.Beaches/ Hill Stations to Visit: Flic en Flac Beach: The Flic en Flac Beach is one of the most popular beaches in Mauritius. A significant part of Mauritius packages itinerary, this is one of the most scenic spots for snorkelling and diving. Flic en Flac Beach is popular mainly for its soft sands and coral reefs. Tamarin Bay: If you want to go dolphin watching early in the morning, Tamarin Bay is where you need to be. Moreover, if you're a surfing enthusiast, you can try that as well. Pereybere Beach: A great spot for swimming. There are several cafes serving delicious Mauritian food. Try the best month to visit Mauritius to explore these water activities. Le Pounce Mountain: The perfect destination for hikers and trekking enthusiasts, it is very popular. This place offers a panoramic view of Port Louis. Montagne du Lion: The Lion Mountain, or Montagne du Lion, is a tall mountain near Vieux Grand Port. It is named for its shape, which resembles a sleeping lion. You can hike along the 2.8-mile loop trail near Old Grand Port. However, it is a challenging trail but mostly popular for hiking.
Mauritius.Temples to Visit: Mangal Mahadev: Located in Ganga Talao, this temple is one of the most important. It is a sacred lake in the crater of an extinct volcano. The Shiva statue here is 108 feet tall, one of the 50 tallest Shiva statues in the world. Indeed, it is one of the best Mauritius places to visit. Sagar Shiva Mandir Temple: This temple is located in the eastern part of Mauritius, at Pte de Flacq. A trip to this temple is said to be very serene and relaxing. The temple has a 30m bronze statue of Shiva. The atmosphere is very peaceful early in the morning. Sri Prasanna Venkateshwara Temple: This temple is in La Laura-Malenga village. It was built in 1921 in honour of Lord Venkateswara. The temple was renovated in 2010 and highlights the skilled craftsmanship of Southern Indians. Pandurang Kshetra Mandir: This temple was built in 1902 on the western coast of Mauritius. The main deity here is Lord Ganesha, known for his wisdom and intelligence. The temple features traditional Indian designs. There are other deities within the temple as well.
Mauritius.Cities to Cover: During your Mauritius trip, you should cover various cities, such as: Port Louis Mont Choisy Grand Baie Trou aux Biches Le Morne. Flic en Flac Chamarel Grand Bassin Most of these places to visit in Mauritius are known for their history and culture. In fact, some of these Mauritius tourist places on the West Coast are known for beaches and water sports. You can also enjoy snorkelling, swimming and various beach activities. Prepare a list of places to go in Mauritius and choose an itinerary that aligns with it. These cities offer some of the fun things to do in Mauritius. Therefore, prepare your adventurous side a little during your Mauritius trip to explore around.
Mauritius.How to Choose the Best Mauritius Tour Pa: Before you head out to Mauritius, you need to find the best Mauritius trip packages. Accommodation Options: Review the accommodation options provided in your Mauritius tour packages. Mauritius.Mauritius Currency: The official currency of Mauritius is Mauritian rupees. 1 Mauritian rupee is equal to 1.93 Indian rupees. You need to plan your Mauritius budget, keeping the exchange rate in mind. Indian Rupee isn't accepted in Mauritius. Therefore, during your Mauritius trip, you'll have to convert the currency at the airport. While you're visiting Mauritius attractions, it is advisable to use the official currency. You must use the local currency to avoid exchange rate issues. Several businessmen in Mauritius will not accept foreign currencies. You must use the correct currency to avoid any confusion, especially around the exchange rate. With the currency available, you can visit numerous places to see in Mauritius. During your Mauritius travel, it is advisable to exchange currency at the airport rather than at other destinations. Exchanging at the airport offers affordability and convenience. Although you'll be carrying credit cards during your Mauritius tour, it is advisable to carry some local currency. If you buy small or less expensive souvenirs during your Mauritius trip, you can save money. The minor unit of the Mauritian Rupee is the Sentimo. The different denominations for the Mauritian Rupee include- Rs 25 Rs 50 Rs 100 Rs 200 Rs 500 Rs 1000 Rs 2000 Although you know the exchange value, it is advisable to check the Mauritian Rupee exchange rate. This currency has been circulating in the market since 1967. The Bank of Mauritius issues Mauritian Rupees. For a hassle-free Mauritius travel experience, consider getting Forex cards. These cards are primarily helpful for Mauritius honeymoon packages. You can choose different options like Travel Forex Cards, Reload Forex Card, Borderless Travel Card and One Currency Card. Dharamsala.Introduction: If you wish to have a relaxing vacation, then Dharamshala tour packages are your top choice. A haven nestled deep within the heart of the Himalayas, it is a treasure trove of natural beauty, characterised by rich culture. That's why we offer Dharamshala trip options tailored just for you, starting from ₹26,199. Soak yourself in local Tibetan culture, scenic beauty, and Dharamshala places to visit, like enchanting Mcleod Ganj, and serene Dal Lake. Our Dharamshala packages ensure you experience the best attractions while enjoying your stay in this idyllic location. Don't miss out on amazing places to see in Dharamshala.

Dharamsala.Cultural Significance Of Dharamshala: When you're planning your Dharamshala trip, one thing that truly stands out is the rich cultural significance of this destination. Dharamshala is not just about scenic views and beautiful landscapes; it’s a place steeped in history, spirituality, and a blend of Tibetan and Himachali cultures. Let’s explore some fascinating aspects that you’ll encounter during your Dharamshala tour: Tibetan Influence It is often referred to as the "Little Lhasa of India," since it hosts the abode of the Dalai Lama and is a significant centre of Tibetan culture in India. The heart and soul of Dharamsala is Tibetan Buddhism, the same that flows through the gullies and crevices of everyday life, knitting together traditions and rituals. You will find monks in red robes, silk prayer flags swaying in the breeze, and the soft rhythmic hum of the prayer wheels at Namgyal Monastery, among many more. It is one of those places that needs to be seen when on Dharamshala tour packages. Kangra Valley's Local Culture Dharamshala is also deeply rooted in the culture of the Kangra Valley. The local Himachali people practise traditional dance forms like Nati, and their folk music is often performed during celebrations. During your Dharamshala sightseeing, make sure to experience these vibrant dance performances at cultural centres or local festivals. Festivals and Rituals Festivals here are marked by great enthusiasm and the events portray an amalgam of Tibetan as well as Himachali traditions. Traditional dances, music, and prayers mark Losar, which is the Tibetan New Year. The Tsuglagkhang Complex will transport you to an experience you will remember with queues of monks chanting their prayers and performing rituals in front of a massive prayer wall. Cuisine and Traditional Food While you're exploring Dharamshala tourism, you must indulge in its local flavours. Restaurants like the Tibet Kitchen and Lung Ta serve authentic Tibetan dishes like momos, thukpa, and tingmo that reflect the region’s rich culinary heritage. You'll also find Himachali dishes like Dham, a traditional meal served during festivals, which you must try to get the complete cultural taste of Dharamshala. Spiritual Significance Your Dharamshala package would only be complete with visiting the various temples and monasteries that make the spiritual atmosphere of the town so rich. A place like the Bhagsunath Temple is significant for both residents and tourists alike, where you'll witness a mix of Hindu rituals alongside local traditions. Art and Handicrafts Don't miss the Norbulingka Institute, a place dedicated to preserving Tibetan art and culture. It's one of those places to see in Dharamshala that offers you a glimpse into traditional Tibetan craftsmanship. You can witness artisans creating beautiful Thangka paintings, wood carvings, and metal statues, giving you a sense of the artistic soul of Dharamshala. This diverse cultural experience in Dharamshala is what makes it so unique and inviting. Whether you’re here for Dharamshala sightseeing or immersing yourself in its cultural roots, you’ll find the blend of spirituality, art, and tradition truly captivating. This beautiful blend of Tibetan and Himachali culture is one of the key reasons why Dharamshala tourism continues to attract visitors from around the world.
Dharamsala.Places to visit in Dharamshala: When you’re planning your Dharamshala tour packages, you’ll realise that this place offers much more than just temples. Numerous Dharamshala tourist places highlight the town's spiritual and natural beauty, creating a truly unique experience. Let me take you through some of the best places to visit in Dharamshala that go beyond temples and let you connect with the soul of the region. St. John in the Wilderness Church This very beautiful neo-Gothic church located amidst dense deodar forests is one of the most attractive places to visit in Dharamshala. This place was built back in 1852 and is dedicated to John the Baptist. It is famous for its stained-glass windows which were a donation by Lady Elgin. The surroundings here are very calm and serene, so a visit to this church is also a must on your Dharamshala sightseeing tour. Norbulingka Institute It is a centre for the conservation of Tibetan culture. Here, you can see traditional art and crafts of Tibet and observe Thangka painting artists, wood carving artists, and much more. It has Japanese-style gardens and ponds with koi fish inside with peaceful courtyards. So, it provides a refreshing break with most Dharamshala tourist places. Tsuglagkhang Complex Tsuglagkhang Complex is the official residence of His Holiness the 14th Dalai Lama and a spiritual center for Tibetan Buddhism. This is among the most important Dharamshala tourist places where you can explore the temple, the Namgyal Monastery, and the Tibetan Museum. The calm and serene atmosphere here adds a deeply spiritual touch to your Dharamshala packages. Triund Hill It is a perfect destination in your Dharamshala tourism itinerary for all the nature lovers and those who enjoy trekking, Triund Hill gives you a lush green forest experience along with panoramic views of the Dauladhar ranges. The sunset on Triund is breathtaking, making it one of the most beautiful places to visit in Dharamshala. Bhagsu Waterfall With its location close to the Bhagsunath Temple, this is a must-see beauty. Cascading water on rock cliffs covered with greenery is a very enticing view. Bhagsu waterfall is a good picnic spot or a refreshing dip with a little bit of adventure to your Dharamshala tour packages. Here are quick overview of popular tourist places to visit in Dharamshala. Places to Visit Known For Marsoor Rock Cut Temple An 8th century rock cut temple built during the Chola Dynasty. Temple is quite spectacular and it is said 3/4th of the temple is still undiscovered. Bhagsu Waterfall A fun tourist attraction, you can hike up and down the fall and enjoy a cool dip on a hot summer afternoon. Norbulingka Institute of Tibetan Culture If you are interested in learning about Tibetan culture and art and crafts, then this is the place to visit. Chinmaya Tapovan A famous Hindu Temple. Kangra Museum Home to a terrific display of Tibetan cultural artifacts, Buddhist relics, wooden carvings, extravagant outfits, tribal jewelry, pottery, rare coins, etc. Bhagsunath Temple A famous temple in the region located at the base of the Bhagsu Falls. Gyoto Monastery One of the most famous monasteries in India. The monastery is beautifully constructed and is a center of learning and training young monks. Tsuglagkhang The official residence of His Holiness the Dalai Lama. The complex has a temple, monastery, numerous relics, a beautiful courtyard, living quarters for the resident monks, and an art gallery and museum nearby. Triund A stunning trekking route that offers spectacular views of the valley. Mcloedganj One of the most popular destinations in the region. Home to many Tibetan refugees who have enhanced this hill station with their culture, religion, and traditions.
Dharamsala.Temples to Visit in Dharamshala: When you’re exploring the captivating beauty of Dharamshala, immersing yourself in its spiritual heritage is essential. The town is home to several remarkable temples that reflect its rich culture and traditions. Here’s a list of some famous temples you should include in your Dharamshala tour packages: Bhagsunath Temple Situated on the village side of Bhagsu, this temple is dedicated to Lord Shiva. One of the most evident places to visit in Dharamshala, the temple is accompanied by a beautiful waterfall close to it. If you want to get closer to the waterfall, there is an excellent trek path that makes it one of the greatest Dharamshala tourist places. The ambience here is peaceful and perfect for meditation or simply enjoying nature. Kunal Pathri Temple Situated at a distance of 2 km from Dharamshala, Kunal Pathri is a temple that worships the goddess Durga. It is built over a rock, and the natural surroundings bring the atmosphere down to a serene setting. This hidden gem is often overlooked, making it a great spot for some quiet reflection. It’s a fantastic addition to your Dharamshala sightseeing itinerary. Namgyal Monastery This is one of the major places to visit during your Dharamshala trip, as it happens to be the personal monastery of His Holiness, the Dalai Lama. The tremendous building is the centre for Tibetan Buddhism and hosts beautiful prayer halls and intricately designed murals. The serenity of the monastery is perfect for those looking to engage in prayer or meditation. It is one of the best places to see in Dharamshala. Gyuto Monastery Approximately 6 km from McLeod Ganj, it is known for its tantric practices and teachings. With the peaceful atmosphere and the magnificent view, it has been an ideal destination for spiritual aspirants. This temple is a perfect addition for those exploring the more profound aspects of Dharamshala tourism. Sherabling Monastery This monastery is a bit off the beaten path but well worth the visit. Located in the nearby Bir region, you can breathe some fresh air relaxing at this place that boasts stunning architecture, a serene environment, a meditation centre, Tibetan Buddhist studies, along with beautiful view that adds depth to your Dharamshala packages. Temple Name Opening Hours Best Time to Visit Festive Time Times to Avoid Bhagsunath Temple Early morning to late evening July-September (monsoon season) Maha Shivratri November-February (peak tourist season) Namgyal Monastery 6 AM to 7 PM March-June, September-October Losar (Tibetan New Year) July-August (monsoon season) Kunal Pathri Temple Early morning to late evening March-June, September-October Navratri July-August (heavy rainfall) Sherabling Monastery Early morning to late evening March-June, September-October Losar (Tibetan New Year) July-August (monsoon season) Gyuto Monastery 5 AM to 4:30 PM, 6 PM to 8 PM March-June, September-October Buddha Purnima July-August (monsoon season) These temples are just a glimpse of what Dharamshala has to offer. Each has its unique charm and significance, making your journey through this spiritual haven rich and fulfilling. Be sure to include these stops when planning your Dharamshala trip to experience the serenity and beauty of this remarkable region!
Dharamsala.Things to Know While Visiting Dharamshal: When you're planning your Dharamshala trip, it's essential to keep a few rules and regulations in mind to ensure a smooth experience. Here’s what you should know: Dharamshala is also a revered center of Tibetan Buddhism so it is pretty important to respect all the places of worship. While visiting temples and monasteries, always dress up appropriately and be considerate towards the local people's cultural and religious activities. While exploring the places to see in Dharamshala, make sure to stick to marked trails and paths. This practice not only protects the natural environment but also ensures your safety while trekking. Some of the sacred places may also have photography restrictions. Make sure to check the sign if photography is allowed there or not and ask permission first if you are not sure, for consideration of those places' sanctity. Engaging with local customs is a wonderful way to enhance your travel experience. Greeting people with a smile and showing politeness go a long way in fostering positive interactions with the local community. It's important to keep the beautiful surroundings clean. Make it a habit to carry your trash with you, especially when visiting scenic spots or participating in Dharamshala sightseeing. Your efforts will contribute to preserving the area's natural beauty.
Dharamsala.Best time to visit Dharamshala: When considering the best time to visit Dharamshala, you'll want to think about the weather and the experiences you wish to enjoy. Here’s a breakdown to help you plan your Dharamshala trip: March to June (Spring to Early Summer) This may be the best time to visit Dharamshala. The weather is warm but pleasant enough and suitable for outdoor activities and Dharamshala sightseeing. One can easily tour the various places to visit in Dharamshala, such as Bhagsunath Temple, and Namgyal Monastery, without worrying about rains or heat. July to September (Monsoon Season) While it brings greenery, it also brings heavy rain. So, if you like nature and peace without many tourists, then you can try visiting at this time. However, be careful because some places may not be accessible. Again, for most of the sightseeing Dharamshala packages, this is not the best time. October to February (Autumn to Winter) The winters in Dharamshala can get quite biting from December to February. However, if you wish to visit and see things at this time, it can indeed be exhilarating. When here, do not forget the cold weather and the possibility of snowfall - but that only enhances the beauty of your Dharamshala sojourn. By keeping these seasons in mind, you can make the most of your Dharamshala tourism experience and enjoy a range of things to do in Dharamshala!
Dharamsala.Quotes and Inspirations: Visiting Dharamshala is not just about the stunning landscapes; it’s also a journey of the spirit. Here are some meaningful quotes and personal affirmations that resonate deeply with the experience: “The mind is everything. What do you think you will become.” – Buddha. This quote reminds you to focus on positive thoughts, reflecting the tranquil energy of Dharamshala. “Do not dwell in the past, do not dream of the future, concentrate the mind on the present moment.” – Buddha. This resonates beautifully with the serene environment, encouraging mindfulness during your Dharamshala trip. “I embrace peace and harmony within myself.” Let this mantra guide you as you explore Dharamshala’s spiritual richness and breathtaking beauty.
Dharamsala.What to Eat in Dharamshala?: When you’re in Dharamshala, you’re in for a treat, as the food scene here is as vibrant as its culture. Here are some must-try dishes and recommended restaurants to include in your Dharamshala sightseeing adventures: Tibetan Momos: You simply cannot leave the beautiful city of Dharamshala without tasting its yummiest dumplings- Momos. Enjoy both the steamed and fried momos at the Tibet Kitchen in McLeod Ganj. Filled with either vegetables or meat, these momos make for the perfect meal during your visit to the places of Dharamshala. Chowmein: Chowmein is easily sought out by many local cafes. Lung Ta is one of the best for its savoury stir-fried noodles, hence popular among tourists. Thukpa: Another Tibetan staple is this noodle soup. Try it at The Corner House, where they serve a fantastic version that’s perfect for warming up after a day of exploring. Butter Tea: This is an exclusive Tibetan drink. Butter tea is creamy in texture and is easily available in local tea houses. Enjoy this at Norling Restaurant where you can relish authentic flavors. Indian Cuisine: For those craving Indian dishes, Himalayan Pizzeria serves delicious pizzas with a local twist. The vibrant atmosphere makes it one of the top Dharamshala tourist places for dining.
Dharamsala.Shopping in Dharamshala: Shopping in Dharamshala is a delightful experience, with plenty of unique items to take home as souvenirs. Here’s what you should know to make the most of your shopping spree during your Dharamshala trip: Tibetan handicrafts: You name it, they make it. From intricately woven carpets to thangka paintings of the Buddha, the Tibetan handicrafts are an extensively beautiful creation. Do not miss visiting the Tibetan Handicrafts Centre, a popular store for authentic and reasonably priced handicrafts. Local Tea and Spices As you browse through the local markets, pick out some scented teas and spices. Dharamsala Tea Company is a great stop to find some exciting blends to enjoy when you get home. Pashmina Shawls No visit to Dharamshala would be complete without buying the fabulous pashmina shawls offered in so many different designs and colours. Norbulingka Institute has an array of good quality shawls which are great to wear personally or give as a gift. Prayer Flags and Beads For a touch of spirituality, consider purchasing colourful prayer flags and Buddhist beads. These items are available at various stalls, especially around McLeod Ganj. Art and Souvenirs For quirky souvenirs, artwork, and some handmade pieces of jewellery, visit McLeod Ganj Main Market. It offers some of the best places to see in Dharamshala for shopping in local culture.

table_of_content: Best Time to Visit in Hampi
Interesting Things to do in Hampi
Hampi.Introduction: Hampi is one of the best jewels in Indian tourism. The Hampi tour packages are a great way to explore this amazing place, situated in the Indian state of Karnataka. You can see the famous tourist places in Hampi, learn about the history and get mesmerised by the natural beauty all around the place. It is a good idea to book one of the many Hampi holiday packages as they allow you to see the city comprehensively and also in a budget-friendly manner. Several Hampi holiday packages come with different features and you can choose the most suitable one for you. Take a look at this article to learn more about the various Hampi holiday packages. People from all over the world go on Hampi tours. These tours are rejuvenating and refreshing and allow you to witness history upfront. You need to get the Hampi holiday packages and visit the city as it has been declared a World Heritage Site by UNESCO. It is filled with old structures, including temples, castles and caves. While some have been meticulously preserved, others are in ruins, but the beauty of the architecture is one of the main attractions of a Hampi trip. The Hampi holiday packages take you to this magical place and help you to tour the best places to visit in Hampi. Apart from this, the Hampi holiday packages also take care of the travel, food and accommodation arrangements, leaving you a lot of time to enjoy the Hampi trip. Do your research and find the best Hampi travel packages that would give you easy access to this wonderful Indian tourist destination.
Hampi.Best Time to Visit in Hampi: If you wish to know more about the best time to visit Hampi, here are some handy details for you: Peak Season (October to March): The ideal time for Hampi tours is during the winter months. From October to March, Hampi experiences pleasant weather with temperatures ranging from 16°C to 32°C, making it perfect for sightseeing. This is the most popular time for Hampi trip packages, so booking in advance is recommended. Shoulder Season (July to September): Monsoon brings a different charm to Hampi. With moderate rainfall, the landscape is lush, and the weather is cooler. Hampi packages during this time offer a serene experience with fewer crowds, making it a great time for a peaceful exploration. Off-Season (April to June): Summers in Hampi are hot, but it’s also when you can find great deals on Hampi travel packages. With proper sun protection, you can still enjoy exploring the historical marvels. This off-season offers a budget-friendly Hampi trip with fewer tourists around.
Hampi.How to reach Hampi?: There are multiple travel options when it comes to reaching Hampi. You can fly in, take the train or travel by road. The travel arrangements are commonly included in the Hampi travel packages, so if you book such a package for your Hampi trip, you won't have to worry about the reservations individually. The options on how to reach Hampi are: By Air: While Hampi doesn’t have its own airport, you can fly into nearby airports like Jindal Vijayanagar Airport (40 km away), Bellary Airport (60 km away), or Hubli Airport (140 km away). Most Hampi packages include air travel, making it easier to start your Hampi tour without any hassle. By Train: The nearest railway station to Hampi is Hospet, just 12 km away. Many Hampi trip packages include train travel, offering regular services from major Indian cities. This makes train travel a convenient option for your journey. By Road: Hampi is well-connected by road to major cities like Bangalore, Pune, and Mumbai. Whether you drive, hire a taxi, or use state bus services, road travel offers flexibility. Some Hampi tours even offer one-day itineraries for those passing through.
Hampi.Places to Visit in Hampi: When you book the Hampi holiday packages and reach this city, you will have a lot of things to see. The place has been declared a UNESCO World Heritage Site, and that tells you how amazing the attractions are! Some of the best things and places to see in Hampi are: Lotus Mahal One of the most stunning places to visit in Hampi, the Lotus Mahal is a massive structure which looks like a lotus flower. It has a huge dome and beautiful carvings on the wall, which make it interesting and beautiful to look at. A visit to the Lotus Mahal is included in most Hampi tour packages so don't miss a chance to see it upfront. Queen’s Bath Another major Hampi sightseeing attraction, the Queen’s Bath is a huge structure that is perfectly preserved with no damage. It is almost 500 years old and is one of the most stunning places to visit in Hampi. You can learn about the history and also admire the architecture, which is amazing to witness. Matanga Hill You can get some wonderful aerial views of Hampi from the top of Matanga Hill, which is the highest point of Hampi. When you get one of the Hampi tour packages and go to the town, you will see many hills and hillocks. Matanga Hill is the most famous one and you should plan a trip here not only for some amazing views but also for capturing some of the best pictures of the Hampi tour. Elephant Stables This majestic structure, which has also been nearly perfectly preserved, once housed a herd of royal elephants. It is an architectural marvel. It is advisable to hire a good Hampi tour guide and see the place thoroughly while learning about its historical significance. Monolithic Bull The best part about getting a Hampi sightseeing package and visiting the town is that you see unique attractions, unlike anywhere else in the country. One of them is the Monolithic Bull, which is a de facto symbol of Hampi. It is a carved statue of Nandi which is slightly damaged, but still glorious in its structure. Old Palace (Gagan Mahal) There is a beautiful palace, Gagan Mahal, in the heart of Hampi. It is a 16th-century structure but sadly, most of it is in ruins. However, the details are still visible and very attractive. The Old Palace is one of the best Hampi places to visit. Riverside Ruins When you get one of the most popular Hampi tour packages and reach the town, you will see a lot of ruins and that adds to the charming ambience of the place. Hampi was an ancient settlement and much of it was buried underground. Discoveries are still being made and when you walk around the city, you notice the Riverside Ruins. There are caves, shrines and other structures in ruins all around, so walk around and learn more about them from your Hampi tour guide.
Hampi.Famous Temples in Hampi: Hampi is called a temple town because of the huge number of temples in the city. Here are some of the most famous ones, which must feature in your list of the best places to visit in Hampi: Virupaksha Temple The Virupaksha Temple is one of Hampi's oldest and most celebrated temples. Spread over a large area, the temple has a courtyard, two front balconies and a Nandi statue at the entrance. It is a temple dedicated to Shri Virupaksha. It was built during the Vijayanagar Dynasty and still stands strong. It is one of the best places to see in Hampi. Vijaya Vittala Temple You need to make a list of temples to see when you buy the Hampi tour packages and go to the town. Ensure you include the Vijaya Vittala Temple in the list. It is dedicated to Lord Vishnu. The temple has the famous chariot, the image of which features on the INR 50 currency notes. This temple is revered for its spirituality and also for its architecture. The stone structure, which has been around since the 15th Century, is one of the most iconic Hampi tourist places. Yantrodharaka Hanuman Temple This is a very pious place and also an elegantly constructed temple, on the top of a hill. Yantrodharaka Hanuman Temple is dedicated to Lord Hanuman. The Tungabhadra River flows at the base of the hill, making it one of the most serene and peaceful places to see in Hampi. Lakshmi Narasimha Temple If you want to see a unique statue of Lord Narasimha, head over to the Lakshmi Narasimha Temple. The idol sits in a Yoga position, with a coil of a seven-headed snake above the statue. It is beautiful to see. Kadalekalu Ganesha Temple The giant Ganesha idol at the Kadalekalu Ganesha Temple is one of the main attractions of a Hampi tour. The idol is 4.6 m high and is one of the largest Ganesha idols in India. Whether you want to explore the spiritual aspect or just get mesmerised by the architecture, a visit to the temples of Hampi can be a wonderful experience, so include them in your Hampi packages.
Hampi.Interesting Things to do in Hampi: Explore the wonders of Hampi with carefully curated Hampi tour packages that cover the best of this UNESCO World Heritage Site. From historical sites to natural beauty, here are some must-do activities during your Hampi trip: Visit the Archaeological Museum A visit to Hampi's Archaeological Museum is a journey into the past. Discover a miniature model of Hampi, ancient idols, and historical arms found over the years. This museum is a must-visit for anyone interested in the rich history of Hampi and should be included in your Hampi itinerary. See the Tungabhadra River The serene Tungabhadra River is a major highlight of Hampi. Enjoy a peaceful boat ride, or capture stunning photographs of this massive water body. Including this in your Hampi packages ensures a tranquil and rewarding experience. Explore Hippie Island Although the bustling cafes and bazaars are no longer operational, Hippie Island remains a scenic spot worth visiting. This picturesque island offers a glimpse of Hampi's vibrant past and is a perfect addition to your Hampi tours. Visit the Daroji Sloth Bear Sanctuary For wildlife enthusiasts, the Daroji Sloth Bear Sanctuary is a must-visit. Spot Indian sloth bears, leopards, and various species of birds in this rugged terrain. It’s one of the top places to visit in Hampi. Relax at Sanapur Lake Sanapur Lake offers adventure and beauty in one spot. Try cliff jumping or take a coracle ride in this lesser-known gem. Ensure your Hampi holiday packages include a visit to this thrilling location.
Hampi.What to Eat in Hampi?: After you book one of the Hampi travel packages and arrive at Hampi, you must try the local cuisine, which is a huge attraction of a Hampi trip plan. Here are some more details on what to eat in Hampi: South Indian Delicacies Savor the authentic flavors of South India with dishes like idli, dosa, and sambhar. These staples are available at nearly every eatery and make for a perfect meal during your Hampi tours. Thali meals, which are clean, fresh, and budget-friendly, are a must-try. Fast Food Delights For those craving something quick, Hampi’s vibrant cafes offer fast food options like burgers, pizzas, and sandwiches. Many cafes have relocated near Sanapur Lake, offering a mix of fast food and the scenic beauty of Hampi. Global Cuisine Given its status as a UNESCO World Heritage Site, Hampi attracts global tourists, and the local eateries cater to them with a variety of cuisines. Whether you're in the mood for Chinese, Italian, or Indian, Hampi has something to offer every palate. Refreshing Beverages Stay hydrated as you explore Hampi’s attractions. Try Goli Soda, a local favorite, or enjoy fresh fruit juices and coconut water from vendors across the city.
Hampi.What to Shop in Hampi?: When exploring Hampi, shopping is an experience you can’t miss. Hampi offers a diverse range of items that are perfect souvenirs to remember your Hampi tour packages. The bustling Hampi Bazaar is a top spot where you can find local handicrafts, clothes, and artificial jewelry at affordable prices. Don't miss the artefacts and miniature models of Hampi’s iconic structures—ideal mementos of your Hampi trip packages. For something truly unique, explore Lambani craft items, known for their intricate embroidery and mirror work. These handmade bags, shoes, and wall hangings are must-buys on your Hampi tours. Additionally, pick up musical instruments like the djembe or ektara, reflecting Hampi's rich musical heritage. Lastly, check out banana fibre items, creatively crafted into bags and home decor pieces. Including these in your Hampi packages ensures you take home the essence of this historic town.
Hampi.Interesting Facts about Hampi: Going on a Hampi tour is an exhilarating experience. You can get to experience different attractions, and flavours and feel the strong cultural heritage in your veins. To make the trip more fascinating, take a look at some of the interesting facts about Hampi listed below: One of the oldest towns in India The town of Hampi dates back to the first century BC, which makes it one of the oldest surviving towns in India. This is an amazing fact that will surely add a lot of interest to your Hampi tour. Link to the Ramayana While there are a lot of historical things to explore in the best places in Hampi, the city is also known for having a mythological link. It is widely believed that the Kishkinda Empire, the home of the monkey warriors, where Shri Ram met Shri Hanuman, was in Hampi. Lies amid a craton A craton is a geological term for a place that doesn't get affected by the earth’s tectonic activities and remains the same for billions of years. Hampi happens to fall amid a craton and so the boulders and rocks found here are presumably from pre-historic times. This is one of the coolest things about Hampi! The erstwhile Hippie Island There used to be a huge hippie settlement in Hampi in the 1960s and 1970s. Free-thinking Westerners found an island on the other side of the Tungabhadra and made it their home. The settlement excited for many decades, but was demolished in 2020 due to licensing and other legal complications. As you can see from the points mentioned above, Hampi is an extremely beautiful, interesting and fascinating place. Make a Hampi tour plan for your next vacation and have a mesmerising time with your loved ones. Book one of the best Hampi packages and set the ball rolling!

More About Buda Castle Houses on Vienna Gate Square Adorned with artistic motifs and bearing the decorations of the ancient Baroque, Rococo, and Neo-Classical styles, these houses are a reminder of the ancient Budapest. More About Houses on Vienna Gate Square Sandor Palace Dating back to more than two hundred years old, this Neo-Classical style residence belongs to the President of Hungary. However, it is open to the public on weekends during the summers More About Sandor Palace Tomb of Gul Baba Featuring the statue of this respectable sixteenth-century poet, who is also a companion of Sultan Suleiman. More About Tomb of Gul Baba Matthias Church Also referred to as the Church Of Our Lady; this one was named after the renowned King Matthias who ruled the country during the 15th century More About Matthias Church The Inner-City Church One of the oldest constructions of Budapest, this church contains a Mehrab that belongs to the ancient Turkish times. More About Medieval Synagogue Dohany Street Synagogue The largest in Europe and regarded to be the second largest synagogue in the world, this one depicts Moorish style which is a favourite among the Jews More About Dohany Street Synagogue Cave Church Situated inside a cave, this Chapel is a great attraction of Budapest. Also referred to as the Saint Ivan’s cave, this is a popular tourist spot that is busy most of the times with visitors flocking around More About Cave Church The Hungarian National Museum Exhibiting the ancient arts and culture of Hungary, this museum reflects the true culture, history, and architecture of the country. Bikaner.Introduction: Think of Rajasthan, think of Bikaner and its golden sand dunes. Explore these sand dunes and the Thar Desert with Bikaner tour packages. The colourful and vibrant city of Jaisalmer has a lot to see and explore. From ornate architecture to forts, from building standing from centuries to the elegant palaces, Bikaner has it all.
Bikaner.Most Popular Bikaner Tour Packages: Tour Packages NameDurationPricePackage InclusivesWhole of Rajasthan9 Nights 10 DaysRs. ₹49 600Flights, Sightseeing, Hotels, Transfers, Meals Best time to visit Bikaner is between October to March. The weather is pleasant and feasible enough to take camel safaris in the Thar Desert. The city is well connected by air, road and railways. The nearest airport is Jodhpur airport. Bikaner is a favourite among travellers all over the world. The rich heritage, local markets, cultural programs, folk art and the authentic local food draw a lot of tourists year after year. When in Bikaner, do visit the Junagarh Fort; understand the history a little more at the Prachina Museum. Pay respects to the gods and the rats at the Karni Mata Temple enjoy the sand dune safari and be wonderstruck by the Rampuria haveli and Lalgarh Palace. Places to Visit in Bikaner Places to Visit Significance More Details Junagarh Fort This fort made from red sandstone houses palaces and temples. The eye-catching architecture and the intricately done windows, galleries and lawns are noteworthy. More About Junagarh Fort Shiv Bari Mandir Built in the late 19th century by the famous DhungarSinghji, this temple is a dedication to Lord Shiva with a bronze Nandi that faces towards the Shiva Lingam. More About Shiv Bari Mandir Lallgarh Palace One of the most magnificent words of the 1942 AD built by the king Maharaja Ganga Sing. Built entirely out of red stone, this architectural wonder is a must-visit place in the entire Bikaner More About Lallgarh Palace Jain Temple 4 Days / 3 NightsThe Jain Temple at Bhandasar lights up to the yellow stone carvings and some beautiful paintings. The walls carry the depictions of the 24 Jain teachers. More About Jain Temple Gajner Palace This was the hunting and vacation lodge of the Maharaja of Bikaner. Made of red sandstone, this palace has intricately carved pillars and is an example of fine craftsmanship More About Gajner Palace Gajner National Park Bliss for all the bird lovers, this national park is home for various kinds of birds from different species. It hosts the famous Blackbucks, Nilgais, Chinkaras and many other animals. More About Gajner National Park Thar Desert The golden sands are perfect for a camel safari. More About Thar Desert Trikuta hill The Trikuta fort is a highlight with its high walls and 99 bastions which attracts tourists. More About Trikuta hill Devi Kund Sagar Situated about 8 km east from the city of Bikaner, the Devi Kund Sagar is a Camel Breeding Farm alongside some natural beauty that you just cannot get enough of. More About Devi Kund Sagar Shri Laxminath Temple One of the oldest temples in Bikaner dedicated to Lord Vishnu and Goddess Laxmi. The temple witness’s grand celebrations during Diwali, Ramnavami and Janmashtami. More About Shri Laxminath Temple Bikaner is a treat for all travellers, especially history and photography enthusiasts. So, all you need to do is relax and soak into the Rajasthani hospitality and vibrant culture and heritage.

faq.question: How many days are ideal for a Dehradun–Mussoorie trip?
faq.answer: Four to five days is the perfect amount of time for a leisurely visit to Dehradun with Mussoorie. Important locations, including Robber's Cave, Sahastradhara, Mall Road, and Kempty Falls, will be covered in two to three days if you select a short Dehradun vacation package. However, if you prefer a more leisurely pace, you can also explore Landour, Lal Tibba, or Dhanaulti with a five-day Dehradun Mussoorie vacation itinerary. The majority of carefully chosen Dehradun Mussoorie package choices provide a good mix of leisure and sightseeing, guaranteeing that your time is well spent. When you reserve through personalised Dehradun vacation packages, the schedule is tailored to your preferences, allowing you to experience the ideal balance of hill station tranquillity and city charm.

faq.question: What is the best itinerary for a Dehradun Mussoorie package?
faq.answer: The ideal travel itinerary for Dehradun Mussoorie typically lasts four days. On the first day of your journey, you will travel to Dehradun, where you will have the opportunity to visit Sahastradhara, the Forest Research Institute, and Paltan Bazaar for shopping. On the second day, you travel to Mussoorie, where you can stroll down Mall Road and possibly take a cable car to Gun Hill. Dehra dun.Top Attractions & Experiences in Dehradu: Nestled in the Himalayan foothills, Dehradun is one of those places that captivates you with its blend of history, spirituality, and nature. Let's take a tour of some of the must-see locations that will make your travel package to Dehradun worthwhile. Robber’s Cave (Guchhupani) This cave, a natural wonder, is well-known for its stream that magically emerges and vanishes underneath. In addition to being rejuvenating, going barefoot in the freezing water is an adventure in and of itself. This trip is included in many Dehradun tour packages, and with good reason; it's one of the city's most distinctive attractions. Mindrolling Monastery The monastery, which is a tranquil example of Tibetan culture in Dehradun, is home to the magnificent 220-foot Great Stupa, verdant gardens, and exquisite murals. It's a serene place to think, which makes it particularly beautiful in a Dehradun tour package for couple looking for serenity and cultural richness. Sahastradhara Known as the "Thousand-fold Spring," this location is a visual and spiritual delight. It's the ideal place for anyone wishing to unwind and revitalise, with its sulfur-rich waters thought to have therapeutic properties, tumbling waterfalls, and a breathtaking ropeway ride. It is frequently featured in family and nature-loving Dehradun packages. Tapkeshwar Temple Tucked up in a cave, this ancient Shiva temple is incredibly fascinating. The temple's name comes from the water that naturally falls on the Shivling. If you're looking for a mix of adventure and devotion, this spiritual destination will add some diversity to your Dehradun Mussoorie package. Forest Research Institute (FRI) This colonial jewel, which spans 450 acres, has the air of a movie set; in fact, it has served as the backdrop for numerous Bollywood productions. Families and students find it to be the perfect addition to a Dehradun trip package because of the educational value of the museums inside. Lachhiwala Nature Park This park, which is encircled by dense forests, is ideal for birdwatching, picnicking, and swimming in the pools. It's quiet but bustling, and if you're watching how much your Dehradun trip cost, it's a perfect addition. Malsi Deer Park (Dehradun Zoo) This little zoo on the route to Mussoorie is a family favourite and a cool green haven. It's a destination that's frequently included in Dehradun Mussoorie travel plans because it blends enjoyment with nature. Paltan Bazaar Without shopping, no Dehradun travel package is complete. This lively market next to the Clock Tower offers you a flavour of the city's lively local life, from street food to local woollens and spices.

Scandinavia.Introduction: About Scandinavia Holidays - There’s something about a Scandinavia holiday that goes beyond any holiday experience elsewhere in the world. Thomas Cook India’s Scandinavia Tour Packages have been designed to highlight the best of all that Scandinavia has to offer. We add convenience to your Scandinavia trip by taking away all the intricacies of planning. Thomas Cook India’s Scandinavia holiday packages come in many sizes. You can even book your Scandinavia holiday package online on our website. Your Scandinavia tour package has been created by our team of in-house experts who truly know how to create a world class holiday experience. Markedly different from any other international holiday destinations, the countries in Scandinavia enjoy most of their holidays outdoors. With easy connectivity with all the major cities in India such as Mumbai, Delhi, Bengaluru, Chennai, Kolkata and Hyderabad, your Scandinavia trip gets off to a great start. Read on to find a handy tour guide to the stunning Scandinavia.
Scandinavia.Scandinavia Holiday Packages: Scandinavia Tour Packages No. of Days Price* Scandinavian Extravaganza (Summer 2023) 9 Nights / 10 Days Rs. 3 19 125.00 Monomugdho Scandinavia - Durga Puja Special 9 Nights / 10 Days Rs. 3 33 313.00 Scandinavian Extravaganza with Midnight Sun 12 Nights / 13 Days Rs. 4 07 688.00 Scandinavia comprises of five countries; Denmark, Finland, Iceland, Norway and Sweden. The home of legendary Gods, thrilling quests and epic wars is an amazing holiday destination full of varied experiences. The people in Scandinavian countries are big on outdoor fun and all their games and activities are made for the great outdoors. The breath-taking landscapes lend a spectacular background to the outdoors. Beautiful by day and stunning by night, the bewitching outdoors of Scandinavia latch on to your imagination and spur you forward on adventurous explorations.Most people think that the aurora borealis is the biggest attraction of Scandinavia. Yes, the first glimpse of the celestial dance of light is a wondrous moment, incomparable to anything else in the world. But the biggest attraction here is the culture and the people, who together make Scandinavia what it is. After all, the legends of the Vikings have fascinated us for centuries! Here, nature shakes off her shackles and is allowed full access to the lay of the land. The awe-inspiring fjords of Norway and Iceland’s surprisingly green beauty stand testament to nature’s bounty. Thomas Cook India has all-inclusive Scandinavia holiday packages that offer you the most comprehensive tour of the five Scandinavian countries. The Best Time to Visit ScandinaviaSummer is the best time to visit Scandinavia. The sub-zero temperatures and heavy snowfall during winter make it challenging to withstand the weather. Although that is exactly the season when adventure seekers start streaming in for some of the best skiing and snowy adventures. If you can brave the winter cold, you’ll be treated to the most spectacular light show on earth, the aurora borealis. Between April to July, the summer temperatures range between 20°C to 30°C all over Scandinavia. Denmark, Norway and Sweden are cooler than Finland which averages in the upper 20s all through the summer. When summer finally arrives after a freezing winter, Scandinavians venture outdoors. Trekking and camping are the most popular summer pastimes. As a tourist you can enjoy sightseeing to the fullest. But, considering the popularity of summer, it gets crowded during the season. For this reason, the shoulder seasons of spring and autumn are also good times to be in Scandinavia. You can enjoy almost all the activities that you can in summer with a couple of added advantages, lower prices and no crowds. Hike the stunning Faroe Islands and take a mini vacation along the Danish coast. Go surfing in Finland and take a time out at Sweden’s sandy resorts. Explore Norway’s fjords which have been blessed with an otherworldly beauty that’s simply magnificent. How to Reach ScandinaviaFlying is the only way you can reach Scandinavia from India. The region has several airports that are internationally well-connected. Denmark’s Copenhagen International Airport sits just 20 minutes, by train or metro, outside the city. Though there are regular flights between India and Copenhagen, there are no direct flights. You will have at least one layover on your way to Denmark. Iceland’s Keflavik International Airport is a 45-minute drive from its capital Reykjavik. Again, there are no direct flights from India and all flights have at least one stop along the way. Finland has the Helsinki Vantaan Airport to handle international flights. You can find a flight to inland from India quite easily, but no direct flights. The shortest flying time to Finland is 13hrs and 25mins. Norway has two main international airports. The Oslo Gardermoen Airport is a 20-minute shuttle ride away from the capital. The Bergen Flesland Airport is good option if you’re starting your trip along Norway’s west coast. There are no direct flights from India to either of these cities, but there are more flights available for Oslo than for Bergen. If you’re headed to Sweden, you’ll land at the Stockholm Arlanda Airport. Like the other countries of Scandinavia, you won’t find any direct flights to Sweden either. From the airport, the Arlanda Express train will take you into the city in 20 minutes. Where to Go in ScandinaviaIn between the 5 countries that make up Scandinavia, you have a wide range of choices to go sightseeing. Each country has it places to visit and things to do. Due to its location Scandinavia is home to many fascinating natural phenomena like the midnight sun and the northern lights. Here are a few places that you must visit while in Scandinavia. Copenhagen, DenmarkThe capital of Copenhagen is a trendy city that lives on the cutting edge of latest trends in music, design and fashion. It is an amazing place to explore by bike or boat. When you’re in the cycling capital of Europe, grab a bike and enjoy the city at its leisurely best. Copenhagen offers a varied sightseeing experience. From historic 12th-century castles to the hippest cafes, the capital has it all. The Danish Royal Gardens and Amalienborg are must-visit attractions as are the Tivoli Gardens. Get yourself a ‘Copenhagen Card’ not only gives you free admission to city’s best attractions, but also allows you unlimited use of the city’s excellent public transport system. Stockholm, SwedenSweden’s capital city is spread out over a series of 14 islands and has an amazingly lively waterfront as a result. With cafes and pubs lining the water’s edge, it is the best place in the city to feel the city’s social pulse. Stockholm revers its traditions and has lovingly preserved the old town for the ages. But the newer part of the city is full of landmark contemporary architectural designs. This coexistence of the new and the old is the essence of this vibrant city. Stockholm is also home to a large number of museums that preserve parts of their culture and traditions. A guided tour of the city is one of the best ways to explore them all. Oslo, NorwayOslo is an amazing international city that has a sizeable collection of museums and art galleries. From Viking adventures to the Koi-Tiki expeditions, you will find a bewildering array of interesting exhibits in the city’s museums. The Botanical Gardens, with over 5,500 plant species from all over the world, are a treat for nature lovers. The Songsvann Lake is an oasis of calm in the middle of Oslo’s hustle and bustle while the Østensjø Lake on the eastern edge provides a beautiful backdrop for unhurried walks. Don’t forget to check out the Holmenkollbakken ski jump which is a major hub of the sport. Keep a day aside for island-hopping between the nearby Oslofjord islands. Helsinki, FinlandFinland’s capital is also its largest city and the uncrowned cruise capital of Scandinavia. Helsinki is best enjoyed on foot or a bicycle. The city centre itself is a beautiful example of Neoclassical design and boasts of wide boulevards and beautifully maintained parks. The fortified islands of Soumenlinna are a short 20-minute ferry ride away from the city. It is a UNESCO World Heritage monument that now houses a museum, a park and an arts venue. The Market Square in the heart of Helsinki is a bustling slice of local life while the Temppeliaokiu Church is a labor of love carved out from the Scandinavian bedrock. Do visit the Seurasaari island to experience the living open-air museum. Reykjavik, IcelandThe land of fire and ice is a wonderful blend of contemporary design married to old traditions. And nowhere is it more apparent that at the Hallsgrímskirkja Church which sports a design unlike a church anywhere. Its massive 25-ton pipe organ and its design are the highlights of this attraction. Downtown Reykjavik is a wonderful place for shopping or enjoying a quiet walk along the water’s edge. The Harpa Concert Hall will astound you with its eye-catching design while the Perlan Museum of Icelandic Natural Wonders will stun you with its strap, ultra-modern design. Take a stroll through the Old Harbour area to soak in the essence of the true Icelandic culture. Food in ScandinaviaThere’s a common streak that runs through the culinary culture across Scandinavia. Though each country has its own unique twist on flavours and textures, they all emphasise on using fresh nutritious ingredients that are either grown or sourced locally. While the flavour palette may change from country to country, the freshness and nutrition aspects don’t. Here are some dishes that you should try in Scandinavia. DenmarkMeat is a staple of Danish cuisine. Denmark’s national dish, Stegt Flæsk Med Persillesovs, is a simple rustic dish. Crispy fried pork served with sides of boiled potatoes, pickled beetroot and a flavour-packed parsley sauce. It is easily available in restaurants everywhere and can be eaten at any time of the day. The Karbonader is yet another simple dish which is a pork patty served with carrots and peas in white sauce. SwedenSwedish cuisine is all about bread, meat and seafood. The Knäckebröd is a crisp bread that has a 500-year-old tradition behind it. It is a hearty side dish that is usually topped with ham, cheese or caviar. Raggmunk is a potato pancake fried in butter and served with pork or lingonberries. The Swedes enjoy their Gravlax (dill-cured salmon) with a healthy helping of mustard sauce. Be sure to try the Smörgåsbord, the Swedish buffet featuring all national favourites. NorwayNorway’s cuisine has been heavily influenced by its culture and Norwegians love to cook their meals from scratch. Pickled Herring is one such dish which has its roots in the Viking culture. It is a simple dish featuring brined herring treated with salt and vinegar. Fårikål is Norway’s national dish which features lamb or mutton slow cooked over a few hours with a bunch of vegetables. Kumla is a tender potato dumpling enjoyed with a side of meat and sour cream. Do try the Krumkake which is a much-loved dessert all over. FinlandThe Finnish cuisine is a hearty fare that mostly uses meat and sea food along with number of unusual ingredients like reindeer. Poronkäristys is a herb-infused preparation of sautéed reindeer served with mashed potatoes and lingonberries reduction. Lohikeitto is a rich salmon soup that is usually eaten with a side of rye bread. Lihapiirakka is an unusual meat pie stuffed with minced beef and cooked rice, though there are variations that use pickles, sausages and fried eggs. Leipäjuusto is a slightly sweet cheese that is baked and served hot with a topping of bright-orange cloudberry jam. IcelandTraditional Icelandic cuisine is inspired by local, easy-to-source ingredients such as fish, lamb, potatoes, bread and dairy. Kjötsupa is the traditional lamb meat soup that combines the tougher parts of the lamb and veggies with Icelandic herbs. Plokkfiskur is mild fish stew that uses boiled haddock or cod fillets and is served with mashed potatoes. Hákarl is fermented shark meat that is buried in sand for 3 months, hung up to dry for 3-4 months and fermented for about 6-9 weeks before being consumed. It is an acquired taste though. Things to Do in ScandinaviaOutdoor activities are the most favourite pastime in Scandinavia. From trekking and surfing in summer to skiing and snowboarding in winter, the outdoors are a big part of daily life. The striking landscapes of Scandinavia for the perfect backdrops for your adventures. Here are some things to do in Scandinavia that are highly recommended. DenmarkDenmark combines its rich history with its share of nature’s brilliance to offer a profusion of experiences. The Copenhagen Zoo is a good place to start your sightseeing. First opened in 1859, it is one of the oldest zoos in Europe. It is home to 264 different species of animals. The Thy National Park lets you observe the local wildlife in its natural habitat and grants you access to grave sites dating back to the bronze age. Denmark has a substantial collection of castles and exploring them is a great way to continue your sightseeing journey. SwedenSweden’s tourism experience is a combination of royal palaces, interesting museums, teeming wildlife parks and thrilling water sports. The Drottningholm Royal Palace is a UNESCO World Heritage Site that offers you a peek into the life of Denmark’s royalty. The Kolmarden Wildlife Park where you can not only observe wildlife up close, but you can also enjoy a gondola safari ride. But one of the best things to do in Sweden is to spend a night or two in the famous Icehotel where everything, including the hotel itself, is made of ice. If you venture outdoors, kayaking is a popular adventure activity. NorwayThe ‘Land of the Outdoors’ in true sense, Norway is an adventurer’s delight. Take a train journey through some the most spectacular sights in Norway. Bergen Railway and Dovre Railway are two of the best rail journeys of Norway. Trek along the Geirangerfjord to satiate the adventurer and the nature-lover in you. Stay a prayer at the spectacular Arctic Cathedral sporting a full glass facade. Travel along the iconic Atlantic Ocean Road that takes you past idyllic villages and the rugged coastline. Visit the Kon-Tiki Museum and the Vigeland Sculpture Park to appreciate Norway’s art and history. But the highlight of a Norway trip are the northern lights which you can observe from near the Arctic Circle. FinlandFinland is the most underrated Scandinavian country. But with its majestic forests and expansive landscapes, it can stand proudly with the others. Meet Mr and Mrs Santa Claus at Rovaniemi and learn to make gingerbread treats. Walk through history of Laplanders at the Siida Museum in Inaari. Explore the 15th-century Olavinlinna Castle and take a guided tour to learn about life back in the days. Experience the thrill of skiing at Levi where you get a choice between 43 different slopes! How about mining for amethysts? Go digging for amethysts at Lampivaara and you can even keep any you find, provided they are smaller than a fist. IcelandIceland’s landscape has been shaped by ice and volcanoes over centuries. The Snæfellsjökull National Park is a place where you can observe both. You can not only explore a series of amazing lava tubes, but you can also walk on the Snæfellsjökull glacier. The Hornstrandir Natural Reserve is the perfect place for a picturesque hike with its wide-open spaces and flat terrain. Pamper yourself with a dip in the Blue Lagoon, which is one of the most famous attractions of Iceland. Let the heated seawater drain away the labours of your hike as you relax in the turquoise waters. Do visit the Viking World Museum which showcases the life of vikings through a series of exhibits. Scandinavia FAQs How many countries make up Scandinavia?Scandinavia is made up of five countries; Denmark, Finland, Iceland, Norway and Sweden. Can I use a Schengen visa to visit Scandinavia?Yes, you can visit Scandinavia using a valid Schengen visa.When is the best time to visit Scandinavia?The summer season is the best time to visit Scandinavia to experience everything that it has to offer. However, the shoulder seasons, spring and autumn, are good times to visit Scandinavia as it is uncrowded during these seasons. How many days are required for a holiday to Scandinavia?Scandinavia included five countries. You will need at least 12 nights and 13 days to be able to explore even a fraction of Scandinavia. What currency is accepted across Scandinavia?While the Euro is accepted in all the Scandinavian countries, each of them, except Finland, prefer to use their local currencies. Finland uses the Euro.Denmark - Danish Kroner (DKK)Sweden - Swedish Krona (SEK)Norway - Norwegian Krone (NOK)Iceland - Icelandic Krona (ISK) Can I use my Indian driver's license to drive through Scandinavia?Norway, Finland, Sweden and Finland will allow you to drive for 3 months to a year if you hold a valid Indian driving license printed in English. Denmark, however, needs a valid International Driving Permit along with a valid Indian driving license. Which is the best place to start a road trip through Scandinavia?There is no best single starting point for a Scandinavian road trip. Depending on what you want from your holiday, you can start your road trip from any one of the five countries. Which Scandinavian country is the best?Every country in Scandinavia has its share of unique attractions and experiences. A leisurely holiday to each one will help you make up your mind. Which is the cheapest Scandinavian country?Denmark is the cheapest to visit among the Scandinavian countries. Which Scandinavian country is the most expensive?Iceland is the most expensive Scandinavian country to visit.

Do Dham.Introduction: The Do Dham pilgrimage circuit is one of the most popular pilgrimages in India for Hindus. The most popular option for the Do Dham Yatra in Uttarakhand is Kedarnath and Badrinath. Kedarnath is one of the holiest sites in India. To avoid confrontation with the Pandavas following the Kurukshetra war, Lord Shiva took the disguise of a bull. However, upon getting recognised by Bhima, he quickly disappeared, leaving some body parts of the bull at certain locations, including the hump at Kedarnath, the site where the Kedarnath Temple was built and has since been worshipped. Badrinath is one of the most significant sacred sites in the country. It is also a part of the original Char Dham Yatra. It is home to the revered Badrinath Temple, one of the 108 Dibya Desams of Lord Vishnu. Many legends surround this sacred site, with one of the most popular being that Lord Vishnu meditated here, unaware of the harsh cold. His consort, Lakshmi, protected him by taking the form of the Badri tree. Pleased by her devotion, Lord Vishnu named the place Badrika Ashram. Though there are multiple legends associated with each Dham, there’s no doubt that all of them have immense religious and cultural significance in India. Our Do Dham Yatra packages help you be a part of this incredible Yatra experience. However, you can also choose to go on a Do Dham Yatra centred around Yamunotri and Gangotri. All four abodes have major significance in Hinduism and are part of the Chota Char Dham Yatra circuit in Uttarakhand. Yamunotri is the origin of the sacred Yamuna River and the abode of Goddess Yamuna. According to legends, it is believed that individuals who take a dip in the Yamuna River will attain salvation and relieve themselves from the torments of death. As for Gangotri, it is believed that the most sacred of all rivers, the Ganga, descended to earth from heaven at this Dham when Lord Shiva released the river from his locks. There are some local traditions and rituals that are practised in Do Dham that tourists should also know about and follow. For example, the Original Chota Char Dham pilgrimage should be completed in a clockwise direction, starting with Yamunotri and ending at Badrinath. So for example, you can start with Kedarnath and end at Badrinath for your Do Dham Yatra. There are sacred rivers flowing by these holy abodes, where devotees take a dip to wash away their sins and attain salvation. Find Do Dham yatra packages in your budget on our website.
