Do Dham.Places to Visit in Do Dham: Adi Shankaracharya Samadhi Just behind the revered Kedarnath Temple is the Adi Shankaracharya Samadhi, the distinguished philosopher who created the original Char Dham Pilgrimage Circuit covering Puri, Rameswaram, Dwarka and Badrinath. He attained Nirvana at the age of 32 and is one of the most prominent figures in Hinduism. It is believed that he was the one who constructed the shrine back in the 8th century. Make sure to pay your respects at his Samadhi. Book your 2 Dham Yatra package on Thomas Cook online in a few easy steps. Vyas Gufa Just a few kilometres from Badrinath is Vyas Gufa, an ancient cave that is believed to be the place where sage Vyas, with the help of Lord Ganesha, composed the Indian epic, Mahabharata. You can consider including the nearby Ganesh Gufa, also associated with this legend, in your Do Dham itinerary. You can find a 2 Dham Yatra package that features this site on our website. Gauri Kund Gauri Kund is yet another popular religious site near Do Dham that you can visit. The place is associated with many legends, including the one where Devi Parvati, Lord Shiva’s wife, did penance to win over Lord Shiva. This site is commonly featured in popular Do Dham tour packages.
Do Dham.Unique features of Do Dham: There are many sacred rivers flowing through the holy abodes of Do Dham, including River Alakananda, Mandakini, Bhagirathi, Ganges, etc. There are also numerous caves, lakes and natural hot springs across the region, most of which are part of the holy journey. Located in Uttarakhand’s Garhwal Himalaya region, it is no wonder that the place is filled with stunning landscapes, mainly of green valleys, snow-capped mountains and lush forests. Most of these sites are featured in Do Dham yatra tour packages.
Do Dham.Famous Temples to Visit on Do Dham Tour: 1. Kedarnath Temple Nestled at an elevation of 3583 metres near the Mandakini River, the Kedarnath Temple is one of the most important Hindu Temples in India. It is one of Lord Shiva’s twelve Jyotirlinga and is only accessible for darshans at a certain period of the year due to the harsh weather conditions of the region. The temple is not directly accessible by road and can only be reached by completing a 22 km trek from Gaurikund. As per Hindu legends, the original temple was built by the Pandavas and Adi Shankara revived the temple centuries later. The temple is also part of the Panch Kedar, along with Madhmaheshwar, Tungnath, Rudranath and Kalpnath. The incredible architecture of this ancient temple and its scenic backdrop make for a visual treat. Inside the temple, there are statues and images of the five Pandava brothers, Lord Krishna, Nandi, Draupadi, and many other prominent figures of Hindu mythology. The presiding deity of the temple is in the form of a triangular-shaped lingam, placed on a giant pedestal. Find Do Dham Yatra packages designed around Kedarnath and Badrinath on Thomas Cook. 2. Badrinath Temple Following Kedarnath, the next destination on the popular 2 Dham Yatra is Badrinath, home to the revered Badrinath Temple. This temple holds immense significance, particularly for Vaishnavas, and is one of India's most important religious sites. It is part of the original Char Dham Yatra established by Adi Shankara and is also one of the 108 Divya Desams of Lord Vishnu. The temple’s main deity is Lord Badrinath, an incarnation of Lord Vishnu, worshipped as a black granite idol. This idol is believed to be one of the eight *Swayam Vyakta Kshetras*, or self-manifested images. Located at an elevation of 3100 metres, it is also only accessible at certain months of the year due to extreme weather conditions. The temple is known for its 50 metres tall colourful main gate, called Singhdwar, a thermal spring, called Tapt Kund, and for housing images of many other deities besides Lord Vishnu. Book Do Dham packages featuring Badrinath and Kedarnath on Thomas Cook. Temple Name General Darshan Timing Visiting Months Kedarnath Temple 6 AM- 3 PM & 5 PM- 7 PM April-November Badrinath Temple 4:30 AM- 1 PM & 4:30 PM- 9 PM April-November
Do Dham.Things to Know While Visiting Do Dham To: Here are some things that you should know before your Do Dham Yatra; Registration is important if you are visiting the holy abodes of Do Dham Yatra. The journey is a bit physically demanding, so make sure to follow a light workout routine a few weeks before the Yatra. Respect the local culture and practices in the region. Wear modest and non-revealing clothes when visiting the temples. Cast off footwear outside the temple premises before entering. Make sure to pack all the essential items for the trip, including food, water, warm clothes, a first aid kit, prescribed medication, a torch, etc. Avoid touching the idols within the temple. Make sure to not take pictures inside the temple premises of Kedarnath and Badrinath.
Do Dham.Best Time to Visit Do Dham: 1. Summer season Summer is the peak season for Do Dham Yatra in Uttarakhand. It stretches from April to June and features pleasant temperatures and clear weather conditions. Though it is termed summer, make sure to carry warm clothes as the temperature stays on the lower side due to the high altitude. Due to high demand, make sure to book accommodation, transfers, Do Dham tour packages, etc., in advance. 2. Monsoon season Monsoon covers the months of July to September in the region. The months of July and August are not ideal for the Do Dham Yatra as the region experiences heavy monsoon rainfall, which makes the yatra difficult due to the increased risk of floods and landslides. It is better to avoid Do Dham Yatra during this time. However, the time from mid-September onwards can be a good time for the Yatra as the rain discontinues around this period. 3. Winter season Winter in the region starts in October and continues till March. Only the early part of winter, that is October, is ideal for going on the Do Dham Yatra. But do note that the weather around this month is very cold, featuring an average range of 5-15°C. As November approaches, the Yatra is closed down due to the oncoming harsh cold and rough weather.
Do Dham.Do Dham Yatra: A Transformative Journey: A visit to Do Dham centred around Kedarnath and Badrinath, has been a life-altering experience for good. Part of the Chota Char Dham or the Uttarakhand Char Dham Yatra, these two sites have immense religious, cultural and historical importance in India for the people of the Hindu faith. After setting foot in Uttarakhand, as we were travelling through the winding roads leading to the first site of the Do Dham Yatra, Kedarnath, the feeling of an elevated thrill and excitement suddenly hit us from within. It was as if our heart was saying, “It is finally happening”. The feeling of visiting and paying our respect at the revered holy sites that many dream of visiting one day has been wonderful. The trek to the temples might be a bit challenging but it feels as if the Lord is testing our faith and perseverance. Once you reach the holy abodes, the feeling of fulfilment goes over the roof. As for stepping inside the temples, catching a glimpse of the deities and offering our prayers, it was a surreal experience. Adi Shankara once said, “Do not be proud of wealth, relations or youth. All these are snatched by time in the blink of an eye”, which resonated with me during the Do Dham Yatra. While we may not have real control over materialistic possessions, the experience and feeling of fulfilment and peace we achieved by visiting the holy abodes of Kedarnath and Badrinath can never be taken away from us.
Do Dham.Famous Foods to Eat in Do Dham?: 1. Thechwani It is a heartwarming vegetarian dish from the region, prepared using potato and radish roots from the mountainous region. They are mashed together with some ginger, tomatoes, onions, and a series of spices and herbs. Thechwani is best eaten alongside bread or rice. 2. Kafuli Kafuli is a leafy vegetable dish, made using locally produced fresh ingredients like ginger, fenugreek leaves, curd, spinach, garlic, onion and a few spices. This Garhwali version of Saag is best consumed with Chapati or steamed rice. 3. Kode ki Roti This flatbread is a staple food in the region. It is a roti made with a type of grain that is grown in the region, called Koda or Ragi. It is served hot with a topping of butter and is usually paired with aromatic daal. 4. Kumaoni Raita It is a type of delicious Raita made using coriander, fresh cucumber, yoghurt, local herbs, and salt (as per taste). These ingredients are chopped and mixed in the yoghurt to form a semi-viscous yield, which will feel divine on your taste buds. 5. Chaunsu Chaunsu is a simple dish made by first roasting black lentils and then grinding them until a powdery texture is achieved. The powder is then cooked alongside some regional herbs and spices in a curry form and paired with rice or chapati at the dinner table.
Do Dham.Divine Souvenirs from Do Dham: Make sure to leave some room on your Do Dham tour itinerary to go shopping in the region. If you are interested in gift and souvenir shopping, consider purchasing these items during your do dham yatra; 1. Religious books and other accessories As you explore the region on your Do Dham Yatra, you may find the urge to learn more about the local culture and religious history. For this, you can shop for religious books, which can be a great read for yourself or a gift for the elderly. You can also shop for religious items like Rudraksha Mala, pictures of deities, incense sticks, etc. 2. Sacred Jal Devotees on their holy Do Dham Yatra like to bring a part of the sacred blessings for their loved ones at home. Hence, they buy sacred jal, available in small sealed containers, which also have many spiritual uses. 3. Woollen clothing There are plenty of stores across the region selling high-quality woollen shawls and other clothing at reasonable prices. These are great for personal use as well as gifts for the loved ones. 4. Wood and stone craftwork The region is filled with skilled craftsmen, who show a glimpse of the local art and culture through their products. If you want, you can shop for wooden and stone craftwork, such as small figurines of deities while on your Do Dham Yatra.

Azerbaijan.Best Places To Visit In Azerbaijan: Regardless of whether you're travelling for adventure, a honeymoon, or simply some peace, these must-see things to see in Azerbaijan are perfect for anyone purchasing Azerbaijan holiday packages from India. Famous Places To Visit The following are some of the best places to visit in Azerbaijan: Yanar Dagh (Burning Mountain): Because natural gas seeps through the ground, this hillside fire never goes out. On your visit to Azerbaijan, you should not miss this strange encounter, which is among the Azerbaijan tourist spots. Gobustan Rock Art Cultural Landscape: Gobustan is a must-see for anybody interested in history and ancient art. A UNESCO site, it features mud volcanoes, more than 6,000 rock carvings, and even a "singing rock." It greatly enhances the value of your Azerbaijan tour packages from India. Mud Volcanoes: It may surprise you to learn that Azerbaijan has more mud volcanoes than any other nation. They are unlike anything else you'll see, and they bubble and burp in the Gobustan area. An essential addition to any Azerbaijan tour packages from India. Palace of Shaki Khans: Visit this palace from the 18th century to get a glimpse of Azerbaijan's royal past. It is among the best sites in Azerbaijan for history buffs to see because of the elaborate carvings and stained glass windows. Khinalig Village: One of the world's oldest mountain communities, Khinalig is nestled in the Caucasus Mountains. For those interested in culture, while on a trip to Azerbaijan, it provides a stunning vista and a genuine glimpse into rural life. Beaches/ Hill Stations to Visit When travelling to Azerbaijan, don’t miss out on these hill stations and beaches: Nabran: Nabran is the place to go if you're looking for a tranquil coastal getaway when visiting Azerbaijan. It is encircled by lush forests and is teeming with resorts. Lankaran: The laid-back atmosphere of Lankaran, which is well-known for its black sand beaches and hot springs, is ideal for opulent Azerbaijan honeymoon packages from India. Resort at Tufandag Mountain: This mountain retreat in Gabala provides summer and winter activities. For couples in particular, it's the ideal addition to your Azerbaijan packages. Resort in Shahdag Mountain: Shahdag is one Azerbaijan tourist attractions for adventurers, whether they prefer hiking in the summer or skiing in the winter. One of the top places to go in Azerbaijan for adventure seekers. Goygol Lake: This breathtaking alpine lake is renowned for its unspoiled natural beauty and glistening water. A serene places to visit in Azerbaijan, ideal for honeymooners or those exploring the outdoors. Temples To Visit For that additional sprinkle of spirituality Add these to your itinerary: Juma Mosque, Shamakhi: This historic location, which is among the oldest mosques in the Caucasus, is stunningly attractive both architecturally and spiritually. An excellent place to learn about the Azerbaijan attractions. Ateshgah Fire Temple: This historic shrine in Surakhani was formerly frequented by Zoroastrian and Hindu devotees. It enhances the spiritual appeal of your Azerbaijan tour guide. Church of Kish: This location, close to Sheki, has great historical and religious significance since it is thought to be one of the first Christian churches in the Caucasus. Besh Barmag Mountain: For the locals, it is also referred to as Five Finger Mountain and is a sacred site. It is one of the most unusual sights in Azerbaijan because of the scenery and serene spiritual atmosphere. Heydar Mosque: Sunnis and Shias pray together in this contemporary masterpiece. It symbolises unity, making it one of the interesting places to see in Azerbaijan while on your trip to Azerbaijan.

Sikkim.Detailed Itinerary for Sikkim Tour: A well-designed Sikkim tour plan should have the right amount of culture, scenery, and relaxation. This is an example of a 7-day Sikkim trip package that Thomas Cook put together. You can use this plan as a guide for your Sikkim vacation packages, whether you want a family-friendly, wedding, or adventure-filled trip. Day 1: Welcome to Sikkim – Arrival at Gangtok Your package trip to Sikkim starts at Bagdogra Airport or NJP Railway Station. Our employee will take you to Gangtok, which is the capital city. Enjoy the four to five-hour drive through mountain ranges and river bottoms. When you get there, check into your hotel and take it easy. Evening at leisure to experience MG Marg, a popular spot included in many Sikkim tours and travels packages. You have today to get used to the altitude before your fun trip. Day 2: Full-Day Gangtok Sightseeing Start your trip to Sikkim packages with a filling meal and a full day of exploring: Tashi View Point Ganesh Tok and Hanuman Tok Flower Exhibition Centre Do Drul Chorten Stupa Namgyal Institute of Tibetology Most Sikkim tours and packages include these must-see sights. Get back to the hotel by nightfall. Have dinner and stay the night. Day 3: Excursion to Tsomgo Lake & Baba Mandir This part of your Sikkim tour plan takes you to high-altitude sights: Locals hold Tsomgo Lake (12,400 ft) in high regard as a holy glacier lake. Baba Harbhajan Mandir is a shrine full of stories for soldiers. Nathula Pass is an extra, weather-permitting thing to add to your trip to Sikkim package that you need to get a permit for ahead of time. Go back to Gangtok and stay the night. Day 4: Journey to Lachung – Into North Sikkim Get up early and go to Lachung (8,600 feet) in North Sikkim. On the way, witness: Seven Sisters Waterfall Singhik Viewpoint Naga Waterfall When you get there, check into a hotel or homestay. Lachung's country charm will enhance your Sikkim holiday packages. Day 5: Yumthang Valley – Valley of Flowers After breakfast, go to Yumthang Valley, which is at an elevation of 11,800 feet and is famous for: Seasonal blooms of rhododendrons (spring) Hot water springs Snow-covered landscapes (winter) You can add Zero Point to your Sikkim trip package if you want. It is the last civilian station before the border with China (permit needed). Spend the night in Lachung again. Day 6: Lachung to Gangtok – Leisure Evening Go back to Gangtok by car. On the way, take breaks to eat snacks and look at the views. Shop for handmade goods in the evening after you check into your hotel. This is an essential part of many Sikkim tour packages. Day 7: Departure with Memories Go to Bagdogra/NJP for your trip home after breakfast and check out. Your beautiful package trip to Sikkim is now over. You could add places like Pelling, Namchi, or Ravangla for a longer Sikkim trip package. You can change your Sikkim tour plan to fit your needs, hobbies, and way of travelling.
Sikkim.Essential Information for Sikkim Tour: To get the most out of your package trip to Sikkim, it's important to know the essential travel information before making your reservation. To help you plan, here’s all the information you need.
Sikkim.Best Time to Visit Sikkim: Your Sikkim trip package will be better if you choose the right season. Spring (March to May): Great for shooters and people who love nature. This is the best time to book Sikkim packages because rhododendrons are in full bloom in the hills. Autumn (October to mid-December): This is an excellent time for family or honeymoon-based Sikkim travel packages because of the clear air and beautiful views of Kanchenjunga. Winter (December to February): People who love the thrill and cold should go to North Sikkim when it snows. Snow-viewing trips to Lachung and Lachen are now included in many Sikkim holiday packages. Monsoon (June to September): Landslides make this area unsafe for long trips. This is not a good time for most Sikkim tour plans. Because of high demand, book your Sikkim tours and packages at least one to two months in advance in the spring or fall.
Sikkim.How to Reach Sikkim?: Every Sikkim trip package starts at one of these main entry points: By Air: The closest major airport is Bagdogra Airport (IXB), which is about 125 km from Gangtok. You can get to Gangtok by helicopter, depending on the weather. By Train: New Jalpaiguri (NJP) is the closest train stop in Siliguri. Taxis and sharing jeeps can take you to Gangtok from there. Most Sikkim tour packages include this. By Road: Sikkim is easy to get to from Siliguri and Darjeeling. Every package trip to Sikkim includes a beautiful drive along well-kept roads. There will be easier access to some Sikkim tours and travels after the upcoming growth of Pakyong Airport, which could be suitable for travellers in the future.
Sikkim.Permits and Regulations: Many places are open, but some places on Sikkim tour packages need special permissions: Restricted Zones: Nathula Pass, Yumthang Valley, Zero Point, and Gurudongmar Lake. Domestic Tourists: Domestic travellers need to get Inner Line Permits (ILP), which will be handled during your trip to Sikkim package company. Foreign Nationals: Need a Protected Area Permit (PAP) and can only go to certain North Sikkim places. Thomas Cook handles all the paperwork for your Sikkim holiday packages, so you don't have to worry about it.
Sikkim.Health and Safety Tips: For a Sikkim tour to go well, health and safety are essential. Altitude Awareness: High places like Gurudongmar Lake (17,800 ft) can make you feel a little sick. You should include slow acclimatisation in your Sikkim travel packages. First Aid Kit: For high altitudes, you should bring special drugs, motion sickness pills, and your own medications. Travel Insurance: Strongly suggested, especially for Sikkim packages with lots of excitement. It's smart to have coverage for trip delays and emergency evacuations. Connectivity: In high-altitude places like Lachen, network service isn't excellent. Let your Sikkim tours and travels company know about any connection needs ahead of time. During your Sikkim trip, remember to stay hydrated, avoid drinking alcohol at high levels, and follow local traditions.
Sikkim.Local Cuisine and Dining in Sikkim: The exceptional food experience that comes with every Sikkim tour plan is one of the best parts of them all. Tasting the local specialities is integral to any package trip to Sikkim, whether you're a foodie or just like to travel. What you can expect:
Sikkim.Traditional Sikkimese Dishes: Sikkim's cuisine is a beautiful blend of Nepali, Tibetan, and indigenous influences. Here are some must-try dishes included in most Sikkim vacation packages: Momos: Steamed dumplings filled with vegetables or meat and spicy chutney. A staple in every Sikkim trip package. Thukpa: A hearty noodle soup with vegetables or chicken, perfect for chilly evenings on your trip to Sikkim package. Phagshapa: Pork cooked with radish and dry chillies; often featured in homestay meals on rural Sikkim tours and packages. Gundruk and Sinki Soup: Fermented leafy greens made into a tangy soup, loved by locals and often introduced in cultural Sikkim holiday packages. Sel Roti: A ring-shaped sweet fried bread, enjoyed during festivals and included in many Sikkim travel packages. Chhurpi: Local cheese made from yak milk, available in both hard and soft forms. Exploring these dishes adds flavour to your overall Sikkim tour experience.
Sikkim.Where to Eat in Sikkim?: Dining in Sikkim ranges from vibrant cafes to cosy village kitchens. Your Sikkim tour plan may include meals at: Cafes in Gangtok: Places like Café Live & Loud and Taste of Tibet offer ambience with authentic food. Popular in most urban Sikkim tours and travels. Hotel Restaurants: Many premium Sikkim packages include buffet meals with Indian and continental options. Local Dhaba-Style Eateries: Try roadside eateries for authentic tastes during your Sikkim trip road journeys. Homestays in North Sikkim: Many a Sikkim trip package offers homestays with traditional home-cooked meals. A well-planned Sikkim trip package always includes time for tasting local flavours, making your journey not just scenic, but delicious too.
Sikkim.Things to Do in Sikkim Beyond Sightseein: Many Sikkim tour packages include visits to famous views and temples. However, the state has a lot more to offer than just sightseeing. A well-planned package trip to Sikkim includes exciting adventures, culturally immersive activities, and shopping trips that put you in touch with the locals. Adventure Activities Our unique Sikkim trip package has a lot to offer people who like to go on adventures: Trekking Trails: Treks like Dzongri and Goechala are well-known in Sikkim. These are usually part of advanced-level Sikkim vacation packages for hikers with a lot of experience. It's fantastic to go trekking in Kanchenjunga National Park. Paragliding in Gangtok: Fly high above plains and mountains for a wide view. Many adventure-focused Sikkim travel packages include paragliding. River Rafting in Teesta: The Teesta River offers thrilling white-water rafting, which is often included as an experience in some Sikkim tours and packages. Mountain Biking: Many off-road tracks in West and North Sikkim are great for people who like to be scared. Bicycle rides are included in some Sikkim packages. If you do these things, you'll have a great time on your Sikkim tour.
Sikkim.Cultural Experiences: Your Sikkim holiday packages will be more meaningful if you include culture immersion: Monastery Stays: Staying the night in a Buddhist monastery is a great way to find peace and meditation. These are a part of unique Sikkim tours and travels. Festival Participation: You can see masked dances, rituals, and feasts during big festivals like Losar (Tibetan New Year) or Pang Lhabsol when you book your Sikkim trip package. Traditional Crafts Workshops: Learn how to paint thangkas or try your hand at making carpets. These activities are now part of a lot of Sikkim tour packages. Travellers who learn about other cultures make stronger links and tell stories that last a lifetime.
Sikkim.Shopping in Sikkim: You can't just take a package trip to Sikkim without checking out the local markets: MG Marg, Gangtok: A shopping heaven with natural goods, woollen clothes, and crafts made by hand. You can spend some free time here on most Sikkim packages. Local Handicraft Centres: By customising it into your Sikkim travel packages, you should go to the Directorate of Handicrafts & Handloom. Village Markets in North Sikkim: Often, you can find handpicked Sikkim tours and packages where you can buy one-of-a-kind local items to take home as gifts. When you participate in these unusual activities, your Sikkim trip will be complete with fun, from art to adventure.

faq.question: What is the best time to visit Nainital?
faq.answer: Nainital is ethereal; the frozen lakes and snow-capped slopes of this hill town draw tourists practically all year round with their timeless beauty. However, the summer/spring season, which runs from March to June, is the best time to visit Nainital.

faq.question: Which are some of the famous places to visit in Nainital?
faq.answer: Nainital is a place that has an abundance of natural beauty to offer. Naini Lake, Tiffin top, Nainital zoo, Bhimtal Lake are some of the famous and best places to visit in Nainital with family.

faq.question: Is going to Nainital safe for females?
faq.answer: The most secure location for women in Uttaranchal is Nainital. You won't encounter any issues if you decide to take a trip to Nainital alone. Nainital has excellent train and bus transportation options. Even AC buses with sleeping seats that travel as far as Nainital are available. So, in essence, you travel without hassle.

faq.answer: There are several ways to get around in Nainital during a Nainital tour, including local taxis, buses, and rented cars or bikes. You can also take a scenic walk around Naini Lake and explore the town's charming shops and cafes. Boat rides on the lake are also a popular mode of transport and offer beautiful views of the surrounding hills.

faq.question: What are some good areas to stay in Nainital for tourists?
faq.answer: There are several good areas to stay in Nainital for tourists, such as Mall Road, which is in the heart of the town and offers a range of accommodations and dining options. Many Nainital tour packages offer accommodations in these areas or in nearby villages, to provide easy access to the town's top attractions.

faq.question: What are some popular shopping areas in Nainital?
faq.answer: Nainital offers a range of shopping options, including the popular Mall Road, which has a variety of shops selling clothes, souvenirs, and handicrafts. Other popular shopping areas in Nainital include Tibetan Market, Bhotia Market, and Bara Bazaar. Many Nainital tour packages include visits to these shopping areas, allowing tourists to explore the local culture and take home some unique souvenirs.

faq.question: What are the best souvenirs to buy from Nainital?
faq.answer: Some of the best souvenirs to buy from Nainital during a Nainital tour include handmade candles, locally grown tea, woolen shawls, handcrafted wooden items, and colorful bangles. The town is also known for its intricate woolen embroidery, which can be found on a variety of clothing items, such as shawls and sweaters.

faq.question: Which is the popular festival celebrated in Nainital?
faq.answer: The most popular festival in Nainital is Phooldei. It is celebrated on the first day of Chaitra month (March) according to the Hindu calendar.

faq.question: Which is the main airport nearest to Nainital?
faq.answer: The Pantnagar Airport, is the nearest airport to Nainital, which is located about 70 km away and can be reached by a two-hour drive.

faq.question: Which is the worst time of year to plan a tour to Nainital?
faq.answer: Monsoon in Nainital can be brutal due to the excessive rainfall. From the months of July to September, the downpours are unpredictable and sometimes last all day, making it impossible to explore the beauty of this hillside destination. The heavy rainfall can also cause trees to fall or waterlog the roads, making it impossible to travel to and from Nainital.

faq.question: Is it safe to book Nainital holiday packages for a solo trip?
faq.answer: The crime rate in Nainital is very low, which makes it one of the safest places for solo trips. Even for women travelling alone, it is quite safe. The local law enforcement organisations as well as the residents, adhere to the strict laws of Nainital, resulting in upholding the highest standards of safety and security for men, women, and children. They are particularly careful with tourists as tourism is one of the major contributors to their economy. You can rest assured that the welcoming hospitality and immense level of security will be maintained during your Nainital travel packages.

faq.question: Is there snowfall in Nainital?
faq.answer: Even as a hill station, Nainital is a subtropical location where the temperature in winter can dip very low. This combination of subtropical climate combined with higher altitude results in snowfall in Nainital every couple of years. The snowfall is a bit unpredictable until the beginning of winter, however, you can rest assured to meet hail storms and heavy rainfall that just as easily mimics the climatic conditions associated with snow.

faq.question: What makes Nainital tours so special?
faq.answer: Nainital is an enigmatic land of nature and scenic beauty. The plantations rolling down the hills and the exquisite waterfalls are alluring to tourists from all over the world. But the most important thing that makes Nainital so special is the Naina Devi temple situated alongside the Naini Lake. Tourists and locals come to pay homage to the temple, some of who walk for miles to pay tribute to the idol.

faq.question: Is there a dress code for a trip to Nainital?
faq.answer: There is no set dress code for Nainital. However, the state government does recommend packing the following items to keep you comfortable and healthy during your Nainital holiday packages:

faq.question: Is alcohol served in Nainital?
faq.answer: Nainital has a thriving nightlife with a high consumption of alcohol thanks to the cold weather. There are several pubs, clubs, and restaurants that serve alcohol. You can even have alcohol delivered to your room of accommodation or purchase it at the bars. As long as you do not threaten the safety of the locals and the tourists after drinking, alcohol is absolutely allowed in Nainital.

faq.question: Can I swim in Nainital Lake?
faq.answer: The Nainital Lake is a tourist attraction for boating and gazing on the horizon of the tranquil water. The liquid flows into the lake from the waterfall, and the highest standard is maintained to ensure it remains clean and hygienic. Therefore, bathing in the waterfall or swimming in Nainital Lake is forbidden.

faq.question: Can I spend the night at Jim Corbett National Park during my Nainital trip packages?
faq.answer: Yes, you can absolutely spend the night at the Jim Corbett National Park during your Nainital holiday packages. There are accommodation facilities available in the core of the wilderness. You will require a permit to do so. Hence you need to plan ahead and preferably discuss it with your expert travel advisor at Thomas Cook. This facility is only available from winter to summer, which is roughly from the month of November to June.

Nainital.Introduction: Nainital tour packages by Thomas Cook are a great way to ensure an unforgettable holiday experience for you and your loved ones. Nestled at an elevation of 2,084 metres in the Kumaon division of Uttarakhand, Nainital is one of the most charming hill stations in India. Formerly the summer capital of the United Provinces, this majestic town lies on the Kumaon foothills of the outer Himalayas, roughly 280 km from Dehradun. Some of the things that make this town a worthy holiday destination are its quaint vibes and picturesque natural beauty, consisting of serene lakes, lush greenery, a year-round pleasant climate and mighty forested hills. The vibrant culture and rich colonial history of the region add a unique flavour to the Nainital tour experience, piquing the interest of tourists from all corners of India. For travel enthusiasts in North India, it is the perfect holiday getaway amidst the mountains, especially during summer. From revered temples, like Naina Devi Temple and Hanuman Garhi, to jaw-dropping viewpoints, like Snow View Point and Tiffin Top, there’s a lot to uncover while on a Nainital trip. Moreover, Nainital caters to the diverse needs of thrillseekers, offering fun adventures like camping, paragliding, trekking, rock climbing and so on. If you are thinking about experiencing all of these on your next vacation, consider opting for the Nainital tour packages by Thomas Cook. We have all kinds of Nainital tour packages to give your holidays the ideal treatment, including themed vacations for families, adventurers and newlyweds. Our Nainital tour packages feature interesting itineraries covering popular tourist places and activities available in the destination. Not only this but our Nainital trip packages are equipped with useful features, such as sightseeing tours, hotels, meals, choice of optional flights and so on. You can find Nainital trip packages on Thomas Cook for both budget-friendly and luxury holidays, starting from only INR 22,700. So, leave the planning of your Nainital tour to us and focus on making the most out of your holidays with our popular Nainital trip packages.

Nainital.Best places to visit in Nainital: Our Nainital trip packages are equipped with well-planned itineraries, featuring several popular tourist sites in the region. There are plenty of wonderful places to visit in Nainital on a trip, such as, Naini Lake: Nestled at the centre of the hill town, the Naini Lake is the gem of Nainital tourism. Surrounded by mighty green hills, it is a stunning, crescent-shaped natural freshwater lake where tourists can witness some of the best scenic views in the region. The lake, which is a common inclusion of Nainital trip packages, offers fun boating activities and is a great site for picnics. For boating, make sure to buy tickets directly from the counter. Also, choose weekdays to avoid large crowds and enjoy the scenic beauty in peace. Book Nainital holiday packages featuring Naini Lake visit on Thomas Cook. Snow View Point: It is a well-known viewpoint in the region, nestled at an elevation of 2270 metres. It offers jaw-dropping panoramic views of the snow-crowned peaks of Nanda Devi, Nanda Kot and Trishul. It is among the most beautiful places to see in Nainital. Find Nainital travel packages featuring this site on Thomas Cook. You can rent a binocular for INR 20-50 for a clearer view of the peaks. Mall Road: The Mall Road in Nainital is adjacent to the Naini Lake, linking Mallital and Tallital. It is a charming hotspot, filled with locals and tourists. There are many shops spread across the road, from well-known restaurants to clothing stores. One of the best things to do on a Nainital tour is to take an early morning or evening stroll along this road. It is also a common feature in most Nainital holiday packages. Eco Cave Park: It is a cluster of natural rocky caves that were turned into a garden. At this spot, you can find six caves formed in the shape of different animals, like a bat, flying fox, panther, tiger, etc. The park also has a musical fountain with visual and audio effects. It is one of the most noteworthy Nainital tourist attractions for family visits. The charge for adults is INR60/person, for children is INR25/person and a camera is INR25. The Nainital travel packages by Thomas Cook feature popular sites like the Eco Cave Park. St. John Wilderness Church: Located on a hilltop, this historic church boasts a stunning Neo-Gothic style of architecture, with surrounding dense deodar and pine trees to complement its beauty. It is one of the most popular Nainital tourist places that you can visit. You can look for Nainital travel packages featuring similar tourist attractions like this one on our website.
Nainital.Hill stations to visit near Nainital: Bhowali: Bhowali, around 13 km from Nainital, is a well-known hill station you can visit on your Nainital tour. Though small in size, it is known for its natural beauty, tranquil charm and off-beat vibes. Tourist attractions in and near Bhowali are Shyamkhet Tea Gardens, Sattal, Ramgarh, etc. Book Nainital packages online in just a few clicks on Thomas Cook. Bhimtal: Bhimtal is among the most noteworthy hill stations near Nainital. It is only 25 km from the City of Lakes but remains free from heavy tourist crowds. The main attraction of this hill town is the majestic Bhimtal Lake, where tourists can enjoy boating and scenic views of nature. Some customisable Nainital travel packages may allow you to add a short stay in Bhimtal. Mukteshwar: The breathtaking beauty of Mukteshwar often draws tourists to visit Nainital for a short stay. This enchanting village is around 49 km from Nainital and offers remarkable panoramic views of the Himalayan ranges. It is also a major hub for adventures and home to a 350-year-old Lord Shiva shrine called Mukteshwar Dham Temple. Looking for Nainital travel packages featuring a stay in Mukteshwar? Check out our page.
Nainital.The best time to visit Nainital: Although you can find Nainital tour packages for any month, the trip experience may differ depending on the season. So, make sure to decide the time of your visit beforehand. Here’s what you need to know about the best time to visit Nainital; Summer in Nainital Summer in Nainital ranges from March to June. The hill station receives the most number of tourists within this period, thanks to its calm and clear weather conditions. You can expect an average temperature range of 12-28°C. It is the best time for a Nainital tour if you are looking to explore all its attractions and engage in fun activities. Tourists from neighbouring areas flock to Nainital to escape from the summer heat, so expect a high tourist crowd and price hikes. For attractive deals on Nainital travel packages for summer, head over to Thomas Cook. Due to it being a peak travel season, make sure to book accommodation, flights, Nainital travel packages, etc., in advance. Monsoon in Nainital Monsoon in Nainital extends from July to September, bringing a significant amount of rainfall to the region. Due to its hilly topography, the heavy rainfall may affect tourism, as it increases the risks of slippery roads, landslides and road blockage. However, it can be a fantastic time for a budget-oriented Nainital travel plan, just be sure to keep up with the latest weather updates before stepping out. Nainital travel packages for these months are easily available on our website. Winter in Nainital If you are not bothered by the cold and are interested in witnessing the majestic snow-covered landscapes, then winter can be the best time to visit Nainital for you. With an average temperature range of 0-15°C, this season covers the months of October to February. Looking for affordable Nainital packages? Check out our website. Additionally, tourists planning a Nainital tour during this season can also be part of the local festivals and events, such as the Nainital Mahotsav. Enjoy the divine natural beauty of the region with Nainital travel packages for winter by Thomas Cook. Nainital.How to reach Nainital?: When booking Nainital travel packages, keep your preferred mode of transport in mind. For your Nainital trip, you can consider travelling by one of these modes; By train:The closest railway station to Nainital is Kathgodam Railway Station, roughly 36 km away from the City of Lakes. It has good connectivity to major cities in India, including Lucknow, New Delhi, Agra, Kolkata, etc. After reaching Kathgodam, one can take a bus or taxi to Nainital. The KATHGODAM SHTBDI (12040) that runs daily between New Delhi and Kathgodam takes around 5 hours and 35 minutes to complete the journey. By road: Travelling to Nainital by road is a popular choice, especially for tourists from other parts of Uttarakhand or neighbouring states. One can travel by car, taxi or state-run or private bus. If you are travelling from New Delhi to Nainital, you can take NH109 and then NH9. By air Located 70 km away, the Pantnagar Airport is the nearest airport to Nainital. It is connected to important cities in India via frequent domestic flights by airlines, such as IndiGo. A direct flight from New Delhi to Pantnagar takes around an hour to complete. Most Nainital tour packages come with the choice of optional flights. You can add the option of flights on applicable Nainital packages during further booking steps. After landing at the airport, you can take a bus to taxi to Nainital, which takes about 2-2.5 hours.
Nainital.What to eat in Nainital?: Get in sync with Nainital’s culture by trying out regional delicacies, such as; Bhatt ki Churkani: It is a traditional Kumaoni dish made using black soybeans, cooked in a slightly tangy and spicy gravy. It is a delicious, protein-rich dish that is popularly eaten with roti or steamed rice. You can try this Nainital food item at Purohit Restaurant in Nainital. Ras: Ras is another regional delicacy that you can try on your Nainital trip. It is a mix of lentils, seasoned with ginger, oil, and spices and slow-cooked in an iron pan. Besides being tasty, it also offers great nutritional value, exactly what you need after a long day of sightseeing. You can try this dish at Shiva Restaurant. Besides accommodation and sightseeing, meals are also included in our Nainital packages. Aloo ke Gutke: It is a vegetarian delight, made using boiled potatoes cooked in mustard oil alongside jakhya, turmeric and red chilli. The savoury flavour of this Nainital food item is best enjoyed with flatbreads. Head over to Soni Restaurant on your Nainital trip for some delicious Aloo ke Gutke. Singodi: If you have a sweet tooth, you must give Singodi a try on your Nainital trip. It is prepared by blending sugar, coconut and roasted milk solids, which are then wrapped in Malu leaf. You can try this dessert at any sweet shop in the hill station.
Nainital.Restaurants to visit in Nainital: Looking for nice places to eat on your Nainital tour? Here are some popular Nainital restaurants you can consider; Embassy Restaurant: The vintage interior, paired with a mouthwatering range of dishes, makes it a prime spot for dining with family and friends in Nainital. Similar to choosing the best place to eat, it is crucial to book the correct planned trip. Browse from our wide list of Nainital packages and pick a plan that resonates with your dream vacation the most.Must-try: Butter Chicken, Veg Manchurian, Embassy Special Chicken Fusion Platter, etc.Address: The Mall, Mall Rd, Mallital, Nainital, Uttarakhand Sher-e-Punjab: Sher-e-Punjab is one of the best places in Nainital if you are looking for some authentic Punjabi cuisine. The lively ambience of the place adds to the dining experience at this restaurant.Must-try: Dal Makhani, Lassi, Amritsari Kulcha, etc.Address: Mall Rd, Tallital, Nainital, Uttarakhand Boat House Club: It is one of the oldest and most reputed Nainital restaurants that you can visit. Eating at this elegant restaurant while soaking in the beautiful lakeside views makes for a great dining experience.Must-try: Kebabs, Grilled Fish, Cheese Platter, etc.Address: near Rickshaw Stand, Mallital, Nainital, Uttarakhand Machan Restaurant: Another amazing restaurant that you can consider visiting on your Nainital tour is Machan Restaurant. The place serves Chinese, North Indian and Continental cuisine and is known for quality of food, service and beautiful views.Must-try: Pizza Alla Napoletana, Chicken Shanghai, Rajma Masala, etc.Address: 9FR4+CQR, Mall Rd, Mallital, Nainital, Uttarakhand
Nainital.Best Honeymoon Places in Nainital: If you are going with this hill station for your honeymoon, make sure to add these romantic spots to the Nainital tour itinerary; Tiffin Top Tiffin Top is by far one of the top places to visit in Nainital to witness the regional scenic beauty. Also known as Dorothy’s Seat, newlyweds love to take a hike to this spot and enjoy a romantic picnic while enjoying the view of the entire town and the surrounding hills. It is a fun activity that honeymooners can indulge in on their Nainital trip. It is commonly featured in honeymoon special Nainital packages. Roundtrip pony rides for this location are available and usually range between INR 500-700/person. Lover’s Point Just a few minutes from Eco Cave Garden, there’s a well-known spot for couples called the Lover’s Point. It is a popular viewpoint in the region, offering breathtaking panoramic views of the surrounding area. Couples can enjoy sunrise and sunset from this point as well as opt for horseback rides to Naina Peak, Tiffin Top, etc. This site is featured in popular honeymoon special Nainital packages. Naini Lake Apart from being a major crowd-puller in Nainital, the Naini Lake is also a wonderful spot for couples seeking picturesque views and a serene ambience. Going for a romantic boat ride on this lake while soaking in the scenic beauty is an unforgettable experience. Additionally, newlyweds can go for fun picnics by the lakeside or take romantic strolls along the Mall Road. Naini Lake is a common feature of most popular Nainital tour packages.
Nainital.Shopping in Nainital: Want to go on a shopping spree on your Nainital tour? Here are some notable places you can check out; Mall Road: When talking about shopping, Mall Road is one of the most notable Nainital places to visit. It is the most popular place for gift and souvenir shopping and remains filled by tourists and locals alike. From quaint cafes and posh restaurants to clothing stores and handicraft shops, you can find a diverse range of products here. What to buy: Woollen shawls and cardigans, handcrafted candles, etc. Bhotia Bazaar: Bhotia Bazaar is another great spot for some fun and casual shopping experiences in Nainital. It has plenty of shops selling all kinds of products, from edible goods to clothing wear. You can even find good quality homemade chutney and dry fruits at this market. The well-planned itineraries of our Nainital packages will give you plenty of opportunities to indulge in fun shopping extravaganzas during the trip.What to buy: Homemade pickles, marmalade, woollen clothes, etc. Tibetan Market: The Tibetan Market is a place where you can buy clothes, accessories, handbags, wristwatches, shoes and many other things on your Nainital trip. The shops here have a vibrant collection of items. Make sure to use your bargaining skills to get good deals on products. Book Nainital packages online in just a few clicks on Thomas Cook's website or mobile app.What to buy: Mufflers, trinkets, ethnic dresses, shawls, etc.
Nainital.Things to do in Nainital: To make the most out of your Nainital trip, make sure to add some fun activities to the Nainital itinerary, such as; Boating at Naini Lake One of the best things to do in Nainital is to go boating at Naini Lake. It is among the most fun and fulfilling things to do while on a Nainital trip, especially for couples seeking romantic activities. Boating is available from 6 AM to 6 PM every day, starting from INR 160/person. Look for Nainital trip packages featuring this activity on our page. Go on the Nainital Ropeway ride Make sure to add the famous Nainital Ropeway ride to your Nainital trip plan. It links Snow View to Mallital and offers alluring views of the town, lake and surrounding hills from a bird’s vantage point. The charge for Aerial Ropeway Nainital is INR300/adult for both ways. Visit Nainital Zoo For a fun family picnic experience on your Nainital trip, you can visit the Nainital Zoo. Also called Pt. Govind Ballabh Pant High Altitude Zoo, it is known for housing a diverse range of animals, including Tibetan wolves, Sambar deer, leopards, etc. Note that there is no entry fee for differently-abled and senior citizens at the zoo.
Nainital.Why should you book Nainital Tour Packag: Here are some reasons why picking Nainital holiday packages from Thomas Cook can benefit you; Easy online booking: Thomas Cook makes browsing, comparing and booking Nainital tour packages online easy via their user-friendly website and mobile app. Budget-oriented tours: You can find Nainital holiday packages for both budget-oriented and luxury holidays. Our Nainital trip packages start from only INR 22,700. Themed vacations: Whether it's a romantic honeymoon or a family trip, find Nainital packages on Thomas Cook with itineraries designed to match your intended holiday theme. EMI option and other offers: We offer exciting offers and effective discounts with our Nainital trip packages. Most of our plans also come with EMI payment options to make holidays more affordable. Useful features: Not just accommodation but our Nainital trip packages feature meals, sightseeing tours and fun activities as standard. You also get the choice of picking optional flights with the majority of our planned trips.

Amsterdam.Interesting Things to do in Amsterdam: Amsterdam is a city that offers a rich array of experiences for every traveller. From world-renowned museums and historic sites to picturesque parks and vibrant districts, Amsterdam's best places to visit ensure a memorable trip. Here are some interesting things to do in Amsterdam that you should include in your Amsterdam trip or look for in Amsterdam packages. Canal Cruise A Canal Cruise to explore the city is something you must not miss out on in your Amsterdam trip. It takes you through the city, offering you a unique perspective. Along the way, you can learn a great deal about the local culture and its centuries-old traditions. An approximately 90-minute ride on a canal cruise is enough to take you to hidden gems of the city that you would possibly miss otherwise. A Day at Museums An Amsterdam tour is incomplete without visiting the city’s iconic museums that provide an experience like no other. Rich in history, culture, tradition and art, museums in the town are truly mesmerising. From the Rijksmuseum to the iconic Van Gogh Museum, there are many options to explore in your next Amsterdam trip. Strolling Around the City If one has to define a good Amsterdam tour, it would never be complete without talking about the peaceful strolls around the city. A morning or evening stroll through Vondelpark is just the right way for a relaxing day. Wrap up your picnic and head to discover the famous Jordan city known for its local markets, street food and beautiful bohemian ambience. A Fun Night Out Planning for a fun night out on your next Amsterdam trip? The Red Light District is your answer. The district is home to the most iconic party places and cultural events. The happening and lively atmosphere gets you in a groovy mood right when you enter.
Amsterdam.Interesting Facts about the Amsterdam pl: Here are ten interesting facts about the city that you should know before planning your Amsterdam trip Amsterdam is known for being a bike-friendly city. There are around 1.2 million bikes in the city and around 821,000 residents. Amsterdam is a city with possibly the most canals in the world. There are over 165 canals that allow travellers to cover around 100 kilometres on the water. The famous Heineken beer brand originated in Amsterdam in the year 1864. On your Amsterdam trip make sure to take the Heineken experience. Amsterdam is home to the most iconic museums and the highest concentration of museums per square metre. Your Amsterdam package takes you to iconic museums. The Amsterdam floating flower market was built in 1862, and it is the only floating flower market in the world. So, make sure that your Amsterdam trip takes you to this market. The smallest house in Europe was built in 1738 in Amsterdam. The house has a measurement of 2.02 meters in width and only 5 meters in depth. In the 17th century, Amsterdam became the centre of the tulip mania, which was so valuable that it was almost used as a currency. The Anne Frank house is a part of almost every Amsterdam tour as it is one of the most significant historic sites in the city. Amsterdam is home to nationals of over 180 different countries, making it a highly multicultural city. The Amsterdam coffee shop is not only known for its unique brews but also for selling cannabis products, making it an integral part of the Amsterdam trip for many.
