Mussoorie.About Mussoorie Holiday: Tucked up in the Himalayas, 290 km away from Delhi, Mussoorie welcomes you to discover its many attractions and breathtaking scenery. The village is charming since it is uncomplicated and has a natural appeal without any over-the-top details. Mussoorie travel plan provides an uncomplicated yet enthralling experience that lets you engage with the local way of life and environment without breaking the bank. This place called the “ Queen of Hills” is located in the Dehradun District of Uttarakhand and stands at 7000 feet above sea level, with a backdrop of Shivalik Range and Doon Valley. Thomas Cook’s Mussoorie travel packages take you on a journey where history and nature merge. In this article, we will help you to decide on the places to visit, modes of transport to reach, the local delicacies to enjoy, and exciting things to do, to make your Mussoorie trip one of a kind journey. A must-see on your Mussoorie travel is the scenic Camel's Back Road, so named because of a rock structure that resembles a camel's hump. You can wander along this winding trail and enjoy the expansive views of the Doon Valley and the Shivalik Range. At Mussoorie's second highest point, Gun Hill, you can enjoy stunning views from the cable car ropeway of the quaint city and its surrounding mountain ranges. Since it was the British Summer Capital during colonial rule, you can see a distinct impact of colonial influence on the architecture of hotels and churches. Your Mussoorie tour will be incomplete without a visit to Mall Road which still gives a colonial vibe. You can book Mussoorie trip packages from major cities like Delhi, Mumbai, Chennai, Bangalore, and Kolkata as per your departure preference. The Mussoorie packages can either be exclusive or can be a part of Uttarakhand or Jim Corbett tours. Hill stations of Landour, Barlowganj, and Jharipani around together form the greater Mussoorie.
Mussoorie.Best Time to Visit Mussoorie: Since Mussoorie is charming all year round, the ideal time for a Mussoorie tour usually depends on the kind of experience you are looking for. The best time to visit Mussoorie is between April and June and between September and November. Summer (March - June): Temperature: Mussoorie experiences considerably cool temperatures during the summer, which range from 15°C to 25°C.With comfortable day temperatures and cooler evenings, this season is just perfect for a Mussoorie trip. No wonder this is regarded as the busiest travel period. Attractions: This is the perfect time to visit outdoor places like Gun Hill, Camel's Back Road, and Kempty Falls. Due to the melting of mountain snow, the streams and waterfalls can be seen at full flow. Monsoon (July to August) Temperature: During the monsoon, Mussoorie receives moderate to heavy rainfall and the daily temperature ranges from 15°C to 20°C. The slippery roads and frequent landslides may make your commute difficult and dangerous for Mussoorie travel. Attractions: Although the rain makes everything greener, the weather may limit some outdoor activities. However, this may be an excellent time to visit if you appreciate the misty view and stunning greeneries. Autumn(September to November) Temperature: After the monsoon, there is a steady decrease in rainfall and temperature that ranges from 10°C to 20°C. This season is there for a short time only. Attractions: This time of year is regarded as one of the greatest for a short Mussoorie tour. The scenery is beautiful, and the temperature is perfect for a serene getaway. This is a great time to go trekking and explore the natural beauty of the town and surrounding hills. Winter (December to February ) Temperature: Mussoorie experiences bitterly cold winters, with temperatures as lows of just 1°C with sub-zero temperatures at night. Snowfall is a definite possibility and gives the landscape a very different look. It is usually heavier in December time. Attractions: Winter is the best season to visit Mussoorie if you want to experience snowfall. This time of year, the town gets into a festive mood as Christmas arrives. When it's snowing, the Mall Road and Landour are very charming. The ideal time to visit Mussoorie would depend on your travel choices. Choose Mussoorie vacation packages keeping the various seasons and their beauties in mind to have the best trip in your life.
Mussoorie.How to reach Mussoorie?: Though it is a tourist-centric area, there is no direct flight to reach Mussoorie fast. Thomas Cook Mussoorie's travel plan will give you options on how to reach this hill station in the best possible way. By Flight The nearest airport to Mussoorie is Jolly Grant Airport in Dehradun, almost 60km away. There are some direct flights from major cities like Delhi, Mumbai, Pune, Bengaluru, Kolkata, and Chennai. However, this is a Domestic airport and the nearest international one is Indira Gandhi International Airport in New Delhi. The domestic airport in Dehradun is connected to Mussoorie via Rishikesh-Dehradun State Highway. You can hire a taxi or Uttarakhand State Road Transport Buses from the airport gate. your Mussoorie itinerary can be tweaked depending on the way you want to reach your destination. By Train Mussoorie tourism is extensively supported by Dehradun railway station. The major station connecting the rest of India to Dehradun is New Delhi Railway Station which has direct trains to Dehradun. Travellers from other cities like Kolkata, Chennai, Bangalore, and Mumbai can reach Dehradun via New Delhi. There are ample trains that run daily like Haridwar-Dehradun Express, Jan Shatabdi Express, Doon Express etc. From Dehradun station, you will find taxis and State buses commuting to Mussoorie. By Road No matter where you come from, your tour to Mussoorie is bound to start with a picturesque road trip. You can take a cab from Delhi, Dehradun, Haridwar, Tehri or Rishikesh or take Uttarakhand State Transport Corporation buses from ISBT Kashmiri Gate in Delhi for a cheaper commute. A paradise for travel photographers the scenic route is bound to captivate your fancy. Tour of Mussoorie is 6 hours from Delhi, 8 hours from Agra and 10 hours from Jaipur. Local rickshaws and taxis are easier and more flexible ways to get around Mussoorie. Your Mussoorie tour guide will be able to advise you on local transport depending on your accommodation and the places you plan to visit.
Mussoorie.Places to Visit in Mussoorie: These are some of the best places to visit in Mussoorie while on vacation. Each has its amazing feature which makes it stand apart. Kempty Falls: Located on the way to Mussoorie from Dehradun it is at a height of 4500 feet above sea level. Falling from such height it breaks into 5 small springs on its way down. It is a well-known picnic spot found during colonial rule by a British officer John Makinen. Start your Mussoorie tour with a fun family picnic on the way. Lal Tibba Considered to be one of the best tourist places in Mussoorie the name literally translates to Red Hill. The name comes from the red-coloured sky which becomes evident during sunrise and sunset. The telescope installed by the Mussoorie municipality is a unique one as you can view the the snowcapped mountains of Banderpunch, Kedarnath and Badrinath. Clouds End For Instagrammable pictures, you can head on to Clouds End which marks the end of Mussoorie. A 7 km ride from the main city this place is surrounded by beautiful Deodar and Oak forests and feels surreal when you go for a nature hike. You can count this as one of the most picturesque places in your Mussoorie itinerary. Gun Hill This is undoubtedly one of the most famous tourist places in Mussoorie, not only for its view but also for the history behind its name. You can not only get a stunning 360-degree view of the Mussoorie city, Doon Valley but also of the entire range of Himalayas including Bunderpunch, Srikantha, Gangotri, and Pithwara etc. After a 20-minute trek, you can reach Gun Point where a gun used to be placed and fired at noon to declare the time in the pre-independence era. Jharipani Falls This is one of the must-include places to visit in your Mussoorie trip packages. The 9 km distance can be covered by a bus or a car ride and then a short trek of 1.5km will take you to this amazing location amongst Shivalik Range. With wildflowers all around this is the perfect place to walk, relax and soak in nature's calmness and serenity. Mussoorie Lake This “A one” artificial lake has everything a family needs to enjoy a vacation. You will never regret to include this in your Mussoorie holiday packages. Other than paddle boating, it has zip lining to keep the active ones busy, souvenir shops to keep shopaholics busy and restaurants all around to keep the foodies in your family busy. George Everest House The Welsh geographer after whom the highest peak of the Himalayas, Mount Everest was named, lived here for thirty years. A place of great historical significance, it has Aglar River valley on one side and snow-capped Himalayas on the other. Due to its past and scenic beauty, this is considered one of the best places in Mussoorie for nature lovers and history buffs.
Mussoorie.Best Hill Stations near Mussoorie: Mussoorie is one of the top hill stations of Uttarakhand. However, you can take a day's trip to a few more hill stations around Mussoorie to explore further. You can add these places as extensions to your Mussoorie tour packages: Landour Hill Station Approximately 7 km away, your Mussoorie tour guide will be able to take you to this lovely hill station for an unforgettable sunrise and sunset view along with a mouth-watering meal at “Char Dukan”. Dhanaulti This trekker's paradise is just 2.5 hrs from Mussoorie. You can include the Ecopark visit and trekking to Thangdhar in your Mussoorie one day tour. Rishikesh Another spot that always beckons adventure junkies is Rishikesh as it's famous for river rafting, bungee jumping, flying fox etc. Make sure you customise Mussoorie travel packages to make enough time for these sports at Rishikesh. The Beatles Ashram here should also be mandatory on your bucket list. Haridwar Famous for its magnificent show during Ganga Aarti, this place is just 3 hours from Mussoorie. The tranquil Sapt Rishi Ashram is the place to be if you want to calm your mind. If you are a spiritual soul, do not miss out on this experience and curate your Mussoorie itinerary around it. Chakrata Another unknown and off-beat place which you can reach in 3 hours from Mussoorie. Other than Tiger Falls, skiing and trekking in Mundali or bird watching in Deoban, you can explore Budher caves here as well. Even in Mussoorie sightseeing, you will not find so much variety of natural wonders. Dehradun Since you will be going to Mussoorie via Dehradun, halt a day or two to experience nature while trekking up to Robert’s cave, having a picnic at Lachhiwala and shopping till you drop in Paltan Bazaar. It will be only a minimum addition to your Mussoorie trip cost, compared to what you will enjoy.
Mussoorie.Best Honeymoon Destination in Mussoorie: The perfect medley of nature, adventure and romance, Mussoorie has become one of the best Honeymoon destinations over the years. Thomas Cook Mussoorie vacation packages can be perfectly tailor-made for newlywed couples. The perfect places for couples to visit and enjoy activities together are here. Do take a look and decide to book Mussoorie tour packages exclusively with these places to visit in Mussoorie together. Dhanaulti You can stay in a Camp and enjoy the solitude away from the crowd. The astounding views of mountains and lush green valleys are worth your time together. Benog Wildlife Sanctuary Amongst other activities slated in your Mussoorie travel plan, also go for bird watching and trekking in this sanctuary. It is a special attraction for honeymooners as you two can explore diverse flora and fauna while enjoying perfect weather. Lake Mist This lake is formed by the waters of Kempty Falls and is one of the best places to see in Mussoorie, especially for romantic duos. The resort by this lake serves as one of the top romantic stays for newlyweds with both water and mountains as its backdrop. Mussoorie Lake If you like some secluded time with your partner, you are in for a treat at this place. The azure water beckons you for some romantic boating time and an excellent scenic background for clicking photos as well. You can also ask your Mussoorie tour guide to take you to some local food shops around this lake and enjoy mouthwatering delicacies. Lal Tibba Though might be crowded, being one of the top Mussoorie tourist attractions the view will make you forget the crowd. Through the Japanese telescope placed on the viewpoint, you can watch the Himalayan ranges of Badrinath and Kedarnath and how they turn bright red during sunset and sunrise.
Mussoorie.Interesting Things to do in Mussoorie: You may think that being a hill station restricts activities to just trekking and exploring the beauty of nature. But Mussoorie has much more to offer. Let us find out some of the interesting things to do in Mussoorie which will keep all age groups busy. Nature Walk Go for a walk in quieter places like Camel Back Road to spend time away from the bustling and crowded city. It is one of the most secluded places you will find in your Mussoorie tour. Exploring Mall Road Enjoy shopping with your family or your better half while exploring and walking along the Mall Road. From handicraft shops, and souvenir shops to small eateries and restaurants, you will find it all. No wonder it has become the busiest place to see in Mussoorie. Food tour If you are in love with your dim sums and Thukpas, your Mussoorie trip plan is unfinished without a food tour. Along with these Tibetan delights, you will also find interesting Garhwali cuisines like Aloo ki Gutke, Jhangora ki Kheer, Gul Gula, Singodi and various fruit juices. Winemaking: An unknown and one of the offbeat things to do in Mussoorie is vinification. Winemaking from apples, pears, ginger, plums and grapes is taught and within a month you can have some self-made bottles of wine. Fishing If patience is your virtue, you can go trout fishing in the Aglar and Yamuna rivers. Since this requires permission from the Divisional Forest Officer and is time-consuming, you might need to take out a considerable amount of time from your curated Mussoorie sightseeing package. Wild Life Safari For an avid animal lover, Mussoorie travel plan must have Jabarkhet and Benog wildlife sanctuary. These are great camping and trekking. While on safari you can spot Leopards, Himalayan bears, Mountain Quails, Langurs etc. Corn Village Sainji village is one of the unique hidden gems in Mussoorie tourism. The people of this village decorate their houses with bunches of corn. You can spend a day interacting with people and exploring their deep connection with nature.
Mussoorie.Adventurous things to do in Mussoorie: Though mainly known for its calm and serene nature, it can surprise you to find the many adventurous things you can do to make your Mussoorie holiday packages more exciting. Trekking and Hiking The obvious one to find in a hill station, the Mussoorie trip will offer various trails for scenic trekking like Hathipaon Trail, Naag Tibba Summit, Benog Hill Trek, Lal Tibba, Kaflani Day hike, Jharipani Waterfall Trek and many others. Some of the best places to hile that you can have in your Mussoorie tour itinerary are the Kempty waterfall hike, Happy Valley hike, Jabarkhet Nature Reserve hike, Nag Tibba etc. Adventure Sports Yes, you read it right. Adventure sports like Sky walking, Zip-lining and Zip swinging are also available for tourists. Be rest assured that it is safe to try these activities in Mussoorie Adventure Park one of the sought-after Mussoorie places to visit for adventure lovers. Zip swings are a combination of Zipline and Giant swings. Harnessed with wire rope you travel to the middle of the rope and then swing at a height of 80 feet. This is not meant for the faint-hearted. Rock Climbing and Rappelling The variety of rocks ranging from 50 to 600 feet offers options for climbers to choose from according to their strengths and fitness. The hidden caves at Buranskhanda near Dhanaulti are surrounded by green Alpine meadows and stunning views of the Himalayas. These are one of the few top places to visit in Mussoorie for a hardcore adrenaline junky. The rugged plains, steep slopes and thrill of wilderness give a new dimension to mountain exploration by biking and ATV riding. Mussoorie tour guide can help you book these activities if you want to have some out-of-the-box experience. Paragliding To maximise the opportunity of your Mussoorie travel, go for a joyride on a 2-seater glider operated by professional and experienced pilots. They will take you for a ride up to a height of 10,000 feet for almost an hour. Some well-known places for this activity are Mussoorie Lake, Jharipani Falls, Buranskhanda etc. Rafting On a half-day Mussoorie tour plan, you can raft along the Alaknanda, Dhauliganga and Kali rivers which have a few challenging rapids to experience. You can also opt for a rafting route from Barkot to Lakhamandal and Damta to Yamuna Bridge. Roller Skating Mussoorie has one the India's finest and largest skating rinks. You will find many such rinks in Jaypee Resort, The Rink in Kulri Bazaar, and Disco Skating Rink, to spend fun moments with your family. These activities might make your Mussoorie tour package price seem a little steeper but it will be worth every penny when you go back with such exciting memories back home.
Mussoorie.What to Eat in Mussoorie?: The food scene is much more than momos and soups in Mussoorie. The Mall Road is a paradise for Mussoorie street food and some famous cafes. The list below will help you decide on what to eat in Mussoorie. As a foodie, you will not want to miss out on anything in your Mussoorie trip packages. You can either combine your food trip with the sightseeing in the Mussoorie travel packages or arrange for a separate food trip. Kafuli This green dish is warm in a bowl. Made of either Spinach or fenugreek leaf this pairs best with steamed rice or “mandue ki roti aur baati”(halwa made of “mandua” flour). Take the help of your Mussoorie tour guide to find the best local shops that can serve this traditional Garhwali dish. Chainsoo If you like a good Daal, this dish might be a favourite in your Mussoorie travel. A variety of roasted lentils go into this stirred with locally made masala and onions tomatoes and garam masala. With steamed rice and spicy baby potatoes, this makes out to be a great lunch. Bhaang ki Chutney As unique as it sounds this chutney has an effect quite unlike the effects of “Bhaang”. Made with roasted hemp seeds and cumin seeds it's one such local dish served in street side shops to expensive restaurants matter where your accommodation is as per your Mussoorie tour packages, you are bound to find this dish on the table. Aloo ke Gutke A dry Garhwali dish made with mountain potatoes and basic spices. You will find this dish if you book Mussoorie packages during festivals. Usually enjoyed best with cucumber “raita” and “Poori” or small flatbread. Sisunik Saag It is a delicacy in Mussoorie. If you are willing to go for a food adventure do try this dish during your Mussoorie city tour, once you are in Mall Road. The Sisun plant once touched, will give you rashes which itch for hours. Hence it is also called “Bichhu Ghaas”.The leaves are mashed into a pulp, boiled and cooked with tomatoes and butter for a mouthwatering meal. Cakes and Bakes Landour Bakehouse in Sister’s Bazaar is the place you should go for a warm cup of tea and desserts or puddings. Emily’s Bakery, a family-run business is also famous for its variety of cakes and pastries, Jams and freshly baked biscuits. If you are tired of visiting places listed in your Mussoorie vacation packages, sit down in these bakeries to enjoy a hot cuppa. Kababs and Momos For a good kebab platter, visit Nostradamus- Writers Bar in Gandhi Chowk and Kalsang Friends Corner for healthy Tibetan momos and thukpas. The Lovely Omelette Centre in Kulri Market at Mall Road makes a variety of Omelette with every possible stuffing including chocolate.
Mussoorie.Must Visit Restaurants or Resorts Mussoo: When it comes to making memories food and restaurants play a dominant part. You can curate your ideal Mussoorie holiday packages, with itinerary around these eateries. Char Dukaan, Landour Begin your Mussoorie travel with a hearty breakfast at Char Dukaan or 4 shops serving Pancakes, Waffles, Paranthas and Ginger lemon honey tea. Kalsangs, Mall Road For freshly cooked authentic Tibetan food you can head to Kalsangs during lunch. One of the best eateries you will find in your Mussoorie packages. The must-try dishes are Chicken Cheese Momos, Spinach Cheese Momos, BBQ Pork Spare Ribs andKalsang Special Thukpa. Tavern, Mall Road At the end of your Mussoorie tour, if you are yearning for typical Indian spicy food, this place will satiate your desire with Tandoori Roti, Dal Makhani, Chicken Khurchan etc. Emily’s Bakery, Landour This is not only an age-old bakery shop, it is also known for continental delicacies like BBQ Chicken, Fish Fingers, Shepherd's Pie, Stroganoff, Sticky Toffee Pudding and many more. Nostradamus, Writer’s Bar It's an in-house dining facility along with a small library open to all with books by writers like Jim Corbett, Ruskin Bond, and Lowell Thomas. This ambience is colonial with specimens of English Gothic architecture all around. A perfect corner to sip into your wine or rum on a chilly winter evening, with a Kabab Platter and Mutton Rogan Josh on the table and a book in your hand. You might want to make your stay special with luxurious and opulent accommodations at various star resorts. Some well-rated resorts are: The Savoy This is a 5-star, hundred-year-old property with gothic architecture with a backdrop of Himalayan ranges. The interiors are inspired by lavish Victorian-style architecture and work as a confluence of the bygone era with modern taste. You can request your stay to be included in Mussoorie tour packages. JW Marriott Mussoorie Walnut Grove Resort & Spa The name needs no introduction when it comes to the hospitality industry. Tucked away amongst the lush green landscape, away from the crowded Mussoorie city centre this resort is famous as a wedding destination. The Silverton Mussoorie If you want to stay near the main city for comfortable sightseeing, this resort will give the ultimate satisfaction of enjoying the valley from its open-air restaurant as well as the best location for a Mussoorie city tour. Fortune Resort Grace This resort stands on a hill slope giving a spectacular view of Doon Valley. Though, only a kilometre away from the city centre, it has a calm and peaceful atmosphere, with aesthetic designs and the latest amenities. Your tour to Mussoorie will become a forever cherished memory if you plan a stay in this resort.
Mussoorie.What to Shop in Mussoorie?: A nice shopping expedition with family on vacation is something you will always look forward to. All Mussoorie holiday packages will give you some exclusive time for shopping. Here are some of the things that you should look out for while shopping in Mussoorie. You can pick up a Mussoorie travel guide to help you find the local places for shopping. Himalayan Weavers Its a one of a one-of-a-kind shop run by a local cooperative society to showcase native products of the Himalayan region. You will find exquisite sweaters, scarves, stoles and even pure pashmina shawls. The Mall Road Always buzzing with shoppers and tourists, local shops at Mall Road have interesting mementoes and souvenirs for you to buy. Handwoven shawls, woollen dresses, pullovers, caps, and socks, this place offers everything that you need for satisfactory retail therapy. If your Mussoorie trip packages have multiple mall road trips you can explore the place in detail. The Tibetan Market Be sure to haggle before you buy anything here, starting from Tibetan shawls to trendy pieces of jewellery, from artefacts to hand-woven carpets. Kulri Bazaar Second to Mall Road Kulri Bazaar is the most vibrant market in Mussoorie. Other than Woolen clothes, local handicrafts are sold at a very reasonable price. From stylish footwear to silver jewellery you will get everything in one place. If you have Mall Road in your Mussoorie travel packages, stop at this place as well to check out some exclusive eateries and jewellery. Library Bazaar at Gandhi Chowk This bazaar is famous for its wooden items and Chinese metal vases and trinkets. If you are fond of home decor shopping, you should visit this place on your Mussoorie trip. Classic Emporium This shop near Mall Road is known for selling Tibetan sculptures, Lampshades and porcelain figures. This 40-year-old place was earlier known for manufacturing walking sticks but has transformed itself with time. Sisters Bazaar Shopping as per the itinerary of Mussoorie trip packages can be exhausting. So end your shopping spree with a Landour Bakehouse treat in Sisters Bazaar. One of the best Bakeries in Mussoorie, where you can find freshly baked brownies, cookies and dry cakes.
Mussoorie.Interesting Facts about Mussoorie: What will make your Mussoorie vacation packages and trips in the city more appreciable are these little-known facts about the place. Winter line phenomenon Apart from Mussoorie, this line can be seen in Switzerland only. A distinct line formed by the meeting of hot and cold air formed at the horizon which lits up with a golden hue due to the refraction of Sunlight during sunrise and sunset. The view is exquisite from Lal Tibba. This is what makes Lal Tibba one of the unique Mussoorie tourist places which not be missed at any cost especially if travelling in winter. Shrub Mansoor The name Mussorie had been derived from a plant called Mansoor which was indigenous to this place. Lambi Dhar Mines If you are game for some spooky experience, add this place to your visit list in Mussoorie travel packages. It is believed that fifty thousand mine workers who worked here died of severe health hazards as no safety precautions were maintained. Since then the mines have been completely deserted and people who tried to stay there always met with accidents. First home to Dalai Lama It became the first abode of “His Holiness” Dalai Lama when he was exiled from Tibet, post-Chinese colonization. The world-renowned spiritual leader came to Mussoorie to form a Tibetan deported government. Favourite Destination of the Nehru Family It was one of the favourite destinations of the famous Nehru family. In Doon Valley was the abode of his sister Vijayalakshmi Pandit. The family used to enjoy their stays between the 1920s to 40s in The Savoy Hotel. If history allures you, Mussoorie packages can still accommodate your stay in The Savoy. Discovery of Mussoorie It was discovered by two young British officers in the 1820s. Captain Young who was serving in the British army fell in love with this quaint hill station and made this his perennial home. Woodstock School This is one of the most expensive schools in India and houses students from 26 different countries such as India, Korea, Australia, America and Germany among others. Sachin Tendulkar’s Resort Mussoorie being the cricketer's favourite vacation spot, was frequently visited by him. He wanted to own a resort along with his partner Sanjay Narang. However, the construction was halted midway as it violated the norms of the Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO). So now you have ample reasons to execute your Mussoorie travel plan without further ado and hesitation. Even your family members will agree with you once they come to know about the fun facts of this place.

table_of_content: Interesting things to do in Bangkok & Adventurous things to do in Bangkok
Bangkok.Introduction: Planning to explore Bangkok and its cultural treasures? Then look no further and grab our Buy 1 Get 1 Free Bangkok package! Bangkok, the capital city of Thailand, is one of the most visited holiday destinations in Asia. This happens for a variety of reasons. First, it is a remarkable vacation spot that showcases the real side of Thailand tourism. Secondly, it is also called the melting pot of diverse cultures and beliefs. Bangkok is a place where you can find beautiful monasteries and ancient temples adjacent to massive skyscrapers and shopping malls. Whether you want to spend a luxurious vacation with top-class modern amenities or witness the beautiful side of Thai culture and traditions, you can find a variety of features and activities included in our Bangkok tour packages. Besides the array of tourist sites, landmarks, events and festivals, Bangkok is also home to some of the most well-known restaurants, hotels, markets, spa centres, nightclubs, etc. Make sure to properly do your research on the destination so you can enjoy everything this city has to offer. There are a lot of steps involved in planning a dream vacation to Bangkok. However, you can still get your targeted trip experience by going for Bangkok tour packages and leaving the planning process to the experts. The Bangkok packages offered by Thomas Cook take care of all the key elements, everything from accommodations and flights to meals and Bangkok sightseeing. This way, you can enjoy your vacation without the need to plan everything yourself. You can easily find Bangkok tour packages with different itineraries on our website. Find further details on the destination and popular Bangkok tour packages on this page.
Bangkok.Most Popular Bangkok Tour Packages: Tour Packages NameDurationPriceThailand - Pattaya And Bangkok - Buy 1 Get 1 Free4 Nights 5 DaysRs. ₹39 600Simply Thailand4 Nights 5 DaysRs. ₹52 200Simply Thailand Fully Loaded - (3N Pattaya And 2N Bangkok)5 Nights 6 DaysRs. ₹60 000Highlights Of Thailand - Winter5 Nights 6 DaysRs. ₹72 500Grand Bargain Tour of Asia - Winter10 Nights 11 DaysRs. ₹152 700
Bangkok.About Bangkok holidays: Planning a trip that should be exciting, exotic, yet affordable at the same time? Well, maybe it's time to explore Bangkok. A city that has a lot to offer, no matter what you want, be it exploring history with art and architecture or diversity and culture with culturally rich temples. From a modern life with nightclubs and fine dining to a simple experience with wholesome Thai food, you can get it all with our Bangkok packages. With these packages, you ensure you simply worry about your OOTD and excitement, while your hotels, flights, sightseeing, meals, and other plans are covered by us. What's more amazing about these Bangkok tour packages is that they are budget-friendly and are available at a wide range of prices, ensuring your Bangkok trip is based on your budget plans and requirements. Starting at a price of just ₹18,800 for 4 nights and 5 days to as luxurious as ₹1,75,600 for 10 nights and 11 days, the choices are many and yours to explore.
Bangkok.Best Time to Visit Bangkok: While packing your bags and planning an exciting trip to Bangkok, one of the first questions that might pop up in your mind is whether it is the right time to visit. And well, it's an important factor to consider. Understanding the right time to visit gives you clarity on weather conditions and what to expect, so your Bangkok trip goes as planned and not the plans of natural extremes. October to February These months are a bit on the colder side and are popular among tourists as one of the best seasons to visit Bangkok. Why? The weather is pleasant, and the vibe is great! The average temperature around this time ranges between 22°C and 32 32°C. However, the temperature around nighttime takes a dive, so make sure you pack accordingly. This season is great for indoor as well as outdoor sports. You might as well get a chance to participate in some local festivals around this time, including Christmas, New Year, Chinese New Year, etc. However, the trips around this time might be comparatively costlier because of the high number of tourists. March- April If you're planning to visit Bangkok during the less crowded seasons, then maybe March or April can be decent choices. The temperature is moderate, ranging between 27°C to 37-38°C. The highlights during this season are indoor activities, nightlife, and beaches. May to September May to September is the monsoon or the rainy season in Bangkok. Usually, the heavy showers happen for a short duration; however, there is unpredictability. Outdoor activities and adventure sports take a dive. The temperature around this time is between 25°C and 35°C. However, if you want to save big from hotel discounts and stays, then you might give it a go around this time.
Bangkok.How to reach Bangkok?: Flights and other transportation are already included in some Bangkok packages. However, make sure to cross-check the same to avoid transportation hassle. Other than that, here is how you may reach Bangkok from India. By air One of the most convenient ways to reach Thailand is by air. The best part is you can get direct flights to Bangkok- the capital city from most Indian cities like Mumbai, Delhi, Chennai, Bangalore, Kolkata, etc. The fastest flight takes around 2 hours and 25 minutes from Netaji Subhash Chandra Bose International Airport. Also, there are a variety of airlines to choose from, including Thai Airways, Jet Airways, SpiceJet, Air India, Thai AirAsia, Indigo, and many more. You may compare the prices and features and choose accordingly. By road While flights are convenient and fast, you may also take a road trip to Bangkok, Thailand. However, determination plays a great role here as it may take around a week to complete your journey. The road journey can be taken via the India-Myanmar-Thailand trilateral highway. However, before you take a road trip, make sure you have all the essential documents, including a valid driving license, vehicle registration certificate, insurance, etc. Once you reach Bangkok, you may use the local bus, tuk tuk, and taxis for local commuting.
Bangkok.Interesting things to do in Bangkok & Ad: One of the most exciting things about planning a trip is planning its itinerary. If you're still looking for ideas on things that can be done in Bangkok, then we have got you covered. Here are some interesting and adventurous sports you might consider looking out for. Chatuchak Weekend Market for Local Shopping If you're someone who loves to explore the local culture and market, then you cannot miss the Chatuchak weekend market. Spread over an area of over 35 acres, the market has several stalls divided into 27 categories, which makes it an absolute favourite of individuals who love shopping. You can get art, food, handicrafts, clothing items, artefacts, and much more. You can also do your gift and souvenir shopping from here. Don't forget to explore the local street food on the way. Luxurious Experience with Traditional Thai Massage Tired after a busy day? Well, having a traditional Thai massage can help relax your mind and body. It's an absolute favourite of thousands and thousands of tourists. Plus, it's affordable. This means you relax without spending out of your budget. Mahanakhon Skywalk to Capture the City's Essence One of the popular things in Thailand for tourists is the Mahanakhon Skywalk. The reason is its structure and elevation of 315 metres, which allows you to have a look at the beautiful view of the whole city. Wakeboarding If you're more into adventurous activities, then wakeboarding can be a good choice. If you're a beginner, you can choose Taco Lake. While Thai Wake Park is a suitable option for pro individuals with experience. Go-Karting Go-karting is another fun and thrilling activity you may engage in with your friends or travel buddies. You get to choose from a variety of well-designed racing tracks and go-karts for children and adults.
Bangkok.What to eat in Bangkok?: When in Bangkok, do not miss out on local Thai delicacies. The menu is diverse, from snacks to desserts, and you have a lot of options to choose from. Here are some popular meal options to include in your Bangkok tour: Pad Thai Pad Thai is one of the most popular Thai dishes out there. The sweet, savoury taste makes it the comfort food of many. It's a simple stir-fried rice noodle that has shrimp, meat, and chopped vegetables as well. The colour and the taste both look delicious. Khao Phat Khao pat is a type of fried rice prepared in a pot with a variety of vegetables and other ingredients like eggs, meat, tomatoes, garlic, Thai jasmine rice, and more. Seasoned with a variety of mouthwatering sauces, the taste is something that you're going to remember for ages. Som Tam Som tam, a local favourite snack, is made with ingredients like papaya, fish sauce, dried shrimp, etc. You may use it as a light meal or snacks. In addition to these, you may explore other options like Tom Yum Noodles, Oyster Omelette, Woon Sen, and much more.

faq.question: How many days are ideal for an Alleppey trip?
faq.answer: Two to three days is the perfect length for a traditional Alleppey tour so that you can take your time seeing the beaches, backwaters, and local sights. You can visit Alappuzha beach, take a houseboat or shikara ride, and see the town's main attractions on a two-day tour. If you have three days or more, you can include trips to ancient temples, the Pathiramanal Bird Sanctuary, or simply unwind with Ayurvedic treatments. Longer excursions enable you to explore the local way of life and environment in greater detail, adding depth and immersion to your Alleppey vacation packages.

faq.question: Is Alleppey best for a honeymoon or family trips?
faq.answer: Alleppey is ideal for families as well as honeymooners. With private Alleppey houseboat packages for families, candlelit dinners, and peaceful backwater views, couples can have a romantic and peaceful stay. In contrast, families can enjoy immersive cultural and nature-based activities, such as canoe excursions and beach and market exploration. Kerala Alappuzha tour packages are suitable for all kinds of travellers, whether they are looking for a peaceful retreat or an exciting group experience.

faq.question: What is the best way to explore the Alleppey backwaters?
faq.answer: The finest ways to explore the backwaters are in canoes, shikaras, or houseboats. The majority of Kerala Alleppey Package options include traditional houseboats, which provide day cruises or overnight stays aboard opulent kettuvallams with private sundecks and onboard meals. Shikaras provide you a more personal, up-close look at rural life by allowing you to manoeuvre through small waterways. For those looking for unusual experiences, canoes or kayaks offer a slower, more intimate relationship with the sea.

faq.question: Do Kerala Alleppey tour packages include meals & stays?
faq.answer: Accommodations and meals are included in the majority of Alleppey trip packages. Overnight tours offer breakfast, lunch, and dinner, while houseboat accommodations typically serve freshly made Kerala cuisine. Alappuzha houseboat package deals frequently include resorts and homestays, which offer cosy accommodations and occasionally extra facilities. To properly control the Alleppey trip cost, always double-check inclusions before making your reservation.

faq.question: What should I pack for an Alleppey holiday?
faq.answer: Bring swimwear, a lightweight jacket or shawl, and light, breathable cotton clothing. Sunscreen, sunglasses, a hat, bug repellent, a reusable water bottle, and a compact daypack are necessities. Remember to bring a camera, local currency, proof of identity, and confirmation of your reservation. During the rains, a rain poncho or travel umbrella comes in handy. These pointers guarantee a comfortable and pleasurable Alleppey one-day vacation package or prolonged stay.

faq.question: Can I combine Alleppey with Munnar or Kochi in one package?
faq.answer: Yes, it is quite typical to combine Alleppey with neighbouring locations. The backwaters and the picturesque hill station of Munnar can be combined in a Kerala Alleppey tour package, which usually includes two nights in Munnar and one night in Alleppey. As an alternative, include Kochi to visit historical monuments and cultural icons; this makes a 3-day itinerary from Kochi the perfect choice. Numerous Kerala Alappuzha tour packages include these combos, which let you take in Kerala's diversity in a single journey.

Best Time to Visit Alleppey The best time to visit Alleppey is thought to be between the winter months of October and March. It's ideal for touring, cruising, and lounging on the beaches because of the nice weather. There are a lot of carefully chosen Kerala Alleppey Tour Package options available because this is also the busiest time of year. Though frequent storms can hinder outdoor plans, the monsoon months (June to September) are magical if you're seeking lush green scenery and fewer visitors. Although the summer months of April through May are hot and muggy, you can take advantage of lower Alleppey travel expenses by booking inexpensive accommodations and packages. How to Reach Alleppey? If you’re planning a trip and wondering how to reach Alleppey, getting there is convenient and straightforward. The closest airport for flights is Cochin International Airport, which is roughly 85 kilometres away. Buses, private taxis, and taxis are readily accessible to take you directly into town from there. Alleppey has a well-established train station that connects it to important cities like Chennai, Trivandrum, and Kochi. State-run and private buses travel regularly on NH 66, which also provides a great road connection. For a more pleasant ride, many tourists even reserve private taxis, particularly when choosing an Alleppey one day tour package. Accommodation Options The experience of staying in Alleppey is unique. Alleppey houseboat tours for families or couples are the best option if you really want to soak up the backwaters. With bedrooms, living areas, and even onboard kitchens, these floating residences are available in the deluxe, premium, and luxury categories. You can immerse yourself in the serene cadence of backwater life by taking an overnight cruise. Alleppey boasts many resorts and boutique hotels along the lakeside that are ideal for a laid-back retreat if you would rather stay on land. Beach resorts close to Marari or Alleppey Beach, where you can combine luxury with views of the sea, are also included in many Kerala Alleppey Package options. Homestays, where friendly service, genuine food, and local advice enhance the Alappuzha tour package experience, are frequently preferred by low-budget tourists. Local Culture & Food Alleppey is distinguished by its distinct culture, which is closely linked to its waters. While cultural events like the Nehru Trophy Snake Boat Race in August offer unparalleled intensity, houseboating along the canals allows you to observe everyday life and get a sense of rural rhythms. This region is also known for its coir industry, and a visit to the International Coir Museum offers a fresh perspective on this age-old craft. Ayurvedic therapies are a must-try for anyone interested in wellbeing; many Alleppey travel packages offer rejuvenating spa treatments. Of course, the best part of every Alleppey trip is the cuisine. Fresh seafood is abundant, and dishes like spicy Kerala fish curry and Karimeen Pollichathu (pearl spot fish wrapped in banana leaf) are must-trys. Appam with stew or puttu with kadala curry are two vegetarian-friendly recipes that highlight the hearty flavours of Keralan cooking. Palada Payasam, a rich, creamy dessert that embodies the essence of our country, is a great way to end your meals.

Alleppey.Top Attractions & Experiences in Alleppe: Alleppey, also referred to as the "Venice of the East," is the place where Kerala's natural beauty is most striking. It should come as no surprise that Alleppey tour packages continue to be among the most popular vacation choices in Kerala. If you’re planning a trip, exploring the top places to visit in Alleppey is a must. From serene backwaters and traditional houseboat cruises to historic temples and lively beaches, there’s something here for every kind of traveler. The following are the must-see sights and activities if you're organising an Alleppey trip. Small Boat Rides and Shikara Hire a smaller, covered shikara boat for a closer look at local life. Many operators provide sunrise and sunset trips, adding a personal touch to your Alleppey holiday packages, and these smaller canals allow you to get a close-up look at village life. Cruises on Houseboats The classic Alleppey experience is floating on the backwaters aboard a traditional houseboat, or kettuvallam. Deluxe, premium, and luxury Alappuzha houseboat package options are available. You can wake up to the soft sound of rippling waterways, verdant paddy fields, and swaying coconut trees when you stay overnight. The Alleppey houseboat packages for family, which include beds, a kitchen, and a deck ideal for lounging or taking pictures, are quite popular with families. Marari Beach and Alappuzha Beach Alappuzha Beach is a family-friendly park that's ideal for strolls and sunset viewing. Marari Beach, which is only 11 km away, has serene white sands and the opportunity to enjoy soothing Ayurvedic treatments. Kuttanad Investigation Kuttanad, a location of verdant paddy fields below sea level, is referred to as the "rice bowl of Kerala." Many Kerala Alleppey tour packages include guided boat excursions over these picturesque waterways, and a visit here highlights Alleppey's rustic appeal. Island of Pathiramanal With hundreds of migratory and native bird species, Pathiramanal Island, which is only reachable by boat on Vembanad Lake, is a sanctuary for environmental enthusiasts and birdwatchers. Religious sites Visit historic temples such as the Ambalappuzha Sree Krishna Temple, Arthunkal's St. Andrew's Basilica, and the forest-bound Mannarasala Temple, which is devoted to serpent gods. These religious locations offer peaceful environments as well as insight into regional customs. Cultural and Historical Attractions The magnificent Gajendra Moksham mural is housed in the 18th-century wonder that is Krishnapuram Palace. While the International Coir Museum showcases the ancient coir-making process, the Alappuzha Lighthouse provides expansive views. Events of the Season The Nehru Trophy Boat Race on Punnamada Lake is a must-see if you go there in August. In the same way, Chettikulangara Temple's Kettukazhcha festival, which takes place in February or March, features colourful processions and devotion. Any Kerala Alleppey Tour Package is made unforgettable by these experiences, which include serene beaches, historic palaces, and floating backwaters. Selecting a carefully thought-out package guarantees that you don't miss these famous attractions while also effectively managing the expense of the Alleppey trip.
Alleppey.Things to Do in Alleppey Beyond Sightsee: Alleppey offers a multitude of immersive experiences that enable you to genuinely interact with its culture, environment, and local life; it's not just about sightseeing and floating down the backwaters. By selecting the best Alleppey tour packages or a Kerala Alleppey Package, you can take advantage of these experiences without losing sight of the town's undiscovered beauties. Here are some fascinating things to incorporate into your Alleppey trip, regardless of whether you choose a one-day tour package or a more extensive schedule. Witness Coir-Making Kerala's coir business is centred in Alleppey. You can observe how coconut husks are processed into fibre for handicrafts, ropes, and mats by visiting the International Coir Museum. You can gain a greater understanding of a centuries-old craft and its significance to local livelihoods by observing artists at work. Enrol in a Cooking Course Why not learn to prepare your own food if you enjoy eating it? Traditional Kerala meals like Fish Pollichathu, Prawns Puttu, and Appam with Stew can be prepared in hands-on workshops offered by numerous local families and cooking schools. In addition to adding authenticity, eating the food you've prepared on a banana leaf provides a window into the local cuisine. Explore Village Life Navigate the narrow canals with a guide in an open country boat or small canoe. Along the backwaters, you can observe customary fishing, toddy tapping, and everyday activities that provide an insight into life away from the hustle and bustle of the city. These private encounters are included in many Alleppey vacation packages. Check Out a Local Toddy Store In Kerala, toddler stores are recognisable. You can engage with locals, try fresh palm wine, and savour seafood specialities that are frequently made on the spot. The vibrant, genuine experience perfectly encapsulates the daily cadence of Alleppey life. Pathiramanal Island Bird Watching This island, which is reachable by boat, is a birder's paradise. This area is home to more than 90 native species and 50 migratory birds, such as Teal and Pintail Ducks. Visits at sunrise or sunset add even more charm to the experience. Kayak Paddle Through Canals Kayaking is a tranquil way for adventurers to explore the tranquil canals. At your own leisure, you can glide beneath small bridges, get up close and personal with birds and plants, and take in the serene beauty of the backwaters. Take in the Beach Life There are serene, less congested places to unwind, like Punapra Beach and Marari Beach. These beaches, which offer everything from massages by the sea to peaceful strolls on white sand, offer a welcome change from the tranquillity of the backwater. Try Local Fishing Techniques Take a village trip with fishermen and experience traditional fishing with nets or bamboo rods. It's an enjoyable, practical approach to experience the backwater landscape and engage with the local way of life.
Alleppey.Essential Tips for a Smooth Alleppey Tri: A combination of careful planning and local knowledge is necessary to ensure a seamless and unforgettable vacation to Alleppey. These crucial pointers will help you get the most out of your trip, whether you're booking a full-fledged Kerala Alleppey Package, an Alleppey one-day tour package, or an Alleppey houseboat packages for family. Select the Appropriate Time to Go The time of your visit has a significant impact. If you want to enjoy your Alappuzha houseboat package without any disruptions, the winter months of November through February are the best time to visit because of the pleasant weather and serene backwaters. Deals on lodging and houseboats are easier to locate during the hot and muggy summer months of March through May. Although there are fewer tourists and more foliage during the monsoon season (June to October), backwater activities can be restricted by heavy rains, so check the weather first. Investigate Outside the Houseboat Although houseboats are recognisable, Alleppey's smaller waterways and towns provide a genuine glimpse of daily life. For a more engaging experience, try riding the government ferries that are part of several Kerala Alleppey tour packages, renting a small canoe, or taking a shikara trip. Autorickshaws are practical for short trips. Reserve Houseboats in Advance If you plan to stay on a houseboat, make sure to reserve far in advance, at least four to six weeks at the busiest times of the year. To make sure your boat lives up to your expectations, ask for a video tour or fresh pictures. Choose between a day cruise and an overnight stay based on your schedule; overnight trips let you take in the sunset, dinner, and sunrise over the calm seas. Verify inclusions like meals, air conditioning, and snacks at all times. Control Your Expectations Keep in mind that houseboats are small and lack amenities. Plan your activities appropriately because they anchor by evening. Because of boat traffic and water quality, it is not recommended to swim in the backwaters. For a more tranquil trip, think about using less-travelled routes like those from Alappuzha to Kottayam or Kumarakom. For comfort, pack wisely Clothing made of light, breathable cotton or linen is crucial for the tropical climate. Bring insect repellent, a hat, sunglasses, and sunscreen, as well as comfortable sandals or walking shoes. To keep devices safe from moisture and spills, a waterproof bag or pouch is essential. Keep your favourite foods, a tiny first-aid kit, and your own medications with you.

Sri Lanka.Best Time To Visit Sri Lanka: Organising a trip to Sri Lanka? It can make all the difference to know when to visit Sri Lanka. Because different places have different climates, choosing the best month to visit Sri Lanka will guarantee nice weather, fewer tourists, and the finest experiences. December to March: If you want to see Sri Lanka's cultural heartlands or plan a beach vacation, now is the best season to visit Sri Lanka. The mild, dry weather is perfect for sightseeing in Kandy and Nuwara Eliya or for tanning in Bentota. Flights and the Sri Lanka trip cost from India may be more during this busy time of year. Nevertheless, it's the ideal time to reserve those popular Sri Lanka trip packages that take you to Sri Lanka tourist places from India. May to August: The central highlands are lush, cool, and picturesque at this time. Imagine peaceful lakeside walks, heritage homes in Nuwara Eliya, and tea gardens in Ella. This is the best time to visit Sri Lanka from India, whether you're looking for romantic honeymoon packages or beautiful Sri Lanka packages holidays. Additionally, the coast is off-season, thus Sri Lanka trip packages will be more affordable. April to September: This is the best time to visit Sri Lanka from India if you're more interested in wildlife, historic temples, or unusual beaches like Trincomalee and Arugam Bay. The north and east are drier, and there are fewer tourists because the Maha monsoon is over. This time of year is best time to visit Sri Lanka for leisurely travel through lesser-known routes in Sri Lanka and for cultural encounters. Every month has a unique flavour, whether it's the Sri Lanka attractions, colourful festivals, or mouthwatering Sri Lanka cuisine. Where you're going will determine the best time to visit Sri Lanka, but with the correct Sri Lanka trip packages, you can find something amazing throughout the year.
Sri Lanka.How To Reach Sri Lanka?: Are you confused about how to get from India to Sri Lanka? This straightforward guide will help you know how to reach the Sri Lanka tour smoothly and make your travel arrangements hassle-free, regardless of whether you want to fly, drive, or take the train to neighbouring cities. By train The fact that there are no direct trains to Sri Lanka should be taken into consideration when making travel plans from India. Nonetheless, you can go by train to Tamil Nadu's southern cities, such as Chennai or Rameshwaram. These are frequently used as the starting points for subsequent flights or ferries and are the closest to Sri Lanka. Even while the train won't take you straight to the island, it's still a useful part of the trip, particularly for low-budget tourists purchasing Sri Lanka tour packages from India that include land travel within the country. By Road There is no direct road connection between India and Sri Lanka because it is an island nation. Here's how to get there, though: take the route to Rameshwaram and continue to Dhanushkodi. Depending on the situation, ferry services to Talaimannar, Sri Lanka, are occasionally available from there. Although this path is a little more daring, it offers a distinctive touch for travellers looking for different ways to start their Sri Lanka journey. Plan carefully if you're planning Sri Lanka honeymoon packages or Sri Lanka holiday packages for your family, though, as it's not as frequent or dependable as flying. By Air The simplest and quickest method to begin your Sri Lanka visit is via air. Direct flights to Colombo are available from major Indian cities like Bengaluru, Delhi, Mumbai, and Chennai. This is how most Sri Lanka packages start. The duration of the flight varies from 1.5 to 3.5 hours, contingent on the place of departure. For individuals looking for personalised Sri Lanka tour packages for family, or Sri Lanka holidays, romantic retreats, or cultural explorations, this choice is ideal. With so many Sri Lanka packages to choose from, flying transports you swiftly to the centre of the island's charm, where you can view the best sights, eat the local Sri Lanka food, and discover the main Sri Lanka attractions.
Sri Lanka.Best Places To Visit In Sri Lanka: These Sri Lanka tourist places make every trip to Sri Lanka genuinely unforgettable, whether you're organising a romantic retreat, a spiritual retreat, or a leisurely vacation. Famous Places to Visit Yala National Park: Yala is the place to go if you enjoy animals. One of the most exciting safari experiences in Sri Lanka is seeing leopards, elephants, and unusual birds. Rock Fortress of Sigiriya: Sigiriya, a huge rock fortress with historic frescoes, water gardens, and breathtaking views, is one of the most famous Sri Lanka tourist spots. Because of its appeal and history, it is a must-see in all popular Sri Lanka tour packages. National Park at Horton Plains: This plateau, which is well-known for its breathtaking vistas and "World's End" cliff, is among the top places to see in Sri Lanka for hikers and nature enthusiasts. The Forest Reserve of Sinharaja: This UNESCO Biosphere Reserve is a must-see for anyone interested in wildlife paths. It is one of Sri Lanka's most serene places to see in Sri Lanka and is abundant in biodiversity. Pinnawala Elephant Orphanage: This sanctuary, which is near Kandy, allows visitors to interact closely with rescued elephants. A well-liked feature of Sri Lanka tour packages for family, perfect for both nature lovers and children. Beaches/ Hill Stations to Visit Beach Mirissa: Mirissa is the ideal place for anyone wishing to relax because of its golden sands, beach bars, and whale watching excursions. Beach getaways and honeymoon packages to Sri Lanka always include it. Nuwara Eliya: This hill station, also known as "Little England", offers historical elegance, colourful tea gardens, and a temperate environment, a feature of many Indian-to-Sri Lanka travel packages. Bentota Beach: a charming seaside getaway popular for leisure and water sports. Because of its tropical atmosphere and things to do in Sri Lanka, this beach is a favourite among those who reserve luxury Sri Lanka packages. Adam's Peak: Adam's Peak offers something for everyone, whether you're a spiritual person or just enjoy a good hike. One of the most unforgettable sights to experience in Sri Lanka is the dawn from the peak. Ella: Ella is a treasure trove of hiking trails, waterfalls, and the well-known Nine Arch Bridge. For both couples and backpackers, it's one of the best places to visit in Sri Lanka. Temples to Visit Temple of Gangaramaya: This temple, located in Colombo, combines modern culture with traditional architecture. One of the best places to visit in Sri Lanka that you should not miss. The Sacred Tooth Relic Temple: This temple, which is among the popular things to see in Sri Lanka for pilgrims, is situated in Kandy and houses Buddha's precious tooth. A spiritual treasure in any travel guide to Sri Lanka. Thirukoneswaram Kovil: This magnificent Hindu temple, perched on a rock in Trincomalee, provides a breathtaking vista in addition to its history. It's one of Sri Lanka's most picturesque things to see in Sri Lanka. Dambulla Cave Temple: This historic location, which is a highlight of practically all Sri Lanka packages and a UNESCO World Heritage Site worth seeing, is home to more than 150 Buddha statues. Jaya Sri Maha Bodhi: This holy tree in Anuradhapura has great spiritual significance and is a must-see for anyone looking for authentic Sri Lankan travel experiences.

Africa.African Food: Alongside researching Africa packages, make sure to take a peek at its local food culture. From the desert land of the North to the alluring coasts of South Africa, each region dishes up a delicacy of their own which are bound to give you a delectable experience. Here are different ailments one should not miss when in Africa. Piri Chicken This is a luscious esculent of smoky grilled chicken which is cooked with pepper, garlic, coconut milk, piri sauce and lime. It tastes best when served with vatapa, which is the traditional sauce made of cassava leaves, cooked in peanut sauce. This scrumptious delicacy is a part of the Mozambique region of Africa. Enjoy more of Africa’s local food on your trip with Africa packages from Thomas Cook. Jollof Rice and Egusi Soup One of the major sins you can commit in Nigeria is not enjoying the highly pleasing feast called Jollof Rice. It is called as the National favourite dish of Nigeria, and is also, quite famous across the west of Africa. It is a basic yet appetizing one pot dish whichconsists of rice, tomatoes, onions and pepper. It can be savoured well with Egusi Soup asit is traditionally served in African festive gatherings. Bunny Chow Brought to South Africa by the Indian labourers who visited this land to work on sugar-cane fields, this is one of the most prominent street foods of South Africa. It is a quarter loaf of bread which is hollowed out and filled with sizzling hot curry. It is available in all the major cities of South Africa, but it is said that the best ones are found in Durban. faq.question: What is the best time to visit Bangalore?
faq.answer: The best time to visit Bangalore or to book a Bangalore tour package is from October to February during the winter. The lowest temperature only drops to approximately 10°C, so it doesn't actually get that cold. The best time of year for sightseeing and outdoor activities because there are so many lakes and gardens to explore.

faq.question: Is it safe to visit Bangalore?
faq.answer: Bangalore is a safe place to visit. But there are too many folks and vehicles on the streets. Visitors should take great care while crossing roads and other areas with few traffic lights and pedestrian crossings.

faq.question: Which is the main airport in Bangalore?
faq.answer: Bangalore's main airport, known as HAL Bangalore Airport or Bangalore International Airport, is situated around 4 kilometers from the center of the city.

faq.question: Which are the languages spoken in Bangalore?
faq.answer: Kannada, Tamil, Telugu, Hindi, English are the main languages spoken in the Bangalore.

faq.question: How many days does one need for a vacation to the Bangalore?
faq.answer: Anywhere between 2-3 days is sufficient for a Bangalore trip. Plan your itinerary in advance so that you can cover all the must visit places in Bangalore.

faq.question: What is the traditional food of Bangalore?
faq.answer: The traditional cuisine of Bangalore that you must try during your Bangalore tour includes Rava idli, which is an important dish that is typically enjoyed with Saagu and Coconut chutney. This particular combination is known for its delicious taste and is considered to be a specialty of the city.

faq.question: What are some popular tourist attractions in Bangalore?
faq.answer: A trip to Bangalore offers many well-known tourist attractions, such as the ISKCON Temple, Nandi Hills, Vidhan Soudha, National Gallery of Modern Art, and Bangalore Palace. Moreover, the city is popularly known as the Garden City due to its abundance of magnificent parks and gardens.

faq.question: Which is the popular festival celebrated in Bangalore?
faq.answer: The Karaga Festival is a well-known festival celebrated in Bangalore that occurs at the Dharmaraya Swamy Temple. It is celebrated during the months of March and April, specifically on the day of Karaga. The festival's main ceremony starts shortly after sunset.

faq.question: How can I get around in Bangalore?
faq.answer: To travel around Bangalore during a Bangalore trip, you can use taxis, auto-rickshaws, buses, metro, rental cars, bicycles, and motorbikes.

faq.question: How many days are enough for Bangalore tour packages?
faq.answer: Bangalore may not appear as a large city due to its circular structure. However, there is a lot to explore when it comes to sightseeing or shopping, adventure activities, indulging in food, and ample nature walks. For a cursory trip to check out the top attractions of Bangalore, two to three days are enough.If you wish to indulge in everything Bangalore has to offer and immerse yourself in Bangalore’s experience of food, adventure, shopping, and exploring the unique sites as well as temple tours, you may need to book your Bangalore packages for three to five days minimum.

faq.question: Is there a perfect time to book Bangalore trip packages?
faq.answer: The southern state of Karnataka is undoubtedly hot, but the city of Bangalore is relatively cooler, more so during the winter months of October to February. Autumn and spring pay a visit during this time but may go unnoticed, although the showers of leaves and the full bloom of the trees are a spectacular vision. It is the perfect time to book your Bangalore vacation packages. You will need to pack warm clothes, but the overall atmosphere and the vibrant city life during this time make it the ideal time to visit.

faq.question: Is a holiday in Bangalore going to be costly?
faq.answer: As a metropolitan city, Bangalore is not particularly cheap. However, with a little budget planning, you can enjoy a trip to Bangalore without it being too costly. As the accommodation will be part of your Bangalore packages by Thomas Cook, you only need to worry about food and sightseeing. Using the metro to travel or taking a bus around the city can reduce costs considerably. There are also plenty of offers in Bangalore restaurants during happy hours and even for delivery, which reduces food costs as well.

faq.question: Is the rainy season ideal for booking Bangalore trip packages?
faq.answer: The cool weather of Bangalore can be accounted for the extended rainy season from June to October. While sometimes the rain is just a light shower, other times it can be quite a downpour. When booking your Bangalore tour packages, it is best to avoid the rainy season, especially during August and September. June still experiences a light shower due to summer bidding adieu, and the weather is lovely, not to mention the scenery is to die for. You can book your Bangalore holiday packages during this time or before June.

faq.question: Which are the best areas to explore for shopping in Bangalore?
faq.answer: The entire city of Bangalore is covered in malls and stores for shopping.Shivaji Nagar, Commercial Street, And Raheja Towers in Koramangala are the best places for discount shopping. You can acquire a lot of unique items without spending a lot of money. For brands, you can visit MG Rd., Forum Mall, Inorbit Mall, Phoenix Mall, and other exuberant shopping destinations. There is also Tibet Mall which carries handmade objects which are one-of-a-kind and exceedingly stylish. From shoes to clothing to toys and souvenirs, you can have a wonderful shopping experience in Bangalore in all of these areas.

faq.question: Can I go to Palace Grounds without an invitation?
faq.answer: Palace Grounds is one of the tourist places in Bangalore where most people like to celebrate important events. The acres of area that it covers and the number of clubs and restaurants that it houses beckons tourists to visit and explore the relics and ancient architecture confined within the walls.You can visit the Palace Grounds without an invitation. You are required to buy a ticket in case you wish to explore the specific palaces and ancient infrastructure. However, for the most part, palace grounds remain open to the public for indulgence in the history of Bangalore.

faq.question: Is there a dress code to follow in Bangalore?
faq.answer: Due to the cultural diversity of the residents of Bangalore, the city remains pretty liberal. There are no particular dress codes to follow. Sports, dresses, trousers, jeans, shorts, sleeveless tops, and shirts, along with saris and kurtas, are all welcome attires to wear for both men and women.When you visit spiritual institutions, make sure to cover up in order not to hurt the sentiments of the people as well as to show respect towards the idols to whom the institution is dedicated. Other than that, Bangalore is a pretty open-minded city where fashion is thriving.

faq.question: Why is Bangalore called the garden city?
faq.answer: The Silicon Valley of India is known for many things, including its thriving IT infrastructure, esteemed colleges for the best education, and dedicated healthcare system. But the best thing about Bangalore, which remains a hidden gem amid the metropolitan lifestyle, is the lush greenery and array of parks and gardens.The Lalbagh Garden is a popular tourist destination, especially during summer, where you can check out the flower show featuring indigenous flora. The garden itself extends for acres covered in banyan trees and sweet blossoms, and it even has a nook of a natural cave. It is a great place to spend some time on your honeymoon or with friends to have a picnic or run around on the grass in the shade of the numerous trees that line the boulevards within the garden.Aside from the Lalbagh garden, there are also some parks, such as Cubban Park, the park on the Ulsoor lakeside, and a street in Jayanagar dedicated to smaller parks on each side throughout the length of the road. Bangalore is a pretty green city, and the Lalbagh Garden, in particular, contributes to its naming the Garden City.

faq.question: Which is the best restaurant for an authentic Bangalore experience?
faq.answer: The city of Bangalore welcomes and celebrates diverse cultures and people from different corners of the world, not just India. Therefore, you can be assured that you can find any cuisine in the city that you are craving. However, if you wish to indulge in an authentic experience of Bangalore food, two restaurant chains stand out. Adiga’s is the most popular purely vegetarian restaurant chain in Bangalore that serves different varieties of south Indian and Chinese food prepared with a touch of Karnataka's speciality. The second most popular is A2B’s. This restaurant serves not only South Indian, Chinese, and North Indian food with the Bangalore touch but also offers sweet dishes which are unique to the region. Additionally, if you wish to explore the Bangalore bakery experience, then try the Iyengar bakery franchise. The softest cakes, pastries, puffs, dry snacks, and other delicacies can only be found in the city of Bangalore.

faq.question: Which is the best mode of transportation to travel in Bangalore?
faq.answer: Despite all its charm and beauty, Bangalore is also very popular for its traffic. If you choose to travel by bus or cab in particular, you may have a hard time reaching your destination in time. Fortunately, the Bangalore metro stations connect most of the major hubs of the city, thus making it the perfect mode of transportation to get around Bangalore. The Bangalore metro is an overhead track, and you can get from point A to point B fairly quickly. Even if you need to reach Majestic, which is the major bus depot from where you can catch buses to travel to the outskirts of Bangalore, the metro can get you there before you know it.

Bangalore.Introduction: The capital city of the Indian state of Karnataka, Bangalore is one of the few destinations that impressively depict the saying ‘old meets new’. Despite being one of the most modish cities of the country, Bangalore has remarkably preserved its glory and beauty of the bygone times. The immense popularity of Bangalore Tour Packages is proof of Bangalore’s viability as a superb travel destination which has the best of both the worlds.
