Bangalore.Best time to visit Bangalore: Bangalore is one of those cities in India that is comfortable around the year. Situated on a plateau the temperature variations can range from 10 to 32 degrees Celsius throughout the year. Most of the time, the mornings and afternoons are a bit warm while evenings are nights experience a subtle breeze. When planning for your Bangalore trip packages, it is best to consider winter or spring for a thorough Bangalore tour. Despite the pleasant weather all around the year winter is particularly beautiful in Bangalore. The temperatures can dip up to 10 degrees Celsius at night and during the daytime, there is still a cool breeze keeping the temperatures low. Starting from October to February, it is the ideal time to explore the sites of Bangalore. There are several lakes, gardens, temples, and hill stations that you can visit to explore the city and its neighbouring towns. Winter shopping in Bangalore is also one of the highlights of the city, as many refugees visit the area to sell unique items. Apart from the usual charms of Bangalore, there is also Christmas which takes on a life of itself in this inclusive city. New Year celebrations are also delightful when the streets are lit, and the locals welcome tourists to celebrate with fireworks and good food. All in all, winter is the best time to visit Bangalore to discover the reasons why it is a hidden gem of India.
Bangalore.How to reach Bangalore?: Bangalore is one of the most well-connected cities in India for both domestic and international travel. Located in the southern parts of the country in the state of Karnataka, it is accessible by trains, planes, and roads. Here is a list of means to reach your destination on your Bangalore holiday packages: By air By train By road By air: The Kempe Gowda international airport is one of the major airports of India that facilitates flights both domestically and abroad. Not only can you catch flights headed to Bangalore from major cities such as Mumbai, Kolkata, Chennai, Hyderabad, Pune, Delhi, and Kerala, but there are flights available from smaller cities as well. Major cities offer direct flights to Bangalore with top airlines, while other cities have connecting flights with a layover at the closest major airport in the state. Additionally, the Kempe Gowda international airport also caters to flights from the Middle East and European countries as well as other major countries in Asia. By train: Bangalore is also easily accessible by train. The most popular choice is the Duronto Express which connects this southern city to several parts of the country, most importantly West Bengal. Other express trains are scheduled from Mumbai, Delhi, Chennai, Hyderabad, Punjab, Chandigarh, and more. Aside from express trains, you can also opt for those that travel via other states before reaching the destination in Bangalore. When boarding a train from anywhere in India to Bangalore, it will take you straight to Yeshwantpur station, which lies in the outer circles of the city. It is very easy to find a cab or bus headed towards the city to reach your accommodation. By road: Karnataka is one of the most well-connected southern states, and the city of Bangalore allows travelling by road to and from all the surrounding states. One of the major options for travel for your Bangalore tour packages is by bus. KSRTC is the state's designated travel bus service that facilitates transportation to surrounding areas in Tamil Nadu, Kerala, Goa, and the cities of Hyderabad, Mumbai, and Pune. There are also private buses such as Red Bus, Orange Travels, and others that facilitate transportation to similar cities, although the cost may be a bit higher. On the plus side, these buses are air-conditioned with sleeper facilities and provide a much more comfortable service. Another popular means of travelling by road to and from Bangalore is by car. Most people residing in cities and states around Bangalore opt to travel by private car or cab service, and sometimes even bikes. Bangalore lies 600 to 1000 kilometres in the range of its neighbouring states and is easily accessible by road with an overnight drive. This may not be the cheapest mode of transportation; however, it is more luxurious, and you have the opportunity to make pit stops at hotels or motels along the way to catch up on much-needed rest.
Bangalore.Best honeymoon destinations in Bangalore: The wonders of Bangalore extend beyond the confines of the city. While the metropolitan city may not be the ideal place to celebrate your honeymoon, Bangalore is surrounded by exciting and romantic destinations where you can choose to start your new life together as a couple. Here are some product choices for honeymoon destinations when planning your Bangalore packages: Chikmanglur – located at the foot of the hills near the western ghats, Chikmanglur is a popular honeymoon tour destination as well as a gateway to the hills and beach sides around Bangalore. Coorg – A hill station near Bangalore, popular for the natural enigma of rolling hillsides and delicacies. Dharwad – A town on the outskirts of Bangalore in the Hubli district, known for its natural beauty and extravagant resorts. Gulbarga – A small town near Bangalore that is home to many hidden gems. Hampi – A honeymoon destination that is now preserved as a UNESCO heritage site due to the numerous ancient structures and items discovered in the area. It is the ideal place to learn the history of the state of Karnataka, which houses Bangalore City. Mysore – A hill station near Bangalore with a rich history of welcoming and hosting refugee camps for generations. Shimoga – the perfect honeymoon destination near Bangalore with vibrant waterfalls and riverside activities.
Bangalore.Best places to visit in Bangalore: The city of Bangalore is charming all year round. There are many places to visit that truly define how the locals of Bangalore preserve the city's balance between nature and man-made aesthetics. You are some of the best places to visit in Bangalore on your Bangalore tour packages: Bannerghatta national park - among the major attractions in Bangalore is the Bannerghatta national park which is home to several species of indigenous fauna. Aside from the popular tiger sanctuary, it is also home to several species of crocodiles and snakes and hosts an aquarium. There is a dedicated children's area as well as a section devoted to the display of prehistoric creatures. It is a one-stop destination for adventure, hiking, and exploring natural wildlife. Bull temple – among the many temples of Bangalore, the bull temple dedicated to Nandi has a special significance. Visit the temple and check out the area of its namesake for an enlightening experience. Church St. - One of the most serene yet busiest places of Bangalore is the road on which sits St. Mark’s Cathedral. This landmark is surrounded by bookstores, antique stores, music stores, cafes and restaurants, gift shops, and a wide range of handicraft shops. There are also tattoo parlours along the road. The entire street is like a reminder of the 90s. Cubbon Park - another prominent landmark in Bangalore known for its natural beauty and surrounding tourist attractions is Cubbon Park. HAL aerospace museum – this museum displaying the history and development of aeronautics, is one of the best places to visit in Bangalore. Explore closely how the different engines, aeroplane structures, and physical influences affect the flight of various types of planes. ISCKON temple – if you are a devotee of lord Krishna, then you certainly cannot miss the ISCKON temple. It is said that lord Krishna resides in the palms of all humans, and how we use it is how he blesses us with prosperity. Pay a visit for a special blessing on your Bangalore trip. Jawaharlal Nehru Planetarium – A fun way to spend the afternoon is to gaze at the different constellations and learn about the astronomical structures that affect the seasons on our planet at the renowned Jawaharlal Nehru Planetarium. Lalbagh Gardens - One of the top attractions in Bangalore, especially during summer, is the Lalbagh Gardens. It is a landmark as much as it is a testament to the city’s undeterred dedication to preserving natural resources. It is good for picnics as well as for a stroll through nature at any time of the year, whereas in the summer, you can catch a glimpse of the flower festival. The Bangalore Palace - Part of the Mysore royal family sits in the Malleswaram area of Bangalore, known as Bangalore Palace. The ancient architecture combined with modern infrastructure and artwork displays the perfect blend of the past and present of the royal family. UB city – One of the most prestigious places to visit in Bangalore, with extravagant shopping destinations, a high-end club, and an amphitheatre. Part of Vijay Mallya's business ventures, UB City is the place for luxury and class. Ulsoor Lake – A perfect destination to spend an afternoon boating on the serene water or walk through the park that surrounds it is located at Ulsoor Lake. There is also a gurudwara overlooking the lake where you can spend time in tranquillity, along with trying authentic Punjabi cuisine at the Dhaba next to it. Vidhana Soudha – the parliamentary building is a testament to architecture in Bangalore. This landmark is a coveted and prestigious place with a jaw-dropping infrastructure.
Bangalore.Best hill stations near Bangalore: DD Hills – this hill station located in Tumkur is popular for its forts and natural beauty. At an elevation sits the ‘fort of god,’ a true spectacle of architecture that invites divinity, serenity, and natural beauty. Nandi Hills – A hill station within a couple of hours of Bangalore. It is a popular destination for road trips as well as for exploring the old-world charms of the city. Ramanagaram – One of the best places to go hiking on the hills. Located only a couple of hours away from Bangalore, it is one of the most thrilling places for adventures on a hillside. Mysore Hills – the perfect destination to chill and explore the charms of the olden days in Mysore. This hill station has a long history of housing refugees and makes for the perfect place to shop, try Tibetan cuisine, and enjoy the serenity of nature. Coorg – an overnight trip during your Bangalore tour packages will take you to Coorg. This hill station is a spectacle of rolling plantations on the hillside and picturesque views of the world around Bangalore.
Bangalore.Things to eat in Bangalore: The culinary charms of Bangalore extend far beyond South Indian cuisine in general. As a city of diversity, the delicacies in Bangalore hail from all over India as well as overseas, but the locals add their special touch to make these dishes even more lip-smacking. From street foods to small eateries, all the way through reputed restaurants, here are some of the foods of Bangalore that you simply must try during your Bangalore tour packages: Thatte idly – if you like steamed idlis, you will go gaga over the palm-sized variation. Thatte idlis are fluffy steamed rice cakes with a diameter of almost 4 inches. They are served with a side of coconut and peanut chutney alongside sambar. These are best enjoyed at food stalls served on banana leaves. Paper dosa - the thinnest, crispiest dosas you have ever tasted will leave your mouth watering for more. Paper dosas are larger than normal masala dosas. You can opt for masala inside or have it plain served with coconut or peanut chutney and sambar. Rava idly - the healthiest form of idlis made in Bangalore consists of Rava instead of rice powder. These idlis are slightly tinted due to the roasted semolina mixed with herbs and spices. They are served with coconut chutney and sambar. Gobi Manchurian - one of the coveted street foods found all over the city at local stalls is Gobi Manchurian. These are cauliflower florets battered and deep-fried, served alongside ketchup and salad. Egg rice – Bangalore’s Street food is also famous for egg rice. It is a recipe derived from Dharwad consisting of scrambled eggs mixed cooked with onions, tomatoes, ginger garlic paste, herbs and spices, and steamed rice. Noodles - Another street-side delicacy in Bangalore is the noodles found at stalls. This plate full of boiled noodles is mixed with different types of sauces and fried with vegetables for a unique street-style taste. Thukpa - Bangalore is full of Tibetan restaurants, and one of the specialities found at these eateries is Thukpa. It is a form of traditional noodle soup mixed with vegetables, meat, and wheat noodles all stewed into a healthy concoction. Momos - You can find Momos on almost every street corner in Bangalore. These northeast Indian delicacies are available in both steamed and fried forms. You can choose vegetable, chicken, or even beef fillings for your selection of momos. Devanagari dosa - this is one of the coveted forms of dosa found in Bangalore. It is made of watery batter that causes tiny holes in the structure of the dosa, much like a block of Swiss cheese. The true experience lies in trying to hold the chutney within the confines of the softest dosa while it tries to slither out of the little holes. Ragi Mudda - a healthy recipe that is rich in nutrients is Ragi Mudda. This is essentially a ball of mixed finger millets served with sweet chutney or sour side dishes as per your preference. Bisi Bele Bhaat - this is a south Indian variation of khichdi. This delicate concoction is made of toor dal and broken rice mixed with vegetables such as carrots, beans, drumsticks, and sometimes brinjals. Puliyogre - another popular variation of rice recipe is Puliyogre. It is a form of masala fried rice where the focal ingredient is fried peanuts. It is served with a side of coconut chutney. Kesari Bhaat - if you are craving sweet dishes, then Kesari Bhaat will hit the right spot. This delicate sweet dish is prepared with semolina mixed with the essence of saffron and cooked with roasted dry fruits such as cashews, almonds, and raisins. Masala puri - One of the favourite street side snacks found in Bangalore is masala puri. Think of it as pani puri with A twist wherein a concoction of green or white peas mixed with onions, tomatoes, herbs, spices, and seasonings to create a thick gravy is spread over broken puris. The layers are then covered with sweet and sour water and sprinkled with sev and pomegranate seeds. Fruit juices – Bangalore locals are big on consuming a lot of fresh fruit juices. Try the watermelon, pomegranate, orange, melon, or mixed fruit juices sprinkled with a homemade spice. Honey cake - when it comes to sweet dishes, the honey cakes found that Iyengar bakery is one of the top choices in Bangalore. It is a dry cake baked with honey essence that you can purchase in slices. Donne biriyani – finally, Bangalore's very own variation of biryani is this aromatic delicacy. It is usually made from steamed or broken rice cooked in chicken broth alongside marinated and spiced pieces of chicken, onions, tomatoes, and mint and coriander chutney. It is served with a side of raita, and you can even opt for chicken kebabs to enhance the flavours of the specialised biryani.
Bangalore.Shopping in Bangalore: Bangalore is a metropolitan area like no other. While shopping in Mumbai is hyped due to the latest trends from fashion week, Bangalore isn't far behind when it comes to style. From unique souvenirs to classy new designs of clothing, you can find it all at the best shopping destinations in Bangalore. Here are some of the places that you simply must try on your Bangalore holiday packages or an all-inclusive shopping experience: Brand factory – an outlet mall spread in franchises all across Bangalore that carries branded products at very low prices. Commercial Street - The ideal shopping destination for unique pieces of footwear, accessories, and souvenirs. This street is known for smaller stores that carry fashionable items, and it is a short walk away from MG Road. Koramangala – A popular shopping destination owing to the carefully situated forum mall and Raheja Towers. Unlike the other malls in the city, the forum mall is home to many popular brands and also fun houses, movie theatres, and restaurants. MG Road – This is the one-stop destination to shop for clothing, footwear, toys, accessories, and souvenirs, and to try local fast-food chains. The amazing selection makes up for the slightly higher prices, but the shopping experience is unforgettable. Shivajinagar – A perfect place to shop for jewellery, clothing, and footwear with a traditional touch. The streets are covered with vendors in small shops as well as large stores carrying a selection of beautiful pieces at very reasonable prices. Tibet Mall – for the most stylish acquisitions on a budget, visit the Tibet Mall. The prices are reasonable for the unique clothing and footwear that they carry. Not to mention they are always one step ahead of fashion in Bangalore. UB City – if you wish to shop for designers, then UB City, built by Vijay Mallya, is the place to go. You will be surrounded by extravagance and come across stores carrying authentic designer clothing, accessories, handbags, cosmetics, and perfumes.
Bangalore.Things to do on your Bangalore trip pack: Here are some of the fun activities to do on your trip to Bangalore: Fun World Wonderla Boating in Sankey and Ulsoor Snow City Fun World Have a great time with friends and family at the game station central to Bangalore. You can go bowling, take part in fun games, and enjoy an afternoon of laughter. Wonderla One of the biggest amusement parks in India, featuring the largest land rides as well as a dedicated water activities area. You will need to purchase tickets for dry rides and wet rides separately or opt for an all-inclusive pass for both. Boating in Sankey and Ulsoor If you love the water, you can hire a boat and go paddling across Ulsoor Lake or Sankey Lake. These water bodies are surrounded by greenery, providing a perfect backdrop for a secluded adventure with your loved ones on your tour to Bangalore. Snow City Your tour packages to Bangalore simply must include the snow city. It is a major attraction with artificial snow to recreate the thrills of playing in the snow amid the heat of this southern city. It is a perfect place to visit on Bangalore travel packages during the summer.
Bangalore.Adventurous things to do in Bangalore: Here are some adventures you can take part in on your tour to Bangalore: Go-karting Quad biking Hiking Go-karting One of the best adrenaline-inducing activities is go-karting in Bangalore. Enjoy the long circuits with your friends and family for a celebratory race during your trip to Bangalore. Quad biking If you want an adrenaline rush, go quad biking in Kanakpura. The thrills are unending on the spiralling roads. Hiking Trekking and hiking are some of the main activities that occupy the time of the locals in Bangalore. Nandi Hills, DD Hills, Ramanagaram, other nearby hillside destinations, and Bannerghatta national park are perfect for a hike during your tour packages to Bangalore. Amid the natural forestry, you may catch a glimpse of indigenous birds, animals, and plants that you cannot spot elsewhere.
Bangalore.Interesting facts about Bangalore: Bangalore is popular as Silicon Valley, but in its bosom, it holds many secrets which make it a hidden gem of India. Here are some of the most interesting facts about Bangalore that you must know when planning your Bangalore travel packages: Founded back in 1537, the city of Bangalore is 486 years old. Since its initiation, it has remained one of the upstanding Dravidian locations in India. Situated on the Deccan Plateau, Bangalore is the only south Indian city with a cooler temperature at a much higher altitude than its neighbouring locations. The residents of Bangalore are very spiritual despite their liberal open-mindedness. The city is covered in temples dedicated to Hindu gods and goddesses, as well as mosques, gurudwaras, Buddhist temples, Jain temples, and hundreds of churches. The city's major infrastructure is built on the founding stone initiated by Kempe Gowda, who was the ruler of ancient Bangalore. Many of the city's major landmarks are named after the fearless leader. Bangalore houses the top aerospace institutions in India, including the national aerospace laboratories as well as the HAL Aerospace or Hindustan aeronautics limited aerospace. The thriving nightlife of Bangalore makes it the pub capital of India. There are pubs, clubs, breweries, bars, and restaurants that contribute heavily to national alcohol consumption in India. Aside from promoting neo-modernism, Bangalore is also a hub of literature and art. The city celebrates the history of literature in India with grandiosity. The amateur Bangalore Radio club is the oldest in India, dating back to 1959. It has since celebrated the practical art of music and promoted many indie bands, rock bands, and classical singers who find their roots in Bangalore. There is a strong reason why Bangalore is the capital of IT, making it the Silicon Valley of India. It was the first city to acquire electric streetlights, making it a pioneer in the field of electricity and advancement, which led to other technological strides.

faq.question: What is the best time to visit Meghalaya?
faq.answer: Meghalaya has a cool climate all year through, as the summers are not as harsh as they are in some other parts of the country. However, tourists like visiting in winter, as the weather is nice. The monsoons are also very attractive as you get to see the hills and waterfalls in all their glory.

faq.question: What languages are spoken in Meghalaya?
faq.answer: English is the official language of Meghalaya, though there are many local languages too. However, when you visit the main tourist attractions, you will find a lot of people speaking in English, so communication won't be a big problem.

faq.question: Can I reach Meghalaya by train?
faq.answer: There isn't proper rail connectivity in Meghalaya, but you can take a train to Guwahati from any Indian city. Once you reach Guwahati, you can drive down to Shillong. Local buses are also available which take you to Meghalaya in an inexpensive way, after which you get to explore all the tourist places near Meghalaya.

faq.question: Is there a good Meghalaya tour package available?
faq.answer: There are many Meghalaya trip packages available. Book your trip with a reliable and reputed travel agent, and you will have a very smooth and enjoyable trip.

faq.question: Is it safe to visit Meghalaya with small children?
faq.answer: Meghalaya is a great destination for a family vacation. However, before you travel with small children or old people, you should remember that there are hilly roads where you may have to trek. There are caves where you may have to crawl in. The weather can get cold and misty after a sudden spell of rain. If you are comfortable travelling as a family under such circumstances, then you will surely have a very memorable and adventurous holiday together. You also have the option of booking a luxury hotel and spending your time there looking out of the window, and that is definitely something you can do with your whole family, including the small babies.

Meghalaya.Introduction: Known as the seven sisters, the northeastern states of India are a jewel of beauty. Nestled in the lap of the Himalayas, the northeast is quite unexplored and yet very vibrant and gorgeous. If you are looking for an offbeat holiday destination, why not plan a trip to the state of Meghalaya, which is one of the most stunning northeastern states of India? You can easily book a Meghalaya trip package and enjoy a serene and peaceful vacation in this wonderful destination. There are many tour packages available, and you can choose one that matches your requirements. You can pick the places to see in Meghalaya, the duration of your trip, and the budget after seeing how much time and money you can spare. There are numerous things to see and do in this gorgeous state, so if you have time, go on a long holiday. You can travel with your family and friends and enjoy a glorious vacation. Reaching Meghalaya is quite easy as the place is well-connected by air, rail, and road. You can find connecting flights and trains from many major Indian cities and towns. The roads are well-developed, and Meghalaya is also a very safe place to be, so you don't have to worry about anything. Just wear the planner’s hat and start preparing a fabulous itinerary. Take a look at this article to know more about famous tourist places of Meghalaya and ensure you include those places in your tour plan.

Meghalaya.Interesting Facts about Meghalaya: There are some very interesting facts about Meghalaya that you may not be aware of. Some of them are: It has the cleanest village Christianity is the main religion English is the official language Matrilineal system It has a sacred forest It has the cleanest village: As we have seen, Meghalaya has the cleanest village in Asia, Mawlynnong. This is a very unique distinction that the locals are very proud of. Christianity is the main religion: Meghalaya is one of the rare states in India where Christianity is the main religion. Over 70% of the people living in Meghalaya follow Christianity, and this is why you will find many Christian religious places in Meghalaya. English is the official language: Another unique distinction, Meghalaya’s official language is English. Though there are many tribal and local languages, such as Garo and Khasi, English is the formal language used in this fascinating state. Matrilineal system: Meghalaya is the only state in India where the matrilineal system is officially followed. Here, the men move into the mother-in-law’s house after marriage. The wife’s family name is used for the whole family, and the daughters of the family inherit the family wealth. It has a sacred forest: The Mawphlang Forest near Shillong is considered to be a sacred forest and is heavily guarded by the local tribes. No one is allowed to take anything out of the forest, including dried leaves that are scattered on the ground. Keep these interesting points in mind when you visit the famous tourist places of Meghalaya. You will then be able to appreciate the culture and understand more about Meghalaya and its people.
Meghalaya.Covid Guidelines: The pandemic that hit the world in 2020 is still lingering. Though the intensity and spread have decreased, everyone still needs to be very careful about it and ensure no amount of the infection spreads. Covid is a highly contagious disease and can affect anyone at any time. It is especially harmful when a tourist gets covid because that means getting stuck in a place that’s far away from home. To ensure no such misfortune strikes you or your family when you go on a tour, you must follow these Meghalaya travel guidelines: Get fully vaccinated, even take your 3rd shot if eligible. Carry the vaccine certificate while travelling. Use a mask when travelling. Sanitise your hands and surfaces at all times to prevent the spread of the infection. Quarantine yourself if you feel unwell. Carry test kits for quick turnaround. The covid guidelines have been relaxed to quite an extent, and the government of Meghalaya does not mandate RT-PCR tests before you enter the state. However, follow the above-mentioned covid guidelines and keep yourself and the people around you safe.
Meghalaya.Best Time to Visit in Meghalaya: Meghalaya is a rare place in India where you can go all year long. The different seasons bring different attractions, and you will have specific things to see and do all year long. Here’s a lowdown: Summer Monsoon Early Winter Peak Winter Summer The summer months in Meghalaya last from April to June. This is often considered to be the best season to visit Meghalaya as the weather is pleasant and there is bright sunshine. The temperature ranges between 15 degrees Celsius and 25 degrees Celsius, and it is quite pleasant. However, if there is unseasonal rain, the temperature drops. In general, there is sunshine during the day at this time of the year, and that makes it very easy for you to explore the sightseeing places in Meghalaya. This is also the peak tourist season, so if you are planning a vacation at this time, make sure you book your tickets well in advance. Monsoon The monsoons in Meghalaya last from July to September, and there is intense rainfall. The monsoons are attractive in Meghalaya because the state is home to Cherrapunji, which is the place that receives one of the highest amounts of rainfall in India. There are some mesmerising monsoon views to enjoy during this time on your Cherrapunjee trip. You can brave the rains and hike around the many waterfalls and enjoy some stunning and jaw-droppingly beautiful natural sights and sounds. Early winter Many people agree that the best time to visit Meghalaya is in October and November, when winter just begins to set in. The weather is wonderfully pleasant, with soft and gentle cold winds. The sun still shines brightly during the daytime, and exploring the famous tourist places in Meghalaya becomes easy. The temperatures usually fluctuate between the 20s. It is very cool but can be a bit humid, so you need to pack accordingly. Peak winter The peak winter months in Meghalaya last between December and February. It is very cold, with the lowest temperatures dipping close to the 4-degree Celsius mark. It is also very foggy and gloomy, and winter rains also happen from time to time. If you are a fan of cold and misty climes, then any month during this time may be the best month to visit Meghalaya. There are different things to enjoy in Meghalaya at different times of the year. Keep the points mentioned above in mind and plan your trip as per your preferences.
Meghalaya.How to Reach Meghalaya: As mentioned above, there are many different ways in which you can reach Meghalaya. The state is accessible by road, rail, and air. Let us take a look at it in greater detail: By road By train By air By road It is quite easy to reach Meghalaya by road. You can take a bus from Guwahati to Shillong. There are several luxury buses, Volvo AC buses, and also regular, non-AC buses available. These buses ply all through the day and night, so finding one won't be a problem. Getting into Meghalaya by bus is a great option as it offers comfort and, most importantly, is very affordable. You can reach Shillong and then take connecting buses to all the smaller cities and towns and see the beautiful places in Meghalaya. You can also visit the place by car. You can drive in your car or hire a tourist vehicle and enjoy a quaint picnic spot in Shillong on the way as well. By train Your next option is to travel to Meghalaya by train. Though there are no railway stations in Meghalaya, there is one in Guwahati, which is just about 100 km away from Shillong. You can get many trains to Guwahati from all major cities in India. Once you disembark at Guwahati, you can travel towards Shillong by road. The road is beautiful and hilly. You can make some pit stops and click some beautiful pictures and also enjoy some refreshing tea and snacks at the local roadside stalls. Travelling by train is also an economical option as compared to travelling by air. By air A lot of the tourists who travel to this beautiful northeastern state do so by air. There is an airport in Shillong, and regular flights come in from important Indian cities such as Guwahati, Delhi, and Kolkata. You can also take a flight to the Guwahati airport and take a cab or bus from there to reach Shillong or any other of Meghalaya's famous places. The air routes are not many so it is highly advisable for you to pre-book your tickets. You can also look towards the Meghalaya packages for tourists, some of which include air tickets as well. You can choose the option that suits you the best after considering factors such as the time you have, and your budget because the travel expenses would affect your overall Meghalaya trip cost.
Meghalaya.Best Honeymoon Destinations in Meghalaya: If you want to have a honeymoon in Meghalaya, you will surely have a lot of options to choose from. You can also go with a Meghalaya trip package which has been specially curated for honeymooners. Here are some of the best honeymoon destinations in Meghalaya: Shillong Jowai Tura Cherrapunji Shillong: A Shillong trip would be really great for honeymooners. Not only does it have a lot of scenic beauty, it also has many restaurants, pubs, and clubs, which makes it very suitable for young people. It has a great music scene which further adds to the charm. Jowai: Located in the middle of the gorgeous Jaintia Hills, Jowai is a serene and peaceful place. A perfect place to enjoy the company of your loved one without any distractions, Jowai is a very suitable honeymoon destination and can be listed as one among many best places to visit Meghalaya. Tura: Another scenic and secluded place nested in the Garo Hills, Tura is a beautiful hilly place that has wonderful weather all year through. It has some very impressive hotels and homestay options, and you can enjoy your Tura honeymoon. Cherrapunji: Snuggle with your beloved while it pours outside! If that sounds romantic, then you must head to Cherapunji for your honeymoon. Among the very best places to visit Meghalaya, Cherrapunji has some wonderful attractions to explore. Choose from any one of the above-mentioned Meghalaya tourist destinations and take your beloved on an unforgettable trip.
Meghalaya.Best Places to Visit in Meghalaya: As mentioned above, this is not one, but many beautiful places in Meghalaya. You can travel to different cities and towns and explore places like never before. Here are a few of them: Shillong Cherrapunji Elephant Falls Kyllang Rock Mawlynnong Village Baghmara Mawsmai Cave Williamnagar Shillong: Shillong is the biggest and the most popular place in Meghalaya. It is the capital city of Meghalaya and is a bustling city. While it has all the modern facilities in terms of schools and hospitals, it also has some of the best places to visit in Meghalaya. You can find everything from waterfalls to caves, from beautiful hills to large lakes, and from bustling hotels to busy marketplaces in the city of Shillong. It also has a big bus terminus and an airport which is connected to many large Indian cities, including Delhi and Kolkata. If you are planning a trip to Meghalaya, you will be visiting Shillong, and your stopover will be quite a memorable one. Cherrapunji: Cherrapunji is a place in Meghalaya that receives one of the highest amounts of rainfall anywhere in the world. It is often said to be the wettest place in Asia and, at times, also become the wettest place in the world. The rain is the biggest attraction of Cherapunji, but you also have many other things to see and do in this pretty Meghalayan town. The town has become very popular for its tourist attractions in Meghalaya and so it now has many good hotels and resorts where you can enjoy a comfortable and enjoyable stay. You can explore the waterfalls, hilly terrains, and stunning views that this town offers in abundance. Elephant Falls: While Niagara Falls are the pride of America, it is a huge and stunning waterfall that sees water gush down from very high up. Situated close to Shillong, this waterfall is very famous and can be considered to be one of the best and biggest waterfalls in India. When you visit Meghalaya, don't forget to add the Elephant Falls to your list of Meghalaya tourist places. Kyllang Rock: The Kyllang Rock is a monolith that juts out in the deep hills of the Khasi Range. It is a natural wonder and is definitely one of the predominantly beautiful places in Meghalaya. It is situated in the middle of a pine forest, and you can climb up to the top of the hill from where you can get some stunning views. There are endless miles of vast forests in the middle of the woods that look very picturesque. Try visiting the Kyllang Rock without fail when you go on your Meghalaya trip. Mawlynnong Village: Made very popular over the last few years, thanks to Social Media, Mawlynnong Village is a small village located in the eastern part of Meghalaya. While it is a small and quaint village amid the Khasi Hills, what sets it apart is its cleanliness. It was chosen as Asia’s Cleanest Village by Discover India Magazine and ever since has become famous. From the houses to the streets, everything is spotless in this town, and it is a truly magnificent thing to see and something that you should not miss out on. Baghmara: Situated in the deep interiors of Meghalaya, close to the border of Bangladesh, the Baghmara village is a treasure trove of activities. You can visit the famous Siju caves. You can also visit the Baghmara Reserve Forest and get to know more about flora and fauna of the place. Then, you can visit the Siju Bird Sanctuary and stroll by the banks of the river Sinsang. All in all, you will not be disappointed as there are many Meghalaya places to visit in this area. Mawsmai Cave: Meghalaya has many ancient caves, but none match up to the beauty and history of the Mawsmai Caves. The Mawsmai Caves is quite a large cave in India, and has everything from ancient relics to fossils inside them. Some parts of the cave are so narrow that you have to crawl to reach them. But if you are an explorer who loves adventure, you simply cannot afford to miss this cave. Williamnagar: Another remote village in the Garo Hills, Williamnagar, is about 245 km away from Shillong. It is very famous for its natural beauty. It has many small streams, rivers, hills, and gardens. You can have a wonderful time in this small town, which is named after Meghalaya’s first-ever Chief Minister, Williamson A. Sangma. Several other small towns and villages offer extremely gorgeous views of the hills, lakes, waterfalls, etc. Ensure you include them in your Meghalaya sightseeing plan, and you will have a very fulfilling trip.
Meghalaya.Best Hill Stations in Meghalaya: The entire state of Meghalaya is surrounded by hills, so you will find lots of pretty hill stations to explore in this place. Many Meghalaya tourism is about visiting the hill stations, so you should visit them too. Some of the most popular ones are: Shillong Mawsynram Jowai Jaintia Hills Shillong: Shillong features on most of the lists related to tourist places in Meghalaya. You can find some of the most beautiful hilly views in Shillong. It is fondly called the Scotland of the East because of its vast highlands, lakes, waterfalls, and overall serenity. Visit Shillong, and you will agree that very few hill stations in India offer the same scenic beauty that the capital of Meghalaya does. Mawsynram: This quaint little hill station in Meghalaya receives a lot of rain, and along with Cherrapunji, is said to be one of the wettest places in the world. Apart from the charm of the rain, Mawsynram also offers some stunning views. You get to explore waterfalls, beautiful peaks, caves, and other attractions when you pay a visit to this famous place of Meghalaya. Jowai: Jowais is quite a well-known hill station in the state of Meghalaya. The place is surrounded by large hills and also by small hillocks. You can climb atop a peak and enjoy the view from there for hours. If you are lucky and you happen to be there on a clear day, you can see a stunning sunrise and sunset too. Visit Jowai and experience the true natural beauty of Meghalaya. Jaintia Hills: The Jaintia Hills are spread across Meghalaya, and there are many small towns and cities around it. Choose to spend a couple of nights in the hills and come back with a lifetime of beautiful memories. Listed above are the best hill stations, which also are considered to be the best tourist places in Meghalaya.
Meghalaya.Best Beaches in Meghalaya: While Meghalaya does not have a coastline, there are some very interesting beaches to visit in the state. These are next to some large rivers. Some of them are: Ranikor River Beach Simsang Beach Nongkhnum Island Beach Damring Beach Ranikor River Beach: A wonderful and sprawling beach in the middle of some rugged mountains, this is a natural wonder that will leave you mesmerised. A lesser-known Meghalaya tourist spot, the Ranikor River beach is secluded and clean. You can enjoy some beautiful moments here, relaxing and clicking photos. You can also go for a swim in the flowing waters and have a great time. Simsang Beach: The white, sandy beach on the banks of the river Simsang is one of the most beautiful places to visit near Meghalaya. The beach is close to Shillong, and you can get some great views and also enjoy the natural wonders around. Nongkhnum Island Beach: Another secluded and hidden wonder in Meghalaya, the Nongkhnum Island Beach should feature on your list of must-visit places in Meghalaya. You will have to trek to the place, and once you reach there, you can indulge in many activities such as boating and fishing. There are also some beautiful waterfalls around, and you can just sit on the soft sand and enjoy the view around you. Damring Beach: A very hidden and secluded beach; you will have to trek and walk to reach it. But once you reach the place, you won't want to leave. It is beautifully maintained by the locals, and there are sun shades, seats, and swings for tourists to have a nice time. These are the most famous and beautiful beaches in Meghalaya. They also make for great picnic spots in Meghalaya. Visit these places and get mesmerised by the unique river beaches in the midst of the beautiful hills.
Meghalaya.What to Eat in Meghalaya?: The food in Meghalaya is as unique and delicious as most of the other attractions of Meghalaya tourism. You can find some of the most delectable dishes, and if you want to savour the local culture, you have to taste the local food. If you book any of the Meghalaya tour packages, the food may be included in it. But if you find you are being served idli dosa or rajma chawal at some upscale hotel, you may consider venturing out for some local food. Here are some of the must-have Meghalaya food items: Jadoh Doh-Khlieh Nakham Bitchi Pumaloi Kyat Jadoh: One of the most famous Meghalayan dishes, Jadoh, is made with rice and meat. It has a delicious mix of spices and tastes unique and different. It is traditionally prepared with pork, but some people also use soya beans as a meat replacer. It is a popular dish, and you will find it everywhere when you explore the best places in Meghalaya. Doh-Khlieh: Doh-Khlieh, is a rustic pork salad. It is healthy and delicious and comprises pork pieces along with herbs and seasoning. It is quite the staple and is cooked in most Meghalayan households on a regular basis. Nakham Bitchi: A very spicy and aromatic dish, the Nakham Bitchi is made with dried fish. It has lots of chillies and is very suitable for the cold climate of Meghalaya. In Meghalaya, the best time to visit is often said to be the cold winters. If you happen to be there on a cold and chilly evening, savour some Nakham Bitchi, and your body and soul will be warmed up. Pumaloi: A steamed rice cake is another staple that is cooked in every household and consumed for breakfast, lunch, and dinner. Pumaloi is very healthy and doesn't have any oil or spice, and is usually eaten with a side dish of meat or veggies. Kyat: Kyat is a fermented rice beer that is locally produced. It has a funky taste that the locals and tourists swear by. It also has a warming quality, so it is very suitable for the weather in the state. Sip some soothing Kyat when you visit Meghalaya next. The cuisine of Meghalaya is predominantly non-vegetarian, with a lot of emphasis on pork. If you are a vegetarian or do not eat certain types of meat for any other reason, your best bet would be to look for simple dishes like dal-rice at your hotel. You can also buy bread, milk, fruits, packaged foods, etc. from any supermarket and munch on them. Enjoy your vacation but remember to keep your tummy filled up and also drink plenty of water when you venture out to visit the famous places in Meghalaya.
Meghalaya.What to Shop in Meghalaya?: Shopping is an integral part of any holiday so when you go on your Meghalaya tour, make sure you carry back some of the local items. Here are the best things to shop for in the state of Meghalaya: Items from Polo Bazar Goodies from Bara Bazar/Lewduh Bazar Bamboo handicrafts Food items Items from Polo Bazar: Polo Bazar is an upscale market in the capital city of Shillong. It is a modern marketplace with shops that sell all kinds of items, from clothes to packaged food. This is a good place to start your shopping. It is organised and easy to get around in. Goodies from Bara Bazar/Lewduh Bazar: The Bara Bazar/Lewduh Bazar is a predominantly old and big market in Meghalaya. Situated in the heart of Shillong, the market has several small lanes and bylanes with shops and stalls on either side. There is nothing that you can't find here! From fashion to fish, everything is sold, and that too at some of the most reasonable rates. Bamboo handicrafts: Traditional bamboo handicrafts are a landmark of Meghalaya. Hence, you should not miss the chance to buy some locally-produced bamboo items such as hats, lampshades, etc. These are usually made by the tribals at home and sold at showrooms. You can also find many people sitting on the side of the road and selling these beautiful items. Buy them not just to support the local businesses but also to come back with a piece of Meghalaya with you. Food items: Look to get some local food back with you. You will get many options, such as pickles and oils, so stock up on them when you go shopping in Meghalaya. Don't forget to go on a shopping spree after you finish exploring the famous tourist places in Meghalaya.
Meghalaya.Things to do in Meghalaya: As mentioned above, there are many places to visit in Meghalaya. You can find some excellent attractions in each city. Here are some of them: Things to do in Shillong Things to do in Cherrapunji Things to do in the rural areas Things to do in Shillong: From exploring the markets of the busy city to chilling in a cool cafe, there are a variety of things to do in Meghalaya’s capital, Shillong. You can also explore the many natural wonders, such as Elephant Falls and Ward’s Lake. Things to do in Cherrapunji: The next place to explore is Cherrapunji. You can go around the town and admire the rugged, mountainous roads and watch a huge waterfall. There are many waterfalls, hills, and lakes all over the town, and you will not have a single dull moment here. Things to do in the rural areas: The rural areas of Meghalaya have their own charm. From the cleanest village of Mawlynnong to the very cool Williamnagar, the countryside of Meghalaya has many gems on offer. As you can see, there is never a single dull moment when you venture out to find a famous place of Meghalaya. Explore all the options listed above and have a great vacation.
Meghalaya.Adventurous things to do in Meghalaya: The state of Meghalaya is quite diverse, and as a result, you get the opportunity to try out some of the most adventurous activities, some of which are: Trekking Exploring the caves Bathing under a waterfall Scuba Diving Adventure sports Trekking: Since the vast majority of Meghalaya is covered in hills and mountains, you get a lot of opportunities to go on treks. While some treks are short and enjoyable, others are long and require a professional guide’s help. Depending on which one you choose, make the proper arrangements and start exploring. Exploring the caves: The various caves of the state are the best sightseeing places in Meghalaya. Many old and interesting caves offer you the chance to see something unique. Rich in history as well as physical features, these caves are large and scenic. Make sure you go inside the caves and learn more about them. Bathing under a waterfall: Waterfalls will greet you at every step in Meghalaya. These gorgeous waterfalls are tall and gushing. Many of the famous tourist places near Meghalaya such as Cherrapunji, are scattered around waterfalls. When you are in Meghalaya, don't forget to visit some waterfalls and if possible, even bathe under one. Scuba Diving: While there aren't any coastal places to see in Meghalaya, there are some very big and clean rivers, such as the Umngot River. And here, you get to scuba dive. Yes, that’s right! You can actually go scuba diving in Meghalaya in the calm and still waters of the Umngot River in Dawki. It is a fascinating experience that you should not miss. Adventure sports: There are various types of adventure sports that you can try out while you vacation in Meghalaya. From zipline to rock climbing, you will find a lot to do, so keep exploring. Not every tourist place you visit will offer such a variety of adventurous things. So when you are in Meghalaya, don't forget to try out all the adventurous activities mentioned above while you continue to explore Meghalaya best places to visit.

Ho Chi Minh City.About Ho Chi Minh City: Ho Chi Minh City, formerly called Saigon, is Vietnam’s most populous city. This bustling city offers a quintessential tourism experience that showcases the smooth blend of Vietnam’s rapid modernity and age-old culture and traditions. The cityscape of Ho Chi Minh City is dominated by skyscrapers as well as vibrant markets, cultural landmarks, colonial architecture, etc. It is also a prominent hub for entertainment and culinary delights. All these aspects make for an ideal holiday experience, which the Ho Chi Minh tour package by Thomas Cook covers. From learning about the rich history of the city while exploring the War Remnants Museum and Cu Chi Tunnels to walking the Bui Vien Walking Street for shopping and nightouts, Ho Chi Minh City caters to diverse vacation needs. Make the most out of your holidays with the Ho Chi Minh tour package by Thomas Cook. We offer pre-arranged Ho Chi Minh package options for all kinds of themed vacations. The Ho Chi Minh tour package on our website starts from only INR 39,700. During booking, you can also avail of attractive discounts of up to 13% off on select Ho Chi Minh tour package options. Check out Thomas Cook for limited-time exclusive offers on pre-arranged Ho Chi Minh trip plans
Ho Chi Minh City.Holidays in Ho Chi Minh City: Ho Chi Minh City, formerly called Saigon, is located in Vietnam’s southeastern region. The current official name of the city was adopted to commemorate one of the most influential leaders of the region, Ho Chi Minh (a.k.a Uncle Ho). It is the largest city in the country in terms of population and covers an area of around 2,061 square kilometres. Ho Chi Minh City (Saigon) served as the capital of South Vietnam till 1975 and was also the capital of French Indochina from 1887 to 1902. Alongside being a major centre of historical significance, the metropolitan beauty, local culture and traditional life of Ho Chi Minh City have made it a massive site of tourism in Asia. Ho Chi Minh City also works as an ideal starting point for your Vietnam tour. So, if you have selected Vietnam as your next international trip location, add Ho Chi Minh City to your list of must-visit places. If you want to go through the authentic Vietnam tour experience, you can consider our Classic Vietnam tour package that includes the major cities, including Saigon (Ho Chi Minh City). And if you are planning on including Saigon as a part of your Vietnam and Cambodia trip, you may consider this Ho Chi Minh City travel package.
Ho Chi Minh City.How to reach Ho Chi Minh City: In terms of connectivity, Ho Chi Minh City is well-linked with other parts of Vietnam and various regions of the world. Here are the best ways to reach Ho Chi Minh City: Reaching Ho Chi Minh City by air Reaching Ho Chi Minh City by rail Reaching Ho Chi Minh City by road Reaching Ho Chi Minh City by air For your Ho Chi Minh trip, travelling by air Travelling to Ho Chi Minh City by air is the best option for both domestic travellers and international visitors. The main airport serving Ho Chi Minh City is called Tan Son Nhat International Airport, located just 5 miles from the city. It features daily domestic flights from and to major Vietnamese cities like Hanoi, Hue, Nha Trang, Da Nang, Ca Mau, and so on. It also has an international terminal operating daily. For instance, if you are travelling from Delhi, Mumbai, Kolkata or any other major Indian cities, you can easily opt for a one or multi-stop flight to Tan Son Nhat International Airport and then take a 20-minute cab ride to reach Ho Chi Minh City. Make sure to book flight tickets for your Ho Chi Minh trip in advance. Book a Ho Chi Minh tour package with included flights on Thomas Cook. Reaching Ho Chi Minh City by rail Reaching Ho Chi Minh city by train is only a sensible option for individuals travelling from other parts of Vietnam. The Saigon Train Station is its main railway hub and features daily trains from and to other Vietnamese cities. Reaching Ho Chi Minh City by road Ho Chi Minh city is served by two main bus terminals and features daily buses from various Vietnamese cities, including Da Lat, Hanoi, Da Nang, etc. Taking Highway 1 from Hanoi to Ho Chi Minh City in a shared taxi is also a way of reaching by road. You can find a Ho Chi Minh package that also include other popular destinations in Vietnam, like Hanoi, Ha Long Bay, etc.
Ho Chi Minh City.Travelling within Ho Chi Minh City: Once you enter Ho Chi Minh City, you don’t have to worry about local transfers. The city has an extensive local transport network, with options like; Taxi: Taxis are the most convenient way to travel within the city during the Ho Chi Minh trip. However, it can get a bit expensive as you will be charged a base fee and a price per KM. Cyclo: These are 3-wheeled vehicles resembling cycle-rickshaws in India. For short-distance travel, a cyclo can be ideal. Local bus: The local bus is a cheap way to get around the city on a Ho Chi Minh trip. It covers over 100 routes across the city and features A/C and comfy seats. Rental bikes and cycles: To enjoy the liberty of exploring the city at your own accord, you can get a cycle or motorbike/scooter on rent. Also, note that a Ho Chi Minh tour package usually covers local transfers. Xe Om: Alternatively, you can try Xe Om or a motorcycle taxi on your Ho Chi Minh trip. It is quite inexpensive and helps you get to your destination without being affected by the traffic.
Ho Chi Minh City.Best time to Visit Ho Chi Minh City: Generally, the dry season (December to March) is considered the best time to visit Ho Chi Minh City. The average temperature range around this period usually remains from 20 to 32°C, with an absence of precipitation for the most part. It features pleasant weather for tourists, allowing them to explore the city without hindrance. This period also features some of the major events and festivals in the city, including Christmas, Gregorian New Year, Tet festival, and so on. During this peak season, the month of January is often referred to as the best time to go to Ho Chi Minh City. Consider booking your Ho Chi Minh tour package in advance to ensure availability. The time from April to July is considered shoulder season for a Ho Chi Minh tour . The average temperature range is usually 24 to 35°C, with a high frequency of rainfall. Tourists usually avoid visiting Ho Chi Minh City during this time as rainfall can often hinder their sightseeing plans. However, this is the best time to visit Ho Chi Minh City for the shoulder season discounts and notable events, including Reunification Day, International Labor Day, Southern Fruit Festival, etc. The off-season for a Ho Chi Minh tour stretches from August to November. Apart from a few notable Ho Chi Minh City holidays and festivals, rain remains prevalent throughout and only starts to slow down in late November. If your Ho Chi Minh City visit is part of a comprehensive Vietnam tour, make sure to learn about the Best time to visit Vietnam as a whole. Find seasonal deals on Ho Chi Minh tour package options on Thomas Cook
Ho Chi Minh City.Ho Chi Minh City COVID-19 tourist guide: A trip to Ho Chi Minh City or any other Vietnamese city is surely exciting, however, travellers must stay informed and follow the regional COVID-19 travel safety guidelines to ensure their, as well as others’ safety. You can check out our detailed International Travel Guidelines page to get details on various popular holiday destinations across Asia and other parts of the world. In addition, don’t forget to wear a face mask, follow personal hygiene, avoid travelling with a cough or fever, and other general COVID-19 health and safety measures.
Ho Chi Minh City.Best Places to Visit in Ho Chi Minh City: Amidst the usual metropolitan cityscape views of Ho Chi Minh City, there are numerous exciting tourist landmarks you can check out. Here are some of the must-visit sites in Ho Chi Minh City: Cu Chi Tunnels Independence Palace Notre Dame Cathedral Basilica of Saigon War Remnants Museum Thien Hau Temple Ho Chi Minh City Museum Cu Chi Tunnels Located about 50 kilometres from the city centre, the Cu Chi Tunnels are one of the most interesting places in Ho Chi Minh City. It is an extensive network of several underground tunnels with an estimated length of more than 250 kilometres. The Cu Chi Tunnels served as a place for hiding, supply routes, hospitals, and other facilities for the Viet Cong during various war eras. It is the perfect place to visit on a Ho Chi Minh tour for history buffs. The Cu Chi Tunnels are also one of the must-visit places in all of Vietnam and a prominent inclusion in several Ho Chi Minh tour package options. Independence Palace The Independence Palace, also known as Reunification Convention Hall, is one of the major landmarks and interesting places in Ho Chi Minh City. This building served as the workplace and residence of the then-president of South Vietnam after the split. However, on April 30th, 1975, a tank of the North Vietnamese army crashed through the palace’s gates, marking the end of the ongoing Vietnam War. This huge building and its dated architecture stand today as a representation of Vietnam’s reunification. Besides seeing the notable features of the palace, like basement tunnels, war command rooms, etc., tourists can opt for a guided tour if they want to learn more about the site’s historical significance. Explore more iconic landmarks like this with a Ho Chi Minh tour package from Thomas Cook. Notre Dame Cathedral Basilica of Saigon The Notre Dame Cathedral Basilica of Saigon was established by French colonists during the late 19th century. Today, its remarkable architectural work and link to Vietnam’s history have made it a massive site of tourist interest. Apart from its grand interior, this red-brick structure features two tall bell towers that stand tall at 190 feet. It is one of the most beautiful structures and interesting places in Ho Chi Minh City. Look for a Ho Chi Minh package featuring this site on our website. War Remnants Museum Among the various museums in Ho Chi Minh City, the War Remnants Museum holds a major place of importance. It opened for public viewing back in 1975 and became one of the most visited museums in the world in recent times. This museum features over 20,000 documented exhibits, with the majority belonging to the Vietnam War era. Featured in popular Ho Chi Minh package options, this museum successfully illustrates the horrifying history and brutality of war to a certain extent. Some famous display items in the museum include various graphics, photos, tiger cages, military vehicles, weapons, guillotine, etc. This museum is also one of the most interesting places in Ho Chi Minh City. Thien Hau Temple The Thien Hau Temple in Ho Chi Minh City is one of the most famous religious landmarks in the region. It was built by a Chinese group that used to reside in the Saigon region. The temple’s beautiful architecture and divine atmosphere attract both locals and international visitors on their Ho Chi Minh tour. The temple is dedicated to Thien Hau, the Chinese goddess of the Sea, and has a very interesting story associated with the site. It is one of the top attractions in Ho Chi Minh City, located in the Chinatown area. Get great deals on a Ho Chi Minh package with limited-time offers from Thomas Cook. Ho Chi Minh City Museum The Ho Chi Minh City Museum, previously known as Gia Long Palace, is a must-visit site near the Independence Palace. It is one of the top attractions in Ho Chi Minh City for history buffs, and you can find it in the itinerary of several Ho Chi Minh package options Beneath the site of this fine establishment lies a series of underground connecting tunnels and refuge bunkers previously used during the time of war. Even though they are not offered for public viewing, the exhibits within the museum are equally interesting. These display items are connected to Saigon’s culture, handicrafts, nature, and struggle for independence.
Ho Chi Minh City.What to Eat in Ho Chi Minh City: Apart from the top attractions in Ho Chi Minh City, the local cuisine is also quite famous among visitors. Here are some of the best dishes to try in Ho Chi Minh City: Banh mi- This food item is a local favourite and can be found across various eateries and street food vendors in Ho Chi Minh City. It is prepared by taking a baguette and then filling it with a healthy serving of chopped vegetables, meat, sauces and other flavourful goodness. It can be a go-to snack to fill your hunger while exploring the city during a Ho Chi Minh tour. Bun bo hue- Bun bo hue is a delectable food option and a must-try dish in Ho Chi Minh City. It is a bowl of rice noodle soup made with chopped pork and has a thick but tasty broth. Even though the dish originated in Hue city, it is a popular street food item in Ho Chi Minh City. Goi cuon- Goi cuon is simply one of the best Vietnamese cuisines you must try on your Ho Chi Minh tour. It is a type of spring roll, ideally made with pork, shrimp, vegetables and some other rich ingredients. It is served alongside a bowl of spicy dipping sauce and makes up for a fantastic light meal or snack. Grilled meat and seafood- While exploring Ho Chi Minh City, make sure to give the local streetside grilled meat and seafood a go. Apart from being delicious, you get tons of options to pick from, including pork, chicken, prawns, crabs, lobsters and so on. While on the topic of food, note that meals are a standard inclusion of most Ho Chi Minh package options from Thomas Cook.
Ho Chi Minh City.Things to Do in Ho Chi Minh City: The massive area of Ho Chi Minh City features tons of exciting activities that you can do for a memorable vacation. Here are the best things to do in Ho Chi Minh City: Take a 360° view of Ho Chi Minh City- While on your Ho Chi Minh tour, make sure to check out the Bitexco Financial Tower, just 10 kilometres from the city centre. It is one of the tallest buildings in all of Vietnam and features a Skydeck on its 49th floor. You can catch a unique 360° view of Ho Chi Minh City and take marvellous photographs. Learn about the local history- There are various sites across Ho Chi Minh City where you can learn more about the rich history of Vietnam. From multiple museums and underground tunnels to significant places and landmarks, Ho Chi Minh City is an iconic place for history buffs. Go on a river cruise- There are multiple options for dinner cruises and sunset city boat tours along the Mekong delta that you can consider during your Ho Chi Minh tour. It offers various facilities, like guided tours, sightseeing, live music, tasty food, etc., and makes up for an excellent way to spend some time in Ho Chi Minh City. Catch a show at Saigon Opera House- If you are searching for some live entertainment on your Ho Chi Minh tour, make sure to catch a show at the famous Saigon Opera House. Apart from the amazing night visuals of the building itself, the Saigon Opera House hosts various events, including classical music, conventional theatre plays, ballet, concerts and so on.
Ho Chi Minh City.Forex/Currency for Ho Chi Minh City: When visiting a foreign holiday destination, your best chance at handling the expenses you make there will be by using their local currency. For example, the main currency used in Ho Chi Minh City is the Vietnamese Dong, and you will need it during your Ho Chi Minh tour to pay for the purchases you make there. In today’s day and age, getting hold of foreign currency over the internet is as easy as ever. For example, you can use Thomas Cook India’s foreign exchange services and get foreign currency notes or forex cards in just a few steps. There are also multiple payment modes and foreign currency options that you can pick from.
Ho Chi Minh City.Visa for Ho Chi Minh City: Similar to other trips abroad, you will be required to apply for a visa in order to visit Ho Chi Minh City. However, as Vietnam offers an eVisa facility for over 80 countries, getting a visa for the Ho Chi Minh City tour is very simple for individuals meeting the eligibility criteria. For example, Indians travelling to Ho Chi Minh City can apply for a Vietnamese eVisa in order to enter the country. The application process for this eVisa is explained on the official Vietnam eVisa website. This eVisa has a 30-day validity, which is perfect for most Ho Chi Minh City trip plans. In addition, if you are a citizen of a country under the Vietnam Visa Exemption list, you can travel to Ho Chi Minh City without a visa if you intend to stay within the stipulated period.

faq.question: How many days are ideal for a Nepal trip?
faq.answer: It truly relies on your desired experience. Seven to ten days should be sufficient if all you desire is a taste of the local culture and scenic splendour. A two-week Nepal vacation package often includes sightseeing in Kathmandu, Pokhara, and sometimes a quick hike like Ghorepani Poon Hill. You can delve deeper into trekking, animal safaris, or cultural vacations if you have three weeks or longer. Many tourists who buy a trekking-focused Nepal package stay for at least two weeks.

faq.question: Is Nepal safe for solo female travellers/trekkers?
faq.answer: Yes, Nepal is generally nice, safe, and hospitable. Nevertheless, it's crucial to use caution, just like you would on any vacation. Avoid going out alone at night, stay in respectable hotels, and keep your stuff safe in busy places. The government now mandates a licensed guide for trekking, which is beneficial for support and safety. Women who travel alone frequently discover that having a guide or going with a group brings more peace of mind, particularly in isolated places.

faq.question: What is the best way to travel within Nepal?
faq.answer: Due to the sluggish road conditions in Nepal, domestic flights are the quickest means of transportation between major towns like Kathmandu and Pokhara. In order to save time, many Nepal tour packages with flight prices. Another low-cost travel option is the tourist bus, which is more comfortable than the local bus and is frequently used on routes like Kathmandu to Pokhara. Hiring a private vehicle and driver allows you greater freedom, although the expense of the journey to Nepal does increase.

faq.question: Do I need a visa for Nepal?
faq.answer: Travelling from India only requires a passport or voter ID; a visa is not required. When arriving at the Kathmandu airport or land borders, people of other nationalities can typically get a tourist visa. A valid passport, a few passport-sized pictures, and the payment in US dollars are required. In order to save time at the airport, many people who are applying for a Nepal tour package with flight price find it better to make these arrangements online before they travel.

faq.question: What are the best treks for beginners?
faq.answer: Nepal offers some amazing short routes if you're interested in trekking but have never done it before. The four to five-day Ghorepani Poon Hill walk is ideal, offering breathtaking views of the Himalayan sunrise. A slightly longer but manageable experience for beginners is the Langtang Valley walk. The Balthali Village hike, which is near Kathmandu, is a simple way to experience mountain scenery and village life. These are frequently included in specially crafted Nepal holiday packages for first-time visitors.

faq.question: What kind of clothes should I carry?
faq.answer: Keep layers in mind when packing. Essentials include a waterproof jacket for unexpected showers, a fleece or sweater for chilly evenings, and lightweight tops and pants for warm days. Comfortable footwear is crucial, and if you're trekking, don't forget to pack thermal layers, warm socks, and sturdy hiking boots. It will also be helpful to have sunscreen, a flashlight, and a reusable water bottle. For those opting for a Nepal honeymoon trip or a city-focused Nepal vacation package, a combination of casual attire and somewhat more elegant dining attire is sufficient. When visiting temples, keep in mind that modesty is respected; therefore, cover your knees and shoulders.

Nepal.Introduction: Picture yourself standing in front of the majestic Himalayas, with prayer flags flapping softly in the breeze and the snow-capped peaks gleaming in the sunlight. That is the enchantment you experience when you first arrive in Nepal, a country where spirituality, culture, and nature all harmoniously coexist. A Nepal vacation is just what you need if you've ever wanted to wake up to stunning mountain views, explore historic temples, or feel the friendliness of the locals. Nepal.Why Nepal is a Must-Visit Destination?: The Himalayas are the first thing that springs to mind when you think of Nepal, yet this country is much more than simply snow-capped mountains. Here are some reasons why you shouldn't pass on a Nepal tour, which is a voyage into culture, nature, and unforgettable encounters. Multicultural Rainbow Nepal is home to a diverse range of customs, dialects, and lively celebrations. Every region of the nation feels like a discovery because there are more than 35 different ethnic groupings. You can easily immerse yourself in this cultural diversity by selecting from Nepal tour packages or a flexible Nepal travel plan. Voyage for All Souls Nepal is made for adventurers, whether they want to go on an animal safari in Chitwan, paraglide in Pokhara, or hike to Everest Base Camp. Heritage That Speaks History permeates every stone and pagoda, from the revered Lumbini birthplace of Lord Buddha to the captivating Kathmandu Valley Durbar Squares. Romance in the Himalayas A Nepal honeymoon package is ideal for couples who want to begin their journey in the middle of nature since it provides tranquil lakes, cosy mountain lodges, and stunning views. A Nepal package is among the most cost-effective travel experiences in Asia because everything in the nation, from meals to lodging, is so reasonably priced.
Nepal.Planning Your Nepal Trip: Essential Info: It's exciting to plan a vacation to Nepal, but in order to get the most out of your trip, you must know when to go, how to get there, what paperwork you'll need, and how to get around while you're there. Having these specifics at your fingertips ensures a smooth and unforgettable journey, whether you are travelling through Nepal tour packages, a customised Nepal vacation package, or even a luxury Nepal honeymoon package.
Nepal.Best Time to Visit Nepal: From the cold Himalayan peaks to the warm Terai plains, Nepal's diverse topography causes variations in its climate. What you wish to encounter will determine the ideal timing, making it essential to consider the best time to visit in Nepal based on your travel preferences. Autumn (September-November): Autumn is the most popular time of year for organising trekking excursions. Major festivals like Dashain and Tihar give colour to your Nepal visit; the sky is beautiful, and the Himalayan views are stunning. However, because it's the busiest time of year, airfare and hotel rates for trips to Nepal are typically higher. It's a good idea to plan ahead when purchasing Nepal trip packages or Nepal travel packages from India. Winter (December-February): Perfect for city-based vacations, Chitwan safaris, and cultural experiences. Kathmandu and Pokhara are still comfortable despite the frigid high mountains. This is also when a Nepal honeymoon package feels extra cosy. Spring (March-May): Ideal for those who enjoy the outdoors. The weather is nice and the hills are covered in rhododendrons. If you reserve a Nepal trip package during this period, you will experience both adventure and the beauty of the flowers on the bustling trekking trails. Monsoon (June-August): Although trekking becomes challenging due to heavy rains, areas like Mustang are still reachable. Since it's a slower time of year, you will frequently find an affordable Nepal tour package with a flight price within your budget.
Nepal.How to Reach Nepal?: It's easy to get to Nepal, especially from India. By Air: The primary entrance point is Kathmandu's Tribhuvan International Airport. Nepal tour packages from India are quite convenient because of the direct flights from Delhi, Mumbai, Kolkata, and other cities. Travellers from other countries frequently pass through major cities like Bangkok, Dubai, or Doha. By Train: You can go by train from India to border towns like Raxaul or Gorakhpur, and then continue on by road. The Jaynagar-Kurtha railway line recently established a direct connection between India and Nepal. By Road: Sunauli, Raxaul-Birgunj, and Panitanki-Kakarbhitta are some of the border crossings into Nepal from India if you'd rather take a more picturesque route. You can drive with a Nepal package that includes transportation arrangements, or you can take direct buses from places like Delhi and Varanasi.
Nepal.Visa Requirements for Nepal: The good news is that Indian citizens do not require a visa in order to travel to Nepal. Simply keep a valid voter ID card or passport with you. Nepal provides visas for foreign nationals upon arrival at the airport and at the country's land borders. All you need is a six-month-valid passport, a photo, and the visa fee. Travel packages to Nepal are appealing to foreign tourists because of this flexibility.
Nepal.Getting Around in Nepal: Travelling around Nepal is an adventure in and of itself, and how you get around will influence how you go. Buses: Important cities like Kathmandu, Chitwan, and Pokhara are connected by tourist buses. They are reasonably priced and cosy. Although local buses are less expensive, they are slower and frequently packed. For added comfort, a lot of Nepal trip packages offer reserved bus transportation. Domestic Flights: Domestic flights are ideal for short trips, such as those from Kathmandu to Pokhara or to isolated trekking airports like Lukla. However, bear in mind that delays due to weather are frequent. Trekking on Foot: The greatest method to get through the Himalayas is sometimes on foot. Guides and porters are typically included in trekkers' Nepal trip packages, ensuring a safe and enjoyable experience. Taxis and Ride-Sharing: Taxis are easily located in urban areas. For a reasonable price, always haggle or use ride-hailing applications like Pathao or InDrive. For convenience, a Nepal package can also include a private vehicle.
